Full unit suite (`test/roadGeometry.test.js` ‚Äî the pure layer never referenced `settings.snap`, so unchanged, 68 road-geom cases green) + lint 0 errors + build green; `MAP.md` drift clean (no export change).

### B694 ‚Äî County / city / ETJ boundary layers ride the cached vector tier: instant paint, no 503 stalls `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature ‚Äî amends B96, touches B176/B287) #site-planner #gis #perf  *(owner brief 2026-07-07, arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B694**. Net-new ‚Äî the boundary-layer consumer B96 anticipated. LIVE-CONFIRMED via V234 on 2026-07-22.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks (Verify: live ‚Äî GIS endpoint behavior is a mandatory live class; the sandbox egress proxy RESETS browser connections to services.arcgis.com, so the live pull can only be confirmed on planyr.io) ‚Üí V234.**
- Verify: live ‚Üí **V234 ‚Äî ‚úÖ CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-22** (Cowork, live on Bain `smr9olizi5ue`): toggling County boundaries + City limits & ETJ off then on again fires ZERO new network requests (served from the client cache; geometry cached in localStorage under `planyr:giscache:v1:juris:county:poly:zoom_bbox`), and the City/ETJ lines render correctly (Houston ETJ / City of Katy edge). Sub-claims left untested ‚Üí spun to **V404**: the cold statewide 254-county paint + time-to-paint, the reload/vintage-age cache line + pan-to-Dallas, the z‚â•13 City/ETJ detail tier, and the offline / dead-endpoint honesty regressions. See VERIFICATION-DONE.md V234.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat
- RENUMBERED on merge-in of `origin/main` 2026-07-07 ‚Äî concurrent sessions took B693 (left-menu de-cream) + B694 (husk-parcel render guard) + B695 (Fort Bend contours CORS proxy) + B696 (husk root-cause) and V233; this branch's B693‚ÄìB696 / V233‚ÄìV235 became **B693‚ÄìB696 / V234‚ÄìV235** (= highest across both files + 1). Code comments were bumped to match.
- **AUDIT-FIRST correction to the brief:** the brief said the vector tier "already shipped for FEMA/NWI" ‚Äî in fact `lib/vectorLayers.js` was UNWIRED groundwork (its only consumer was its own test; the earlier `vectorOverlay.js` had been deleted as unwired, and FEMA/NWI render via the raster f=image path to this day). This session built the tier's **first live consumer**. ‚öÝ Standing discrepancy left in place: `VECTOR_SOURCES.wetlands.query.url` still points at the dead `fwspublicservices` host (dormant row, no runtime consumer ‚Äî repoint it if/when NWI vectors get wired).
- **What shipped:** `jur_county` / `jur_city` / `jur_etj` flip `kind:"esriFeature"` ‚Üí **`kind:"vector"`** and render through the new **`lib/vectorOverlay.js`** (`cachedVectorLayer`): paint the last-good copy from the browser SWR cache (B96 `gisCache`) instantly, refresh in the background, swap fresh geometry in via a new `fetchCached onFresh` passthrough, and report `{ts, stale}` so the panel shows the copy's age. Engine additions (`lib/vectorLayers.js`, all pure + unit-tested): **detail tiers** (`pickTier`) with **server-side generalization** (`maxAllowableOffset` ‚Äî the agency thins vertices before the wire, replacing client Douglas‚ÄìPeucker for tiered pulls, which is the brief's per-zoom simplify done cheaper), tier-stamped cache keys (`vectorKey`), and **outward grid-snap** (`snapBbox`) so pans reuse entries.
- **Cache design (measured live 2026-07-07 via curl):** county coarse tier = ONE statewide source-level entry (`vec:jur_county:all@0.002`, 254 counties, **337 KB** @0.002¬∞ ‚âà 200 m ‚Äî fits the 512 KB `maxEntryBytes` cap, so no per-county split; the brief's open question, answered empirically); ETJ coarse = one H-GAC-region entry (608 features, **204 KB** @0.001¬∞); city is always bbox-scoped (statewide too heavy; a metro cell ‚âà **120 KB**). Fine tiers (z‚â•12/13) are bbox-scoped @0.0002¬∞ on a snap grid. TTLs: county 30 d, city/ETJ 14 d (annexations/SB2038 move these occasionally; SWR shows age either way). Per B96's own note, the **refreshed-age stamp folded into the B236 vintage line** ‚Äî one surface: `as of: <vintage> ¬∑ refreshed <age> (updating‚Ä¶)`, stale in `--warn-text` (the standalone age chip is gone).
- **Never-blank fallback (the decideVectorOrImage intent, adapted):** boundaries are always-vector (no image service exists) ‚Äî `minVectorZoom:0`/`maxAreaDeg:‚àû` so no gate can flip them; a **failed pull with nothing cached** engages the injected live fallback = the previous esri-leaflet `featureLayer` path **with its B287 retry/backoff**, LOUD through the same status channel (`liveFallback`, dependency-injected from `layers.js` so `vectorOverlay` stays esri-leaflet-free). A cold-cache fetch failure now THROWS in `fetchCached` instead of resolving a silent null (LOUD-FAILURE). B176 invariant preserved: same `GIS_SOURCES` endpoints as the jurisdiction identify ‚Äî the line you see is the line the identify reports.
- **Verified here (sandbox):** 48 vectorLayers unit tests (tiers/snap/keys/onFresh/cold-throw/registry) + headless with a seeded cache + the TxDOT host BLOCKED: boundaries painted in **~50 ms with zero network**, one statewide cache entry (`vec:jur_county:all@0.002p4`), folded age line rendering, toggle-off clears clean, zero page errors. The correctly-shaped statewide query (no envelope + `maxAllowableOffset=0.002`) observed leaving the browser before the sandbox proxy reset it ‚Äî the live leg is V234.
- **üîç Adversarially reviewed (2026-07-07, 4 dimensions; verify agents hit the session token cap so every finding was verified BY HAND against the code): 18 raw findings ‚Üí 10 root causes confirmed, all fixed same-session.** The theme: the overlay's race guards and the fallback's honesty. Fixed: (1) `engageFallback` + the zoom-gate branch now bump the request `seq` ‚Äî an in-flight fetch can no longer paint stale vectors OVER the live fallback or BELOW the zoom gate; (2) a SUPERSEDED request's failure no longer latches `lastVectorError` (and a successful pull clears it) ‚Äî a mid-pan blip can't permanently downgrade the layer to the fallback; (3) the fallback layer gets the `setOpacity` shim esri-leaflet FeatureLayers lack (the opacity slider was dead after engagement) and reports "loading", not a false-healthy "loaded", right after the failure that engaged it; (4) the basemap status dot keys off `tileload` ‚Äî Leaflet fires layer-`load` even when EVERY tile errored, which showed green over a gray canvas; (5) a DFW-scale city cell measured **560 KB stored** (over the 512 KB cap ‚Üí silent L1-only) ‚Äî the widest city tier now thins at 0.003¬∞/3 dp with a mid tier at z11‚Äì12; (6) cache keys stamp `precision` too (`@0.002p4`) so a registry retune busts the cache instead of serving old-detail geometry for a TTL; (7) the identify popover closes on toggle-off/fallback (it used to outlive the layer); (8) a FAILED background refresh clears `lastKey` so the next map move retries (was: "(updating‚Ä¶)" forever with no retry); (9) cold pulls no longer paint twice (swr's cold-fetch `onFresh` is skipped via the `lastKey` guard); (10) select mode neutralizes the interactive fill's `cursor:pointer` (`.pf-select-mode` + `pf-boundary-hit`, index.css) so the +/‚àí parcel cursor survives ‚Äî B98 extends to the cursor. **Accepted as documented limitations:** gisCache's oldest-write-first eviction could recycle the standing statewide entry under a full 3 MB namespace (a later re-pull, not a failure ‚Äî changing B96's shared eviction policy would touch the identify/overpass consumers), and an over-cap entry staying L1-only is graceful degradation by design.
> **Dedup:** consumes B96's cache exactly as it anticipated; keeps B287's retry as the fallback leg; does NOT collide with B371 (analysis-side offline pre-cache, still Later) or B629 (parcels).

The four programmatic `setBasemapOn(true)` call sites (identify mode, analysis-layer framing, geocoded add) became `ensureBasemapOn()` ‚Äî turns the aerial on without stomping a user-chosen USGS source.

- **Fix (folder-id targeting through the whole stack):** the server `treeParentForUpload` now accepts an explicit `folderId` (a `project_folders` row id) that resolves straight to that row's mirrored Drive folder, winning over the discipline route (`x-planyr-folder-id` header, honored by `functions/api/files.js` + `‚Ä¶/resumable.js`). `reviewStore` threads `folderId` through `fileNewReview`/`storeSource`/`pushFileToDrive`/`uploadLargeToDrive` and persists it on the review (`data.folderId`, surfaced by `listReviews`, no DB migration). `FileBrowser` files a drop into the selected folder (skip auto-file, `needsFiling:false`), `placedFolder` honours `f.folderId` over the discipline placement, and the drop-strip copy + drop-anywhere overlay now say exactly where a drop lands ("Filing into 02. Electric" vs. "drop drawings on All files to auto-sort by title block").
- **Also folded in (B685 adversarial-review polish):** re-file no longer clobbers the source filename (fixed in the B685 PR); a non-PDF whose best-effort facts write missed is still classed correctly (the source filename is now AUTHORITATIVE on the review via `listReviews` `sfile`, not just the file-facts index); opening a non-PDF in Review **downloads** it instead of a dead-end note (and non-PDFs are barred from becoming the resume target, so no surprise download on load); the display-label extension strip only removes a real letter-first extension, so "Rev.3" keeps its name.
- **Tests:** `treeParentForUpload` explicit-folder cases (folderMirror.test.js), `stripFileExt` (docPersistence.test.js). Full suite green (2,836), build green, lint clean, logged-out headless smoke renders root/Library/Review with no JS errors.

- Scope note: `atlas14Wse100Ft` is NOT wired ‚Äî the 100-yr rasters have no county-wide mosaic (44 per-watershed services only, enumerated live); a 100-yr feed would need per-watershed multiplexing (mint separately if wanted).
- Verify: live (GIS-endpoint class, mandatory) ‚Üí **V284 ‚Äî ‚úÖ CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-21** (Cowork, live on Bain `smr9olizi5ue`, Fort Bend County, against the live county Image Server): the BUILDABILITY/FFE card reads the DRAFT 0.2% (500-yr) WSE from the county Atlas-14 rasters and renders the DRAFT-study screening caveat correctly on a real Fort Bend parcel. Two sub-claims (non-Fort-Bend makes no call; PDF export carries "(0.2% WSE ‚Äî DRAFT study)") left untested this round ‚Üí spun to **V399**. See VERIFICATION-DONE.md V284.
- Sandbox: `test/fbcdWse.test.js` + engine-seam tests green; the live raster fixtures probed from the sandbox ‚úì; headless ctx 4 of `ui-audit/verify-v276-fortbend-ffe.mjs` (25 checks ALL PASS, 0 page errors); full suite green; lint 0 errors; build green.

### B775 ‚Äî School district (ISD) boundaries layer + identify `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) #site-planner #gis  *(owner review 2026-07-11 as "NEW-5"; ¬ßB1. ‚Üí V281 (LIVE-CONFIRMED 2026-07-21). RENUMBERED B764‚Üí**B775** + V277‚Üí**V281** on merge-in of `origin/main`, which took B763‚ÄìB773 + V276‚ÄìV279 for a concurrent floodplain/detention session; the code/tests/commit keep the provisional **B764** label.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî added a statewide "School districts (ISD)" overlay to the Jurisdictions group riding the standard registry ‚Üí vector ‚Üí cache ‚Üí identify pipeline, plus ISD in the jurisdiction identify AND the B763 header badge. EVIDENCE-FIRST source, verified LIVE via the sandbox HTTPS proxy (curl): TEA's `Current_Districts_2023/FeatureServer/0` (owner GISAdmin_TEA_Texas) ‚Äî 1,018 districts statewide, CORS `*`, `NAME` (already carries the ISD/CISD suffix) + `DISTRICT_N` (TEA number), native NAD83 Texas Lambert so a query passes inSR/outSR 4326 + geometry SR. Coverage confirmed live: Baytown ‚Üí Goose Creek Consolidated ISD, downtown Houston ‚Üí Houston ISD, Katy ‚Üí Katy ISD, Sugar Land ‚Üí Fort Bend ISD.**
- Verify: live ‚Äî **GIS-endpoint + zoom-/data-density-dependent rendering** class ‚Üí **V281 ‚Äî ‚úÖ CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-21** (Cowork, live on Bain `smr9olizi5ue`): toggling "School districts (ISD)" ON paints a real violet TEA district boundary on the aerial (visible zoomed out) and the Site Analysis "School district" reads the correct **Katy ISD** for Bain's Fort-Bend/Katy-edge parcel. - Recipe: `GIS_SOURCES.isd` (sources.js) ‚Üí `VECTOR_SOURCES.jur_isd` (bbox tiers like city; `nameTemplate:"{name}"` since NAME already has the suffix) ‚Üí `JURISDICTIONS.jur_isd` (violet `#7c3aed` ‚Äî the hue freed when ETJ moved to blue) ‚Üí `LAYER_VINTAGE.jur_isd` ‚Üí `JURISDICTION_SOURCES.isd` + `role:"isd"` in the default identify. `formatJurisdictionBadge` appends `j.isd`; the Site Analysis + point-identify cards row out "School district". ### B685 ‚Äî Library upload was PDF-only; accept ANY file type `[Library / Doc Review]` (bug) #library #files #filing #doc-review  *(owner chat 2026-07-06 ‚Äî "it doesn't let me upload non pdfs, i should be able to upload any file type." Implemented + shipped; LIVE-CONFIRMED via V227 on 2026-07-21. Branch `claude/file-upload-types-aj45dn`. DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî no prior item; B664 was folder-aware drops, B685 is the type gate itself. Net-new.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + live-confirmed (V227).** The Library's upload (drop zone, "Choose files" picker, and folder drop) now takes **any** file type ‚Äî DWG, images, spreadsheets, Word docs, ZIPs ‚Äî not just PDFs.
- Verify: live ‚Üí **V227 ‚Äî ‚úÖ CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-21** (Cowork, live on Bain `smr9olizi5ue`, signed in + Drive connected): the core non-PDF round-trip works ‚Äî a `.png` uploads, files under "Other" with a PNG chip, does NOT stick in "Reading title block‚Ä¶", and downloads with the correct original filename/bytes. Two sub-claims (whole-folder mixed drop + junk-skip note; PDF title-block regression) left untested ‚Üí spun to **V398**. See VERIFICATION-DONE.md V227.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **Root cause:** `isAcceptedFile` (`shared/files/uploadQueue.js`) gated on `application/pdf` / `.pdf`; the `<input accept="application/pdf">` also hid non-PDFs in the OS picker; `partitionAccepted` skipped every non-PDF in folder drops.
- **Fix:** `isAcceptedFile` now accepts any non-empty file (only empty/unreadable is rejected, honestly). New pure helpers `isPdfName` + `isJunkFile`; `partitionAccepted` now skips only OS/app junk (dotfiles, Thumbs.db, `~$` lock files, zero-byte) on folder sweeps, files everything else of any type. `<input accept>` removed. Copy generalized ("Choose files", "any file type is welcome", etc.).
- **PDFs keep their powers:** title-block auto-filing + sheet stitching still run for PDFs only (`processItem` skips the title-block read for non-PDFs so pdf.js never chokes on a DWG). A non-PDF inside a project is FILED as "Other" (visible in the tree, re-file anytime) rather than dumped into Needs-filing; cross-project (no project) it still needs a home.
- **Storage correctness:** `guessContentType(name,type)` (`reviewStore.js`) derives a sensible MIME from the extension when the browser hands back an empty `file.type` (dragged CAD), so Drive/Supabase don't mislabel a DWG as a PDF; item label strips any extension, not just `.pdf`.
- **Open behavior:** the markup canvas only renders PDFs, so clicking a non-PDF in the Library **downloads** it (Drive-first, Supabase-fallback; loud on failure) instead of opening a blank canvas; a small type chip (DWG/XLSX/‚Ä¶) marks non-PDF rows. `DocReview.fetchSourceBytes` guards the resume-last-doc path so a non-PDF (or corrupt PDF) shows a clear note instead of stalling. Filename threaded onto the file-facts index (`sourceFile`) so the Library can tell PDF from not without a round-trip.
- **Tests:** rewrote `test/uploadQueue.test.js` acceptance/junk/partition cases + `isPdfName`/`isJunkFile`; added `guessContentType` cases to `test/docPersistence.test.js`. Full suite green (2831), build green, lint clean, logged-out headless smoke renders root/Library/Review with no JS errors.

### B942 ‚Äî Extend the zoom-out declutter (B149) to user-drawn Measurement labels AND Easement labels `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #markup  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 in chat as "NEW-1" ‚Äî draw Measure-tool distances (a long 3,500‚Ä≤ + short ~275‚Ä≤) and place labeled easements ("50‚Ä≤ Utility Esmt", "10‚Ä≤ Utility Esmt"), zoom out to full site-overview: their labels still painted at fixed screen size while the auto infrastructure dims already dropped under B149 ‚Äî illegible clutter. "should not see stuff like that this far out ‚Ä¶ just want the buildings." Minted **B942** against fresh main.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4835 unit tests ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ logged-out headless canvas e2e, all 15 checks green).** At full site-overview zoom, user-placed Measure-tool value labels (distance / path / area / perimeter / count) AND Easement labels ("50‚Ä≤ Utility Esmt") now drop out of the way ‚Äî like the auto dimensions already do ‚Äî so only the buildings and site-level content read. The measured lines / witness ticks and the easement hatched fill + centerline **stay** (just their labels hide, so nothing flickers on/off). Each label comes back the moment you zoom in past working zoom, and a **selected** measurement or easement always keeps its label so you can read what you're editing at any zoom.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Zoom-/data-density-dependent render, but it's a logged-out, no-auth/GIS/real-data canvas interaction on a blank site ‚Üí per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was driven headless here, not filed for a live pass. New permanent harness `ui-audit/verify-new1-measure-easement-lod.mjs` seeds a large site (fit ppf ‚âà 0.135, below the floor) with a 3,500‚Ä≤ + two 275‚Ä≤ measures and a 50‚Ä≤/10‚Ä≤ Utility easement, then asserts: at overview the "3,500‚Ä≤"/"275‚Ä≤"/"‚Ä¶Utility Esmt" labels are ABSENT while "Building 1" + the easement hatched-fill polygons + the measure hit-geometry PERSIST; clicking the long measure reveals its label at overview; clicking the 50‚Ä≤ easement reveals its label at overview; zooming past the floor reveals every label.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-1").
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`), one shared gate for both classes:**
  - *Measurement value labels* ‚Äî the line/polyline/area/perimeter `labelVisible` now gates on the PURE zoom floor `isSel || dimCalloutVisible(view.ppf)`, replacing B939's `detailLabelVisible(spanFt, view.ppf)` (min-on-screen-length). Rationale per the owner's open decision (default = zoom-floor, not min-length): a **long** run projects to hundreds of px even at overview, so a length-style rule keeps it painting there ‚Äî the floor drops short AND long labels together, and they reveal together. The count label was already on `dimCalloutVisible` (unchanged) ‚Äî now all four measure kinds share the one gate. Removed the now-unused `spanFt`.
  - *Easement centroid label* ‚Äî was effectively ungated (`view.ppf > 0.05`); now `isSel || dimCalloutVisible(view.ppf)`, the SAME floor. The B620/B678 opt-in *inline repeating* labels (`inlineLabelEls`, a different self-thinning mechanism) and the selected-only area readout are deliberately untouched.
  - The gate is scoped to those two `<text>` conditions only, so it can never catch the always-on OVERVIEW tier (building name/SF, site-summary chip, parcel acreage chips ‚Äî separate ungated render paths).
- **PDF-PARITY:** holds by construction ‚Äî `buildExportSvg` does `svgRef.cloneNode(true)` and strips only `data-export="skip"` nodes, so the label `<text>` is the SAME node in the export as on screen; the gate omits it identically in both. There is no separate export-side label render to drift.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open / ‚è≥ Verify / Done for measure-label / easement-label / declutter / zoom LOD. This **amends B939** (which decluttered the measure labels on `detailLabelVisible`) ‚Äî switching that gate to the pure floor per the owner's follow-up ‚Äî and **extends the declutter to easements** (never gated before), so it is net-new + a B939 amendment, NOT a B939/B149 recurrence. Reconciled against B121 (label-overlap engine), B149/B911 (`detailLabelVisible`/`dimCalloutVisible` LOD tier ‚Äî reused, not forked), B150/B151/B620 (easements + inline labels), B940/B941/B938 (measure move/props). No hit-test or zoom-gate path forked.
- **Named rules:** AUDIT-FIRST (traced B939's actual gate + the easement render before editing, and recorded that the archive claim matched the code). MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS / LOUD-FAILURE / TOMBSTONE-DELETES n/a. No `MAP.md` change (the new file is a ui-audit harness, not a tracked source module; no export change).
- **Verified:** `test/labelLayout.test.js` +1 case locking the gate choice (pure floor drops a long run at overview where the length rule wouldn't); full suite green (4835); lint 0 errors; build green; MAP/doc-pointer/backlog-index gates clean; headless harness 15/15.

### B940 ‚Äî Measurements should be click-and-draggable to reposition `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #markup #selection  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat ‚Äî "I should be able to click and drag measurements. but right now it's not letting me." Provisionally minted B936; late-renumbered to **B940** at push ‚Äî a concurrent session's own B936 (FINAL UI SPEC Part B) merged into main first, so per the reconcile-on-merge rule the newer (this) item took the next free number. The pushed commit + code comments still say B936.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4795 unit tests ¬∑ real logged-out canvas e2e).** A finished measurement can now be grabbed by its line/area body (or a count marker) and dragged to a new spot, exactly like a drawn element or markup. Previously a press on a measurement only ever SELECTED it ‚Äî there was no whole-measurement move, only per-vertex reshape.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Pure canvas interaction on a blank site (no auth/GIS/real-data) ‚Üí driven headless here per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK (new `e2e/measure-drag-props.spec.js` draws a Length measure, drags its midpoint, and asserts BOTH endpoints translated by the same vector ‚Äî a move, not a reshape).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** `selectMeasure` became `startMoveMeasure` ‚Äî it keeps the B910 select + smaller-area-first stack cycle, then arms a new `measureMove` drag (snapshot `orig` + `pushHistory`) that translates the measure via the existing `translateMeasure`; a plain click with no travel just selects (the history stack dedupes the no-op frame). New `onMove` branch `measureMove`; the generic `onUp` tail flushes it. The three body/marker hit paths now bind `startMoveMeasure` + `cursor: move`. Reshape grips still call `startMeasureVertex` (unchanged) and stop propagation, so a grip press reshapes while a body press moves.
- **Verified:** e2e above; `test/measureHit.test.js` unchanged-green; full suite green (4795); build green; MAP/doc-pointers clean.

### B941 ‚Äî Shift locks a measurement/line perpendicular (45¬∞) to the parcel boundary it starts on `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #ui #markup  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat ‚Äî "click a parcel boundary to start the measurement, hold shift and ‚Ä¶ keep my measurement perpendicular ‚Ä¶ just like bluebeam except that bluebeam holds it to 90 deg and 45 deg." Provisionally minted B937; late-renumbered to **B941** at push ‚Äî a concurrent session's own B937 (pond-inspector truncation) merged into main first, so per the reconcile-on-merge rule the newer (this) item took the next free number. The first pushed commit's message says B937; the code carries no B# label (it uses the `edgeConstrain` lib name).)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4795 unit tests ¬∑ real logged-out canvas e2e).** When a measurement (or a markup line/polyline) STARTS on a parcel boundary ‚Äî the first click snaps onto the property line ‚Äî holding **Shift** now locks the drawn direction to the nearest 45¬∞ **relative to that edge**: parallel (along the line), perpendicular (the setback case), or the 45¬∞ diagonals. Bluebeam's Shift snaps to page-absolute 90¬∞/45¬∞; this snaps in the edge's frame so "perpendicular to the property line" is one press. Off a boundary, Shift falls back to the page-absolute 45¬∞ lock (which the measure tool also gains, matching the line tools).
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Reachable on a blank site with a drawn parcel (no auth/GIS/real-data) ‚Üí driven headless here (e2e draws an axis-aligned parcel, starts a Length measure on its horizontal top edge, Shift-clicks down-and-to-the-side, and asserts the committed segment is a true vertical = perpendicular).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Implementation:** new pure lib `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/edgeConstrain.js` (`nearestBoundaryEdge` projects a click onto the nearest parcel edge within `EDGE_LOCK_PX`; `constrainToEdgeAngle` snaps a segment to the nearest step relative to the edge angle) with `test/edgeConstrain.test.js` (11 cases: perpendicular/parallel/tilted-edge/tolerance/degenerate). `SitePlanner.jsx` closes over `drawStartPt` (edge-or-grid start snap, Alt-bypassable), `anchorEdgeAngle` (recovers the edge from the draft's first point ‚Äî which was snapped onto it), and `shiftLockPoint` (edge-relative when anchored, else `snap45`). Wired into the measure commit + preview and the mline/mpolyline commit + preview; the anchor edge is stateless (recovered from the first point), so nothing to clear on finish/cancel.
- **Verified:** e2e perpendicular case + 11 unit tests; full suite green (4795); build green; MAP updated.

### B938 ‚Äî Measurements need a Properties menu, like every other element `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #ui #markup #selection  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat ‚Äî "MEASUREMENTS SHOULD HAVE A PROPERTIES MENU JUST LIKE EVERY OTHER ELEMENT." Minted **B938**.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4795 unit tests ¬∑ real logged-out canvas e2e).** A measurement now has the same Properties affordances as any element: **right-click** it for a menu (Properties‚Ä¶ ¬∑ Lock/Unlock ¬∑ Delete), and **double-click** (or the Properties tab) opens the docked inspector ‚Äî showing its type + measured value(s), an editable **Label** that rides the on-canvas value, a **Lock** toggle (blocks accidental move/reshape), and Delete.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Pure UI on a blank site ‚Üí driven headless here (e2e double-clicks a measure, asserts the "Measurement" Properties panel opens, types a Label, and asserts it persists onto the measure model).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Implementation (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** `companionSel` + `propsMatches` + the props close/takeover effects extended for the `measure` kind (matched by index `sel.i`, since measures have no `sel.id`). New `selMeasure` selector + `toggleMeasureLock`; a measure inspector `<Section>` (value readout ¬∑ Label input ¬∑ Lock ¬∑ Delete) beside the element/markup/callout inspectors. New `onMeasureContext` + a `measure` branch in the shared `mapMenu`. Double-click reconstructed via `isDoubleTap` in `startMoveMeasure` (pointer capture eats the native dblclick). `m.label` prefixes the canvas value label; `m.locked` gates `startMoveMeasure`/`startMeasureVertex`/`editablePath` + hides the reshape grips (additive model fields, preserved through explode/rebuild sync + `objArr`/`ensureZ`).
- **Verified:** e2e properties+label case; full suite green (4795); build green.

### B939 ‚Äî Measurement value labels don't declutter on zoom-out (stay large at overview scale) `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #markup  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat with a zoomed-out screenshot (big orange "275'/274'/3,500'" labels dominating the aerial) ‚Äî "when you zoom out ‚Ä¶ these measurement labels are still showing ‚Ä¶ We fixed this on plenty of other elements, implement the same fix here." Minted **B939**.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4795 unit tests ¬∑ real logged-out canvas e2e).** A measurement's number now rides the SAME level-of-detail gate as building/parcel dimensions (the B911 family): it hides at overview zoom (or when the feature is too small to project past ~30 px) instead of stamping a big fixed number over a pinhead. A SELECTED measurement always shows its value so you can still read what you're editing.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Zoom-dependent render, but reproduced + confirmed headless here per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK (e2e draws a Length measure, asserts its ‚Ä≤ label is present at working zoom, wheels out, and asserts the label count drops to 0 ‚Äî the exact declutter the owner asked for).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** the line/polyline/area label gates on `detailLabelVisible(spanFt, view.ppf)` (span = length, or ‚àöarea for an area) and the count label on `dimCalloutVisible(view.ppf)` ‚Äî both already the shared `lib/labelLayout.js` gates every other dimension uses ‚Äî OR the measure is selected. No new thresholds.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** applies the existing B911 `detailLabelVisible`/`dimCalloutVisible` LOD tier (parcel-edge + element dims) to the measure layer, which had been left on a fixed-size always-on label.
- **Verified:** e2e label-hide-on-zoom-out case; full suite green (4795); build green.

### B935 ‚Äî Double-click an easement/road/line opens PROPERTIES (never an inline text editor) + an "Inside the road/easement" label-placement option `[Site Planner]` (bug+feature) #site-planner #markup #road #selection #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 in chat ‚Äî "in no circumstance should double-clicking on an easement or a road go into a text editor to add the inline label ‚Äî that should be reserved for the properties ‚Ä¶ I should have an option, for a road or an easement, to place an inline label within the balance of the road or easement so it's not sitting on top of said element." Developed under provisional B932; late-bound to B934 just before push, then a concurrent session's own B934 (FINAL UI SPEC Part A, still Open in BACKLOG.md) merged into main first ‚Äî so per the reconcile-on-merge rule the newer (this) item renumbered to the real **B935** via `npm run next-id`. Heading + code/tests all carry B935; the pushed commit + PR title still say B934.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4782 unit tests ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ MAP/backlog-index clean ¬∑ two headless e2e proofs, logged-out).** Two owner asks, both done:
  1. **Double-click ALWAYS opens Properties** for an easement, a centerline road, or a line/polyline markup ‚Äî it never opens the old on-canvas inline-label text editor. Previously a *second* double-click on an already-selected text-bearing feature popped a floating in-place editor; the owner wants label text edited **only** in the Properties panel's "Inline label" field. The on-canvas editor was **removed entirely** (dead once the four double-click triggers were rerouted).
  2. **New "Label position" option (road & easement only):** a checkbox under the Inline-label controls ‚Äî **On the line** (default, unchanged) vs **Inside the road/easement (off the line)**. When "Inside", the repeating label is pushed perpendicular into the body of the feature so it sits in clear space instead of riding on top of the drawn centerline / boundary line: a road or strip easement tucks a quarter of its width off the centerline; a boundary easement aims its labels toward the ring's centroid (a fraction of the ring's smaller extent), so they land inside the boundary. Plain lines/polylines have no interior, so the control only shows for road + easement.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Both behaviours are logged-out, no-auth/GIS/real-data interactions on a blank site ‚Üí per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK they were driven headless here, not filed for a live pass.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** the four double-click deciders (`startMoveMarkup` + `startMoveEl` reconstructed double-taps, and the `onMarkupDouble` + `onElDouble` native-dblclick fallbacks) now unconditionally `setPropsFor(...)` ‚Äî the `dblWasSelRef && textBearing ‚Üí beginEditInline` branch is gone. Removed the now-dead machinery: `editInline` state, `beginEditInline`/`commitEditInline`/`cancelEditInline`, the `<foreignObject>` editor render, the Escape-handler `cancelEditInline()`, the `editInline?.id !== ‚Ä¶` render gates, the `editInlineId` param of the module-level `renderElPx`, and the orphaned `inlineLabelAnchorFeet` helper. **Callouts are untouched** ‚Äî a callout IS a text box, so its already-selected double-click-to-edit-text stays.
- **"Inside" geometry (pure):** `inlineLabelPlaces` gained an optional `interiorScreen` arg ‚Äî when present it aims the perpendicular offset toward that point (a closed ring's centroid) instead of the screen-"up" side. `inlineLabelEls` gained `opts.insetFt` (feet, scales with zoom) + `opts.interiorFeet`; on "Inside" the offset is the feet inset ALONE (a midway-into-the-body distance that already clears the line ‚Äî stacking the ~1-font on-line clearance on top could shove a narrow feature's label past its own edge). New module helpers `ringLabelInsetFt` (0.2 √ó the ring's smaller bbox extent) and `easementInsetOpts(m)` (strip ‚Üí width/4; boundary ‚Üí ring inset + centroid). New per-feature field **`labelInside`** (falsy default = unchanged classic ride; additive, no model migration ‚Äî persists like the existing `labelSpacing`/`labelSize`/`labelHalo`).
- **PDF-PARITY:** holds by construction ‚Äî `buildExportSvg` does `svgRef.current.cloneNode(true)`, so the inline labels ride into the PNG/PDF as the same `<text>` nodes; this change only moves their x/y, touching no separate export render path.
- **Named rules:** AUDIT-FIRST (traced the four real double-click paths + the shared render helpers before editing). MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS / LOUD-FAILURE / TOMBSTONE-DELETES n/a. No `MAP.md` change (no file add/remove/rename; no primary-export change ‚Äî `renderElPx` is module-internal, not exported).
- **Verified (sandbox):** `npm test` 4782 pass (updated the B620 + B750 source-scrape guards in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` to the new reality; renamed/retargeted the two `e2e/click-behavior.spec.js` road+line specs). Headless #1 (`e2e/click-behavior.spec.js`, on the local preview): a fresh AND an already-selected double-click on both a centerline road and a Line markup open Properties and never the inline overlay. Headless #2 (Part 2): a road's "Inside" label sits **31.6 px** off the centerline (vs 11.6 px "On line") yet stays within the 40.5 px half-band; a boundary easement's labels sit **inside** the ring, off the boundary line. Also refreshed the manual `ui-audit/verify-b620-inline-label.mjs` (edits the label via the Properties field now, asserts NO on-canvas editor).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open/‚è≥ Verify/Done for inline-label / double-click / beginEditInline / label-placement. This **supersedes the B750/NEW-1 gate** (which made an already-selected text feature's double-click edit its label in place) and **removes the B620 on-canvas editor** ‚Äî both are amended here, not duplicated. Net-new: the "Inside the road/easement" placement option has no prior item.

### B933 ‚Äî Click / panel-toggle flashes the aerial: it's B837's ghost-on-resize, not a tile-wipe ‚Äî fixed at the source with panBy `[Site Planner / Map]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #perf  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 ‚Äî "clicking between elements and parcels and menus makes the screen flash a ton" ‚Äî a live recurrence of the B821/B837 click-flash after both shipped. Minted **B933 / V392** ‚Äî provisional B929 collided with a concurrent parcel-defaults B929, then B931 collided with a concurrent callout-overflow B931 (both merged their number first); late-renumbered to B933 via `npm run next-id` against fresh main. DEDUPE-FIRST: cross-linked to B821/B837 ‚Äî distinct ROOT CAUSE (their ghost, not a wipe), supersedes their toggle-path approach; a new cross-linked number, per the B821-vs-B65 precedent.)*
`[x]` **ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED at the source + headless-proven + owner-confirmed live.** Clicking a drawn element/parcel or opening a left-rail panel used to make the aerial photo flash/blink for a moment. Fixed and now confirmed clean on the live site.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED. Owner confirmed 2026-07-21 on planyr.io: clicking between elements/parcels and opening/switching/closing the left-rail panels no longer flashes or blinks the aerial.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("‚Ä¶screen flash a ton").
- **AUDIT-FIRST correction of B821's diagnosis:** B821 blamed a "docked-panel resize ‚Üí un-ghosted `setView` tile-wipe." Instrumented in a headless build, a panel toggle actually fires **zero** `viewprereset` ‚Äî Leaflet converts a same-zoom, in-viewport `setView({animate:false})` into an internal `panBy`, so it never wipes on a toggle. The real flash is the **ghost B837 added**: B837 made the `sizeChanged` branch ALWAYS `spawnGhost()` (a full `wrap.cloneNode(true)` of the tile layer) before re-syncing; that frozen clone sits on top at the PRE-shift position and drops up to 5 s later, so every click shows the aerial off-position then snap back.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx` basemap view-sync effect):** `viewprereset` (the wipe) only needs to fire on a ZOOM change. A panel/rail toggle and a pure-pan settle are same-zoom re-centers, so `commit()` now moves with `map.panBy` (translates the pane, lazy-loads only the newly exposed edge tiles, never wipes) and spawns NO ghost. The ghost is reserved for a genuine zoom change (setView really wipes ‚Üí B65's snapshot masks it). `sizeChanged` now calls `invalidateSize({pan:false})` (resize with no pan/wipe) then re-centers through the no-wipe commit. Supersedes B821/B837's wipe-then-mask for the toggle path; B65's zoom-gesture ghost is left intact.
- **Named rules:** AUDIT-FIRST (instrumented the real event flow, corrected the prior claim in code); LIVE-VERIFY (aerial paint with real tiles ‚Üí V392, now PASSED); VIEWPORT-STABLE (the drawing must not jump ‚Äî B837's geometric invariant re-run green). No `MAP.md` change (inline effect edit; the one new file is an e2e spec, not a `src/` module).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless, logged out ‚Äî ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK):** new `e2e/panel-toggle-flash.spec.js` seeds a located site, exposes the backdrop map behind a `window.__PLANYR_E2E` hook (never runs in prod), and counts ghost clones appended to the map clip across open/switch/switch/close ‚Üí **0** (reintroducing the ghost makes it fail at 2, proving the guard bites). `e2e/panel-toggle-viewport.spec.js` (B837 no-jump) + `click-behavior` + `measure-select` + `context-menu-viewport` all green; full unit suite + lint 0 errors + build green (SitePlannerApp/DocReview still separate lazy chunks).
- **‚úÖ V392 CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-21 (owner, live on planyr.io):** with the aerial ON over a real located site, clicking features/parcels and opening/switching/closing the left-rail panels no longer flashes, blinks, or shows the aerial off-position-then-snap. Subsumes B821's V310 + B837's V328 toggle-flash halves.

### B931 ‚Äî Site Planner callout / text-box text still overflows its box for wide all-caps text `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #markup  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat with a fresh Site Planner screenshot ("Sylvestri / Concept A") ‚Äî "I'm still dealing with the issue of text appearing outside of its box," the callout "COULD ADD VLOUME TO ADJACENT MASON BASIN" spilling past its rect. Provisionally minted B929; late-renumbered to **B931** at push (concurrent sessions merged their own B929 (parcel defaults) + B930 (info-popover flash) into main first). **This is the recurrence B918's scope-correction missed** ‚Äî see B918's ‚öÝ correction note.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4776 unit tests ¬∑ real logged-out canvas e2e).** A callout / text box whose text is wide ‚Äî long all-caps lines like the owner's "COULD ADD VOLUME TO ADJACENT MASON BASIN" ‚Äî drew its text straight past the right/left edge of its box. The box hugged the text too tightly, so the annotation looked broken.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Callout rendering is data-independent and fully reachable on a blank site with no auth/GIS/real-data ‚Üí per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was reproduced + driven headless here (new e2e places the exact all-caps string on the real logged-out Site Planner canvas and asserts the committed `<text>` is strictly narrower than its `<rect>`). Verified at three zoom levels. The fix touches the box-sizing math shared by the committed SVG render AND the inline editor (they read the SAME `calloutLayout`), so screen ‚Üî editor cannot drift; the PDF/print export clones the same committed `<text>`/`<rect>` JSX, so PDF-PARITY holds by construction (no separate export sizing path).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST):** B913's `calloutLayout` (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/calloutLayout.js`) sized the AUTO-width box from a flat `line.length * charW` estimate with `charW = fontPx * 0.56` ‚Äî a monospace guess. The committed `<text>` renders in proportional **Inter**, where wide glyphs (all-caps M/W/A) run well past 0.56¬∑fontPx, so the estimate under-sized the box and the real glyphs spilled out. This is the SAME class of bug B909 fixed for the Doc Review shared engine ‚Äî but the Site Planner callout path never adopted the real-measurement module. (B918's "already fixed by B913" claim was therefore wrong; corrected there.)
- **Fix:** `calloutLayout` now measures REAL glyph widths via the shared `src/shared/markup/textWrap.js` `bestMeasurer` (a real `<canvas>` `measureText` in the browser, matching the app's Inter face to sub-pixel precision; the per-char heuristic in Node so the module stays pure/Node-testable) ‚Äî bold/italic fed through so heavier glyphs size wider. AUTO width hugs the widest REAL line; EXPLICIT (dragged) width now wraps via `wrapText` (width-based, mirroring the editor's soft-wrap) instead of the old char-count `wrapLines`. The flat `charW` remains only as the coarse floor inside `minCalloutWidthFt`.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** recurrence of the Site Planner half of the callout-overflow class (B918 punted it as "fixed by B913"; B913 introduced the flat estimate). Not the Doc Review half (B918, separate `MarkupRenderer`/`textWrap` path ‚Äî unaffected).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** unit ‚Äî `test/calloutLayout.test.js` updated to real measurement + a new invariant test (the AUTO box encloses every line at ppf 0.2/0.35/0.9) + a proof the box is now wider than the old flat estimate; full suite green (4776). e2e ‚Äî new `e2e/dim-callout-edit.spec.js` "B929" places the exact all-caps string on the logged-out canvas and asserts the committed `<text>` fits inside the box `<rect>`. Build green; MAP/doc-pointers clean.

### B932 ‚Äî Alt+Z autosizes (shrinks-to-fit) the selected text box / callout, Bluebeam-style `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #ui #markup  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat ‚Äî "let's make sure that alt z works to shrink a text box just like it works in blue beam." Provisionally minted B930; late-renumbered to **B932** at push (main's own B930 = info-popover flash merged first). Bluebeam Revu's Alt+Z = "Autosize Text Box" ‚Äî fits the box to its text; confirmed against Bluebeam docs.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED + verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4776 unit tests ¬∑ real logged-out canvas e2e).** Pressing **Alt+Z** with a text box or callout selected now shrinks the box to fit its text ‚Äî the keyboard peer of the "‚Üî Fit to text" button ‚Äî matching Bluebeam's "Autosize Text Box" shortcut. Works whether the box is just selected or still being typed in.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Pure interaction on a blank site, no auth/GIS/real-data ‚Üí driven headless here per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK (new e2e drags a side handle to set a fixed width, then presses a real Alt+Z and asserts the fixed width clears back to auto). No live gate.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Behaviour:** Alt+Z clears the callout's explicit `boxW`, returning it to AUTO width (which, post-B931, hugs the real text). Equivalent to the "Fit to text" button ‚Äî a fixed-width box that the user dragged wide collapses back onto its content; an already-auto box is a no-op (the key is still consumed so nothing else fires, and no empty undo frame is pushed).
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** (a) the main keydown handler ‚Äî `e.altKey && e.code === "KeyZ"` (physical Z, since Alt+Z emits a dead/other char on some layouts) on a selected callout ‚Üí `pushHistory()` + clear `boxW`, reading live `selRef`/`stateRef` so a stale keydown closure can't miss a just-dragged width; (b) the inline editor's `<textarea>` `onKeyDown` ‚Äî same chord clears `boxW` mid-type so the box re-flows to fit on the spot (Bluebeam parity). Discoverability: the "‚Üî Fit to text" button now shows an `Alt Z` kbd hint, the shortcuts overlay lists it, and the Callout/Text tool hints mention it.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** no prior Alt+Z / autosize-shortcut item; builds on B913's `boxW` model and its existing "Fit to text" clear (the same operation, now keyboard-bound).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** e2e ‚Äî new `e2e/dim-callout-edit.spec.js` "B930" on the logged-out canvas; full unit suite green (4776); build green.
### B881 ‚Äî Bottom map furniture overlaps when a docked panel narrows the map pane (scale bar ‚Üî "‚óè Scaled" badge ‚Üî coordinate chip) `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(Claude-spotted 2026-07-18 while diagnosing the setback bug ‚Äî owner's Mueschke Rd screenshot, Parcels panel docked; handoff `handoffheaderandfurnitureglitches.md` NEW-1. Minted **B881** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Code/tests carry the provisional label; a late renumber touches only this heading + V362.)*
- **‚úÖ V362 CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-21** (Cowork verification round, live on planyr.io ‚Äî Bain / Concept A, Parcels panel docked to narrow the map pane, two zooms ~1,000 ft & ~500 ft): the graphic scale bar, "‚óè Scaled" calibration badge, north arrow and coordinate chip all stayed clear/legible with ZERO overlap on the scale-bar numbers. Live zoom-/data-density gate met ‚Üí moved to BACKLOG-DONE.
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî zoom-/data-density-dependent rendering; the sandbox proves geometry headless, but the live docked-panel eyeball on planyr.io is the class's gate) ‚Üí V362.** On the in-planner Site map, with the Parcels panel docked (map pane collapsed to ~250‚Äì300 px), the graphic scale bar, the "‚óè Scaled" calibration badge, and the lat/long+elevation coordinate chip piled onto each other ‚Äî "Scaled" overlapped the `500 / 1,000` scale numbers into an unreadable mush.
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from the header/furniture handoff (Claude-spotted, NEW-1).
- **Root cause:** the bottom furniture was corner-pinned by fixed offsets in `SitePlanner.jsx` ‚Äî coordinate chip `left:10 bottom:8`, calibration badge `left:56 bottom:40`, scale bar `right:14 bottom:40`, north arrow `left:14 bottom:40`. B609 verified the chip clears the `bottom:40` band, but nothing accounted for the **badge (`left:56`) and the scale bar (`right:14`) sharing that row**. The badge is the one text-width item (the scale bar auto-caps to the viewport, the north arrow is tiny), so as the pane narrows its right edge runs into the scale bar's left edge.
- **Fix (width-aware, pure + measured):** new pure `calibBadgePlacement` in `sheetFurniture.js` decides ‚Äî from the true pane width, the measured badge width, and the scale-bar plate dims ‚Äî whether the badge would collide; when it would, the badge **lifts to its own row just above the scale bar** (clearing the bar below AND the bottom-right zoom controls above, which start at `bottom:100`) and its width is **capped so it truncates with an ellipsis** instead of overflowing. Wide panes keep the original bottom-row layout unchanged. The badge's natural width is measured in a `useLayoutEffect` off the label's `scrollWidth` (cap-independent ‚Üí no raise‚Üîmeasure oscillation; pre-paint so no flash ‚Äî VIEWPORT-STABLE). The coordinate chip also gets a `maxWidth` + ellipsis so it can't run off a narrow pane. The true (unclamped) pane width is now tracked as `size.rawW` (the existing `size.w` is clamped to 320 for the coordinate math).
- **Verified (sandbox):** new unit tests in `test/sheetFurniture.test.js` (+5: `calibBadgePlacement` ‚Äî wide keeps `bottom:40`, never raises before measure, lifts+clears the zoom row when narrow, and **ZERO badge/scale-bar/zoom overlap across paneW 240‚Üí1200 √ó badge 120‚Äì260 √ó bar 120‚Äì240**). Headless harness `ui-audit/bottom-furniture-harness.*` + `bottom-furniture-verify.mjs` drives the REAL furniture plates at 6 pane widths (260‚Üí800) and asserts no pairwise bounding-box overlap among scale bar ¬∑ badge ¬∑ north arrow ¬∑ coordinate chip ¬∑ zoom controls ‚Äî **91/91 checks, 0 page errors**; screenshots confirm the badge lifts+ellipsizes at ‚â§320 px and rides the normal row at ‚â•360 px. Full suite (4260) + lint + build + MAP/backlog-index green.
- **‚è≥ V362 (live):** see VERIFICATION.md ‚Äî on planyr.io, open a site, dock the Parcels panel to narrow the map pane, zoom so the scale bar reads ~1,000 ft, and confirm the scale bar, "‚óè Scaled" badge, north arrow and coordinate chip all stay clear/legible (repeat at two zooms).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new ‚Äî no prior item makes the bottom furniture width-aware (B609 relocated the coordinate chip but only checked the `bottom:8` vs `bottom:40` bands, not the same-row badge‚Üîbar case). Relatives (not dupes): B609 (chip relocation), the `sheetFurniture.js` export-quality NEW-1 (a different, export-frame concern).

### B930 ‚Äî Info "‚ìò" popover flashes rapidly while hovering (Yield "Sources ‚ìò" + Layers-panel per-row ‚ìò) `[App-wide / UI]` (bug) #ui #site-planner  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat with a screenshot of the Yield ‚Üí "Sources ‚ìò" legend ‚Äî "when you hover it flashes a ton", same for the Layers-tab per-row "i". Minted **B930** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Branch `claude/info-icon-hover-flash-2ku7ju`.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4774 unit tests ¬∑ headless proven on the REAL component, both pre-/post-fix, logged-out).** Resting the pointer on either hover-opened info popover ‚Äî the Yield panel's "Sources ‚ìò" legend (`SourcesLegend`) and every Layers-panel row's ‚ìò (`RowInfo`, also the `SourceTag` "Basis" ‚ìò) ‚Äî made the popover strobe open/closed continuously instead of opening once and staying put.
- **Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST):** every one of these popovers renders through the shared `AnchoredMenu`, whose click-away layer is a **full-viewport transparent backdrop** (`position:fixed; inset:0; z-index:4000`, pointer-events default = **auto**) that sits *above* the little ‚ìò trigger. On hover-open the backdrop instantly covers the trigger, so the browser fires `mouseleave` on the button ‚Üí the 160 ms close timer arms ‚Üí the popover closes ‚Üí the backdrop unmounts ‚Üí the trigger is exposed under the still-stationary pointer ‚Üí `mouseenter` fires ‚Üí it reopens ‚Üí the backdrop covers it again ‚Üí loop. Every micro-movement of a real hand re-armed the cycle, so it flickered many times a second. (Click-opened `AnchoredMenu` consumers never hit this ‚Äî they have no `mouseleave`-driven auto-close on the trigger.)
- **Fix (`src/shared/ui/AnchoredMenu.jsx` + the two hover consumers):** added an opt-in **`hoverSafe`** mode to `AnchoredMenu`. When set, it renders **no interactive backdrop** (so nothing can steal the pointer off the trigger) and instead dismisses on any document `mousedown` (capture) whose target is outside **both** the anchor (its own click still toggles) and the panel (its content isn't click-away). `RowInfo.jsx` and `SourcesLegend.jsx` now pass `hoverSafe`. Escape-to-close and the pure placement math are untouched; **all click-opened consumers keep the default backdrop unchanged** (AccountControl, ProjectBreadcrumb, AppHeader, ContextMenu, ScheduleToolbar, FloatingPanel, DocReview, SitePlanner), so this is additive and scoped to the two hover popovers.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. A logged-out hover interaction with no auth/GIS/real-data wall ‚Üí per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was reproduced + driven headless here. Bundled the **real** `RowInfo` (esbuild) and rested the pointer on the ‚ìò with tiny real-hand jitter for ~1.5 s: the **pre-fix** build (hoverSafe off) opened the popover **11√ó** / closed it **10√ó** in that single hover (the strobe); the **fix** opened it **1√ó** / closed **0√ó** and it stayed visible. Click-away outside still closes it; the trigger's own click still toggles it. Not filed for a live pass ‚Äî the component behaviour is identical wherever the ‚ìò renders.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Named rules:** AUDIT-FIRST (traced the real `mouseleave`‚Üîbackdrop cycle in the component, not the report's wording). VIEWPORT-STABLE / LOUD-FAILURE / MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS / PDF-PARITY n/a (no render-surface shift, no fail path, components already module-scoped, no export path). No `MAP.md` change (no file add/remove/rename; `AnchoredMenu` still default-exports the same component ‚Äî a new optional prop is not a primary-export change).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new. Searched Open/‚è≥ Verify/Done for flash / flicker / hover / RowInfo / SourcesLegend / info-icon ‚Äî every existing "flash" item (B65, B821, B837, B838, B842) is the **map/basemap tile-wipe** class (Leaflet `setView` re-raster on a docked-panel resize), an entirely different subsystem and mechanism. No prior item covers the info-popover hover strobe.

### B929 ‚Äî Standards ‚Üí Parcels: expose ALL parcel properties as defaults for new parcels, not just setback `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 in chat with a screenshot of the Standards ‚Üí Parcels card ‚Äî "fix this so all the appropriate parcel properties are shown here and can be set to a default". Developed under provisional **B924**; late-bound to the real **B929** just before push ‚Äî concurrent sessions had merged B924‚ÄìB928 into main since this branched, so `npm run next-id -- --against-main` gave B929 (heading + code/test/audit labels renumbered to match).)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ 8-test unit lib ¬∑ 11-check headless e2e, logged-out, draws a real parcel and reads back the stamped style).** The Standards "Defaults for new elements" ‚Üí **Parcels** section exposed only **Default setback**, even though a parcel carries several more of its own properties (the ones already editable in the parcel inspector). Now the section also sets **Outline color**, **Line weight**, **Line style** (solid/dashed/dotted), and an optional **Fill** (on/off ‚Üí Translucence + Fill color) as the starting values for every NEW parcel. Any parcel can still override any of these on itself in its Boundary inspector, exactly as before; per-edge setbacks remain a per-parcel property (the existing cross-link caption stays).
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Pure defaults-plumbing + panel controls, fully exercisable logged-out on a blank site (no auth/GIS/real-data), so per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was driven headless here: set weight=5 + dash=dashed + fill=on in the panel, drew a parcel on the canvas, and read the persisted site back to confirm the new parcel was born `{weight:5, dash:"dashed", fill:"#5b6650"}`. **PDF-PARITY does not apply** ‚Äî the render/export path already painted `pc.stroke/weight/dash/fill/fillOpacity` (per-parcel styling already existed and already exported); B929 only adds a way to *stamp* those fields at creation, changing no render or export code.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Model:** the defaults are **stamped at creation** (not resolved at render) ‚Äî matching the panel's literal "Defaults for new elements" wording, so changing a default only affects parcels drawn *afterward*. This deliberately differs from the element `typeStyles` render-fallback because a parcel's fill is optional and stores `null` for "explicitly off"; a render-time `??` fallback would collide "off" with "unset". A duplicated / merged / split / imported-that-preserves parcel copies its source style and never stamps.
- **New pure export `parcelDefaultStyle(settings)` in `lib/planStyle.js`** (Node-tested, `test/parcelDefaultStyle.test.js`, 8 cases): returns only the style keys the user actually customized in `settings.parcelStyle`, so an untouched default leaves the parcel to the theme-aware built-in render fallbacks (no stroke ‚Üí the theme's parcel color; no weight ‚Üí 2; no dash ‚Üí solid; no fill ‚Üí unfilled). Fill is opt-in ‚Äî `fillOpacity` rides along only with a fill color; `dash:"solid"` and `fill:null` are treated as "not customized".
- **Wiring (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** new `settings.parcelStyle` (default `{}`) + `setParcelStd` (settings-only, RC-6 not-undoable). The Standards ‚Üí Parcels controls mirror the parcel inspector's Outline/Weight/Style/Fill controls but write to `parcelStyle` (color swatches use `livePick(‚Ä¶, false)` ‚Äî no dead undo frame). Every fresh-parcel creation site spreads `parcelDefaultStyle(settings)`: `closePoly` (draw), `addRectParcel`, `importFeature` (county lookup), `parcelsFromRings` (identify-a-lot). Merge/split/duplicate untouched (source-style-preserving).
- **Guard update:** `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` B567 color-picker count 14 ‚Üí 16 (the two new settings-only Standards-Parcels swatches), plus a new assertion that they pass `hist=false`.

### B923 ‚Äî Clicking inside a text box / callout editor doesn't move the caret to the click point `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #markup  *(owner-reported 2026-07-21 in chat ‚Äî "when I'm editing text box, it doesn't seem like clicking with my mouse works to put my cursor where I want it to go." Minted **B923** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ 4774 unit tests ¬∑ headless e2e proven BOTH directions, logged-out).** In the Site Planner, opening a callout/text box (or the inline-label / numeric inline editor) and then clicking with the mouse to reposition the typing cursor did nothing ‚Äî the caret stayed wherever it was (autoFocus leaves it at the end of the text), so you couldn't click into the middle of what you'd typed to edit it. Root cause: the SVG canvas carries `onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}` (it suppresses page text-selection during a canvas drag). The inline editors are `<foreignObject>` children INSIDE that SVG and stop only the POINTER event (`onPointerDown` ‚Üí `stopPropagation`); **mousedown is a separate event** that still bubbled up to the SVG's handler, whose `preventDefault()` cancels the mousedown's default action ‚Äî placing the text caret at the click point. Focus landed on the field but the caret never moved.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Caret placement is standard, data-independent browser behaviour, fully exercisable on a blank site with no auth/GIS/real-data ‚Üí per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was reproduced + driven headless here (fails on the pre-fix handler, passes on the fix); not filed for a live pass. The fix touches only interactive editing, not the committed render or its PDF/print path, so PDF-PARITY does not apply.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`, the `planner-canvas` `<svg>` `onMouseDown`):** guard the handler so it does NOT `preventDefault()` when the mousedown target is an inline text editor ‚Äî `if (t.tagName === "TEXTAREA" || t.tagName === "INPUT" || t.isContentEditable) return;`. One central fix covers all three foreignObject editors (callout/text box `<textarea>`, inline-label `<input>`, numeric `<input>`) plus any future one, and canvas drags on everything else still suppress text-selection exactly as before.
- **Dedup note (DEDUPE-FIRST):** no prior item ‚Äî searched Open/‚è≥ Verify/Done for caret / cursor / callout-click / foreignObject / mousedown. Adjacent to the B913 (resizable text box) / B912 (editable dimension) callout-editing lineage but a distinct defect (caret placement inside the editor, not sizing/opening it). The equivalent Doc Review text editors are plain absolutely-positioned HTML with no mousedown-cancelling ancestor, so they were already correct ‚Äî no change there.
- **Verification harness:** new `e2e/dim-callout-edit.spec.js` "B923" ‚Äî place a Text box, type a long string (caret at end), click near the box's left edge, assert `selectionStart` moved OFF the end (`< text.length`). Confirmed FAILING on the pre-fix naive handler (caret stuck at the end) and PASSING on the fix.

### B924 ‚Äî Shared ContextMenu component; migrate every bespoke right-click menu `[App-wide / UI]` (feature) #ui #infra  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-2"; minted **B924** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Sibling of the B923-batch umbrella, which renumbered to **B928** after a concurrent session merged its own B923 (Site Planner caret fix, PR #736); B924‚ÄìB927 don't collide and keep their numbers. DEDUPE-FIRST: this is the completion/verification of **B915** (the shared viewport-aware ContextMenu primitive, shipped the prior session) ‚Äî NOT a new primitive.)*
`[x]` **VERIFIED-COMPLETE + a headless in-viewport spec added this session (green build ¬∑ 4748 unit tests ¬∑ new e2e pass logged-out).** NEW-2 asked for one shared ContextMenu in `src/shared/ui/` ‚Äî body portal, positioned at the click point, clamped to the viewport by MEASURING the rendered menu (never a hardcoded footprint like FolderContextMenu's old `H = 232` guess) ‚Äî and every bespoke cursor-menu migrated onto it. **B915 already built exactly this** (`src/shared/ui/ContextMenu.jsx` + pure `contextMenuPlacement.js`, `useLayoutEffect` + `getBoundingClientRect` flip/clamp, opaque surface, `role="menu"`, click-away + right-click-away + Escape, ResizeObserver re-place, `maxHeight`+scroll for over-tall menus). This session AUDITED the migration and confirmed **all** cursor menus now route through it ‚Äî SitePlanner (`vtxMenu` / `parcelMenu` / `mapMenu` / `typeMenu` / `ovMenu`), MapFinder (`mapMenu` / `statusMenu`), Doc Review (`ctxMenu`), Library `FolderTree` (the ex-`FolderContextMenu`, its hardcoded height guess gone), and the `ProjectBreadcrumb` ‚Äî zero remaining per-instance fixed-position math.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED. Pure placement math already unit-tested (`test/contextMenuPlacement.test.js`, 7 cases incl. bottom-right corner + over-tall clamp). NEW-2's explicit "headless spec asserting a tall menu near the bottom-right corner stays fully in-viewport" is now satisfied by **`e2e/context-menu-viewport.spec.js`** (added this session): opens the canvas right-click menu hard against the bottom-right corner on a short 1000√ó540 viewport and asserts every edge of the REAL rendered menu box sits inside the viewport. ### B925 ‚Äî Kill opacity-as-hierarchy in rail and menu chrome `[App-wide / UI]` (task) #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-3"; minted **B925** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + verified this session (contrast audit green both themes ¬∑ screenshots ¬∑ green build ¬∑ 4748 unit tests).** The house rule is louder = more important, hierarchy via weight/size/letter-spacing/color tokens ‚Äî never fading text toward the background. Confirmed + fixed both named violations plus a rail/menu sweep.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED (`node ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs` green in both themes for every replacement pair; the dark Site Planner screenshot in `ui-audit/screenshots/chrome/` shows the rail shortcut hints reading as legible muted tokens, not near-invisible fades; logged-out reachable ‚Üí no live gate).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-3").
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** (1) the right-rail keyboard-shortcut hints (`V`/`H`/`M`/‚Ä¶ and the `‚ñæ` split carets) were `opacity: 0.6, fontSize: 10` spans ‚Üí a new shared **`railHint(active)`** helper: a muted `--chrome-muted` token (or `--on-accent` on an active ember row) at a readable size + lighter weight, so the hint reads as secondary through COLOR + WEIGHT, never a fade. (2) disabled menu rows were `opacity: 0.45` in the overlay `item()` helper, the `arrRow` helper, and the type menu ‚Üí a new `PAL.disabled` (`--text-tertiary`) token on the text, with a new `.menu button:disabled { opacity: 1; }` rule in `index.css` so the global disabled fade doesn't double-dim the tokened text into illegibility. (3) swept the rail/menu chrome for the remaining `opacity: 0.x` hierarchy uses and converted them: the header plan-label caret (`--chrome-muted`), the "+ Add" caret, the easement-type caret (`PAL.muted`), and the two "add a parcel first" disabled-placeholder menu rows (were an `opacity: 0.7` container ‚Üí `PAL.disabled` text). Left genuine translucency alone (SVG grid/marquee opacity, map dimming, halos).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open/Verify/Done for opacity/hierarchy/rail-hint. Net-new cleanup adjacent to the B316‚ÄìB320/B341 theming lineage (the text-hierarchy rule); no prior item owned these inline fades.

Consolidated to ONE shared definition so the four families read as one design language, and made keyboard operability verifiable.

Added `aria-pressed` (reflecting the active tool) across the right-rail tool buttons (Select/Pan/Marquee/Measure/Building/Car Parking/Road/Easement primaries + paving/trailer/pond + markup tools + Callout/Text) to match the left rail's existing `aria-pressed`, and `aria-haspopup="menu"` + `aria-expanded` on the split menu-openers (the `‚ñæ` buttons + the Parcel group). No layout changes ‚Äî states/semantics only; desktop layouts byte-identical.

Net-new consolidation; the `.rbtn`/`.dbtn`/`.menu` hover classes already existed (B526 lineage) ‚Äî this unifies the focus/press skeleton over them and closes the aria-pressed gaps, no prior item owned it.

### B920 ‚Äî A large unfilled/invisible closed markup swallows clicks across its whole interior `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #selection #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-1"; minted **B920** ‚Äî late-bound per the collision rule after a hot race: filed as NEW-1/2/3, the numbers were reassigned as concurrent sessions merged into the same pool ahead of this branch (B914 Doc-Review resume-leak; B915 viewport ContextMenu; B916/B917; then B918/B919 Doc-Review callouts), landing on the next truly-free block **B920/B921/B922** against fresh main; code/tests/harness re-labeled to match)*
`[x]` **FIXED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ 15-test unit lib ¬∑ headless e2e pass logged-out).** Root-caused on the owner's live plan (site `smrp1wrgg6u5`): a single closed markup ‚Äî `kind:"polygon"`, `fillOpacity:0` (invisible), locked, bounding box ‚âà5,117 ft √ó 4,430 ft ‚Äî blanketed the whole site, so clicking anywhere in its interior (all the empty corridor between the road strips) selected the invisible shape instead of the road you meant to grab; because it was also locked you just got the four-arrow move cursor and nothing happened, and "Send to Back" was powerless (it's already at the back, **and** it's a hit-AREA problem, not paint order). Root cause: an UNFILLED closed markup still set `pointerEvents:"all"` (`fillProps`, the old B155 whole-interior grab) even at `fillOpacity:0`, turning a ~5,000-ft invisible boundary into one giant click-catcher over everything below it (the markup layer paints/hit-tests **above** the element layer, so it shadowed the road elements too).
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED (green build + `test/markupPick.test.js` (15) + `e2e/markup-reach.spec.js` "B920" logged-out all pass; the mechanism is a general, data-independent hit-test rule fully exercisable on a blank site with no auth/GIS, so per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was reproduced + driven headless here ‚Äî an unfilled markup laid over a building, click falls through to the building ‚Äî not filed for a live pass. The owner's existing Sylvestri boundary becomes non-blocking automatically once this deploys; nothing for him to do.)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-1").
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx` `renderMarkupNode`):** a closed markup (polygon/rect/ellipse) now grabs by its whole INTERIOR only when it is FILLED (`closedFill = fillOpacity>0`). Each closed shape renders as a **fat transparent hit companion** (`stroke rgba(0,0,0,0.001)`, `strokeWidth={MK_HIT_PX}`) UNDER a pointer-inert visible shape ‚Äî the exact line/polyline technique ‚Äî with the companion's `pointerEvents` = `"all"` when filled (whole-body grab, B155/B156 small annotations preserved) vs `"stroke"` when unfilled (grab only along the stroke + tolerance, interior falls through to whatever's under it). `fillProps` retired.
- **New pure lib `lib/markupPick.js`** (Node-tested, zoom-agnostic ‚Äî tolerance is feet = on-screen px √∑ `view.ppf`): encodes the SAME `fillOpacity>0` rule the render uses, so the declarative hit area and the JS picker (B921 below) can never diverge. `markupHitModel`/`markupUnderPoint`/`markupsUnderPoint`/`nextMarkupSelection` + geometry helpers (`ringArea`/`pointInRing`/`distToPolyline`/`distToRing`/`boxCorners`/`ellipseRing`).
- **Dedup note (DEDUPE-FIRST):** tightens **B155/B156** (unfilled-closed-shape interior grab ‚Äî right for a small annotation, pathological for a huge invisible one) without regressing them (filled shapes still grab by interior; open paths unchanged). Reconciled with the shared `src/shared/markup/hitTest.js` ‚Äî its own header note says the Site Planner deliberately keeps SVG-native picking, so this stays declarative rather than re-routing through the imperative picker (which is fill-agnostic and used by Doc Review, where unfilled-interior-grab is intended). Not forked.
- **Verification harness:** `e2e/markup-reach.spec.js` "B920" drives the real SVG canvas logged-out ‚Äî draw a building, lay a big UNFILLED markup rect over it, deselect, click the building's centre ‚Üí the markup is NOT grabbed (`data-testid="markup-selected"` absent) AND a double-click there opens the ELEMENT properties (the click reached the building through the invisible markup); the markup is still reachable by clicking near its stroke.

### B921 ‚Äî No way to reach a covered shape; arrange no-ops give no feedback `[Site Planner]` (bug/feature) #site-planner #selection #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-2"; minted **B921** ‚Äî see B920's renumber note)*
`[x]` **BUILT + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ unit lib ¬∑ headless e2e pass logged-out).** When a markup is hit-covered by another there was no escape hatch to select the one underneath, and Send-to-Back/Send-Backward silently did nothing when the target is already at the bottom (`reorderByZ` returns `null`, the menu row is greyed, but the ‚åò/Ctrl+[ chord just no-oped with no cue ‚Üí read as "nothing works").
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED (green build + `test/markupPick.test.js` (15) + `e2e/markup-reach.spec.js` "B921" logged-out; interaction is fully exercisable on a blank site with no auth/GIS ‚Üí driven headless here per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-2").
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx` `startMoveMarkup` + `arrangeSel`):** (1) a **repeat-click OR Alt-click cycles the selection DOWN** through the markups stacked under the pointer (`markupsUnderPoint` ‚Üí smaller-area-first, so a small shape on a big one is reached first, then wraps) ‚Äî a fresh click (current selection not under this point) keeps the DOM-topmost hit the browser already resolved; anchors on the CURRENT selection (like the B910 measure cycle) so the walk is stable across repeats. The under-point stack honours the B920 fill-aware rule (an unfilled boundary only joins the cycle near its stroke) and skips easements hidden with an inactive parcel. (2) `arrangeSel` was refactored to compute its peer set once and, on a genuine no-op-at-end of a real stack (`arrangeFlags(peers).count > 1`), **flash "Already at the back/front."** instead of dead silence ‚Äî a lone item stays silent (nothing to reorder).
- **Dedup note (DEDUPE-FIRST):** adjacent to the **B141/B230** overlapping-hit-test lineage and the **B310/B420** "who wins a click" hit-priority tranche; mirrors the **B910** measure-cycle pattern (`measureHit.js`) as a sibling (`markupPick.js`) rather than forking the shared picker. Reconciled, not forked.
- **Verification harness:** `e2e/markup-reach.spec.js` "B921" ‚Äî two rectangles whose top edges overlap; a click on the shared stroke selects one, a repeat click (spaced past the double-tap window) cycles to the other (`data-mk-id` changes), a third wraps back.

### B922 ‚Äî Locked markup shows the "move" cursor + a false "drag a dot to reshape" hint `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui #selection  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-3"; minted **B922** ‚Äî see B920's renumber note)*
`[x]` **FIXED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ headless e2e pass logged-out).** A locked markup wore the four-arrow `move` cursor (shared `common.style`, no `locked` check) and the Properties panel still printed "Drag a dot to reshape‚Ä¶" even though `editablePath` returns `null` for a locked shape (so no dots render) ‚Äî a false affordance: it looked draggable but did nothing.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED (green build + `e2e/markup-reach.spec.js` "B922" logged-out ‚Äî lock a markup, assert the honest hint + on-canvas üîí + non-move cursor + `data-mk-locked`).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-3").
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** (1) a selected locked markup shows a non-`move` cursor (`pointer` ‚Äî it's still clickable to select/Unlock, just not draggable) via `mkCursor`, applied to every markup `<g>` (neutral + semantic); (2) the reshape hint is rewritten to "Locked ‚Äî click üîì Unlock below to move or reshape it." when locked; (3) a small on-canvas **üîí** rides the SELECTED locked markup (top-centre of its bbox, `data-export="skip"`), mirroring the element lock glyph ‚Äî so it's obvious WHY dragging does nothing. Parity: the same locked-aware cursor was applied to the three element render `<g>`s (a locked element is likewise select-only, `startMoveEl`), closing the same gap the handoff flagged (reconciled with B310/B420).
- **Dedup note (DEDUPE-FIRST):** honesty/affordance cleanup adjacent to B310/B420; no new hit-test path. Also added a `data-testid="markup-selected"` marker (+ `data-mk-id`/`data-mk-kind`/`data-mk-locked`) mirroring `measure-selected`, so markup selection is headlessly assertable (feeds B920/B921/B922 e2e).
- **Verification harness:** `e2e/markup-reach.spec.js` "B922" ‚Äî draw a rect, open its Properties, Lock it ‚Üí the hint switches to the honest "Locked ‚Äî" line, a üîí appears on the canvas, `data-mk-locked="1"`, the shape's cursor is no longer `move`; Unlock reverts the hint.

### B915 ‚Äî Context menus must be viewport-aware (one shared ContextMenu primitive, no more clipped-off Delete) `[Global / UI]` (bug) #ui #infra  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-1", top-of-queue priority; provisional **B914**, RENUMBERED **B915** on the pre-push `--against-main` check ‚Äî a concurrent session merged its own B914 (Doc Review resume-leak) first, so B915 is the real next free ID. Per repo precedent the code/tests/harness were re-labeled B915 too, since these are brand-new files and a stale B914 would misattribute to the merged item. ‚Üí V387.)*
`[x]` **FIXED + verified this session (green build ¬∑ 4690 unit tests incl. 7 new placement tests ¬∑ headless real-browser harness 8/8 driving the exact repro ¬∑ 4 migrated menus re-verified via their own harnesses).** Repro: right-click a project pin/row near the BOTTOM of the map ‚Äî the menu anchored top-left at the cursor and ran past the bottom edge, so "Delete project‚Ä¶" was clipped and unreachable; same class near the right edge. Root cause: every context menu positioned at raw `clientX`/`clientY` with a **hardcoded, ASSUMED height** fed into a one-sided `Math.min(y, innerHeight ‚àí 288)` clamp ‚Äî no measurement of the menu's real box, no flip, no max-height. When the real menu (status + share + delete) was taller than the guess, its bottom rows fell off-screen.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED for the primitive + the map/canvas/breadcrumb surfaces (green build + `test/contextMenuPlacement.test.js` (7) + `ui-audit/verify-b915-context-menu-viewport.mjs` (8/8, real Chromium, logged-out) drives the exact bottom-right/bottom-edge repro and confirms the whole menu incl. "Delete project‚Ä¶" lands inside the viewport with the 8px margin; the migrated **project-manage** (`verify-b439`, 7/7), **canvas map/deed** (`verify-b627`, all pass), **element Arrange** (`verify-b820`, all pass), **overlay** (`verify-b461`, 11/12 ‚Äî the 1 fail is a pre-existing Lock flake, confirmed identical on pristine), and **site-row** (`verify-b158`) menus all still pass). The two surfaces whose EXISTING harnesses are environmentally broken in this sandbox (Doc Review Arrange, Library folder tree ‚Äî both fail the SAME on pristine `main`, a canvas-drawing setup issue, NOT this change) are covered by code-parity with the 4 verified surfaces + the primitive's own 8/8 in-browser pass ‚Äî they're logged-out-reachable with no auth/GIS/real-data wall, so no separate live gate (no blocker-less `V###`).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-1").
- **The one shared primitive (DEDUPE-FIRST):** reused the existing portal/placement pattern rather than writing a new positioner. `AnchoredMenu`/`anchoredMenuPlacement.js` (B734) anchor to a TRIGGER element (dropdowns/flyouts); a context menu anchors to the CURSOR and must FLIP, which that math doesn't do ‚Äî so the new pair sits beside it:
  - **`src/shared/ui/contextMenuPlacement.js`** ‚Äî pure `placeContextMenu({cursorX,cursorY,menuW,menuH,viewportW,viewportH,margin,gap})`: (1) anchor top-left at the cursor; (2) flip LEFT if it would overflow the right edge; (3) flip UP if it would overflow the bottom edge; (4) a final hard-clamp to an 8px margin on all four edges (covers corner clicks where both flips fire, browser zoom, and a menu larger than the viewport); (5) a `maxHeight` of viewport‚àí2¬∑margin so an over-tall menu scrolls. Framework-free, 7 unit tests.
  - **`src/shared/ui/ContextMenu.jsx`** ‚Äî portal-to-`document.body` (escapes any `overflow:hidden`/transform-trapped ancestor), `position:fixed`, MEASURES itself in a `useLayoutEffect` (before paint ‚Äî no flash) ‚Üí `placeContextMenu` ‚Üí applies left/top/maxHeight, hidden until placed. Re-places (not closes) on content-grow via a `ResizeObserver`. Closes on Escape, outside click, and page scroll / window resize (a scroll INSIDE the menu is exempt). Props: `x,y,onClose,width/minWidth,zIndex,margin,gap,panelStyle,className,role,ariaLabel,testId`.
- **Every cursor-positioned menu migrated to it** (removed each one's assumed-dimension math): MapFinder `statusMenu` (the repro) + empty-map `mapMenu`; SitePlanner `vtxMenu`, `parcelMenu`, `mapMenu` (canvas/deed), `typeMenu` (element Arrange), `ovMenu` (overlay); Doc Review markup `ctxMenu` (Arrange); Library `FolderContextMenu`; ProjectBreadcrumb project-manage menu. `data-testid` (`folder-context-menu`, `project-manage-menu`) + role/aria-label preserved via props. `createPortal` imports dropped where they became unused (FolderTree, DocReview).
- **Named rules:** VIEWPORT-STABLE-adjacent (measure the real box, never an assumed dimension); MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS (the primitive is module-scope). `MAP.md` regenerated; `bugHuntGuards` B557 guard repointed to the prop form (rendered output unchanged).
