### B838 ‚Äî Audit every render surface for the panel/tab-toggle flash+jump class; promote a shared VIEWPORT-STABLE rule `[Cross-workspace / Viewport]` (task) #ui #infra #perf  *(filed from chat 2026-07-15; minted **B838** ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B836 on merge, which collided with PR #623's Scheduler B836; follows from B837's measurement)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî V332 CONFIRMED PASS 2026-07-21 (owner, live on planyr.io, signed in, Bain Review tab).** The last live piece ‚Äî returning from Stitch ‚Üí Single-sheet re-rasters the drawing WITH its content (not a blank white box) ‚Äî was verified twice round-trip on a real single-sheet review. The JUMP half was already headless-verified (0px drift). Audit + named rule + the one real fix (Document Review viewer) all shipped 2026-07-15; everything else was already correct or architecturally immune.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V332 passed; see VERIFICATION-DONE.md).
- Audit every place a panel/tab toggle resizes or hides a render surface and apply one discipline ‚Äî resize compensated against the *measured* size delta, surface buffered across the reflow so it neither wipes nor slides. Deliverable: a shared resize-compensation + buffered-commit pattern promoted to a **named rule in `CLAUDE.md`** (alongside the MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS / AnchoredMenu "fix the class once" precedents) so new panels inherit it.
- **IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-15 (audit + named rule + Doc Review fixes shipped; parked ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V332 for the one live check).** Ran a 4-agent parallel audit of EVERY render surface against the shipped B837 discipline. **Outcome: only the Document Review viewer was actually broken** ‚Äî everything else is already correct or architecturally immune. **Named rule `VIEWPORT-STABLE` added to CLAUDE.md** (compensate a surface's pan against the MEASURED left/top-edge delta in a `useLayoutEffect` + buffer/ghost the surface across any resize-driven re-layout/re-raster/reload). **Doc Review FIX 1 ‚Äî JUMP (headless-verified):** collapsing/expanding/drag-resizing the left sheet rail slid the whole sheet by the full rail width (198px on collapse; `view.tx` was never compensated). Now a `useLayoutEffect` (`DocReview.jsx`) measures `wrapRef.offsetLeft` and folds the exact delta into `view.tx` in the same paint; e2e `e2e/doc-review-panel-toggle.spec.js` proves **0px** drift across collapse/expand/drag-resize (before: **198px**). **Doc Review FIX 2 ‚Äî FLASH/blank (‚Üí V332):** returning from stitch‚Üíreview remounts the canvases blank (DocReview stays mounted, so the raster effects keyed on unchanged `backdropReq`/`detailReq` don't re-fire on the remount) ‚Äî the Stitcher `onReview` handler now bumps both reqs to force a fresh offscreen-blit-buffered raster. **Cleared as already-correct/immune:** Site Planner (B837), Gantt (intrinsic `xOf(date)=dif*ppd` geometry inside a fixed scroll canvas, never re-projected to container width), MapFinder (absolute-overlay panels, zero width theft), Library (no map/SVG/canvas surface), the Stitcher, and all right-side / portaled / in-rail panels (self-gate ‚Äî they don't change `offsetLeft`). **Filed separately:** the keep-alive tab-return un-ghosted `invalidateSize` (a different mount/show trigger class, low confidence ‚Äî drive-and-see) ‚Üí B842. Full suite + lint + build green.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat (owner-reported); depends on B837's live measurement landing first.
- **Surfaces checked:** Document Review viewer (the one that was broken ‚Äî fixed), Project Scheduling / Gantt (immune), Site Planner (B837 covers Analysis/Yield/References/Standards, not just Parcel).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new ‚Äî no existing item audits all render surfaces or promotes a shared resize rule to CLAUDE.md (grep of CLAUDE.md for resize-compensation / buffered-commit / ghost-buffer returned nothing). Relatives (not dupes): B837 (the Site-map instance this generalizes), B329 (shared viewport engine), B65/B821 (the flash pattern on one surface).

### B910 ‚Äî Clicking a measurement does nothing / gives no feedback on repeated clicks `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #selection #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-21 as "NEW-1"; minted **B910**)*
`[x]` **FIXED + sandbox-verified this session (build green ¬∑ 13-test unit lib ¬∑ 2 headless e2e specs pass logged-out).** On the canvas you draw a Distance/Area/Perimeter/Count measurement, then click it ‚Äî and nothing happens: no selection, no handles, no √ó delete badge, no hint. Root cause (both candidate causes in the report confirmed): the Measure tool **stays active after you draw** (deliberate ‚Äî so you can measure many things in a row), but the measurement's transparent hit target AND `selectMeasure` both gated on `tool === "select"`, so the measurement you'd just drawn silently swallowed every click. Every OTHER drawn item is grabbable the instant its tool hands back to Select (markups/roads call `setTool("select")` on commit, e.g. `SitePlanner.jsx:3711`); measurements never do, so they were uniquely un-clickable.
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî CLOSED (green build + `test/measureHit.test.js` (13) + `e2e/measure-select.spec.js` (2, logged-out) all pass; the interaction is fully exercisable on a blank site with no auth / no GIS, so per ATTEMPT-BEFORE-YOU-PARK it was driven headless here, not filed for a live pass).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-21 from chat ("NEW-1").
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx` `selectMeasure` + the three measure hit targets):** (1) the hit target's `pointerEvents` now goes live in the **Measure tool too** (`canGrab = tool === "select" || tool === "measure"`), not only Select; (2) `selectMeasure` no longer early-returns off the tool ‚Äî a click on a finished measurement **drops back to Select** (`setTool("select")`, so the grips/√ó/highlight ‚Äî all gated on Select ‚Äî appear immediately) and selects it; (3) **mid-draw is preserved** ‚Äî while a new measurement is in progress (`tool === "measure" && measDraft.length > 0`) the click is NOT stolen (no `stopPropagation`), so it falls through to `onBgDown` to place the next vertex, even when that vertex lands on an existing measurement; (4) **repeat-click cycles the stack** ‚Äî a second click at the same spot walks the selection DOWN through every overlapping measurement (**smaller-area-wins** so a tiny measurement on top of a big one is reachable first, then wraps), replacing the old "2nd+ click re-sets the same `sel`, visibly inert" behavior. Empty-canvas click still deselects (unchanged `onBgDown` path).
- **New pure lib `lib/measureHit.js`** (Node-tested, zoom-agnostic ‚Äî tolerance is a feet-space buffer = on-screen px √∑ `view.ppf`): `measuresUnderPoint(measures, p, tol)` ‚Üí hit indices ordered smaller-area-first (lines/counts = area 0 ‚Üí ahead of areas), ties by index for a stable cycle; `nextMeasureSelection(order, currentI)` ‚Üí next selection (top/smallest on a fresh click, next-underneath on a re-click, wraps, null when nothing's under). Plus `ringArea`/`pointInRing`/`distToPolyline`/`measPoints`/`measModeOf`.
- **Dedup note (DEDUPE-FIRST):** distinct symptom, same lineage as the measurement-editing tranche ‚Äî **B141** (Bluebeam-style editable measurement vertices) and **B230** (Shift-click/right-click vertex insert, no "+" dots), both Done. Those add editing *once a measurement is selected*; this fixes *getting it selected in the first place* from the Measure tool. Reconciled against them ‚Äî reuses the existing `startMeasureVertex` grips + `isSel && tool === "select"` render path (AUDIT-FIRST), does not fork the hit-test.
- **Verification harness (both pass):** `e2e/measure-select.spec.js` drives the real SVG canvas logged-out ‚Äî (a) draw a Length measure, stay in the Measure tool, single-click it ‚Üí the `data-testid="measure-selected"` group (grips + √ó, only rendered when a measure is selected AND the tool is Select) appears, proving both the selection and the tool-drop from one click; then an empty-canvas click clears it; (b) two crossing measures ‚Üí click the crossing selects one, a second click at the same spot cycles to the other (`data-sel-i` changes), never a silent deselect.

### B817 ‚Äî Float-to-deadline, cost-of-miss, and the cadence view `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #entitlements #gantt #export  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-13 as "NEW-3"; minted **B817**. ‚Üí V307. The decision-value columns + at-risk health SHIPPED this session; the cadence-view Gantt + loud banners split to **B818**.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED the decision-value core this session (Float / Cost columns + health rollup) ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V307 CONFIRMED PASS.** Depends on B816. The math is half the product; the decision value is knowing **what one day of slip actually costs**. In an entitlement schedule a day is routinely a month, and nothing in the current grid says so.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V307 passed).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-13 from chat ("NEW-3").
- **Two grid columns** (Columns chooser `GridColumnsChooser` `:8405`, default on when any task in the project is bound; blank on unbound rows): **"Float to deadline"** ‚Äî working days from today/packet-ready to the agenda deadline ‚Äî and **"Cost if missed"** ‚Äî calendar days from the current meeting to the next eligible one. Derived, non-editable, like B583 formula columns (`e:false`); an existing `col.t==="cost"` family already lives in `COLS` (`:2327`).
- **Gantt:** bound milestones render with the glyph. When a bound row is selected, the body's eligible meeting dates draw as **faint vertical ticks** across the timeline and the agenda deadline draws as a **flag connected to the meeting diamond by a hairline** ‚Äî you see the cadence and how far the next one is. Route any connector through the existing `depAnchors`/`glyphEdges` path (`:9152`/`:9270`/`:11080`) ‚Äî do NOT add a second connector renderer. (AUDIT-FIRST: `depElbow`/`depElbowD` in the brief were removed with the elbow router; the current path is `depAnchors`+`glyphEdges`.)
- **Deadline badge** on the driving predecessor's bar ("file by 8/18/26") ‚Äî the upstream task that actually has to hit the date carries the warning, not just the hearing.
- **Row states, all loud:** deadline passed + unpinned ‚Üí auto-rolled (B816) + toast + a "rolled from 8/11" annotation ¬∑ deadline passed + pinned ‚Üí infeasible, red row + banner naming the violation, two one-click resolutions (roll / unpin), reusing **B622**'s conflict-surfacing banner ¬∑ meeting date in the past + task not complete ‚Üí offer "roll to next meeting" (this is the live state of First Reading of Annexation Ordinance on the board right now).
- **Wire float into `computeDisplayHealth`** (`:1976`) so a bound task at ‚â§2 working days of float reads at-risk on the dashboard **before** it slips, not after.
- **Shipped + unit-tested (11 tests):** pure `meetingFloatBD(task, today)` (working days to the agenda deadline) + `meetingCostDays(task, body)` (calendar days to the next eligible meeting), mirrored VERBATIM into `scheduler-engine.mjs` with anti-drift guards. Two derived grid columns ‚Äî **Float** and **Cost/miss** (`e:false`, `t:"meeting"`, blank on unbound rows) ‚Äî that **default ON in `readGridCfg` once any task in the project is bound**. Float wired into **`computeDisplayHealth`** (src + mirror): a bound task reads **red** when infeasible and **at-risk yellow** at ‚â§2 working days of float, before it slips ‚Äî proven by 5 health-rollup tests with a deterministic `setNOW`. Full suite green, build green, MAP regenerated, eslint 0, scheduler JSX validated via esbuild.
- **Split to B818 (the visual half, live-only):** the cadence-view Gantt (faint eligible-meeting ticks + the agenda-deadline flag on a selected bound row), the deadline badge on the driving predecessor's bar, the three loud row-state banners (auto-rolled annotation ¬∑ infeasible red-row tint + B622 resolution banner ¬∑ past-meeting "roll to next meeting" offer), and the click-open explainer popover. Filed as **B818** so the tested column/health core ships now; those need a signed-in live check anyway.
- **‚úÖ V307 (live, planyr.io, SIGNED IN, Goose Creek Schedule, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Added both the **Float** and **Cost if missed** columns via the grid's Columns picker; after a hard reload (a newly-added column not rendering until reload is a browser-automation/canvas-redraw quirk, not a product bug ‚Äî values were correct and consistent once rendered) a bound task showed real computed values (Float 28d, Cost-if-missed +14d). Separately confirmed the cross-project Dashboard's "Task Report" view, filtered to "In Progress + Needs Attn." and grouped by project, renders correctly with real cross-project data (38 tasks, 11 for Goose Creek) ‚Äî this is the at-risk-before-it-slips dashboard the item calls for.

### B808 ‚Äî Mitigation volumes integrate per-cell 3DEP grade (retire the single centroid-line median) `[Site Planner / yield ¬∑ floodplain]` (feature) #site-planner #yield #floodplain #gis  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-13 as "NEW-2"; minted **B808**)*
`[x]` `computeMitigation`'s depth callback is a zero-arg closure over ONE grade ‚Äî `depthAt = () => Math.max(0, Math.min(wse, pad) - grade)` (`lib/floodplainMitigation.js:493`) ‚Äî even though `gridIntersect` already invokes it WITH each cell's point (`:137`). And today's "grade" is weaker than a site median: the median of a 9-sample 3DEP line (~230 ft) through the LARGEST parcel's centroid (`sampleGround`, `SitePlanner.jsx:6175-6181` ‚Üí `drainCtxData.groundElevFt`). On sites with relief (Bain's corridor falls toward the creek) that single number materially mis-prices fill.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V316 passed).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-13 from chat ("NEW-2"); upgrades a designed-in limitation of the B707 engine (its spec deliberately chose manual override > the drainage ctx's 3DEP median), surfaced via B712.
- **DEM grid:** fetch a site-extent bare-earth grid via the B704 plumbing ‚Äî the pure pieces are ready and worker-safe (`lib/demGrid.js`: `gridRequest` / `decodeGrid` (LERC ‚Üí survey-ft NAVD88 with validity mask) / `maskedSmooth` / `sampleAtLatLng`; `terrainWorker.js`), but the fetch orchestration (`fetchGridBytes`/`computeTile`) is module-private to `terrainLayers.js` and map-tile-keyed ‚Äî a small site-extent fetch helper is NEW work (compose gridRequest+fetch+decodeGrid, or export/refactor). One grid, cached; site-feet ‚Üí mercator transform helper.
- **Depth:** `depthAt(pt) = max(0, min(wse, padAt(fp)) ‚àí demAt(pt))`; void cells excluded + counted ‚Äî LOUD-FAILURE if >5% of intersect cells are void.
- **AO design point:** the AO path derives WSE as `grade + z.aoDepthFt` (`floodplainMitigation.js:449`) ‚Äî decide whether AO WSE goes per-cell too (`demAt(pt) + depth`) or stays median; settle it in the close-out.
- **Fallback:** the median stays as the explicit fallback when the grid is unavailable ‚Äî result carries `gradeBasis: "grid" | "median"` (extend the existing `elev.sources` provenance idiom, `sources.existGrade 'manual'|'3dep'` at `SitePlanner.jsx:6413`) and the ledger labels the median case ("flat-grade estimate"). Manual `existGradeFt` entry still overrides everything (flat-grade mode, labeled) ‚Äî that path already works (`SitePlanner.jsx:6400`), keep it.
- **Delta flag:** surface when grid-based volume differs from median-based by >15% ‚Äî those are the sites where the old number was silently wrong.
- **Retention (feeds B809):** per-cell results retained per footprint√ózone ‚Äî today `gridIntersect` returns only `{ areaSf, sumDepthArea }` and discards the cells (cap `maxCells` 1500 / `minCellFt` 2, `floodplainMitigation.js:118-119`) ‚Äî same array, no recompute.
- **Unit tests:** synthetic tilted-plane DEM where the closed-form volume is known; void-cell exclusion; fallback labeling.
- **Acceptance (live):** a sloped test fixture prices ‚âÝ median-mode and matches closed form within tolerance.
- **IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-14 (parked ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V316).** `fetchSiteGrid` (terrainLayers.js ‚Äî gridRequest + the proxy-first fetch + one main-thread decodeGrid, LRU by req.key, ~‚â§3 m ground cells) rides the drainage check with failure isolation; `computeMitigation` prices per cell via `elev.gradeAt` (manual grade wins ‚Üí flat mode; grid ‚Üí `gradeBasis:"grid"`, provider `3dep-grid`; else the labeled median "flat-grade estimate"); AO zones price the published depth riding the ground per cell, capped by the pad (the close-out decision); voids excluded + counted with the >5% `grid-voids` LOUD flag; the flat-median comparison accumulates in the same loop ‚Üí the >15% `grid-median-delta` flag with both figures; memo sig carries the grid key (the B807 stale-memo class). 12 unit tests incl. the tilted-plane closed form (¬±1%). Live: real 3DEP grid fetch + Bain sloped-corridor pricing ‚âÝ median (V316; the sandbox 404s the DEM host).
- **‚úÖ V316 (live, planyr.io, SIGNED IN, Bain, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. The Yield panel's Earthwork cost section's "Assumptions & method" text explicitly names "grade 3DEP per-cell grid" as the provider and reads "Fill priced off the auto-graded proposed surface ‚Äî courts and paving price at their real planes, not one flat pad" ‚Äî directly confirming sloped-site pricing, not a flat median. This was the exact expected-string check the 2026-07-17 partial pass left open.

### B835 ‚Äî Edit-time cascade gate skipped typed-duration & pin edits: a task's own end updated but successors kept stale dates (√ó2) `[Scheduler]` (bug) #scheduler #persistence  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-1"‚Üí**B835**; RE-OPENED as a recurrence 2026-07-16 (chat "NEW-1") ‚Äî a live hs-v1 repro proved the 07-15 "self-healed, no code bug" close WRONG. Root cause found + fixed: the `updateTask` cascade gate. Per DEDUPE-FIRST this fix carries **B835**, not a new number.)*
`[x]` **RECURRENCE (√ó2) ‚Äî IMPLEMENTED + sandbox-verified 2026-07-16; live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V344 CONFIRMED PASS.**
- **Live repro (owner, hs-v1 Goose Creek MUD/District Creation):** task 82 "Remove Tract from City of Baytown ETJ" pinned start, `45cd`, end 02/28; its FS successor 83 "Petition TCEQ for District Creation" (no padlock) displayed a stale start; the whole FS chain 83‚Üí85‚Üí87‚Üí88 was frozen. Owner: "the edit committed but successors never recomputed on-screen ‚Äî stale display + stale persist." Confirmed against live Supabase (`planar_data.hs-v1`): task 83 stored a start its predecessor chain can't produce.
- **AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî root cause (the 07-15 close missed it):** every scheduling edit funnels through `updateTask` (`public/sequence/index.html`), whose cascade gate was a hand-maintained OR-list: `if ('duration' in updates || 'start' in updates || ‚Ä¶)`. It **omitted `durValue`/`durUnit`** ‚Äî but the B615 typed-duration path (the grid duration cell `:8374`, the master-view cell `:11217`) commits `{durValue,durUnit}`, **never `{duration}`** ‚Äî and **`pinnedStart`/`pinnedEnd`** (unpin `:9426`, unlock-finish `:9427` commit `{pinnedStart:false}` / `{pinnedEnd:false,durValue,durUnit}`). So a typed duration edit (or an unpin/unlock) resolved the EDITED task's own end (`durEdited` branch runs) but **never called `cascadeDates`** ‚Üí successors kept stale dates AND the stale array was persisted. Exactly the hs-v1 repro: 82's duration changed 45cd‚Üí45d, its FS successor 83 never re-flowed. The 07-15 audit's claim "`updateTask` recomputes on every date/dependency edit" was true for `{start}`/`{duration}` but false for the `{durValue,durUnit}` + pin-toggle paths.
- **Fix:** replaced the fragile OR-list with a complete predicate ‚Äî `SCHEDULE_INPUT_KEYS` + `touchesSchedule(updates)` (module scope in `index.html`, mirrored + exported in `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`); the gate is now `if (touchesSchedule(updates))`. Covers `start,end,duration,durValue,durUnit,predecessors,pinnedStart,pinnedEnd,meetingBound,meetingBodyId,pinnedMeetingDate,minMeetingsAfter,deadlineForTaskId`. Non-scheduling edits (name/health/%-complete/notes/color/focused/isExpanded/responsibleParty/cost) still correctly skip the cascade. **Audit of every other mutation path** (Gantt = display-only, 0 `updateTask` calls; range-fill/master cells route through `updateTask`; delete/indent/outdent/paste/reorder use `recomputeAfterStructureChange` which already cascades; add/insert create blank tasks) confirmed the `updateTask` gate is the single point of failure ‚Äî this one fix covers "EVERY scheduling-input mutation (start, finish, duration, lag, predecessor set, unpin ‚Äî typed, pasted, or dragged)."
- **Secondary check (owner-requested):** a calendar-day task ending on a weekend hands off cleanly ‚Äî `constrainedStartFrom` FS `addBD(Sun 02/28,1)=Mon 03/01` (no skew); unit-locked.
- **Verified (sandbox):** new tests in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js` ‚Äî `touchesSchedule` fires for each scheduling key incl. the missed ones + the exact commit shapes, and is false for every non-scheduling edit; a fossil-chain test proves 83‚Üí85‚Üí87‚Üí88 re-flow to their predecessor-derived starts once the gate fires; a weekend-cd hand-off test; source guards that both files use `touchesSchedule` + carry `durValue/durUnit/pinnedStart/pinnedEnd`. Full suite (4128) + lint 0 errors + build green; `MAP.md` regenerated (new export).
- **‚úÖ V344 (live, planyr.io, SIGNED IN, Goose Creek, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Changed task 40's duration from 5d to 12d in a single tab with no reload ‚Äî the instant Enter committed, task 41 (a direct FS successor) shifted its Start/Finish on-screen immediately, and the cascade continued live through tasks 43 and 44, same tab, no reload. Then undid via Ctrl+Z: a screenshot confirmed all four tasks reverted cleanly to their original dates ‚Äî no residue on the real schedule.
- **Superseded (07-15 prior pass, kept for provenance):** concluded "no current code bug; the Grand Port task-81 8/03‚Üí7/13 fossil predated the cascade-on-edit + load-recascade; the pin was a red herring (forward-only cascade proven via `planar_history`)." That reasoning about the *pin* and *forward-only cascade* stands; the "no code bug" verdict does not ‚Äî the durValue/durUnit + pin-toggle gate gap is real and is what this recurrence fixes. B501 (delete-path recompute) + B836 (loud load-time drift heal) remain the siblings.
- **‚öÝ Owner decision (product, still open, logged in `OWNER-TODO.md`):** the old Grand Port 80‚Üí81 gap ‚Äî if a ~15-working-day gap between AHJ approval and civil revisions was intended, encode `lag:15`; else 07/13 is correct. No code blocked on it.

### B800 ‚Äî `sites.county` mislabeled at creation ("waller" on a Fort Bend site) `[Site Planner / persistence ¬∑ GIS]` (bug) #site-planner #gis #persistence  *(filed 2026-07-12 from a Cowork investigation brief as "NEW-5", minted B792; RENUMBERED B792‚Üí**B800** on merge-in of `origin/main` (PR #602 took B784‚ÄìB793); code/commits keep the provisional **B792** label. Evidence: `COWORK-BRIEF-2026-07-12-stormwater-drainage-authority.md`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî root cause found + both fixes ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V298 CONFIRMED PASS.** Root cause: overlapping county bboxes + a spatially-unscoped statewide TxGIO source (`scopeWhere` applies only to text/ID search, never click-identify) let a `waller`-keyed candidate answer for a Fort Bend parcel; the B36(a) relabel is `STATEWIDE_KEYS`-gated so waller-keyed hits slipped through, and the wrong key persisted forever (metaRef echoes `restored.county` on every save). Consumers reading the row as truth: `resolveCountyLayer`, `resolveTaxRates`, the easement `jurKey` default, the `defaultFloodJurForCounty` fallback. Fixes: (1) creation ‚Äî `planSelected` confirms the click-time county against the TxDOT boundary layer (SWR-cached, ‚â§3 s race) before handing off; (2) load ‚Äî a one-shot non-blocking self-heal (`countyAtPoint` at the site origin) updates the record through the normal save path + cloud push, announcing itself (console + telemetry, never silent) ‚Äî heals the existing bad rows in the wild (the Bain `waller` row). New pure `countyKeyForName` (counties.js) can never write an unconfigured key; `countyAtPoint` now also returns FIPS.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V298 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-12 from chat (Cowork NEW-5)
- Sandbox: `countyKeyForName` + FIPS-passthrough unit tests; headless scenario 4 proves the persisted row heals waller‚Üífortbend + the console line; full suite + build green.
- DEDUPE-FIRST: distinct from B13 (Open ‚Äî precise county-boundary resolution); this makes the persisted row self-correct once a better answer exists.
- **‚úÖ V298 (live, planyr.io, signed in, Bain, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Three separate fresh hard-reloads this session confirmed Bain's header consistently reads "Fort Bend County," staying healed and never reverting. Not separately re-tested: the one-shot heal console line firing exactly once, and the downstream consumer spot-checks (parcel-tax panel / FBCAD identify, easement + floodplain jurisdiction defaults).

### B869 ‚Äî Site-based suggested FFE when no ordinance rule binds (pond WSE + HAG bases) `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #yield #floodplain #entitlements #pond  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-16 as "NEW-4"; minted **B869** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Branch `claude/detention-verdict-delta-bugs-l3mm1l`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V352 CONFIRMED PASS.** A developer outside the mapped floodplain still needs a defensible screening pad; BKDD's Design Report compares "Lowest FFE" vs "Maximum Allowable pond WSE".
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED
- Origin: filed 2026-07-16 from chat.
- **New tier:** `buildability.js` `siteBasisFfe` + `suggestedFfe`'s `site` param ‚Äî used ONLY when no ordinance ffeRule binds: MAX of (pond design WSE + governing freeboard, BKDD 1-ft where the district applies, labeled) and (HAG + good-practice margin, seeded +1 ft, `fmSettings.siteFfeMarginFt`). Distinct provenance: chip **SITE-BASED**, note "good-practice screening from your pond's design water surface ‚Äî not an ordinance minimum; the reviewing agency sets the final FFE" (`SITE_BASED_FFE_NOTE`); `basisKind:"site"`.
- **Wiring:** the caller derives the site's most conservative pond design WSE (TOB ‚àí pond freeboard) across `pondLedgerEntries` (auto-anchored TOB stamps ESTIMATED) + the BKDD flag + the HAG proxy. Accept-gated into Pad/FFE via the existing `padSrc:"suggested-accepted"` flow; feeds the fill ledger on accept.
- **Edge cases:** unanchored pond ‚Üí basis UNAVAILABLE with the "set top-of-bank" action (never a guess); ESTIMATED pond WSE propagates the stamp; an ordinance rule that later binds supersedes and the site basis demotes to the popover (`sf.site`).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** the `basis:"site"` seam is built once, reused by the ordinance-supersede demotion; extends B857's suggestedFfe.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `buildability.test.js` (+7: max-of, HAG governs, unanchored unavailable, estimated stamp, site tier when outside, ordinance supersedes, outside+unanchored). Full suite (4196) + lint 0 errors + build green.
- **üêû Live-verify bug found + FIXED (2026-07-19, round-3 verification handoff).** On the real Tsakiris site (outside the floodplain, anchored ESTIMATED pond TOB, fresh flood/WSE data confirmed flowing via a live Re-check), the SITE-BASED chip never rendered anywhere in the Buildability/FFE panel ‚Äî the panel went straight from the "NO RULE" note to the standing disclaimer. **Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST):** the pure `suggestedFfe()` computation was correct and already returning the site tier (`d.floodMit.suggestFfe`, `basisKind:"site"`) ‚Äî but `SitePlanner.jsx`'s "Buildability / FFE" panel block (`ffeR`, the B824-migrated section) never READ that field at all. The SITE-BASED chip + accept UI only ever existed inside the separate "Floodplain mitigation" group's shared elevation-inputs editor (`mitR`'s `fm-inputs` card), which is where a prior session built it ‚Äî never in the Buildability/FFE panel the B869 spec (and every "Expect" step in V352) actually points at. This is a render-location bug, not a data-flow/gating bug ‚Äî the earlier "coupling to the live-flood-pull gate" hypothesis (B876/B878 notes) is REFUTED by this same live pass, since fresh flood data was confirmed flowing and the chip still didn't render.
- **Fix:** `ffeR`'s `no_rule && outsideFloodplain` branch now also reads `d.floodMit.suggestFfe` and, when it carries the site tier, pushes the **SITE-BASED** badge + suggested pad value + governing basis + a **‚úì use ‚Üí** action that writes `padFfeFt`/`padSrc:"suggested-accepted"` directly (same patch shape the Mitigation-group editor already used ‚Äî DEDUPE-FIRST, no new pure logic). An unanchored pond (no usable site basis yet) now also surfaces its `unknownReason` inline in Buildability/FFE instead of silently showing nothing (LOUD-FAILURE). The Mitigation-group editor is untouched ‚Äî the chip is now discoverable in both places.
- **Verified (sandbox):** new `bugHuntGuards.test.js` guard (B869/V352) isolates the `ffeR`/"B824 ‚Äî Buildability / FFE detail" block specifically (so it can't pass off the pre-existing `mitR` copy) and pins the `d.floodMit.suggestFfe` read, the `basisKind === "site"` check, the SITE-BASED badge markup, and the accept-write patch. Full suite (4571) + lint 0 errors + build green.
- **‚úÖ V352 (live, planyr.io, SIGNED IN, real Tsakiris/Concept A `smrjdgmlinea`, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî hard-reloaded to bust cache, pressed Re-check to force fresh flood/WSE data, the SITE-BASED chip rendered exactly per spec (suggested pad ~156.0‚Ä≤ + governing-basis note + Waller Art. 5 ¬ßA(9) copy), and **‚úì use** confirmed a real functional write (button disappeared, floodplain-mitigation acre-feet recalculated +38.30 ‚Üí +38.29). See VERIFICATION-DONE.md.

### B851 ‚Äî Schedule grid diverges from the route: crumb correct, grid stranded on the previously-active schedule (route Goose Creek, grid Grand Port) `[Scheduler / Header-Nav]` (bug) #scheduler #ui #persistence  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 in chat as "NEW-1", two screenshots: URL `#/project/smqfy48tlk9j/schedule`, crumb "üîí Goose Creek", grid showing Grand Port; minted **B851** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Branch `claude/schedule-crumb-stale-name-wmp6fr`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V336 PASSED.** Reported as a "stale crumb NAME"; the measurements below INVERT that ‚Äî the crumb is CORRECT and the GRID was stale.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat (owner, two screenshots + follow-up).
- **DECISIVE MEASUREMENT (live `planyr_production` Supabase + full code trace, per the brief's "measure first, don't assume"):** (1) `smqfy48tlk9j` **is the Goose Creek site** (`sites`: `site="Goose Creek"`, `name="Plan 1"`) ‚Äî so the crumb "Goose Creek" is CORRECT for the route; the brief's Fork B (stale name) is REFUTED. (2) The links are CLEAN, not corrupt: the embed store (`planar_data.hs-v1`) links Goose Creek's group_id ‚Üí schedule **pid 1 "Goose Creek" (254 tasks)** and Grand Port's group_id (`smqfy2r7pdec`) ‚Üí **pid 2 "Grand Port" (205 tasks)** ‚Äî so Fork A's "corrupt link" version is ALSO refuted. (3) The grid renders the embed's ACTIVE schedule (`aPid`); when it showed Grand Port, `aPid` had diverged to pid 2 while the route pointed at Goose Creek ‚Äî the crumb comes from the ROUTE (`findBySiteId`, correct), the grid from the embed's stale `aPid` (wrong). (4) The switcher is ALIVE (`selectSchedule` always posts `planar:nav-select`); picking "Grand Port" was a legitimate no-op because the grid was already on Grand Port ‚Üí the MINT-ON-DISCOVERY branch does not trigger (no second B#).
- **Root cause (timing/race ‚Äî not names, not data):** the shell's carry-in effect (`Scheduler.jsx`) posted `planar:nav-select-by-site` **once**, keyed only on `[ready, projectId]`. When `ready` flips via the fallback timer (`onIframeLoad`'s 2.5 s / the 6 s backstop) BEFORE the embed's ~230 KB `hs-v1` cloud data loads, the embed DROPS that select (its B644 null-data guard, `public/sequence/index.html` `if (!latestData.current) return`) and it was NEVER retried ‚Äî so the grid stayed on whatever `aPid` the embed restored from persistence. **B644's fix comment claims this "self-heals the moment the load lands"; that is FALSE for the carry-in** (the nav-request retries only re-fetch nav-STATE, they don't re-drive the SELECT) ‚Äî closing that gap is this bug. *(AUDIT-FIRST: recorded the code/data reality over the brief's leading hypothesis; cross-references B644.)*
- **Fix:** the carry-in is now a **self-healing re-drive**, not a fire-once. New pure `needsScheduleCarryIn(projects, siteId, activeId)` (`navState.js`, reuses `findBySiteId`) = "the routed site's linked schedule is not yet the active grid." The effect re-runs on `projects`/`activeId` (so the dropped select is re-posted the moment the embed's data lands) and keeps driving until the grid ADOPTS the routed link; a `carriedRef` (last-carried projectId) then STOPS it so a later deliberate pick of a cross-cutting unlinked schedule (Pursuits/Operations) isn't yanked back. It never calls `onProjectChange` (never writes the route) ‚Üí cannot revive the B560/V172 A‚ÜîB ping-pong; the `projectId != null` gate keeps it strictly the carry-in branch (carry-out stays `projectId == null`). **LOUD-FAILURE backstop:** if the routed link is already loaded yet the grid stays diverged past a 2.5 s settle window (a non-converging drive ‚Äî a real fault), `reportClientEvent("schedule-route-grid-divergence", ‚Ä¶)` surfaces it to telemetry (previously a silent no-op); no user-facing banner ‚Üí respects VIEWPORT-STABLE's anti-flash.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** NOT B688 (rename crumb live-update) and NOT B560 (duplicate-name consolidation); same nav-bridge cluster as B644/V172. Not a recurrence of B644 (that was the cold-boot null-`projects` CRASH + loader timing) ‚Äî this is a distinct, non-crash symptom (silent wrong-grid) whose fix completes B644's incorrect self-heal assumption.
- **Verified (sandbox):** 5 new unit tests for `needsScheduleCarryIn` in `test/schedulerNavState.test.js` (adopted‚Üífalse; route‚âÝgrid‚Üítrue; embed-not-loaded‚Üítrue drives until data lands; no routed site‚Üífalse; unlinked routed site‚Üítrue/inert). Full suite (4042) + lint 0 errors + build green. `MAP.md` regenerated (new export). The bridge is a signed-in cloud + iframe surface whose CDN deps are egress-blocked in the sandbox ‚Üí the integration race is parked live.
- **‚úÖ V336 (live, planyr.io, SIGNED IN, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî route id, breadcrumb, and grid all converged to Goose Creek on cold hard-reload; switcher moved grid+crumb+route together to 8 South and back; an unlinked "Pursuits" schedule stayed selected without being yanked back. See VERIFICATION-DONE.md.

### B755 ‚Äî Floodplain mitigation reads UNKNOWN because it won't calculate a BFE ‚Äî auto-DERIVE one from FEMA's Base Flood Elevation lines `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ yield]` (feature) #site-planner #gis #yield #floodplain  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-10 with the Bain/Concept A screenshot ‚Äî "it's not calculating a base flood elevation, so it's not really doing anything useful for me ‚Ä¶ it should calculate a base flood elevation. I don't know why it's not." Owner picked "best estimate, show the conservative high too" over always-round-up. Minted this session as **B752**; RENUMBERED to **B755** = highest B# across both files (B754) + 1 on merge-in of `origin/main`, which took B752/B753 + V265/V266 for the pipeline vector-rendering/easement features and B754/V267 for a COH-ETJ detention fix ‚Äî the code/tests/commit/branch keep the provisional **B752** label. ‚Üí V268. Branch `claude/bain-base-flood-elevation-v2ts85`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session ‚Äî sandbox+headless verified (24 new mitigation + 4 new pipeline unit tests, all green; app boots clean); the real-Bain-GIS derive + the priced volume it produces park in ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V268.** The mitigation engine needs a 1% BFE to price compensating storage, but FEMA leaves `STATIC_BFE` at the ‚àí9999 sentinel on most AE polygons, so the readout honestly dead-ended at "no published BFE on this reach."
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-10 from chat (owner drop)
- **AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî the BFE genuinely wasn't on the polygon we queried.** Confirmed against the live FEMA NFHL for the Bain reach (29.7701, ‚àí95.8502): the AE zone's `STATIC_BFE` IS ‚àí9999, but the BFE lives on the SEPARATE **S_BFE line layer (NFHL sublayer 16)** ‚Äî 24 whole-foot contour lines (133‚Äì146 ft, all Feet/NAVD88) cross the site ‚Äî which the app never fetched. `assessHydraulicRegime`/`computeMitigation` were correct to read UNKNOWN; the gap was a missing data source, not a bug.
- **Fix (new `bfe-line-interp` provider, slotted after manual, before UNKNOWN):**
  ‚Ä¢ **GIS pipeline** (`vectorLayers.js`) ‚Äî `featuresToGeoJson` now emits LineString/MultiLineString from Esri `geometry.paths` (it silently dropped every line feature before ‚Äî the fabricated-empty-result trap); `simplifyGeoJson` passes non-polygons through unthinned (thinning would shift a line off the query point); new `bfeLines` (sublayer 16) + `crossSections` (14, registered-not-consumed) sources.
  ‚Ä¢ **Derivation** (`floodplainMitigation.js`, pure) ‚Äî `bfeLinesFromFeatureCollection` (feet+NAVD88 guard; ‚àí9999/NGVD29/meters excluded and counted ‚Äî never a mixed-datum silent error) + `deriveBfeFromLines` (distance-weighted interpolation between the nearest line and the nearest DIFFERENT-elevation line ‚Äî "read a spot elevation between two topo contours"; degrades to nearest-line ¬±0.5 ft, then to null ‚Üí the existing honest UNKNOWN when no line is within 2500 ft).
  ‚Ä¢ **Wiring** (`SitePlanner.jsx`) ‚Äî `checkDrainage` pulls S_BFE on the same explicit click + SWR cache and derives one BFE at the fill-footprint centroid; threaded through `fmElev.derivedBfeFt`, `fmGoverningBfe` (buildability/FFE), and the mit/pond memo sigs. It NEVER writes to `settings.floodMitigation.bfeFt` (would fake a manual entry); a published static BFE, an AO zone's grade+DEPTH, and a manual entry all still outrank it.
  ‚Ä¢ **Honest labeling (owner option A)** ‚Äî priced off the central estimate with the conservative upper bracket shown alongside; Yield row tagged "BFE derived", FloodMitigationCard provider "derived (BFE lines)" + a note stating the interpolation, bracket, and "screening estimate ‚Äî confirm before design; type a BFE to override"; BFE input placeholder shows "~x derived"; print sheet appends "(derived BFE ‚Äî screening est.)".
- **Named rules:** LOUD-FAILURE (the polyline-drop trap fixed; a failed/empty/out-of-range pull ‚Üí honest UNKNOWN, never a fabricated number), AUDIT-FIRST (reconciled against the existing STATIC_BFE path ‚Äî the ‚àí9999 was real), PDF-PARITY (export mirrors the derived tag). Docs: `MAP.md` regenerated same commit (new exports).
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/floodplainMitigation.test.js` +24 (distToPolyline, the deriveBfeFromLines edge matrix, parser datum/unit guards, provider precedence ‚Äî manual/static/AO all beat derived, derived-null ‚Üí unchanged UNKNOWN) and `test/vectorLayers.test.js` +4 (paths‚Üíline geometry, non-polygon pass-through, the two source rows); full suite **3338 green**, build green, lint 0 errors, headless boot clean.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open/‚è≥/Done for BFE¬∑base flood elevation¬∑S_BFE¬∑derive. Not a recurrence ‚Äî the B707 regime/mitigation engine + its "no published BFE" honesty are the base this EXTENDS (it fills exactly that gap); cross-sections (WSEL_REG) and HCFCD MAAPnext are deliberately deferred (v2; the S_XS source is registered, not consumed).
- **üêû Live-verify bug found + FIXED (round-3 live verification, 2026-07-18):** on the real Bain plan, the "BFE (1% WSE)" field stayed empty and "Set BFE ‚Üí" only scrolled/focused it ‚Äî no derived value ever appeared, despite FEMA genuinely publishing 24 usable S_BFE lines a short distance from the Bain reach (confirmed live via curl at the time of the original B755 ship). **Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST):** `deriveBfeFromLines`/`governingCrossSectionWsel` will defend a contour up to 2500 ft away (two-line interpolation up to 6000 ft combined) ‚Äî a deliberate design choice ‚Äî but `SitePlanner.jsx` fetched the S_BFE lines / cross-sections over the SAME tightly-padded `fmBbox` used for the zone polygon + DEM grid (`floodGeoBbox(..., 0.001)` ‚âà 350-450 ft pad), which almost never reaches that far. So on any site where the nearest usable contour sits farther than the immediate parcel/fill edge (the common case ‚Äî S_BFE lines run along the creek/floodplain corridor, not necessarily the buildable pad), the fetch silently returned zero lines and the field stayed "no published BFE," even though the derivation math itself was correct and live-tested against real FEMA data. The zone POLYGON fetch (V257, large-area feature) never showed this gap because a floodplain polygon is far more likely to intersect a small bbox than a thin contour line is. **Fix:** widen JUST the BFE-lines/cross-sections fetch to a second bbox (`bfeSearchBbox`, `BFE_SEARCH_PAD_DEG = 0.025` ‚âà 1.7 mi pad) sized to the derivation engine's own search radius; the zone-polygon fetch and the site DEM-grid fetch are unchanged (kept tight on purpose ‚Äî an oversized elevation pull is wasted bytes, and zone polygons don't need the extra reach). New cache key (the wider bbox coordinates) means no stale-cache carryover from the old narrow pull.
- **Verified (sandbox):** new `floodplainMitigation.test.js` case proving a line ~2000 ft from the site (within the engine's 2500 ft ceiling) falls OUTSIDE the old tight bbox but INSIDE the widened one; new `bugHuntGuards.test.js` source guard pinning the wiring (BFE-lines/cross-sections fetch keyed to the wider bbox, zone/DEM fetch unchanged). Full suite (4527) + lint (0 errors) + build green.
- **üêû Round 1 fix INSUFFICIENT ‚Äî live re-test still failed (2026-07-18); root cause #2 found + FIXED same day.** Michael re-tested the real Bain plan (production, project `smr9olizi5ue`, Concept A, hard reload) after round 1 shipped ‚Äî the field was STILL empty and "Set BFE ‚Üí" still did nothing. **Confirmed the round-1 fix WAS deployed** (diffed the live minified bundle at `planyr.io` byte-for-byte against a local build off the merged commit ‚Äî the widened-bbox call site matched exactly, ruling out a stale/undeployed build). **AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî the fetch was no longer the bottleneck; the ENGINE'S OWN acceptance radius was.** Queried the live FEMA NFHL S_BFE layer directly at the Bain reach's reference point (29.7701, ‚àí95.8502): the nearest usable line measures **~2980 ft** away ‚Äî comfortably inside round 1's widened fetch, but just past `deriveBfeFromLines`'/`governingCrossSectionWsel`'s OLD `maxLineDistFt`/`maxDistFt` = 2500 ft cap, so the engine kept returning null even once the data was successfully fetched. Widening the fetch alone doesn't help if the pure derivation math still rejects a line that far out. **Fix (round 2):** raised the engine's own radius ‚Äî `maxLineDistFt` 2500‚Üí5000 ft, `maxGapFt` 6000‚Üí10000 ft (`deriveBfeFromLines`), `maxDistFt` 2500‚Üí5000 ft (`governingCrossSectionWsel`) ‚Äî defensible because BFE contours change slowly over distance on the flat Gulf-Coast reaches this tool screens (a few tenths of a foot per thousand feet is typical), so a wider "close enough to defend" radius is still a legitimate screening estimate, not a guess; the real, measured Bain distance (2980 ft) is now comfortably inside the new bounds. Bumped `BFE_SEARCH_PAD_DEG` (`SitePlanner.jsx`) 0.025‚Üí0.04 (‚âà2.8 mi) in step so the fetch stays ahead of the new engine ceiling.
- **Verified (sandbox):** new `floodplainMitigation.test.js` case reproducing the EXACT measured Bain distance (2980 ft) ‚Äî proves the old default (2500 ft) rejects it and the raised default accepts it with the right elevation; `bugHuntGuards.test.js` source guard updated to the new `0.04` pad. Full suite (4528) + lint (0 errors) + build green. Confirmed via direct live FEMA-NFHL query (not just unit tests) that 2980 ft is the real measured gap this fix must clear.
- **üêû Round 2 fix ALSO insufficient ‚Äî real root cause #3 found via direct DB inspection (2026-07-18).** Michael re-tested again (Bain, production, three successive hard-reloads confirming three different deployed bundle hashes ‚Äî genuinely fresh code each time) and captured network + console output around the "Set BFE ‚Üí" click: **zero requests, zero console output.** That observation is the key: **"Set BFE ‚Üí" (`jumpToDrainField`) was NEVER meant to compute anything ‚Äî it only opens the mitigation group and scroll/focuses the field (`setDrainGroupOpen`/`setDrainEditField`/`scrollIntoView`/`.focus()`).** All three "fixes" so far only touched the ACTUAL derivation, which runs during `checkDrainage()` (behind "‚õÜ Check drainage criteria" / "‚Üª Re-check") ‚Äî a button click that does nothing observable is expected FOR THAT BUTTON, not evidence the derivation is still broken. **Queried the real Bain site directly via Supabase MCP (project `lyeqzkuiwngunutlkkmi`, site `smr9olizi5ue`, "Concept A"):** `updated_at` = 2026-07-17 21:54 ‚Äî a full day BEFORE any of these three fixes shipped ‚Äî and its persisted `settings.drainage.lastCheck.mitigation.summary.providers.wse1pct` = **`"xs-wsel"`** (the cross-section-derived 1% WSE, **138.4 ft**, already priced into a real 25.12 ac-ft mitigation volume) ‚Äî proving the CROSS-SECTION derivation path already worked, even under the pre-fix code, for this exact site. **The real bug (distinct from rounds 1-2, both of which were genuine but non-blocking for THIS site): the "BFE (1% WSE)" input's auto-value ternary (`SitePlanner.jsx`, the `autoField("BFE (1% WSE)", "bfeFt", ...)` call) ONLY ever read `fm.derivedBfe` (BFE-line interp) and `fm.derivedWse100` (FBCDD Atlas-14 DRAFT raster) ‚Äî it never received or checked `derivedXsWsel` (the cross-section estimate) AT ALL, even though that's a real, already-computed, already-priced provider that OUTRANKS both of the others in the engine's own precedence** (`zoneWaterSurface`: derivedXsWsel ‚Üí derivedBfe ‚Üí derived1pct). So the mitigation ACREAGE priced correctly (silently, under the hood) while the discrete BFE INPUT FIELD stayed permanently blank for this site, regardless of anything rounds 1-2 fixed. **Fix (round 3):** thread `derivedXsWsel: fmDerivedXsWsel` into the `floodMit` props object, and rewrite the field's auto-value/label ternary to check `derivedXsWsel` FIRST (matching the engine's real precedence), using the existing `wseProvLabel("xs-wsel")` ‚Üí "derived (cross-sections)" label (already defined in `WSE_PROVIDER_LABEL`, just never wired to this one consumer).
- **‚öÝ ALSO ‚Äî an operational gap that compounded all three rounds, not a code bug:** `hydrateDrainageContext` (`detentionRules.js`) deliberately drops `floodGeo` (and therefore `derivedXsWsel`/`derivedBfe`/`bfeLineFlags`) on a REMEMBERED/rehydrated load ‚Äî "Geometry-dependent extras... stay absent... the render path null-guards both" (by design, since raw geometry isn't persisted). Only the priced `mitigation.summary` survives a reload; the discrete BFE input's live derived-value objects do NOT. **So a plain page reload (even a hard reload that busts the JS bundle cache) will NEVER show any of these three fixes taking effect ‚Äî only clicking "‚Üª Re-check" (or "‚õÜ Check drainage criteria" if no prior check exists) re-runs `checkDrainage()` and rebuilds `floodGeo` fresh.** This means Michael's prior three test passes may never have actually exercised the fixed code paths at all if he reloaded-and-looked rather than re-checked. Flagging this loudly so the next live pass uses the right click sequence.
- **Verified (sandbox):** new `bugHuntGuards.test.js` guard pinning the `derivedXsWsel` prop threading + the ternary's precedence order (cross-section checked before BFE-line before FBCDD DRAFT). Full suite (4532) + lint (0 errors) + build green.
- **‚úÖ LIVE CONFIRMED (owner, 2026-07-18) ‚Äî CLOSED.** Michael hard-reloaded, opened Concept A ‚Üí Yield ‚Üí Stormwater, clicked **"‚Üª Re-check"** ‚Äî the check ran live ("As of 4:35 PM ¬∑ live check ¬∑ manual ¬∑ flood data just now"). The **"BFE (1% WSE)"** field now shows **~142.8‚Ä≤ ¬∑ derived (cross-sections)**, tagged ESTIMATE; the **"0.2% (500-yr) WSE"** field shows **~139.0‚Ä≤ ¬∑ DRAFT (FBCDD)**, also tagged ESTIMATE; Buildability/FFE picked up a real required value (144.80‚Ä≤, ASSUMED badge) where it was previously blank. (The live number, 142.8‚Ä≤, differs slightly from the 138.4‚Ä≤ read off the stale 2026-07-17 remembered snapshot during investigation ‚Äî expected, since a live re-check samples fresh data; same provider/format, confirming the fix genuinely computes rather than replaying a stale figure.) All three rounds verified working end-to-end on real production data.

### B898 ‚Äî Map Layers panel redesign: decision-first grouping + function-first naming + consolidated Water & sewer / Electric / Fire hydrants `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) #site-planner #gis #ui  *(owner brief 2026-07-18, chat "redesign the map LAYERS panel" ‚Äî the panel was organized by data PROVIDER (a "Harris County ¬∑ City of Houston" group sat above generic groups even in Waller/Katy), water/sewer scattered across 3 groups, fire hydrants √ó3 and electric √ó3 by source, provider names leaking into labels. Minted **B898** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main` (fresh main folded in ‚Äî see B899 for why the FIRST mint attempt collided).)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + unit + headless-verified this session.** Reorganized `lib/layers.js` (+ the flattened `AHJ_LAYERS`/`JURISDICTIONS`/`EVIDENCE`/`STATEWIDE` configs) into a data-driven registry: every layer now carries a `group` + `order` field, and `LAYER_GROUP_ORDER`/`LAYER_GROUP_LABEL` define the canonical decision-first sequence ‚Äî **1) Base & terrain ¬∑ 2) Flood & drainage ¬∑ 3) Utilities serving the site ¬∑ 4) Environmental & hazards ¬∑ 5) Access & infrastructure ¬∑ 6) Jurisdictions & authority** (deal-killer first, reference last) ‚Äî replacing the old provider-ordered groups (Basemap ‚Üí per-county Harris group ‚Üí Jurisdictions ‚Üí Utility evidence ‚Üí mislabeled "Environmental & hazards" catch-all). `LayerPanel.jsx` is now purely data-driven off these fields (`groupEntries`/`groupRows`) instead of hard-coding which JS object renders where.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: owner brief 2026-07-18 from chat
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open/‚è≥ Verify/Done for `#gis`/`#site-planner` + "layers panel"/"water sewer"/"consolidat" ‚Äî net-new; builds on but doesn't recur B760/B761/B762 (the ‚ìò de-text + City/ETJ merge + county-fold mechanics, all left untouched and still guarded by `test/layerPanelV2Guards.test.js`).
- **The THREE consolidations (a new `cfg.mergeGroup` mechanism, N-ary generalization of the existing pairwise `mergeWith`/`mergeLabel` City-limits-&-ETJ merge, which is untouched):** one panel row now drives several source adapters, each adapter's fetch/URL/kind untouched.
  - **Water & sewer** ‚Äî merges `ccn_service` (PUC CCN, via Harris County GIS), `jur_mud` (TCEQ water districts, via HARC ‚Äî moved out of the old "Jurisdictions" boundary group), `coh_water` + `coh_ww` (City of Houston mains, moved out of the old per-county `JURISDICTION_LAYERS.harris.layers` nesting into a new flat `AHJ_LAYERS` registry, same `county` tag convention as the existing `EVIDENCE.coh_hydrants`). Shows mains AND service territory (who's entitled to serve) together.
  - **Electric (lines, substations & poles)** ‚Äî merges `osm_power` (OSM power lines/poles), `hifld_tx` (HIFLD transmission), `hifld_substations` (HIFLD substations).
  - **Fire hydrants** ‚Äî merges `osm_hydrants` (OSM), `coh_hydrants` (City of Houston Public Works), `mapillary` (crowdsourced street-imagery detections, which also surface nearby poles ‚Äî noted honestly in its own ‚ìò line). Point de-duplication across sources is a named follow-up (`coh_hydrants` is a server-rendered raster export today, not a queryable vector layer, so there's no client-side geometry to de-dupe against yet) ‚Äî flagged in the `MERGE_GROUPS.fire_hydrants` note, not silently dropped.
- **‚õî INCLUSIVE BY DESIGN, never AHJ-exclusive (owner correction mid-session, chat NEW-2):** every merge-group member toggles TOGETHER and renders whatever its own source returns for the current view ‚Äî nothing filters by the parcel's jurisdiction. A site near a boundary or in an ETJ can be served by a neighboring provider (CCN extension, wholesale supply, a nearby main), so hiding a provider based on "which AHJ contains the parcel" would hide a real option. The existing coverage/Relevance system (`coverage.js`, buffered ~2.5 mi default, defaults to "Dim" not "Hide") is the only dimming mechanism ‚Äî it was already inclusive-by-default, not changed. The merged row's ‚ìò lists every contributing provider by name (`mergeGroupInfoSections`) so a user can tell whose main/territory each feature is.
- **Function-first naming:** provider names (OSM/HIFLD/TxRRC/COH/HCFCD/TCEQ) moved out of visible labels into each config's `source` field, surfaced only in the per-row ‚ìò as a "Source: ‚Ä¶" line (reusing the B895 Yield-panel provenance pattern) ‚Äî e.g. "Wetlands (NWI)" ‚Üí "Wetlands" + `source: "National Wetlands Inventory (USFWS)"`; "HCFCD channels & ROW" ‚Üí "Drainage channels & ROW"; "Houston storm sewer" ‚Üí "Storm sewer" (both moved from the old `JURISDICTION_LAYERS.harris.layers` nesting into `AHJ_LAYERS`, now always listed in Flood & drainage rather than gated behind "is Harris the current county" ‚Äî auto-scoped, no hard-coded "Houston" label anywhere visible).
- **No provider-specific top-level group:** `JURISDICTION_LAYERS.harris.layers` is now empty (its 4 layers moved into `AHJ_LAYERS`) ‚Äî the old "Harris County ¬∑ City of Houston" heading that used to sit ABOVE the generic groups no longer renders for any county. The existing single-layer county fold (Fort Bend contours ‚Üí Basemap) and the ‚â•2-layer-county-gets-its-own-group mechanism are untouched (currently dead for every county, kept for a future county that publishes several layers of its own).
- **Named rules:** LOUD-FAILURE (the fire-hydrant de-dup gap is called out explicitly, not silently dropped), DEDUPE-FIRST, MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS (all new render logic ‚Äî `mergeGroupRow` ‚Äî defined at module scope inside `LayerPanel`, matching the existing `compositeRow`/`row` pattern, no new inner-component).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** full unit suite green (**4560 tests**, incl. 21 new `test/layerConsolidation.test.js` cases pinning the acceptance criteria ‚Äî group order, one Water & sewer/Electric/Fire-hydrants layer each, no provider-named labels survive, every merge member keeps its fetch ‚Äî plus new `buildGroupSlots`/`mergeSlotAnyOn`/`mergeSlotOpacity`/`mergeGroupInfoSections` pure-helper tests in `test/layerPanelInfo.test.js`); lint 0 errors; build green; `test/layerPanelV2Guards.test.js` (the B760‚ÄìB762 anti-drift guards) still green unchanged. **Headless Playwright** (`ui-audit/layerpanel-verify.mjs` against a live `vite` dev server, real Chromium): 50/50 checks PASS, including new B898 assertions (all six decision-first group headers present in order; no "Harris County ¬∑ City of Houston" heading; ONE "Water & sewer" row whose ‚ìò lists the CCN/MUD/City-of-Houston provenance; ONE "Electric" row; "Fire hydrants" present once; old provider-named rows gone) plus every pre-existing B760‚ÄìB764 assertion (merged City/ETJ toggle, Fort Bend fold, ISD ‚ìò, jurisdiction badges) ‚Äî screenshot-confirmed visually. `gis-verify/jurisdictions-overlay-verify.mjs` (a live-GIS-only manual script, not run in this sandbox) updated to match the new "Water & sewer"/"Jurisdictions & authority" labels.
- **Guardrails honored:** same data, no fetch/URL/kind weakened on any of the 10 consolidated adapters; on/off/opacity/Relevance/screening-footer semantics preserved; nothing hard-codes "Houston" as a visible label (COH is now just one of several sources a merge group MAY include).

### B899 ‚Äî `next-id.mjs --against-main` silently degraded to a stale local-only max on BACKLOG-DONE.md (1.4 MB+, past the default 1 MB execSync buffer) ‚Üí caused a real B896 mint collision `[Tooling]` (bug) #infra  *(self-discovered 2026-07-18 mid-session while late-binding B898's real number for the Layers-panel redesign (B898 chat brief): `npm run next-id -- --against-main` reported "highest assigned: B895" (stale) right after a concurrent session had already shipped B896/B897 to `main` for an unrelated feature (numeric-typeface unification) ‚Äî this session's code/tests/comments had already been written under the provisional label **B896**, matching the just-taken number exactly. Caught before push via a manual `git show origin/main:BACKLOG-DONE.md` cross-check; renumbered this session's work to **B898** before shipping.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + unit-verified this session.** `readOriginMain` (`scripts/next-id.mjs`) calls `execSync("git show origin/main:${file}", ‚Ä¶)` with NO explicit `maxBuffer` ‚Äî Node's default is 1 MB, and `BACKLOG-DONE.md` is 1.7 MB and only grows (write-only archive). The call threw `ENOBUFS`, which the `catch { return null; }` swallowed with zero visible signal, silently degrading `--against-main` to the LOCAL-ONLY max (exactly as if `--against-main` had never been passed) ‚Äî the one failure mode this flag exists to prevent, and a LOUD-FAILURE violation in the shared tooling every session's late-bind mint depends on.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: self-discovered mid-session, 2026-07-18
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open/‚è≥ Verify/Done for `#infra` + "next-id"/"ENOBUFS"/"maxBuffer" ‚Äî net-new; B755/B779/B780 built `next-id.mjs` and its collision guards but never hit this because BACKLOG-DONE.md crossed 1 MB only recently.
- **Fix:** `readOriginMain` now passes an explicit `maxBuffer: 32 * 1024 * 1024` (32 MB ‚Äî comfortably ahead of the archive's growth for a long while) to `execSync`, and its catch block now writes a visible `‚öÝ next-id: couldn't read origin/main:‚Ä¶` warning to stderr for any REAL read failure (vs. the expected "no origin/main configured" case, which stays silent) ‚Äî so a future failure mode is loud, not silently-wrong.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `npm run next-id -- --against-main` now correctly reports **B898** (not the stale B896) against the real fetched `origin/main`. New `test/nextId.test.js` regression coverage: `readOriginMain` reads the real 1.7 MB `BACKLOG-DONE.md` from `origin/main` in full (skips gracefully if no `origin/main` ref is fetched, e.g. an isolated sandbox ‚Äî never a false CI failure) and a second test asserts the configured `maxBuffer` literal is comfortably larger than the file's actual on-disk size (so the guard can't silently rot as the archive grows past today's number). Full unit suite green (4560, incl. the 2 new tests), lint 0 errors.

### B739 ‚Äî GIS raster/shaded overlay layers (FEMA floodplain, TxRRC pipelines, wetlands, utilities, ground relief) now composite into the PDF/PNG export `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ export]` (bug) #site-planner #export #gis  *(surfaced 2026-07-09 by the B738 adversarial review; provisional **B736**, renumbered **B739**. Owner asked 2026-07-10 for "everything I have on" to print ‚Äî the RASTER/shaded class ships here; the vector/thin-line class is split to **B745**. ‚Üí V257.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî PDF/export-parity + GIS-endpoint classes; the sandbox can't reach the live agency hosts through the cert proxy, so the actual imagery-paint needs a live click-through).** The shared GIS overlay layers render only on the Leaflet basemap `<div>` (a `data-export="skip"` sibling of the exported `<svg>`), so `buildExportSvg` ‚Äî which clones only the SVG ‚Äî had NEVER included them; after B738 added the aerial the sheet *looked* complete while silently dropping a floodplain/pipeline overlay the user turned on ‚Äî a bad omission on a regulatory drawing. Now, mirroring the B738 aerial synthesis, each ENABLED raster layer (`kind` absent/`dynamic`/`esriImage`) is re-fetched as a frame-exact TRANSPARENT `/export` PNG for the print frame and composited above the aerial, below the drawn geometry, in the on-screen paint order at its layer opacity.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (round-3 live verification, Michael's signed-in Chrome, planyr.io): **V257 PASSED live ‚Äî the FEMA floodplain hatched polygon + "Zone AE" label rendered correctly, aligned to the aerial/parcels.** ‚Üí VERIFICATION-DONE.md V257.
- Verify: live
- Origin: surfaced 2026-07-09 by the B738 4-lens review (completeness lens); owner-confirmed scope 2026-07-10.
- **Implementation:** pure builders `overlayExportRequest` (`lib/layerRequest.js` ‚Äî derives the sublayers / renderingRule from the SAME `dynamicLayerOptions`/`imageLayerOptions` the live layer uses, so print can't drift ‚Äî PDF-PARITY) + `overlayExportPlacement` (`lib/arcgis.js` ‚Äî transparent png32, shares the extracted `aerialGeom` so overlays pixel-align to the aerial + parcels). `exportOverlaysForFrame` (`SitePlanner.jsx`) enumerates active raster layers in `ALL_LAYERS` order (= z-order), skipping `failed` hosts; `buildExportSvg` composites the `<image>`s (tagged `data-export-overlay` + `data-fallback-href`); `inlineImages` fetches proxy-first with a direct-agency CORS fallback and returns `overlaysDropped[]`; a **batched LOUD-FAILURE** banner names any layer whose imagery couldn't be fetched (never a silent omission). New "Print map layers" checkbox (default ON) in the print toolbar, shown only when a raster layer is on. `gisProxyEnabled` exported from `layers.js`.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** sibling of **B738** (aerial imagery) + **B745** (the vector/thin-line class, split out and still Open). Reuses B738's `feetExtentToBbox` / `inlineImages` / `data-export-*` machinery.
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** 7 new unit tests (`test/overlayExport.test.js` ‚Äî overlay placement pixel-aligns with the aerial; dynamic URL carries `layers=show:‚Ä¶` + png32 + transparent; MUD `layers:null` ‚Üí no `layers=`; esriImage ‚Üí `/exportImage` with the exact renderingRule; proxy-swap keeps the direct fallback). `ui-audit/verify-overlay-print.mjs` (logged-out, built app on :4173): FEMA + pipelines on ‚Üí the print toolbar shows "Print map layers" ‚Üí Download PDF composites **2 `image[data-export-overlay]` nodes** into the export SVG (the node is created before the fetch, so this proves the wiring independent of live-host reachability). Full unit suite green (3229) ¬∑ MAP.md regen ¬∑ build green.

### B754 ‚Äî Detention: a City-of-Houston **ETJ** parcel was wrongly treated as City-of-Houston for detention criteria `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ yield]` (bug) #site-planner #gis #yield  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-10 on the Bain/Concept A readout ‚Äî "Why does it think this site goes to the city of Houston? It's not in the city of Houston ‚Ä¶ it's just not the city of Houston," then the domain correction: "it IS city of Houston ETJ, but in the city of Houston ETJ, that doesn't mean that city of Houston drainage criteria apply." Minted this session as **B752**; RENUMBERED to **B754** = highest B# across both files (B753) + 1 on merge-in of `origin/main`, which took B752/B753 + V265/V266 for the pipeline vector-rendering feature ‚Äî the code comments/tests/commit keep the provisional **B752** label. Follows B751, which introduced the "detected from city-limits GIS" wording that exposed this latent mis-routing.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session ‚Äî sandbox+headless verified (unit tests + a new headless spec `verify-etj-detention.mjs`, all green); the live-GIS confirm on the real 27211 Hoyt Ln (Katy) site on the SIGNED-IN app parks in ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V267.** Being inside a city's ETJ means the city reviews the PLAT, but the COUNTY drainage district's criteria GOVERN detention ‚Äî it must NOT set the detention reviewer to that city.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (round-3 live verification, Michael's signed-in Chrome, planyr.io): **V267 PASSED live ‚Äî navigated to the repro coordinates (29.7722, -95.8548), badge text and detention-authority resolution to Fort Bend County matched the curl-probe prediction exactly, clean exact match.** ‚Üí VERIFICATION-DONE.md V267.
- Verify: live
- **AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî verified the repro against the LIVE GIS via curl at the parcel's coordinates (29.7722, -95.8548):** city-limits layer (TxGIO) returns **empty** (NOT in any city), the H-GAC ETJ layer returns **HOUSTON**, the county layer returns **Fort Bend**. So the parcel is unincorporated Fort Bend land inside Houston's ETJ. The bug: `authorityForJurisdiction` (`lib/detentionRules.js`) routed `cities.includes("houston") || etjs.includes("houston")` ‚Üí `primary="coh"`, so an ETJ-only hit set the reviewer to City of Houston, and the UI hardcoded "(detected from city-limits GIS)" ‚Äî doubly wrong (it was an ETJ hit, and the city-limits layer says nothing there). Root cause predates B751 (lived in B93/B629); B751's new wording surfaced it.
- **Fix:** ETJ membership WITHOUT actual city-limits membership no longer sets `primary` to `coh`. The COUNTY drainage authority governs (Fort Bend ‚Üí FBCDD here), and the Houston-ETJ status is surfaced as an informational `overlays` note + `houston-etj` flag: *"This parcel is in the City of Houston ETJ ‚Ä¶, not its city limits. Houston reviews the plat, but the COUNTY drainage-district criteria govern detention here ‚Äî Houston's own detention rate does not apply. Verify with both the City and the county."* Rendered in the Yield/Stormwater readout (`d.etjNotes`). The genuine in-city-limits Houston path (`cities.includes("houston")`) is unchanged, so the >20-ac greater-of + "detected from city-limits GIS" wording now fire ONLY for real city-limits parcels. Removed the now-false `appliesInEtj:true` rule param + rewired the `jurisdiction-partial` flag (a failed ETJ query can no longer imply a hidden City answer; only a failed CITY-limits query can).
- **Named rules:** AUDIT-FIRST (reconciled the claim against the live endpoints before patching), LOUD-FAILURE (the ETJ note is explicit, never a silent re-label), MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS (inline JSX note, no inner component).
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/detentionResolver.test.js` updated (Houston-ETJ‚Üícounty authority + `houston-etj` flag; Fort-Bend-ETJ‚Üí`fortbend`; ETJ-outage no longer flags partial), full suite **3308** green, build green; new `ui-audit/verify-etj-detention.mjs` (Fort Bend + Houston-ETJ, no city limits) ‚Üí reviewer reads **Fort Bend County Drainage District**, never City of Houston, ETJ note present ‚Äî 5/5 pass; existing `verify-b629-detention.mjs` COH pass (real city-limits) still green.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open/‚è≥/Done for ETJ¬∑extraterritorial¬∑reviewing authority¬∑city of houston drainage. Distinct from B751 (transparency/override ‚Äî works) and B93 (jurisdiction identify). Net-new correctness bug.

### B740 ‚Äî Shift-click multi-select + shared property editing (opacity/style) `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #selection #ui #markup  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-09 as "NEW-1"; provisional **B734**, renumbered **B740** ‚Äî concurrent `main` took B734‚ÄìB739 + V250‚ÄìV252. `[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify only for one AUTH-ONLY cloud-persistence check (Verify: sandbox for everything else) ‚Üí V253.** Shift-click (Windows-first additive modifier) TOGGLES an element in/out of the selection; a shared Properties panel edits opacity/color/style for the WHOLE selection at once, with a "Mixed" indicator where they disagree. Immediate driver: opacity was lowered on a building + truck courts + trailer parking with no way to raise them all back ‚Äî now one opacity slider restores them together.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (Cowork live-verification session, signed-in on planyr.io): **V253 PASSED live ‚Äî Shift-selected 2 parcels, the "2 SELECTED" panel showed shared Outline/Line-weight/Dash only, changed the shared line-weight, reloaded, both parcels retained it.** ‚Üí VERIFICATION-DONE.md V253.

B569 made Shift *add-only*; B740 makes Shift *toggle* (add AND remove) and adds the missing multi-element property panel + per-member visuals. - **Selection:** `startMoveEl`/`startMoveMarkup` use `hasSelMod` ‚Üí toggle; `sel` repoints to a surviving member (or null) after a remove; a bump-out (dog-ear) enters the set as its host building. Empty-space click still clears; marquee box-select (which leaves `sel` null) now opens the shared panel too.
- **Panel:** new pure `lib/multiStyle.js` (`styleCapsOf` / `commonStyleState` / `selectionRingFeet`, 20 unit tests). The inline panel shows only the COMMON editable props (opacity/fill/outline for elements; +weight/dash where all are markups), each uniform-value-or-"Mixed" (native color input overlaid under a hatched "Mixed" swatch ‚Äî no dialog, inline-editor rule); `companionSel` widened so a sel=null marquee still opens it; single-element sections guarded off while multi>1.
- **Write:** `applyMultiElPatch`/`applyMultiMarkupPatch` fan one patch across the selection (host-id `Set` dedup so a building selected directly + via a bump-out is patched once) in a single setEls/setMarkups ‚Üí one batched cloud commit + local mirror read-back verify (optimistic UI + LOUD-FAILURE inherited). Color/opacity drags = one undo frame.
- **Visuals:** per-member rotation-aware OBB outlines (reuse `elRingFeet`/`ptsOf`) that hug an angled strip's real footprint, wrapped in `data-export="skip"` (also closes a latent export-leak of the old bare multi chrome); single-element transform grips suppressed while multi>1 (property editing only ‚Äî no group transform handles).

### B734 ‚Äî Account dropdown opens pinned to the top-left corner instead of under the account pill `[App Shell / UI]` (bug) #ui #infra  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-09 as "NEW-1"; **B734** = highest B# across both files (B733) + 1. Branch `claude/account-menu-positioning-frp60c`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + sandbox/headless-verified this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî the account dropdown is signed-in-only and the sandbox can't sign in) ‚Üí V250.** Clicking the signed-in account pill ("Michael Butler" + avatar) at the header's top-right opened its dropdown (Profile / Team / Settings / Sign out) pinned to the **top-left corner** of the viewport; it now drops straight down under the pill, right-aligned, staying inside the screen edge.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (Cowork live-verification session, signed-in on planyr.io): **V250 PASSED live ‚Äî verified on BOTH the Schedule and Site tabs, the dropdown consistently anchors under the pill, right-aligned, fully on-screen, across module switches.** ‚Üí VERIFICATION-DONE.md V250.
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-09 from chat
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open / ‚è≥ Verify / Done (BACKLOG*.md + BACKLOG_OPEN.md) ‚Äî **net-new**, no existing item covers the account/user/profile dropdown position. Nearest prior art to BUILD ON, NOT a dupe: **B127** (Done ‚Äî built `src/shared/ui/AnchoredMenu.jsx`, the portal-to-body rect-anchored menu the account dropdown already uses; that bug was menus *clipped/behind* the rail, a different symptom). Account-name/identity-pill work (B297/B298) is the pill itself, not its menu position.
- **AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî the report's guess was wrong.** The report suspected a missing `position`/lost `position:relative`. Neither: `AnchoredMenu`'s math and the account menu's `placement="below-right"` were already correct. **Real cause:** Shell built ONE `authControl` element (with a single `acctAnchor` ref + `<AnchoredMenu>`) and the keep-alive render (2026-07-05) passes that SAME element into **every visited workspace's `AppHeader`** ‚Äî up to 5 mount at once (Site map header + Site plan header + DocReview + Library + Scheduler), most `display:none`. All the button copies shared one `acctAnchor`, so `acctAnchor.current` resolved to whichever copy React committed last ‚Äî usually a hidden one. `getBoundingClientRect()` on a `display:none` element is all-zeros, so `below-right` (`left = a.right - mw`) went negative ‚Üí clamped to (8px, 8px) = top-left. Explains why it was fine on a fresh load but broke after switching workspaces/modes once. Every OTHER `AnchoredMenu` consumer already creates its anchor ref *locally inside its own component* (e.g. `SettingsMenu`, `ProjectBreadcrumb`) ‚Äî so it never had this bug.
- **Fix:** extracted the whole auth surface into a self-contained **`src/app/AccountControl.jsx`** (own `acctAnchor` ref + `acctOpen`/`cloudNote` state per instance). Passing the same `<AccountControl/>` element to multiple `{authControl}` slots creates an INDEPENDENT component instance (independent hooks) at each fiber position, so each mounted header anchors to its OWN visible button and only the clicked one opens ‚Äî the SettingsMenu/ProjectBreadcrumb pattern (MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS). Shell now just builds `<AccountControl user profileApi onOpenAuth onOpenAccount />` and keeps driving the `AuthPanel` modal; no workspace forwarder or `AppHeader` changed. Secondary class-wide hardening: lifted `AnchoredMenu`'s placement math into pure, unit-tested **`src/shared/ui/anchoredMenuPlacement.js` (`placeMenu`)**, which returns `null` for a zero-sized anchor so a mis-anchored menu stays *hidden* instead of pinning to the corner (all three placements ‚Äî `left`/`below-left`/`below-right` ‚Äî preserved, so no other consumer regresses).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** new `test/anchoredMenuPlacement.test.js` (8 cases ‚Äî below-right/below-left/left anchoring, right/bottom/left clamps, zero-rect ‚Üí null, missing ‚Üí null) ¬∑ B557 account-email null-guard test repointed to `AccountControl.jsx` (the guard rode with the code) ¬∑ full unit suite green (3147) ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP.md + shared folder-pointer regenerated. Headless logged-out smoke: app boots clean, no stray menu at the corner, and another `AnchoredMenu` consumer still positions correctly (the shared-`placeMenu` refactor didn't regress it).

### B719 ‚Äî Road curb/border drawn far too thick: size it to a true 6‚Ä≥ (0.5‚Ä≤) curb, to scale `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #road #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-08 as "NEW-2"; minted **B719** = highest B# across both files (B718) + 1. Branch `claude/road-editing-curb-scale-r1u2sw`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî zoom-/data-density-dependent rendering + PDF/print export parity, both mandatory-live classes) ‚Üí V248.** A road's edge/curb line rendered as a fat pixel band unrelated to real size; it now represents a true **6‚Ä≥ (0.5 US survey ft)** curb, sized `curb √ó ppf`, so it reads as a thin to-scale line at site zoom and grows proportionally on zoom-in.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V248 PASSED live 2026-07-18 (headless self-verify, real app) ‚Äî to-scale curb + PDF-PARITY confirmed empirically (curb ratio 2.00 on screen AND in the exported print sheet).**
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-08 from chat
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** B70/B71 define the curb *contract* (6‚Ä≥ default / 12‚Ä≥ trailer, three-way draw/calc/dimension rule); B180/B181 cover curb in the *cost/area* takeoff; B601 renders the centerline road surface+curb. None address the render *stroke weight* ‚Äî this is a pure visual-scale fix, no new number needed elsewhere.
- **Root cause:** the centerline road's pavement edge + curb stripes (and the legacy rect road's) were stroked with `strokeZoom(...)` ‚Äî a near-constant pixel weight (‚âà5‚Äì7px edge / ‚âà3px stripe at site zoom), clamped, with no tie to the 6‚Ä≥ curb. A real 6‚Ä≥ curb is `0.5 √ó ppf` px (sub-pixel at overview zoom), so the strokes read as a fat band many times the true curb.
- **Fix:** new pure `curbStrokePx(curbFt, ppf, minPx)` in `lib/roadGeometry.js` (floored so it stays visible sub-pixel, NO ceiling ‚Üí proportional on zoom-in). `SitePlanner.jsx` road render: the centerline pavement edge + curb stripes and the legacy rect road's border + stripes now use `curbStrokePx(roadCurbWidth(el), ppf, CURB_STROKE_MIN_PX=0.75)`. No new geometry / data ‚Äî purely edge styling sized to 6‚Ä≥ (a 12‚Ä≥ trailer curb renders exactly 2√ó via the same formula). Selection cue stays the blue handles (B619). Export parity holds by construction (`printSheet.js` clones the live SVG).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** `test/roadGeometry.test.js` `curbStrokePx` cases (to-scale above floor / floored sub-pixel / non-finite guard) ¬∑ `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` new B719 guard (roads use `curbStrokePx`, not `strokeZoom`) ¬∑ full suite green ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP regenerated. **`ui-audit/verify-b718-b719.mjs` (logged-out, real app): the 6‚Ä≥ curb stripe = 1.03px at fit (vs the old ~3px band) and a 12‚Ä≥ curb road = 2.06px ‚Äî ratio exactly 2.00, proving stroke = curb √ó ppf (to scale), not a fixed pixel weight; the road still renders as a filled, legible strip; 0 page errors.**
- **‚è≥ V248 (live, on planyr.io):** eyeball the thin to-scale curb on a real project at several zooms (and nudge the 0.75px floor up if it reads faint at overview zoom), and confirm the PDF/print export shows the same thin curb (PDF-PARITY ‚Äî the export clones the live SVG so parity is expected, but this is a mandatory-live class).
