### B715 ‚Äî Site acreage double-counts overlapping active parcels: DISSOLVE the rings so shared ground counts once (Martini 176.6 ‚Üí ~88.6 ac) `[Site Planner / Yield ¬∑ Analysis]` (bug) #site-planner #yield  *(owner/Cowork intake 2026-07-08, arrived as "NEW-1"; **B715** = highest B# across both files (B714) + 1. Owner chose "count the ground once" (union). Branch `claude/site-acreage-dedup-p9ksd4`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + sandbox-verified this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî repro cites real project data `smqsfzqc72pw`) ‚Üí V245.** Site acreage now DISSOLVES overlapping active parcels (counts shared ground once) instead of an additive shoelace sum.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V245 PASSED live 2026-07-18 (headless self-verify, real app) ‚Äî the dissolved acreage figure confirmed matching across the Yield panel, Site Analysis header, AND the map-finder site list; the B652 overlap banner + the non-overlapping fast path both confirmed correct.**
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-08 from chat
- **Root cause (grounded on `main`):** three additive sums, none union ‚Äî `SitePlanner.jsx:5608` `siteSqft`, `:11229` analysis header, `MapFinder.jsx:201` map tooltip/list (the last also summed EVERY parcel, no active filter). Martini (`smqsfzqc72pw`) read 176.55 ac; two attr-less hand-drawn outlines (38.76 + 49.19 ac) overlapped the real parcels ‚Üí 176.55 ‚àí 87.95 = 88.60 geometric.
- **Fix:** new pure `dissolvedParcelSqft(parcels[, overlapPairs])` in `lib/polyClip.js` ‚Äî reuses the already-bundled **`clipper-lib` `ClipType.ctUnion`** (same engine as `pondOffset.js`; feet‚Üícenti-feet integer grid). FAST PATH returns the exact additive sum when there are 0/1 valid rings or NO overlapping pair (`overlappingParcelPairs` empty ‚Äî adjacent lots share only an edge), so every normal, non-overlapping site is byte-identical to the old number (no rounding jitter); clipper runs only on genuine overlap. Wired into all three sites; the hot render path computes the overlap pairs ONCE and reuses them for both the area and the B652 warning. MapFinder now also filters to active parcels. B652 warning copy updated (area is now correct ‚Äî the warning flags the duplicate to review, no longer claims inflation).
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/polyClip.test.js` +9 tests (disjoint/adjacent ‚Üí exact sum; identical/partial/contained ‚Üí union; the Martini shape: two outlines over four tiles ‚Üí 10,000 not 30,000; inactive excluded; husk/degenerate rings skipped; precomputed-pairs contract) ¬∑ full suite **3,107 green** ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP regenerated.
- **‚è≥ V245 (live, signed-in):** open a site with overlapping active parcels on planyr.io and confirm the header/site acreage counts the shared ground once (Yield/Analysis + the map-list tooltip agree), and a normal non-overlapping site's acreage is unchanged. (Martini's own phantom rows were removed by B716, so it now reads ~88.6 from its 12 real parcels regardless ‚Äî this check confirms the union math on any still-overlapping site.)

### B703 ‚Äî Elevation layer painted Houston terrain as one flat green sheet ‚Äî replaced with view-relative DRA ground relief `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) #site-planner #gis  *(owner brief 2026-07-07, arrived as "NEW-1"; **B703** = B702 + 1. Branch `claude/elevation-houston-relief-7co7do`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + probe-measured + headless-verified this session; parks (Verify: live ‚Äî GIS endpoint behavior class) ‚Üí V239.**
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V239 PASSED live 2026-07-18 (headless self-verify against REAL USGS 3DEP data via the deployed gis-cache proxy) ‚Äî objective pixel-color-vs-real-elevation correlation (r=0.83) confirms low=blue/high=red on real terrain; panning fires a genuinely new exportImage request with DRA:true (re-stretch proven, not just unit-tested).**
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat
- **AUDIT-FIRST (brief vs code ‚Äî two stale claims):** the brief cited `EVIDENCE.elevation` and the rule string `"Elevation Tinted Hillshade"`; in reality B696 had moved the layer to `TERRAIN.elevation` and B603 had already fixed the string to the valid `"Hillshade Elevation Tinted"`. The B603 defect (never painted ‚Äî invalid template name) is DISTINCT from this one (paints fine, but the named template colors on a FIXED national ramp, so the whole Houston MSA ~0‚Äì150 ft lands in one green band and 3‚Äì8 ft of coastal-plain relief is invisible) ‚Üí new number; the B603 guard was re-pinned, not removed.
- **Measure-before-patch (mandated by the brief; the sandbox egress blocks the USGS host, so every probe ran through the production `/api/gis-cache` proxy ‚Äî nationalmap.gov allowlisted there since B518):** `allowRasterFunction: true` (ArcGIS 11.3; native SR **3857** ‚Äî the brief's "ground units are degrees" guess was wrong); rendered PNGs compared at neighborhood + ~350 m pad scale: the current named rule = the flat green sheet (bug reproduced); **`Colormap(blue‚Üícream‚Üíred) over Stretch(PercentClip 2/2, DRA:true)`** = detention ponds/ditches deep blue, freeway embankment red, re-stretching per view; 2/2 clip beat 0.5/0.5 (spread the midtones); a hillshade `Local`-multiply blend **washed out the tint** ‚Üí the shipped chain carries NO z-factor/hillshade term (measured worse than plain DRA tint).
- **What shipped:** `layerRequest.imageLayerOptions` passes an OBJECT `cfg.rendering` through verbatim as the whole `renderingRule` (string configs unchanged); `TERRAIN.elevation` carries the measured chain, plain-language label **"Ground relief (low = blue, high = red)"**, `source: USGS 3DEP`, and a note that colors are **RELATIVE TO THE CURRENT VIEW** (DRA re-shuffles on pan ‚Äî without the note that reads as a bug), NAVD88, screening-only. Layer id stays `elevation` (saved toggles + vintage row survive, per the B696 warning).
- **Verified here (sandbox):** new unit test (object passthrough) ¬∑ full suite green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless (`ui-audit/verify-terrain-layers.mjs`, real bytes via the planyr.io proxy from node): the exportImage request carries Colormap + DRA:true + PercentClip 2/2, the esri image paints, the relative-to-view note renders.
> **Dedup (DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî grepped elevation / hillshade / 3DEP / tint / relief across Open/‚è≥/Done):** B603 (Done) = invalid-name-never-painted; B533 (Done) = survey-foot conversion; B518 (Done) = proxy allowlist; B696 (Done) = panel regroup. None cover the fixed-national-ramp semantics. V189's old "elevation layer paints" check is superseded by V239 ‚Äî the rule it would have verified no longer exists.

### B704 ‚Äî 1-ft labeled contour lines, client-generated from the raw 3DEP DEM in a Web Worker `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) #site-planner #gis #perf  *(owner brief 2026-07-07, arrived as "NEW-2"; **B704** = B703 + 1. Branch `claude/elevation-houston-relief-7co7do`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks (Verify: live ‚Äî GIS endpoint + zoom-/data-density-dependent rendering classes) ‚Üí V240.**
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V240 PASSED live 2026-07-18 (headless self-verify against REAL USGS 3DEP data) ‚Äî contour-vs-cross-section agreement confirmed within 0.40 ft and contour-label-vs-hover within 0.50 ft, both on real Katy DEM data; the z16 zoom-gate honest note confirmed.**
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat
- **Why client-side:** the public service's canned contour renderings are raster-only, unlabeled, and (at best preset) 2-ft ‚Äî no labeled 1-ft vector lines exist server-side. The raw grid path was probe-verified first: `exportImage?format=lerc&pixelType=F32&renderingRule=None` returns LERC1 (`CntZImage`), decoded with the official `lerc` npm package; a real Katy tile is committed as `test/fixtures/dep-katy-463x400.lerc` (463√ó400 F32, 120.9‚Äì149.8 ft ‚Äî drives the decode-contract tests without network).
- **What shipped (new deps: `lerc` + `d3-contour`, both small ‚Äî the two the brief authorized):**
  - **Pure core** (`lib/demGrid.js`, `lib/contours.js`): deterministic snapped-tile grid requests (key ‚Üî bbox bijection; smoothing margin baked in; oversized viewports coarsen deterministically; `adjustAspectRatio=false` + size computed to match the bbox aspect exactly ‚Äî a silently adjusted bbox would shift every contour, and a size mismatch on decode REFUSES loudly); LERC magic-byte sniff (the dev server SPA-fallbacks `/api/*` to index.html ‚Äî "response arrived" ‚âÝ "response is a grid"); meters ‚Üí survey-ft on decode (`M_TO_FT` now exported from `elevation.js` ‚Äî one conversion for every 3DEP consumer); normalized MASKED gaussian smooth (voids never bleed; unsmoothed 1-ft contours from ¬±0.1‚Äì0.3 ft LiDAR noise are jagged spaghetti); marching squares via `d3-contour` with **sentinel-embedded voids** (never NaN ‚Äî d3's smoothing emits NaN coords and Leaflet throws) and **two strip passes** (grid-border frame + dilated-void halo, so lines BREAK at water instead of bridging or outlining it); pixel-space Douglas‚ÄìPeucker (the newly exported `vectorLayers.douglasPeucker`; degree-space tolerance is anisotropic on the ground); index flag every 5 ft; sparse labels capped ~30.
  - **The repo's first Web Worker** (`lib/terrainWorker.js`, Vite `?worker`): decode/smooth/trace off the main thread; import list pinned by `test/terrainWorker.test.js` (a Leaflet/DOM import would crash at worker RUNTIME, not build time); the raw grid transfers back for the B706 readout.
  - **Leaflet glue** (`lib/terrainLayers.js`): overpass-pattern view-driven layerGroup (moveend refresh, B56d busy/pending trailing edge, `gisCache.swr` last-good painting with data age) with the terrain specifics ‚Äî fetch on the MAIN thread (gisCache is localStorage-bound; proxy‚Üídirect one-shot fallback mirrors wireRaster), ONE deduped fetch+compute per tile shared with B705 and the readout, grids in a small LRU Map (a Float32Array must never ride `gisCache.write` ‚Äî it JSON.stringifies unconditionally), canvas-renderer polylines (`interactive:false`; hierarchy by WEIGHT per the salience rule), white-halo divIcon labels, restyle-in-place `setOpacity`, worker-crash lazy rebuild.
  - **Registry** (`lib/layers.js`): `TERRAIN.contours` ("Contour lines (1 ft)"), `kind: "contours"` dispatch, vintage row; both surfaces pick it up automatically. **Zoom-gated ‚â• 16** (honesty: at z15 a capped grid is ~6 m cells ‚Äî 1-ft contours would be mush).
  - **Calibration:** the ramp test pins d3-contour's smoothed output to the cell-center convention (measured: level 20 of an x-ramp lands at exactly px 20.5), so contours, the hover readout, and the cross-section tool can never disagree by half a cell. Elevations are NAVD88 orthometric ‚Äî the same vertical datum FEMA BFEs use (stated in the layer note; relevant to the future FFE-vs-BFE feature).
- **Verified here (sandbox):** 60+ new unit tests across the `demGrid`/`contours`/`terrainWorker` suites (calibration, cone closed-ring, void break-not-bridge with halo assertion, masked-smooth void-bleed, fixture decode contract, artifact under the 512 KB cache cap) ¬∑ full suite green ¬∑ build green (worker chunk emits self-contained) ¬∑ headless: ONE LERC fetch, 30 halo labels painted, real canvas ink, vintage + caveat lines render.
> **Dedup (DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî grepped contour / DEM / topo / LERC across Open/‚è≥/Done):** B691 (Open) = the Fort Bend COUNTY contour GIS layer (different source, CORS/proxy plumbing ‚Äî untouched); B497/B500 (Done) = pond stage contours (detention geometry, not terrain); B58/B23 (Done) = the getSamples profile path whose conventions this reuses. Client-generated terrain contours are net-new.

### B705 ‚Äî Drainage flow-direction arrows (which way the site sheets) `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) #site-planner #gis  *(owner brief 2026-07-07, arrived as "NEW-3"; **B705** = B704 + 1. Branch `claude/elevation-houston-relief-7co7do`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks (Verify: live ‚Äî zoom-/data-density class) ‚Üí V241.**
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V241 PASSED live 2026-07-18 (headless self-verify against REAL USGS 3DEP data) ‚Äî confirmed zero extra grid fetches + new canvas ink painted; independently re-decoded the real DEM grid to confirm the fetched tile has genuine flat AND steep terrain for the arrow/no-arrow claim to be meaningfully tested against.**
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat
- **What shipped:** `lib/flowField.js` (pure): downhill direction from a central-difference **gradient** over the harder-smoothed grid, windowed to the sample spacing ‚Äî raw per-cell D8 at sparse samples reads as random 8-quantized noise on near-flat Houston terrain, so the gradient drives the ARROWS while **classic D8 is kept alongside, pure, as the seed of the future storm-outfall flow-accumulation feature** (extend D8 ‚Üí accumulation to answer "which outfall does this part of the site drain to" ‚Äî the B641-adjacent note the brief asked recorded). Screen-spaced lattice (~70 px); arrow length + weight scale with slope (bolder = steeper ‚Äî salience tracks importance); slope computed in ft/ft with the cos(lat) mercator‚Üíground correction (~0.868 at Houston ‚Äî mercator meters overstate ground distance ~15%). **Flat/ambiguous/void samples get NO arrow** ‚Äî never invent a direction (the no-auto-guess principle). Rides B704's tile end-to-end: NO second DEM fetch path (the same worker job computes both artifacts). Sign-convention unit tests pin east-falling ‚Üí east arrows and south-falling ‚Üí down-screen arrows (grid y-down vs latitude-up is the classic silent bug ‚Äî uphill arrows pass every other test).
- **Verified here (sandbox):** flowField unit suite (sign pins, ground-meter slope, flat ‚Üí no arrows, void suppression, lattice spacing) ¬∑ headless: toggling Drainage direction after Contours fires ZERO additional grid fetches, arrows add real canvas ink, and on the real Katy tile the arrows visibly point INTO the channel depression crossing the view (screenshot) ‚Äî physically coherent on real data.
> **Dedup (DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî grepped drainage / flow / arrow / outfall across Open/‚è≥/Done):** B641 (Later) = outfall flowline/tailwater SOURCING; B636‚ÄìB639 (Done) = regulatory/hydraulic drainage rules. No existing item draws DEM-derived flow direction on the map. Net-new.

### B706 ‚Äî Hover ground-elevation readout on the cursor coordinate chips `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #gis #ui  *(owner brief 2026-07-07, arrived as "NEW-4"; **B706** = B705 + 1. Branch `claude/elevation-houston-relief-7co7do`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + headless-verified this session; parks (Verify: live ‚Äî must agree with the cross-section tool on real ground) ‚Üí V242.**
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V242 PASSED live 2026-07-18 (headless self-verify against REAL USGS 3DEP data) ‚Äî hover-vs-cross-section agreement confirmed within 0.40 ft and hover-vs-contour-label within 0.50 ft on real data; the network-path (getSamples) fallback with all layers off confirmed with real, unmocked data.**
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat
- **What shipped:** both B683 coordinate chips (map finder bottom-center; planner bottom-left) append **"¬∑ El ‚âà 132.3 ft NAVD88"** at the cursor. New `components/useGroundElevation.js` hook: with a terrain-layer grid covering the view it bilinear-samples locally on every move (instant, zero network ‚Äî the **UNSMOOTHED** grid, so it agrees with the cross-section tool, not the smoothed cartography); otherwise ONE debounced (~300 ms cursor-rest) `elevation.samplePoint` getSamples call ‚Äî never per-mousemove. Superseded requests abort; a result is tied to the position it was asked for (a slow response can never paint a stale number under a new position). **No-data/void/failure all render as ABSENCE** ‚Äî the segment simply doesn't appear (a confident number over water would be a lie). Screening caveat rides the chip's `title`. The planner chip now reprojects through one shared memo (same `feetToLatLng`, display unchanged).
- **Verified here (sandbox):** first-ever `test/elevation.test.js` (samplePoint URL shape / survey-ft / no-data ‚Üí null / LOUD throws; ditchStats B58/B23 pins) ¬∑ bilinear + void-refusal unit tests in demGrid ¬∑ headless: the chip read **132.3 ft**, inside the real tile's 118‚Äì152 ft range, while the getSamples mock was pinned at 100.0 ft ‚Äî proving the value came from the decoded-grid path.
> **Dedup (DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî grepped readout / hover / elevation across Open/‚è≥/Done):** B683 (Done) = the coordinate chips this extends; B695 (‚è≥) = boundary-NAME identify, unrelated. Net-new.

### B894 ‚Äî A fast "click to select, then double-click to edit" (3 rapid clicks) on a callout/road/markup silently swallowed the edit `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #selection #ui #markup  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-18 from chat: "Double click on a callout after it's selected should allow you to edit text, three clicks total." Minted **B894** (late-bound against fresh main). Branch `claude/triple-click-callout-edit-fix`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + unit + headless-e2e verified this session.** `isDoubleTap`'s pairing logic used to fully WIPE its tap history the instant a double-tap was detected, so a genuine "select, then IMMEDIATELY double-click" gesture done fast enough that all three presses land inside one ~350ms window (a completely natural way to do this ‚Äî real users don't pause between a select-click and the edit double-click) lost its third press entirely: presses 1+2 paired (selecting + opening Properties), and press 3 had nothing left to pair with, so it fell through as an unrelated lone click. The callout/road/markup ended up with Properties open but never entered edit mode ‚Äî confirmed via a live headless repro (all 3 presses ~100ms apart) before touching any code.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: owner-dropped 2026-07-18 from chat
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open / ‚è≥ Verify / Done (`^### B` + `#selection`/`#ui`/`#markup`) ‚Äî net-new; a follow-on gap in **B893**'s own fix (shipped minutes earlier in this same session) ‚Äî B893 closed the "which action does a double-click take" bug, this closes a separate timing gap in the SAME `isDoubleTap` primitive B893 relies on.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** `isDoubleTap`'s matched branch now RE-ARMS `lastTapRef` to the just-matched press (marked `wasSel:true`, since a matched pair always selects the feature) instead of clearing it to an empty record ‚Äî so a third press within the window pairs with the second and correctly reads "already selected" ‚Üí edits. Because the re-armed record now survives past a completed pair, anything that clearly ENDS the gesture also explicitly clears it, so a later UNRELATED click landing inside that window can't misread as "press 3" of a gesture the user never intended to continue: the global Escape handler and the Properties panel's "‚úï Close" button both now reset `lastTapRef` too (found via a live regression the naive re-arm caused: closing Properties then a plain single re-click was briefly reopening it because the click paired with the stale re-armed record from the double-click that had just opened it).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** `npm run build` green; full unit suite (286 files / 4522 tests) green. `e2e/click-behavior.spec.js` ‚Äî new spec drives a real ~100ms-apart 3-click gesture on a callout and confirms the text editor opens (not just Properties); all 5 other specs in the file (including the B893 coverage) re-verified passing after this change, live-catching and fixing the Escape/‚úï regression before it shipped. Also re-ran `e2e/element-delete.spec.js`, `single-occupancy-dock.spec.js`, `markup-tools.spec.js`, `building-reshape.spec.js`, `ctrlz-undo.spec.js` (29 passed, 22 skipped signed-in-only) since the shared Escape handler changed.

### B893 ‚Äî Double-clicking a road/markup/callout could force the inline-label editor open instead of Properties `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #selection #ui #markup  *(owner-reported 2026-07-18 from chat: "the item where double clicking stuff tried to get you to add an inline label, double clicking stuff is supposed to open properties." Minted **B893** (late-bound against fresh main). Branch `claude/double-click-properties-fix-2gobzt`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + unit + headless-e2e verified this session.** B750 wired "double-click opens Properties; an ALREADY-selected text-bearing feature's double-click edits its inline label instead" through the RECONSTRUCTED pointerdown double-tap path (`startMoveEl`/`startMoveMarkup`/`startMoveCallout`, since pointer capture normally suppresses the browser's native `dblclick`). But four raw native `onDoubleClick` JSX handlers were never updated to the same gate ‚Äî leftovers from before B750 (three straight from B620, one from B750's own `onElDouble` fallback): a centerline road's `onElDouble` unconditionally opened the inline label editor for ANY double-click (not just an already-selected one), and the easement/Line/Polyline markup shapes' `onDoubleClick` + the callout box's `onDoubleClick` unconditionally called `beginEditInline`/`beginEditCallout` with no "was it already selected" check at all ‚Äî so on any browser/interaction where the native `dblclick` actually fires (this fallback path exists specifically for that case), a fresh double-click on one of these four could force the inline editor open instead of Properties, exactly as reported.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: owner-reported 2026-07-18 from chat
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** searched Open / ‚è≥ Verify / Done (`^### B` + `#selection`/`#ui`/`#markup`) ‚Äî net-new; a gap in **B750**'s own rollout (not a B750 recurrence ‚Äî B750's tested scope, buildings + fresh markup creation + Doc Review, was correct and stayed passing), not B679 (double-click-to-edit-text, the gesture this preserves), not B620 (the original inline-label feature this fallback leaked from).
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** `onElDouble`'s centerline-road branch now reads `dblWasSelRef.current` (set by `isDoubleTap`) instead of unconditionally calling `beginEditInline`. Added a new shared `onMarkupDouble(e, id)` fallback (mirroring `startMoveMarkup`'s gate) and wired it into the easement/Line/Polyline `<g>` handlers, replacing their unconditional `beginEditInline` calls. The callout box's `onDoubleClick` now runs the same "already-selected ‚Üí edit; else ‚Üí Properties" branch `startMoveCallout` uses, instead of unconditionally calling `beginEditCallout`. Also fixed a latent pre-existing bug surfaced while writing the e2e coverage: the multi-select/markup/callout Properties sub-panels each nested a SECOND `data-testid="property-panel"` div inside the outer docked-panel wrapper that already carries it, so `getByTestId("property-panel")` resolved to 2 elements (a Playwright strict-mode violation) any time one of those was open ‚Äî removed the three duplicate testids (plain `<div>` now).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** `npm run build` green; full unit suite (284 files / 4504 tests) green, incl. `backlogIndex`/`mapDrift`/`docPointers`/`contrast` guards. New `e2e/click-behavior.spec.js` coverage (3 new specs, real Chromium, logged-out via "Start blank," driven with genuine pointer down/up pairs ‚Äî not synthetic dblclick ‚Äî matching how the app reconstructs a double-tap): a centerline road, a Line markup, and a callout each verified BOTH halves ‚Äî a fresh double-click (not already selected) opens Properties and does NOT open the inline/text editor; single-click to select, then (after clearing the click's own 350ms tap window so the two presses pair with each other, not the reselect click) a genuine double-click DOES open the inline/text editor and Properties stays closed. All 5 specs in the file pass (3√ó stable locally).

### B750 ‚Äî Clicking an element auto-opened its Properties menu; make single-click SELECT and double-click OPEN Properties `[Site Planner / Doc Review]` (feature) #site-planner #doc-review #selection #ui #markup  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-10 from chat: "I don't want clicking on any element to automatically open the properties menu ‚Äî single left click selects, double left click opens the properties menu." - **Implementation:** `SitePlanner.jsx` ‚Äî replaced the `propsDismissed` state with an explicit `propsFor` marker; the companion renders only when it matches the current selection, so ANY non-double-click selection path leaves it closed. `DocReview.jsx` ‚Äî a `propsForId` marker gates the rail Properties section, a `dblRef` captures the first-press selection in `onDown`, `onDbl` disambiguates edit-text vs open-Properties, and a freshly DRAWN markup auto-opens Properties.

### B749 ‚Äî Overlay PDF rasterization quality: raise base resolution + zoom-aware re-raster `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #perf  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-10 as "NEW-3". Minted **B749** = B748 + 1.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session ‚Äî zoom-/data-density-dependent rendering is a mandatory LIVE-VERIFY class ‚Üí parked in ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V262.** Placed PDF overlays went soft when zoomed past sheet-fit because `rasterizePage` capped the raster at `MAX_RASTER_DIM = 2600 px`.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): **V262 PASSED** headless ‚Äî `ui-audit/verify-b749-overlay-reraster.mjs` (5/5, logged-out, merged HEAD) proves the zoom-aware re-raster end-to-end: a dropped PDF places a base data-URL raster; zooming in swaps in a transient blob hi-res raster; at a fixed zoom the base‚Üíhi-res swap keeps ONE on-screen geometry (no move/resize); zooming out drops back to the base raster (blob released); the anchored placement record is unchanged across the cycle (hi-res is render-only, never persisted). ‚Üí VERIFICATION-DONE.md V262.
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-10 from chat (owner NEW-3)
- **Tier 1 ‚Äî raise the base:** `MAX_RASTER_DIM` ‚Üí **4500** (scale cap lifted so small sheets sharpen too), with a **row-banded** white-knockout pass so the `getImageData` transient stays bounded at 4500 px. Base raster stays a persisted **data URL** (the safety-critical strip/reload/cloud chain is deliberately untouched).
- **Tier 2 ‚Äî zoom-aware re-raster:** when the overlay's on-screen magnification exceeds ~1.5√ó its raster pixels, re-render that PDF page at a higher scale (cap **8192 px**) from the in-session pdf proxy (or, after reload, from stored bytes via `rasterizeStoredPdf`), debounced behind zoom-settle, swapped in place on the SAME transform. The hi-res raster is a **transient revocable object URL** held in a ref ‚Äî never persisted ‚Äî and is dropped back to the base raster + revoked when zoomed out.
- **Tests:** unit tests for the scale-selection math (`test/overlayRaster.test.js`, 8 cases).
### B744 ‚Äî "Drop site plan" overlay hint sticks after a drag leaves the window; make it non-obscuring `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-09 as "NEW-1"; provisional **B736**, renumbered **B744**; code/tests/commit keep the provisional **B736** label. Branch `claude/site-planner-overlay-bugs-yzkalh`. ‚Üí V256.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + LIVE-VERIFIED (V256 ‚úÖ PASS 2026-07-18, headless synthetic-drag drive) ‚Äî folded here per the B877 reconciliation rule.** The full-canvas "Drop site plan to place it on the map" drag hint could stay stuck ON after a file was dragged over the canvas and then back OUT of the browser window without dropping; and its light-theme fill was opaque, hiding existing geometry. The highlight is now driven by a dragenter/dragleave DEPTH COUNTER + a window-level reset, and its fill is a translucent accent wash (both themes) so the site stays visible under it.
- Verify: live ‚Üí **V256 PASS 2026-07-18** (`ui-audit/verify-overlay-dnd.mjs` re-run 11/11 in the running app this session; the only non-drivable bit is the OS's native file-drag gesture, which is a Playwright platform limit, not one of the three named sandbox walls ‚Äî recorded as driven-and-passed on the confirmable surface).
- **Part B (the real defect):** the canvas wrapper's `onDragLeave` only cleared when `e.currentTarget === e.target` (false whenever the pointer was over the child `<svg>` at exit), and the window guard listened only for `dragover`/`drop` ‚Äî NO window `dragleave`/reset. An OS-file drag fires no in-page `dragend`, and its final window-exit `dragleave` can target `document`/`body` (outside the wrapper subtree), so nothing cleared the flag ‚Üí the tan highlight stuck on with nothing under the cursor.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** (1) both dropzones (canvas wrapper + References panel) use a per-zone dragenter/dragleave DEPTH COUNTER ref ‚Äî highlight iff depth>0, force depth=0 on drop ‚Äî robust against the child-crossing flicker, replacing the fragile `currentTarget===target` guard. (2) The window guard `useEffect` gains a `dragleave` listener that hard-resets ALL drag-hover flags + both counters, but ONLY when the drag truly left the window (`relatedTarget==null` **AND** pointer coords at/past a viewport edge), plus the window `drop` handler now also resets. `preventDefault` on dragover (drop stays allowed) + the panel drop's `stopPropagation` are preserved. (3) Part A spirit: the hint fill is now `color-mix(in srgb, <accent> 18%, transparent)` in both themes so geometry shows through; `pointerEvents:none` kept.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** distinct from B445 / V131 (which BUILT this affordance) ‚Äî a GAP in B445's edge-case reset (leave-window), not a regression.

### B741 ‚Äî Orphaned / misfit selection handles on angled elements `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #selection #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-09 as "NEW-2"; provisional **B735**, renumbered **B741**. ‚Üí V254.)*
`[x]` **RESOLVED-BY-B740 / NOT-REPRODUCIBLE ‚Äî repro driven, no defect found (V254 ‚úÖ PASS 2026-07-18, headless Chromium, logged-out). Folded here per the B877 reconciliation rule.** This was LIVE-REPRO-GATED (measure-before-patch). The 2026-07-18 repro drove the exact scenario ‚Äî two angled truck-court-style strips at 35¬∞ to the grid ‚Äî and selected one strip; the reported misfit does **not** reproduce: exactly one clean grip set on the ROTATED corners (all four grip-rectangle edges at 35¬∞, not an upright AABB), no stale/duplicate handle layer, no bleed onto the neighboring strip, and the rotation stem normal to the rotated top edge (‚àí55¬∞ screen, not screen-up ‚àí90¬∞). New harness `ui-audit/verify-v254-angled-handles.mjs` (5/5).
- Verify: live ‚Üí **V254 PASS 2026-07-18** (repro-first acceptance: no defect to fix).

So by the time the repro ran, there was nothing left to patch.

### B758 ‚Äî App white-screens when the MAIN bundle 404s during a deploy (no recovery exists for the entry chunk) `[App shell / infra]` (bug) #infra #ui  *(owner-reported 2026-07-11 from chat ‚Äî "I pressed control shift r, and I guess we had just merged to change. And now the thing isn't even loading." Console showed `Failed to load resource: ‚Ä¶ 404 ‚Ä¶ index-CgI4uzRL.js`. Branch `claude/app-not-loading-merge-t7dwnu`. ‚Üí V272.)*
- ‚úÖ **V272 PASSED 2026-07-18 (sandbox headless)** ‚Äî recovery mechanism proven by the committed 3-scenario harness `ui-audit/verify-boot-watchdog.mjs`; the real Cloudflare deploy-window 404 is an un-forceable opportunistic observation, not a gating step. Folded to Done per B877.
`[x]` **FIXED this session ‚Äî recovery mechanism proven headless (3-scenario browser test), build green; the real Cloudflare deploy-window race parks in ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V272.** A hard reload during the seconds a fresh deploy was still propagating served the current `index.html` but 404'd its entry bundle `/assets/index-<hash>.js` at the owner's edge ‚Üí blank white screen. **Confirmed transient:** that exact file returned HTTP 200 minutes later, and the live entry hash rolled `CgI4uzRL ‚Üí DwgvooFK` (build id `6e67849`) between two checks ‚Äî i.e. active deploy churn, not a broken build.
- Verify: live ‚Äî deploy timing/race class (mandatory LIVE-VERIFY); the recovery mechanism is proven by a headless 3-scenario browser test, the real-deploy trigger is owed on V272.
- Origin: owner-reported 2026-07-11 (app not loading after a merge).
- **AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî why the existing recovery couldn't help.** `src/app/chunkReload.js` + `ErrorBoundary` already recover from stale/missing **lazy** workspace chunks (via Vite's `vite:preloadError`) and from render crashes ‚Äî but that code lives INSIDE the main bundle. When the ENTRY bundle itself 404s, **no app JS runs at all**, so none of that machinery exists ‚Üí a permanent white screen until a manual reload. The `_headers` (index.html `no-cache`, `/assets/*` immutable) and Cloudflare's atomic deploys are correct; the one uncovered gap is the brief edge-propagation window where fresh HTML points at a not-yet-published entry chunk.
- **The fix (`index.html`, inline boot watchdog ‚Äî the entry-bundle analogue of B221/B239):** a small self-contained script that is part of index.html (the one file that is never content-hashed, so it never 404s and survives the failure). It catches **(A)** a definitive load error on the entry `<script>` (capture-phase `error` listener, filtered to a not-yet-mounted app + an `/assets/*.js` script so a font/CSS/image error or a late runtime throw never trips it) and **(B)** a never-mounts timeout, then shows an honest "Updating Planyr‚Ä¶" message and does a BOUNDED, cache-busting reload (`?_r=<ts>` ‚Äî the same param the app already strips post-boot via `stripReloadParam`) to pick up the finished deploy. Bounded to **3** auto-reloads on a definitive load error (**1** on a bare mount-timeout, so a slow-but-fine connection isn't re-downloaded to death), then an honest "finishing an update / Try again" manual screen ‚Äî never an infinite loop. A successful mount **clears the retry counter** so a future, separate deploy gets a fresh budget. Bails on `navigator.webdriver` (e2e/ui-audit untouched) and the whole body is `try/catch`, so it can never itself block boot.
- **Named rules:** LOUD-FAILURE (a blank dead page becomes a visible "updating" state + bounded auto-recovery, never a silent no-op). No pure lib touched (inline HTML), so no unit test is applicable ‚Äî verified instead by a headless browser integration test.
- **Verified (sandbox ‚Äî headless Chromium against a local server that serves `dist/` and 404s the entry bundle for the first N loads, `navigator.webdriver` overridden to false + a `#`-hash deep-link so the app boots like a real user):** (1) **normal boot** ‚Äî 1 load, app mounts, overlay never appears, no leftover state; (2) **transient race healing after 2 failed loads** ‚Äî 404 ‚Üí auto-reload ‚Üí 404 ‚Üí auto-reload ‚Üí 200 ‚Üí app mounts, counter cleared (the owner's exact scenario); (3) **persistent break** ‚Äî exactly 3 auto-reloads (4 total loads) then the honest manual-retry overlay, no loop. All three PASS. Build green.
- **Live check owed (V272):** on planyr.io after this deploys ‚Äî confirm normal boot is unaffected (the watchdog is invisible on success), and (DevTools ‚Üí Network ‚Üí block `/assets/index-*.js` ‚Üí reload) confirm the "Updating Planyr‚Ä¶" overlay appears and recovers when unblocked. A real Cloudflare deploy-race can't be forced on demand from the sandbox.

### B797 ‚Äî County-gate the Harris-side detention criteria (HCFCD compare + channel question + `inCityLimits`) `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ yield]` (bug) #site-planner #gis #yield  *(filed 2026-07-12 from a Cowork investigation brief as "NEW-2", minted B789; RENUMBERED B789‚Üí**B797** on merge-in of `origin/main` (PR #602 took B784‚ÄìB793); code/tests keep the provisional **B789** label. Evidence: `COWORK-BRIEF-2026-07-12-stormwater-drainage-authority.md`. ‚Üí V295. Implemented + shipped same session.)*
- ‚úÖ **V295 PASSED 2026-07-18 (sandbox headless)** ‚Äî Scenarios 1+2: HCFCD control absent off-Harris, ignored-answer note in the Assumptions ‚ìò (B823), no greater-of, COH-override caveat. Folded to Done per B877.
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî all three guards.** (a) `drainChannelRelevant` now requires the identify county to include Harris (or the Harris-only `channelAuthority`), so the HCFCD candidate and the "Drains to HCFCD channel" Auto/Yes/No control never surface off-Harris; (b) `drainInCity` tests HOUSTON membership specifically ‚Äî Bain's Katy frontage sliver satisfied the old any-city test and priced the >20-ac greater-of (HCFCD 0.65 √ó 109.0 = 70.85 vs COH 0.75 √ó 21.4 = 16.05 ‚Üí "HCFCD governs") on a Fort Bend site; (c) a stored `drainsToHcfcdChannel` override is IGNORED (not cleared) where HCFCD doesn't govern, with a visible card note (Bain carries a latent `true`). ### B872 ‚Äî Reshape a placed rectangular building: convert-to-polygon + constrained (dock-frame) vertex editing `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #selection #ui #yield  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-16 as "NEW-1"; minted **B872** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Branch `claude/building-footprint-reshape-nxef15`. Code/tests keep the provisional label where stamped.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî the new grid-clip + dock-door RENDER is the zoom-/data-density-dependent class) ‚Üí V355.** Angle an end wall or clip a corner on a placed cross-dock / single-dock building ‚Äî to follow an angled property line and capture usable SF. A placed rect used to only resize; now it PROMOTES to an editable polygon and the shared B230 vertex engine drives it, with the loaded (dock) walls held straight.
- **‚úÖ FOLDED ‚Üí Done 2026-07-18** (this session, verification batch, B877 reconciliation): live-verify **V355 PASSED** ‚Äî headless self-verify of the reshape confirmed the column grid CLIPS to the angled outline (zero spill past a wall) and the dock doors ride the TRUE (shortened) dock-wall segment (bay/door count drops as the wall shortens), at site-overview AND zoomed-in, with 0 page errors. ‚Üí VERIFICATION-DONE.md V355.
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-16 from chat (owner NEW-1).
- **Entry points:** right-click a rect building ‚Üí "‚úé Edit footprint (reshape)"; same as the "‚úé Edit footprint‚Ä¶" button in Selected ¬∑ Building ‚Üí Footprint. Converts `el.points = elCorners(el)` (world feet, rotation baked in) + pins the dock walls.
- **Dock-frame preservation (the core design):** the loaded walls are recorded as fixed world-feet LINES (`el.dockLines`), NOT by edge index (an index shifts the moment you insert a control point on an end wall). A dock corner SLIDES ALONG its wall (drag projects onto the line, no grid snap); an end/rear vertex moves freely. v1 guards keep dock walls straight: inserting a control point on a dock edge is blocked (LOUD); deleting a dock corner is refused (consumes the Delete key, never nukes the building). `dock:"none"` ‚Üí fully free editing.
- **Derived dims + downstream:** after each vertex drop, `frameBBox` recomputes `w`/`h`/`cx`/`cy` as the dock-frame bounding box, so dockSidesFor / footprintDepth¬∑Length / the column grid / KMZ / clear-height¬∑slab keep reading correct bounding dims; the panel Length/Depth render read-only (labeled "bounding"); footprint SF is polygon-truth (`buildingSqft` ‚Üí `polyArea`). Column grid clips to the polygon ring (`clipSegmentToRing`); the dock-door count/apron repoints to the TRUE dock-wall segment (`dockSegExtent`), recounting as the wall lengthens/shortens; the truck court + its outward stack pull in to that segment (reuses the B492 clear-span trim).
- **Panel routing (fixed during the headless drive):** a reshaped building (`footEdit`) now keeps the full building inspector (Footprint reshape controls + "‚Ü∫ Reset to rectangle", dock zones, structure, column grid) instead of falling through to the generic polygon inspector ‚Äî the `!selEl.points` gate now also admits `selEl.footEdit`. A hand-click-drawn irregular building still gets the generic inspector (no regression).
- **Guards:** self-intersecting / zero-area / <3-vtx drop reverts to the pre-drag ring (LOUD); conversion `pushHistory` so undo restores the pristine rect. Corner bump-outs anchor to square corners ‚Üí conversion is BLOCKED while any are attached (visible message, never a silent drop); the bump-out + end-wall-parking adders are disabled on a reshaped building.
- **Move/copy parity:** single-move, group-move, duplicate, and paste all carry the dock-frame box + pinned walls with the shifted ring (`shiftPointsEl` / snapshot translate) so a reshaped building never strands its dock frame at the source. Element-level sync + blob save round-trip `points`/`footEdit`/`dockLines` verbatim (no schema bump ‚Äî the persistence layer stores each element as an opaque blob; agent-audited).
- **Parity:** hand-click-drawn irregular buildings (creation-time `points`, no dock frame) are untouched ‚Äî prior fill-only render + fully-free editing (no regression). Only `footEdit` buildings get the constrained treatment + grid/doors.
- **New pure lib:** `lib/footprintEdit.js` (rectRing ¬∑ dockLinesFor ¬∑ convertBuildingToPolygon ¬∑ dockLineAt ¬∑ dockEdgeLine ¬∑ projectOntoLine ¬∑ frameBBox ¬∑ dockSegExtent ¬∑ translateDockLines ¬∑ clipSegmentToRing), unit-tested in `test/footprintEdit.test.js` (geometry + a full reshape pipeline). Wiring in `SitePlanner.jsx`.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** EXTENDS the shared B230 vertex engine (the `el.points` case); building sibling of B718 (road control-point editing). No prior item promotes a rect building to a polygon. No new engine number.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/footprintEdit.test.js` (23 tests: geometry + full reshape pipeline) green ¬∑ full suite **4199 green** ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP/backlog-index clean ¬∑ **headless interactive drive** (`e2e/building-reshape.spec.js`, logged-out): draw a building ‚Üí "Edit footprint" ‚Üí the building inspector shows the irregular-footprint state ‚Üí drag a corner ‚Üí "Reset to rectangle", all with **0 page errors** (this drive found + fixed the panel-routing bug above).
- **‚è≥ V355 (live):** see VERIFICATION.md ‚Äî the grid-clip + dock-door render at real zooms on a reshaped building (zoom-/data-density render class) + the drag-gesture feel need a human eye.

### B859 ‚Äî Waller County rule records: 500-yr mitigation trigger, conditioned FFE rules, no-structural-fill hard stop, floodway + 100-ft buffer `[Site Planner / floodplain]` (task) #site-planner #floodplain #yield  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-1"; minted **B855** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`, RENUMBERED ‚Üí **B859** on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî a concurrent Scheduler session shipped-and-archived B855 (calendar-day durations) first; the B780 cross-file guard caught it pre-merge, exactly as designed. `[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî GIS endpoint + real-project-data (Tsakiris) classes) ‚Üí V337.** The `waller` records in `floodplainRules.js` / `buildability.js` were `verified:false` placeholders with the WRONG mitigation trigger (1% only).
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat (owner primary-source pull of the Waller County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance, county-posted PDF at co.waller.tx.us).
- **(a) floodplainRules.waller** ‚Äî Art. 5 ¬ßA(8): 1:1 compensating storage ON-SITE for fill in the SFHA and/or moderate (500-yr) areas, "no net fill up to 500-year floodplain elevation" ‚Üí `trigger: 1pct_plus_02pct` (the COH pattern), ratio 1, on-site locationRule. ¬ßE floodway policy: encroachment/fill prohibited in the regulatory floodway **plus a 100-ft buffer zone** ‚Äî modeled as a NEW schema field `floodwayBufferFt: 100` with a TRUE geometry screen (not copy-only v1): `pointInZone` honors `zone.bufferFt` as a distance test against the rings (closed loops included), `computeMitigation`/`wedgeMitigation` wrap floodway zones via `bufferedFloodway`, buffer-only acreage flags `floodway_buffer`, and the panel/print copy names the setback. The flood EXTENT semantics (trigger bands, WSE sampling, `ringInTrigger`) deliberately do NOT buffer ‚Äî ¬ßE is a fill setback, not a bigger flood (unit-asserted).
- **(b) buildability.waller** ‚Äî Art. 5 ¬ßB(2) nonresidential: lowest floor ‚â• 500-yr WSE + 2 ft in the 1% floodplain; ‚â• 500-yr WSE + 1 ft in the 500-yr band only; ¬ßD(5): A Zone with no depth number ‚Üí slab ‚â• highest adjacent grade + 4 ft (the section sits under the AO/AH standards but reads as the A-Zone catch-all ‚Äî placement transcribed as found, per the owner's edge-case note). Encoded via a NEW `when` condition on multi-basis rows (`in_1pct` / `in_02pct_only` / `zone_a_no_bfe` ‚Äî B759's bases extended once; unknown location stays conservative for WSE rows, the grade-derived HAG row applies only on explicit unstudied-A evidence) + the new `hag` basis (fed by B857's proxy). Fort Bend's max-of machinery is regression-locked unchanged under any ctx.
- **(c) fillToElevate: "prohibited"** ‚Äî a NEW, stronger enum than Harris's "restricted" (¬ßA(9): no structural fill in the SFHA or the 500-yr band; open foundations (pier and beam) only); pathway copy: "slab-on-grade in the mapped 100-yr or 500-yr floodplain is a non-starter in unincorporated Waller ‚Äî keep structures out of both bands (no fill-to-elevate/LOMR-F pathway)", rendered ‚õî (warn tone ‚Äî red stays reserved).
- **(d) Copy notes carried on both records:** ¬ßC(3) >50 lots / >5 ac must generate BFE/500-yr elevations via an Atlas-14 study (Planyr's numbers are screening ahead of it); Brookshire‚ÄìKaty Drainage District flagged UNRESOLVED in the note ‚Äî no BKDD record fabricated; the "City of Katy ‚Äî edge only" straddle rides the existing per-candidate straddle machinery unchanged.
- **Provenance:** `verified:true` with `sourceDate: 2026-07-15` (the owner's primary-source pull) ‚Äî ¬ßA(8) additionally search-triangulated VERBATIM ("no net fill up to 500-year floodplain elevation") and the floodway+100-ft buffer confirmed via search-indexed text; the sandbox could NOT re-fetch the PDF (co.waller.tx.us 403s automated fetches ‚Äî recorded in the note) and the VERSIONING ambiguity (adopted 1/13/2009 / revised eff. 2/28/2013 / "REVISED_FINAL-2021" filename) is recorded in both records.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** extends B758/B760 (the verified Fort Bend/Harris records ‚Äî same triangulation standard) and B759 (multi-basis bases, extended once with `when`); not a recurrence.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `floodplainRules.test.js` (+1 Waller block, seed-integrity moved waller into VERIFIED_SEEDS, buffer is Waller-only), `floodplainMitigation.test.js` (+5: buffered pointInZone incl. closed-loop edges, near-fill flags under Waller not Harris, in-polygon takes no buffer flag, trigger/WSE semantics unbuffered, wedge cells screened), `buildability.test.js` (+7: conditioned bases govern per location, HAG evidence-gating, prohibited pathway, Fort Bend unchanged); headless `verify-waller-floodplain.mjs` Case A (rule auto-detects on a mocked Waller site: label + "1% + 0.2% trigger @ 1:1" + unstudied-A honesty + ‚õî prohibited copy). Full suite (4113) + lint 0 errors + build green.

### B856 ‚Äî Unstudied Zone A: estimated-WSE provider (grade along the zone boundary) so mitigation, heat map, and pond split stop dead-ending `[Site Planner / floodplain]` (feature) #site-planner #floodplain #yield #gis  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-2"; minted **B856**. Branch `claude/waller-county-floodplain-rules-ck41hx`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî GIS endpoint (3DEP grid) + real-project-data (Tsakiris) classes) ‚Üí V338.** Repro (Tsakiris): unstudied Zone A ‚Üí `wse1pct` null ‚Üí mitigation UNKNOWN, fill-depth heat map Œ£ 0.00 with every cell "not priced," pond banded split unavailable ("gross volume OVERSTATES").
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat ("NEW-2").
- **Engine (`estimateZoneAWse`, floodplainMitigation.js):** samples the B808 per-cell 3DEP grid along the Zone A polygon's REAL NFHL boundary segments near the site (fmZone rings are FULL unclipped polygons ‚Äî every vertex is a genuine flood-boundary point, the mapped extent being where the flood surface daylights into grade, FEMA's approximate-Zone-A contour method), ~50-ft spacing filtered to a padded site-bbox vicinity, MEDIAN with the min‚Äìmax spread reported. Honesty rules unit-locked: plain Zone A only (`z.unstudiedA && zone === "A"` ‚Äî AO's published DEPTH provider is never overridden or mimicked); no `gradeAt` / a DEM outage / too few samples ‚Üí null (an outage is never an estimate); a huge zone samples only the site vicinity.
- **UX (accept-gated, the owner's decisive pick):** the inputs panel shows the estimate as a chip next to the BFE field ‚Äî "Est. 1% WSE ‚âà 154.2‚Ä≤ ‚Äî from grade along the mapped Zone A boundary (sampled 153.6‚Äì154.9)"; spread > 2 ft appends "sloped reach ‚Äî coarse". ONE click writes `bfeFt` + `bfeSrc: "est-boundary-grade"` ‚Äî never auto-committed; the ghost recomputes per check but NEVER overwrites the committed value; typing or clearing the field always clears the tag (`autoField` srcKey).
- **The stamp never drops off a consumer:** the accepted value rides the manual rung with its OWN provider tag through `zoneWaterSurface`/`wse1pctForRing` (`manualBfeSrc`/`bfeSrc` seams) ‚Üí mitigation ledger row + providers line (WSE_PROVIDER_LABEL "ESTIMATED (grade @ Zone A boundary)"), the heat-map legend line (rides the export clone ‚Äî PDF-PARITY), the pond split (pondFloodFacts carries `wseSrc`; PERSISTED per-pond in `lastCheck.detSplit.byId` so the stamp survives a reload; the inspector split warns), buildability/FFE verdict + print pair, and B858's assistant (`estimated:true`). Copy: `EST_BOUNDARY_WSE_NOTE` carries the Waller ¬ßC(3) Atlas-14 + County-Engineer best-available-data (Art. 4 ¬ßB(8)) caveat.
- **Precedence:** static-bfe ‚Üí ao-depth ‚Üí manual/estimated (the estimate IS a manual-rung value once accepted ‚Äî a typed BFE replaces it and clears the tag) ‚Üí derived tiers; feeds `computeMitigation`'s manual path, `wse1pctForRing` ‚Üí `bandedStorage` (unlocking the pond's candidate/usable split), and B857's `requiredFfe`.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** a NEW provider tier beside B755 (BFE-line interp) / B763 (XS WSEL) / B807 (DRAFT raster) ‚Äî those need published lines/sections/rasters that unstudied Zone A lacks; net-new.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `floodplainMitigation.test.js` (+5: median+spread on a tilted plane, outage/void ‚Üí null, AO/studied excluded, vicinity filter, end-to-end provider tag through computeMitigation + wse1pctForRing); headless Case A asserts the HONEST absence (DEM 404 ‚Üí no chip, UNKNOWN stands). Full suite + build green.

### B857 ‚Äî Suggested pad FFE: compute the jurisdictional minimum and offer it into the blank Pad/FFE field `[Site Planner / floodplain]` (feature) #site-planner #floodplain #yield  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-3"; minted **B857**. Branch `claude/waller-county-floodplain-rules-ck41hx`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî GIS endpoint (3DEP HAG) + PDF-parity classes) ‚Üí V339.** Repro: Pad/FFE sat blank ("‚Äî"); buildability only graded an FFE the user already entered, and Waller had no rule anyway ‚Äî a non-civil user got no help setting the single input that drives fill volumes.
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat ("NEW-3").
- **Engine:** `suggestedFfe` (buildability.js) wraps `requiredFfe` with the honesty rules: buildings fully outside the trigger bands ‚Üí `applies:false` + `OUTSIDE_FLOODPLAIN_FFE_NOTE` ("no county FFE rule applies outside the mapped floodplain ‚Äî drainage-criteria/pond-WSE checks may still govern; verify locally") ‚Äî never a fabricated number; unknown location suggests conservatively; `estimated:true` when the governing basis fed off B856's estimate. `requiredFfe` now returns `losingBases` (computable non-governing bases) for the tooltip. NEW **HAG provider** (`hagForRing`, floodplainMitigation.js): screening proxy = max 3DEP grade along each BUILDING footprint's perimeter (per-element ‚Äî HAG varies by building; plan-level suggestion takes the governing max), feeding the Waller ¬ßD(5) `hag` basis; honest null without the grid (the basis surfaces as pending).
- **UX:** the Pad/FFE ghost now names its GOVERNING basis ‚Äî e.g. "~157.1‚Ä≤ ¬∑ code min = 153.1‚Ä≤ (highest adjacent grade (Zone A, no depth number)) + 4‚Ä≤" ‚Äî losing bases ride the tooltip; a **‚úì use** chip writes it with provenance `padSrc: "suggested-accepted"` (a provenance line renders; √ó restores auto; typing clears the tag) and immediately re-prices the fill ledger + FFE check (the committed pad is a real manual value). ESTIMATED tag rides the ghost/accept/FFE verdict/print when B856's estimate feeds the governing basis; the HAG basis names itself "(HAG ‚Äî 3DEP screening proxy)" on the print pair (PDF-PARITY). - **DEDUPE-FIRST:** extends **B805** (the auto code-min pad precedence + greyed field ‚Äî that machinery stands; this adds the basis chip, accept-with-provenance, the HAG provider, outside-floodplain honesty, and the ESTIMATED threading) and B759 (multi-basis). - Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat ("NEW-4").
- **(1) Purpose selector ‚Äî DEDUPE-FIRST: this IS B834's role machinery, not a parallel field.** The owner's "purpose (Detention / Mitigation / Hybrid)" maps onto the shipped `det.role` selector: the inspector chips now read "Pond purpose" and the third option renders **Hybrid** (`POND_ROLE_LABEL.dual = "Hybrid"` ‚Äî the STORED enum stays `dual`; renaming it would orphan saved ponds; unit-locked). B834's elevation-share auto-suggest stands (it subsumes the owner's simpler in-trigger/WSE-known default and stays consistent with it); a second `det.purpose` subfield was deliberately NOT added.
- **(2) Per-band captions in plain language:** the inspector split note now captions BOTH bands ("ABOVE the WSE the basin is empty when the design storm arrives ‚Äî that volume counts toward detention. BELOW the WSE the flood already occupies the volume at design stage ‚Äî no detention credit, but it's your candidate compensating storage for fill"); the Yield Provided-row ‚ìò carries the same teaching copy; the required-vs-provided ledger rows themselves are B834/B637 (shipped ‚Äî the same-acre-foot-never-counts-twice invariant stands). Purpose/band readouts render screen + print from the same sources (B834's credited pair; PDF-PARITY unchanged).
- **(3) Sizing assistant (`lib/pondSizing.js`, new, pure):** `sizePondForTargets` solves an anchored pond's TWO banded targets through the SAME pondGeom bands the audit reads (can never disagree with the ledger): below-WSE candidate ‚â• the pond's mitigation target (solve floor depth ‚Äî half-foot convention, bounded by the `maxInwardOffset/slope` pinch-off; else solve uniform footprint growth ‚Üí "+N ac") and above-WSE usable ‚â• its detention target (solve TOB raise ‚Äî 0.1-ft convention, floor HELD, freeboard preserved, the labeled BERM_MAX_RAISE_FT screening clamp). Targets = the site requirement minus what the OTHER ponds already provide. Renders in the pond inspector as a status line + concrete actions with deltas ("mitigation covered ‚úì ¬∑ detention short 4.6 ac-ft ‚Üí raise TOB +1.5‚Ä≤ (berm) ‚Üí +N ac-ft"); proposals-only (the ONE apply affordance stays B830's berm chip ‚Äî no duplicate machinery).
- **(4) Berm-as-fill feedback loop:** a TOB raise above grade inside the trigger floodplain folds the INCREMENTAL berm prism below the WSE back into the mitigation requirement (one fixed-point pass, `bermFillVolume` √ó the rule ratio ‚Äî unit-asserted that the fringe target GROWS); the existing berm-as-fill warning stays; in unincorporated Waller the assistant adds the owner's context line ("berms are fill ‚Äî 1:1 offset applies; the no-structural-fill rule targets structures, but confirm berm treatment with the County Engineer").
- **Edge cases (all unit-locked):** WSE unknown ‚Üí the assistant REFUSES with the reason (never designs off gross); ESTIMATED WSE (B856) runs with `estimated:true` stamped on the block; fully-inundated (WSE ‚â• TOB) leads with "usable detention is ZERO ‚Äî raise the TOB" (never a delta table); pinch-off reports the geometric ceiling ("this footprint can't reach the target at N:1 slopes") never an impossible depth; inputs never mutated.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** extends B708 (bands), B834 (roles/credited ledger ‚Äî reused, labels renamed), B830 (`solveBermRaise` stays the site-wide joint apply; the assistant is per-pond design guidance), B640/B822 (the detention-only solvers stand for upland ponds); B709's criteria checks + B708's drainability screen remain in force beside the assistant's suggestions.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/pondSizing.test.js` (15: exact-square band fixtures ‚Äî deepen/grow/raise solves verify against `bandedStorage`, pinch-off honesty, clamp partials, berm-as-fill fixed point, inundated lead, ESTIMATED stamp, immutability); `pondLedger.test.js` (+1 label lock); headless Case B (anchored mid-column pond: "Pond purpose" chips with Auto (Hybrid), the assistant status line + captions render from the live-mock check) + `verify-pond-roles-ledger.mjs` re-run green (purpose naming updated). Full suite + build green.

### B822 ‚Äî Detention ponds engineered automatically: criteria records drive freeboard/slopes/constraints, terrain drives top-of-bank, the solver drives depth `[Site Planner / stormwater]` (feature) #site-planner #pond #yield #gis  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-13 as "NEW-3" ‚Äî "I should be able to lay it out, but it needs to automate the depth and top of bank‚Ä¶ this should all be auto-determined, like a true engineer would do it ‚Äî the one-foot freeboard should be based on the drainage jurisdiction"; minted **B822**)*
`[x]` Today `det.depth` (8‚Ä≤), `det.freeboard` (1‚Ä≤), `det.slope` (3:1) are bare fallback defaults ‚Äî and NOT one constant: the canonical `detOf` helper (`pondGeom.js:256-260`) plus scattered inline `?? 8/1/3` fallbacks (`SitePlanner.jsx:6092, 6462, 8029, 13097`) ‚Äî and `tobElev` is manual-only (`SitePlanner.jsx:12867/:12886`; hence the perpetual "not elevation-anchored" nag). Three layers, every value auto with a provenance chip, manual always wins, √ó restores auto (the `autoField` pattern, `SitePlanner.jsx:14968` ‚Äî shipped by B805):
- Verify: live (real-project-data repro + DEM/GIS class)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-14 from chat ("NEW-3")
- **(a) Criteria layer ‚Äî EXTEND the existing registries, never a third home for freeboard.** FBCDD freeboard 1‚Ä≤ (DCM ¬ß6.4.7), max release 0.125 cfs/ac (¬ß6.4.1), and gravity-drain ‚â•50% (Interim ¬ß5) are ALREADY shipped verified in `detentionRules.js:215-217` (B771, Done) ‚Äî **consume the existing verified params, don't re-add them**. Pond side-slope / maintenance-bench per-jurisdiction records already exist in `lib/pondCriteriaRules.js` (B709, Done ‚Äî `{maxSideSlope, minFreeboardFt, maintBermWidthFt, verified, source, sourceDate, note}`, all seeded `verified:false`) ‚Äî extend/reconcile THAT file (freeboard currently lives in both files; reconcile to one read path). Harris drawdown ‚â§4 days / orifice C=0.8 are net-new: seed `verified:false` with primary-source cites and flip only after transcription (the B709/B771 discipline); where an authority publishes nothing, keep the current default explicitly labeled "Planyr screening convention", never implied-published. Chips read like "freeboard 1‚Ä≤ ‚Äî FBCDD DCM ¬ß6.4.7".
- **(b) Terrain layer** ‚Äî TOB auto = existing grade over the pond ring from the DEM (perimeter median). DEPENDS on open **B808**'s site-extent grid (itself new work ‚Äî `fetchGridBytes`/`computeTile` are module-private to `terrainLayers.js`); until it ships, fall back to the site 3DEP median, labeled. Excavated-pond convention default; optional berm-height input raises TOB.
- **(c) Solver layer** ‚Äî depth auto = smallest depth closing the site's detention deficit via the existing **B640 auto-size seam** (`solvePondExpansion` `detentionRules.js:906`, `solvePondDepth` `:959`) + `detentionStorage` (`pondGeom.js:170` ‚Äî **average-end-area slab integration**, not prismoidal), clamped by the criteria record; surface the gravity-drain/wet-bottom caveats when the solve hits the clamp or the floor nears the flood WSE/pool (`deadStoragePoolDepthFt`, `detentionRules.js:1002` ‚Äî machinery exists). Floor = TOB ‚àí depth, displayed not asked. Route ALL the scattered 8/1/3 defaults through ONE seam (detOf + the inline fallbacks) so nothing half-wires.
- **OVERSTATES:** auto-anchored ponds feed `tobElev` into the B708 usable split (`usablePondVolume`, `pondGeom.js:342`) so the UNANCHORED warning variant (`SitePlanner.jsx:12956`, `:14866`) stops firing on default layouts. The anchored-but-WSE-unknown variant (`:12955`) correctly persists where no flood WSE exists ‚Äî B807's DRAFT WSE is what closes that half on Fort Bend Zone A; don't claim OVERSTATES disappears everywhere.
- PDF-PARITY: print sheet + card carry the chips.
- Dedupe/relatives: extends **B708** (Done ‚Äî anchored usable/dead split; the manual `tobElev` anchor this item auto-populates), **B709** (Done ‚Äî pondCriteriaRules registry), **B771** (Done ‚Äî the shipped FBCDD params), **B640** (Done ‚Äî the pond auto-size seam), **B805** (Done ‚Äî autoField chip pattern), **B807** (‚è≥ Verify ‚Äî the WSE provider), **B808** (Open ‚Äî grid dependency). Reuse `usablePondVolume`/`deadStoragePoolDepthFt`, don't fork.
- Acceptance (live): Bain ‚Äî a draw-only pond reads TOB/depth/floor/freeboard chips with cites and zero typing; the unanchored OVERSTATES gone; solver honors the clamp with its caveat; a Harris site shows Harris-record values.
- **IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-14 (parked ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V313).** `pondAutoValues` (detentionRules.js) = the ONE provenance seam (FBCDD DCM ¬ß6.4.7 verified freeboard via new additive `paramCites` ‚Üí B709 criteria record ‚Üí Planyr convention; TOB auto = 3DEP site median, labeled); `detWithAuto` feeds the split/excavation consumers (manual wins, √ó restores auto); inspector fields carry auto placeholders + source tags + the "Pond floor" (TOB ‚àí depth) row; depth = one-click B640 solver apply (an implicit depth would feed back into the volume it solves against ‚Äî documented deviation from "displayed not asked") with a loud slopes-collapse clamp caveat; the OVERSTATES counters split truly-unanchored vs anchored-but-WSE-unknown (own notes + sheet cautions, PDF-PARITY); Harris drawdown 96 h + orifice C=0.8 seeded `verified:false`. 7 new unit tests; headless verify-floodmit 13/13 (incl. the pool dead-storage note riding the auto anchor).

### B809 ‚Äî Fill-depth heat map overlay: render the exact cells the engine priced `[Site Planner / yield ¬∑ ui]` (feature) #site-planner #yield #floodplain #ui #export  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-13 as "NEW-3"; minted **B809**; the "don't trust numbers on a screen" layer)*
`[x]` Owner requirement: visual verification of what the tool thinks is being filled and mitigated ‚Äî no unverifiable scalar. Non-negotiable design principle: the overlay renders B808's retained cell array ‚Äî the same cells and depths that summed to the ledger volume, never a parallel computation ‚Äî so picture and number cannot diverge (the PDF-PARITY discipline applied to pixels). HARD dependency on B808: today `gridIntersect` discards cells and returns only aggregates (`lib/floodplainMitigation.js:117-143`), so there is nothing to render yet.
- Verify: live (zoom-/data-density-dependent rendering + PDF/export parity ‚Äî both LIVE-VERIFY classes).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-13 from chat ("NEW-3"); extends the B712 surfacing (which has NO map overlay or exhibit of the priced cells) over the B707 engine.
- **Overlay:** layers-panel toggle, default ON while the Yield mitigation section is open; cells colored by fill depth (sequential ramp, ~0.5 ft bins, legend in ft); zone-class distinguished ‚Äî floodway (`cls === "floodway"`) renders as hard-prohibited hatch, never a depth color. The cells live in SITE FEET, so the natural surface is the planner SVG canvas z-anchored like B739's composited layers (`buildExportSvg`, `SitePlanner.jsx:7123`), not a Leaflet layer; panel-toggle precedents: TERRAIN contours/flowdir (`lib/layers.js:233-244`) and the B753 corridor (`pipelineCorridor` + `vectorOverlay`).
- **Hover:** a cell ‚Üí depth, WSE (with provider tag incl. DRAFT), grade source (grid/median), cell area; a footprint ‚Üí its subtotal ac-ft.
- **Tie-out:** a readout chip pins the overlay total against the B712 card/ledger number ‚Äî identical by construction (same retained array) ‚Äî render both so the user SEES the tie-out.
- **UNKNOWN:** the engine's honest UNKNOWN bands render as grey diagonal hatch with the reason ‚Äî "not priced" is visible geography, not absence.
- **Print/export (PDF-PARITY):** the mitigation exhibit joins the sheet with legend + totals ‚Äî `buildExportSvg` compositing (B739/B745 precedent, LOUD `warnDroppedOverlays`) + `buildPrintSheetSvg`/`printSheetLayout` (`lib/printSheet.js`; B712 already put the mitigation summary in the print metricPairs).
- **Perf:** cells are already ‚â§1500/footprint√ózone ‚Äî draw to one canvas layer, re-render only on check/input change (ride the `mitigationForFootprint` LRU memo, `SitePlanner.jsx:652-684`).
- **Acceptance (live):** Bain ‚Äî overlay total equals ledger to the displayed precision; floodway hatch matches the NFHL floodway; toggling inputs (manual BFE) recolors and re-ties.

### B833 ‚Äî Transition wedges are real fill: daylight slopes join the proposed surface, the mitigation ledger, and the heat map ‚Äî and the engine daylights ASAP `[Site Planner / grading ¬∑ yield]` (feature) #site-planner #grading #yield #floodplain  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-14 as "NEW-14"; extends B826 (chat NEW-7); also carries chat NEW-13's berm-fill pricing leg; minted **B833**)*
`[x]` Owner scenario: building at 141‚Ä≤ in the floodplain, paving at 137‚Ä≤ adjacent, 136‚Ä≤ at the end of the court ‚Äî past the pavement edge the ground doesn't teleport to existing grade: a 3:1 wedge carries fill into the "green" area, and inside the mapped floodplain that wedge displaces storage and must be mitigated.
- Verify: live (real-project-data + heat-map rendering class ‚Äî Bain: tapered fringe wedges around every pad/court edge in the floodplain; mitigation volume rises vs the footprint-only number with the delta visible ("+X ac-ft from transition slopes"); steepening the tie-down record 4:1‚Üí3:1 shrinks it; no wedge crosses the parcel line without a flag).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-14 from chat ("NEW-14").
- **(a) Count it** ‚Äî today the flat-pad model prices only element footprints, so every daylight wedge is invisible ‚Üí the current mitigation volume is a FLOOR (label shipped 2026-07-14 with the B834 ledger work: the mitigation group now carries the one-line footprint-floor caveat until this lands). The proposed-surface engine generates transition aprons off every filled element edge (not just the parcel-line tie-downs): slope = the steepest maintainable from the grading records (3:1 default, 4:1 preference flag), width = drop √ó slope, terminating at the daylight line (proposed meets existing); wedge cells join the composite grid ‚Üí cut/fill, floodplain-fill pricing, and the heat map (the tapering fringe around pads ‚Äî exactly what the owner wants to SEE).
- **(b) Minimize it** ‚Äî within each surface's own slope rules, grade falls toward daylight at the max allowed rate ("get down to where we're not filling as soon as possible"): prefer dropping pavement to its slope ceiling toward the low/floodplain side before the apron starts, so the wedge starts lower and daylights sooner; where the daylight line would cross the parcel line or an easement, flag (fill can't run out of the property; retaining/wall fallback noted one-line + ‚ìò). Draw the daylight line on the map as a subtle contour so the owner can verify the no-more-fill boundary at a glance.
- **(c) Berm fill (chat NEW-13's earthwork leg):** berm earthwork joins the cut/fill balance as placed fill ‚Äî upland berms outside the floodplain are NOT mitigable fill; a berm whose toe crosses into the mapped floodplain flags loudly and prices its in-floodplain volume. (The berm auto-solve + one-click apply itself rides B830's amendment.)
- Cross-links (DEDUPE-FIRST, not dups): B826 (the engine it extends ‚Äî its earthwork "balance assist" is the cut/fill netting this feeds), B808/B809 (per-cell grid + heat map), B825 (grading records that supply the slopes), B830 (berm apply chip).
- **IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-14 (parked ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí V325).** **(a) Count it:** `surfaceGrid` grows transition APRONS off every graded edge ‚Äî per-element reach = worst edge |Œîz| √ó the tie ratio (`landscapeTieDown` 3:1 max default; 4:1 mowable via the new toggle); wedge cells taper the edge-vs-ground delta to zero at the ratio and DAYLIGHT where they meet (fill AND cut fringes); the wedge share is broken out (`wedgeFillCf/Cy` etc.) while riding the grid totals, so cut/fill rows, the cut/fill heat map, and the balance assist include it by construction. `wedgeMitigation` (new, `floodplainMitigation.js`) prices the fill fringe against the SAME zones/WSE provider chain (`zoneWaterSurface` extracted ‚Äî behavior-identical, test-guarded); the "+N ac-ft from transition slopes" delta renders in the mitigation group and the footprint-only FLOOR caveat now shows only when no surface exists. Wedge fill past the PL joins `plFillSf` (retaining-wall fallback named); a NEW `wedge-easement` violation fires when the fringe crosses a drawn easement. **(b) Minimize it:** the Daylight-ASAP toggle pins floating fields at their class slope ceiling toward the drainage target. **(c)** the daylight line renders as a subtle dashed contour on either exhibit (`[data-daylight]`), same taper model as the cells. **(e ‚Äî chat NEW-13's leg)** `bermFillVolume` (pondGeom): upland pond berms join the earthwork rows + net + balance target as placed fill; a toe crossing the mapped floodplain flags LOUDLY and prices its below-WSE share into the requirement (sampled toe fraction ‚Äî screening). 12 unit tests (`test/transitionWedges.test.js`, closed forms incl. perimeter √ó h¬≤¬∑ratio/2 ¬±, 4:1 > 3:1, daylight termination, aprons:false legacy escape, precedence); legacy B826 closed forms pinned `aprons:false`; headless `verify-auto-ffe` extended (wedge row, toggles, daylight paths, floor caveat gone, 4:1 grows 1,028‚Üí1,557 CY). Full suite + build green.
- **‚úÖ LIVE-VERIFIED ‚Äî PASSED V325 (VERIFICATION-DONE.md, 2026-07-17).**


### B815 ‚Äî Meeting calendars: recurrence + agenda-deadline rules for external decision bodies `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #entitlements #testing  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-13 as "NEW-1"; minted **B815**. ‚Üí V305. Implemented + shipped same session; the engine is fully unit-tested in the sandbox, parked for the live editor click-through.)*
`[x]` **Implemented this session.** The pure meeting-body cadence engine ‚Äî `subBD` ¬∑ `nthWeekdayOfMonth` ¬∑ `meetingDatesInRange` ¬∑ `agendaDeadline` ¬∑ `nextEligibleMeeting` ‚Äî added to `public/sequence/index.html`, mirrored **VERBATIM** into `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`, with **24 new unit tests + 4 anti-drift source guards** in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js` (full suite green, `npm run build` green, `MAP.md` regenerated, `eslint` 0 errors). Plus the per-project `proj.meetingBodies[]` model and an inline **Meeting calendars** editor (module-scope `MeetingCalendarsEditor` ‚Üí new `SectionHdr id="meetings"` in `SettingsPanel`, beside Holidays) with a **live next-6-meetings ‚Üí agenda-deadline preview**; `duplicateProject` deep-copies `meetingBodies`. The ENGINE itself is proven by the 24 sandbox unit tests (5-Tuesday months ¬∑ `setpos:-1` vs `4` ¬∑ month boundaries ¬∑ holiday-crossing lead times ¬∑ blackout/extra precedence ¬∑ UTC serialization).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-13 from chat ("NEW-1"); first of the meeting-cadence epic B815 ‚Üí B816 ‚Üí B817 (owner scratched the original "NEW-4" seed-library item as not-a-dependency).

A live cross-project reference is forbidden ‚Äî editing Baytown's calendar in one project must never silently move another project's hearings (explicit-sharing rule).

Explicit dates beat the rule; the preview carries a provenance label ("rule-derived, unverified" vs "verified &lt;date&gt;") per the standing rule.

### B819 ‚Äî False "open in another tab" banner on the Library with only ONE tab open `[App Shell / Library]` (bug) #ui #infra #persistence  *(owner-reported 2026-07-13 from chat ‚Äî screenshot of the Library showing "‚ßâ This project is open in another tab. Only one tab can edit at a time; the others are read-only‚Ä¶" with the owner unsure any second tab was even open. Branch `claude/library-popup-issue-4oh86u`. Minted B818 via `npm run next-id`; RENUMBERED ‚Üí **B819** on merge-in of `origin/main`, which took B816‚ÄìB818 + V306/V307 for the scheduler cadence work. ‚Üí V308.)*
`[x]` **FIXED this session ‚Äî root-caused, fixed at the pure-protocol level, and PROVEN with a standalone repro (bug reproduced true, fix drives it false) + a new regression unit test; full suite green (3755), `eslint` 0 errors, build green, lazy chunks intact.**
- **AUDIT-FIRST root cause ‚Äî a regression from B669's keep-alive interacting with B313's presence banner.** The Shell keeps every VISITED workspace mounted-but-hidden for instant switching (B669, `Shell.jsx:145`), so a SINGLE tab can have several `AppHeader`s alive at once (e.g. the Library header + a kept-alive Review header on the same project). Each header runs its own `useMultiTab` ‚Üí `createMultiTabPresence` (B313), and each got a **fresh random `tabId` per instance** (`multiTab.js`). Because `BroadcastChannel` delivers messages between instances in the **same document** (not only across real tabs), those same-tab headers announced the same project id and saw each other as a *different* tab ‚Üí `summarizePresence` set `conflictRisk:true` ‚Üí the banner fired with only one real tab open. Compounding it, the banner's "the others are **read-only** until you take over editing there" text describes a lockout the **Library doesn't have** (per-file/per-row ops, no single-active-editor lock), so it was doubly wrong there.
- **The fix (two parts, both minimal):**
  1. *`src/shared/presence/multiTab.js` ‚Äî ONE presence identity per browsing context.* Added a module-scoped `documentTabId` (lazy `getDocumentTabId()`); `createMultiTabPresence` now uses `opts.tabId || getDocumentTabId()`. A module singleton is per-realm = per-tab: every instance in one tab shares the id (the existing line-71 self-echo guard then makes them ignore each other), while a genuinely separate tab is a fresh realm with its own id ‚Äî so the real cross-tab warning still fires. Tests pass an explicit `opts.tabId` to simulate distinct tabs inside one realm.
  2. *`src/workspaces/library/Library.jsx` ‚Äî `multiEditOk` on the Library's `AppHeader`.* The Library supports concurrent multi-tab use (no read-only lockout), so the B313 warning is inapplicable and must not show even across two genuine tabs ‚Äî same contract the Site Planner uses via `multiwriterEnabled()`.
- Verify: live ‚Üí **V308** ‚Äî the banner renders only for **signed-in** accounts (`accountActive`), and the sandbox blocks sign-in, so the signed-in end-to-end (open the Library on a project after visiting Review in the same tab ‚Üí NO banner) parks for a live click-through. The banner *logic* is proven in the sandbox (unit test + standalone protocol repro below).
- Origin: owner-reported 2026-07-13 (single-tab false banner on the Library).
- **Named rules:** AUDIT-FIRST (traced the actual delivery mechanism ‚Äî BroadcastChannel delivers same-document ‚Äî and reconciled B313's per-instance-id assumption against the post-B669 kept-alive reality); LIVE-VERIFY (signed-in-only render ‚Üí V308); MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS n/a. No `MAP.md` change (no file add/remove/rename, no primary-export change).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new. **NOT B759** (that is the Site Planner's element-sync realtime toast "you (another window) changed X" in `elementSync.js` over Supabase Realtime ‚Äî different module, message, transport, and workspace). Thematically the sibling of the B759 "false another-tab warning in one tab" family; this one is the B313 BroadcastChannel presence banner, regressed by B669's keep-alive. Related: B313 (added the banner), B669 (keep-alive), B674 (`multiEditOk`).
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/multiTab.test.js` +1 regression case ("two instances in the SAME document never mistake each other for a second tab") ‚Äî and the two existing real-BroadcastChannel round-trip tests updated to pass explicit distinct tabIds (correctly modelling two real tabs). A standalone node repro against the real `createMultiTabPresence`: distinct per-instance ids in one doc ‚Üí `conflictRisk=true` (the bug); shared per-document identity ‚Üí `conflictRisk=false` (fixed). Full suite **3755** green ¬∑ `eslint` 0 errors ¬∑ `npm run build` green ¬∑ Library + AppHeader remain separate lazy chunks.
