### B788 ‚Äî Route FEMA flood + other rasters through the same-origin cache proxy with a BOUNDED upstream timeout so a degraded source fails open fast, never blank `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) #site-planner #gis #infra #floodplain  *(coworker live-diagnosis handoff 2026-07-12 as "NEW-1"; late-bound to **B788 / V288** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Origin: filed 2026-07-12 from chat. Same branch as B790. AUDIT-FIRST: the durable half was already built ‚Äî see below. Implemented + sandbox-verified same session.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED (the missing guardrail) ‚Äî the server-side GIS cache proxy now bounds every upstream agency fetch (`UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT_MS = 25 s`), so a degraded source (FEMA held/killed exports 20‚Äì30 s on 2026-07-11) can't hang the Function to the platform subrequest ceiling; it fails OPEN fast instead.** AUDIT-FIRST finding that reshaped this item: the handoff's open question ("does the FEMA export already route through `/api/gis-cache`?") is **YES** ‚Äî `syncOverlayLayers` routes rasters through the B445 proxy by default (`gisProxyEnabled()`), and the proxy ALREADY serves the last-good Drive copy instantly on a cache HIT and refreshes in the background (the stale-while-revalidate "never blank a previously-seen area" behavior NEW-1 asked for **already exists**). So the durable win was present; the only real gap was an unbounded server fetch on the FIRST-EVER (uncached) view of an area during an outage.
- **Fix:** a shared `fetchUpstream(fetchImpl, url, timeoutMs)` helper (`functions/api/gis-cache/_handler.js`) wraps both upstream fetches (cache-miss + background refresh) in an `AbortController` timeout; `handleGisCache` takes an injectable `upstreamTimeoutMs`. On timeout the existing fail-open path runs (cache-miss ‚Üí 302 to the real agency; refresh ‚Üí keep the copy already served). The cache-HIT serve-last-good path is untouched ‚Äî the 25 s cap only ever affects the uncached first view.
- **Honest residual limit (documented, not hidden):** the truly-first-ever view of an area during an outage still can't paint (there is no last-good copy to serve, and no client/proxy trick invents one) ‚Äî it now fails open *promptly* instead of hanging. Every previously-viewed area is immune.
- Verify: live (server-side proxy behavior against a degraded source ‚Äî can only be confirmed with the live edge + a real slow/again-degraded FEMA; the sandbox can't reach the agency or the deployed Function) ‚Üí **V288**.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** hardens **B445** (Done ‚Äî the cache proxy itself) and is the map-overlay sibling of **B367** (Done ‚Äî the *screening* SWR cache). Not **B371** (Later ‚Äî regional pre-cache snapshot).
- Sandbox: 2 new `test/gisCacheHandler.test.js` cases (a hanging agency + a tiny cap ‚Üí 302 fail-open; the upstream fetch carries an `AbortSignal`) + the existing 13 handler cases green (the UA-header test unaffected). Full suite green ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green.
- **‚úÖ LIVE-VERIFIED ‚Äî PASSED V288 (VERIFICATION-DONE.md, 2026-07-17).**


### B790 ‚Äî FEMA flood zones showed a healthy "loaded" (blue) dot over a BLANK map during a source slowdown ‚Äî honest per-layer "source slow / data may be missing" status `[Site Planner / layers panel]` (bug/feature) #site-planner #gis #ui #floodplain  *(coworker live-diagnosis handoff 2026-07-12 as "NEW-3"; late-bound to **B790 / V290** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Origin: filed 2026-07-12 from chat. Branch `claude/nfhl-service-degradation-uy5fi1`. Implemented + sandbox-verified same session per STANDING RULE #1; parked here for the live "row goes amber on a real stall" check.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî a toggled-on raster overlay no longer reports "loaded" (blue) the instant it's added; it stays "loading" until the server-rendered image ACTUALLY lands, and flips to amber "source slow or unavailable ‚Äî the map may be missing data" if the export never arrives.** This is the owner's exact complaint from the 2026-07-11 NFHL slowdown ("FEMA showing blue as if it's working, but nothing paints"). AUDIT-FIRST root cause found in the live code: `syncOverlayLayers` (`lib/layers.js`) called `onStatus(k, "loaded")` **optimistically on `addTo(map)`**, before the export `<img>` resolved ‚Äî and esri-leaflet fires NO error for a silent hang, so a degraded agency (FEMA held/killed exports 20‚Äì30 s) left the row on a false blue forever.
- **Fix:** extracted the status machine into a pure, unit-testable `wireRasterStatus` (`lib/layerRequest.js`, leaflet-free): status starts at "loading"; only a real `'load'` event ‚Üí "loaded"; a **stall watchdog** (`RASTER_STALL_MS = 15 s`) flips to a NEW amber `"slow"` state when no `'load'` lands in time; a real error event ‚Üí "failed" (or one proxy‚Üídirect fallback first, which no longer claims a premature "loaded"); a later `'load'` clears "slow" back to "loaded" (recovery on a pan). `layers.js` now wires through it and reports "loading" (not "loaded") on add.
- **Amber, not red (KEY DECISIONS palette rule):** RED (`--danger`) is reserved for a genuine error; a stall is "too slow, data may be missing," which is retryable ‚Äî so `LayerPanel` renders `"slow"` on `--warn-text` amber with an inline reason, and `combineLayerStatus` ranks it just under "failed".
- Verify: live (GIS-endpoint-behavior + zoom/data-density rendering class ‚Äî the sandbox has no egress to `hazards.fema.gov` (HTTP 000), so a REAL stalled export flipping the actual on-map row to amber can only be seen on planyr.io) ‚Üí **V290**.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** the map-panel counterpart to **B366** (Done ‚Äî the Site Analysis *screen's* UNAVAILABLE state); no existing item makes the *layers-panel* raster status honest. Relates to but does not duplicate **B717** (panel layout/floating) ‚Äî that's chrome, this is status truth. Not **B739** (Done/Verify ‚Äî export composite; its loud drop is correct and untouched).
- Sandbox: 12 new `wireRasterStatus` unit cases in `test/layerRequest.test.js` (no optimistic "loaded"; watchdog‚Üíslow; load clears it; proxy fallback once; onRemove/isActive gating; recovery) + `layerPanelInfo` "slow" precedence. Full suite **3,620 green** ‚Äî lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP.md regenerated (new `wireRasterStatus`/`RASTER_STALL_MS` exports).
- **‚úÖ LIVE-VERIFIED ‚Äî PASSED V290 (VERIFICATION-DONE.md, 2026-07-17).**


### B877 ‚Äî Harden the reconciliation audit: MERGED ‚âÝ LIVE (an item is "live" only with a PASSED V#) `[Site Planner / process]` (task) #site-planner #testing #infra  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-16 post-ship as "NEW-5"; minted **B877** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Branch `claude/detention-verdict-delta-bugs-l3mm1l`.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the rule + reasoning are inspectable; no live gate).** Why tonight's surprise happened: the B873 pre-pass audit reported B867's surface + B868/B869/B875 as "shipped-and-live," and the Cowork pass found them non-functional on the page. The audit's deploy-currency sub-check PASSED while features it blessed were dead.
- **The gap (explained):** B873 checked **presence of code/commits + a current Cloudflare deploy** ‚Äî i.e. that the bytes are *served* ‚Äî which **no static audit can extend to runtime behavior.** A real ambient bug (B874's stuck flood-refresh) froze/starved the very features the audit blessed. "Merged to main" and "deployed" are necessary-not-sufficient for "works."
- **The hardening (shipped):** added a standing rule to `CLAUDE.md` (under the three-state lifecycle) ‚Äî **a reconciliation/audit may call a `Verify: live` item "live" ONLY when it has a PASSED `V###` on record.** Three distinct states, never two, never conflating the middle with the last: `üî≤ Open/unshipped` ¬∑ `‚è≥ merged ‚Äî V### PENDING` ¬∑ `‚úÖ live (V### PASSED, dated)`. "Merged" is only ever reported **`merged ‚Äî live-verify pending`**; the words "live"/"confirmed"/"working" require the dated PASS note in `VERIFICATION.md`. When an audit can't run the live check itself (sandbox / signed-in / real-project-data), it says so and reports `merged ‚Äî V### pending`, never green.
- **Audit-checklist effect:** B873's step (e) "deploy check" now explicitly proves *served-current*, NOT *working* ‚Äî it can never again be read as a live-blessing. Future reconciliations cite the merged-‚âÝ-live rule.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new process rule; the concrete cause of the B873 false all-clear.
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-17** (branch `claude/detention-verdict-delta-bugs-l3mm1l`).

### B855 ‚Äî Task DURATIONS can count CALENDAR days (type `cd`), like the predecessor lag `cd` (B854) `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #ui #testing  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15, right after B854 merged: "add an option to use 'cd' for durations too." Minted **B855** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Filed AND shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the pure resolve is unit-tested [exact calendar window, weekend-crossing, cd-vs-d divergence, milestone/no-start, cascade]; the editor + master-grid fix verified by a 4-check headless Playwright smoke; screenshot delivered.)** Durations typed `10d`/`3w` count WORKING days and `2mo`/`1y` are calendar-real; now `30cd` is a **30-calendar-day window (weekends counted)** ‚Äî the natural way to express statutory periods (a "30 calendar day protest period").
- **`cd` is a third calendar-based unit beside `mo`/`y`** in `resolveDuration` (both `public/sequence/index.html` + verbatim mirror `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`): `end = addD(start, value-1)` (an EXACT window, weekends counted, **no** roll-forward ‚Äî matching how the predecessor `cd` counts straight days; it may end on a weekend, which the app tolerates), and the canonical working-day `duration` is DERIVED via `workdaysBetween` (never an input) exactly like `mo`/`y`. `DUR_UNIT_ALIASES` gains `cd|cds|calday|caldays|caldy`. Because every date-math consumer reads the resolved `end`/`duration` and display uses `fmtTaskDuration` (value+unit), the unit is fully absorbed ‚Äî no other consumer branch. Anti-drift `.toMatch` guards in both files.
- **Discoverability:** the main-grid duration cell gains a placeholder `e.g. 10d, 3w, 30cd, 2mo`; `durTooltip` gains a `cd` case ‚Üí hover shows "30 calendar days (weekends counted) ‚âà N working days"; the two `parseDurationInput` error hints list `cd`. `fmtTaskDuration` already re-emits `30cd`; the commit path (`parseDurationInput` ‚Üí `updateTask({durValue,durUnit})` ‚Üí `resolveDuration`) needed no change.
- **Bonus AUDIT-FIRST fix ‚Äî the cross-project "master" grid duration editor was unit-BLIND** (`parseInt(val)` silently dropped any suffix; hardcoded `${t.duration}d` display), so typing `10cd`/`2w`/`3mo` there silently stored the number as working days. Rewired to `parseDurationInput` + `fmtTaskDuration` (+ its autosize), matching the project grid ‚Äî a LOUD-FAILURE trap closed for `cd` and for `w`/`mo`/`y` too. Updated the anti-drift guard that pinned the old `parseInt` clamp.
- **Deliberately unchanged:** the print/PDF/cascade-banner spots that render the derived working-day count with a literal `d` already show `w`/`mo`/`y` that way ‚Äî `cd` is consistent (a `cd` task prints its working-day equivalent). The on-screen main grid (what Michael edits) shows `30cd` + the conversion tooltip.
- **Verify:** 4079 tests + build green; JSX parse green; headless smoke ‚Äî `30cd` on a leaf task shows "30cd", tooltip "30 calendar days ‚âà 20 working days", Finish 01/25/27 vs `30d` at 02/05/27 (cd ends sooner, exact window); zero page errors.
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`, restarted from latest main).

### B854 ‚Äî Predecessor lags can count CALENDAR days (type `cd`), for statutory "N days before/after" timing `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #ui #testing  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 alongside a deadline-cascade question, on the Goose Creek "MUD Creation" schedule: "ADD A FEATURE WHERE AS IM TYPING THE PREDECESSOR, THERE'S A WAY TO MAKE IT USE CALENDAR DAYS INSTEAD OF REGULAR DAYS." Extends the B815‚ÄìB853 scheduler epic. Minted **B854** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Filed AND shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the pure engine is unit-tested [calendar vs working FS/SS, weekend-crossing, negative lags, unit round-trips] with anti-drift mirror guards; the editor verified by a 3-check headless Playwright smoke driving the real grid; screenshot delivered.)** Every predecessor lag used to count WORKING days only (weekends + holidays skipped, via `addBD`). Statutory deadlines like "78 calendar days before an election" or "130 calendar days after X" need straight days ‚Äî now you type a `cd` suffix.
- **Syntax:** `85FS+130cd` = 130 **calendar** days after task 85 finishes (weekends counted); `85FS+130d` / `85FS+130` = working days (unchanged default). A per-predecessor optional `lagUnit:"calendar"` ‚Äî omitted for business, so stored data + existing tests are byte-identical.
- **Engine (both `public/sequence/index.html` + the verbatim `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`):** `parsePreds` tokenizes the `(cd|d)?` suffix; `normPreds` carries `lagUnit` through; `constrainedStartFrom` applies the lag with `addD` (calendar) vs `addBD` (working) ‚Äî the FS "next business day" base step stays business either way, so the working-day result is unchanged. `fmtPreds` (source-only) re-emits `cd`. Follows the `agendaLead.unit` (business/calendar) + `durUnit` (text-suffix) precedents. Anti-drift `.toMatch` guards added in both files.
- **Editor UX (`PredEditor`, new module-scope component):** an uncontrolled input (never fights the caret) with a live **plain-English readback** below it ‚Äî "After task 85 finishes, then 130 calendar days" ‚Äî plus a tip: "'d' = working days ¬∑ 'cd' = calendar days (weekends counted)". The cell drops its `overflow:hidden` only while being edited so the hint escapes the 24px row. The committed chip shows `85FS+130cd`; its hover tooltip repeats the plain English so the owner can confirm it took. New `data-col-key` grid attribute (test hook, consistent with `data-task-row`/`data-picker-cell`).
- **Companion decision (NO code):** the same owner message asked to "set the deadline as a successor and cascade it that way." After discussing, the owner chose **plain tasks, no alarm** ‚Äî normal cascading tasks (MUD forms ‚Üí Call Election ‚Üí Conduct Election), which already cascade. No engine change; the shipped deadline-row feature (B852) stays as-is and available. Guidance handed to the owner: right-click a deadline row ‚Üí "Unlink (keep date)" turns it back into a normal task, then set its predecessor.
- **Verify:** 4073 tests + build green; JSX parse green; headless smoke confirmed the live calendar/working hint + the committed `1FS+5cd` chip on the real grid; zero page errors (only sandbox Supabase-offline network noise).
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`, restarted from latest main).

### B853 ‚Äî Meeting-calendars window: explicit Save (draft/apply gate) instead of silent live-apply `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15: "add a save option to it so its clear that the changes will be implemented" ‚Äî the editor applied every keystroke live, so it never felt like anything "took". Extends the B815‚ÄìB852 meeting-calendars epic. Minted **B853** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Filed AND shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî JSX parse + 4064 tests + build green + an 11-check headless Playwright smoke of the full Save/Discard/dirty-guard flow; screenshot delivered).** `MeetingCalendarsModal` now edits a **working copy** ‚Äî nothing touches the schedule until **Save changes**, making the apply moment unmistakable.
- The modal holds an internal `draft` (deep clone of `bodies` on mount); every editor helper (`patch`/`patchRuleAt`/`addRuleAt`/`removeRuleAt`/`patchLead`/`add`/`remove`) mutates the draft via `setDraft` ‚Äî the parent `onChange` (which applies + re-cascades) runs ONCE, on Save. `savedRef` snapshots the last-applied JSON for the `dirty` flag.
- **Footer:** a live state line ("‚óè Unsaved changes ‚Äî nothing on the schedule has moved yet" / "‚úì Saved ‚Äî the schedule updated" / "All changes saved") + **Cancel/Close** and **Save changes** (enabled only when dirty). Save applies, flips to the Saved flash, and resets dirty.
- **Unsaved-close guard:** the ‚úï, backdrop click, and Escape route through `requestClose` ‚Äî with pending edits they raise an inline **Keep editing / Discard / Save & close** bar instead of silently dropping the work; a clean modal still closes instantly.
- Behavioral proof (smoke): editing a body's lead from 78‚Üí30 days leaves the grid on 08/16/27 (unchanged) until Save, which then moves the deadline row to 10/03/27; Discard after a further edit keeps the last-saved 10/03/27.
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`, restarted from main post-#641).

### B852 ‚Äî Deadline rows: the call/file-by deadline becomes a REAL task on the grid; coherent bind flyout; pred-less bound-task ratchet fix `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #ui #testing  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 with two screenshots: the bind flyout showed "Unbind from X" AND a ‚úì-marked "X" simultaneously ("think thru how counterintuitive this is"), and "what is the point of the agenda section if it doesn't show up as a task??? ‚Ä¶would be more useful if it would either bind to an existing task or create a task that shows up on the grid". Extends the B815‚ÄìB849 meeting-calendars epic. Minted **B852** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Filed AND shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the cascade/renumber/health logic is fully unit-tested [23 new tests incl. idempotency] with anti-drift mirror guards; the UI verified by a 15-check headless Playwright smoke; screenshot delivered).** The "call the election ‚â•78 days before election day" deadline now lives ON the schedule, and the bind menu has one coherent model.
- **Deadline rows (`deadlineForTaskId`):** right-click a bound task ‚Üí **"+ Add deadline row"** creates a milestone ("Deadline ‚Äî ‚ü®body‚ü©") directly above it, or right-click any unbound row ‚Üí **"Use as deadline row for ‚ü®bound task‚ü©"** links an existing task. The row's date is ONE-WAY derived from the anchor's `meetingDeadline` in a `cascadeDates` post-pass (both files, anti-drift guarded) ‚Äî it can never diverge and rolls with the election. Deliberately NOT a predecessor link (circular with meeting-eligibility). Derived `deadlineInfeasible` (row's own preds land after the call-by date) ‚Üí red via a new `computeDisplayHealth` branch; flag glyph + plain-English tooltip in the Start cell; read-only "deadline ¬∑ ‚ü®body‚ü©" in the Meeting column; "Unlink (keep current date)" to detach. Mutually exclusive with `meetingBound`.
- **`renumberTasks` now remaps ALL task-id pointers** ‚Äî `deadlineForTaskId` (dangling ‚Üí null on anchor delete) **and the pre-existing `minMeetingsAfter.taskId`, which was MISSING from the remap** (latent re-pointing bug on any insert/delete/indent; fixed in both files, unit-tested).
- **Bind flyout redesign:** the parent item now reads the row's state ("Meeting: ‚ü®body‚ü©" / "Deadline for: ‚ü®anchor‚ü©" / "Bind to meeting calendar"); the flyout is one radio list ("Snap this row to": ‚Äî none ‚Äî + bodies, ‚úì on current). The contradictory "Unbind from X" + ‚úì"X" pair is gone. Multi-row semantics kept.
- **Cadence edits re-cascade LIVE:** the modal's onChange now re-cascades the project (same discipline as the Holidays editor) ‚Äî changing a lead from 78‚Üí30 days moves bound tasks + deadline rows immediately, not on the next unrelated edit.
- **‚öÝ Pre-existing B816 bug found by the smoke and FIXED: pred-less bound-task RATCHET.** `applyMeetingBinding` fell back to the task's own snapped start as "packet ready"; since a deadline always precedes its meeting, the current meeting was never self-eligible ‚Äî every cascade rolled the task one meeting forward (repro: bind ‚Üí add row ‚Üí edit lead = 3 elections of silent drift, 2027‚Üí2029). Fix: a fixed-point guard ‚Äî with no external constraint, a start that IS one of the body's meetings is kept (blacked-out dates still release forward). Unit-tested for idempotency across repeated cascades, in both files, anti-drift guarded.
- **Modal copy:** "Agenda deadline" ‚Üí "Deadline before each meeting (file the agenda / call the election by)" + a hint pointing at "+ Add deadline row".
- **Verify:** 4060 tests + build green; smoke: created the 78-calendar-day election body ‚Üí bound a task (snapped 11/02/27) ‚Üí deadline row at 08/16/27 ‚Üí lead 78‚Üí30 ‚Üí row moved LIVE to 10/03/27 with the anchor unmoved (no ratchet) ‚Üí unlink offered; zero page errors.
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`, restarted from main post-#638).

### B849 ‚Äî Meeting-calendars editor legibility: drop presets, plain-English "Tuesday after the first Monday" control, live "Resolves to ‚Üí next dates" per rule `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as three linked complaints on the B848 window: "get rid of the presets"; "the next 6 dates seem wrong / how do I know it's taken effect"; and ‚Äî with a screenshot of the "on or after day [2]" field ‚Äî "this input doesn't really translate to normal English". Extends B848 / the B815‚ÄìB817/B844/B845 meeting-calendars epic. Minted **B849** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Filed AND shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî esbuild JSX parse + 4033 tests + build green + a 12-check headless Playwright smoke driving the real window; screenshot delivered to the owner).** The cadence editor never told the owner, in plain English, what a rule *resolves to* ‚Äî so a rule that was silently "1st Tue of every month" looked like a broken preview, and the "on or after day N" primitive read as engineer-speak.
- **Removed the preset buttons** (`MB_PRESETS` + the modal's `setRules` helper deleted). The Texas-election quick-buttons were a mystery-box; their one real value (a correct Election-Day config) is now a clearly-labelled control (below).
- **Replaced the cryptic `‚òê on or after day [N]` numeric field** with a plain-English checkbox: **"Use the 'Tuesday after the first Monday' rule ‚Äî the U.S. / Texas Election Day pattern (defaults to November; the plain '1st Tuesday' is wrong when the 1st is a Tuesday)."** Ticking it sets `{weekday:2, setpos:[1], onOrAfter:2, months:[11]-or-current}`; in that mode the weekday dropdown + Weeks chips are hidden (implied) and the top row reads "‚Äî the Tuesday after the first Monday". (The engine still stores `onOrAfter` generally; only the UI is specialised to the one real use.)
- **Every rule card now shows a live `Resolves to <plain English> ‚Äî next: <3 real dates>` line** (`mbRuleSummary` extracted from `cadenceSummary` at module scope, with the "Tue after the 1st Mon" construct named), and a **"This body meets <summary>"** line sits above the "Next 6 meetings" preview. This makes each rule independently verifiable ‚Äî the fix for both "seems wrong" (a monthly rule now visibly says "1st Tue monthly") and "how do I know it took effect" (English + dates update live on every edit).
- **Verify:** the Election-Day control resolves to "Tue after the 1st Mon of Nov ‚Äî next: Nov 3 2026 ¬∑ Nov 2 2027 ¬∑ Nov 7 2028", confirmed live in the headless smoke; live-update on tick/untick confirmed; 4033 tests + build green.
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`, restarted from main post-#636).

It now lives in its own centered window.
- **New `MeetingCalendarsModal`** (module-scope, replaces `MeetingCalendarsEditor`): HistoryPanel-class shell (`min(860px,96vw) √ó min(640px,90vh)`) with NotesModal's close hardening (mousedown-on-backdrop tracking so a text-selection drag can't dismiss; capture-phase Escape). **Master-detail:** left rail lists the project's bodies (name + plain-English `cadenceSummary` one-liner, accent bar on the selected one, "+ Add meeting body" pinned at the bottom); detail pane gives the selected body a real form ‚Äî Name/Jurisdiction/Remove on one row, MEETS section (presets on one row, per-rule cards with Weeks/Months chip rows that no longer wrap, on-or-after control), AGENDA DEADLINE on one line, CUTOFF/same-day, EXCEPTIONS & SOURCE in a 2-col grid, and the "Next 6 meetings ‚Üí file by" preview as a tinted band. The `setData` write path + `rebuildMeetingBodyIndex` call carried over verbatim; all data helpers unchanged (no engine/mirror/test impact).

Branch `claude/loving-albattani-mdqvse`.)*

### B844 ‚Äî Right-click "Bind to meeting calendar" + make meeting calendars findable `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #entitlements #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-1"; minted **B844** via `npm run next-id`. Extends the B815/B816/B817 meeting-calendars epic ‚Äî not a parallel feature.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî JSX parse-checked via esbuild + a headless Playwright render/click smoke; deterministic client-side flow, no auth/live-edge path).** The cadence engine had no front door: binding a task meant defining a body in Settings ‚öô ‚Üí Meeting calendars AND un-hiding the hidden-by-default "Meeting" grid column. Now you bind straight from the task right-click menu, and the entry points are discoverable.

With no bodies yet, the item reads "Add a meeting calendar‚Ä¶" and deep-links to the editor.

Inline editors only (no modal) preserved.
- **Bonus fix found via the headless smoke:** the grid's visible-columns `useMemo` (L8299) didn't list `proj.meetingBodies` / `proj.tasks` in its deps, so the Meeting (and the pre-existing B817 Float/Cost) auto-shows went stale and never appeared until an unrelated column change forced a recompute ‚Äî added both deps.
- **Verify:** 4008 tests green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless smoke green (Settings deep-link + auto-shown column [30 bound dropdowns] + Bind flyout all confirmed in a real browser).
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`).

### B845 ‚Äî Government date patterns for the meeting-cadence engine (month restriction + "Tuesday after the first Monday" + TX uniform-election-date presets) `[Scheduler]` (feature) #scheduler #entitlements #testing  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-2"; minted **B845** via `npm run next-id`. Extends the B815/B816/B817 meeting-calendars epic; distinct from the roadmap "user-editable schedule conventions" item, which is working-week/holidays, not cadences.)*

Now it can restrict a rule to specific months / once-a-year from the UI (the previously-unused `r.months` field is exposed), and it has an Election-Day primitive: "1st Tuesday on/after Nov 2" (the correct reading of "Tuesday after the first Monday", which "1st Tuesday of November" gets wrong whenever Nov 1 is a Tuesday ‚Äî a week early in 2033/2039).
- Engine: added `nthWeekdayOnOrAfter(y,m,weekday,dom,nth)` to `public/sequence/index.html` AND its verbatim mirror `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`; `meetingDatesInRange`'s monthly branch routes `r.onOrAfter != null` rules through it. `cadenceSummary` rewritten to describe every rule + month/on-after honestly (source-only; not mirrored).

Per monthly rule: a 12-month "only in months" picker (blank = every month, one = yearly), an "on or after day N" control, and one-click presets incl. "Election Day ¬∑ Nov" and "TX uniform election dates (May + Nov)" (Tex. Elec. Code ¬ß41.001). The "next 6 meetings ‚Üí file by" preview reflects the new rule types automatically.

- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/meeting-calendars-binding-7qaedb`).

### B843 ‚Äî Terrain contour/flow layers share B36(e)'s stale-render-after-unmount gap (audit the whole view-driven-layer class) `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ terrain]` (bug) #site-planner #gis  *(found 2026-07-15 while fixing B36(e) ‚Äî the terrain layers copied the same overpass "moveend refresh + busy/pending + gisCache.swr" pattern; minted **B842**, RENUMBERED **B843** on merge ‚Äî PR #630 (the B838 viewport audit) took B842 for its "keep-alive tab-return invalidateSize" item + V333 for its Doc Review fix; my V333‚ÜíV333 too. Filed AND shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1.)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: live ‚Äî V333; Leaflet can't load in the node-only test env, so no unit test).** `terrainLayer` (`lib/terrainLayers.js` ‚Äî the factory behind `contourLayer` + `flowLayer`, the B703‚ÄìB706 terrain overlays) checked `if (!map) return` only BEFORE its `await fresh`, so a job that resolved AFTER the layer was toggled off (`onRemove` nulls `map`) still ran `paint()` ‚Üí `group.clearLayers()` + `render()` into the detached group + `onStatus("loaded")` for an off layer. The window is LARGER than evidence's ‚Äî the terrain job is a heavy DEM decode + contour/flow compute, not a quick fetch.
- **Fix:** one post-`await` `if (!map) return` mount guard (after `busy = false`), identical to B36(e)'s overpass path. Like overpass, the terrain fetch rides `gisCache.swr` (cached), so it is intentionally NOT aborted (that would poison the shared cache) ‚Äî just not painted. Documented inline.
- **Class audit (the "fix the class once" half ‚Äî grepped every `moveend` view-driven layer under `site-planner/lib`):** `evidenceLayers.js` overpass+Mapillary ‚Üí fixed in B36(e); `terrainLayers.js` ‚Üí this fix; **`vectorOverlay.js` (both the boundary + pipeline/corridor layers) were ALREADY correct** ‚Äî they use the stronger `mySeq = ++seq` supersession token + `!map` check after every await AND an `onRemove` `seq++` invalidation (the model pattern); **`parcelDisplay.js`** refresh is fully synchronous (reads an in-memory snapshot, no await) so it can't strand a stale async paint; `layers.js`'s esri-leaflet layers manage their own lifecycle (+ the B55 `map._loaded` probe guard). So after this, no view-driven layer paints a stale response into a detached group.
- **Verify:** lint 0 ¬∑ **3998 tests** (no regression) ¬∑ build green. **V333** logs the live check (toggle the terrain contour/flow layer off mid-compute ‚Üí no stale contours/arrows appear, no "loaded" for the off layer).
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/next-backlog-item-2lots0`).

**B36(e) ‚Äî Abort in-flight evidence-layer fetches + guard against stale post-unmount renders** `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) #site-planner #gis  *(the fetch-AbortController sub-item of the 2026-06-15 bug-audit B36 cluster ‚Äî recorded as a bold entry, NOT a `### B36` heading, so it doesn't read as a duplicate id; parent B36 stays OPEN for a/d ‚Äî the county-resolution work tied to B13)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: live ‚Äî V331; the Leaflet layers can't load in the node-only test env, so no unit test).** The two view-driven evidence overlays (`lib/evidenceLayers.js` ‚Äî OSM Overpass power/hydrants, Mapillary detections) checked `if (!map) return` only BEFORE their `await`, so a fetch that resolved AFTER the layer was toggled off (`onRemove` nulls `map`) still ran `group.clearLayers()`, drew markers into the detached group, and fired `onStatus("loaded")` for a layer the user had turned off ‚Äî and nothing cancelled the now-useless request.
- **Fix:** (1) a post-`await` `if (!map) return` mount guard in BOTH `overpassLayer` and `mapillaryLayer`, so a stale response never paints/reports into a detached group. (2) An `AbortController` on the Mapillary path (a direct, un-cached `fetch`): `fetchMapillary(bounds, token, signal)` forwards the signal; `onRemove` aborts the in-flight request; the catch swallows the resulting `AbortError`/`sig.aborted` silently (the removal owns the UI ‚Äî no false "failed", no `lastKey` reset that `onRemove` already did). The Overpass path rides `gisCache.swr` (cached), so it is intentionally NOT aborted ‚Äî aborting a cached fetch would poison the shared SWR cache; the mount guard alone is correct there (the request still warms the cache but never renders detached). Documented inline.
- **Verify:** lint 0 ¬∑ 3989 tests (no regression) ¬∑ build green. Leaflet needs a DOM (`window is not defined` in the node test env ‚Äî the existing suite mocks these modules), so the layer lifecycle can't be unit-tested here; **V331** logs the live check (toggle the OSM/Mapillary evidence layer off mid-load ‚Üí no stale markers appear, no "loaded" flips on for the off layer, and the request shows cancelled in the network panel).
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/next-backlog-item-2lots0`).

### B255 ‚Äî Collapse the duplicate indent/outdent + column-autosize functions `[Scheduler / code health]` (task) #scheduler  *(orig "M2"; minted **B255**)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: live ‚Äî scheduler runs on planyr.io, not runnable in the sandbox; V327 logs the click-through).** Two near-identical copies each of: indent/outdent (keyboard `indentTask`/`outdentTask` vs right-click `indentTaskById`/`outdentTaskById`) and column autosize (`autoSizeCol` grid vs `autoSizeMCol` master), in `public/sequence/index.html`.
- **Equivalence verified first (as required):** read all four. **indent/outdent** ‚Äî the keyboard pair was byte-for-byte the same tree logic as the by-id pair, differing only in reading `selectedId` from closure vs a `taskId` param (the outer `if (selectedId===null) return` guard is subsumed by the by-id guards: a null id ‚Üí `findIndex` ‚Üí -1 ‚Üí `idx<=0` no-op / `tasks.find` ‚Üí undefined ‚Üí early return). **autosize** ‚Äî these had DRIFTED (as the backlog warned): they operate on different column universes (grid: predecessors/successors/cost/formula; master: project/parent/notes), different row sources (`tasks` vs `sortedRows`), different width stores (`persistWidths` vs `setColWidths`+`setData`), and different floors (36 vs 40). So the canonical merge is the shared **measurement skeleton**, not one function over two column sets.
- **What shipped:** indent/outdent collapsed to a single source ‚Äî the keyboard handler now calls `indentTaskById(selectedId)` / `outdentTaskById(selectedId)` (the exact functions the right-click menu already calls), and the duplicate `indentTask`/`outdentTask` `useCallback`s were deleted (~76 lines). The keydown effect's deps note updated (the by-id helpers are stable `useCallback([])`; `selectedId` is already a dep so the listener re-binds with a fresh selection). New module-scope **`measuredColWidth(headerLabel, cellValues, floor)`** holds the canvas/font/+24-pad/ceil/floor skeleton both `autoSizeCol` and `autoSizeMCol` now call; each keeps its own column-value switch + width-persistence + floor (36 grid / 40 master) verbatim. No user-visible change (pure maintainability).
- **Verification:** static equivalence (indent/outdent are now literally ONE function per direction, called by both entry points ‚Äî divergence impossible). Ran a one-off esbuild JSX transform over both `<script type="text/babel">` blocks (the scheduler is served verbatim from `public/`, so the Vite build gives it zero coverage) ‚Üí both blocks transform clean before AND after the edit. lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **3985 tests** pass ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP.md regenerated (`+measuredColWidth`). **V327** logs the live scheduler click-through (keyboard indent/outdent == menu; double-click column autosize fits, both grid + master) since the CDN-React scheduler can't be driven in the sandbox.
- **SHIPPED 2026-07-15** (branch `claude/next-backlog-item-2lots0`).

### B825 ‚Äî Grading standards records: slope rules per surface class, cited, jurisdiction/ADA-aware `[Site Planner / grading]` (feature) #site-planner #grading  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-14 as "NEW-6"; minted **B825**; first item of the grading track, with B826)*
`[x]` **DONE (Verify: sandbox).** The constants a grading concept needs, as registry records with source cites + verified flags ‚Äî the boolean-`verified` record family (the `pondCriteriaRules.js` / `buildability.js` / `floodplainRules.js` shape: value + `verified` + `source` + `sourceDate` + `note`; note `detentionRules.js` uses `verifiedOn`, a different marker) ‚Äî not hardcoded. NO grading/slope-standard constants exist anywhere in src/ today (audited ‚Äî net-new territory).
- Verify: sandbox (registry + ‚ìò rendering are headless-checkable; no GIS/live-data class)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-14 from chat ("NEW-6")

### B721 ‚Äî Ingestion adapter #1: City of Houston Major Thoroughfare & Freeway Plan (MTFP) `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ ingestion]` (feature) #thoroughfare #gis  *(filed 2026-07-08 from chat, provisional NEW-2; epic B720‚ÄìB726)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî LIVE PULL COMPLETE 2026-07-11 (V274). 26,697 City of Houston MTFP centerlines loaded into `public.thoroughfare_segments` (jurisdiction `coh`) on `planyr_production`, verified + idempotent.** Built + unit-tested earlier (branch `claude/thoroughfare-model-houston-ejq2zg`); the owed live pull ran this session once the owner opened houstontx.gov egress ("load the Houston thoroughfare data").
- Verify: live ‚Üí **PASSED** ‚Äî V274 archived in `VERIFICATION-DONE.md` (GIS-endpoint class: reconciled against the live ArcGIS layer + loaded prod).
- **Live result (2026-07-11):** the COHGIS "Major Thoroughfare Plans (MTFP)" layer served **26,699** features; **26,697 upserted, 2 skipped** (OBJECTID 26588/26591 ‚Äî null geometry at source, correctly skipped, confirmed not real roads). Distinct source ids = 26,697 (no duplicates); re-running a page left the total unchanged (idempotency on `(jurisdiction, source_feature_id)` proven). 0 null `geom_2278`, 0 invalid geometries; every vertex in the Houston metro (lat 29.5‚Äì30.2, lon ‚àí96 to ‚àí94.9). Class split: major_thoroughfare 18,081 ¬∑ freeway 3,645 ¬∑ collector_major 2,986 ¬∑ collector_minor 1,500 ¬∑ transit_corridor 484 ¬∑ other 1 ‚Äî matching the source ST_TYPE tallies exactly (only the single "N/A" fell to `other`).
- **AUDIT-FIRST reconciliation vs the original guessed config** (`houston.js` + `test/thoroughfareIngest.test.js` updated in the same commit; 37 tests green):
  1. The clean classification field is **`ST_TYPE`** (10 values covering 100% of features), NOT `HIER_TABLE`. HIER_TABLE is a design-hierarchy CODE encoding lanes + a per-segment ROW width (e.g. `MJ-4-80`, `TCS-2-varies(80-90)`) ‚Äî ranges/typos/blanks ‚Äî which would dump everything to `other`. (HIER_TABLE's per-segment ultimate ROW is logged as a future enhancement for B724's exact dedication math.)
  2. No coded-value domains exist ‚Äî crosswalk keys are the real live strings: Freeway/Tollway/Frontage ‚Üí freeway; Thoroughfare/Principal Thoroughfare/Major Thoroughfare ‚Üí major_thoroughfare (the MTFP width table's own header groups these three); Major/Minor Collector ‚Üí collector_major/collector_minor; Transit Corridor Street ‚Üí transit_corridor.
- **Verified ROW widths seeded into `jurisdiction_row_standards`** (from the City's published "MTFP Minimum ROW Width by Street Classification" table + ¬ß42-122): major_thoroughfare **100 ft**, collector_major **80 ft**, collector_minor **60 ft**; transit_corridor **null** ("No specific ROW minimum") and freeway **null** (TxDOT controlled-access, not in the table) ‚Äî left null, never guessed.
- **Plan vintage:** `plan_adopted_date = 2024-12-01` (MTFP Policy Statement amended Dec 2024) + `plan_name` "‚Ä¶2018 MTFP, amended through 2025" (per-segment `LastMTFPA` runs to 2025) ‚Äî the defensible as-of B726 keys freshness off.
- **Load method (AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî deviation from the handoff's Option-B, recorded for audit):** the layer returned 26,699 rows (not the "few thousand" the handoff assumed), so routing every geometry through Supabase-MCP SQL would have been prohibitively expensive. Instead loaded **DB-native**: enabled the `http` extension and had Postgres fetch each GeoJSON page itself (`http_get` ‚Üí `ST_GeomFromGeoJSON` ‚Üí `ST_Transform(‚Ä¶,2278)`), widths joined from the standards table, crosswalk/status mirroring `houston.js` exactly. Proven equivalent to the shared spine: `ST_Transform(4326‚Üí2278)` == `projectToGrid` to <0.001 ft on a spot vertex. The runnable adapter (`server/ingest/thoroughfare.mjs`) remains the sanctioned re-ingestion path once a service-role key / Cloud Run cron exists (B726); the temporary loader function was dropped after the run (schema left clean; `http` extension left enabled for B726).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-08 from chat (provisional NEW-2)
- DEDUPE-FIRST (net-new). Built on B629 (scheduled-ArcGIS-ingestion pattern) ¬∑ B245/B369 (registry pattern) ¬∑ B287 (retry/backoff). Consumes B720's tables + reuses the shared EPSG:2278 projection.

### B755 ‚Äî Stop wasting tokens hunting the next B#/V#: one-command `npm run next-id` `[repo / tooling ¬∑ workflow]` (tooling) #infra  *(owner chat 2026-07-10 ‚Äî "we waste a bunch of time just figuring out which b number to ship with or merge with ‚Ä¶ we're wasting a ton of tokens just making sure we find the right number in the list. How do we fix this? I thought we already addressed this." **B755** = highest B# across both files (B754) + 1, obtained with the very tool this item ships. Branch `claude/b-number-selection-5sur66`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî 11 new unit tests `test/nextId.test.js`, full suite 3360 green, lint 0, build green, and the CLI itself prints `Next free ‚Üí B755 ¬∑ V268` matching a raw grep of both files).**
- **What the owner saw:** nearly every session burned time (and a lot of model tokens) reading the 464 KB `BACKLOG.md` + the 1.4 MB write-only `BACKLOG-DONE.md` into context just to find the highest B# and add 1 ‚Äî the recurring "which number do we ship/merge with?" tax.
- **Why the earlier attempt (B638, `BACKLOG_OPEN.md`) didn't fix it (AUDIT-FIRST):** that index was built for **DEDUPE-FIRST** (seeing what's already filed), lists **only Open + ‚è≥ Verify** items, and is grouped by theme, not sorted by number. The highest id routinely sits on a **Done** item in the archive, which is *not* in the open index ‚Äî so a session still had to grep both big files to find the max. Nothing ever gave a one-command answer.
- **Fix:** new dependency-free `scripts/next-id.mjs` + `npm run next-id`. Scans the two authoritative id forms ‚Äî `### B123` headings and `**B123**` bold mints, including range ends (`B300‚ÄìB302`) ‚Äî across `BACKLOG.md` + `BACKLOG-DONE.md` (and both `VERIFICATION*.md` for V#) **on disk, at zero model-token cost**, and prints `Next free ‚Üí B### ¬∑ V###` in one line. `--json` for a machine object, `--b`/`--v` for the paste-ready label. Parse is deliberately immune to a stray inline `B99999` prose typo inflating the max (it only reads headings/bold), and it can never under-count (every assigned id has a `### B###` heading in one of the two files).
- **Wired into the workflow so sessions actually use it:** `CLAUDE.md` (the token-lean intro's new üî¢ rule, the three-state-lifecycle "next B#" line, and the **DEDUPE-FIRST** named rule) and the `/improve` command now all say "run `npm run next-id` ‚Äî never grep the archives for the max."
- **Named rules:** none of the render/persistence rules apply; this is workflow tooling. Mirrors the existing `build-map.mjs` (B637) / `build-backlog-index.mjs` (B638) generator conventions (dependency-free, exports pure fns the unit test imports, runnable standalone). `MAP.md` unchanged ‚Äî the builder scans only `src/`, so a `scripts/` file adds no inventory row.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** not a recurrence ‚Äî no prior B# shipped a next-id resolver; B638 shipped the *open-index* for a different purpose (see above).
- Verify: sandbox


### B737 ‚Äî Deed importer reads legacy .doc + PDF, and accepts several deeds at once (drop-many ‚Üí list ‚Üí place each); fixes the DEG./MIN./SEC. bearing mis-parse `[Site Planner]` (feature+bug) #site-planner #files #metes-and-bounds  *(owner 2026-07-09 from chat ‚Äî screenshot of the metes-and-bounds drop zone rejecting a .doc: "getting a notification that it can't read dot dot files. So let's fix that. And then also, I should be able to drop multiple meets and bounds in at once." Follow-up same session: "make it also read pdfs too" + supplied the real `.doc` and five real PDFs. **B737** = highest B# across both files (B736) + 1 ‚Äî RENUMBERED from a provisional B736 on merge-in of `origin/main`, whose concurrent doc-review batch took B736; per repo precedent the code/tests/commit + `ui-audit/verify-b736-multiformat-deed.mjs` keep the provisional B736 label, only the backlog ID bumped. Branch `claude/dotfile-bulk-meets-bounds-ye2tb7`)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî 12 new unit tests `test/docText.test.js` (8) + `test/metesAndBounds.test.js` DEG/MIN/SEC block (4), full suite 3184 tests green, build green, and headless end-to-end `ui-audit/verify-b736-multiformat-deed.mjs` 11/11: drops the real .doc + a text-layer PDF + a .txt at once ‚Üí 3 queued rows, all three parsed to calls (PDF via the real in-browser pdf.js worker), row-click loads the textarea, "Plot all 3" runs the "Deed 1 of 3 ‚Üí 2 of 3 ‚Üí All 3 placed" sequence into 3 independent deed groups, zero console errors).**
- **What the owner saw:** the "2 ¬∑ Plot a metes-and-bounds description" drop zone rejected `M&B - POA Parcel 3 (0.6 AC).doc` with "Can't read a .doc here‚Ä¶", and dropping several files only ever read the first.
- **Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST):** `readDeedFile` (`shared/files/docxText.js`) handled only `.docx` (a ZIP of XML) + plain text. A legacy `.doc` is a different binary entirely ‚Äî an OLE/Compound-File container with the text stored via a *piece table* ‚Äî so it fell through to the friendly-but-dead-end throw. The drop zone had no `multiple`, and both handlers hard-indexed `files[0]`.
- **Fix:**
  - **Legacy `.doc` reader** ‚Äî new dependency-free `shared/files/docText.js` (`docToText`): parses the CFB container (FAT/DIFAT + **mini-FAT**, the path a real deed's small Table stream always takes), the Word FIB (guards `wIdent`, **nFib ‚â• 193** so Word 6/95 don't slip through and mis-read, `fEncrypted`, `fcClx`/`lcbClx`), and the CLX **piece table** ‚Äî decoding each piece with the native `TextDecoder("windows-1252")` (compressed) or `"utf-16le"`, clamped to `ccpText`. windows-1252 is load-bearing: surveyors' minute/second marks are 0x92/0x94 (‚Üí U+2019/U+201D), which a naive `String.fromCharCode` turns into control chars the bearing parser drops. Nesting-aware field-code handling; loud, specific errors (very-old/encrypted/corrupt ‚Üí "Save As .docx or paste"). Same hand-rolled, dependency-free spirit as `docxText.js`.
  - **PDF reader** ‚Äî new `shared/files/pdfText.js` (`pdfToDeedText`), **lazily imported** so pdf.js stays out of the Site Planner bundle until a PDF is actually dropped (own 1.5 kB chunk). Pulls the embedded text layer via pdf.js (the engine the Review workspace already uses) and **reflows** word-wrapped survey courses back onto one logical line each (`reflowLines`) ‚Äî without which PDF word-wrap splits "‚Ä¶23 SEC. / EAST, 403.47 FEET" across lines and the bearing loses its quadrant + distance. Scanned/image-only PDFs throw loudly ("looks scanned ‚Äî paste it or drop the Word file"). Owner's five PDFs: the three written descriptions parse (5/7/10 calls); the two Carlson **exhibit drawings** (plat sheets, title-block only, no prose) honestly report "no calls" ‚Äî correct.
  - **Dispatch** ‚Äî `readDeedFile` gains ordered `.doc`/`.pdf` branches (`.pdf` via the lazy import); `accept` + copy widened; input gains `multiple`.
  - **Multi-file intake** (`SitePlanner.jsx`) ‚Äî `readDeed(file)` ‚Üí `readDeeds(files)`: reads every file (one batch-guarded commit via `deedReqRef`), builds a `deedQueue` of `{name, calls, exceptions, closes/gap, error}`, auto-loads the first readable into the textarea (single-file drop unchanged). New queue list UI (renders at ‚â•2) with per-file summaries + red rows for unreadable files; clicking a row loads it; the textarea edits the active row. **"Plot all"** threads a queue through `pobMode` and advances through **one** `advanceDeed` helper (all `anchorEncumbrance` exits routed through it, so no early-return aborts the run); the POB banner is now dynamic ("Deed K of N ‚Äî name"); the per-deed basis-of-bearings align hint is gated to single plots (skipped mid-run to avoid banner churn + an O(parcels) solve per deed); per-deed notes roll into the final "All N placed ‚Äî verify: ‚Ä¶" summary.
  - **Bearing parser fix (bundled, found via the real deed):** the `BEARING_SRC` degree/minute/second separators matched the single-char class `[¬∞¬∫o*:d-]` *before* the spelled-out words, so the bare `d` ate the "D" of "DEG" and the stray "E" was mis-read as the East quadrant ‚Äî every "N/S ## DEG. ## MIN. ## SEC. E/W" bearing lost its minutes/seconds and read West as East. Reordered to match "deg"/"min"/"sec" (optional trailing period) first. `NORTH 02 DEG. 29 MIN. 38 SEC. WEST` now ‚Üí N 2¬∞29'38" W (az 357.51, not 2¬∞). All 13 existing parser tests still pass; also now reads fully spelled-out "87 degrees 04 minutes 16 seconds".
- **Named rules:** LOUD-FAILURE (every unreadable file ‚Üí a specific visible error, never a silent empty), AUDIT-FIRST (reconciled the reader against the actual dispatch seam), MODULE-SCOPE-COMPONENTS (queue list is inline JSX, no new inner component). Docs: `MAP.md` + `shared/CLAUDE.md` pointer regenerated/updated same commit; real fixtures committed (`test/fixtures/deeds/deed-poa-parcel3.doc` + `.pdf`, `deed-simple.txt`).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** not a recurrence ‚Äî no prior B# covered `.doc`/PDF deed reading or multi-file deed drop (B-metes work to date was single-file .docx/.txt + the parser + deed‚Üîparcel align).

### B736 ‚Äî Doc Review measurements/annotations: close the last kind-locked color gaps (measures teal-by-default + editable, WYSIWYG draft, label follows stroke, perimeter fill) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature) #doc-review #markup  *(owner brief 2026-07-09 as "NEW-1"; provisional **B734**, renumbered **B736** ‚Äî concurrent `main`'s account-menu batch merged first and took B734/B735/V250. Per repo precedent the code/tests/commit keep the provisional B734 label; only the backlog + verification IDs bumped. Branch `claude/doc-review-measurement-styling-rh1rxk`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî unit tests + logged-out e2e panel drive + build/full-suite green).** An Area/Distance/etc. measurement's fill/stroke/weight/dash are now field-driven and editable per object, measures read teal by default (their historical ink) and stay legible, and what you pre-set is what you draw.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-09 from chat (NEW-1)
- **AUDIT-FIRST / DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî the brief described PRE-ENGINE code.** The bulk of "NEW-1" (per-object fill/stroke/weight/dash on every markup + a driven `PropertyPanel` + persistence) ALREADY SHIPPED as **B424‚ÄìB432** (the shared markup engine); the `draw(m, selected)` fn the brief cites is gone (‚Üí `MarkupRenderer`), the `area` tool already exposes fill, and edits already round-trip through the `data` jsonb. "NEW-1/NEW-2" are recurring scratch labels ‚Äî this is the genuine REMAINDER on top of that engine, **reuse not dupe**, not a re-implement.
- **What was actually still broken + fixed:**
  ‚Ä¢ **Latent regression:** `commit()` stamped the generic orange column default (`#c2410c`) onto measure strokes (+ area fill), so committed measures rendered an ORANGE line with a hard-coded TEAL label ‚Äî inconsistent, and measures had lost their teal identity. New shared pure `src/shared/markup/markupStyle.js` (`resolveMarkupStyle` + `kindDefaults`) resolves per-object style with a **kind-keyed fallback** (measures teal, annotations burnt-orange); `commit()`/`seedStyle()` now seed measures teal (stroke + area/perimeter fill). `MarkupRenderer` resolves through the same helper, and the **measure LABEL + count marker now follow the object stroke** (paintOrder white halo kept, so fill never drops the label below readable contrast).
  ‚Ä¢ **Draft ‚Üî commit drift:** the live draw preview hard-coded teal/`#b91c1c` and ignored the sticky style; a shared `seedStyle(kind)` (propStyle > tool default > kind default > column default) now drives BOTH `commit()` and `drawDraft()` **and** the armed-tool panel swatch, so a pre-set color previews before release and the old red-draft/orange-commit mismatch is gone. Inline text editor color follows the pending `fontColor`.
  ‚Ä¢ **Perimeter fill:** the `perimeter` matrix row gained `fill`/`fillOpacity` (deliberate owner-directed matrix spec change ‚Äî OFF by default; painted behind the loop when turned up), mirroring `area`.
- **Files:** new `src/shared/markup/markupStyle.js`; `MarkupRenderer.jsx`, `PropertyPanel.jsx` (additive optional `schema` prop ‚Äî reused by B737), `tools.matrix.js` (perimeter row); `DocReview.jsx` (seedStyle + WYSIWYG draft + text color). New tests `test/markupStyle.test.js`. **No DB/persistence change** ‚Äî the fields ride the existing proven `data` jsonb path (`docPersistence` tests), which is why this is Verify: sandbox not live.
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** `test/markupStyle.test.js` (kind defaults ¬∑ per-object override ¬∑ legacy weight/dash alias ¬∑ perimeter exposes fill) ¬∑ full suite **3160** green ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP + shared pointer regenerated. **Logged-out Playwright drive (real app, fixture PDF open):** the armed **Area** panel shows Fill color + Fill opacity + Line color + Line weight + Dash with the **teal `#0e7490`** default on both the line AND fill swatch (never the generic orange); **Distance** shows the stroke controls but **no fill** (capability-driven, hidden not disabled); 0 page errors.### B735 ‚Äî Shift+click parcel selection cleared the prior parcel instead of accumulating (broke parcel merge) `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #selection  *(owner "NEW-1" 2026-07-09 ‚Äî "Shift+click parcel selection clears prior selection instead of accumulating (breaks parcel merge)"; **B735** = highest B# across both files (B734) + 1 ‚Äî RENUMBERED from a provisional B734 on merge-in of `origin/main`, whose concurrent PR #557 took B734 (account-menu positioning). Origin: filed 2026-07-09 from chat. Branch `claude/site-planner-shift-click-select-si6ipm`)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî 12 unit tests `test/parcelSelect.test.js` + headless `ui-audit/verify-b735-shift-accumulate.mjs` 9/9: plain-click A shows no merge banner; plain-click A then Shift-click B ‚Üí "2 parcels selected" (A kept); Shift-click B again toggles off ‚Üí 1 (A retained); plain-click empty canvas clears the set; end-to-end plain-select A + Shift-add B ‚Üí Merge fuses the two lots + clears the selection; plus a resurrection-guard check that removing the seeded parcel doesn't bring it back on the next Shift-click ‚Äî caught by an adversarial review pass). lint 0 ¬∑ 3160 tests ¬∑ build green.**
- **Repro (owner):** click parcel A (it selects), hold Shift and click parcel B ‚Üí A deselected and only B was selected, so the 2-parcel set the Merge needs never formed and Merge stayed disabled.
- **Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST ‚Äî confirmed in code):** the Site Planner keeps TWO parcel-selection stores ‚Äî `sel` (the ONE primary parcel, drives the props panel / move) and `combineSel` (the SET picked for the Combine/Merge tool). A plain click selects into `sel`; a Shift-click accumulated into `combineSel` **and** reset `sel` to the clicked parcel. So A ‚Äî plain-clicked, thus only ever in `sel`, never in `combineSel` ‚Äî was silently dropped, and Shift-clicking B just moved the single highlight to B while `combineSel` held only `[B]`. (The bug only showed on the natural *plain-click-then-Shift-click* flow; Shift-clicking BOTH parcels already worked, since both went to `combineSel`.)
- **Fix:**
  - New pure `lib/parcelSelect.js` ‚Üí `extendMergeSelection(current, clickedId, { primaryId, isActive })`: **SEEDS** the set from the current single selection (`primaryId`) the first time you Shift-extend, then toggles the clicked parcel. Reuses the shared `nextSelection` reducer (`shared/markup/selection.js`, B569) for the toggle math ‚Äî one source of truth, no drift between workspaces ‚Äî and layers on the two Site-Planner-specific rules: the seed, and the B170 inactive-parcel guard (never ADD an inactive parcel, but an already-picked one can always be removed). 12 unit tests.
  - `SitePlanner.jsx`: a new `shiftPickParcel(id)` wrapper drives `extendMergeSelection` from `sel`; the three Shift-click entry points (canvas body `onBgDown`, boundary `startMoveParcel`, left-rail parcel row) now call it instead of the bare `toggleMerge`. `shiftPickParcel` also OWNS `sel` (the call sites no longer `setSel` after it): a click that ADDS a parcel makes it primary, but a click that REMOVES one falls `sel` back to the last remaining picked parcel (or clears) ‚Äî never leaving `sel` on the just-removed parcel, which would otherwise **re-seed and resurrect** it on the next Shift-click (a defect an adversarial review pass caught + a headless resurrection-guard check now pins). A **plain** click on a parcel (boundary tap in `onUp`, unlocked press, rail row) or a genuine **tap on empty canvas** (tagged `tapEmpty`, tiny-travel-only so a pan never wipes the set) now clears `combineSel` ‚Äî matching "plain click replaces the selection." `startMergePick` (the B720 button) also seeds from `sel` so pressing Merge keeps your already-selected parcel.
  - Merge precondition is already enforced (Merge disabled below 2 picks; `mergeParcels` refuses non-adjacent lots) ‚Äî the blocking `alert()` for "don't all share a boundary" was swapped to the app's non-blocking `flashWarn` (no dialog-box UI). Full greedy fusion of 3+ lots with partial shared edges is a separate `mergeRings` concern, unchanged here.
- **Gotchas checked:** the road/vertex-insert Shift+click already yields to a merge-pick when the press is inside another parcel (B718), so no shortcut collision; every parcel in the set renders with the same solid-blue picked style (no per-parcel opacity fade); empty-click deselect ignores drags/pans.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** NOT a recurrence of **B443** (that fixed Shift-clicks that never *registered* ‚Äî a marquee stole the interior click; that fix is intact). This is a distinct root cause ‚Äî the `sel`/`combineSel` two-store reconciliation B443 never touched. Related: B442/B444 (merge-banner z-index/layout), B720 (click-to-pick merge mode), B420 (interior click-through), B170 (inactive-parcel exclusion).
- Verify: sandbox

### B718 ‚Äî Road control-point editing: right-click Add/Remove + Shift-click add (reuse the shared vertex engine) `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #road #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-08 as "NEW-1"; minted **B718** = highest B# across both files (B717) + 1. Branch `claude/road-editing-curb-scale-r1u2sw`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî fixed + shipped + headless-verified same session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî interactive editing, not a mandatory-live class; driven end-to-end in a real logged-out browser).** After a road is drawn and selected you can now reshape its centerline: **Shift+left-click anywhere on the pavement** drops a control point (no menu); **right-click** a selected road ‚Üí **Add control point** (on an edge) / **Delete control point** (on a vertex). Inserted points project onto the centerline (no shape jump) and drag with the existing vertex-drag; endpoints are protected and a road can't drop below 2 points.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-08 from chat
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** this EXTENDS the shared B230 Bluebeam vertex engine (`editablePath`/`hitEditPath`/`insertVtx`/`deleteVtx`) ‚Äî which shipped before the B599 centerline-road model and so excluded roads (its `sel.kind==="el"` branch keyed off `el.points`; roads store `el.pts` + a parallel `el.vtx` treatment list). Predecessors B141/B147 added vertex-edit to measures/markups; B599/B600 built the road model ‚Äî none added road control-point add/remove. No new number minted for the engine: this is the road slice of the same engine.
- **What shipped:** two pure, unit-tested helpers in `lib/roadGeometry.js` ‚Äî `insertRoadVertex(pts,vtx,edgeIndex,pt)` (splices the point + a matching `{}` treatment, staying length-matched; the new interior vertex defaults to arc and renders straight until dragged because the projected point is collinear on its sparse segment) and `removeRoadVertex(pts,vtx,index)` (returns null for an endpoint or a 2-point road) + `canRemoveRoadVertex`. `SitePlanner.jsx`: `editablePath()` returns a `{layer:"road", pts:el.pts, closed:false, min:2, edgeTolFt:‚Ä¶, noEndpointDelete:true}` case (checked before the polygon `el.points` case); `hitEditPath()` honors a per-path `edgeTolFt` (‚âà strip half-width, so a Shift-/right-click anywhere on a wide road projects onto the centerline); `insertVtx`/`deleteVtx` gain a `road` branch that keeps `pts`+`vtx` in lock-step and re-syncs the strip bbox via `reRoad`; the plain-click + context-menu wiring arms only INTERIOR road vertices for the Delete key (an endpoint/body leaves selVtx null so Delete still removes the whole road ‚Äî no behavior change there).
- **Also fixed (latent bug this surfaced):** the Shift-insert `onOtherParcel` guard used `pc.id !== (path.layer==="parcel"?path.id:null)`, which was true for EVERY parcel when editing a road/el, so a road inside a parcel (the normal case) could never Shift-insert. Gated it to `path.layer==="parcel"` (also fixes the same latent case for polygon elements).
- **Verified (sandbox/headless):** `test/roadGeometry.test.js` +17 cases (insert index/length-match/endpoints-stay-{}/collinear-no-jump; remove interior/endpoint‚Üínull/min-2‚Üínull/sole-interior‚Üí2-pt; `canRemoveRoadVertex`) ¬∑ `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` updated (B617‚Üímarkup-only + new B719 guard) ¬∑ full suite **3,127 green** ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green (SitePlannerApp lazy chunk intact) ¬∑ MAP.md regenerated. **`ui-audit/verify-b718-b719.mjs` (logged-out, real app, road INSIDE a parcel): select ‚Üí 3 handles ‚Üí Shift-click a segment ‚Üí 4 (proves the onOtherParcel fix) ‚Üí right-click the new vertex ‚Üí "Delete control point" enabled ‚Üí 3 ‚Üí right-click an endpoint ‚Üí Delete disabled ("min reached"); 0 page errors. Existing `verify-centerline-road.mjs` still green (draw/drag/migration unaffected).**

### B717 ‚Äî Focus funnel hid overdue tasks that display red "Needs Attn." ‚Äî Focus now classifies by the grid's display health `[Scheduler]` (bug) #scheduler  *(owner chat 2026-07-08 ‚Äî arrived as "NEW-1"; **B717** = highest B# across both files (B716) + 1. Branch `claude/focus-overdue-tasks-hidden-w0vmq9`)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî fixed + shipped this session. Verify: sandbox ‚Äî pure classification unit-tested (12 cases) AND confirmed end-to-end in a headless browser driving the REAL funnel (`ui-audit/verify-focus-overdue.mjs`): the overdue-red row survives Focus, the count reads 2 not 3, tooltips correct. lint 0 ¬∑ build green.**
`[ ]` was: With the conditional-format rule cfRules.overdueRed ON, a Not-Started (gray) leaf whose end date has passed rendered red "Needs Attn." in the grid's Status column, but clicking the parent group's Focus funnel HID it along with the genuinely completed / not-started rows (live example: "Submit to Baytown P&Z" under the PUD group, end 6/17/26).
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-08 from chat
- AUDIT-FIRST root cause (two sources of truth disagreed): the grid colours a leaf via `computeDisplayHealth` (applies cfRules), but Focus decided visibility from the RAW stored health. In the `flatTasks` memo (index.html ~L6209), `rolledStatus`'s leaf branch read `byId[id].health` directly (gray‚Üí"upcoming"), and `hideStatusOf` hides anything not "active" ‚Üí an overdue leaf displayed red yet was classed "upcoming" and filtered out at the `walk` step. The `hideableUpcoming` count/tooltip was wrong for the same reason (tallied it as not-started). Overdue In-Progress/yellow leaves already mapped to "active", so only the overdue-but-Not-Started ‚Üí red case vanished.
- Fix (single source of truth ‚Äî same class as PDF-PARITY: grid status and Focus filter must never diverge): the leaf branch now classifies via `computeDisplayHealth(byId[id], settings)`. `data?.settings` (so cfRules is visible) and `NOW` (so the midnight/focus overdue rollover re-runs Focus) added to the memo deps. Deliberate design choice per the brief: a `dueSoonYellow`-promoted Not-Started leaf now also survives Focus ‚Äî consistent with the grid showing it "In Progress"; red is ALWAYS kept.
- Tests: `test/focusOverdueStatus.test.js` (+12) over the faithful engine mirror ‚Äî the exact repro, an old-raw-health regression witness, rule-gating (overdueRed off ‚Üí still hidden), normal hiding preserved (done / paused / future-not-started), and the dueSoonYellow choice. Mirror helpers `computeDisplayHealth` / `leafFocusStatus` / `rolledStatusLeaf` / `hideStatusOf` + settable `NOW` added to `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`, with anti-drift guards asserting the source's leaf branch + memo deps. ### B708 ‚Äî Elevation-anchored ponds: banded storage split at the flood WSE, credit rules, drainability `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #pond  *(owner consolidated brief 2026-07-07, arrived as "NEW-2" ‚Äî the elevation-anchored-pond item; **B708** = B707 + 1. Branch `claude/floodplain-mitigation-engine-0hw2jt`)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped this session (branch `claude/floodplain-mitigation-engine-0hw2jt`; 3,077 tests green ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ 12/12 headless self-checks). Verify: sandbox** ‚Äî the banded math is fully unit-tested (`test/pondGeom.bands.test.js`, 20 cases: prisms vs hand calc, WSE below/mid/above-bank, pool subtraction + band exclusivity, anchored/estimate/gross precedence incl. the anchored-but-ctx-missing fallback, excavation integral, drawdown + berm screens); the inspector states render in the headless pass; the real-data anchored-split check rides V243 with B712.
`[ ]` was: Anchor ponds to NAVD88 and split storage at the flood WSE so an in-floodplain basin stops reporting gross volume as usable detention.
- Verify: sandbox (pure banded math + inspector states unit/headless-testable; the real-data anchored-split check folds into B712's live pass)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat
- Anchor: `det.tobElev` (ALREADY EXISTS as the contour-label elevation ‚Äî reused as the NAVD88 anchor, no new field) + additive `det.poolElev` (permanent pool) + `det.receivingFlowlineElev` (channel-bottom of the receiving ditch/bayou; `det.availDepth` precedent, manual v1 w/ named LiDAR/HCFCD hook). Additive det.* subfields ride the whole-element jsonb write path (elementSync commits full objects ‚Äî confirmed) ‚Äî write `null`, never `undefined`/NaN (an `undefined` subfield diffs forever vs the Postgres-seeded shadow).
- Pure `volumeBetween(elevLo, elevHi)` + `bandedStorage` extending pondGeom's `detentionStorage` machinery (memo sig extended w/ elevation params). **Exclusive bands** so no acre-foot lands in two ledgers: dead = below the pool floor ¬∑ mitigation-candidate = pool‚ÜíWSE ("hydraulic connection + stage distribution: engineer confirms") ¬∑ detention-usable = above max(WSE, pool) up to the water surface (freeboard respected). WSE ‚â• top of bank ‚Üí usable 0 (fully inundated ‚Äî explicit test).
- **One shared `usablePondVolume` helper** consumed by the site metrics loop, the pond auto-size solver (`volumeAt` + depth fallback), and the B655 per-pond card ‚Äî precedence: anchored (tobElev + WSE known) ‚Üí banded split; anchored-but-ctx-missing ‚Üí fall back to the existing Regime-B `deadStoragePoolDepthFt` estimate (NEVER silently zero); unanchored ‚Üí the estimate. This supersedes the site-level estimate per pond without forking it (AUDIT-FIRST vs B632/B655; the code comment at the providedUsableCf block already holds the no-contradiction invariant).
- Credit location rule (explicit copy, never silent): cut OUTSIDE or above the trigger floodplain earns no screening mitigation credit. Drainability: pond bottom below `receivingFlowlineElev` ‚Üí "gravity drawdown unlikely below X ft ‚Äî pump station or shallower basin" + cap the suggested usable depth (reconciles with **B641** ‚Äî this is its pond-side consumer; HCFCD flowline data plugs into this slot later). Berm-as-fill: tobElev above existing grade inside the floodplain ‚Üí "bermed basin ‚Äî the berm itself is fill requiring mitigation and may block conveyance"; floodway hard-stop copy targets fill/structures (pond CUT in the floodway can be permissible with no-rise review ‚Äî informational, never a green light). Groundwater caveat near mapped channels stands. All figures sum across every pond element.
- Rendering (salience tracks importance): anchored + in-floodplain ‚Üí "Usable detention (above flood WSE) X ac-ft ¬∑ Below flood WSE Y ac-ft ‚Äî no detention credit; candidate mitigation." Unanchored + intersecting ‚Üí gross volume + a loud "set the pond's top-of-bank elevation" prompt. NEVER silently present gross as usable on a floodplain site.
- DEDUPE-FIRST: extends B632 (regime) / B633 (solver) / B655 (per-pond card) ‚Äî same machinery, per-pond anchored refinement; NOT a recurrence of any.
