### B690 ‚Äî Cold-start crash: a fresh/incognito client tripped the whole Site module into the error boundary (one points-less parcel record kills the finder) `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(surfaced 2026-07-07 as V231 #18 in the Cowork element-sync live run: fresh client ‚Üí "Site couldn't load" error card right after boot. Minted **B690** = highest B# across both files (B689) + 1. DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî no existing B# covers the finder acreage crash class; distinct from the B592 union tripwire (merge-time) and B54 (empty-list scare): this is a RENDER throw.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî diagnosed to the exact line from the minified production stack, fixed + sandbox-verified same session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the crash is a pure render throw, fully reproducible headless).**
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from the Cowork V229‚ÄìV231 run report (V231 #18)
- **Diagnosis (from the live stack, confirmed by rebuilding the deployed commit):** the reported minified frames `ym‚ÜíHp‚ÜíIj` in the `SitePlannerApp` chunk were mapped by building 40884e6 (the deployed build) locally with Supabase env configured ‚Äî they are **`shoelace` ‚Üí `siteAcres` ‚Üí `MapFinder`** (`MapFinder.jsx`). `siteAcres` guarded `site.parcels?.length` but ran `shoelace(p.points)` unguarded ‚Äî a stored record whose parcels array holds a parcel WITHOUT a `points` array (attr-only / legacy / a malformed row round-tripped verbatim through storage or `site_elements`) throws `undefined.length`, and because the finder's site list + map-marker tooltip call `siteAcres` for every record, ONE bad record crashes the entire Site workspace into its error boundary. (MapFinder's own map-drawing path already skips such parcels ‚Äî `p.points?.length` ‚Äî the acreage helpers just never got the same guard.)
- **Fix:** guard the two ring-area helpers at the read boundary ‚Äî `shoelace` (`MapFinder.jsx`) and the planner's `polyArea` (`SitePlanner.jsx`) return 0 for a missing/empty ring, so a degenerate parcel reads "no boundary" in the list and contributes 0 area on the canvas instead of killing the module. Value-preserving for every real ring.
- **Regression net (the reason this escaped):** every headless smoke seeded a well-formed site, so the fresh-client and bad-record paths were never exercised. `ui-audit/verify-b671-element-sync.mjs` now additionally runs (5) a **NO-SEED cold start** ‚Äî a truly fresh context must boot the Site module with zero page errors and no error-boundary card ‚Äî and (6) a **bad-record boot** ‚Äî a seeded site whose parcel has NO points must list as "no boundary" without crashing. Both new checks pass against the fixed build.
- **Negative control (diagnosis proven, not just plausible):** running check (6)'s seed against the UNFIXED deployed commit (40884e6, rebuilt locally) reproduces the production failure verbatim ‚Äî the Site error card with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')" ‚Äî while the fixed build lists the record as "no boundary". Also confirmed the React boundary swallows the throw (no `pageerror` fires), which is why the smokes detect the error CARD text, not page errors alone.
- **Note on the live repro's confound:** the crash fired right after the "[Supabase] connected" console line, which made it look auth/boot-order-related ‚Äî it isn't. That line is just the phase-1 connection test resolving at about the same moment the finder first renders the stored list; the trigger is purely the bad record. Signing in "resolving" it is consistent: the signed-in pull merges/normalizes a different store copy.

### B689 ‚Äî Open left menu rendered warm cream in light mode instead of the cool app surfaces `[Site Planner / theming]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(owner report 2026-07-07 with screenshot, Site Analysis open: "there's a cream color and everything else is like cool white. There should not be this weird cream color on this left menu." Minted **B689** = highest B# across both files (B688) + 1. DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî the SAME `--planner-*` surfaces as **B686** but the OPPOSITE ask: B686 deliberately KEPT the warm parchment in light (only fixed dark); the owner now overrides that light-parchment choice. Not a recurrence ‚Äî B686's dark fix stuck; this is a new decision. Branch `claude/left-menu-warm-color-cgdljn`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped + headless-verified this session in BOTH themes (Verify: sandbox). eslint 0 ¬∑ contrast audit green (the 7 planner pairs clear AA on the new cool surfaces) ¬∑ guard tests green (contrast/mapDrift/backlogIndex/docPointers) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-b689-leftmenu-cool.mjs` 6/6 (light panel = cool #EAEEF3 and MATCHES the 54px rail; NOT the retired cream; dark panel = B686 slate #15171D unchanged); `verify-b656-close.mjs` 14/14 and `verify-mobile-no-autopanel.mjs` 2/2 after the detection rewrite.**
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat (screenshot)
- **Repro (owner's screenshot):** Site Planner ‚Üí open any left-rail menu (Yield / Parcel / Site Analysis / ‚Ä¶). The flyout panel behind the cards was warm cream while the 54px icon rail beside it, the map, and the right tool palette were all the cool gray-white theme ‚Äî the panel read as a lone warm surface.
- **AUDIT-FIRST root cause:** NOT a stray inline hex (the handed-in diagnosis was pre-B686 and stale). The flyout (`SitePlanner.jsx:9519`) already used the theme token `var(--planner-panel)`; B686 (2026-07-06) made that token theme-aware (cream in light / slate in dark) but, per the owner ask *at that time*, kept the LIGHT value as the exact prior cream. The cream is that deliberate light-parchment choice, now reversed.
- **Fix ‚Äî retune the three LIGHT `--planner-*` tokens to the cool app surfaces (`src/index.css` `:root`):** `--planner-panel` #efe9dd ‚Üí **#EAEEF3** (= `--chrome-bg`, so the flyout matches the tool rail), `--planner-raised` #f8f6f0 ‚Üí **#FFFFFF** (clean white cards), `--planner-border` #ece6d9 ‚Üí **#E1E5EB** (cool hairline). One change fixes the flyout, the shell backdrop (7929), every raised readout card, and all inspector hairlines at once, because they all reference these tokens. **Dark values untouched** ‚Äî B686's slate hierarchy is preserved (the complaint was light-only).
- **Same-class cleanup in the left menu (`components/SiteAnalysis.jsx`):** the inert "Not connected" status chip recoloured from warm cream (`#f1efe9`/`#8a8473`/`#e0dacb`) to a quiet cool neutral (`#F3F5F8`/`#4B5263`/`#E1E5EB`) ‚Äî the five semantic status tints (present/absent/unavailable/unknown/info) are intentional hues and left alone; the three dead warm `PAL?.x || "#‚Ä¶"` fallbacks repointed to their cool light-token equivalents (they never render ‚Äî PAL is always passed ‚Äî but no warm literals linger in a left-menu file).
- **Test robustness:** two self-tests (`verify-b656-close`, `verify-mobile-no-autopanel`) located the flyout by its exact cream RGB ‚Äî already brittle since B686 gave the shell the same surface. Added `data-testid="left-menu-panel"` to the flyout and switched both to query it ‚Äî color-independent, and it correctly targets only the flyout (not the shell), which the passing runs confirm.
- **Files:** `src/index.css` (3 token values + comment), `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx` (flyout `data-testid`), `src/workspaces/site-planner/components/SiteAnalysis.jsx` (pending tint + fallbacks), `ui-audit/verify-b656-close.mjs` + `verify-mobile-no-autopanel.mjs` (testid detection), `ui-audit/verify-b689-leftmenu-cool.mjs` (new spec).
- **Nothing on the owner's end.** Color-only render change, fully visible logged-out and verified headless in both themes ‚Äî no auth/cloud path, so no `V###`.
- **Flagged, out of scope (not the left menu):** latent warm-hex fallbacks remain in the PDF/print export path (`lib/printSheet.js`, `lib/sheetFurniture.js` ‚Äî `pal.ink||"#26231e"` etc.), the app crash screen (`ErrorBoundary.jsx` `#efeadf`), the "Loading your sites‚Ä¶" toast (`SitePlannerApp.jsx`), and a doc-review placeholder rect (`Stitcher.jsx`). All are dead fallbacks or separate surfaces; the print ones touch the export path (PDF-PARITY / live-verify) so they're a distinct future cleanup, not this fix.

### B688 ‚Äî Header crumb didn't live-update when you rename the CURRENT project (Review / Library) `[Header-Nav / Review + Library]` (bug) #ui #doc-review #library  *(owner report 2026-07-07 with screenshot: "I changed the name below and it should auto update but didn't." Minted **B688** = highest B# across both files (B687) + 1. DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî distinct from B439 (row snap-back) and the B191/B204 planner-crumb-live-via-`commitSiteLabel` path.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped + sandbox+headless-verified this session (Verify: sandbox). 2,840 unit tests green (+4 `resolveCurrentName` cases) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-review-rename-header.mjs` 6/6, with a negative-control run confirming it reproduces the owner's exact symptom pre-fix.**
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-07 from chat (screenshot)
- **Repro (owner's screenshot):** in **Review**, rename the open project in the Row-1 switcher dropdown ‚Äî the dropdown ROW updated to the new name and the store persisted it, but the breadcrumb crumb ABOVE the dropdown kept the OLD name until a reload/project-switch.
- **AUDIT-FIRST root cause:** the shared `ProjectBreadcrumb` renders the crumb from the `currentProject` **prop**. In the Site-Planner planner header that prop tracks a rename live (routes through `commitSiteLabel` ‚Üí `setSiteLabel`), but **Review** (`markupProject`, a `useMemo` keyed on `[projectId, meta.projectId, meta.project]`) and **Library** (`libraryProject`, recomputed per render) derive it from the **route id** and never re-derive the NAME from the store on a same-tab rename. So `commitRename ‚Üí storeRename ‚Üí refresh()` updated the breadcrumb's own list (the row) but not the parent's prop (the crumb) ‚Üí stale crumb.
- **Fix (self-contained in the shared component ‚Äî one place fixes Review, Library, and cross-tab):** new pure `resolveCurrentName(currentProject, projects)` in `projectModel.js` prefers the freshly-refreshed switcher list's name for the current project, falling back to the prop (cold/empty list, or a project not in the list) ‚Äî never a regression. `ProjectBreadcrumb` now renders `currentName = resolveCurrentName(currentProject, projects)` for the crumb. Because the list is refreshed on rename **and** on the cross-tab `storage` event, a rename in another tab now updates the crumb for free too. Controlled hosts (Scheduler) are unaffected ‚Äî they feed a fresh `projects` list + `currentProject` back over the bridge; the planner header is unaffected ‚Äî its prop is already live and the list agrees (synchronous store write before `refresh()`).
- **Files:** `src/shared/projects/projectModel.js` (+`resolveCurrentName`, +export), `src/shared/ui/ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx` (import + `currentName` in the crumb span), `test/projects.test.js` (+4), `ui-audit/verify-review-rename-header.mjs` (new headless spec), `MAP.md` (regenerated ‚Äî new export).
- **Nothing on the owner's end.** All paths are visible logged-out (same code path signed-in ‚Äî the fix is display-only, independent of cloud), so no auth-only surface and no `V###` needed.

### B686 ‚Äî The Site Planner inspector / left panel now themes to dark (was hardcoded cream) `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(found + fixed 2026-07-06 during/after the B657-5B convergence pass; minted **B685**, RENUMBERED to **B686** on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî PR #532 independently minted B685 for the Library any-file-type work (referenced in its title/commits/V227), so that keeps the number and this item took B686 (matching the Open-item renumber main already recorded). The full **`YIELD_PAL`** (panelBg/border/cardBg/hairline/track/iconTile ‚Üí planner tokens; text/rowLabel/muted/faint ‚Üí `--text-*`; warnText/dangerText ‚Üí `--warn-text`/`--danger-text`) now themes; the chart hues (building/paving/green/detention) stay fixed (one semantic colour = one meaning). The stale "the Yield panel is a FIXED light-parchment surface" comment is removed.
- **Contrast** ‚Äî 7 new `PAIRS` in `ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs` (`--text-primary`/`-secondary`/`-tertiary` + `--warn-text`/`--danger-text` on the planner surfaces); all clear AA in both themes.

### B657 ‚Äî Terminology & consistency pass `[App-wide / UI]` (task) #ui  *(owner brief 2026-07-05, arrived as "NEW-6"; minted **B657**. Branch `claude/site-planner-setup-menu-ui-euzpyf`; landed across PRs #527/#529 + the convergence PR.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED 2026-07-06 (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî eslint 0 ¬∑ contrast audit green ¬∑ 2,826 tests ¬∑ build green; screenshot-verified).** Names + chrome now agree across the app, and the tabs share one control design language.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-05 from chat
- **(1)** "Boundary tool (right rail)" ‚Üí "Parcel tool (right rail)" (both parcel empty-states); geometry-sense "boundary" left intact. **(2)** pond a11y label "Detention Basin" ‚Üí "Detention Pond" (matches the 4 visible uses + `planStyle.js`); Yield short-form "Detention" kept as a category shorthand. **(3)** breadcrumb assessed ‚Äî Map (Site) / Dashboard (Schedule) home labels are intentional (B204), already one shared `ProjectBreadcrumb` chrome, "Concept" correctly Site-only ‚Üí no change. **(4)** File menu "Export JSON" ‚Üí "Export project file" (+ Import parity, titles).
- **(5) Design-language convergence (owner chose the full scope 2026-07-06):**
  - **Shared primitives** ‚Äî NEW `src/shared/ui/controls.jsx`: `Button`/`ToggleChip`/`IconButton`/`Field`/`Section`/`MenuItem` on ONE radius/pad/type scale (`RADIUS`/`PAD`/`FONT`), token-driven (never raw hex), module-scope. One documented active-accent rule: `accent` defaults to `var(--accent)`; a host overrides with the matching on-accent token, so module accents (green/purple/amber/teal) are preserved, not flattened. The shared `Section` uses `var(--border-default)` (correct for themed surfaces like Library).
  - **Token fixes** ‚Äî ToolRail hardcodes `#EF9F27`/`#231a0e` ‚Üí `var(--accent-review)`/`var(--on-accent-review)` (dark-mode fix); the contrast audit gained the `--on-accent`/`--accent` pair (shared primary Button ‚Äî passes AA both themes).
  - **Convergence** ‚Äî control radius normalized to **8** app-wide: Site Planner `btn`/`rbtn` (was 9, + stale ember `rgba(232,89,12)` shadows ‚Üí neutral), DocReview + Stitcher `btn` (was 7), Library FileBrowser controls (were 6); Library facet chips adopt the shared `ToggleChip`. Auth/Team popups were already radius-8/token ‚Äî no change needed.
  - **Scheduler** embedded app (`public/sequence/index.html`, iframe) deliberately out of scope ‚Äî walled module, keeps its purple accent.
- **Findings filed, not force-fixed:** (a) the app was already ~90% converged (all token-driven, radius-8 dominant), so "convergence" was mostly normalizing the last radius outliers + cleaning stale shadow colors rather than a big-bang refactor; a full component-swap (every call site literally using the shared components) is optional future polish. (b) **B685** ‚Äî the Site Planner inspector/left panel is hardcoded cream and does NOT theme to dark; discovered here (a Section-border theming attempt would have created a dark-border-on-cream mismatch, so it was reverted and the real issue filed as its own item).

### B683 ‚Äî Coordinate readout shows WGS84 GPS lat/long (not State Plane E/N) `[Site Planner / Map]` (task) #site-planner #coordinates  *(owner brief 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B683** = highest B# across both files (B682) + 1. Branch `claude/hopeful-fermi-3yaqcj`. DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî no prior coordinate-display / lat-long item.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped + sandbox+headless-verified this session (Verify: sandbox). lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `gis-verify/kmz-gps-verify.mjs` 13/13.** Display-only: wherever a cursor coordinate is shown, show WGS84 lat/long (decimal degrees, lat,lon order, 6 dp ‚âà 0.1 m) instead of the EPSG:2278 State Plane E/N grid it showed before.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **AUDIT-FIRST reconcile:** the brief's "2278‚Üí4326" is the owner's mental model ‚Äî the code reality is that the planner canvas stores geometry in a per-site **feet frame** about a lat/lon origin, and `feetToLatLng` (arcgis.js) is the transform ALREADY in the render path (map redraw of footprints). The EPSG:2278 `projectToGrid` was used ONLY by the old HUD chip (B609). So the one shared 2278‚Üî4326 util both this and B684 reuse is **`feetToLatLng`** ‚Äî a single projection path, no second one added.
- **Site-planner canvas:** the bottom-left HUD chip (was `{gridOrigin.x+cursor.x} E ‚Ä¶ N`, State Plane feet) now shows `{lat.toFixed(6)}¬∞, {lon.toFixed(6)}¬∞` via `feetToLatLng(cursor, origin.lat, origin.lon)`. Removed the now-dead `gridOrigin` memo + `projectToGrid` import.
- **Map viewer (MapFinder):** had NO coordinate readout ‚Äî added a "you are here" lat/long chip (bottom-center, clears the zoom + scale controls), updated on mousemove and coalesced to one update per animation frame so a fast move can't thrash React.
- EPSG:2278 stays the internal frame for ALL geometry / measurement / storage ‚Äî this is a formatting change at the display layer only; measurements/areas stay in feet.
- **Verified (headless, logged-out):** seeded a georeferenced Katy site ‚Üí both the map-viewer chip and the canvas chip render lat/long; the canvas chip read `29.785977¬∞, -95.829959¬∞` over the seeded origin (29.786, -95.83). (`gis-verify/kmz-gps-verify.mjs`.)

### B655 ‚Äî Detention sizing card in the pond inspector, with a pumped-outfall toggle `[Site Planner / detention]` (feature) #site-planner #pond #yield  *(owner brief 2026-07-05, arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B655**. Branch `claude/site-planner-setup-menu-ui-euzpyf`.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED 2026-07-06 (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî headless `ui-audit/verify-b655-detention-card.mjs` 9/9 incl. the pumped-outfall path; lint 0 new ¬∑ 2,799 tests (+13 engine cases) ¬∑ build green).** A per-pond required-vs-provided **screening** card lives in the pond inspector, directly below DETENTION STORAGE.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-05 from chat
- **Engine (pure, additive, carrier-shaped, in `lib/detentionRules.js`):** `runoffCoefficient(impPct)` (Schaake composite, clamped, null on unknown), `DESIGN_STORMS` (NOAA Atlas-14 Harris/Houston IDF, `secondarySource:true` pending owner primary verification) + `DESIGN_STORM_PERIODS` (100/25/10/2-yr), `stormIntensity(period, durMin)` (interpolated via the existing `interpolateCurve`), `computeRateBasedDetention(...)` (Modified Rational: max over a duration sweep of `(C¬∑i¬∑A ‚àí Q_allow)¬∑t`, critical-duration picked; **never fabricates ‚Äî a missing release/imp/storm/area flags** `unknown`), `computePumpedCredit(...)` (difference of two MRM runs; self-capping ‚Äî pump ‚â• peak inflow zeroes required, never over-credits; always carries the `regime-b-tailwater-suppressed-by-pump` flag + an explicit assumption string). `assessHydraulicRegime` left untouched ‚Äî suppression is additive, in the pumped carrier + card only.
- **Card UI (pond IIFE, `SitePlanner.jsx`):** editable Drainage area (default = site `acresActive`, "Site" reset chip), Impervious % (default = plan `impPct`, "Auto" reset chip), a Design-storm `<select>`, Allowable release (cfs), an Outfall **Gravity/Pumped** chip toggle, and a Pump rate + cfs/gpm unit (gpm‚Üícfs at the card boundary) shown only when Pumped. Readout: Provided (this pond), the rate-based Required (+ a "‚àí pumped credit" line), and a **Surplus/Shortfall** pass/fail delta against the responsive rate-based number (success/danger tokens); the authority **site total** (`detReq`) is shown as a reference with its `ruleBadge`; every number badged with its basis + the screening caveat; the pumped assumption note replaces the Regime-B wet-bottom penalty. Token-only (no raw hex).
- **Persistence:** all inputs in the additive `selEl.det` bag (`daAcres/daImpPct/designStorm/releaseRateCfs/outfallMode/pumpRateCfs/pumpRateUnit`) via the existing `setDet` (pushHistory + setSelEl) ‚Äî **no `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump / migration**.
- **AUDIT-FIRST honoured:** the brief's "wire Expand this pond to the pass/fail target" was already DONE (B640); this delivers the genuinely-new (a) per-pond card, (b) editable rate-based screening inputs beside B636's fixed authority coefficients, (c) the pumped-outfall credit (net-new ‚Äî nothing in B636‚ÄìB642 modelled a pump).
- **OWNER-TODO:** the Atlas-14 IDF table ships area-representative + flagged; owner can confirm it against the official NOAA Atlas-14 grid for exact site coordinates (screening still valid meanwhile).

### B682 ‚Äî Line-label spacing: a LIVE slider (+ a more responsive anti-overlap floor) `[Site Planner]` (bug/enhancement ‚Äî follow-on to B678) #site-planner #ui  *(owner chat 2026-07-06 right after B678 shipped ‚Äî "i just tried revising the line label spacing and its not clear that it changed anything, add a slider to it too"; **B682** = highest real B# across both files (B681) + 1. Branch `claude/polyline-label-controls-6hvn8e` (restarted from `main` after PR #520 merged).)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî headless-verified). lint 0 new ¬∑ 2,783 tests (+1 guard) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-b678-681-label-callout.mjs` now 22/22 (adds: the slider is present + LIVE ‚Äî a tight setting shows 13 labels vs 1 at a wide one; long-label non-overlap holds at the new floor) ¬∑ B620 regression 12/12.** The B678 spacing control was a number box that commits on Enter/blur, so a change gave no live feedback ‚Äî and at the owner's working zoom the anti-overlap minimum quietly absorbed small changes (especially trying to make labels *tighter* ‚Äî they were already as close as they fit), so it read as "nothing happened."
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **What shipped:** a **live range slider** (30‚Äì2000 ft) sits beside the number box in the "Label spacing (ft)" row, both editing the same value ‚Äî dragging it re-spaces the labels on the plan **immediately**. The whole drag coalesces into **ONE undo frame** (`coalesceLabelWrite` + `labelSessionRef`: `{live:true}` pushes history only when the session opens; release/blur closes it), and stays NON-STICKY (direct `setMarkups`/`setSelEl`, never `mkStyle`) ‚Äî same discipline as B678.
- **More responsive floor:** the anti-overlap minimum was `label.length¬∑fs¬∑0.9`; measuring the REAL rendered width showed all-caps Inter runs ‚âà 0.78/char, so `0.9` left a wide dead-band. Tightened to **`0.85`** ‚Äî just above the true glyph width, so it ONLY prevents literal overlap (verified: a long all-caps label at a tight 20 ft still self-thins to non-overlap) and the slider stays responsive across the rest of its range. (An intermediate `0.7` draft was caught in-session by the headless overlap check ‚Äî long labels collided ‚Äî and corrected to 0.85.)
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** follow-on refinement to **B678** (kept a distinct B# ‚Äî it's a new owner report + a net-new control against the shipped build, not a recurrence of a B678 defect; B678's behavior held). No other spacing/slider item (grepped `labelSpacing` / `type="range"`).  *(owner chat 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-1"; first minted provisionally **B677**, RENUMBERED to **B678** on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî a concurrent session took **B677** for the Library right-click-context-menu item; **B678** = highest real B# across both files (B677) + 1. All in-code/test/harness labels use the REAL **B678‚ÄìB681** (renumbered before commit). Branch `claude/polyline-label-controls-6hvn8e`.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the whole flow drives logged-out; headless-verified). lint 0 ¬∑ 2,774 tests (+4 guards) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-b678-681-label-callout.mjs` 20/20 ¬∑ B620 regression harness still 12/12.** The repeating label that rides a line/polyline/road/easement (e.g. `12" WATER`, `18" SS`) used a fixed feet-spacing, so it restamped far too often and read too large at the owner's normal working / zoomed-out zoom. Three per-feature controls now sit under the "Inline label" field in each feature's Properties: **Label spacing (ft)**, **Label text size**, and **Label background** (halo on/off).
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **Anti-overlap self-thinning (the real fix, before you touch a knob):** `inlineLabelEls` floors the effective spacing to the label's OWN on-screen width + ~50% breathing room (`minGapPx = labelLen¬∑fs¬∑0.9`, converted back to feet at the live zoom), and takes the LOOSER of that and the requested feet-spacing. So restamps can never physically overlap, a SHORT label keeps its per-type/typed spacing at working zoom (unchanged), and only labels that would collide are thinned ‚Äî which also self-thins further on zoom-out. Deliberately NOT a big fixed floor (an earlier `max(150, ‚Ä¶)` draft dominated every zoom and made the spacing control inert ‚Äî caught by the adversarial review and fixed before merge). Mirrors the `dimCalloutVisible` / label-collision level-of-detail patterns.
- **Controls persist per-feature, NON-STICKY:** new `labelSpacing` / `labelSize` / `labelHalo` fields written by direct `setMarkups` / `setSelEl` (never `mkStyle`/`liveMarkup`), so a tweak can't bleed into the next drawn shape (the B620 trap). One undo frame per commit. **No schema migration** ‚Äî the fields ride through `markups`/`migrateRoad` verbatim (additive, like B620's `inlineLabel`). Defaults keep the per-type spacing (150/350/700) ¬∑ size 11 ¬∑ halo on; a short label at working zoom renders exactly as before, only overlapping / zoomed-out cases thin. The "background on" state reuses the existing white halo (`paintOrder` stroke `#fff`) for legibility over aerial; "off" removes it. Panel controls live in a render-body FUNCTION `inlineLabelControls` (returns JSX, not a `<Component>` ‚Äî no remount).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** direct follow-on to **B620** (inline line labels ‚Äî shipped the renderer + per-type spacing); this adds the per-label knobs + self-thinning the owner asked for on live use. Not a recurrence ‚Äî B620's behavior held; these are net-new controls. Not shared with Doc Review (`inlineLabelEls` lives only in `SitePlanner.jsx`; DocReview has no labeled-line renderer).
- **PDF-PARITY note (pre-existing, honest):** the export path (`buildExportSvg`) CLONES the live SVG, so inline-label density in a PNG/PDF tracks whatever zoom the export ran at. This zoom-dependence already existed pre-B678 via the `minSegPx` LOD (B620); the anti-overlap min-gap mildly amplifies it but is far gentler than the rejected fixed floor. No `data-export="skip"` on the label, so halo/size/spacing render identically on screen and in export at a given zoom. A fully zoom-independent export (re-render at the export scale) is a larger, separate change, noted for a future pass.

### B679 ‚Äî Double-click a callout or text box to enter edit mode `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(owner chat 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B679** ‚Äî see B678's renumber note. Branch `claude/polyline-label-controls-6hvn8e`.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî proven with a REAL physical double-click gesture headlessly). Covered by `verify-b678-681-label-callout.mjs` (callout, text box, AND line all open their editor on a real double-click) + a source guard.** Double-clicking a callout / text box did nothing ‚Äî the `onDoubleClick` handler existed but never fired for the user: `startMoveCallout` calls `setPointerCapture` on pointerdown to keep a drag alive past the SVG edge, and pointer capture SUPPRESSES the browser's synthetic `dblclick` (the B620 harness even worked around this by dispatching a raw event).
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **Fix ‚Äî reconstruct the browser's own double-click test:** `e.detail` is 0 on a `pointerdown` (per the Pointer Events spec ‚Äî click-count only rides mouse events), so a small `isDoubleTap(e, id)` replays the native rule ‚Äî a second press on the SAME feature within **350 ms AND within 14 px** of the first (time **and** distance gates). Added to `startMoveCallout` (box part), `startMoveMarkup` (line/polyline/easement), and `startMoveEl` (non-grouped centerline road) ‚Üí opens the in-place editor instead of starting a move. The **distance gate is what stops a "click here to select, then press over there to drag" gesture from misfiring as an edit** (an adversarial-review finding ‚Äî fixed before merge). Edits only **UNLOCKED** features (a locked line/road stays select-only ‚Äî also a review finding). Opens the same editor the old `onDoubleClick` did (`beginEditCallout` / `beginEditInline`), kept as a secondary path. Also restores the promised double-click-to-edit for the B620 inline labels (same root cause), so the tool hints ("double-click to edit") are now true for callouts, text boxes, and labeled lines/roads.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** the shipped `onDoubleClick` wiring (B620/B32) was present but non-functional under pointer capture ‚Äî this is the missing gesture reconstruction, not a duplicate. No prior double-tap helper (grepped `isDoubleTap` / `setPointerCapture`).

### B680 ‚Äî "Edit text" overlay lined up exactly with the callout box (no second offset box) `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #ui  *(owner chat 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B680** ‚Äî see B678's renumber note. Branch `claude/polyline-label-controls-6hvn8e`.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session (Verify: sandbox). `verify-b678-681-label-callout.mjs`: at NORMAL zoom box 144√ó51 == editor 144√ó51 (exact overlay, Œîcenter 0); ZOOMED OUT box 52√ó19 ‚Üí editor floors to a typeable 64√ó30, committed box hidden both times.** Clicking ‚úé Edit text (or double-clicking) popped a rounded edit box offset OUTSIDE the selected callout, doubling it. Root cause: the committed callout box stayed VISIBLE behind the editor, and the editor's size floor made it wider/taller than a small/zoomed-out box, so it spilled out both sides.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **Fix:** the committed `<rect>` + blue selection chrome are **hidden while THAT callout's editor is open** (`editCallout?.id !== c.id`), so only ONE box ‚Äî the textarea (its own accent focus outline + the callout's fill) ‚Äî is ever on screen. That kills the doubling at the source, so the editor keeps its committed-box geometry (same origin / size / corner radius ‚Üí **exact overlay for a normal callout**) while retaining a small screen-px minimum (64√ó30) purely so a tiny / zoomed-way-out / empty callout stays a usable typing target (the review caught that removing the floor made a zoomed-out new callout un-typeable ‚Äî restored, now safe because the box is hidden).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** distinct root cause from B679 (this is overlay geometry, not event wiring ‚Äî verified independently). Composes with **B616** (WYSIWYG editor symmetric margins) and **B566** (callout padding) ‚Äî the padX/padY math is unchanged; the fix is hiding the committed box + chrome during edit.

### B681 ‚Äî Callout/text alignment buttons: Word-style icons instead of cryptic glyphs `[Site Planner]` (task) #site-planner #ui  *(owner chat 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-4"; minted **B681** ‚Äî see B678's renumber note. Branch `claude/polyline-label-controls-6hvn8e`.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session (Verify: sandbox). `verify-b678-681-label-callout.mjs` asserts the align button renders the SVG icon (4 stacked `<line>` rows), not a glyph.** The align L/C/R buttons used cryptic unicode (`‚á§ ‚â£ ‚á•`, ex-`‚§ô ‚â° ‚§ö`). Replaced with a module-scope `AlignIcon` component ‚Äî the familiar Word-style stacked-rows glyph (long rows span the width; short rows anchor left / centre / right per `dir`), drawn in `currentColor` so it inherits the button's active/idle state. Existing `seg()` active styling + titles/aria-labels kept.
- Verify: sandbox

Doc Review's parallel text `align` control is the shared `PropertyPanel` `EnumControl`, a plain **dropdown with readable word options** ("left/center/right") ‚Äî no cryptic glyph to fix ‚Äî so it was deliberately left as-is (converting the generic enum control to icon buttons would touch every enum property and is out of scope). Noted for transparency.

- **Final rail: ‚àë Yield ¬∑ ‚¨° Parcel ¬∑ ‚öê Analysis ¬∑ ‚ñ¶ References ¬∑ ‚öô Standards** (owner confirmed 2026-07-06: keep Yield and Analysis separate ‚Äî no "Insights" merge). Parcel selection still opens the Parcel panel (a true list+detail destination).
- **Canvas-shift discipline:** still ONE reconcile effect (`panelShiftRef`) ‚Äî the companion's presence just joins the `want` predicate, so select/deselect never visually jumps the drawing and narrow rotation can't strand an offset.
- **Small-screen (B556-compatible, verified at 400px):** tap = select only (guard intact, now structural); a **‚úé Properties pill** (bottom-left, clear of the Tools FAB) appears for a qualifying selection and explicitly opens the companion overlay (`narrowProps`), with an ‚úï close + auto-close on deselect; a panel open on narrow stacks the companion inside the existing overlay. The auto-open effect now handles ONLY parcels; the two markup flows that force-opened the props tab rely on selection alone.
- Dedup: B556 (Done) set the phone guard this preserves; B313/B464-B468 unrelated locking UX.
- **Follow-up 2026-07-06 (owner refinement, √ó1 ‚Äî Verify: sandbox, headless `ui-audit/verify-b656-close.mjs` 14/14):** the companion had no explicit **close** on desktop ‚Äî only the fold chevron (the ‚úï existed on the phone overlay only). Owner asked for a close that **hides the panel but KEEPS the element selected** (handles stay), reopening on the next click of the element. Added `propsDismissed` state + a header ‚úï (always present; on the phone-pill overlay it still just closes the overlay, else it dismisses); `companionOpen` gains `!propsDismissed`; the canvas-shift `want` predicate now drops the shift when dismissed-with-no-panel (else a blank rail / phantom offset); an effect keyed on `sel` identity clears the dismiss on any (re)selection (every canvas click re-fires `setSel({...})` through `startMoveEl`, so re-clicking the same element reopens); the ‚úé Properties pill also clears it so a narrow reopen can't get stuck. Verified: ‚úï hides props while the element stays selected (12 selection-chrome nodes persist), no leftover panel column, re-click reopens, and with Yield open the ‚úï leaves Yield in place ‚Äî all in `SitePlanner.jsx`, no data-model change.

- Verify: sandbox

- **`lib/overlayPdf.js`:** `rasterizePage(pdf, page, {knockout})` + the pixel pass factored out as pure exported `knockoutNearWhite` (unit-tested; threshold ‚â•247/channel, alpha-only).

### B73 ‚Äî Calibrate the overlay to the drawing scale (default), with optional precise alignment `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner  *(long-standing; closed 2026-07-06 with B654 ‚Äî its last leg (one SHARED calibration flow) landed there; the bulk shipped earlier across B576 + the B73-fallback cluster.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî every path exists in the References panel:** auto-read scale note via PDF text (`detectedScale` + "Read from sheet ‚Ä¶ Apply" readout with override); sheet-size sanity vs standard plot sizes (`detectSheet`, non-standard = visible "may be shrunk" warning, never silently trusted); common-civil-scale dropdown + Bluebeam-style custom `page=real` entry; **trace-a-known-dimension fallback** (now shared with the aerial via B654); **opt-in 2-point/N-point "Align to map"** as a similarity transform with the fit-**residual** readout.
- **Conscious deviation, recorded:** 3+ pairs solve a least-squares **similarity** (rigid) fit + residual ‚Äî NOT the affine/rubber-sheet the original text floated; the residual readout is the honest tell when a distorted sheet needs more than rigid. GeoPDF auto-placement stays unbuilt (the item itself de-emphasized it: rare for civil site plans).

### B671 ‚Äî Element-level sync, phase 2/5: per-element write path in cloudSync (edit lock still ON; dual-write bridge) `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #persistence  *(owner brief 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B671** ‚Äî see B670. Branch `claude/site-elements-schema-sync-4p2x5b`.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED this session (Verify: sandbox; the signed-in dual-write parity spot-check parks as V221). lint 0 ¬∑ 2,728 tests (+30 across `elementSync`/`elementApi`/`zOrder`) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless `ui-audit/verify-b671-element-sync.mjs` 3/3.** The save side now commits PER ELEMENT through the B670 `commit_elements` RPC, alongside the unchanged whole-doc blob save (dual-write ‚Äî the read path stays on the blob until B672), all while the single-tab edit lock is still on (single writer ‚Üí safe to ship alone). Signed-out mode is untouched (the engine only activates cloud-active).
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **New:** `lib/elementSync.js` (the injectable diff engine), `lib/elementApi.js` (commit_elements RPC wrapper + fetch + pure unload keepalive), `lib/zOrder.js` (explicit z utilities) + `test/elementSync.test.js` / `test/elementApi.test.js` / `test/zOrder.test.js`.
- **Diff-based committer:** a shadow map of last-committed elements keyed by (kind,id), diffed at the central autosave effect ‚Äî so all ~68 scattered `setEls` sites, undo/redo (snapshot restore re-commits as a normal rev-checked edit, NO special case), paste, generation, and prunes are covered by construction. Boundaries: create/delete immediate, ~750ms per-element trailing debounce for text/live-pickers, `flushElems()` at the onUp gesture-end sites (groupMove, markup-draw, and the draw/resize/vertex fallthrough) for immediacy. A group drag / 30-element paste commits as ONE batch RPC. The engine SEEDS its shadow from the site's current rows before diffing (a ready-gate), so a plain load never re-creates existing elements.
- **Rev-guarded LWW:** every commit sends its expected rev; a miss adopts the RPC's returned current row and re-commits local on top (last-write-wins) via the debounce cadence (never a hot loop), emitting a typed conflict event that B673 will turn into a toast. Per-SITE serialized batches (reuse `serializeWrites.js`) ‚Üí two commits to the same element can't interleave (retires the B528/B529 in-tab write-write race class). Dirty queue + exponential backoff (1s‚Üí30s √ó5 ‚Üí 'failed', stays queued) surfaced through the existing cloud badge (syncing(n)/retrying/failed) + `reportClientEvent` ‚Äî LOUD-FAILURE. Unload keepalive posts pending ops straight to the RPC endpoint.
- **z_index (v12):** the `Z_LAYER` type table still dominates paint order; `z` replaces ARRAY POSITION as the within-type tiebreak in `byZ` (render + hit-test now deterministic by (type-layer, z, id)). `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 11‚Üí12 assigns a gapped z (`ensureZ`, idx*1024 ‚Äî the same rule as the B670 SQL backfill) only when missing, returning the same reference untouched otherwise (no re-load churn). New elements get a top-of-collection z assigned in the engine and patched back onto the canvas so the row's `z_index` and `data.z` agree (the B672 rebuild reads z from data). Fixtures + the identity/idempotence tests updated for the additive field.
- **Dual-write (sequencing amendment held):** the whole-doc blob save + `deletedIds`/union-merge machinery are UNCHANGED this phase ‚Äî reads stay on the blob until B672's cutover, so a row-commit failure risks nothing. Killing the blob write path moves to B672.
- **‚è≥ V221 (signed-in):** the engine only runs cloud-active, which the sandbox can't reach ‚Äî the live check confirms edits land in `site_elements` (rev bumps, new-element z on top, group = one batch, offline dirty-queue flush). See VERIFICATION.md V221.
- DEDUPE-FIRST: net-new (see B670); supersedes the whole-doc save path's write side for signed-in use.

### B670 ‚Äî Element-level sync, phase 1/5: `site_elements` schema, migration, RLS, realtime publication, batch RPC `[Site Planner / Platform]` (task) #site-planner #persistence #infra  *(owner brief 2026-07-06, arrived as "NEW-1" of a 5-phase element-sync program; minted **B670** ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B666 on merge-in of `origin/main`: a concurrent module-nav session took B665‚ÄìB669, so this program is **B670‚ÄìB674**. Branch `claude/site-elements-schema-sync-4p2x5b`.)*
`[x]` **SHIPPED this session (Verify: sandbox ‚Äî DB fidelity proven headlessly against LIVE prod via Supabase MCP). lint 0 ¬∑ 2,636 tests (+13 `elementRows`) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP/backlog drift green.** New canonical per-element store `site_elements` (one row per drawn element) replaces the whole-site jsonb blob as the source phases B671‚ÄìB674 build on. Applied to prod `lyeqzkuiwngunutlkkmi`; backfill exploded all 28 sites ‚Üí **496 el + 80 parcel + 8 markup + 6 measure + 3 callout + 34 tombstone rows**; fidelity SELECT = **0 mismatches** (rebuilt collections `is distinct from` the blob, deletedIds compared as sets).
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-06 from chat
- **Files:** `db/site_elements.sql` (table + partial index + 4 RLS policies + realtime publication + `commit_elements` RPC), `db/site_elements_backfill.sql` (re-runnable explode), `db/site_elements_down.sql` (rows‚Üíblob emergency revert), `db/site_elements_fidelity.sql` (read-only 0-row check), `lib/elementRows.js` (pure JS mirror `explodeModel`/`rowsToModel`) + `test/elementRows.test.js`.
- **‚öÝ PK is `(site_id, kind, id)`, not the brief's `(site_id, id)` (owner-approved).** The fidelity check caught a legacy id reused ACROSS collections ‚Äî `e6327` is BOTH an el and a markup on one site (minted before the B591 per-tab salt). `(site_id, id)` would drop one; `(site_id, kind, id)` keeps both and is the truer model (an id is unique within its collection). Ops carry `kind`, so the RPC/realtime/client changes are mechanical. Owner chose "keep all IDs as-is" over re-minting the stray (AskUserQuestion 2026-07-06). Resolves the brief's internal conflict between "reuse IDs verbatim / do NOT mint new IDs" and "PK (site_id, id)" in favor of the former.
- **Scope = all 5 vector collections** (els, markups, measures, callouts, parcels), not just els[] ‚Äî killing union-merge later (B672) with any collection left blob-side would re-open the resurrection class (B276/B556/B612). Raster-bearing underlay/sheetOverlays/parcelDrawings + settings/meta/elevation stay in `sites.data` as the slim "site header".
- **RLS mirrors the parent site's ACTUAL predicate** (own-or-team via `is_team_member`, per `team_sharing.sql`) through an exists-subquery on `sites`. Tombstoning is an UPDATE (team members can delete elements); hard DELETE = purge-only (owner / team-admin). Verified: RLS enabled, 4 policies, function `SECURITY INVOKER` with pinned `search_path=public` (no advisor flags).
- **Realtime:** `site_elements` added to `supabase_realtime` (was empty ‚Äî first table). Replica identity stays default/PK: tombstones are UPDATEs carrying the new row, old-record payloads never needed. Actual browser event delivery is live-verified in B672 (its own V-entry).
- **`commit_elements(p_site, p_ops)` batch RPC** ‚Äî SECURITY INVOKER, ONE transaction, per-op rev guard, stamps `updated_by=auth.uid()`; ops `{op:create|update|delete|restore, id, kind, z, expected, data}`; returns per-op status (`ok|conflict|deleted|exists|missing`) + the CURRENT row on any miss so the client needs no refetch round trip. `create` over a same-kind tombstone auto-restores; `delete` of an already-tombstoned row is idempotent. Smoke-tested: a kind-less op RAISES (loud). Authenticated-through-RLS calls are exercised in B671.
- **Blob freeze deferred to B672's read cutover** (sequencing amendment): B671 dual-writes so cross-device reads stay fresh; killing blob writes while reads still come from the blob would serve stale data. The backfill is re-run at the B672 cutover (only-if-row-older guards make it safe).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** per-element sync is net-new ‚Äî no prior B#. Cross-refs: B257 (Later ‚Äî Scheduler realtime, different module/project), tombstone lineage B276/B556/B591/B594/B595/B596/B612, doc-level-conflict lineage B455/B460/B471/B480/B528/B529/B558 (all Done ‚Äî the bug class this program retires by architecture).

### B666 ‚Äî Open the app exactly where you left off (last module + project) `[Shell / routing]` (feature ‚Äî owner request) #ui #persistence #infra  *(owner chat 2026-07-05 ‚Äî "if I open Planyr, it should open me to the last project that I was working on"; owner picked "exactly where I left off" (last MODULE too) over always-the-map when asked; **B666** = B665 + 1. Branch `claude/module-nav-project-persistence-ryftwj`.)*
`[x]` **Filed AND implemented + shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1. Verify: sandbox ‚Äî proven by unit tests + headless e2e (the flow is fully drivable logged-out).**
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-05 from chat
- **What shipped:** new `src/app/lastRoute.js` ‚Äî every route change writes a `{module, projectId, cross}` pointer (`planyr:lastRoute:v1`) from one Shell effect; an EMPTY-hash boot seeds the URL from it via `location.replace` BEFORE first render (Shell module scope, after `route.js` captures `INITIAL_HASH_EMPTY`), so a fresh tab lands on the last module + project. Explicit deep links ‚Äî including a literal `#/` ‚Äî always win. `resumeAllowed` stays true on a seeded boot, so the Site Planner's existing `pickResumeTarget` still picks the specific PLAN within the group (zero changes to `bootResume.js`); a deleted/foreign project in the pointer resolves to the map and the URL self-heals. Corrupt pointer ‚Üí cleared + clean boot (LOUD in effect, never a wedged startup). `RESTORE_LAST_MODULE` const documents the owner's choice (one-line flip to project-only restore).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** no prior last-route/module item (grepped `lastRoute` ¬∑ `last module` ¬∑ `resume`); `planarfit:currentSite:v1` (the Site plan pointer) is the composed-with prior art, untouched.
- **Verified:** `test/lastRoute.test.js` 12 green ¬∑ e2e `module-keepalive.spec.js` "open where you left off" pair green headless (empty-hash boot reopens `#/library`; explicit `#/` and `#/markup` honored verbatim) ¬∑ build green.

### B664 ‚Äî Review rail UX batch from live use: partial-title grouping, cryptic cal marks removed, resizable/collapsible rail, Takeoff as a tool, on-screen-first scan `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug/UX batch ‚Äî owner live walkthrough #2 of the GPL set) #doc-review #markup #ui #perf  *(owner chat 2026-07-05 with a live screenshot; **B664** = highest real B# across both files (B663) + 1. `[x]` **Filed AND implemented + shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 ¬∑ 2,616 tests green (+2 grouping cases) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless Playwright on the real GPL PDF: 10/10 checks.** Five reports:
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-05 from chat
- **1. A302 orphaned outside its own A303‚ÄìA306 group:** its wrapped-title join read "WALL SECTIONS" while the others read "WALL SECTIONS AND DETAILS" ‚Äî strict key equality broke the run. `groupSheets` now chains adjacent consecutive pages whose same-discipline titles are word-boundary PREFIX-compatible (`titlesCompatible`); a shared first word alone ("WALL TYPES") does NOT match; the group's label uses the LONGEST read (the most complete cell text). Unit-pinned both ways.
- **2. The "¬∑‚âà"/"¬∑‚úì" marks** ("a dot and an equivalent sign‚Ä¶ not sure why it's there"): removed from rail rows ‚Äî calibration status lives in the row tooltip ("scale 1\"=40' ‚Äî verify") and the Calibrate/Takeoff UI. Labels are clean: "A304", "FM101 - FIRE MARSHAL".
- **3. Left rail improved + user-sizable:** drag the right edge to resize (160‚Äì460px, persisted per device), collapse to a slim "Sheets ‚ñ∏" strip and re-expand to the saved width (persisted); rows restyled ‚Äî sheet CODE bold (how the owner navigates), title lighter beside it; group headers show "range - TITLE ¬∑ N sheets" with the same treatment.
- **4. Takeoff demoted from a docked panel to a TOOL:** a Takeoff toggle in the right tool rail (own icon, active state); the panel is hidden by default and the old always-there vertical reopen tab is gone; the choice persists. ("Why do we even have a separate section for takeoff‚Ä¶ it could just be one of the tools.")
- **5. Speed (first slice):** the sheet scan reads the ON-SCREEN page first then spirals outward, with a macrotask yield between pages ‚Äî the label you're looking at fills in immediately on a 50-page set and intake can't hog the main thread. The deeper work (Worker offload of the vector text read; pre-rasterising adjacent sheets for instant rail jumps) is scoped under the open **B484** perf item with a concrete plan note.
- **Headless-verified (built app + real GPL PDF):** no ¬∑‚âà/¬∑‚úì in the rail ¬∑ number-first labels intact ¬∑ Takeoff hidden by default, opens/closes from the rail button ¬∑ resize handle drags 224‚Üí344px ¬∑ collapse ‚Üí slim strip ‚Üí re-expand restores the saved width ¬∑ zero page errors.

- Origin: filed 2026-07-05 from chat

- **2. Nothing said a switch was in progress. Now: the stale frame **dims to 35%** and a centered chip names the target ("**Opening A305‚Ä¶**", `data-testid="sheet-switching"`), both clearing the moment the new sheet's backdrop lands (`renderedPage` tracks actual raster completion, superseded renders can't claim it). ### B652 ‚Äî Warn when active parcels overlap in Yield/Analysis `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature) #site-planner #yield #ui  *(owner brief 2026-07-04, arrived as "NEW-2"; **B652** = B651 + 1. Safety net beyond B651's split fix; DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî net-new, no prior overlap-detection item. Branch `claude/parcel-split-double-count-vk8f0j`; Origin: filed 2026-07-04 from chat)*
`[x]` **Filed AND implemented + shipped the same session per STANDING RULE #1 (paired with B651). lint 0 ¬∑ 2,526 tests (+13 in `test/polyClip.test.js`) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless `ui-audit/verify-b651-b652-parcel-split.mjs` scenario B (2 overlapping active parcels ‚Üí banner) PASS.** A non-blocking safety net beyond the split fix: the Yield panel now detects when any two ACTIVE parcels overlap and surfaces a warning banner naming them ‚Äî catching the general case (overlapping county parcels, a hand-drawn overlap, or a merge-skew reactivation) that B651's lineage guard alone can't.
- **New pure module `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/polyClip.js`** ‚Äî real polygon **intersection AREA** (there was only a boolean `ringsOverlap` + a union `mergeRings` before): ear-clipping triangulation of both lots + Sutherland‚ÄìHodgman triangle‚à©triangle clip, so it's exact for convex AND concave lots. `overlappingParcelPairs(parcels)` returns every active pair whose intersection clears `PARCEL_OVERLAP_TOL` (max of a 10 sf floor and 0.5% of the smaller lot ‚Äî so edge-adjacent lots that merely share a boundary never false-warn). 13 unit tests (containment / partial / edge-adjacent=0 / disjoint=0 / concave / parent+two-children).
- **Yield banner** (`YieldPanel`, `SitePlanner.jsx`): computed from the active parcels each render (`parcelOverlaps`), rendered as a non-blocking amber `role="alert"` banner ‚Äî "‚öÝ Active parcels overlap ‚Äî acreage may be double-counted", naming the offending parcels (lineage-aware names) + the overlap acreage, with a fix hint. Uses the panel's fixed AA-on-parchment `warnText` literal (not the theme token, per the B341/B683 parchment-surface rule).
- Verify: sandbox (pure intersection-area function unit-tested; banner headless-verified). Origin: filed 2026-07-04 from chat.

### B661 ‚Äî Parcel-snapshot builder: switch Chambers/Waller off the dark state `/query` to the AGO StratMap layer (source-resilience) `[parcel-cache / build-parcel-snapshot]` (bug) #gis #drive  *(Cowork handoff 2026-07-04 ‚Äî the B629 nightly build couldn't run because the Texas state parcel `/query` went dark on both hosts; minted **B650** = highest real B# across both files (B649) + 1; branch `claude/chambers-county-gis-down-ttqfnv`.)*
> **ID note:** first archived as **B650**, which COLLIDED with the folder-tree feature B650 (PR #495 had minted it on `main` first); briefly renumbered B658 ‚Äî which then collided AGAIN with main's landing-copy item ‚Äî and settled as **B661** (= highest real B# across both files (B660) + 1, 2026-07-05). Any code comments/commits saying B650 or B658 for this parcel fix mean THIS item.
`[x]` **Fixed + verified live this session.** The B629 builder pulled Chambers/Waller from `feature.tnris.org/.../stratmap_land_parcels_48/MapServer/0/query`, which is **dead** (503), and the migrated host `feature.geographic.texas.gov/...` returns 400 "operation is not supported" on every `/query` form (the B627 outage ‚Äî a state-side regression; that layer served Chambers `/query` fine on 2026-06-14). So the nightly build could not produce Chambers/Waller snapshots at all.
- **Root cause (verified live 2026-07-04 from this environment):** state parcel `/query` is dark on BOTH state hosts simultaneously (the exact "parcel service goes dark" pattern B629 exists to cover ‚Äî and it also degrades live Chambers/Waller clicks on planyr.io right now).
- **Fix:** `scripts/build-parcel-snapshot.mjs` is now **source-resilient** ‚Äî each county carries an ORDERED candidate list tried until one clears its min-count (keep-last-good if none). Chambers/Waller PRIMARY is now the **public AGO-hosted "StratMap25" FeatureServer** (`services1.arcgis.com/1mtXwieMId59thmg/.../2019_Texas_Parcels_StratMap/FeatureServer/0`, TPWD-owned, 2025 vintage, `capabilities:Query`, on an independent host immune to the TxGIO outage). It has **no county field**, so a new `county-poly` provider kind fetches the county boundary polygon (TxDOT `Texas_County_Boundaries`, field `CNTY_NM`) and **POST-spatial-queries** the parcels layer scoped to it. TxGIO `/query` is kept as a documented preferred-when-healthy fallback; Fort Bend stays on FBCAD (a plain query). The builder stamps `county` on each feature (the AGO layer lacks it) so the "Cached copy ¬∑ <County>" badge works.
- **Verified live end-to-end:** county-polygon scoping returns **Chambers 38,293** (matches the 2026-06-14 CCAD figure ~37.5k) ¬∑ **Waller 48,741**; paged POST pulls real parcels (owner + situs) at ~2.7 s/2000; full dry-runs of all 3 counties page ‚Üí strip/quantize ‚Üí gzip cleanly (Chambers ~2.9 MB / Waller ~6 MB / Fort Bend ~39 MB projected). +7 unit tests (`test/parcelSnapshotBuilder.test.js`: request-shaping per provider kind, county-stamp, SOURCES wiring). lint 0 ¬∑ 2458 tests ¬∑ build green.
- Also (same commit): the builder now **exits 0 cleanly when Drive isn't configured** (so a pre-turn-on nightly is a silent no-op, not a failure that files an @claude issue), and `main()` is guarded so tests can import its pure helpers without triggering a build.

### B645 ‚Äî Three-state item lifecycle (Open ‚Üí ‚è≥ Verify ‚Üí Done) with a live-verification gate `[Infra / Backlog]` (task) #infra #testing  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-04 as chat "NEW-1"; minted **B645** ‚Äî RENUMBERED from a provisional **B636** on merge-in of `origin/main`, which took B636‚ÄìB644 for the Detention-engine batch; the scripts / tests / build.yml in-code labels for this cluster keep the provisional **B636‚ÄìB640** (the BACKLOG headings, `CLAUDE.md`, and `docs/PERSISTENCE.md` use the real B645‚ÄìB649); branch `claude/backlog-infra-verify-gates-xsdxsi`; Origin: filed 2026-07-04 from chat)*

Straight-to-Done for a live item = protocol violation. Verify items **park** ‚Äî never block a session.

- Codified in the `BACKLOG.md` header protocol AND `CLAUDE.md` (lifecycle callout + the LIVE-VERIFY named rule). Verify: sandbox (protocol/doc change; green build proves it).

Cost-yield / Schedule / Doc Review / Library), exported symbols (ES `export` decls; for the sequence iframe's inline script its top-level functions ‚Äî GanttView, buildGanttSVG, ‚Ä¶), and a one-line responsibility. `/server` = folder structure only, never contents/env values.

### B649 ‚Äî Named engineering rules + Definition of Done in CLAUDE.md `[Infra]` (task) #infra  *(chat "NEW-5" 2026-07-04; minted **B649** ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional **B640** on the `origin/main` merge (Detention batch took B636‚ÄìB644); the scripts / tests / build.yml in-code labels keep the provisional B636‚ÄìB640 (CLAUDE.md uses the real B649); branch `claude/backlog-infra-verify-gates-xsdxsi`; Origin: filed 2026-07-04 from chat)*

### B643 ‚Äî Eager parcel identify prefers the authoritative county CAD over the statewide fallback, so FBCAD's OWN data shows (not just the false notice suppressed) `[Site Planner / GIS]` (enhancement ‚Äî follow-on to B634)  *(spun out of the B634/V194 fix, 2026-07-03; minted **B643** = highest real B# across both files (B642) + 1 on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî concurrent sessions took B636‚ÄìB642 for the detention-rules batch; branch `claude/cowork-verification-planyr-pxwda7`)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session ‚Äî completes the V194 job.** B634 (`isStatewideBackup`) made the false "county server unavailable" notice honest, but it left the underlying eager RACE untouched: the statewide TxGIO layer still out-raced FBCAD's Esri identify, so a Fort Bend click could select TxGIO's coarser statewide copy (no false notice now, but missing FBCAD's native appraisal values ‚Äî V194 step 3). This makes the authoritative source actually win.
- **Fix (`lib/arcgis.js`, `MapFinder.jsx`):** identify candidates are tagged `statewide` (in `resolveCandidates`); `identifyParcelEager` now **prefers a healthy real CAD** ‚Äî a real-CAD hit resolves immediately, while a statewide hit waits a bounded `BACKUP_GRACE_MS` (~1.2s) for any still-in-flight real CAD before it's accepted. A healthy-but-slightly-slower FBCAD therefore wins the race and its own parcel/appraisal data shows; a genuinely-hung CAD lets the grace elapse and TxGIO stands in ‚Äî so the click can't stall past the grace (the B244 eager guarantee is preserved, NOT the old ~8s wait). Because the CAD now wins, `hit.county` is the CAD (not statewide), so `isStatewideBackup` reads false too ‚Äî belt and suspenders on the notice.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ¬∑ **2429 tests green** (post `origin/main` merge; incl. my 3 new eager-preference/grace cases in `test/arcgis.test.js`: healthy-CAD-wins-within-grace, hung-CAD-falls-back-after-only-the-grace, ROW-resolves-on-statewide; the existing eager tests pass unchanged since they don't tag `statewide`) ¬∑ build green. **Live re-verify ‚Üí V194** (needs a browser that can reach the GIS hosts; the sandbox resets them). If a healthy FBCAD still loses in the field, `BACKUP_GRACE_MS` is a one-line tune.

Municipal adopt-by-reference overlays as thin records (Missouri City: redev <20 ac 0.75 √ó added impervious, ‚â•20 ac defers hcfcd|fortbend by watershed; Magnolia: MoCo + 10% runoff-reduction). Conflict rule: COH vs HCFCD more-restrictive governs; >20 ac in city limits draining to an HCFCD channel ‚Üí greater of (0.65 √ó tract) vs (0.75 √ó impervious). Straddle or MUD ‚Üí `ambiguous`/flag, never a silent default. Mirrors the `jurisdiction.js` registry + injectable-fetch pattern (Node-testable, no Leaflet). Subsumes the "storm outfall" rate-rules half of the `docs/ROADMAP.md` Tier-1 entry (annotated there).

BOTH Houston IDM Ch. 9 editions ‚Äî the June-2026 rewrite (flat 0.8 ac-ft/ac <20 ac + 0.4 redevelopment credit, secondary-sourced + flagged) discovered during the build, AND the superseded 2019 Fig 9.2 curve, date-picked by `ruleFor()`** ‚Äî plus municipal overlays (Missouri City, Magnolia), the greater-of conflict rule, and carrier objects that structurally cannot ship a number without its rule record (band authorities cannot emit a point ‚Äî tested). Resolver composes `identifyJurisdiction` + the **queried** TCEQ MUD layer (new `GIS_SOURCES.mud` row; TYPE-filtered so county-blanket authorities never false-flag) + nearest HCFCD channel unit (new `hcfcdChannels` row, named UNIT_NO + distance) + HCFCD watersheds (`hcfcdWatersheds`); straddle ‚Üí `ambiguous`, county-lookup outage ‚Üí `jurisdiction-unavailable` (never silently 'no requirement'). 86 detention tests ¬∑ gis-source-audit extended to this file.

### B638 ‚Äî Analysis-tier / DIA-trigger indicator `[Site Planner / Stormwater]` (feature)  *(owner brief 2026-07-03, arrived as "NEW-3"; first filed **B631**, RENUMBERED to **B638** on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî see the B636 note; code/tests keep provisional **B631**)*

Unknown channel adjacency lands in `unknowns`, never a fired trigger; flood-query outage renders 'tier can't be assessed', never clean. ### B639 ‚Äî Hydraulic regime detection: floodplain/tailwater-governed vs flowline-governed `[Site Planner / Stormwater]` (feature)  *(owner brief 2026-07-03, arrived as "NEW-4"; first filed **B632**, RENUMBERED to **B639** on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî see the B636 note; code/tests keep provisional **B632**)*
`[x]` The gate before any coverage-% claim: **Regime A** (flowline-governed ‚Äî outside SFHA, tailwater below usable pond depth ‚Üí deep outfall = real savings) vs **Regime B** (in SFHA or BFE above outfall ‚Üí BFE + mitigation govern; a deep flowline is a drowned outlet and must NOT be credited). First cut from data already flowing: FEMA zone + `STATIC_BFE` (now datum-tagged via `V_DATUM`) vs ground elevation (USGS 3DEP `sampleProfile`). BFE within ~pond-depth of ground ‚Üí Regime B. Renders as a labeled banner on the Stormwater group: regime, why (elevations used, each datum-tagged NAVD88 ‚Äî reject/flag any elevation without a datum), and the consequence sentence. Regime B adds the wet-bottom warning (permanent pool below static WS stores nothing ‚Äî Goose Creek: 393 ac-ft excavated vs 264 usable). Missing BFE (Zone A) ‚Üí honest "regime unknown ‚Äî floodplain present but no published BFE"; never guess.
- **`[x]` Shipped 2026-07-03.** `assessHydraulicRegime` ‚Äî Regime A/B/unknown from FEMA zone + STATIC_BFE (now datum-tagged via the new `V_DATUM` registry field) vs 3DEP ground (median of a centroid line, NAVD88): BFE within pond depth of grade ‚Üí B with the drowned-outlet consequence sentence + wet-bottom warning (Goose Creek 393-vs-264 in comments); Zone A no BFE ‚Üí the exact honest 'regime unknown ‚Äî floodplain present but no published BFE'; undatumed BFE rejected+flagged; ‚àí9999 sentinel handled. Banner in the Stormwater group; Regime A appends the B634 outfall screening line (fed by a user cross-section's LiDAR ditch stats when present).

### B640 ‚Äî Pond auto-size from required volume `[Site Planner / ponds]` (feature)  *(owner brief 2026-07-03, arrived as "NEW-5"; first filed **B633**, RENUMBERED to **B640** on merge-in of `origin/main` ‚Äî see the B636 note; code/tests keep provisional **B633**)*
`[x]` Close the loop: given required volume (B630) and a drawn pond, solve the footprint (bisection on the B139 expand offset via `expandPolygon`) or depth ("dig N ft deeper", same solver on depth) that satisfies it ‚Äî the inverse of `detentionStorage()` ‚Äî and surface "Size for required detention" next to "Expand this pond", applying the solved geometry through the existing expand-mode plumbing (baseline + ghost + overlap/property-line warnings, all built). Per-authority defaults (side slope, freeboard, wet-bottom geometry) from B629 params, never hardcoded. Edge cases: solver can't converge (slopes meet before depth ‚Äî `maxDepth` plateau) ‚Üí explicit message; Regime B (B632) ‚Üí subtract permanent-pool dead storage from usable volume before solving, and refuse honestly when the pool depth can't be established.
- **`[x]` Shipped 2026-07-03.** `solvePondExpansion` (bracket-doubling + bisection over an injected `volumeAt`, integer-ft rounded UP + re-verified; failure taxonomy already-sufficient / no-bracket / geometry-failed) + `solvePondDepth` (slopes-collapse via the maxDepth plateau) ‚Äî tested against the REAL `detentionStorage`. Pond panel button "‚á± Size for required detention (N ac-ft short)" solves the site shortfall through the existing B139 expand plumbing (ONE history push ‚Üí baseline + ghost + overlap/property-line warnings ‚Üí Done/Reset); Regime B adds the dead-pool volume to the target (bottom-slab trick) and REFUSES when the pool depth can't be established; failure messages offer the dig-deeper depth; per-authority pond defaults (`pondDefaultsFor`) surfaced with an Apply chip, never hardcoded. E2E: solve lands in expand mode and the Yield panel flips to Surplus.
