### B574 ‚Äî Overlay header buttons render at inconsistent sizes `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-1"; minted **B574** ‚Äî renumbered B560‚ÜíB564‚ÜíB566‚ÜíB574 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1808 tests ¬∑ build green.** The hide (eye) / lock / remove (√ó) buttons in an expanded overlay row were visibly different sizes ‚Äî the eye carried `padding:"5px 7px"` while lock/√ó used the bare `chip` style, and lock/√ó were **emoji** (üîí/‚úï) whose glyph metrics never matched the eye's inline SVG. **Fix:** one shared square **`iconBtn`** style (30√ó30, padding 0, flex none, centered) for all three, and the emoji swapped for inline-SVG `LockIcon`/`UnlockIcon`/`XIcon` matched to the eye icon's 13px box ‚Äî so the three are now provably identical (headless: all three 30√ó30, square, equal). Titles `Hide overlay`/`Show overlay`/`Remove` preserved (the B276/B277 regression harness still drives them). Verified in `ui-audit/verify-overlay-panel-controls.mjs` (B574 checks) + `verify-overlay-delete-hide.mjs` 13/13 regression.

### B575 ‚Äî Opacity: numeric input alongside the slider `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-2"; minted **B575** ‚Äî renumbered B561‚ÜíB565‚ÜíB567‚ÜíB575 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1808 tests ¬∑ build green.** Kept the range slider; added a two-way-bound numeric percent field to its right. The stored value is a 0.1‚Äì1.0 float (slider `min 0.1 max 1 step 0.05`); the field shows it as an **integer percent** (`Math.round(opacity*100)`) and writes typing back as `n/100` **clamped to 10‚Äì100%**. Dragging the slider updates the number; typing updates the slider + overlay live. Implementation holds a raw **draft** string while the field is focused (so a half-typed value isn't clobbered by the controlled round-trip) and clears it on blur so the field follows the overlay again. Verified: shows 85% for 0.85; typing 40 ‚Üí 0.40 live; below-range 5 clamps to 10% (0.1) and re-displays 10 after blur. `data-testid="overlay-opacity-pct"`.

### B576 ‚Äî Scale control: dead "custom‚Ä¶" rebuilt as Bluebeam-style page-distance = real-distance entry `[Site Planner]` (bug + feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-3"; minted **B576** ‚Äî renumbered B562‚ÜíB566‚ÜíB574‚ÜíB576 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1808 tests (+18 unit) ¬∑ build green.** The old scale `<select>` skipped its own `custom` value and only revealed the custom input when the overlay *already* sat at a non-preset size ‚Äî so picking "custom‚Ä¶" did nothing. It was also a feet-per-inch model with the page side hardcoded to 1‚Ä≥, so only `1‚Ä≥=X‚Ä≤` scales were expressible. **Rebuilt to Bluebeam Revu parity:** a preset dropdown (Engineering `1‚Ä≥=10/20/30/40/50/60/80/100‚Ä≤` + Architectural `1/8‚Ä≥ ‚Ä¶ 1‚Ä≥=1‚Ä≤-0‚Ä≥`, grouped) where picking a preset populates page=1 + real=preset and **Custom‚Ä¶** reveals editable paired fields **`[page][unit ‚ñæ] = [real][unit ‚ñæ]`** (page in/ft, real ft/in/m; page side accepts decimals AND fractions ‚Äî `0.5` or `1/2`). The page‚Üíreal ratio is the single source of truth: `feetPerInch = realFt / pageIn` feeds the **untouched** `applyOverlayScale`/`ftPerPx` model, so `0.5‚Ä≥=60‚Ä≤ ‚Üí 1‚Ä≥=120‚Ä≤` is now expressible (was impossible). The custom/preset distinction is driven off **explicit editor state** (`ovEdit`), not the overlay's current size, so Custom always reveals the fields; the dropdown **round-trips** to the matching preset when the size lands on one, else shows Custom with the fields populated. Resolved scale still drives EPSG:2278 sizing and survives reload (only `ftPerPx` changes). **Extended `lib/overlayScale.js`** (not forked): pure `parseDistanceInput` (decimals/fractions/mixed), `feetPerInchFromPair`, `SCALE_PRESETS` + `feetPerInchForPreset`, `matchScalePreset`; **+18 unit tests** (`test/overlayScale.test.js`, 30 total green). Headless: round-trips to eng-30; `1/4‚Ä≥=1‚Ä≤` ‚Üí `1‚Ä≥=4‚Ä≤`; Custom `0.5‚Ä≥=60‚Ä≤` and `1/2‚Ä≥=60‚Ä≤` both ‚Üí `1‚Ä≥=120‚Ä≤ ¬∑ 4320‚Ä≤ wide`. **Review-driven precision guard:** the custom fields default-DISPLAY a rounded value but COMMIT full-precision defaults, and the page/real inputs carry **per-field dirty flags** so an idle focus‚Üíblur of an un-edited field never re-commits the rounded display string (which would have quantized e.g. a metric `1‚Ä≥=1m`/3.2808 to 3.3). Headless: idle focus+blur leaves the scale exactly put.

### B577 ‚Äî Overlay panel cleanup: remove Width, Reset rotation, and obsolete instructional text `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-4"; minted **B577** ‚Äî renumbered B563‚ÜíB567‚ÜíB575‚ÜíB577 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1808 tests ¬∑ build green.** Removed: (1) the **Width [input] ft Ôºã/Ôºç** row (scale now owns sizing; the live width still shows in the "Now ‚âà ‚Ä¶ ¬∑ N‚Ä≤ wide" readout); (2) the **Reset rotation** button (the Rotate stepper stays); (3) the drop-zone hint tail ‚Äî "Drag it to move; set size, rotation & opacity below, then Lock it to draw on top." and the obsolete "(Sizing to the drawing scale comes next.)" parenthetical (B576 *is* that) ‚Äî keeping the core "Drop a site-plan PDF or image here or on the map ‚Äî or browse." plus the real-behavior "White paper is knocked out so the map shows through." sentence; (4) the redundant "Scale to the drawing ‚Äî sizes the sheet to true real-world feet." line above the scale control (the "Sheet: ‚Ä¶" line beneath it stays). Verified each is gone from the overlay card / drop hint while the kept lines remain.
- **Review-fix folded in (image overlays):** "scale now owns sizing" holds for **PDFs** (which carry a `sheet`, so the new scale picker renders) but NOT raster images ‚Äî the picker is gated on `o.sheet` and `openOverlayFile` returns `sheet:null` for PNG/JPG (no physical-inch dimension). So the Width row (+ ¬±10% nudge) is **kept for `!o.sheet` (image) overlays** as their one numeric size control. Caught by the post-ship adversarial review. Headless: image overlay keeps Width + has no scale picker; sheet overlay unchanged.

### B578 ‚Äî Overlay filename gets its own full-width row `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-5"; minted **B578** ‚Äî renumbered B564‚ÜíB574‚ÜíB576‚ÜíB578 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1808 tests ¬∑ build green.** The filename chip was `flex:1`, sharing the header row with the eye/lock/√ó buttons and truncating with a single-line ellipsis. Moved it to its **own full-width row above the action buttons** (`width:100%`, `whiteSpace:normal`, `overflowWrap:anywhere`, line-height 1.35) so a long name **wraps** and is fully readable; the hide/lock/√ó controls drop to their own row beneath (done together with B574's button-sizing fix, same header). Button `textContent` stays exactly the filename (the overlay-row regression harness matches it). Verified: a 72-char name takes ~full panel width (248/268px), wraps to 3 lines (63px tall), and sits above the action buttons.

<!-- Bug-hunt ROUND 14 (B559 ‚Äî lap 2 of 4 owner-requested laps): a 6-lens Workflow (doc-review-pipeline /
     shared-markup / storage-read / shell-routing / date-handling / coverage-logic). 9 raw ‚Üí 9 "confirmed"
     by the panel, but on my own re-read only ONE survived ‚Äî the rest were over-confirmed false positives /
     by-design and were REJECTED: crossSections (NO producer ‚Äî always empty; round-5 already refuted),
     pdf.js width fallback (modern pdf.js always sets .width; proposed fallback ‚âà font height, not run width),
     measure.js dimension-in-rollup (dimension is category:"shape" ‚Äî rollup deliberately sums measure-tools;
     including it would double-count), DocReview docIntent "race" (the `=== undefined` one-shot guard can't be
     overwritten ‚Äî misread), coverage hydrate bounds (already validates all four via `.every(Number.isFinite)`),
     fileIndex epoch fallback (a missing date sorting oldest is a fine default), nearby-radius slider cap
     (a 10-mi UI cap is a defensible deliberate choice). -->

### B559 ‚Äî Timestamp comparisons assumed a number ‚Üí a string `updatedAt` silently picks the OLDER copy (merge + legacy-prune) `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug ‚Äî latent data-loss) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 14, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B558 a concurrent main took for the take-over-editing fix below)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1789 tests ¬∑ build green.** `mergeSiteContent` (siteModel.js newer-wins) and `pruneMigratedLegacy` (storage.js) compared `updatedAt` with `>=` assuming a ms number ‚Äî but `createSiteModel` keeps whatever it's given (`p.updatedAt || Date.now()`), so an imported/legacy record can carry an ISO **string**, and `"2025-‚Ä¶" >= 1718‚Ä¶` is a silent **false**. In the merge that would pick the OLDER copy as "newer" (scalar/meta from the stale side; union still keeps drawn work, but the wrong meta + a wrong prune could strand/drop a newer edit). **Fix:** a shared exported `toMs(v)` coercion (string‚Üí`Date.parse`, number‚Üíitself, else 0) applied at both comparison sites. Unit tests (`test/siteModel.test.js`: toMs + a newer-ISO-vs-older-number merge) + anti-drift guard. Latent (updatedAt is ~always a number in practice) but the consequence is data-loss, so worth the cheap guard. **This lap's hunt was mostly false positives (7 of 9 rejected on re-read) ‚Äî this is the one genuine find.**
### B558 ‚Äî Phantom "changed in another session / Take over editing" re-prompt after a delete (no real 2nd tab) `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-29 on planyr.io: "I click take over editing and a second later it still offers it‚Ä¶ I'm not even sure another tab has it open"; the decisive clue ‚Äî "if I MOVE a building it doesn't pop back up, but if I DELETE one it does"; minted **B558** = highest real B# across both files (B557) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B556 two concurrent mains took (B556 mobile properties-panel, B557 round-13 batch); branch `claude/take-over-editing-reprompt-fxteik`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1786 tests (8 new) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless 6/6 (`ui-audit/verify-delete-tombstone.mjs`).**
- **Root cause (ONE bug, not two ‚Äî explains BOTH the re-prompt AND the "no other tab" phantom):** the banner re-appearing is the **blue cloud-CONFLICT banner** (`cloudConflict`, "changed in another session"), driven by the **B459 thin-clobber guard** (`wouldThinClobber` in `cloudSync.js`) ‚Äî NOT the amber Web-Locks read-only banner. That guard blocks a push that drops **‚â•2 items the cloud still has with no delete-tombstone to explain the drop**, treating it as a stale-tab clobber. The bug: **`deleteSel` removed the building AND all its bonded children (dog-ears / sidewalks / parking, `attachedTo`) from `els` but recorded NO tombstone** ‚Äî only `removeOverlay` (B276) ever tombstoned. So deleting one building dropped ‚â•2 items unexplained ‚Üí the guard FALSELY rejected the save as a cross-session conflict, with **no second session anywhere**. A **MOVE** keeps the item count (`lost = 0`), so it never tripped ‚Äî exactly the owner's move-vs-delete tell. Secondary latent bug from the same gap: "Take over editing" union-merges the cloud copy back, **resurrecting the just-deleted building** (the never-ending loop).
- **The whole CLASS, not just building-delete (owner asked "what else causes this?"):** any DELIBERATE local op that shrinks the plan by ‚â•2 vs the last-synced baseline with no tombstone. Found + fixed all of them:
  - **Deletes** (building+assembly, multi-select, parcel, callout, markup, parcel-drawing, stranded-zone auto-prune on reshape) ‚Üí now **tombstone** the removed ids (mirrors `removeOverlay`/B276). New shared `tombstone(ids)` helper in `SitePlanner.jsx`; wired into `deleteSel` (both branches), `deleteDrawing`, and the strand-prune. This both clears the false conflict AND makes deletions stick through `mergeSiteContent` (no resurrection).
  - **Undo of a multi-element add** (drawing a building adds building+parking+sidewalk in ONE undo frame ‚Üí one Ctrl-Z drops ‚â•2), **Redo**, **cancelled drag**, and **Restore to a thinner version** ‚Üí tombstones are wrong here (a redo must re-add the items), so instead the active tab rebases its own thin-clobber baseline via new **`noteLocalContent(id, model)`** (`cloudSync.js`, re-exported through `storage.js`), called from `applySnapshot` (undo/redo/cancel) and the version-restore handler.
- **Why the 8 South stale-clobber protection (the whole point of B459) is untouched:** `noteLocalContent` only ADJUSTS an existing baseline and is called ONLY on an explicit user undo/redo/restore ‚Äî a passive STALE background tab never does those, so its baseline stays at the cloud's fuller count and its thin push still BLOCKS. The cross-session CAS **version** guard (B314) also still rejects a genuine concurrent change. Proved by a dedicated unit test.
- **NOT triggers (verified):** move / resize / rotate / group / add / split-parcel / vertex-delete / cross-tab+takeover merges (all union-up or count-neutral).
- **Files:** `SitePlanner.jsx` (`tombstone` helper + `deleteSel`/`deleteDrawing`/strand-prune tombstoning; `noteLocalContent` in `applySnapshot` + restore), `lib/cloudSync.js` (`noteLocalContent`), `lib/storage.js` (re-export). Tests: `test/cloudThinGuard.test.js` (+4 ‚Äî undo rebase, restore rebase, never-fabricate-baseline, 8-South-still-blocks) + the headless harness.
- **PR #418 ‚Üí main.** Live signed-in confirmation (the conflict banner itself only renders when cloud-active) owed as **V171**.

     gis-builders / calculators / auth-profile / layer-status / a11y-2). 8 raw ‚Üí 7 confirmed; all 7
     clean low-risk fixes shipped as one umbrella. print-export, gis-builders, and calculators came
     back CLEAN (no wrong output/requests/numbers ‚Äî consistent with the round-12 yield audit).
     Renumbered B556‚ÜíB557 ‚Äî a concurrent main took B556 for the mobile properties-panel fix below. -->

### B557 ‚Äî Round-13 robustness + accessibility batch (auth null-guards ¬∑ layer-lifecycle cleanup ¬∑ LayerPanel/menu ARIA) `[Site Planner / robustness + a11y]` (bug umbrella)  *(bug-hunt round 13, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B556 a concurrent main took)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1786 tests ¬∑ GIS audit OK ¬∑ build green.** Seven small, confirmed, low-risk fixes from the lap-1 hunt:
- **Auth email null-guards** ‚Äî `Shell.jsx` (pill title + dropdown) and `AuthPanel.jsx` (Profile tab) rendered `user.email` unguarded ‚Üí a missing email would show "undefined". Now `user?.email || "(no email)"`/`""`, matching the codebase's existing defensive pattern.
- **Layer-lifecycle cleanup (`layers.js`)** ‚Äî (HIGH) `attachFeatureRetry` scheduled a backoff `setTimeout(refresh)` never cleared when the layer toggled off ‚Üí a timer fired `refresh()` on a detached layer; now `onRemove` clears it (Leaflet calls onRemove on `map.removeLayer`, chained to the original). `reportCacheAge`'s async cache-age fetch now checks `lyr._map` before `onStatus`, so it can't report age on a since-removed layer.
- **LayerPanel ARIA** ‚Äî the group collapse buttons (Map layers / Jurisdictions / Utility evidence) and the "Show N layers with no local data" reveal button gained `aria-expanded` + a descriptive `aria-label`.
- **ProjectBreadcrumb manage menu** ‚Äî the portal Rename/Delete menu gained `role="menu"`/`role="menuitem"` + the backdrop `role="presentation"`.
- Anti-drift guards in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. Pure-additive ARIA + defensive guards ‚Äî no behavior change for the happy path.

### B556 ‚Äî On a phone, tapping an element auto-opens the properties panel (buries the canvas) `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-28 on iPhone; minted **B556** = highest real B# across both files (B555) + 1; branch `claude/mobile-safari-pinch-menu-3gon8x`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1782 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless 2/2.** On a phone the left menu is a full overlay (`position:absolute`, ~320px, zIndex 1100) that buries the canvas, so the Bluebeam-style "select ‚Üí open its inspector" auto-open was the wrong default there ‚Äî a single tap to select an element/parcel covered the drawing. Two narrow-gated changes (‚â§760px, the existing `narrow` state; desktop unchanged):
  - **Auto-open gated (the fix).** The selection‚Üípanel effect now early-returns `if (narrow && !leftPanel)` ‚Äî on a phone with the panel closed, tapping just **selects** (handles/rotation/resize controls still appear) and does NOT pop the overlay; the user opens Properties from the **Element** tab when they want it. If a panel is **already** open it still re-syncs to the new selection. One guard covers all selection kinds (`el`/`callout`/`markup`/`parcel`) ‚Äî verified by the adversarial review (the per-handler `startMoveEl/Markup/Callout/Parcel` paths all route through this one effect).
  - **View-shift reconciliation (review-found, B556 finding #1).** The pan-compensation effect (which offsets the drawing when the desktop panel resizes the canvas) was rewritten to track the **exact** px shift applied in a ref and reconcile to the desired value on `[leftPanel, narrow]` ‚Äî so the phone overlay never shifts the drawing, and a mid-session **rotation** (crossing the 760px breakpoint with a panel open) can't leave a residual sideways offset. Replaces the old `prevPanelOpen` open/close toggle.
- **NOT changed (deliberate, confirmed by the review):** explicit **create** actions still open their panel ‚Äî dropping/importing a drawing or duplicating an overlay (`setLeftPanel("overlay")`), finishing an easement/markup draw. Those are deliberate, not incidental taps; the overlay move/scale/rotate tap handlers never opened the panel to begin with.
- **Verify:** `ui-audit/verify-mobile-no-autopanel.mjs` 2/2 ‚Äî phone keeps the panel CLOSED after selecting a building (handles still show); desktop still OPENS it (unchanged). Adversarial review workflow (6 findings ‚Üí 3 confirmed; 2 already-handled/no-op, 1 view-shift fix applied; overlay finding dismissed as a wrong-premise false positive). The on-device feel (tap to select, then open Properties manually) is owed as **V170**.

### B555 ‚Äî Planner two-finger pinch is "super buggy" on mobile Safari `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-28 once actually planning a parcel on mobile Safari; minted **B555** = highest real B# across both files (B554) + 1; branch `claude/mobile-safari-pinch-menu-3gon8x`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1782 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless 3/3.** The Site Planner's pinch-zoom ran on **pointer** events. iOS Safari's multi-touch pointer-event support is unreliable ‚Äî it routinely fires a spurious `pointercancel` on the first finger the instant a second touches, which tore down the pinch mid-gesture and let a one-finger pan fight it (the "super buggy" feel: jumpy, dropping out, sticking). Rewrote the gesture on **native touch events** (`onTouchStart/Move/End/Cancel` on the canvas `<svg>`):
  - `e.touches` always carries every active finger each frame, so the gesture never "loses" a finger the way pointer events did on iOS.
  - View updates are throttled to **one per `requestAnimationFrame`** so a heavy plan doesn't re-render on every raw `touchmove` (the other half of the jank).
  - On the 2nd finger landing, any one-finger pan that just started is torn down **and the view restored** to its pre-pan offset, so the pinch can't jump in.
  - While ‚â•2 fingers are down (`touchCountRef`), the pointer-driven pan/draw/vertex-edit handlers all bail, so nothing competes with the pinch. Single-finger pan/draw are untouched (the guard is false for one finger).
  - The canvas already sets `touch-action: none`, so the browser's own pinch-zoom is suppressed and the (passive) React touch handlers need no `preventDefault`. Blur/visibility recovery (`abortGesture`) also clears the new pinch state + cancels any pending frame. The old pointer-pinch refs (`pinchRef` etc.) are left vestigial only where `abortGesture` still clears them.
  - Reuses the shared `pinchZoom`/`midpoint`/`distance` engine (`shared/viewport/viewportTransform.js`) ‚Äî the math was already correct; only the event source changed.
- **Verify:** `ui-audit/verify-planner-pinch.mjs` (CDP multi-touch, 390√ó844) 3/3 ‚Äî pinch-out zooms in, pinch-in within the same gesture zooms back out, a second pinch still works (not stuck). The actual iOS flakiness only reproduces on a real device (headless Chromium's pointer multi-touch is well-behaved) ‚Üí real-device confirm owed as **V169**.

### B554 ‚Äî Mobile Safari: pinch pops the tab overview; left menu open by default; Select-parcels button covered `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-28 on mobile Safari; minted **B554** = highest real B# across both files (B553) + 1 ‚Äî the B25431/B5263/B3340 hits in this file are CSS hex colours, not IDs; branch `claude/mobile-safari-pinch-menu-3gon8x`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless 6/6.** Three mobile complaints, all fixed:
- **Pinch opened all Safari tabs.** A two-finger pinch on the page was hitting Safari's own page-zoom / "show all tabs" gesture instead of zooming the map. `index.html` now `preventDefault`s `gesturestart`/`gesturechange`/`gestureend` (registered before React, `{passive:false}`) and the viewport meta adds `maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no, viewport-fit=cover`. Safari's `gesture*` events are SEPARATE from the touch/pointer events Leaflet (map) and the planner SVG canvas use for their own pinch-zoom, so in-app zoom is unaffected ‚Äî only the browser's page-zoom/tab-grid is suppressed.
- **Left "Your sites" menu opened by default.** `MapFinder.jsx` now defaults the sites panel **closed** on a phone (‚â§760px `matchMedia`); desktop keeps its persisted state. Tap the "Your sites" header to open it.
- **"Select parcels" button covered.** On a phone the three top panels (centered search pill `maxWidth: calc(100% - 540px)`, the left sites list, the right layers box) overlapped and buried the Select-parcels button. Added a phone reflow (mirrors the planner's B113 ‚â§760px mode): the search pill becomes a **full-width top bar** (raised z-index), the sites + imagery/layers panels drop **below** it as collapse-to-tap chips (mutually exclusive when opened so they don't stack), the parcel-info card spans full width (its old `calc(100% - 540px)` went negative on a phone), and the Leaflet +/- zoom control moves to **bottom-left** (it was peeking out behind the bar once the side panels moved). Desktop layout is byte-for-byte unchanged (every phone style is `narrow ?`-gated).
- **Verify:** `ui-audit/verify-mobile-mapfinder.mjs` (390√ó844, logged-out) 6/6 ‚Äî Select-parcels visible/on-screen/not-covered, Your-sites collapsed by default + tap header present, bar clears the layers chip. The real iOS-Safari pinch confirm can't run in headless Chromium (no `gesture*` events) ‚Üí owed as **V168**.

<!-- ROUND 11 (scheduler hardening) ‚Äî the focused B550 lap (filed in round 10): break parentId cycles
     at the data-load boundary so the Scheduler's tree-walks can't hang the tab on corrupt data. -->

### B550 ‚Äî Scheduler: a parentId cycle in loaded data could hang the tab (recursive tree-walks) `[Scheduler / robustness]` (bug ‚Äî hardening)  *(filed round 10; fixed round 11, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1782 tests ¬∑ JSX parse OK ¬∑ build green.** `public/sequence/index.html` has many recursive parent‚Üíchild tree-walks. **Sharpened analysis vs the original filing:** `sortByVisualOrder`'s `walk(null)` actually CANNOT loop on a cycle ‚Äî a cyclic node's parent chain never reaches `null`, so it's unreachable from the null-root walk (it's just orphaned). The genuine hang is the **arbitrary-root** walks ‚Äî `getSubtreeIds` (outdent) and the per-op `walk()` closures (delete/indent/insert) ‚Äî which START at a specific node that can itself be inside a cycle (e.g. `1‚Üí3‚Üí2‚Üí1`), infinite-looping. **Fix (one place protects all):** a cycle-break pass at the data-load boundary in `normalizeIds` ‚Äî for each task, walk its ancestor chain with a `seen` Set; if it loops, null that task's `parentId` (degrade to a root). After load the in-memory data is acyclic, so EVERY downstream walk terminates. The UI already prevents *creating* a cycle live, so the load path was the real exposure. Deliberately did NOT add a `visited` guard to `sortByVisualOrder` ‚Äî it can't loop on a cycle anyway, and the guard would have changed duplicate-id handling (dropping a dup ‚Üí data-loss regression). Faithful copies in `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`; tests in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js` (3-task cycle broken, valid hierarchy unchanged, arbitrary-root-walk safety) + anti-drift guard. No persistence migration (load-time normalize, lossless ‚Äî a cyclic task just becomes top-level).

     7 confirmed; 2 clean live bugs shipped, the scheduler cycle-hang class filed (B550, defensive +
     high-churn). My two fixes were minted B548/B549 then RENUMBERED to B551/B552 when a concurrent
     main took B548/B549 (+ V166) for the building-panel work below; V166‚ÜíV167. TWO rejected on my own
     re-read: ringCentroid "Math.abs(a)" (the signed area is REQUIRED ‚Äî the cross-term sign cancels
     num/denom; abs() would INVERT the centroid for CCW rings, introducing the very bug claimed) and a
     MarkupRenderer callout-baseline "too low" (6px descender room isn't a clip ‚Äî cosmetic non-defect). -->

### B551 ‚Äî Stitcher left pointer-capture held on a blur/visibility abort ‚Üí frozen grab cursor `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug ‚Äî event binding) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 10, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B548 a concurrent main took)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1774 tests ¬∑ build green.** The pan-interrupt recovery (`abortGesture`) needs the pointerId to `releasePointerCapture`, but the window-blur / visibility-hidden handlers called it with NO argument, so `pid != null` was false ‚Üí capture was never released. Alt-tabbing mid-pan left the SVG with a frozen grab cursor that swallowed clicks until refocus. **Fix:** store `pointerId` in `drag.current` on pan-start and have `recover` pass `drag.current.pointerId` so the capture is actually released. Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ Runtime eyeball (pan + alt-tab ‚Üí no stuck cursor) ‚Üí V167.**

### B552 ‚Äî pendingLegacyCount over-counted vs the list/import (the B128 count-mismatch class) `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug ‚Äî count) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 10; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B549 a concurrent main took)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1774 tests ¬∑ build green.** `pendingLegacyCount` looped raw localStorage keys and counted records with a missing/falsy normalized id, but `importLegacyIntoCloud` (and `pendingLegacySites`) skip those ‚Äî so the "N sites to import" badge could read 3 while only 2 actually import (the B128 symptom). **Fix:** `pendingLegacyCount` now returns `pendingLegacySites(uid).length`, so the count can never disagree with the list or with what import copies. (Likely resolves the root of the filed B128 ‚Äî left B128 open pending an owner repro to confirm.) Anti-drift guard added.

### B548 ‚Äî Building dimensions mislabeled + mis-bound: "Width/Depth" ‚Üí dock-relative "Length/Depth" `[Site Planner / massing panel]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 as "NEW-1"; minted **B548** = highest real B# across both files (B547) + 1 ‚Äî twice-renumbered as concurrent merges took the lower IDs first (B542 Files-button, B543 Deed/Title, then B544/B545 bug-hunt round 9); branch `claude/building-dimensions-binding-andglj`)*
`[x]` **DONE + shipped this session ‚Äî fix + headless V166. lint 0 ¬∑ 1746 tests ¬∑ build green.** The Selected ¬∑ Building panel bound "Width"‚Üí`el.w` and "Depth"‚Üí`el.h` (raw axes), so a tall footprint (`w 328 √ó h 1159`) printed the impossible **Depth 1159**. The dock-relative depth (`footprintDepth`, B417/B418) was already used by the canvas red dimension but never by the panel.
- **Fix:** added pure `footprintAxes(el)` (which raw axis is depth vs length, off `dockSidesFor`) + `footprintLength(el)` in `lib/dockZones.js`; refactored `footprintDepth` onto the shared resolver (byte-identical output). The building panel now shows **Length** (= dock-parallel wall, `footprintLength`) and **Depth** (= dock-perpendicular span, `footprintDepth`); each edit drives the mapped physical edge via `resizeSelEl({ [ax.length|ax.depth]: n })`. The reported building now reads **Length 1159 / Depth 328**. The dock-doors readout repointed from `max(w,h)` to `footprintLength` (count unchanged, now explicit). "Width" retired for buildings (paving/pond keep their own labels).
- **No persistence migration** ‚Äî buildings store only `w`/`h`; this is display + edit-binding only, stored geometry untouched and reinterpreted via the dock axis at render (lossless). Dock-door RENDERING (~L9663) already arrays doors along the long wall ‚Äî left correct. Rotation independence verified (359¬∞ leaves Length/Depth unchanged).
- **Verified:** `test/dockZones.test.js` +4 (footprintAxes/footprintLength incl. the exact 328√ó1159 case) ¬∑ full suite **1746 green** ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless **V166** (`ui-audit/verify-b548-b549-building-panel.mjs`, 11/11). Reachable logged-out ‚Üí fully verified here, nothing signed-in pending.
- **Dedup:** not a re-file of B417/B418 (those made the CANVAS depth dock-relative; this is the PANEL finally adopting it). Distinct from B121/B225/B226 (dimension-callout overlap/visibility).

### B549 ‚Äî Reorganize the Selected ¬∑ Building panel into 4 concept groups; promote Loading; compact dock features `[Site Planner / massing panel]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 as "NEW-2"; minted **B549**; sibling of B548 ‚Äî same branch/PR; twice-renumbered as concurrent merges took the provisional B543 then B545)*
`[x]` **DONE + shipped this session ‚Äî headless V166. lint 0 ¬∑ 1746 tests ¬∑ build green.** The flat, interleaved building inspector is now one grouped block (`SitePlanner.jsx`), real-building-only (`isBuilding`), in four weight/size-labeled groups (hierarchy by weight/size, never fading ‚Äî house rule):
- **Footprint** ‚Äî Length, Depth (the B548 dock-relative pair, bare labels). **Loading** ‚Äî Docks promoted as the section anchor, with the dock-feature controls beneath it (Dock zones / Car parking / Bump-outs, the Add-layer choosers, per-zone depths). **Structure** ‚Äî Clear height, Slab (auto ‚áÑ override affordance kept). **Placement** ‚Äî Rotation, moved to the end.
- **Compacted** the three dock-feature rows from the tall label + sub-label + two-big-buttons block into single-line `label ¬∑ [‚àí] count [Ôºã]` steppers (sub-caption ‚Üí button tooltip); standardized affordances. Nothing dropped ‚Äî full field inventory preserved (Footprint-area + Dock-doors readout + Pin/Delete stay at the bottom). Dog-ear (bump-out) selection keeps its prior Width/Depth + Rotation + Docks path unchanged.
- **"Rear-load" never existed** in the code (only Single-load / Cross-dock / No docks) ‚Äî nothing removed; "No docks" stays (a legitimate spec-building state).
- **Verified:** headless **V166** asserts the four groups render in order with Rotation last and every control present, alongside B548's checks (11/11); 1746 tests green; lint 0; build green; `SitePlannerApp` still its own lazy chunk.
- **Dedup:** net-new; no prior Open/Done item covers a building-panel reorg (checked both files + origin/main; the lower IDs were taken by the Files-button removal, Deed/Title rail, and bug-hunt round 9).

     empty-collection / number-display / export-safety / effect-correctness). 7 raw ‚Üí 6 adversarially
     confirmed; 4 shipped this lap. TWO were REJECTED on my own re-read: a FileBrowser "await refresh()"
     (its reqRef already guards stale state ‚Äî a micro-opt, not a defect) and a DocReview boot-effect dep

### B544 ‚Äî PDF export leaked a blob URL via an 8-second revoke timer `[Site Planner / export]` (bug ‚Äî resource leak) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 9, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1772 tests ¬∑ build green.** The Download-PDF path created an object URL and revoked it on an 8s `setTimeout`, so rapid re-exports (or navigating away) accumulated blob URLs in memory. **Fix:** revoke immediately after the synthetic `click()`, matching the proven PNG-export path in the same file (which already revokes inline). Anti-drift guard added.

### B545 ‚Äî MapFinder address search had no request-token ‚Üí a slow result clobbered a newer search `[Site Planner / map]` (bug ‚Äî stale response) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 9; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1772 tests ¬∑ build green.** `goAddress`/`selectParcelAt` awaited geocode + parcel-identify with no generation guard, so two quick searches raced: a slow first could land after the second, setting `parcelInfo` (and ADDING a parcel) for the wrong address, and its `finally` cleared the shared busy flag mid-second-search. **Fix:** an `addrTokRef` generation counter ‚Äî `goAddress` claims a token and drops its result if a newer search started; `selectParcelAt` takes the token and neither applies info NOR adds the (stale) parcel once superseded. Reuses the codebase's existing cancelled-flag pattern. **‚è≥ Rapid-search runtime check ‚Üí V165.**

### B546 ‚Äî Stitcher calibration could display a non-finite scale ("1\" ‚âà ‚àû'") `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug ‚Äî number display) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 9; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1772 tests ¬∑ build green.** The composite-scale readout gated on `ftPerUnit ?`, but `Infinity`/`NaN` are truthy, so a corrupt loaded `ftPerUnit` (or a future bad calibration) would render "Scale set ¬∑ 1\" ‚âà ‚àû'". **Fix:** `Number.isFinite(ftPerUnit) && ftPerUnit` on both the color and the text; plus a belt-and-suspenders `u >= 1` guard in `commitCalibrate` so a near-zero span can never produce `Infinity` (mirrors `doCalibrate`, also catches NaN). Anti-drift guard added.

### B547 ‚Äî Stitcher takeoff rollup could show "NaN ac ¬∑ NaN sf ¬∑ NaN ft" `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug ‚Äî number display) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 9; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1772 tests ¬∑ build green.** The stitched-takeoff totals rendered `totals.areaSf`/`totals.distFt` unguarded; one degenerate measure (NaN from malformed/loaded geometry) propagates through the reduce and shows "NaN" for the whole rollup. **Fix:** `Number.isFinite` guard on each total, falling back to "‚Äî" (matches the per-measure display's conditional). Anti-drift guard added.

<!-- ROUND 8 (UX honesty) ‚Äî cleared the last two small round-5 filed items: geocoder error
     distinction (B540) + cloud-badge popover persistence (B541). -->

### B540 ‚Äî Address geocoder conflated "service unreachable" with "address not found" `[Site Planner / geocode]` (bug ‚Äî honesty/UX)  *(filed round 5; fixed round 8, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1761 tests ¬∑ build green.** `geocodeAddress` returned `null` for BOTH a real "no candidates" result AND any network failure ‚Äî so when the lookup host was down/blocked, the user was told the address doesn't exist (and might abandon a valid one). **Fix (B540 contract):** track `reachedAny` (did any provider respond OK?). Return `{lat,lon,label}` on a hit, `null` only when a provider was REACHED and authoritatively found nothing, and `{ error }` when NO provider was reachable. Both callers updated to show the distinct "lookup unavailable" message vs "not found": `MapFinder.goAddress` + `SitePlanner.addByAddress` (each gained `if (hit && hit.error) ‚Ä¶`). `test/geocode.test.js` updated (the old "null on throw" test now asserts `{error}`) + 3 new contract tests. Anti-drift guard added.

### B541 ‚Äî Cloud-sync badge popover closed itself on benign sync-state churn `[Shared / UI]` (bug ‚Äî UX)  *(filed round 5; fixed round 8)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1761 tests ¬∑ build green.** `CloudSyncBadge` keyed its error-boundary on `String(state)`, remounting the whole badge (and discarding the inner `Badge`'s open popover) on every save-cycle transition. **Fix:** the boundary is no longer remounted via a `key`; instead it takes a `resetKey` prop and clears a transient crash in `componentDidUpdate` when it changes. So the popover survives state transitions (the Badge's own effect still closes it when the state stops being actionable) while crash-recovery is fully preserved (a crash clears on the next state change, as before) and the badge still always renders the live `state`. Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ Signed-in eyeball that the detail popover stays open across a save cycle ‚Üí V164** (same signed-in session as the B528/B529 check).

### B543 ‚Äî Promote the Deed / Title reader (Schedule B + metes-and-bounds) to a first-class tool-rail entry `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 as "NEW-1"; minted **B543** = highest real B# across both files (B542) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B542 that collided with the already-merged üóÇ Files-button-removal B542; branch `claude/deed-title-tool-rail-ldvtxe`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1749 tests (+7) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless 12/12.** The Title reader & metes-and-bounds plotter (`setTitleOpen(true)`) was fully implemented but its only launcher was a menu item buried at the bottom of the top-toolbar **File ‚ñæ** (export) dropdown ‚Äî semantically wrong (reading a deed / plotting a boundary is not an export action) and undiscoverable. **Promoted it to a first-class rail launcher**, no modal-logic change:
- **Rail launcher** added to the bottom of the right-rail "Tools" group (after the Boundary `AnchoredMenu`, before `railHdr("Site elements")`): shared `rbtn(false)` style, `<ToolIcon id="deed" />` glyph, two-line anatomy like Building/Road ‚Äî label **"Deed / Title‚Ä¶"** + sub-label "Schedule B ¬∑ metes & bounds". `onClick` = `if (narrow) setMobileTools(false); setTitleErr(""); setTitleOpen(true);`. It's a **launcher, not a tool**: never calls `selectTool`, never gets active styling, no keyboard shortcut.
- **New `deed` glyph** in `ICON_PATHS` ‚Äî a folded-corner document page over text lines, same `currentColor`/stroke style as the other tool icons (a missing case would render the button blank).
- **Old export-menu launcher removed cleanly** ‚Äî deleted the "Title reader / metes & bounds‚Ä¶" `menuItem` **and** its preceding divider from the export `AnchoredMenu`; Export PNG + Download PDF remain, no dangling separator. `setTitleOpen(true)` now fires from exactly one place (relocated, not duplicated).
- **Edge cases honored:** phone/narrow rail dismisses the scrim (`setMobileTools(false)`) so the `zIndex:3000` modal isn't shown over a dimmed overlay; renders unconditionally (the modal handles the missing-API-key case internally).
- **Dedupe:** distinct from **B180** (the Files repository, which only *reuses* the metes-and-bounds parser for title-commitment ‚Üí boundary) and from the existing Title-reader modal work (B25/B26 parser, B48 client key, "Plot as easement") ‚Äî purely a launcher relocation/discoverability fix.
- **Tests:** `test/deedTitleRail.test.js` (render-free anti-drift guard, bugHuntGuards-style) ‚Äî asserts the `tool-deed` rail launcher exists, opens the modal, has the glyph, and is not a tool mode; and that the export menu no longer lists the title-reader item. Live headless run (`scratchpad/verify-b542.mjs`, logged-out, seeded site): launcher present/visible/labelled/glyphed/not-active, click opens the modal (Plot on canvas), File ‚ñæ no longer lists it but keeps Export PNG / Download PDF, 0 page errors.

<!-- ROUND 7 (concurrency) ‚Äî the careful lap for the two HIGH save-race bugs (B528/B529) that
     rounds 4-5 filed and deferred as "needs its own pass". Fixed via a SHARED per-id write
     serializer so the two modules can't drift, with a unit test that models the exact race and
     proves the genuine cross-device guard is NOT weakened. -->

### B528 ‚Äî Doc Review: overlapping same-id cloud saves false-tripped the self-conflict guard ‚Üí autosave lockout `[Doc Review / persistence]` (bug ‚Äî concurrency) ‚Äî HIGH  *(filed round 4; fixed round 7, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1752 tests ¬∑ build green.** `usePersistence`'s `writeNow` (debounce flush + visibility/unmount/manual flush) could fire a second cloud write for the same review id BEFORE the first resolved; both read the same `reviewVersions[id]`, so the loser failed the CAS and was reported as a foreign "conflict" ‚Äî which trips `canCloudSave` and silently stops autosave until reload (no data loss, but alarming + sticky). **Fix:** a per-id write serializer (`src/shared/cloud/serializeWrites.js` `makeWriteSerializer`) wraps `upsertReview` ‚Üí the second write for an id WAITS for the in-flight one, so it reads the version that write threaded back and the CAS succeeds. The genuine cross-device guard (`casUpsert`) is untouched. Unit test `test/serializeWrites.test.js` models the exact race (serialized ‚Üí both succeed; na√Øve ‚Üí one false-conflicts) AND asserts a real stale version still conflicts. Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ Signed-in runtime confirm (rapid edit + tab-hide doesn't flip to "saved elsewhere") ‚Üí V164.**

### B529 ‚Äî Site Planner: overlapping same-id cloud saves false-tripped the self-conflict guard `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug ‚Äî concurrency) ‚Äî HIGH  *(filed round 4; fixed round 7)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1752 tests ¬∑ build green.** Same root cause in `cloudSync.cloudUpsert` (Site Planner). **Fix:** the SAME shared serializer wraps `cloudUpsert` per site id, so serialization also keeps the B459 thin-clobber content baseline (`siteContent`/`siteTombs`) fresh for the second write. Lifting the latch into one shared helper means the two modules can't drift. Covered by the shared `test/serializeWrites.test.js` + an anti-drift guard. **‚è≥ Signed-in runtime confirm ‚Üí V164.**

<!-- ROUND 6 (a11y) ‚Äî shipped the B530‚ÄìB532 accessibility group that rounds 4-5 filed but deferred
     (cleanly-actionable work shouldn't keep getting deferred). Escape-to-close + dialog roles on the
     two scrim modals; keyboard-reachable rows/section headers. B530 verified live headless. -->

### B530 ‚Äî AuthPanel modals: no Escape-to-close, no dialog role `[Site Planner / a11y]` (bug ‚Äî accessibility)  *(filed round 4; fixed round 6, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified LIVE (headless). lint 0 ¬∑ 1736 tests ¬∑ build green.** The shared `Wrap` (sign-in / Profile / Settings / password views all route through it) had no Escape handler and no dialog semantics. **Fix:** a window `keydown` Escape listener mounted while open (cleaned up on unmount) + `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Account"` ‚Äî one change covers every AuthPanel view. **Verified:** `ui-audit/verify-b530-modal-escape.mjs` drives a real Chromium (Supabase-configured build) ‚Üí modal opens as a dialog, Escape closes it, repeatably (5/5). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B531 ‚Äî Reviews row + planner Section header weren't keyboard-reachable `[Doc Review + Site Planner / a11y]` (bug ‚Äî accessibility)  *(filed round 4; fixed round 6)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1736 tests ¬∑ build green.** Click-only `<div>`s: the `ReviewsBar` review row and the `SitePlanner` left-rail `Section` header. **Fix:** both got `role="button" tabIndex={0}` + an Enter/Space `onKeyDown` mirroring the `onClick` (the Section header also `aria-expanded={open}`; the row an `aria-label`). The Reviews bar TOGGLE was already a real `<button>` ‚Äî only the row/header needed it. Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ The signed-in row-activate + the Section-header keyboard-toggle eyeball ‚Üí V163.**

### B532 ‚Äî SiteReviewModal: no Escape-to-close `[Site Planner / a11y]` (bug ‚Äî accessibility)  *(filed round 4; fixed round 6)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1736 tests ¬∑ build green.** The unsaved-on-device review modal closed only by √ó / scrim-click. **Fix:** an Escape `keydown` listener (closes with the current `savedCount`) + `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label` ‚Äî the same pattern B530 verified live. Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ The legacy-gated Escape eyeball (needs pending on-device sites) ‚Üí V163.**

### B542 ‚Äî Remove the now-redundant üóÇ Files button + ProjectFilesDrawer from the Site Planner banner `[Site Planner / Library]` (cleanup)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "get rid of the files option in the top banner as it's likely now redundant" ‚Äî follow-up to B496 once the Library tab shipped; branch `claude/library-drive-module-fpzane`; minted **B542** = highest real B# (B541) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite 1732 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-library-tab.mjs` 8/8 (real Chromium, logged-out ‚Äî Library tab + Review empty state unaffected).** The Site Planner's row-1 **üóÇ Files** pill opened the old `ProjectFilesDrawer` slide-over ‚Äî a second, in-context copy of the file browser that the new **Library** tab (B496) now does in full. Redundant, so removed.
- **`[x]` Removed in `SitePlanner.jsx`:** the üóÇ Files `<button>`, the `<ProjectFilesDrawer>` render, the `filesOpen` state, and the `import` ‚Äî that button was the drawer's ONLY trigger, so the drawer was fully orphaned.
- **`[x]` Deleted the orphaned `src/workspaces/doc-review/components/ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx`** (no remaining importer). The shared file-storage data layer (`reviewStore`/`autofiling`/`fileFacts`) is untouched ‚Äî Library + Review still use it.
- **`[x]` Retired the obsolete B527 guard** in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` (it read the now-deleted drawer file for a dark-mode theming check ‚Äî moot once the component is gone).
- The shared `onOpenReviewInDocReview` Shell handler stays (Library uses it); its now-unused SitePlanner prop is harmless. Logged-out-verifiable (pure planner UI) ‚Äî no signed-in path owed.

     async-errors/parse-robust/react-correctness/coords-projection/storage-migration). 13 raw ‚Üí 11
     adversarially-confirmed real; the 7 clean crash-safety/correctness ones shipped this lap, 2

### B533 ‚Äî 3DEP elevation converted metres with the international foot, not the US survey foot `[Site Planner / units]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 5, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests ¬∑ build green.** `elevation.js` used `M_TO_FT = 3.280839895` (international foot) while the whole project ‚Äî EPSG:2278 State Plane, `FT_PER_M` in `shared/coordinates` (B57b) ‚Äî is US survey feet (3937/1200). A ~0.0002% drift (~0.66 ft per 100 m), low-impact today (elevation is transient/screening-only) but inconsistent with every other foot in the app and a latent error if a ditch invert is ever persisted. **Fix:** `M_TO_FT = 3937 / 1200`. Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B534 ‚Äî Stitcher boot-resume IIFE had no rejection handler ‚Üí unhandled promise on a resume failure `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests ¬∑ build green.** The mount effect that resumes the last stitch session ran an async IIFE with no `.catch`; a throw from `loadReview`/`currentUid`/`reconcile`/`loadStitch` was an unhandled rejection. **Fix:** `.catch(() => {})` on the IIFE ‚Äî `loadStitch` owns its own `busy` via `finally`, so swallowing falls cleanly to the empty stitcher instead of a half-started boot. Anti-drift guard added.

### B535 ‚Äî DocReview boot-resume IIFE had no rejection handler ‚Üí unhandled promise `[Doc Review]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests ¬∑ build green.** Same class as B534 in `DocReview.jsx`: the boot-resume IIFE guarded only the localStorage reads; `currentUid`/`loadReview`/`reconcile`/`loadSingleReview` awaited outside any catch. **Fix:** `.catch(() => {})` ‚Äî `loadSingleReview` owns its own "Opening‚Ä¶" overlay via `finally`, so a resume failure falls to the empty state (the documented intended behavior). Anti-drift guard added.

### B536 ‚Äî Stitcher addGroup: one failed page-render silently aborted the WHOLE group-drop (no sheets, no message) `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests ¬∑ build green.** `renderPageToImage` inside addGroup's page loop had no per-page guard; a single corrupt page threw past the loop (the `finally` cleared `busy`, so not a stuck spinner ‚Äî but `setPlaced` never ran, so a drop of N pages produced NOTHING with no explanation). **Fix:** per-page try/catch collects `renderFailed`, skips the bad page, and the notice now reports "N page(s) couldn't render (re-drop to retry)" ‚Äî or, if every page failed, an explicit "couldn't render ‚Ä¶ re-drop" instead of a silent no-op. Anti-drift guard added.

### B537 ‚Äî buildFilingPlan crashed ("not iterable") on a set missing pageNums `[Doc Review / auto-filing]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests (+2) ¬∑ build green.** `disciplineSplit.buildFilingPlan` spread `...s.pageNums` in three places (the `allSeen` reduce + the `claimed`/`entries` build) with no guard; a malformed `decision.sets` entry lacking `pageNums` threw and crashed filing. **Fix:** `...((s && s.pageNums) || [])` at all three sites ‚Üí a missing array degrades to empty, not a throw. Behavioral tests in `test/disciplineSplit.test.js` (single + multi-discipline malformed input ‚Üí no throw).

### B538 ‚Äî sheetScale fracToNum allowed a zero denominator ‚Üí Infinity/NaN `[Doc Review / scale]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests ¬∑ build green.** `fracToNum` divided user-supplied callout text without a zero-denominator guard ("5/0" ‚Üí Infinity, "0/0" ‚Üí NaN). Downstream `parseSheetScale` range checks neutralize it today, but the helper should not leak non-finite values. **Fix:** return 0 when the denominator is 0 (both mixed and plain fraction branches). Anti-drift guard added.

### B539 ‚Äî currentAccessToken: one corrupt auth-token blob aborted the whole scan ‚Üí signed-in user looked signed-out to the keepalive push `[Auth / Storage]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1733 tests ¬∑ build green.** `supabase.currentAccessToken` wrapped the WHOLE key-scan loop in one try/catch, so a single corrupt/truncated `sb-*-auth-token` blob threw at `JSON.parse` and returned null ‚Äî missing any later valid session (the B452 keepalive unload-flush would then push as anonymous ‚Üí a contributing factor to "edits not saved"). **Fix:** parse each entry inside its own try/catch so a bad key is skipped and the scan continues. (The agent's first-pass fix ‚Äî a type-check after JSON.parse ‚Äî was rejected: the throw happens AT JSON.parse, before any check; only a per-iteration try works.) Anti-drift guard added.
