     confirmed; the 6 mechanical/low-risk ones shipped this lap (incl. B527 drawer theming), the
     remaining 5 larger/riskier ones filed (2 HIGH save-race fixes B528/B529 + an a11y group
     B530‚ÄìB532 for dedicated careful laps). overlay-stitch came back clean. -->

### B522 ‚Äî Mapillary edge proxy crashed (uncaught 500) on a no-body upstream status (204/304) `[Edge / GIS]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 4, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1725 tests ¬∑ build green.** `functions/api/mapillary/[[path]].js` forwarded the upstream status verbatim into `new Response(JSON.stringify(...), {status})`; a 204/304 (body forbidden) throws a TypeError ‚Üí uncaught platform 500, the exact hard error this graceful proxy promises never to emit. **Fix:** clamp a no-body/out-of-range status to 502 before responding (honest "Mapillary HTTP N" message kept). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B523 ‚Äî /api/file edge proxy crashed on a no-body upstream status (204/304) `[Edge / Doc Review]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1725 tests ¬∑ build green.** Same class as B522 in `functions/api/file.js` (dormant-gated behind `DOC_FILING_URL`). **Fix:** clamp the forwarded status to 502 for a no-body/empty-body upstream (a 200-with-empty-body no longer reports a self-contradicting "ok:false ‚Ä¶ HTTP 200"). Anti-drift guard added.

### B524 ‚Äî Library workspace had no loader skin ‚Üí generic "Loading‚Ä¶" caption `[Shell / loader]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1725 tests (+1) ¬∑ build green.** `LOADER_SKINS` had no `library` entry, so the shipped Library workspace fell back to the generic gantt skin + "Loading‚Ä¶". **Fix:** added a `library` skin ("Opening library‚Ä¶"). Test in `test/moduleLoaderTheme.test.js`.

### B525 ‚Äî ProjectBreadcrumb cloud-unreachable warn row hardcoded a light-amber box (light slab in dark mode) `[Shared / theming]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1725 tests ¬∑ build green.** The warn row inside the token-themed breadcrumb dropdown hardcoded `#fef3c7`/`#92400e`, so it read as a light slab in dark mode (B341 trap). **Fix:** repointed to `var(--surface-page)` + `var(--warn-text)` border/text. Anti-drift guard added.

### B526 ‚Äî ToolRail was permanently dark regardless of theme (dark slab between light chrome in light mode) `[Shared / theming]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1725 tests ¬∑ build green.** The Doc Review tool rail hardcoded `#191613`/`#ece7db`/`#9b9482` + dark border/divider, so in light theme it was a dark slab sandwiched between light chrome ‚Äî the eye-strain case B318 forbids. **Fix:** repointed CHROME/INK/MUTED + border + divider to theme tokens (`--surface-raised`/`--text-primary`/`--text-secondary`/`--border-default`); the active-button text stays near-black on the light amber module accent (correct in both themes). Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ Light + dark eyeball of the rail ‚Üí V162.**

### B527 ‚Äî Project Files drawer rendered a white slab with invisible text in dark mode `[Doc Review / theming]` (bug) ‚Äî HIGH (visible)  *(bug-hunt round 4, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ tests green ¬∑ build green.** `ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx` already had a token-backed `PAL`, but ~14 scattered spots hardcoded light hexes ‚Äî the drawer panel + refresh/select/place/refile/queue-row backgrounds at `#fff`, the drop-zone/needs-filing/place-plan boxes at light creams (`#faf8f3`/`#fffbeb`/`#f3f6f4`/`#faf9f5`), and light text (`#92400e`/`#b45309`/`#2c2a26`/`#4b5563`/`#6b7280`). In dark mode the panel stayed white while `--text-primary` went light ‚Üí **white-on-white invisible content**; the B341 trap at full scale. **Fix:** repointed every background to `var(--surface-raised)`/`var(--surface-page)`, amber labels to `var(--warn-text)`, danger √ó/fail to `var(--danger-text)`, success/on-map/spatial to `var(--success-text)`, the "both" tag to `var(--status-active)`, and chip-on-accent text to `var(--on-accent)`. Solid-green action buttons (File/Place) keep white-on-green ‚Äî readable in both themes (green doesn't theme). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. **‚è≥ Light + dark eyeball of the open drawer ‚Üí V162.**

<!-- Bug-hunt ROUND 3 (B517‚ÄìB521): a 3rd 6-lens Workflow (markup-engine/pdf-ocr/print-export/
     mobile/calculators/request-builders), each finding adversarially confirmed; all fixed same lap.
     Two lenses (pdf-ocr, calculators) came back CLEAN ‚Äî correctly reported, nothing invented. -->

### B517 ‚Äî Site Planner: oil & gas / pipeline MAP OVERLAY still on the retired Harris-clipped TxRRC host ‚Üí false all-clear `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ‚Äî HIGH  *(bug-hunt round 3, 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1720 tests ¬∑ GIS audit OK ¬∑ build green.** B369 migrated the Site Analysis *count* path to the authoritative statewide RRC service, but the *map overlay* (`layers.js` txrrc_wells/txrrc_pipe) still requested `www.gis.hctx.net/.../TXRRC/{Wells,Pipelines}` ‚Äî a Harris-County republication ~99.8% incomplete outside Harris. So the panel could report thousands of wells while the map painted ~14 (the documented Chambers Co. 14-vs-8,014 false-clean) ‚Äî a genuine industrial-siting hazard. The CI guard only scanned siteAnalysis.js/jurisdiction.js, never layers.js, so the overlay was missed. **Fix:** repointed both overlays to the registry's authoritative `gis.rrc.texas.gov/.../RRC_Public_Viewer_Srvs/MapServer` (wells = sublayer 1, pipelines = sublayer 13), matching `sources.js` + the analysis path. Anti-drift guard (`test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`) asserts the retired host is gone + the authoritative one present. **‚è≥ Over-aerial eyeball (well/pipe density on a Chambers/Gulf-coast site) ‚Üí V161.**

### B518 ‚Äî Site Planner: 5 raster overlays 400'd at the GIS cache proxy (hosts missing from the allowlist) ‚Üí no outage caching `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1720 tests ¬∑ build green.** `gisProxyEnabled()` is on by default, so every raster layer is built pointing at the cache proxy ‚Äî but `ALLOWED_GIS_HOST_RE` omitted `hctx.net` (HCFCD ROW), `nationalmap.gov` (3DEP elevation), and `harcresearch.org` (MUD boundaries), so the proxy returned HTTP 400 and those layers paid a wasted round-trip every paint then fell back to a direct, **uncached** fetch (zero B445 outage resilience). **Fix:** added the three hosts to the allowlist (anchored regex keeps the anti-spoof property). Unit tests assert the hosts pass + spoofs still blocked (`test/gisProxyCore.test.js`). (Complements B517, which moved the TxRRC overlays off hctx.net onto the already-allowlisted texas.gov.)

### B519 ‚Äî Scheduler: exhibit/PDF dependency arrows drew NaN coordinates for an unparseable date `[Schedule / export]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1720 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild parse OK.** `buildGanttSVG`'s arrow guard skipped only MISSING (falsy) dates, not a truthy-but-unparseable one (e.g. an imported `2026-13-99`) ‚Äî the bar renderer correctly blanks such a task, but the arrow code still ran `xOf()` ‚Üí NaN and emitted a garbage `<path d="MNaN,‚Ä¶">` into the exported SVG (web snapshot + print PDF). **Fix:** extended the guard with `isNaN(pd(...))` on both endpoints, matching the bar renderer (`public/sequence/index.html`).

### B520 ‚Äî Site Planner / shared header: fixed notification banners overflow phone width ‚Üí action button off-screen `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1720 tests ¬∑ build green.** Every fixed top banner used `position:fixed; left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); maxWidth:<bare px>` (680/740/620/720) with no viewport cap, so on a ~390px phone the box spilled past both edges and its right-pinned action/dismiss button (Take over editing / ‚úï / Retry) was unreachable. **Fix:** changed all six SitePlanner banners + the AppHeader conflict banner to `maxWidth: min(<px>, calc(100vw - 16px))` (desktop unchanged; matches the modals' existing `92vw` pattern). **‚è≥ Phone-width tap-through of a banner button ‚Üí V161.**

### B521 ‚Äî Shared markup: hitMarkup couldn't select a 2-corner closed box (rect/ellipse/cloud/snapshot) except on its diagonal `[Shared / Markup]` (bug) ‚Äî MED (latent)  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1720 tests (+2) ¬∑ build green.** The shared `hitMarkup` gated the closed-shape interior/outline tests on `pts.length >= 3`, but Document Review commits rect/ellipse/cloud/snapshot as exactly 2 opposite corners ‚Äî so only the diagonal segment was hittable; the rendered shape was almost entirely un-clickable. Latent today (no production path imports `pickMarkup` yet ‚Äî DocReview still carries its own picker), but a real contract violation in the shared engine meant to be adopted. **Fix:** `hitMarkup` now expands a 2-corner closed box to its axis-aligned interior + outline (ellipse via the normalized equation, so a bbox corner correctly misses). Unit tests in `test/markupHitTest.test.js` (interior, each edge, ellipse-rejects-corner). Tightens the shared module toward its documented contract before DocReview migrates onto it.
     react-effects), each finding adversarially confirmed; all filed AND fixed the same lap. Two
     candidate findings were REFUTED by the skeptic (elevation.crossSections has no producer; the
     doc-review setProjectStatus path is dead code) and correctly not shipped. -->

### B511 ‚Äî Site Planner: pruneMigratedLegacy dropped a NEWER logged-out site by id alone ‚Üí silent data loss `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ‚Äî HIGH  *(bug-hunt round 2, 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1716 tests (+3) ¬∑ build green.** `pruneMigratedLegacy` (runs in `pullCloud` on every sign-in) deleted any on-device legacy site whose id merely EXISTED in the cloud map, with no timestamp check ‚Äî so: create a site logged-out ‚Üí sign in + migrate (legacy copy kept) ‚Üí sign out ‚Üí edit it logged-out ‚Üí sign back in ‚Üí the NEWER local edit was deleted before the migration modal (`pendingLegacyCount`, which keeps a legacy id newer than cloud) could ever surface it. **Fix:** prune only when `cloudMap[id].updatedAt >= legacy[id].updatedAt` (the inverse of `pendingLegacyCount`'s predicate), so B473's duplicate-reclamation still works but a newer local copy survives. Exported the function + 3 unit tests (`test/storage.test.js`: keeps-newer, prunes same/older, keeps cloud-absent). **‚è≥ Signed-in end-to-end flow ‚Üí V160.**

### B512 ‚Äî Doc Review: parseSheetScale read a rise:run SLOPE/grade ratio (or a clock time) as the drawing scale ‚Üí silent mis-calibration `[Doc Review / parsing]` (bug) ‚Äî HIGH  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1716 tests ¬∑ build green.** Step 4's bare `1:N` match had no scale-context guard, so a civil/grading sheet printing "MAX SLOPE 1:4" / "1:3 TYP" (or even "1:30 PM") was auto-calibrated to a garbage scale ‚Äî every measurement off by orders of magnitude ‚Äî on the common "scale-bar-only + slope notes" sheet. **Fix:** the ratio match now skips any `1:N` whose surrounding text carries a slope/grade keyword (slope/grade/pitch/batter/ratio/H:V/typ/max/min) or that is immediately followed by AM/PM; a real "SCALE 1:200" / bare scale-box "1:100" still parses (engineer/arch/NTS steps run first). Unit test in `test/sheetScale.test.js`. (`src/shared/files/sheetScale.js`.)

### B513 ‚Äî Doc Review: parseRevision dropped the value for the common "REVISION NO. 3" / "REV. NO. 5" forms `[Doc Review / parsing]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1716 tests (+2) ¬∑ build green.** The revision regex had no slot for an intervening NO./NUMBER/# label, so the most standard title-block wording filed with no revision tag. **Fix:** allow an optional `no.`/`number`/`#` token between the rev-word and the value, keeping the rev-word `\b` (so the "REVISIONS" heading isn't read as "Rev S") and the value `\b` (so "revision label" isn't "Rev L"). Unit tests in `test/titleBlockParse.test.js`. (`src/shared/files/titleBlockParse.js`.)

### B514 ‚Äî Doc Review: findDates accepted impossible calendar days (Feb 30, Apr 31) `[Doc Review / parsing]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1716 tests (+1) ¬∑ build green.** `valid()` capped the day at 31 for every month, so a garbage date (e.g. "02/30/2025") could become the auto-named filing date. **Fix:** real per-month day count + leap-year rule. Unit test (incl. non-leap 02/29 rejected, leap 02/29/2024 kept). (`src/shared/files/titleBlockParse.js`.)

### B515 ‚Äî Site Planner: coverage SR set aliased ESRI WKID 102739 (Texas CENTRAL/EPSG:2277) to 2278 ‚Üí layer failed CLOSED `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1716 tests (+1) ¬∑ build green.** 102739 is a DIFFERENT projection (Central zone) than 2278/102740 (South Central); a Central-zone regional extent run through the 2278 math produced a garbage bbox and marked the layer out-of-coverage across all of Texas ‚Äî violating the module's fail-OPEN guarantee. **Fix:** removed 102739 from `STATEPLANE_2278`, so an unknown SR returns null ‚Üí unknown ‚Üí available (fails open). Unit test in `test/coverage.test.js`. (Latent today ‚Äî all current regional layers are South Central.) (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/coverage.js`.)

### B516 ‚Äî Scheduler: PDF-preview postMessage listeners skipped the origin check (one persists to saved data) `[Schedule / security]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1716 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild JSX transform of the index.html block OK.** Three preview-iframe message handlers (`planarToggleCollapse`, `planarColResize`, `planarPreviewHeight`) lacked the `e.origin !== window.location.origin` guard the nav/toolbar bridges enforce ‚Äî so a cross-origin page framing `/sequence/` could postMessage `planarColResize` and mutate the user's PERSISTED export column widths. **Fix:** added the same-origin guard to all three (the legitimate same-origin blob preview iframe is unaffected). (`public/sequence/index.html`.) **‚è≥ Cross-origin attempt confirmation ‚Üí V160.**

### B504 ‚Äî Site Planner: truck-court paving double-subtracted a corner bump-out footprint ‚Üí yield understated impervious/coverage `[Site Planner / yield]` (bug)  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1707 tests (+4) ¬∑ build green.** Since B492 `relayoutSide` trims a same-side truck court IN to the clear face between corner bump-outs (`usableCourtSpan` ‚Üí the court element's `w`/`h` already excludes the bump column), but the yield loop (`SitePlanner.jsx`) STILL subtracted the bump's full footprint (`e.w*e.h`) from `paving` when a same-side court existed ‚Äî a stale de-double-count whose overlap no longer exists post-B492. Net: paving/impervious understated by the bump area (~3300 sf on the 55√ó60 default) and `open` overstated by the same; only fired when a building had both a same-side truck court AND a corner bump-out. **Fix:** removed the spurious de-double-count block ‚Äî the court area already excludes the bump, and the bump (type `building`) is counted in `bldg`, so there's no remaining overlap to remove. Premise locked by new `usableCourtSpan` unit tests in `test/dockZones.test.js` (along = full ‚àí bumpStart ‚àí bumpEnd, asymmetric shift, 1-ft floor). **This clears the entire 2026-06-27 bug-hunt queue (B500/B510, B501‚ÄìB509).**

### B505 ‚Äî Doc Review: Stitcher `loadStitch()` rejection unhandled ‚Üí noise + stuck `pendingStitch` `[Doc Review / stitch]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1703 tests (+anti-drift) ¬∑ build green.** `loadStitch()` awaits PDF/Drive/Storage I/O that can reject, but its caller `loadStitch(loadReq).then(() => onConsumeLoad())` had no `.catch` ‚Äî a corrupt/partial PDF surfaced an unhandled rejection AND `onConsumeLoad()` never ran, so the load stayed half-consumed (`loadedId` already set ‚Üí effect won't re-fire). **Fix:** added `.catch(() => onConsumeLoad && onConsumeLoad())` so the request is always consumed (`Stitcher.jsx`). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. ‚è≥ Live confirmation of the corrupt-PDF path ‚Üí V159 (needs a deliberately-bad file).

### B506 ‚Äî Site Planner: device-full autosave `pushModelToCloud().then` had no `.catch` ‚Üí stuck "saving" badge `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1703 tests ¬∑ build green.** In the B473 device-full autosave branch the `.then` set "saving" but had no `.catch`; the degrade path awaits a raw supabase upsert that can reject on a transport error, leaving the badge spinning forever (the watchdog is on a different push path). **Fix:** appended `.catch(() => { setSaveStatus("unsaved"); setSavedToCloudOnly(false); setCloudSaveFailed(true); })` (`SitePlanner.jsx`). Anti-drift guard added. ‚è≥ Live confirmation needs a forced transport failure ‚Üí V159.

### B507 ‚Äî Site Planner: manual "Save now" device-full `pushModelToCloud().then` had no `.catch` ‚Üí stuck banner `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1703 tests ¬∑ build green.** Same class in the manual Save-now handler: a rejected push left "Saving to your account‚Ä¶" on screen and never tripped the loud failed state. **Fix:** appended `.catch(() => { setSaveNowMsg(""); setLocalSaveFailed(true); setSavedToCloudOnly(false); })` (`SitePlanner.jsx`). Anti-drift guard added. ‚è≥ ‚Üí V159.

### B508 ‚Äî Site Planner: LayerPanel relevance control hardcoded warm-dark hex instead of theme tokens (B341 trap) `[Site Planner / theming]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1703 tests ¬∑ build green.** The Relevance (Show all/Dim/Hide) active segment used `#3b3a36`/`#fbfaf6` and the stale-age stamp used `#b45309` ‚Äî hardcoded, despite the file's own header rule to use theme tokens (the dark active fill barely separated from the dark frosted panel in dark mode; `contrast-audit.mjs` can't see inline hex). **Fix:** repointed the active segment to `var(--accent)`/`var(--on-accent)` and the stamp to `var(--warn-text)` (`components/LayerPanel.jsx`); no layout change. Anti-drift guard asserts no hardcoded hex + the tokens are used. ‚è≥ Dark-mode eyeball of the Relevance control ‚Üí V159.

### B509 ‚Äî Markup: PropertyPanel number/range/enum/color controls had no accessible name `[Shared / Markup / a11y]` (bug) ‚Äî MED  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1703 tests ¬∑ build green.** Non-bool rows render the caption as a detached `<div>` with no `<label>`/`htmlFor`/`aria-label`, so NumberControl/RangeControl/EnumControl/ColorControl exposed no accessible name (a screen reader announced only "number edit"/"slider"/"combo box") ‚Äî affecting every editable markup property in both workspaces. **Fix:** threaded the schema `label` into each control as `aria-label` (`src/shared/markup/PropertyPanel.jsx`). Anti-drift guard asserts all four controls receive `label` and apply `aria-label`. ‚è≥ Screen-reader pass ‚Üí V159.

### B501 ‚Äî Scheduler: deleting a task never recomputed parent roll-ups / cascade ‚Üí stale summary dates `[Schedule]` (bug) ‚Äî HIGH  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1699 tests (+4) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild JSX transform of the 625 KB index.html block OK (board still parses).** `deleteTask`/`deleteTasks` (`public/sequence/index.html`) filtered the removed rows then `renumberTasks(...)` with NO recompute ‚Äî every other structural handler (indent/outdent/paste/move) wraps with `recomputeAfterStructureChange = rollupParentDates(cascadeDates(...))` (the exact class B487 fixed). So a deleted child left its parent's summary bar/row showing a span for work that no longer existed until an unrelated edit re-triggered a rollup. **Fix:** both delete paths now wrap the filtered list with `recomputeAfterStructureChange(...)` before `renumberTasks`. New behavioral unit test (delete the later child ‚Üí parent end shrinks 06-26‚Üí06-22) + an anti-drift guard asserting both handlers carry the recompute wrap (`test/schedulerEngine.test.js`, against the mirrored engine `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`). **‚è≥ Running-app click-through ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V158** (the board can't boot headless here ‚Äî vendored React/Babel absent; covered by the unit test + esbuild parse).

### B502 ‚Äî Scheduler: InlineDate stripped the year ‚Üí silently rewrote a non-current-year date to this year on commit `[Schedule]` (bug) ‚Äî MED, data corruption  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1699 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild parse OK.** `InlineDate` (`public/sequence/index.html`) rendered month/day only; on commit `parseFlexDate` defaulted the missing year to the current year, so just opening + Enter/blur mutated a `2025-‚Ä¶` date to `2026-‚Ä¶` with no warning. **Fix:** seed the display with `toShortDate(value)` (M/D/YY ‚Äî keeps the year, reads back losslessly via `parseFlexDate`), matching every other date editor in the file. Anti-drift guard added. **‚è≥ Running-app click-through ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V158.**

### B503 ‚Äî Scheduler: MasterView `fmtDate` lacked the malformed-date guard `fmtD` got in B489 ‚Üí could render NaN/NaN/yy `[Schedule]` (bug) ‚Äî low  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1699 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild parse OK.** The dashboard's inline `fmtDate` split on `-` with no presence check (unlike the B489-hardened module-level `fmtD`), so a non-ISO value rendered `NaN/NaN/26` (and `y.slice(2)` could throw on a missing year). **Fix:** added `if (!y||!m||!d) return ""` before formatting. Anti-drift guard added.

### B510 ‚Äî Markup: Dimension tool showed "‚Äî" instead of its calibrated length `[Shared / Markup]` (bug) ‚Äî HIGH  *(filed AND fixed the same lap per STANDING RULE #1; surfaced by the 2026-06-27 bug-hunt Workflow ‚Äî found by two independent hunters, adversarially confirmed)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1691 tests (+1) ¬∑ build green.** The Dimension tool is declared `measureOutput:"length"` (`tools.matrix.js`) and rendered via `measureLabel` in `MarkupRenderer.jsx`, but `measureValue` (`src/shared/markup/measure.js`) had no `dimension` branch ‚Äî it fell through to the generic return with no `lengthFt`, so `measureLabel` printed **"‚Äî"** for every calibrated Dimension (the tool's whole purpose ‚Äî a ticked line labelled with its real length ‚Äî was broken). Verified empirically: pre-fix `measureLabel({kind:"dimension",pts:[{0,0},{10,0}]}, 2)` returned `"‚Äî"` while the identical distance returned `"20.0 ft"`. **Fix:** `measureValue`'s distance branch now also handles `dimension`, preserving `m.kind` so it reports the calibrated `lengthFt` while callers still render the ticked variant. Deliberately NOT added to `MIN_MEASURE_PTS`/rollup ‚Äî a dimension is an annotation, not a takeoff total, so totals are unchanged. New regression test in `test/takeoff.test.js` (`measureLabel` ‚Üí "20.0 ft" calibrated / "set scale" uncalibrated / no-throw on empty). Pure-function fix; the on-canvas label is the visible win.

### B500 ‚Äî Pond grading tool produced invalid slope contours + floor (acute-corner spikes, no valid floor, silent mis-report) `[Site Planner / detention]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-27 on the live triangular pond off Hwy 6 N: "the pond drawing tool ‚Ä¶ calculates how the pond curvature/slopes would work ‚Ä¶ does not seem like it worked whatsoever"; civil-engineer read attached; provisional "NEW-1"; minted **B500** = highest real B# (B499) + 1; branch `claude/pond-storage-site-plan-pzkjnf`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite 1694 tests (+19 new: pondOffset 8, pondContours 11) green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-pond-contours.mjs` 9/9 (real Chromium, logged-out) on the actual acute-triangle repro.** The B497 contours reused the codebase's hand-rolled `offsetPolygon` (per-edge miter/bevel), which **spiked and self-intersected at acute corners**, returned a single ring (no pinch-off / no split), re-formed a bogus same-winding polygon past half-width (so the `aBottom===0` over-taper guard **silently misfired** ‚Üí wrong area/volume, no warning), and `smoothRing` then rounded the garbage. Root-and-branch fix:
- **`[x]` Robust offset via clipper-lib** (new `lib/pondOffset.js`, pure/unit-tested): `offsetInward(ring, dist)` runs Clipper's `ClipperOffset` with **round joins** (rounds contour corners ‚Äî also how earthwork actually grades) in the planner's **feet frame** (no reprojection), normalising orientation so a negative delta always shrinks. Returns an **array of rings**: `[]` on clean pinch-off, `[r]` normally, `[r1,r2,‚Ä¶]` when a basin splits ‚Äî never a spike, never throws (degenerate/bowtie input ‚Üí `[]`). `maxInwardOffset(ring)` binary-searches the max inscribed reach.
- **`[x]` `pondContours` rewritten** (`lib/pondGeom.js`): each level carries `rings[]`; stops at pinch-off; reports `feasible` + `maxDepth = maxInwardOffset/slope`. `smoothRing` (Chaikin) **deleted** ‚Äî round joins already produce true smooth geometry; smoothing distorted areas.
- **`[x]` `detentionStorage` rewritten** (SitePlanner.jsx): stage areas from `offsetInward`; **average-end-area volume** integrated only over slabs that actually exist (a basin that daylights before design depth no longer inflates the number); returns `feasible`/`maxDepth`. 1-entry memo so panel+label+chip don't re-run the offsets each frame.
- **`[x]` Fail LOUD on infeasibility** (silent-failure rule): renderer draws the real partial contours **plus** a red on-canvas `‚öÝ Too deep ‚Äî max ‚âà N‚Ä≤ at S:1`; panel states the exact max gradeable depth and that the volume shown is for that achievable basin; the on-plan "Holds ‚Ä¶" line reports the **achievable** depth `(max)`, not the over-stated design depth.
- **Untouched on purpose:** `offsetPolygon` stays for **parcel setbacks** (per-edge variable offset a uniform buffer can't express) and `expandPolygon` for pond "push banks out" ‚Äî neither is the reported bug.
- **Dep:** added `clipper-lib` (CAD-grade polygon-offset engine) to the lazy `SitePlannerApp` chunk (+~29 kB gzip). *(Investigated the civil-engineer-suggested JSTS first; jsts 2.12 ships no package entry point ‚Üí unimportable under Vite/vitest, so used clipper-lib, which is purpose-built for offsetting and imports cleanly ‚Äî same robust round-join + multipolygon + pinch-off behaviour.)*
- **‚è≥ Visual-over-live-aerial eyeball ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V157** (headless draws over the gray surface, not live tiles).

### B124 ‚Äî Data loss: saved work disappeared / reverted to a prior state on Schiel Road and JFK `[Persistence]` (bug) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS, critical
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî root cause fixed + shipped to `main` (PR #81) and DEPLOYED to live planyr.io; confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27, verify + adversarial agents both clean, refuter re-ran the test suite).** The core defect was in `pullCloud` (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/storage.js`): it rebuilt the per-user cache from the **cloud list alone**, silently dropping any local-only site the cloud hadn't returned (a not-yet-pushed/slow-network/new site), so the resume's `loadSite(cur)` missed and bounced to the map ‚Äî "my work disappeared," re-firing on a tab-focus `SIGNED_IN` re-emit. Fixed: `pullCloud` now **merges** local+cloud via the pure, unit-tested `mergePulledSites` (storage.js ~L75) ‚Äî local-only work is kept **and re-pushed**, newer-wins on overlap; a redundant same-user `SIGNED_IN` re-emit is skipped; sign-out no longer wipes the per-user cache; cloud confirmed authoritative (data was never deleted in Supabase ‚Äî verified). Remaining ongoing-loss *incidents* are tracked separately (B134/B473 track), not this drop. No new code owed for B124.
- Part of: **B648** (Persistence & Sync epic).

### B125 ‚Äî Make cloud save status visible and write failures loud ‚Äî no silent failures `[Persistence]` (feature)
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî shipped (PR #81 + the B473/B474/B485 persistence track); confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27, verify + adversarial both clean, refuter re-ran 15/15 persistence tests + build).** All core asks are implemented in the tree: a **loud, dismissible red cloud-save-failure banner** + "Retry now" + a ~6s stall watchdog (`SitePlanner.jsx` `cloudSaveFailed`/`cloudPushWithWatchdog`/`retryCloudSave`, `role="alert"`); a **persistent header save-status indicator** (`headerSaveState` ‚Üí `AppHeader`); a **silent-no-op guard** (drawn-count read-back ‚Üí `local-save-failed` banner); a **durable IndexedDB fallback** (`lib/localDb.js`, self-healing, cloud authoritative) wired into `storage.js` (write-through + hydrate/migrate + raster eviction); and **beforeunload/visibility synchronous flush** + `flushRegistry.js` + doc-review parity. The genuinely-open remnants are separate filed follow-ons B125 does not require (B479 perf, B483 100%-full-localStorage edge, B474's owner-agreed deferral). Only auth-only/signed-in live-browser verification remains (not blocking).
- Part of: **B648** (Persistence & Sync epic).

### B36 ‚Äî Map/GIS minor robustness `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî all five sub-items shipped; confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27, verify + adversarial both clean, refuter ran lint/test 57/57).** (a) statewide-TxGIO county mislabel ‚Äî real fix: `countyAtPoint` (true point-in-county via the TxDOT layer, `lib/jurisdiction.js`) + `COUNTY_NAME_TO_KEY`, relabel wired in `MapFinder.addParcelHit` with the query point threaded through all call sites; unit-tested. (b) evidence-layer opacity slider ‚Äî shipped PR #33. (c) `largestRing` (max-|area|, winding-agnostic so a hole can't win) + area-weighted `ringCentroid` + multipart `outerRingsLngLat` (`lib/arcgis.js`); unit-tested. (d) multi-county straddle ‚Äî moot by design (a point is in one county ‚Üí `hits[0]`; multi-parcel adds each lot per click). (e) AbortController ‚Äî moot (evidence/address fetches serialized + busy-gated). The non-configured-county (Austin/DFW) relabel edge is carved into the parked B150, not B36. Only a live browser confirmation of the (a) relabel path remains (not blocking).

### B57 ‚Äî Coordinate/units consistency (core verified clean) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî closed by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27). lint 0 ¬∑ 1684 tests ¬∑ build green.** The core units/coordinate audit was already verified clean (FT_PER_DEG, lat/lng order, aerial aspect, ppfToZoom inversion, takeoff px¬≤‚Üíft¬≤, metes-and-bounds, north-up Y-flip), and sub-items (a) underlay Calibrate disabled for a from-map underlay and (c) address-search projecting via the shared 365223 model both shipped in the 2026-06-15 follow-up. The last open sub-item **(b)** is now fixed: `FT_PER_M` in `src/shared/coordinates/index.js` was the **international** foot (`1/0.3048`) while `PROJECT_CRS.unit` is `us-ft` (EPSG:2278) and the projection math uses `US_FT_M = 1200/3937` ‚Äî a ~2 ppm inconsistency. Changed `FT_PER_M` to the **US survey foot** (`3937/1200`) so the lone `metersToFeet` consumer matches the grid; `FT_PER_M`/`metersToFeet` have no other consumers (grep-confirmed), so zero behavioral impact on live code. `test/coordinates.test.js` updated to assert the us-ft value (the two assertions had enshrined the international foot). No headless/UI surface; logged-out-N/A.

### B485 ‚Äî Phone header clips its controls instead of scrolling sideways `[Shared header / mobile]` (bug)  *(surfaced 2026-06-27; minted **B485** = highest real B# across both files (B484) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional **B481** a concurrent `main` (#373) took)*
`[x]` **DONE + merged (PR #376, branch `claude/resume-planner-persistence-36zqh1`); confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27).** The shared two-row `AppHeader` overran phone width and clipped its controls under `overflow:hidden` + flex-shrink. Fix: new `useNarrow()` (matchMedia `max-width:760px`) gives each header row `overflow-x:auto` (bar hidden via `.no-hscrollbar` in `index.css`) with natural-width zones below the breakpoint so nothing clips ‚Äî it scrolls sideways exactly as V11 specifies; the brand drops to the mark to reclaim width; every change is `narrow ?`-gated so desktop is byte-identical. Verified: lint 0 ¬∑ full suite green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `ui-audit/verify-phone-layout.mjs` (real iPhone-13 emulation) 10/10 with a regression guard that fails the pre-fix build. Confirmed merged (commit ancestor of HEAD) + adversarially re-checked. **‚è≥ One physical-device check (real-finger touch + rotation/landscape)** ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V11.

### B497 ‚Äî Pond storage on the plan: stage contour lines + on-canvas storage, communicated extremely well `[Site Planner / detention]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "add a feature where pond storage calculations is shown on the site plan with contour lines and is communicated extremely well", + two refinements: "make the contours smooth like a pond would be, ie no sharp angles" and "the edge of the pond is where the slope starts when I draw it, no ‚Ä¶ 5‚Ä≤ buffer"; branch `claude/pond-storage-site-plan-pzkjnf`; minted **B497** = highest real B# (B496) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from provisional **B493‚ÜíB496** as concurrent `main` PRs kept taking B493/B494/B495/B496 while this was in flight; the commit/PR still read B493)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite 1656 tests (+15 new) green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW `ui-audit/verify-pond-contours.mjs` 12/12 (real Chromium, logged-out).** A placed detention pond now shows its **basin shape and storage right on the plan**, not just in the side panel:
- **Stage contour lines (the topographic depth rings).** Each ring is the inward offset of the top-of-bank footprint at a depth below the top ‚Äî exactly the construction `detentionStorage` already uses (`offsetPolygon(ring, slope*down)`), so the rings and the storage numbers come from one source and can never disagree. The **water surface** ring is emphasized (solid + light tint, "WS" label), the **basin floor** dashed ("Floor" label); intermediates are thin. Labels read as **real elevations** when a top-of-bank elevation is entered (e.g. "WS 95.0"), else **depth below top** ("WS ‚àí1.0‚Ä≤"). Water anchored to the top of its ring, floor to the bottom, so the callouts frame the centred pond name instead of stacking on it.
- **Smooth, pond-like rings (owner refinement).** A pure **Chaikin corner-cutting** pass (`smoothRing`) rounds the rings at draw time ‚Äî display-only; every area/volume number still reads from the true (un-smoothed) offset polygons.
- **No buffer (owner refinement).** The drawn edge **is** the top of bank where the side slope starts (`down=0` = the footprint); nothing inserts an offset.
- **On-plan storage line.** The pond label gains "Holds N ac-ft ¬∑ D‚Ä≤ deep" (same `detentionStorage` the panel reads), gated with the rings.
- **Default ON, zoom-aware, degrades cleanly.** Contours show automatically (`det.contours !== false`), with a panel toggle to hide; an **auto** ring interval (~4‚Äì6 rings, user-overridable) + an optional **top-of-bank elevation** input. The overlay is gated by `detailLabelVisible` (the B149 LOD floor) and skips sub-pixel rings (anti-flicker), so a site-overview stays clean and it reveals on zoom-in. An **over-taper** pond (side slopes meet before full depth) stops at the last valid ring and shows a "‚úï slopes meet" marker ‚Äî the on-canvas echo of the panel's existing `aBottom===0` warning.
- **Reuse, no new primitives:** new pure helpers `pondContours` / `smoothRing` / `autoContourInterval` / `contourLabelPoint` live in the existing `lib/pondGeom.js` (15 new unit tests; `offsetPolygon` injected to keep the module React-free); rendering rides the existing `renderElPx` pond branches + the existing `layoutLabels` pool; the model fields (`det.contours`, `det.contourInterval`, `det.tobElev`) are additive/lossless (no migration). One signed-in/visual-over-live-aerial click-through is logged at **V155**.

### B496 ‚Äî Promote file storage to its own **Library** workspace tab (stop overloading Review) `[Library / Doc Review / Shell]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "create a library or drive module instead of trying to do too much with the review module"; owner picked the name **Library** + "move browsing fully into Library"; branch `claude/library-drive-module-fpzane`; minted **B496** = highest real B# (B495) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional **B493** that concurrent `main` PRs took (B493 cross-module link + B494/B495) while this was in flight; the commit/PR/branch still read B493)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite 1641 tests ¬∑ build green (separate `Library` lazy chunk) ¬∑ contrast audit green incl. 5 new Library accent pairs ¬∑ NEW `ui-audit/verify-library-tab.mjs` 8/8 (real Chromium, logged-out).** The file browser was overloading **Review** (FileBrowser was literally Review's landing screen). Lifted it into its own top-level workspace so Review goes back to "open one drawing and mark it up."
- **`[x]` New `library` workspace** (`src/workspaces/library/Library.jsx` + `components/FileBrowser.jsx` *moved* git-tracked from `doc-review/components/`). Registered in the Shell `WORKSPACES` registry, `modulePrefetch`, `route.js` slug maps (`#/library`, `#/project/<id>/library`, `#/all/library`), and the AppHeader `MODULES` tab + accent maps. Lazy-loaded ‚Äî opening Review never pulls Library in and vice-versa.
- **`[x]` Module accent** `--accent-library` **teal `#0E7490`** (index.css light+dark, `palette.js`, `moduleAccent.js`) ‚Äî chosen dark enough that **white-on-teal clears WCAG AA in BOTH themes** (5.36:1), unlike Review's amber which needs dark on-accent. The 5 new pairs are registered in `contrast-audit.mjs` (all PASS). FileBrowser's own chips/drop-zone repointed from `--accent-review` ‚Üí `--accent-library`/`--on-accent-library`.
- **`[x]` Data layer stays shared, NOT moved.** `reviewStore` / `autofiling` / `fileIndex` stay in `doc-review/lib` (project-scoped, canvas-independent); Library imports them cross-workspace ‚Äî the SAME established pattern as Site Planner importing `ProjectFilesDrawer`. No new backend, tables, or keys.
- **`[x]` Review slimmed down.** Removed the `FileBrowser` import + the `browsing` landing state. With nothing open Review shows a clean empty state ("No drawing open" + a teal **Browse the Library** button + Open PDF‚Ä¶); the header **üóÇ Library** button + the open-error banner button now switch to the Library tab (`onShellSwitch("library")`); boot now resumes the last open drawing on mount (project deep-links included, keeping the wrong-project guard). Clicking a file in Library opens it in Review via the **existing** Shell `onOpenReviewInDocReview` intent (already used by the Site Planner files drawer).
- **Left intact (not "Review doing too much"):** the Site Planner's slide-over `ProjectFilesDrawer` (in-context map peek) still opens files into Review unchanged.
- **‚è≥ Signed-in file-flow click-through ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V154** (the file list is account-gated; logged-out + headless fully green here).

### B495 ‚Äî Flexible outward "layer stack": choose what goes behind a building (road / landscape buffer / sidewalk / parking) via an "Add layer ‚ñæ" chooser `[Site Planner / dock features]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "add options to switch parking behind a building for a road, or a landscape buffer behind parking ‚Ä¶ or a road behind the trailer parking's buffer"; owner confirmed: keep the fast "+" default AND add a dropdown; all four layer types; branch `claude/sidewalks-truck-courts-bump-outs-0u7o6v`; minted **B495** = highest real B# (B494) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B493 that concurrent `main` PRs took for B493/B494)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 ¬∑ full suite 1655 tests (+8) green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW `ui-audit/verify-zone-catalog.mjs` 6/6 (real Chromium, logged-out).** The two hardcoded "stack outward from a building wall" sequences (dock = court‚Üítrailer‚Üíbuffer; rear = sidewalk‚Üíparking‚Üírows) are now extensible: the fast Ôºã keeps its preset, and a new **"Add layer ‚ñæ"** chooser appends a specific catalog layer.
- **`[x]` Data-driven catalog** (`lib/dockZones.js` `ZONE_CATALOG` + `catalogDepthDefault`): court/trailer/buffer/sidewalk/parking/road, each with element type, default depth, valid sides (`dock`/`nondock`/`any`), and a `terminal` flag. **Road is terminal** (nothing stacks behind it); **landscape buffer is one concept** (`type:"landscape"`, `buffer:true`) shared with the dock buffer; trailer/court stay dock-only (B416).
- **`[x]` Generalized layout** `layoutZoneByKind(b,side,i,depths,kinds,opts)` lays an ARBITRARY chain (strip/trailer; a road along a wall is geometrically a strip ‚Äî its curbs are a render detail). `layoutZone` is now a thin wrapper ‚Üí **byte-identical** for the court/trailer/buffer sequence (parity test guards it).
- **`[x]` Generic `prevZone` chain** (SitePlanner `nextInChain`/`chainFromHead`/`dockChainOnSide`): the dock sequence + any appended layers are ONE linked list rooted at the wall. `nextInChain` follows `prevZone` and **falls back to the legacy `forCourt`/`forTrailer` bonds**, so old saved plans walk identically with **no migration**. `relayoutSide` lays the whole chain; `removeWithChildren` + `strandedZoneIds` cascade on `prevZone`; LIFO "‚àí" peels the outermost (appended road ‚Üí buffer ‚Üí trailer ‚Üí court).
- **`[x]` "Add layer ‚ñæ" chooser** in the building's Dock-features panel: two rows ‚Äî **"Behind the dock stack"** (road / landscape buffer, only once the court stack exists) and **"Rear / non-dock sides"** (sidewalk / parking / landscape / road) ‚Äî each applying to every eligible side. Dock appends join the relayout chain (`noFit`); non-dock appends are placed flush beyond the outermost element as ordinary wall-kids (the proven `fitKid` resize path; `road` added to `WALL_KID_TYPES`). Sidewalk/parking reuse the existing `addSidewalkSide`/`addParkingRowSide`.
- **Verified headless:** seeded a cross-dock building, built the preset stack, then via the chooser appended a **road** (bonds `prevZone`‚Üíbuffer, carries `travelW`, sits flush beyond the buffer at full 600‚Ä≤ wall) on both dock sides, a **landscape buffer** on the rear sides, and confirmed LIFO removal peels the road first (buffer kept). Pure planner canvas ‚Üí no signed-in path owed.
- **Known limitation (filed, minor):** a DOCK-appended layer's depth edited via the generic element editor reverts to its catalog default on the next building-resize relayout (it's not yet wired to a `zd` editor). Non-dock appended layers (wall-kids) edit normally. Follow-up only; the add/place/remove flow is complete.

### B494 ‚Äî Schedule module loading overlay sticks for a full 9 s "sometimes" (slow/buggy ready signal) `[Scheduler / loader]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "fix the loading icon for the scheduling module, seems kind of slow or buggy sometimes"; minted **B494** = highest real B# (B493) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B493 a concurrent `main` took for the cross-module-link feature)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/pdf-viewer-quality-x40e5a`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1641 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-scheduler-loader.mjs` 3/3 (+ diagnostic `observe-scheduler-loader.mjs`).**
- **Repro (headless, observed):** switch to the Schedule module ‚Üí the "assembling schedule" loading overlay (`ModuleLoader`, B224) sat visible for the FULL **9 s** before cross-fading out, regardless of how fast the embedded `/sequence/` app was. Root cause: the overlay only dropped on the embedded app's FIRST `planar:nav-state` message, whose ONLY backstop was a lone `setTimeout(‚Ä¶, 9000)`. So whenever that first signal was slow or missed ‚Äî a network hiccup, the embed's deps loading slowly, the embed not yet interactive ‚Äî the loader stuck for a flat 9 seconds (the "slow/buggy sometimes").
- **Fix (`Scheduler.jsx` + a tiny responder in `public/sequence/index.html`):** make the ready signal **reliable + bounded**. (1) **Handshake** ‚Äî on the iframe's `onLoad` the shell posts `planar:nav-request` (retried ~every 380 ms for ~2.3 s, in case the embed's own listener isn't attached yet); the embedded app answers by re-emitting `nav-state` from `latestData` (a no-op until its data has loaded), so a missed/slow first emit can no longer strand the loader ‚Äî it drops the instant the embed is interactive. (2) **Bounded fallbacks** ‚Äî reveal the embed ~2.5 s after its document loads even if it never answers (a slow/broken embed shouldn't hold a full-screen spinner), with a 6 s absolute backstop if `onLoad` itself never fires. `markReady()` + a ref make every path idempotent (one cross-fade). The proactive `nav-state` fast path is unchanged, so a clean boot still drops the loader immediately.
- **Verified:** `verify-scheduler-loader.mjs` 3/3 (real built shell, the embed stubbed via request-interception so the handshake/fallback paths actually run ‚Äî the real embed's CDN is blocked in the sandbox so its iframe never finishes loading here): a **responsive** embed drops the loader at **~520 ms** via the handshake (was 9000 ms); a **silent** embed is bounded to **~3000 ms** (onLoad+2.5 s), not 9 s, and doesn't drop prematurely. Diagnostic `observe-scheduler-loader.mjs` shows the sandbox worst case (embed never signals, `onLoad` never fires) improved 9.5 s ‚Üí 6.5 s (the backstop).
- **Follow-up filed (deliberately deferred):** the embed still `await`s a Supabase round-trip (no local cache) before first paint ‚Üí genuine first-load instant-ness is **B495**.
- **‚è≥ Live/signed-in check ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V153** (the real embedded app's `nav-request` responder needs its CDN + Supabase, blocked here).

### B493 ‚Äî Connect projects across modules: a schedule linked to a site (also CLOSES B477) `[Cross-module / Schedule ‚Üî Site ‚Üî Review]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "connect the projects within modules, like I have pappadoupolos in schedule and site planner but I don't have a way to connect them‚Ä¶ or create a schedule connected to a site"; branch `claude/cross-module-project-connections-bemcqi`; minted **B493** = highest real B# across both files (B492) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B491 that concurrent `main` PRs took for B491/B492 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/cross-module-project-connections-bemcqi`). lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1629 unit tests green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-cross-module-link.mjs` 4/4.**
- **The gap:** a project = a Site Planner **site group** (`group_id`). Site‚ÜîReview already follow the same project (Review reads the shared `sites` table by `project_id=group_id`), but the **Schedule** is the embedded `/sequence/` iframe with its OWN backend + numeric project ids ‚Äî "Pappadoupolos" there had no link to the site. This ships the link + the project-aware Schedule tab, which also **closes B477** (the project-selection half of the bridge).
- **Owner shape (from Q&A):** linking is **suggest-and-confirm** (offer a same-named match + manual pick, never auto-link); the **payoff is the top-header tabs carrying the project** (no new jump buttons); **all three modules** read as one connected project.
- **Where the link lives (mirror; schedule authoritative):** the pairing is stored on the schedule project (`linkedSiteId`/`linkedSiteName`, free in the already-serialized `hs-v1` blob); a lightweight HINT (`scheduleProjectId`/`scheduleProjectName`, Site Model **schema v8‚Üí9**, additive) is mirrored onto the site so the Site Planner shows "has a schedule" without booting the iframe. The two live in separate backends; the Shell brokers the mirror write.
- **Navigation:** `Scheduler.jsx` now honors the routed `projectId` ‚Äî posts `planar:nav-select-by-site` to activate the linked schedule, and pushes the active schedule's `linkedSiteId` back up via `onProjectChange` so the Site/Review tabs carry the same project. Land on a site with no linked schedule ‚Üí a `LinkSchedulePanel` resolution card: **Create a schedule for this site** / **Link an existing one** (same-named suggestion + manual picker).
- **Files:** `siteModel.js` (v9 fields + migrate), `storage.js` (`setScheduleLink`/`scheduleLinkOf`), `shared/projects/projectModel.js`+`projects.js` (surface the hint + pure `suggestNameMatch`/`normalizeProjectName`), `public/sequence/index.html` (nav-state carries the link; new inbound `nav-select-by-site`/`nav-link`/`nav-create-linked`; outbound `link-changed`; `addProject` accepts name+link), `scheduler/lib/navState.js` (`findBySiteId` + carry link through `sanitizeProjects`), `scheduler/Scheduler.jsx` + new `scheduler/components/LinkSchedulePanel.jsx`, `app/Shell.jsx` (`onScheduleLinkChanged` ‚Üí mirror), `shared/ui/ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx` (üìÖ connectedness chip). +28 unit tests across navState/projects/siteModel.
- **Verified:** the new React-wrapper path end-to-end headless (panel ‚Üí suggested match ‚Üí link mirrors `scheduleProjectId` onto the site store ‚Üí panel closes once linked) via the same-origin bridge contract; the embedded `index.html` edits parse clean (Babel, zero page errors).
- **‚è≥ Live-edge (signed-in, both backends):** the genuine cross-iframe round-trip needs the Schedule's own Supabase (unreachable from the sandbox) ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md **V152**.
### B158 ‚Äî Replace inline ‚úï delete button with right-click context menu on site list rows `[Site Planner / UI]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B158)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1613 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-b158-site-row-context-menu.mjs` 9/9 (real Chromium, logged-out).** Most of the target state had **already shipped under B168** ‚Äî the inline single-click ‚úï on a site row was removed (only the non-destructive ‚äï locate icon remains on hover) and the right-click menu (`statusMenu` in `MapFinder.jsx`, opened from a card row OR a map marker) already carries both **Rename‚Ä¶** (‚Üí inline-edit rename flow) and **Delete project‚Ä¶** (‚Üí the existing confirmation modal), positioned at the cursor and clamped to the viewport, dismissing on click-outside. The one unmet acceptance criterion was **Escape-to-close**: the menu closed on click-outside but ignored Escape.
- **Fix:** added a `useEffect` (keyed on `statusMenu`) in `MapFinder.jsx` that listens for `keydown` and closes the menu on `Escape`, matching the click-outside behavior the B158 acceptance calls for.
- **Verified here (logged-out headless):** `verify-b158-site-row-context-menu.mjs` 9/9 ‚Äî site row renders; ‚äï locate present on hover; no inline ‚úï; right-click opens the menu with Rename + Delete project; **Escape closes it**; click-outside closes it; re-open + Rename opens the inline edit input.
- **Note on the original spec:** B158 proposed a brand-new two-item portal menu, but the existing `statusMenu` (status picker + share + Rename + Delete) is the established one-menu pattern (B7/B168 deliberately consolidated to "one menu, not two"), so the fix completes that menu rather than forking a second one. The ‚äï locate icon behavior is unchanged.

### B492 ‚Äî Bump-outs vs. sidewalks & truck courts: sidewalk should span the full building side; truck court should pull in between bumps + be length-editable `[Site Planner / dock features]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "sidewalks should default to the full side of the building when I add or edit bump outs ‚Ä¶ I can't edit truck courts length along the dock and [it should] get pulled in when I put a bump out"; branch `claude/sidewalks-truck-courts-bump-outs-0u7o6v`; minted **B492** = highest real B# (B491) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional **B488** that concurrent `main` PRs (#377 PDF-viewer / #380 schedule-output / pen-test) took for B488‚ÄìB491 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite 1625 tests (+11) green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW `ui-audit/verify-b492-bump-sidewalk-court.mjs` 11/11 (real Chromium, logged-out).** Three coupled defects in how a building's corner **bump-outs** (dog-ears) interact with its **sidewalks** and its **truck court**, fixed at the shared geometry so the canvas + the panel agree:
- **`[x]` Sidewalk now spans the FULL building side incl. the bump-out.** Was: a bump-out grew the perpendicular sidewalk by a *fixed* 60‚Ä≤ (`adjustSidewalkForBump`), ignoring the bump's real projection and not following a later resize. Now: new pure `sidewalkSpanForBumps(b, swSide, bumps)` (`lib/dogEar.js`, unit-tested) recomputes the strip's run + along-centre from the wall length **plus every bump-out that lengthens that wall**; `sidewalkFullRunPatch`/`relayoutBumpSidewalks` (SitePlanner) re-lay it on bump **add / remove / resize** and when a sidewalk is added to a wall that already has bumps ‚Äî preserving the strip's thickness + outward offset. (Headless: left/right sidewalks 300‚Ä≤‚Üí**420‚Ä≤** with two 60‚Ä≤ bumps; back to 300‚Ä≤ on removal.)
- **`[x]` Truck court PULLS IN to the clear dock face between the bump-outs.** Was: `layoutZone` hard-locked the court to the full wall (`along = b.w/b.h`), so the paving overlapped the bump corners (only the dock *doors* pulled in). Now: pure `usableCourtSpan(full, bumpStart, bumpEnd)` + an optional 5th `opts` arg on `layoutZone` (zone 0 only) pull the court in to `full ‚àí bumpStart ‚àí bumpEnd`, centred on the clear face ‚Äî the **same `bumpAlong` model the door renderer already uses**, so doors + court agree. Trailer/buffer keep the full wall (a 60‚Ä≤ bump doesn't reach them). Wired via `courtBumpOpts` in `relayoutSide` (so add-zone / depth-edit / building-resize all benefit) + an explicit re-lay on bump add/remove/resize. (Headless: court 600‚Ä≤‚Üí**490‚Ä≤** with two 55‚Ä≤ bumps, stays centred.)
- **`[x]` Truck-court length is now USER-EDITABLE along the dock.** New **‚ÄúTruck court length (ft)‚Äù** inline field on the selected court (next to its depth), `setCourtLengthAll`/`courtLengthShown` store `alongLen` per dock side; relayout caps a typed length to the clear face so it never re-overlaps a bump. (Headless: set 300‚Ä≤ ‚Üí court along-span = 300‚Ä≤.)
- **Reuse, no new primitives:** reuses `bumpSidewalkSide`, `dogEarSize`, `SIDE_N`, `rot2`, `swSide`, `findCourtIn`, `relayoutSide`, `outwardUnit`; the old fixed-increment `adjustSidewalkForBump`/`isBumpSidewalk` were deleted (no remaining callers). Logged-out-verifiable (pure planner canvas) ‚Äî no signed-in path owed.

### B489 ‚Äî Schedule module: output (export / dashboard / Gantt render) bugs `[Schedule / output]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "now check output bugs and ship fixes"; the output counterpart to B487's input batch; branch `claude/schedule-input-bugs-jtfplb`; minted **B489** = highest real B# (B488) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1627 tests (+14) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild JSX OK ¬∑ NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-schedule-output-bugs.mjs` 7/7 + input harness `verify-schedule-input-bugs.mjs` 8/8 (no regression).** A fan-out review of every output path the scheduler PRODUCES (web/JSON/PDF export, the on-screen Gantt, the cross-project Dashboard) found defects where the output disagreed with the data or showed a stale brand. Fixed at the output boundary; pure logic extracted + unit-tested.
- **`[x]` Web Snapshot (.html) + JSON export ‚Äî stale brand & unguarded values.** The snapshot hardcoded a **"Hillwood Schedule"** heading, `<title>planar`, and a `hillwood-schedule-export.html` filename (and JSON `hillwood-schedule.json`) regardless of project ‚Äî all retired brands (CLAUDE.md: brand is **Planyr**). Now the title is the real project name (single) or **"Planyr Schedule"** (multiple), the filename is `planyr-schedule-<date>.html` / `planyr-schedule.json`; percent guards `green‚Üí100`/`||0` (was `undefined%`), duration uses `Number(t.duration)||0` (no bare "d"), and the status color is `escapeHtml`'d.
- **`[x]` PDF / Print Exhibit Gantt (`buildGanttSVG`) ‚Äî NaN geometry + wrong shapes.** An **unscheduled** task (blank/invalid dates) produced `NaN` SVG coords (an invisible, broken row) ‚Äî it's now skipped and tagged **"Unscheduled"** like the on-screen Gantt. A **duration-0 parent** drew as a single milestone diamond ‚Äî it now renders as a **summary bracket** (the `isParent` branch is checked before `isMilestone`). Dependency **arrows** skipped plain-number predecessors (seed/imported shape) ‚Äî they now `normPreds` like the table. The exhibit **table %Done** now matches the bar's `green‚Üí100` convention.
- **`[x]` On-screen Gantt ‚Äî duration-0 parent.** Same B2 fix: a duration-0 parent renders as a bracket, not a diamond (`(isMilestone && !isSummary)`).
- **`[x]` Dashboard / reports (`MasterView`) ‚Äî parent status wrong + stale.** Parent rows ran `computeDisplayHealth` on their own stale `health` instead of the **worst-of-children rolled** status ‚Äî so a parent of a red task showed gray, got **dropped from the "at-risk" filter**, and sorted wrong. Now uses a shared `computeRolledHealth` (extracted to module scope; the project grid's `rolledHealthMap` reuses the SAME helper so the two can't drift), attaches `_disp` per row, and the memos recompute on `data.settings`/`NOW` so overdue/due-soon + CF-rule edits stay live.
- **`[x]` `computeDisplayHealth` overdue rule** no longer flags a **100%-complete** task overdue (was only guarded behind the `completeGreen` toggle); **`fmtD`** guards a malformed (non-ISO) date so it never prints `NaN/NaN/yy`.
- **Verified here (logged-out headless):** `verify-schedule-output-bugs.mjs` 7/7 ‚Äî board boots; web snapshot carries the Planyr brand (not Hillwood/planar), the right filename, and no `undefined%`/`NaN`/bare-`d`; the Gantt view and the Dashboard both render rows with no page errors. Plus `computeRolledHealth` units (incl. a random-tree match against the prior inline algorithm) + anti-drift string guards in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. *(Sandbox quirk: the board's React/Babel CDNs are closed to the browser here, so the harness used pre-downloaded copies via `SEQ_VENDOR=<dir>`; in CI/normal envs run it with no env var.)*
- **‚è≥ Two running-app click-throughs ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V151** (the PDF/Print exhibit's "Unscheduled" tag + summary-bracket render; a parent's rolled color/at-risk inclusion in the Dashboard) ‚Äî not cleanly drivable headless (the PDF preview), covered here by unit + anti-drift + the boot/render checks.
- **Reviewer scope notes (NOT bugs):** Gantt bars use calendar-day spans by design (correct); the leaf-only rolled **cost** sum is the documented B212 model (left as-is). The Web Snapshot exporting all projects (vs the PDF's selected subset) is now honest via the multi-project "Planyr Schedule" title.
- **Follow-up (owner, same session) ‚Äî export filenames now match the Site Planner.** New shared `scheduleExportName(projects[, date])` (mirrored to `scheduler-engine.mjs`, 6 unit tests) produces the Site Planner's exact PDF/PNG format (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/printSheet.js` `sheetFileName`): **"YYYY.MM.DD {Project} - Schedule"** (single project ‚Üí its name; multiple ‚Üí Planyr; filesystem-illegal chars stripped via the same rule). Applied to all three exports ‚Äî Web Snapshot `.html`, JSON `.json`, and the PDF/Print Exhibit `<title>` (which the browser's "Save as PDF" uses as the filename). Replaces the prior `planyr-schedule-<iso>.html` / `planyr-schedule.json` / cover-label `<title>`. Headless `verify-schedule-output-bugs.mjs` updated + 7/7.

### B488 ‚Äî Document Review PDF viewer quality: wire pdf.js support assets (fonts/CMaps/ICC/WASM) + supersample the detail layer + deeper zoom & snappier settle `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task ‚Äî quality)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 "improve the PDF viewer quality‚Ä¶ I'm sure it's lacking somehow"; minted **B488** = highest real B# (B487) + 1 ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B486 that a concurrent `main` (#374/#376) took for the team-RLS + schedule-input fixes)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/pdf-viewer-quality-x40e5a`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1599 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW headless `verify-pdfjs-assets.mjs` 9/9 (incl. a fix‚Üîcontrol A/B) + `verify-docreview-viewer.mjs` 13/13 (no regression).**
- **ROOT GAP:** `loadPdf` called `getDocument({ data })` with NONE of pdf.js v6's asset URLs ‚Äî but v6 ships its render-support assets as on-disk folders (`pdfjs-dist/{standard_fonts,cmaps,iccs,wasm}`) that are NOT bundled into the worker. Verified against the installed worker source: without `wasmUrl` BOTH the WASM and the JS-fallback image decoders fail to load (both build their URL from `wasmUrl`), so **JBIG2 (scanned B&W) and JPEG2000/JPX (scanned colour/aerial) images decode to BLANK**; ICC colour is explicitly disabled (`warn("No ICC color space support due to missing wasmUrl")`); Symbol/ZapfDingbats + CID/CJK glyphs drop without `standardFontDataUrl`/`cMapUrl`. Exactly the surfaces a survey/scan review hits.
- **FIX:** (1) new inline vite plugin `pdfjsAssets()` (`vite.config.js`) exposes the four folders at `<base>pdfjs/<folder>/` ‚Äî streamed from node_modules in dev (`configureServer`), emitted verbatim into the build (`generateBundle`, fixed `fileName` ‚Üí no content-hashing) so they stay in lock-step with the installed pdfjs version (no committed, drift-prone blobs). (2) `loadPdf` (`doc-review/lib/pdf.js`) passes `cMapUrl`+`cMapPacked` / `standardFontDataUrl` / `iccUrl` / `wasmUrl` derived from `import.meta.env.BASE_URL` (root-absolute ‚Üí resolves identically on the main thread, inside the worker, and under any deploy subpath). Production-safe: no `/* /index.html` SPA catch-all and the `/assets/*` rule never matches `/pdfjs/*` (B449), and Cloudflare serves real static files before consulting `_redirects`.
- **ALSO (crispness/responsiveness, same module ‚Äî high-value low-risk set from the audit synthesis):** the DETAIL layer now supersamples to a 2√ó density floor even on a 1√ó monitor (`DETAIL_DENSITY_TARGET`/`DETAIL_DENSITY_CAP`, `renderBudget.backingScale`, still budget-clamped) for cleaner anti-aliased CAD linework/text ‚Äî the residual "softer than Bluebeam" gap left after B415 (which only restored *device* density); the settle debounce dropped **140‚Üí90 ms** (sharp detail returns sooner after a gesture); `VIEW_MAX` **6‚Üí10** (read the finest survey bearing/dimension text); the whole-page backdrop floor `BACKDROP_PX_BUDGET` **8e6‚Üí16e6** (the soft floor *during* a gesture on a retina E-size sheet rises ‚âà73‚Üí103 dpi; no-op on a 1√ó monitor; under Safari's single-canvas cap); the detail pan-halo margin **0.25‚Üí0.40** (a decisive flick-pan stays sharp to its leading edge); and the high-dpi density cap **2‚Üí2.5** (a 3√ó/4√ó panel renders a bit denser). +4 budget unit tests (15 total).
- **Verified:** `verify-pdfjs-assets.mjs` 9/9 ‚Äî serves all four asset types (200; wasm as `application/wasm`); the pdf.js **WORKER** fetches `FoxitDingbats.pfb` + `FoxitSymbol.pfb` from `/pdfjs/standard_fonts/` (a CONTROL with the getDocument wiring reverted drops to 6/8: no worker font fetch + logs "Cannot load system font: ZapfDingbats/Symbol"); zero degradation warnings; sheet non-blank; detail backing = **2.00√ó** on a 1√ó display. `verify-docreview-viewer.mjs` 13/13 ‚Äî zoom-anchor/pan/sheet-switch/fit/count/drag/text all unaffected (also repointed its stale "Library" boot to the `#markup` route, B418).
- **Follow-ups filed (deliberately deferred):** remaining render-engine polish ‚Üí **B489**; an optional-content (OCG) layer toggle ‚Üí **B490**.
- **‚è≥ Live-edge:** production serving of `/pdfjs/*` on planyr.io ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md **V149**. Verified this session against the Cloudflare **branch-preview** deploy (same Pages project + build that serves planyr.io): all four `/pdfjs/*` asset types return 200 with correct content-types (`application/wasm`, `application/x-font-type1`, `application/vnd.iccprofile`, `application/octet-stream`) and none falls through to the SPA `index.html`; the post-merge planyr.io curl is the last confirmation.

### B487 ‚Äî Schedule module: input edits silently dropped, mangled, or left stale state `[Schedule / inputs]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ‚Äî "find all the bugs you can as it relates to the inputs for the schedule module and fix them and ship"; branch `claude/schedule-input-bugs-jtfplb`; minted **B487** = highest real B# (B486, the concurrent team-RLS fix) + 1 ‚Äî the B526 hit in BACKLOG-DONE is a CSS hex, not an id)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ¬∑ 1613 tests (+16) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ esbuild JSX transform OK ¬∑ NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-schedule-input-bugs.mjs` 8/8 (real Chromium, logged-out).** A fan-out review of every user-input path in the embedded scheduler (`public/sequence/index.html`) found six places where a reasonable typed value was silently lost, mangled, or left the model inconsistent. All fixed at the input boundary; the engine itself was already hardened (B246‚ÄìB251).
- **`[x]` Predecessor edits validated (self / unknown / cycle).** New pure `validatePredEdit(tasks, id, parsed)` (in index.html + mirrored to `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`, 7 unit tests): drops self-references (existing Fix #16), references to task ids that don't exist in the project (were stored + displayed as a live dep but did nothing ‚Äî `cascadeDates` filtered them), and references that would close a **circular dependency** (A‚ÜíB‚ÜíA produced silently-arbitrary dates) ‚Äî each surfaced with a toast. Wired into `applyUpdate`.
- **`[x]` Grid date input hardened.** A date cell can now be **cleared** (empty ‚Üí `""`, was silently reverted); an **unreadable** date gives a toast ("Couldn't read that date‚Ä¶") instead of silently reverting; and **Finish before Start** is rejected with a toast instead of silently collapsing the task to a 0-day milestone and discarding the typed date.
- **`[x]` Finish on an unscheduled task.** Setting a Finish on a task with no Start used to set `duration = ""` (rendered a bare "d") and the typed finish was then wiped by the cascade (`calcEnd("",‚Ä¶)===""`). It now anchors a 1-day task on that date (start=end, duration=1). Added a defensive duration-cell render guard so a stray empty duration can never show a bare "d".
- **`[x]` Indent / outdent / paste now recompute roll-ups.** `indentTask`, `outdentTask`, `indentTaskById`, `outdentTaskById`, and `pasteTaskAfter` changed `parentId` but never ran `cascadeDates`/`rollupParentDates`, so the old and new parents kept **stale summary start/end/duration** until the next in-subtree date edit. New shared `recomputeAfterStructureChange` (= `rollupParentDates(cascadeDates(...))`, the same recompute `updateTask` runs) is applied in all five handlers before renumber.
- **`[x]` Master ("All tasks") grid duration clamp.** It lacked the project grid's `Math.min(100000, ‚Ä¶)` upper bound, so a fat-fingered duration there could freeze the layout. Clamp added to match.
- **`[x]` Export cover fields escaped.** The PDF/print cover **Date** (`cfg.docDate`) and **Prepared for** (`cfg.preparedFor`) were interpolated raw while their four siblings used `escapeHtml` ‚Äî a typed `<`/`&`/quote corrupted the cover (and was an injection vector into the generated preview). Both now use `escapeHtml`.
- **Verified here (logged-out headless):** `verify-schedule-input-bugs.mjs` 8/8 ‚Äî board boots with all fixes in (proves the whole Babel block still parses), and self-ref / nonexistent-id / circular-dependency / Finish-before-Start / unreadable-date each toast with no uncaught errors. Plus `validatePredEdit` units + `rollupParentDates` move-rollup unit + anti-drift string guards in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. *(Sandbox quirk: the board's React/Babel CDNs are closed to the browser here, so the harness used locally pre-downloaded copies via `SEQ_VENDOR=<dir>`; in CI/normal envs run it with no env var.)*
- **‚è≥ Three running-app click-throughs ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V148** (indent roll-up widening visible in the grid; Finish-on-blank-task showing "1d"; export-cover escaping in the generated PDF) ‚Äî not drivable as cleanly headless, covered here by unit + anti-drift + the boot check.
- **Scope note (NOT a bug):** the on-screen Gantt has **no bar drag/resize** (bars are click-to-select only; the only chart drag is whole-canvas pan, and the Snap/Free toggle drives the grid‚ÜîGantt split divider) ‚Äî so there's no second date-input path that could disagree with the typed/`addBD` path. New/inserted tasks are intentionally born blank (unscheduled until given a date or predecessor). Both confirmed during the review.

### B486 ‚Äî SECURITY: a non-owner team member could RE-HOME a shared project to another team `[Site Planner / Team / RLS]` (bug, security)  *(found 2026-06-27 by an owner-requested Team Workspaces bug audit; minted **B486** = highest real B# (B485) + 1)*
`[x]` **Fix WRITTEN + committed (`db/team_rehome_guard.sql`); ‚è≥ owner must RUN it in Supabase to activate (like every team migration). lint 0 / build green (SQL-only + docs ‚Äî no app code).**
- **The bug:** `db/team_sharing.sql`'s `update own or team ‚Ä¶` policies enforce sharing via WITH CHECK, whose second branch `(team_id is not null and is_team_member(team_id))` only checks the RESULTING team_id. RLS WITH CHECK can't see the OLD row, so it can't distinguish "kept the same team" from "moved to a different team." A NON-OWNER member who belongs to two teams could therefore UPDATE the owner's shared row and change its `team_id` from team X to team Y (a team the member is on) ‚Äî re-homing the owner's project and cutting team X (and the owner, if not on Y) out of access. Contradicts the documented owner-only re-home/unshare intent. (Unshare-to-null was already owner-gated ‚Äî only re-home-to-another-team slipped through.)
- **Why it's not an emergency (but should be run before teams go live):** it requires a member on two teams with the project shared to one ‚Äî and the team feature isn't actively used yet (no invites sent; B406's signed-in round-trip is still owed, V118). So no real team with overlapping members exists today; the exposure is latent.
- **The fix (`db/team_rehome_guard.sql`):** a BEFORE UPDATE trigger `guard_team_rehome()` on `sites`/`doc_reviews`/`file_facts` that raises `42501` if `team_id` changes and `old.user_id <> auth.uid()` ‚Äî i.e. only the row OWNER may change `team_id` (share/unshare/re-home); teammates edit CONTENT freely (team_id unchanged ‚Üí allowed). Idempotent; SECURITY INVOKER (reads OLD/NEW + auth.uid(), no RLS bypass). Audited the rest of the team surface too (teams.js, teams.sql helpers + invite-claim, casUpsert owner-preservation, team_storage.sql) ‚Äî all sound; this was the one real gap.
- **‚è≥ Owner step (handed over + OWNER-TODO):** run `db/team_rehome_guard.sql` in the Supabase SQL editor. Verify (2 accounts) ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V147.

<!-- archived 2026-06-26: B437/B472/B475/B476 were already fixed + merged to main but left stale in BACKLOG.md Open; moved here per the move-to-DONE-on-ship rule. -->

### B437 ‚Äî Callout tool missing from the Document Review rail (matrix‚Üîrail drift) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug/feature)  *(found 2026-06-24 by the B432 loop)*
`[x]` **DONE (wired by a prior session; VERIFIED + archived 2026-06-26).** `callout` is now in `DocReview.jsx`'s `TOOLS` rail with its full `point`-mode placement (first click = leader tip/target, second click = text-box anchor ‚Üí inline editor ‚Üí commit), double-click to edit, and rendering via the shared `MarkupRenderer` (the same callout path the Site Planner uses). The stale Open entry described the gap as still-open; it isn't.
- **Verified 2026-06-26:** matrix‚Üîschema conformance for `callout` passes (`schemaForMarkup({kind:"callout"})` keys === `propsForTool("callout")` ‚Äî both `[align,bold,fill,fillOpacity,fontColor,fontSize,italic,lineHeight,padding,stroke,underline]`), `callout` is in `toolsForWorkspace("doc")`, and headless `ui-audit/verify-b437-callout.mjs` 6/6 (logged-out, fixture PDF): the `tool-callout` rail button is present and ARMS (`aria-pressed=true`), no JS crash. (The two-click draw + inline editor isn't drivable by synthetic CDP events headless ‚Äî same limitation noted for paste in V120 ‚Äî so it's exercised by the auth-gated `e2e/markup-tools.spec.js` Section B, which now arms callout instead of skipping it.)

### B472 ‚Äî Restore the parking-field split as a true EXPLODE into individual rows + aisles `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-1" ‚Äî "with a parking field selected there's no longer a tool to split it into its rows + drive aisles; it worked before"; minted **B472** = highest real B# across both files (B471) + 1)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/restore-parking-split-tool-hgdg7f`).** *NOTE ‚Äî this session was push-to-branch only; no PR/merge to `main` was opened (next session/owner merges). Kept in Open until merged.*
- **GROUNDED THE REPORT FIRST (git log/blame).** The split control was **NOT removed** ‚Äî not in the markup-engine unification (B423‚ÄìB432) the brief flagged, nor anywhere else (zero removing commits). `splitParkingRows` + the pure `splitParkingPieces` (B130) were intact and wired in **two** places (the Parking panel + the right-click "Parking" menu). So the "lost in the consolidation" hypothesis is **refuted**; nothing was reintroduced as a fork.
- **The REAL gap (why the owner saw it as gone/wrong):** the existing tool split a field into double-loaded **MODULE** elements ‚Äî each piece *still* bundled 2 rows + 1 aisle ‚Äî so a 2-module field gave a **2-element** result, and it was hidden below 3 rows. That is exactly the owner's complaint: *"do not collapse the aisle into a row or force a 2-element result."*
- **Fix ‚Äî a true explode into the field's constituent bands.** New pure `explodeParkingBands(h, sd, ai)` in `lib/parking.js` returns the ordered `{kind:"row"|"aisle", depth}` sequence (rows pair into double-loaded modules exactly as the field was built ‚Äî aisles = ‚åàrows/2‚åâ; depths always sum to `h`, so stall count + pavement are preserved). `splitParkingRows` now maps each piece to an **independent, separately selectable element**: a **stall row ‚Üí `type:"parking"`** (one-row depth, renders a single row) and a **drive aisle ‚Üí `type:"paving"`** (a plain lane). Counts scale: single-loaded (1 row+aisle) ‚Üí **2**, double-loaded ‚Üí **3** (row, aisle, row), 8-row field ‚Üí **12** (8 rows + 4 aisles).
- **Behaviour decisions made explicit (per the brief):** *destructive but cleanly UNDOABLE* ‚Äî replaces the parent + `pushHistory()` so Ctrl+Z restores it (never vaporizes geometry); pieces are editable-layer geometry (never touches any imported backdrop); child centres are derived from the parent edges (rotated local offsets, depths sum to `h`) so **no coordinate drift**; curbs auto-suppress at the new seams via the existing `edgeAbutsPaving` (parking/paving are both paved neighbours). The control is now offered whenever the field explodes to **‚â•2 real pieces** (was ‚â•3 rows), and a degenerate/empty field (or a bare already-exploded single row) **surfaces a loud notice instead of a silent no-op** (and the gate hides the button there too). Renders for **already-saved** fields, not just freshly-drawn (reads only persisted `h/w/rot/cx/cy/cfg`).
- **Dedup:** net-new ‚Äî NOT B130/B131 (parking *generation* + boundary clip) and NOT B416 ("Split **a parcel**" = lot boundary). REUSES the B130 `lib/parking.js` math + the `carStalls` band model; the B130 `splitParkingPieces` (module split) + its tests are left intact (no fork).
- **Verified:** lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **1560 tests** (+9 `explodeParkingBands`/no-op) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless **V138** (`ui-audit/verify-b472-explode-parking.mjs`, **9/9**): a double-loaded module ‚Üí 3 elements (2 parking rows + 1 paving aisle, depths sum to 60‚Ä≤); a 6-row field ‚Üí 9 elements; a bare exploded row no longer re-offers the control.

### B475 ‚Äî Markup breadcrumb switcher and the Markup Library read different project sources and can diverge `[Doc Review / Shared header]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-26 as "NEW-1"; minted **B475** = highest real B# across both files (B474) + 1 ‚Äî the B25431/B5263 hits in BACKLOG-DONE.md are CSS hex colours, not IDs)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/markup-switcher-cache-divergence-ceprhv`).** *NOTE ‚Äî push-to-branch only this session; no PR/merge to `main` opened. Kept in Open until merged.*
- **Repro (grounded in code, not guessed):** sign in on a device that hasn't opened the Site Planner this session (cold/empty local cache), go straight to Markup, open the breadcrumb "‚ñæ" project switcher ‚Üí it showed no/stale projects, while the Markup **Library / Files** drawer listed the real cloud projects. Same `sites` table, two read paths: the breadcrumb runs **uncontrolled** in DocReview (no `projects` prop) ‚Üí `shared/projects/projects.js listProjects()` ‚Üí `loadSitesList()` ‚Üí the Site-Planner local cache (`planarfit:sites:cloud:<uid>`, only filled after a Site-Planner cloud pull). The Library uses `reviewStore.listProjects()` ‚Üí a **live Supabase** query. Purely a source/freshness divergence (IDs are consistent ‚Äî `groupId` === `group_id`), contradicting B191's "no parallel project store" intent.
- **Fix (option 2 from the hint ‚Äî warm the cache, don't fork into a controlled list):** new `warmProjectsIfEmpty()` in `projects.js` ‚Äî when signed in **and** the on-device project cache is empty, it runs the SAME `pullCloud(uid)` the Site Planner runs on login (no-ops logged-out or already-warm; never throws), populating the shared cache both paths read. `ProjectBreadcrumb`'s uncontrolled mount effect now kicks it off once and re-`refresh()`es when it lands. New `activeUid()` getter on `storage.js`. Chosen over passing a controlled `projects` prop because that path drops the breadcrumb's rename/delete + cross-tab `storage`-event refresh and mismatches the project shape (no `updatedAt`/`relTime`) ‚Äî warming fixes the actual root cause and benefits every uncontrolled host with no behavior change.
- **Dedup:** net-new; touches the shared breadcrumb + projects helper only (no change to controlled mode / Schedule). Verified: lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **1571 tests** ¬∑ build green (DocReview still its own lazy chunk).
