- **‚è≥ One signed-in spot-check (owner, planyr.io):** on a fresh tab/device, open Markup signed-in without touching the Site Planner first ‚Üí the breadcrumb switcher now lists the same projects as the üóÇ Files drawer. (Sandbox can't reproduce ‚Äî sign-in is blocked there; the cold-cache path is signed-in-only.)

### B476 ‚Äî "At-risk save" messaging promised automatic re-sync on reconnect that doesn't happen `[Shared header]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-26 as "NEW-2"; minted **B476** = B475 + 1)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed the SAME session (same branch as B475).** *Push-to-branch only; kept in Open until merged.*
- **Repro:** with a failed cloud write (save badge "unsaved" ‚Üí breadcrumb saveState "error"/"offline"), the breadcrumb toast + dropdown banner + the `CloudSyncBadge` tooltip all said the changes "will sync **automatically when you reconnect**." But there is **no** `online`/reconnect listener and no background retry anywhere in `src/` ‚Äî a failed write is only re-attempted on the next edit (debounced `writeNow`) or on tab close (the dirty-gated flush). Reconnecting and doing nothing else never triggered a sync ‚Üí false reassurance about a data-safety state.
- **Fix (option B from the hint ‚Äî soften the wording to match reality; the honest, complete, low-risk fix for a LOW-severity messaging bug, chosen over wiring a new `online` retry into the just-stabilized B473 save paths):** all three strings now read "‚Ä¶they'll sync **the next time you make a change or close this tab**." Files: `ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx` (the `flagIfAtRisk` toast + the dropdown at-risk banner) and `CloudSyncBadge.jsx` (the offline tooltip ‚Äî same false promise, fixed for honesty even though the report only named the breadcrumb).
- **Dedup:** net-new; messaging-only, no behavior change. Verified: lint 0 ¬∑ 1571 tests ¬∑ build green. (If a true reconnect-retry is wanted later, that's a separate enhancement ‚Äî a `window` "online" handler re-triggering the pending `writeNow` in each workspace's persistence.)

### B481 ‚Äî Large site-plan overlay didn't re-render after reload (stranded on the "Re-add" placeholder despite the raster being in IndexedDB) `[Site Planner / overlay]` (bug)  *(Cowork signed-in verification 2026-06-26, "NEW-1" ‚Äî a 7.5 MB overlay showed "Re-add ‚Ä¶ not synced to this device" after reload though its ~10 MB raster sat in IndexedDB; minted **B481** = highest real B# (B480) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1597 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ NEW headless `verify-b480-overlay-large.mjs` 8/8 + `verify-b474-overlay-idb.mjs` 7/7 (no regression).**
- **Repro (Cowork, signed-in):** load a >~5 MB image as a sheet-overlay ‚Üí it stores in IndexedDB (`raster:<proj>:overlay:<id>`, ~10 MB) with localStorage small ‚Üí reload ‚Üí overlay shows the "Re-add ‚Ä¶ not on this device" placeholder, even though the bytes are in IndexedDB *on this device* and the record carries an `idbKey`. The same large image as the **aerial underlay re-rendered fine** (separate, simpler effect).
- **ROOT CAUSE (not Cowork's guess of "gates on storageKey" ‚Äî the effect DOES try `idbKey` first, SitePlanner.jsx:3551).** The overlay rehydrate effect had a `cancelled`-flag stranding race: a SLOW `idbGet` of a multi-MB raster was discarded whenever any other setState re-ran the `[sheetOverlays]` effect before the read resolved ‚Äî the result was dropped AND never re-fetched, so the overlay sat src-less forever. A small raster resolves before any re-render (why the 7/7 small-overlay test always passed); the underlay uses a separate effect (why it worked).
- **FIX:** apply each rehydrate result via a functional patch-by-id guarded on `!x.src` (safe across re-runs ‚Äî fills only an overlay still present + still src-less), and DROP the `cancelled` guard so a slow `idbGet` is never stranded. Plus the placeholder now reads "Loading drawing‚Ä¶" when an `idbKey` OR `storageKey` exists (recoverable) and only "Re-add ‚Ä¶ image not on this device" when neither does.
- **Verified:** `verify-b480-overlay-large.mjs` 8/8 ‚Äî a 2.1 MB overlay (idbKey only, no storageKey, heavy src off the cap) re-renders after reload + view churn, no placeholder. Original 7/7 overlay test unchanged.
- **‚è≥ Signed-in re-confirm of Cowork's exact 7.5 MB case ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V146.**

### B482 ‚Äî Cold Review tab: project switcher shows "No projects yet" though the projects exist `[Doc Review / shared header]` (bug)  *(Cowork signed-in verification 2026-06-26, "NEW-2"; the B475/V140 cold-cache fix resurfacing on the direct-to-Review path; minted **B482**)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1597 tests ¬∑ build green; signed-in cold-tab re-confirm ‚Üí V146 (the warm only fires signed in).**
- **Repro (Cowork, signed-in):** open a fresh tab straight into Review (`/project/<id>/markup`) without opening the Site Planner first ‚Üí the project "‚ñæ" switcher shows "No projects yet ‚Äî start one below" though localStorage holds all projects (26 known). A *warm* load (Site opened first) lists them ‚Äî so it's cold-vs-warm, not the lock.
- **ROOT CAUSE:** `ProjectBreadcrumb` (uncontrolled) called B475's `warmProjectsIfEmpty()` **only once on mount** ‚Äî but on a cold tab `isCloudActive()` is usually still false at mount (auth hasn't resolved), so the warm no-opped, and it **never retried**. Opening the switcher only re-read the still-empty cache.
- **FIX:** warm the cache when the switcher **opens** too (auth is settled by then), not just on mount, via a shared `warmThenRefresh()`; a `warming` flag shows "Loading projects‚Ä¶" instead of the misleading "No projects yet" while the one-time cloud pull is in flight. No-ops fast when logged out or already warm; the logged-out path is byte-identical (warm returns false ‚Üí "No projects yet" as before).
- **‚è≥ Signed-in cold-tab re-confirm ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V146.**

### B480 ‚Äî "Take over editing here" was a pointless loop (conflict ‚Üí take over ‚Üí bounce to map ‚Üí same conflict); lock/conflict scoping confirmed PER-PLAN `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, HIGH PRIORITY)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-26 live on planyr.io ‚Äî "it's a fucking pointless loop ‚Ä¶ it needs to be site specific"; minted **B480** = highest B# (B479) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1597 tests (+3) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless `verify-readonly-takeover.mjs` 7/7 (no regression).**
- **Repro (owner, signed-in):** editing a plan that was ALSO open in another tab on the same computer ‚Üí the cloud version-CAS (B314) correctly flagged "changed in another session" + offered "Take over editing here." Clicking it ran `window.location.reload()`, which (a) bounced to the MAP (out of the plan) and (b) re-entered the version race with the still-open other tab, so reopening the plan re-flagged the SAME conflict ‚Üí an endless take-over ‚Üí map ‚Üí conflict loop that never resolved.
- **ROOT CAUSE:** `takeOverEditing` reconciled a conflict by reloading ("boot union-merge"). The reload left the planner AND, because the other tab still held the lock / kept the cloud version ahead, the reloaded tab's stale optimistic-version token (`siteVersions[id]`) still mismatched the cloud ‚Üí conflict again.
- **FIX ‚Äî reconcile IN PLACE, never reload:** Take over now (1) steals the per-plan Web Lock + broadcasts a yield so the other same-browser tab steps down to read-only and STOPS pushing; (2) calls the new focused `reconcileSiteFromCloud(id)` (`cloudSync.fetchSiteForReconcile` ‚Äî a single-row fetch that refreshes `siteVersions[id]` to the cloud's CURRENT version + returns the cloud copy; deliberately NOT `pullCloud`, whose fire-and-forget re-push could race our own push and re-trigger the conflict); (3) unions the other session's content into the canvas (nothing lost from either side); (4) clears the conflict ‚Äî the state change drives a single autosave push at the fresh version ‚Üí it lands, this tab becomes the active editor, and you never leave the planner.
- **Scoping CONFIRMED already correct (owner's 2nd ask) ‚Äî PER-PLAN, not module-wide.** The editor lock name is `planyr-editor:<siteId>` (`setProject(siteId)`, SitePlanner.jsx ~1629) and the cloud version-CAS is keyed `siteVersions[<siteId>]`. Two people on DIFFERENT projects/plans share NEITHER ‚Üí zero contention; only two sessions on the SAME plan contend (correct ‚Äî that's exactly the case being arbitrated). No code change needed; verified in code.
- **Tests:** `test/reconcileSite.test.js` (3) ‚Äî the version-token refresh that breaks the loop. Existing read-only take-over headless 7/7 (no regression ‚Äî the logged-out lock hand-off path is unchanged).
- **‚è≥ Signed-in confirm ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V145** (the cloud-conflict reconcile only fires signed in; the sandbox is logged-out).

### B485 ‚Äî B473/B474 adversarial review hardening: close the IndexedDB-offload data-loss gaps the audit found `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner asked 2026-06-26 to "double check the persistence implementation ‚Ä¶ world-class"; an 8-dimension adversarial review raised 35 findings, all 35 confirmed by independent verifiers, 0 refuted; this ships the safe/high-value data-loss + honesty fixes, defers the perf refactors to B479. **Renumbered B477‚ÜíB485** to clear a collision: a concurrent session had already taken B477 for the Schedule cross-module-nav bug (still Open). Shipped via PR #370, whose title still says "B477" ‚Äî the backlog number is the canonical one.)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1583 tests (+9) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless: existing B474 suite 7/7√ó3 + `verify-new-site-save` 10/10 (no regression) + NEW `verify-b474r-underlay-confirm.mjs` 7/7 (proves the headline fix).** The review found that B473/B474, while curing the original loss, opened narrower IndexedDB-specific data-loss paths. Root cause of most: **a raster's inline `src` was dropped from the saved record on the mere PRESENCE of an `idbKey`, never confirming the fire-and-forget `idbPut` actually committed** ‚Äî and the aerial underlay had no second home (cloud Storage) to mask a failed/slow/evicted stash.
- **`[x]` Confirm-before-strip (the headline, #2/#4/#6/#7/#10).** All three stash sites (`onUnderlayFile`, `addOverlayFile`, `addDrawingFromRaster`) now attach `idbKey` to the record **only after `idbPut` resolves true**; until then `src` stays inline (the safe localStorage copy `dropIdbBackedSrc` won't strip). A failed/slow/in-flight stash can no longer delete the only on-device copy. Proven headless with IndexedDB writes forced to throw: the record keeps `src`, no `idbKey`, the aerial survives reload (`verify-b474r-underlay-confirm.mjs`).
- **`[x]` IndexedDB connection self-heal (#1/#3).** `localDb.js openDb()` nulls the cached `dbPromise` on every non-success path (a transient `onblocked`/`onerror`/throw no longer poisons IndexedDB for the whole session), and registers `db.onversionchange` (close + drop so another tab's future schema bump / `deleteDatabase` isn't blocked) and `db.onclose` (reopen on the next op after a browser force-close/eviction).
- **`[x]` Underlay cloud-Storage backup (#5/#16, the pre-existing cross-device gap).** New `uploadUnderlayDataUrl` in `overlayStorage.js` (uid-first key `‚Ä¶/site-underlay/‚Ä¶`, reuses the private bucket + RLS); `onUnderlayFile` uploads the downscaled data-URL when signed in (`storageKey`), and the rehydrate effect gains a cloud fallback after the idb miss. The aerial ‚Äî previously the ONE raster with no cross-device / post-eviction recovery ‚Äî now restores on a second device. (Cross-device underlay loss was pre-existing, not a B474 regression ‚Äî `slimForCloud` always stripped it; now there's a real backup.)
- **`[x]` `persist()` request (#9).** `idbPersist()` (`localDb.js`) asks the browser to keep our IndexedDB durable (not best-effort/evictable); called once at boot in `SitePlannerApp`. IndexedDB is now the version-ring's home + the underlay's local cache, so eviction protection matters.
- **`[x]` Leak cleanup ‚Äî `idbDelete` was dead code (#13/#15/#22/#23/#24).** New `idbDeleteByPrefix` (cursor); `deleteSite` evicts `raster:<id>:*`; `deleteDrawing` / `removeOverlay` (ref-counted like `storageKey`) / the underlay Remove button evict their entries. No more orphaned raster bytes accumulating forever.
- **`[x]` `backupNow` honesty (#14).** `writeHistoryAll` now returns ONLY the synchronously-verified localStorage result ‚Äî it no longer counts the fire-and-forget idb write it can't confirm, so the Restore safety gate can't claim a backup that may not exist (and wipe the canvas). Deep history still lands in IndexedDB; this governs only what we CLAIM.
- **`[x]` Save-banner honesty (#8/#17/#18).** The conflict / read-only / cloud-failed banners ("safe on this device") are gated on `!localSaveFailed` so a failed device write never shows a reassuring message beside the truthful red one; the device-full cloud fallback clears any stale amber and routes a cloud CONFLICT to the conflict UI instead of a never-clearing "will retry" banner.
- **`[x]` Honest underlay re-drop prompt (#16).** A missing aerial that can't be recovered from idb OR cloud now shows a "couldn't recover ‚Äî re-drop the screenshot" prompt instead of an invisible `<image>`.
- **Tests (+9):** `test/idbRobustness.test.js` (prefix-delete, deleteSite eviction, backupNow honesty), `saveFallbackCloud.test.js` (mixed idb-backed + inline over-quota strip), `storage.test.js` (explicit IndexedDB-ABSENT faithfulness assertion), `overlayStorage.test.js` (underlay key + safe null paths).
- **‚è≥ Signed-in / second-device checks ‚Üí VERIFICATION.md V144** (cross-device underlay restore, `persist()` grant, the device-full + conflict edge) ‚Äî the sandbox is logged-out so these can't be self-driven here.

### B478 ‚Äî Resume-into-planner: a signed-in deep link / refresh bounced to the finder (route stripped to `#/`) `[Site Planner / Persistence / Routing]` (bug, HIGH PRIORITY)  *(Cowork re-confirmed ‚ùå 2026-06-25 on planyr.io ‚Äî see VERIFICATION.md V13/V28; minted **B478** = highest real B# across both files (B477) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + LIVE ‚Äî filed, fixed, logged-out-verified, AND merged to `main` the SAME session (PR #368, branch `claude/v13-resume-into-planner`).** The signed-in end-to-end confirm is owed as `VERIFICATION.md` V143 (the async-gap repro needs Supabase, which the sandbox lacks).
- **Repro (Cowork, signed-in on planyr.io):** open 8 South (URL `#/project/smqiljx5fngg/site`, planner rendered) ‚Üí `location.reload()` ‚Üí at both ~2 s and ~7 s the app **bounced to the finder**, breadcrumb "Select a project", URL **stripped to `#/`**; a cold navigate straight to a project URL likewise bounced. Data durability is fine (the site stays intact + reopenable) ‚Äî purely the resume UX. Re-confirms the 2026-06-20 finding; HIGH-PRIORITY and long-standing.
- **ROOT CAUSE (pinned in `SitePlannerApp.jsx`):** on a **signed-in** deep link/refresh the user's cloud sites aren't in the local store at the first synchronous render (auth + `pullCloud` are async), so `activeSiteId` is momentarily null even though the route names a project. Two boot reactions then destroyed the resume **before** the pull finished: (1) the active-project‚ÜíURL sync wrote that transient `null` over the route, **stripping `#/project/<id>/site` ‚Üí `#/`** (‚Üí finder); (2) the "tidy a dangling `currentSite`" cleanup **nulled the pointer** because the cloud site only *looked* absent. (Logged-out / no-Supabase never reproduced it ‚Äî the local store is synchronous, so there's no gap; which is exactly why the sandbox + every logged-out self-test missed it.)
- **FIX:** a `bootResolved` gate (new pure `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/bootResume.js`) holds BOTH the URL sync and the dangling-pointer cleanup until the first auth + pull settles (true from the start when there's no Supabase to wait on, so logged-out is byte-identical). The resume target is now one shared, unit-tested `pickResumeTarget` (used by the first-render boot AND the post-pull resume, so they can't drift). The `bootResolved=true` flip is batched with the resume's `setActiveSiteId`, so the URL-sync effect only ever sees the resolved view.
- **Verified:** lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **1585 tests (+11 `test/bootResume.test.js`)** ¬∑ build green ¬∑ logged-out headless **8/8** (`ui-audit/verify-resume-into-planner.mjs` ‚Äî deep-link + reload stays in the planner, route intact, `currentSite` preserved, 0 page errors) + `verify-new-site-save.mjs` **10/10** (no regression). **The signed-in async-gap repro can't run in the sandbox (no Supabase ‚Üí no gap), so the signed-in confirm is owed ‚Äî `VERIFICATION.md` V143.**
- **Dedup:** net-new; the resume regression sat under V13/V28 (which referenced B124/B125/B134 ‚Äî those were the *data-durability* halves, already ‚úÖ). This is the *resume-into-planner* half. Distinct from B477 (Schedule project carry).

### B471 ‚Äî Saving stalled with no banner / couldn't take over; cross-session+device conflict now has a real "Take over" + auth-transition tracing `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug/robustness)  *(owner reported a recurrence 2026-06-25 right after B466 shipped ‚Äî "it's literally not saving‚Ä¶ I should be able to take over‚Ä¶ maybe open on another PC"; minted **B471** = highest B# (B470) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1528 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless V (`ui-audit/verify-readonly-takeover.mjs`, 7/7 ‚Äî the read-only take-over path is intact after the handler reorder).** Owner correction taken on board: it was **NOT** a second tab on his PC. The exact incident cause is **un-pinned and I won't pretend otherwise** ‚Äî "no banner + a fresh tab fixed it" most fits a STALE loaded build (an old tab from before B455/B458/B459/B460 + this batch deployed), whose recurrence is self-resolved by the now-deployed fixes on reload; a silent token lapse is ruled out (a real SIGNED_OUT bounces to the map, a 401 shows the red banner ‚Äî neither matches "still in the plan, nothing obvious"). Rather than build a speculative banner for a cause that doesn't match the symptoms, this closes the two real gaps + makes the next occurrence precisely diagnosable:
- **B466's take-over only reached SAME-browser tabs (Web Locks are per-browser).** A plan open in another SESSION/DEVICE blocks saving via the version-conflict path (B314 CAS), which only had a passive "Reload" button. `takeOverEditing` now handles BOTH: same-browser ‚Üí steal the Web Lock + push in place; cross-session/device conflict ‚Üí flush to the device mirror then reload, whose boot UNION-merges (this device ‚à™ the other session ‚Äî nothing lost, B453) and resumes saving at the fresh version. The conflict banner now reads "your changes **aren't reaching the cloud** ‚Ä¶ **Take over editing here**" (was "Reload"), so there is ONE consistent take-over for every blocked-save reason.
- **Auth transitions are now traced** ‚Äî `reportClientEvent("auth-signed-in"/"auth-signed-out")` in `SitePlannerApp.applyUser` (only on a real change; the same-user re-emit returns early). The cloud-save path is gated on being signed in, so a silent session change was previously invisible to telemetry; now a "saving stopped after my session changed" report is diagnosable after the fact via `window.pfTelemetry.recent()` / `public.client_errors`.
- **Honest limit (deferred, not built):** true simultaneous multi-device single-editor locking (forcing a remote PC read-only) needs a cloud editing-lease (heartbeat + realtime) ‚Äî the deferred per-site-sharing / multi-device territory. An **inaccessible** remote tab is idle and, post-B460, doesn't advance the cloud version, so it can't block this device anyway; the conflict take-over covers the actively-editing-elsewhere case. Work is never lost (device mirror B458 + boot union-merge B453).

### B464 ‚Äî Read-only/locked tab silently accepted edits that never synced; the read-only signal was too easy to miss `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-1"; minted **B464**; EXTENDS B455 + B458)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1528 tests (+11) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ V (`ui-audit/verify-readonly-takeover.mjs`, 7/7, logged-out).** **Audited first, FLAGGED a contradiction:** on current `main` (post-B458/B455) a read-only tab's IMMEDIATE local-mirror write + version snapshot run UNCONDITIONALLY (`writeMirror`, SitePlanner.jsx ~L1461) ‚Äî only the CLOUD push is gated (~L1477). So the report's "snapshots froze / never persisted anywhere" is NOT reproducible on current main; it predates B458 (shipped earlier 2026-06-25), which made the device mirror immediate. The REAL gap: those read-only edits never reach the CLOUD, and the B455 read-only banner was a passive `role="status"` strip easy to work past for an hour.
- **Fix:** the read-only banner is now LOUD + actionable (`role="alert"`, `data-testid="readonly-banner"`): it states the changes are saved on this device but **aren't syncing to the cloud**, that **reloading won't help** while the other tab is open, and offers **"Take over editing here"** (B466). The immediate local mirror (B458) is kept as the recovery net ‚Äî read-only edits are never lost, just not synced until you take over or close the other tab.
- **Telemetry (B468):** an edit whose cloud push is suppressed now emits a `save-suppressed` event, so a silent non-syncing stretch is diagnosable after the fact without DevTools.
- **Deduped:** EXTENDS B455 (the Web Locks lockout) + B458 (immediate mirror); not a re-file.

### B465 ‚Äî Cloud indicator showed green "synced" while the tab was read-only and NOT saving `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-2"; minted **B465**)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1528 tests ¬∑ build green; signed-in indicator glance ‚Üí Cowork (logged-out the badge correctly reads "local").** **Reproduced in code:** `headerSaveState` (SitePlanner.jsx ~L6040) had NO read-only branch ‚Äî a read-only tab fell through to `return cloudActive ? "synced" : "local"` ‚Üí the green cloud-check badge, even though read queries (the connection) succeed but writes don't. Exactly the owner's "the cloud indicator stayed green the whole time."
- **Fix:** `headerSaveState` returns a new `"readonly"` state when `cloudActive && readOnly && !cloudConflict`. `cloudBadgeView("readonly")` (shared `CloudSyncBadge`) = amber + a distinct **lock** glyph + an actionable popover titled "Read-only ‚Äî not saving" that explains why and what to do ‚Äî never the green save-badge. Unit-locked in `cloudSyncBadge.test.js` (readonly differs from synced on BOTH glyph and color; title says "read-only"). Connection ‚âÝ write-capability is now the contract; the salient state ("your edits aren't saving") is loud, not green.

### B466 ‚Äî "Opened in another tab" recovery was broken: reload didn't take the lock; no way to take over or force-save `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug/feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-3"; minted **B466**; depends on B464)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). V (`ui-audit/verify-readonly-takeover.mjs`, 7/7): two tabs of one browser context share Web Locks ‚Üí tab B goes read-only, "Take over editing here" makes B active AND hands tab A down to read-only via the cross-tab bus.**
- **`editorLock.takeOver()`** (`src/shared/presence/editorLock.js`): STEALS the Web Lock (`{steal:true}`) so this tab is unambiguously the editor even if the other tab is gone or on an older build, THEN broadcasts a `yield` on a BroadcastChannel so the prior holder steps down to read-only cooperatively. Steal-first-then-broadcast so the prior holder's re-request queues behind the steal. Degrades open where Web Locks is unavailable. (+3 unit tests: steal-makes-active, bus hand-off, degrade-open no-op.)
- **`takeOverEditing()` (SitePlanner):** the banner button calls `takeOver`, then flushes the live canvas to the device mirror AND force-pushes to the cloud (`cloudPushWithWatchdog`) ‚Äî so the pent-up un-synced work reaches the cloud immediately. The B459 thin-clobber + B314 CAS guards still protect that push (a genuine conflict surfaces the loud BLOCKING banner + reload-merge; never a silent overwrite), so takeover-and-push is safe.
- **Reload-recovery premise CORRECTED (flagged):** "work done before reload is lost on reload" is mitigated by B458 ‚Äî a read-only tab's edits ARE written to the device mirror immediately, so boot's union-merge restores them; the remaining gap (cloud sync) is what takeover closes. The brief's "manual Save now / force flush" is subsumed by "Take over editing here" (the only SAFE way to push from a read-only tab is to first own the lock; the device mirror already keeps the work, so a user is never stranded). The B313 presence banner copy was updated to the single-active-editor reality.

### B467 ‚Äî Restore's "can be undone" guarantee was unverified; Restore wasn't lock-gated `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-4"; minted **B467**)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1528 tests (storage.test.js +3) ¬∑ build green.** The Version History dialog promises "your current version is backed up too, so a restore can be undone," but that relied on the autosave's shape-DEDUPED snapshot firing after the restore ‚Äî which can no-op (same collection shape, different content) ‚Üí restore overwrites current work with no real undo.
- **Fix (`storage.js` + `restoreVersion`):** `snapshotVersion(model, { force })` now **returns whether it persisted** and `force` bypasses the shape-dedup; new `backupNow(id)` force-snapshots the current stored state and returns true only when a backup actually persisted (or there's genuinely nothing at risk ‚Äî empty/missing record). `restoreVersion` (a) is **lock-gated** ‚Äî a read-only tab can't Restore (loud message + the takeover way out); (b) flushes the live canvas, then **verifies `backupNow` before replacing** ‚Äî if the backup can't be written (device storage full) it CANCELS the restore loudly + emits a `restore-backup-failed` telemetry event, rather than destroying current work on a false promise. Fits the verify-it-persisted / no-silent-success discipline.

### B468 ‚Äî Lockout / suppressed-save / write-failure / zero-row-delete events now leave a telemetry trace `[Site Planner / Observability]` (task) ‚Äî EXTENDS B279  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-5"; minted **B468**)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1528 tests (clientErrors.test.js +2) ¬∑ build green; signed-in row-write ‚Üí Cowork.** The whole 8 South lockout diagnosis required live DevTools spelunking because nothing about it was traceable. Fixed: `reportClientEvent(kind, message, extra)` in `clientErrors.js` records structured NON-error events to the existing `public.client_errors` table ‚Äî **no schema change / no owner SQL step** (stamped with a per-page-load `TAB_ID` embedded in the message so multi-tab contention is reconstructable) ‚Äî surfaced via `window.pfTelemetry.recent()` + `pfTelemetry.tab`. Wired at the exact gates B464 touches: `readonly-enter`/`readonly-leave` + `readonly-takeover` (SitePlanner lock onChange + takeover), `save-suppressed` (the autosave cloud-push gate), `cloud-conflict` (CAS 409 + thin-clobber) / `cloud-write-failed` / `delete-zero-rows` (cloudSync). Fail-safe is the whole point ‚Äî every path swallows its own errors (unit-tested: never throws on a circular / null / empty payload), reuses B279's dedup + per-session cap.

### B469 ‚Äî Fort Bend FLOODZONE layer blocked by CORS; route the health probe through the existing GIS proxy `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-6"; minted **B469**)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1528 tests (gisProxyCore.test.js +2) ¬∑ build green; live "load a Fort Bend parcel ‚Üí no CORS errors" ‚Üí Cowork/prod.** **Audited first: the server-side GIS proxy already EXISTS (B445) and `fortbendcountytx.gov` is already in its allow-list; the export-image raster already proxies + renders via a CORS-exempt `<img>`.** The 6√ó CORS console errors + `net::ERR_FAILED` were `probeService` (layers.js) fetching `?f=json` **directly** (bypassing the proxy) ‚Äî `arcgisweb.fortbendcountytx.gov` sends no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, so the browser killed the probe.
- **Fix (`layers.js`):** `probeService` is now **direct-first, proxy-on-CORS-failure** (refactored into `probeOnce`): the many CORS-clean agency hosts take NO extra hop and never depend on the proxy being deployed, while a host that throws (CORS/network) retries through the same-origin B445 cache proxy (`/api/gis-cache/svc/<b64>?f=json`), which fetches it server-side and returns the JSON same-origin ‚Äî so the service can still be health-checked AND its `fullExtent` read for the coverage engine. `fb_contours` marked `kind: "dynamic"` for clarity. Round-trip unit-locked (the proxied Fort Bend probe decodes back to the real request for an allowed host). The "one proxy adapter" the brief asked for = the existing single B445 module (paired with its SWR Drive cache); no new server, no regression for CORS-clean hosts.

### B470 ‚Äî pdf.worker "TT: undefined function" Trusted Types warning ‚Äî triaged benign `[Document Review / PDF]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-7", low priority; minted **B470**; CLOSED benign)*
`[x]` **CLOSED ‚Äî triaged benign, no code change (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`).** Confirmed the repo enforces **no** Content-Security-Policy and **no** `require-trusted-types-for` directive anywhere (checked `public/_headers`, `index.html` meta, all `functions/`, `vite.config.js`). So the `TT:` log from `pdf.worker.min.mjs` is pdf.js's own internal Trusted Types feature-PROBE during worker bootstrap (it looks for a TT policy to harden itself; with no enforcing policy it falls back to the normal path) ‚Äî purely informational, no render impact, matching the owner's "PDF rendering appears to work." Adding a TT policy solely to silence a harmless log would add complexity and risk breaking the worker, so no change is warranted; re-open only if a strict CSP is ever introduced (then pdf.js needs its `pdfjs` policy allow-listed).

### B461 ‚Äî Right-click context menu for site-plan overlays (Copy / Duplicate / Paste / z-order / Lock / Align to base / Delete) `[Site Planner / overlay]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-1"; minted **B461**; surface confirmed by the owner ‚Äî see below)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/wizardly-mccarthy-oi8xl7`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1517 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ V137 (`ui-audit/verify-b461-b462-overlay-menu.mjs`, 13/13, logged-out).** **Flagged + corrected the premise:** the brief tagged this `[Document Review]` and assumed an "overlay & version-compare surface" there ‚Äî that surface does **not exist** in Doc Review yet (unbuilt Track-2 item). Asked the owner which "overlay" he meant; he **chose the Site Planner's site-plan overlays** (`sheetOverlays`), the one real object matching the brief (a transform layer + a source-file reference + lock + align). Built there.
- **Menu (`ovMenu`):** a portalled, viewport-clamped floating menu at the cursor (mirrors the element `typeMenu` / B127 portal pattern), opened by `onOverlayContext` from the **canvas overlay `<g>`** (unlocked) AND its **Overlay-panel row** (so a locked overlay ‚Äî pointer-inert on the map ‚Äî is still reachable to unlock). Entries: **Copy, Duplicate, Paste** (Paste disabled on an empty clipboard), **Bring to front / Send to back** (disabled at the ends; draw order = array order), **Lock / Unlock**, **Align to base edge‚Ä¶** (disabled when locked or no parcel), **Delete**. Right-click `preventDefault`+`stopPropagation` so the canvas pan/marquee never eats it.
- **Copy/Duplicate/Paste touch only the overlay's transform layer + its source *reference*, never the source bytes:** a copy/dup/paste shares the same `src` + `storageKey` (no re-import). `removeOverlay` is now **ref-counted** ‚Äî it only deletes the cloud source object when no OTHER overlay still points at that `storageKey`, so deleting one of a duplicated pair can't orphan the sibling's image. Copy ‚Üí `overlayClip` ref; Paste lands at the cursor (B417 pattern) or offset; `Ctrl/‚åò C/V/D` wired for a selected overlay (separate from the element clipboard; selecting an overlay sets `sel=null`, so no collision).
- **Delete is count-verified (silent-failure rule):** `removeOverlay` refuses if the id isn't present, and a `setTimeout` re-reads `stateRef` to confirm the row actually went ‚Äî a no-op delete flashes a loud "‚öÝ Delete didn't take" warning instead of a phantom success.
- **Single-overlay note:** the Site Planner selects one overlay at a time (`selOverlay`), so the menu is single-target (the brief's multi-select-for-Delete/z-order is moot until overlay multi-select exists). Every op is undoable (`pushHistory`). Esc clears the menu + any armed align.

### B462 ‚Äî "Align to base edge" for overlays, reusing the Site Planner building‚Üíparcel align `[Site Planner / overlay]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-2"; minted **B462**; depends on B461)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified with B461 (V137 above; rot 0 ‚Üí 30¬∞ on a 30¬∞-rotated parcel). lint 0 ¬∑ 1517 tests ¬∑ build green.** The "Align to base edge‚Ä¶" menu entry **reuses the existing building‚Üíparcel primitive ‚Äî no second align written:** it arms `ovAlignBase`, and the next click on a parcel boundary (caught in `startMoveSheetOverlay` / `startMoveParcel` / the background handler, the same three consumption points as the element `alignFor`) runs `alignOverlayToParcelEdge`, which finds the nearest parcel edge via `segDist` and snaps the overlay's rotation parallel to it via `snapParallel` (the very functions the building‚Üíparcel "Align rotation‚Ä¶" uses).
- **Edge-snap = rotation only** (translation + rotation, scale unchanged); the **2-point translate+rotate+scale** path is the overlay's existing "Align to map" (`solveSimilarityLSQ`), left in place. Rotation is a persisted real-world angle (degrees on the model) so the alignment survives zoom/pan/reload; the base parcel is never mutated. **Guards:** refuses a **locked** overlay with a visible reason; rejects a **degenerate** (too-short) edge rather than applying a NaN rotation; "no parcel to align to" flashes a clear message; one undo step; re-aligning replaces the prior rotation.

### B463 ‚Äî Replace the rotation slider with a numeric stepper (hundredth-degree, shared widget) `[Doc Review + shared markup engine]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-3"; minted **B463** = highest real B# (B460) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/wizardly-mccarthy-oi8xl7`). lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1517 tests (+11 unit) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ lazy chunks intact ¬∑ headless V (`ui-audit/verify-b463-rotation-stepper.mjs`, 12/12, logged-out).** The owner's "verify first" was right: the ONLY actual rotation **slider** in the app was the Site Planner overlay's "Rotate" (`SitePlanner.jsx` ~L7416); the markup + element panels already used a NumInput (element even had ¬±1¬∞ spinners, B431). Document Review has no rotation control today. So this was a real swap on the overlay + a unify on the other two, NOT a one-for-one replacement everywhere.
- **New shared widget `src/shared/ui/RotationStepper.jsx`** ‚Äî THE one rotation control the whole app imports (overlay panel, markup panel, element panel today; any future Doc-Review rotatable object next). Behavior to the owner's spec: **type-to-set** up to two decimals (hundredth-degree); commit on **Enter / blur / spinner** only (never live per keystroke); **‚ñ≤‚ñº spinners nudge ¬±1¬∞**, in-field ArrowUp/Down = ¬±1¬∞, Shift+Arrow = ¬±0.1¬∞; **normalize/wrap into [0,360)** on commit (370 ‚Üí 10, ‚àí5 ‚Üí 355); **display ‚â§2 decimals but store full precision and step the stored value** so repeated nudges never drift; **empty on blur reverts** to the last committed value (never zeroes); **garbage flashes the field red + reverts** (never silently clamps to 0); a **locked** target disables the input AND the spinners with a visible reason (refuses, doesn't fail). Pure helpers `normalizeDeg`/`parseRotationInput`/`formatDeg` exported + unit-tested (`test/rotationStepper.test.js`, 11). Inline editor only (owner rule); theme tokens only (WCAG AA).
- **Wired into all three Site Planner rotation controls** (overlay slider ‚Üí stepper; markup NumInput ‚Üí stepper; element NumInput+spinners ‚Üí stepper). Each commit is **one undo step** (the hosts' `patchOverlay`/`setSelMarkupGeom`/`rotateAssemblyTo` already `pushHistory`); rotation is a persisted real-world angle (degrees on the model), so it survives reload. The now-orphaned `bumpRot` helper was removed; `spinBtn`/`NumInput` stay (still used elsewhere).
- **Multi-select note (owner asked to confirm):** the stepper's contract already gives his recommended behavior ‚Äî a **typed value = absolute set** (`onCommit`), a **spinner press = ¬±1¬∞ delta** (`onStep`). The current Site Planner rotation panels are single-selection (selEl/selMarkup/one overlay), so absolute-vs-delta only matters once multi-select rotation is wired; the widget is ready for it.

### B457 ‚Äî Recover lost buildings on 8 South / Plan 1 (one-time) `[Persistence]` (task) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-9"; CLOSED 2026-06-25)*
`[x]` **CLOSED ‚Äî unrecoverable, owner declined further pursuit.** Cowork's Phase-B account-wide sweep (signed in, planyr.io) found 17 of the user's plans intact, but **8 South / Plan 1** (id `smqiljx5fngg`) is gone everywhere reachable: cloud row = 0 buildings (version 40), on-device history = 5 snapshots all 0 buildings (Jun 17‚Äì18), device mirror = 0 buildings. The free Supabase tier has no PITR, so the overwritten cloud state isn't server-recoverable. The only untried avenue was another device's local Version History; **the owner said he doesn't care about recovering 8 South ‚Äî close it out** (2026-06-25). The recurrence is prevented by B458 (immediate mirror) + B459 (no thin-clobber) + B460 (no spurious conflict) + B453/B455 (boot union-merge + conflict lockout).

### B460 ‚Äî Opening/reloading a plan re-pushed it and bumped `version` with no edit ‚Üí spurious "changed in another session" conflicts `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, UX)  *(Cowork Phase-B flag #2, 2026-06-25; minted **B460** = highest real B# (B459) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1506 tests (storage.test.js +2 net) ¬∑ build green.** Data-safe before the fix (B459's CAS + content guard means the re-push can't clobber), but the version churn was firing a benign-but-scary loud "reload to merge" banner in any OTHER open tab/device.
- **Root cause:** `mergePulledSites` `toPush` re-pushed a row when `(local.updatedAt) > cloud.updatedAt` ‚Äî and B458's immediate per-edit mirror write advances the local `updatedAt` on every edit while the cloud push is debounced/lagging, so EVERY reload re-pushed identical content, bumped `version`, and made other sessions see a (false) conflict.
- **Fix (`storage.js`):** new stable `contentSig(model)` (drawn collections sorted by id + tombstones, as JSON; both sides are `createSiteModel`-normalized so identical content hashes equal). `toPush` now re-pushes ONLY when the row is cloud-absent OR `contentSig(merged) !== contentSig(cloud)` ‚Äî i.e. the merge actually ADDED/CHANGED/DELETED content. `map[id]` is the union (‚äá cloud) so this never pushes a thinner row; a no-op re-open pushes nothing (no version churn ‚Üí no spurious conflict). A real add/move/**tombstoned delete** still re-pushes (delete propagation preserved ‚Äî explicit regression test). Dropped the obsolete `updatedAt >` and `contentCount >` clauses.
- **Live confirm (no spurious conflict on a benign two-tab re-open) ‚Üí Cowork / VERIFICATION.md V135.**

### B459 ‚Äî The cloud save guards the version NUMBER but not CONTENT, so a stale/thin tab silently overwrote a fuller cloud row `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS ‚Äî the actual 8 South 5-building killer)  *(owner pushed back on the 400ms-debounce story 2026-06-25 ‚Äî "a 400ms gap can't lose 5 buildings I watched save"; he was right; minted **B459** = highest real B# (B458) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1504 tests (cloudThinGuard.test.js +9) ¬∑ build green.** Owner's skepticism was correct and is the key insight: the 400 ms debounce (B458) can only ever lose the ONE edit in flight ‚Äî it cannot explain losing 5 buildings placed over minutes and watched save. Losing 5 needs a SECOND failure, which I'd wrongly waved off as "benign" (the Q3 re-push); Cowork independently refused to take it on faith.
- **Root cause (proven by code audit):** the CAS guard (`optimisticUpsert.js` `casUpsert`) is `WHERE id=‚Ä¶ AND version=‚Ä¶` ‚Äî it compares the version SCALAR only, **never the `data` content**. So a tab holding a stale/thin model (e.g. road-only after a bad load / the deploy-crash reload) at a version that still MATCHES the cloud would PATCH right over a fuller cloud row (5 buildings ‚Üí road-only) and bump the version, badge still "Saved" (it did save ‚Äî the wrong, thinner copy). There was **no fetch-merge and no content check** anywhere on the write path.
- **Fix (`cloudSync.js`):** alongside `siteVersions` we now remember the **content baseline** we last synced per site ‚Äî `siteContent` (item count) + `siteTombs` (tombstone count) ‚Äî populated by `cloudList` (from the cloud row) and advanced on every successful write. New pure `wouldThinClobber(model, baseCount, baseTombs)` blocks a push that would drop **‚â•2 items the cloud still has with no delete-tombstone to explain it** (`lost = (baseCount ‚àí newTombstones) ‚àí contentCount(model) ‚â• 2`). `cloudUpsert` returns `{ ok:false, conflict:true, thinned:true }` on a hit ‚Üí the existing **B455 loud + blocking** conflict banner fires; because the write is REFUSED, the cloud stays intact and the next reload union-merges the fuller copy back (no loss). The keepalive forced-reload push (B452) is guarded the same way. Legit incremental **undo** (‚â§1 unexplained drop/push) and any **tombstoned delete** pass through untouched.
- **Why ‚â•2, not ‚â•1:** an undo-of-a-synced-add drops exactly 1 non-tombstoned item ‚Äî common; the stale-tab clobber drops the whole divergence (5+) in one push. The threshold cleanly separates them; a rare large legit drop trips a recoverable "reload to merge", never silent loss.
- **Closes the loop with B458/B453:** B458 keeps the on-device mirror current; B453 union-merges on boot; **B459 stops the cloud being thinned in the first place.** All three together make the 8 South loss-shape impossible.
- **Live signed-in two-tab / stale-tab clobber repro ‚Üí Cowork Phase B / VERIFICATION.md V134** (self-tests are logged-out; the clobber path needs auth + the cloud).
- **Follow-up noted:** Document Review's `reviewStore` cloud upsert has the same version-only CAS shape (different content model) ‚Äî mirror the guard there next (low priority; the owner's loss was a Site Planner plan).

### B458 ‚Äî Autosave debounced the on-device mirror too, so a reload-within-400ms lost the edit everywhere `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(follow-up to B452/B453 after Cowork's live Phase-A diagnosis on prod, 2026-06-25; minted **B458** = highest real B# (B457) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1495 tests (storage.test.js +3) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ V133 (`ui-audit/verify-immediate-mirror.mjs`, 6/6, logged-out).** This is the REAL structural fix the 8 South building-loss needed ‚Äî B452 (keepalive on the app's own forced reload) and B453 (boot union-merge, verified correct) were necessary but not sufficient.
- **Root cause (Cowork reproduced on prod, confirmed against the merged code):** the Site Planner autosave (`SitePlanner.jsx` ~L1438) debounced **everything** by 400 ms ‚Äî the localStorage mirror (`planarfit:sites:cloud:<uid>` / `:v1`) AND the version-history snapshot were both written only inside `setTimeout(saveSite, 400)`. So a reload/crash within 400 ms of an edit lost the building from cloud, the on-device mirror, AND history **at once**, and boot's union-merge (`mergePulledSites`/`mergeSiteContent`, correct per B453) had nothing fuller locally to restore. *Push-abandoned, structural ‚Äî not a CAS conflict* (matches Cowork's mechanism conclusion; Document Review already split immediate-mirror from debounced-cloud in `usePersistence.js`, so only the Site Planner was the laggard).
- **Fix:** the autosave effect now writes the device mirror **immediately** on every edit, decoupled from the debounced cloud push. `storage.js` `saveSite(partial, { skipHistory })` gained an option; the **immediate** write is a DEFAULT save (history ON ‚Äî `snapshotVersion` backs up the prior version before overwriting, the B126 thinning safety net, AND makes the rollback snapshot reload-safe), coalesced to ~50 ms so a same-shape drag can't thrash `writeSites` (and `snapshotVersion`'s count-based sig-dedup already skips same-shape frames). The **settle tick** (400 ms) keeps the cloud push and passes `{ skipHistory: true }` so it can't double-snapshot. Conflict/read-only/CAS gates on the cloud push are unchanged; the immediate local write always runs (a local save is always safe ‚Äî it's the whole recovery net).
- **Flagged correction vs the approved plan:** the plan put `skipHistory` on the immediate write; that would have lost the prior version on a thinning edit (the B126 safety net). Moved history ONTO the immediate write instead ‚Äî strictly more correct AND makes history reload-safe too. (Unit-test'd: immediate-then-settle backs up the prior version exactly once per shape.)
- **Reconciled Cowork's other audit Qs (no code beyond B458):** Q2 boot does union-merge (it was only moot because the input was debounced away ‚Äî B458 fixes the input). Q3/Q4 the version-creep-on-open is the benign CAS-guarded boot re-push (`pullCloud` `toPush`) ‚Äî can't clobber a fuller row; left as-is (tightening it risks under-pushing).
- **Live signed-in cloud round-trip (survive-a-reload with a real cloud row) ‚Üí Cowork Phase B / VERIFICATION.md V133** (self-tests run logged-out).
