     B# across both files was B448, so minted **B449‚ÄìB457**. Branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`:
     lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1468 tests (8 test files added/extended) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ lazy chunks intact. The
     live-edge paths (Cloudflare 404, the deploy-escape screen, survive-a-forced-reload, the read-only
     lockout) are delegated to Cowork on preview/prod per the brief ‚Äî flagged in VERIFICATION.md, not
     marked done on the sandbox alone. B457 is an owner+Cowork ACTION (not code) and stays Open. -->

### B449 ‚Äî SPA fallback serves index.html for a missing /assets/* chunk (masks a 404 as a text/html MIME error) `[App Shell / Deploy]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1468 tests ¬∑ build green. Live-edge 404 confirmation delegated to Cowork (the sandbox's vite-preview can't reproduce Cloudflare's edge ‚Äî verified it serves index.html for a missing /assets path, which is exactly the masking bug, but only Cloudflare honors `_redirects`).** Repro: requesting a content-hashed chunk a newer deploy deleted (`/assets/SitePlannerApp-<deadhash>.js`) returns 200 + index.html (text/html) instead of a real 404 ‚Üí the browser tries to parse HTML as a JS module and dies with "Expected a JavaScript module script ‚Ä¶ MIME type text/html" / "Failed to fetch dynamically imported module".
- **Root cause:** the Cloudflare Pages project answers unmatched paths with index.html (SPA not-found handling), which catches missing `/assets/*` too. There was no in-repo routing file to stop it.
- **Fix:** added **`public/_redirects`** (copied to `dist/` like `public/_headers`) with **NO** `/* /index.html 200` catch-all ‚Äî Planyr uses HASH routing (`#/‚Ä¶`, `src/app/route.js`), so every real navigation already lands on a static file and no catch-all is needed. A single rule `/assets/*  /404.html  404` forces a missing chunk to a real 404 (Cloudflare serves real static files BEFORE `_redirects` rules, so it can NEVER 404 a live asset ‚Äî only a deleted one). Added a tiny dependency-free **`public/404.html`**. Confirmed `_redirects`/`_headers`/`404.html` all emit to `dist/`. The brief's prescribed catch-all was the WRONG fix here (it would *cause* the masking); flagged + corrected.
- **Deduped:** related to B221/B239 (stale-chunk recovery) but distinct ‚Äî this is the server-side 404 honesty those rely on.

### B450 ‚Äî Chunk-recovery dead-ends with no escape when the FRESH build is also missing the chunk `[App Shell / Deploy]` (bug, follow-up to B239)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; shipped same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1468 tests (chunkReload.test.js +8) ¬∑ build green. The live escape-screen render is a Cowork preview/prod check (the sandbox e2e harness `verify-chunk-reload.mjs` is timing-flaky on the heavy full-app reload ‚Äî confirmed it fails identically on unmodified `main`, so not a regression; the pure decision is deterministically unit-tested).** Repro: parked on "A new version of Planyr is ready / Reload to update"; clicking re-runs `reloadFresh()`, the fresh index.html still references a 404ing chunk, the 10s cooldown suppresses the retry ‚Üí the same dead screen returns.
- **Fix:** the cache-buster `?_r=` param is now the "already tried a fresh reload" signal. New pure `recoveryStage(arrivedViaFreshReload, now, lastReloadAt)` ‚Üí `reload | stuck | cooldown`; `installChunkReloadGuard` captures `hasReloadParam(win)` BEFORE `stripReloadParam` tidies it and only auto-reloads on `reload` (so `stuck`/`cooldown` surface to the ErrorBoundary instead of looping ‚Äî one automatic attempt, then a manual escape). `ErrorBoundary.jsx` shows a distinct **"Planyr is finishing a deploy ‚Äî try again in a minute"** message whose **Try again** clears the cooldown stamp (`clearReloadGuard`) before reloading. (`src/app/chunkReload.js`, `src/app/ErrorBoundary.jsx`.)

### B451 ‚Äî Set Vite `base:"/"` for the Cloudflare root deploy `[App Shell / Deploy]` (task, hardening)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-3"; shipped same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). build green; built index.html references `/assets/‚Ä¶` (absolute); `/sequence/` + all three lazy chunks resolve at root.** `vite.config.js` set `base: command === "build" ? (process.env.PLANYR_BASE || "/") : "/"` ‚Äî production is Cloudflare Pages at the domain root, so root-absolute asset URLs resolve identically on every page (the old relative `"./"` was a retired-GitHub-Pages artifact; `PLANYR_BASE` env-gates a subpath build if ever resurrected). Hardening, not the B449 cause; done regardless.

### B452 ‚Äî A forced reload drops unsaved work: `reloadFresh()` / the ErrorBoundary reload don't cloud-flush first `[App Shell / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-4"; shipped same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1468 tests (flushRegistry +5, keepalivePush +4) ¬∑ build green. Survive-a-forced-reload is a Cowork signed-in check (self-tests run logged-out, so the cloud-push path needs auth).** Corrected-scope note (flagged vs the brief): the LOCAL half already runs ‚Äî `reloadFresh` navigates via `location.replace`, which fires `beforeunload`/`visibilitychange`, and the Site Planner already saves localStorage synchronously there. The real gap was the **cloud** push (debounced/async, abandoned mid-navigation).
- **Fix:** new cross-workspace **`src/app/flushRegistry.js`** (`registerFlush`/`flushAll`); `reloadFresh` calls `flushAll()` before navigating. Site Planner + Doc Review register a flusher that does the synchronous local save PLUS a **keepalive cloud push** (`keepaliveCasPush` in `optimisticUpsert.js` ‚Äî a `fetch({keepalive:true})` PATCH that outlives the navigation, **version-guarded** exactly like `casUpsert` so it can never clobber a newer row, and never inserts; reads the token synchronously via `currentAccessToken()`). `keepaliveCloudPush`/`keepaliveFlushSite` (sites) + `keepaliveFlushReview` (doc_reviews). Gated off when read-only/conflict (B455). The synchronous local save + the next-load boot merge (B453) remain the guarantee; this shortens the lost window.

### B453 ‚Äî Boot reconciliation must UNION-merge the on-device mirror with the cloud pull `[Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-5"; verified same session)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî verified ALREADY-CORRECT + locked with a regression test (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1468 tests (storage.test.js +1) ¬∑ build green.** Flagged vs the brief: this is **already implemented**. `saveSite` writes the on-device autosave mirror to `planarfit:sites:cloud:<uid>` ‚Äî the SAME key `pullCloud ‚Üí mergePulledSites` reads as `existing` ‚Äî and the boot path builds the map from the local cache FIRST, folds each cloud row in via `mergeSiteContent` (union-by-id), and re-pushes anything fuller (`toPush`). So a building present in EITHER copy survives a reload (B124/B126/B127). Added the explicit incident-shape regression test (5 local buildings + a road vs a road-only/0-building cloud row ‚Üí all 5 survive AND `toPush` heals the cloud). No code change needed; documented the verified-already finding.

### B454 ‚Äî Root-cause: how a building-loss got past the active B314 CAS guard `[Persistence]` (bug, diagnostic)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-6"; investigated same session)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî written root cause + audit (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`).** Audited every write to `public.sites` / `public.doc_reviews`:
- **(a) CAS-bypass via a raw `.upsert` ‚Äî RULED OUT.** The only raw `.upsert` of the `data` column are the degrade fallbacks in `cloudSync.js`/`reviewStore.js`, which fire ONLY when the `version` column is absent (migration ran 2026-06-20, so they don't). `sharing.js` raw updates touch `team_id` only (never `data`). The merge re-push (`pullCloud` `toPush`) and `setProjectStatus` both route through the guarded `cloudUpsert`. No unguarded `data` write exists.
- **Most likely cause = (b) conflict-then-strand during the concurrent deploy.** The fuller (5-building) state existed only in a tab whose every save **conflicted** (a racing/thin tab advanced the version), so the buildings stayed local-only there; meanwhile a thin tab that DID hold the current version won the CAS and wrote the road-only row. The conflict banner was **dismissible and didn't block**, so editing continued, every save re-conflicting. Then the **forced reload** (the chunk-crash incident) landed in a context whose durable local cache was the thin copy, so the boot merge had nothing fuller to union. Secondary possibility: an erroneous `deletedIds` tombstone (the only way `mergeSiteContent`'s union drops a building). `mergeSiteContent` proven to union `els` by id otherwise.
- **Closed by:** **B452** (keepalive cloud push on forced reload) + **B455** (conflict is now loud+blocking, and the Web Locks read-only lockout stops a stale/thin tab winning the CAS over the active one). **B453** is the recovery net. **B456** explains why the snapshots looked unusable (the "0 buildings" label hid recoverable history). Reconcile with Cowork's live two-tab repro before final conclusions; the fixes are correct either way. Regression coverage: the B453 incident-shape test + the B455 `canCloudSave` gate test.

### B455 ‚Äî Conflict + at-risk saves must be loud AND blocking; finish the deferred read-only lockout `[Persistence]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-7"; built same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1468 tests (editorLock.test.js +11, canCloudSave gate) ¬∑ build green. The live single-active-editor lockout is a Cowork two-tab signed-in check.** Three parts, mirrored into Site Planner AND Doc Review:
- **(1) Conflict loud + BLOCKING.** A `casUpsert` conflict now PAUSES further cloud autosave until reload (the local save still runs, so work is preserved + reload union-merges it). Site Planner's conflict banner lost its dismiss ‚úï (blocking) and is reworded ("Saving is paused‚Ä¶ Reload to merge"); Doc Review gates the same via the pure `canCloudSave(status, readOnly)`.
- **(2) Web Locks single-active-editor lockout (the deferred half of B313).** New `src/shared/presence/editorLock.js` (`createEditorLock` + pure `lockRole`): only ONE tab holds the editor lock per project; a second tab on the same plan/review goes **read-only** and can't push over the active one ‚Äî the direct structural fix for the stale-tab clobber. Degrades OPEN where Web Locks is unavailable; hands off automatically when the active tab closes. Site Planner shows a read-only banner; both gate the cloud push (+ keepalive) on `readOnly`.
- **(3) Unconfirmed-save watchdog.** A cloud write that doesn't resolve within ~6s escalates to the loud B125 banner with Retry (Site Planner) / flips the badge red (Doc Review). Tied to the in-flight write, so sustained editing never false-triggers it.
- Builds on B125/B313/B314; extends B313, doesn't rebuild it.

### B456 ‚Äî Version History unusable for picking a restore point: "0 buildings" on every row, indistinguishable timestamps `[Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-8"; fixed same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1468 tests (storage.test.js +5) ¬∑ build green.** Root cause: the row label used the snapshot's stored `mainBuildingCount`, which excludes attached additions AND dog-ears ‚Üí it read "0 buildings" while buildings existed (crash-class: it hid recoverable work), and rows minutes apart looked identical.
- **Fix:** new pure `summarizeVersion(model)` computes a real content summary ("12 parcels ¬∑ 1 road ¬∑ 5 buildings ¬∑ 3 markups") from the **stored full model** (so OLD snapshots benefit too) counting REAL buildings (`isBuilding`, excludes only dog-ear sub-pieces). `listVersions` now returns `{ at, buildings, summary }` and de-dupes adjacent same-second/same-shape rows; the dialog renders the summary and a **seconds-precision** timestamp so two backups in the same minute are distinguishable. (`lib/storage.js`, `SitePlanner.jsx`.)

### B445 ‚Äî Site-plan overlay drop target has no affordance + the panel isn't a dropzone `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 via screenshot as "this does not allow anything to actually be dropped in, should work like enterprise software"; minted **B445** = highest real B# across both files (B444) + 1)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/hopeful-turing-1x24sg`). lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1425 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless V131 (`ui-audit/verify-overlay-dnd.mjs`, 7/7).**
- **Repro (owner):** Site Planner ‚Üí open the "Site-plan overlay" panel ‚Üí its copy says "Drop a site-plan PDF onto the map (or browse)", but dragging a file over the panel gives **zero feedback** and releasing it does nothing (or the browser opens the PDF in a new tab) ‚Üí reads as broken / "doesn't allow anything to be dropped in."
- **Root cause (confirmed in code):** the canvas wrapper (`wrapRef`, `SitePlanner.jsx`) already HAD a working `onDragOver`/`onDrop` ‚Üí `addOverlayFile`, but (1) there was **no drag-hover affordance** so the live zone looked dead, and (2) the **left panel itself** (where the cursor goes ‚Äî the "Add site plan‚Ä¶" button + copy live there) had **no drop handlers**, so a release there hit the browser default. On narrow screens the panel is `position:absolute; zIndex:1100`, outside the `wrapRef` subtree, so its drops could never reach the canvas handler.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`), reusing the existing ingestion path (`addOverlayFile` ‚Üí `openOverlayFile`/`uploadOverlayFile`) + the Doc-Review `FileBrowser` dropzone pattern:**
  1. **Panel is now a real dropzone** ‚Äî the overlay `Section`'s button + copy are wrapped in a dashed-border `<div>` that highlights on hover (accent border + `PAL.accentSoft` bg, anti-flicker via `e.currentTarget === e.target`), routes drop ‚Üí `addOverlayFile`, and shows "Drop to add this site plan" while dragging. The button stays the click-to-browse path.
  2. **Canvas "drop to place" hint** ‚Äî a full-bleed dashed overlay (`pointerEvents:none`, above the SVG's `zIndex:1`) mounts only during an active file drag, with a centered "Drop site plan to place it on the map" pill.
  3. **Window-level guard** ‚Äî a `useEffect` blocks the browser's default file-drop everywhere (`dragover`/`drop` preventDefault when the drag carries Files), so releasing a few pixels off-target no longer navigates away / opens the PDF (the core "nothing happens / data loss" feeling).
- **Edge cases:** multi-file drop takes the first file + `flashWarn`s ("one overlay placed at a time"); panel `onDrop` `stopPropagation`s so it can't double-fire to the canvas; all new colors use the existing `PAL` theme tokens (no raw hex), keeping WCAG AA.
- **Deduped:** distinct from the Doc-Review file-drop dropzones (`ProjectFilesDrawer`/`FileBrowser`) ‚Äî this is the Site Planner *site-plan overlay*, a separate surface; the pattern was reused, not the component.

### B446 ‚Äî Dropping/opening a file gives no clear signal it registered `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; fixed same session per STANDING RULE #1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/vibrant-pascal-wkg50i`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1434 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless V131 (`ui-audit/verify-open-feedback.mjs`, 6/6, logged out).** Owner repro: drop a PDF (especially with a doc already open, or open from the Files panel) ‚Üí no immediate, unmistakable sign anything is happening; it can look like nothing registered ("silence is a crash").
- **Root cause:** `setBusy(true)` existed but the only "Opening‚Ä¶" text rendered in the EMPTY state. The B294 drop-over-open path and the `openReview`/Files-panel/`loadSingleReview` paths render no empty state, so they showed no loading signal at all; a non-PDF reject set `err`, which the canvas never displays, and a null drop / `loadReview‚Üínull` could no-op silently.
- **Fix:** a canvas-level **"Opening &lt;name&gt;‚Ä¶" overlay** (`data-testid="opening-overlay"`, spinner + filename, reuses the `spin` keyframe) driven by `busy`, rendered in BOTH the empty-state and the canvas branches (so it covers the prior sheet on a drop-over-open). `busy`/`busyLabel` are now set on EVERY entry path ‚Äî `openFile`, `openReview`, `loadSingleReview` ‚Äî and cleared in token-guarded `finally`s so a rapid switch can't let an older load's finally hide the newer overlay. **Silence-is-a-crash:** `openFile` surfaces a null drop + a non-PDF reject + a read failure on the always-visible `openErr` banner (role="alert") AND the inline `err`; `openReview` surfaces a missing-row and a `loadReview‚Üínull`. A superseded (older) load stays intentionally silent ‚Äî the newer load owns the feedback.
- **Deduped:** a GAP in **B294** (which only wired the drop *handler*, not feedback) + the **B405/docIntent** open paths ‚Äî not a duplicate of any.

### B447 ‚Äî Switching between files is flaky and "forgets which file I was on" `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; fixed same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/vibrant-pascal-wkg50i`). Code complete + logged-out paths verified V131; the auth-only switch-determinism check (two saved cloud reviews) logged for a signed-in live pass in VERIFICATION.md V131.** Owner repro: open A, switch to B, switch back to A ‚Üí A's backdrop/markups/calibration/view/breadcrumb don't reliably return; clicking the file list feels like it drops the doc you were on.
- **Root cause (the clobber):** the debounced autosave's pending cloud write is cancelled the instant a switch changes the persistence `deps` (the effect cleanup clears the timer), so the outgoing review's last edit lived ONLY in the synchronous localStorage mirror. `openReview` then loaded the **stale cloud copy** by id and never reconciled with that mirror ‚Äî so switching back overwrote the newer local state with the older cloud one.
- **Fix:** `openReview` now (1) `await saveNow()` to **flush the outgoing review's pending write** before the incoming load begins, and (2) loads the incoming via `reconcile(await loadReview(id), readDraft(uid, id))` so it picks up that file's newer local mirror if the cloud is behind ‚Äî exactly the pattern the resume-on-mount path already uses. The lazy-mount **resume effect was confirmed to already stand down** for an in-workspace switch (its `booted`-once guard + `projectId` early-return + `bootDocIntentRef` mean it never races an explicit `openReview`), matching the cross-workspace docIntent contract the brief asked to mirror.
- **Depends on B448** (the in-memory bytes cache) so a switch-back is reliable even while the file's first upload is still in flight.
- **Deduped:** a GAP/regression of **B52** (the load-supersede token, intact) + the resume/docIntent fix ‚Äî not a duplicate.

### B448 ‚Äî Backdrop vanishes on switch/reload while a fresh drop's upload is still in flight `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-3"; fixed same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/vibrant-pascal-wkg50i`). New pure `lib/sessionBytes.js` + 5 unit tests (1434 total); logged-out render-from-cache verified V131; the keyless-mid-upload signed-in confirm logged V131.** Owner repro: drop a PDF, then immediately switch files (or reload) before the Drive/Supabase upload resolves ‚Üí the source is still **keyless** (no storageKey/driveKey), so the backdrop can't be re-fetched and you land on the re-drop banner / a blank canvas with orphaned markups.
- **Fix:** a session-lifetime **byte cache** (`sessionBytes`, module-scoped Map keyed by `srcId`, capped FIFO at 8) holds the dropped **File** (never a raw ArrayBuffer ‚Äî pdf.js transfers a buffer to its worker and detaches it; a File re-reads cleanly). `openFile` caches the bytes the moment a srcId is minted; `fetchSourceBytes` checks the cache **before** `classifySource`/Drive/Supabase, so a dropped backdrop stays viewable for the rest of the session regardless of upload state. Survives the lazy DocReview workspace unmounting/remounting (a tab switch mid-upload). Only when the bytes are truly absent does the precise B405 re-drop banner show.
- **Deduped:** net-new ‚Äî the safety net that makes B447's switch-back reliable mid-upload; complements (does not replace) B405's source-state taxonomy.

### B439 ‚Äî Rename / delete projects from the breadcrumb switcher (right-click + kebab) `[Header-Nav / Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; built same session per STANDING RULE #1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/beautiful-archimedes-0qjr3w`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1416 tests (4 new) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless `ui-audit/verify-b439-b440-project-manage.mjs` 7/7 (Site path).** Triggered by the owner's live report: "i cant change names or delete in the schedule module."
- **What shipped:** every project row in the Row-1 switcher dropdown (`ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx`) now carries a hover-revealed kebab `‚ãØ` **and** a right-click menu (both open the same menu) ‚Üí **Rename** (inline `<input>` in the row; Enter/blur commit, Esc cancel, trim, empty/whitespace rejected ‚Üí keeps prior name) + **Delete** (a two-step inline confirm, never `window.confirm`/`prompt` per the owner rule). The menu is a SECOND portal layer (zIndex 5000/5001) above `AnchoredMenu`'s click-away backdrop (4000/4001), so a click inside it never closes the parent dropdown (the documented gotcha ‚Äî handled by stacking, not an outside-click exclusion list).
- **New props** `onRenameProject(id,newName)` / `onDeleteProject(id)` (threaded through `AppHeader.jsx`). Uncontrolled (Site/Markup) ‚Üí drives the store directly + `refresh()`; controlled (Schedule) ‚Üí the workspace's callbacks (B440).
- **Store layer:** `projects.js` gains `renameProject`/`deleteProject` thin wrappers; `storage.js` gains **`deleteSiteGroup(groupId)`** ‚Äî deletes every plan in the group locally + cloud, returning an aggregated `{ ok, removed, error? }`. Honest-failure rule honored: a signed-in delete that matches **zero rows** (RLS/ownership mismatch) is reported as `ok:false` (would otherwise reappear on reload claiming "deleted"); the breadcrumb surfaces it as a toast. Deleting the **active** project routes to the all-projects view.
- **Tests:** `test/deleteSiteGroup.test.js` (4) ‚Äî group rename relabels every plan, group delete removes only that group + reports `removed`, unknown-group no-op, single-plan case. Unique ids per test to respect the B372 resurrection tombstone.
- **Deduped:** distinct from **B158** (still Open) ‚Äî that's the YOUR SITES *sidebar* row ‚úï; this is the *breadcrumb switcher dropdown* (different component; a "project" here = a whole Site group).

### B440 ‚Äî Extend the Schedule iframe bridge for rename / delete `[Scheduler / Header-Nav]` (feature) ‚Äî depended on B439  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; built same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/beautiful-archimedes-0qjr3w`). Code complete + symmetric with the existing nav bridge; signed-in/online LIVE check logged in VERIFICATION.md (the embedded scheduler can't boot in the offline sandbox ‚Äî CDN + its own Supabase backend are blocked, the same limitation `verify-schedule-picker.mjs` hits here, so the bridge half isn't headless-verifiable).** This is what the owner actually reported broken.
- **The brief's "verify first" CONFIRMED:** the embedded app (`public/sequence/index.html`) ALREADY had internal `renameProject`/`deleteProject` (the latter with a snapshot + `window.confirm`). So B440 was just command-plumbing, exactly as the `Scheduler.jsx` comment implied ‚Äî NOT a from-scratch in-app rename/delete.
- **What shipped:** `Scheduler.jsx` passes `onRenameProject ‚Üí post planar:nav-rename` and `onDeleteProject ‚Üí post planar:nav-delete` (alongside the existing nav-select/dashboard/new). The embedded app handles both: `nav-rename` ‚Üí `renameProject(id, name.trim())` (re-guards empty); `nav-delete` ‚Üí `deleteProject(id, { skipConfirm:true })` then routes to the Dashboard (reports) if the deleted project was active. `deleteProject` was refactored to accept `{ skipConfirm }` so the breadcrumb's inline confirm doesn't double-prompt with the embedded `window.confirm` (standalone scheduler UI untouched ‚Äî still confirms). Both re-emit `planar:nav-state`, so the breadcrumb updates automatically.

### B442 ‚Äî Merge/Clear banner buttons unclickable; canvas grab-cursor + pan bleeds through `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 via screenshot+voice as "NEW-1"; minted **B442** = highest real B# across both files (B441) + 1)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/gallant-euler-sk8kwd`). lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1421 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless V (`ui-audit/verify-merge-banner.mjs`, 6/6).**
- **Repro (owner):** Select tool ‚Üí Shift-click 2+ parcels ‚Üí the floating "N parcels selected ¬∑ Merge parcels ‚èé ¬∑ Clear" banner appears ‚Üí hovering Clear showed the canvas **grab hand** (not a pointer), and clicking it **panned the map** instead of clearing the selection; Merge was likewise dead.
- **Root cause (confirmed in code):** the banner is a sibling of the SVG canvas under `wrapRef`, and the SVG carries `zIndex:1` while the banner had **no z-index** (auto). A positioned `zIndex:1` paints *over* an auto sibling, so the transparent canvas sat on top of the banner ‚Äî it swallowed the clicks and showed its own `cursor:grab`. The buttons' own `cursor:pointer` never won because the press never reached them.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`, merge-banner block ~L6871):** gave the banner an explicit **`zIndex:6`** (clears the SVG's `1`, below the slide-in tool rail) so its buttons are the topmost element ‚Äî clicks land, the pointer cursor applies, Clear runs `setCombineSel([])`, Merge runs `mergeParcels()`, neither starts a pan. Added defensive `onPointerDown`/`onMouseDown` `stopPropagation` on the banner container (belt-and-suspenders for any future reparenting under a pointer-handling ancestor ‚Äî the z-index is the actual fix). Applied the **identical guard to the sibling parcel-edge easement banner** (`tool==="easement" && easeMode==="parceledge"`), which had the same latent bug.
- **Dedup:** NOT **B271** (that fixed interrupted-gesture recovery via pointercancel/blur ‚Äî a stuck-capture *recovery* path); this was a plain missing z-index on a normal press. NOT **B441** (that was MapFinder county-map identify *latency*; this is the planner's banner stacking). Cross-referenced both.

### B443 ‚Äî Shift-click parcel multi-select unreliable / "needs several tries" `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; minted **B443**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/gallant-euler-sk8kwd`). Headless-verified: 4 interior shift-clicks ‚Üí "4 parcels selected" on the first try (`ui-audit/verify-merge-banner.mjs`).**
- **Repro (owner):** with the Select tool, Shift-clicking parcels to add them to the merge selection often didn't register ‚Äî it took several clicks.
- **Root cause (confirmed in code ‚Äî broader than first hypothesized):** **B420** makes a parcel's *interior* click-through ‚Äî only its ~12px boundary hit-stroke grabs the press. So a Shift-click on the parcel **body** misses the parcel and falls through to the canvas background handler `onBgDown`, whose `tool==="select" && e.shiftKey` branch started a **marquee select** (not a merge-toggle). The pick only registered when the click happened to land on the thin boundary line ‚Üí the "click a couple times" feel. (Secondary, rarer: when a parcel is *unlocked + selected*, the capture-phase vertex-insert `onCanvasVtxDownCapture` could steal a Shift-click meant to merge a neighbor sharing that edge.)
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):**
  - `onBgDown` select+shift branch (~L1990): before starting a marquee, ray-cast (`pointInRing`) the press against the parcels; if it lands inside one, `toggleMerge(hit.id)` + select it and return. Topmost (last-drawn) parcel wins, matching the boundary-stroke path. Gated on `settings.parcelSelect` (respects the "Select parcels: off" browse mode). Marquee still works on genuinely empty canvas.
  - `onCanvasVtxDownCapture` (~L2508): the Shift+edge vertex-insert now yields when the press is inside *another* parcel's body ‚Äî that's a merge-pick on a neighbor, not an edit of the selected path ‚Äî letting it fall through to the merge-toggle.
- **Verified after B442** (a residual stuck pointer-capture from the banner bug could have masked this) on a clean canvas.

### B444 ‚Äî Selection-count text overlapped/garbled by the Merge button in the merge banner `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 as "NEW-3"; minted **B444**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/gallant-euler-sk8kwd`). Headless-verified the count label and Merge button bounding boxes do not intersect (`ui-audit/verify-merge-banner.mjs`).**
- **Repro (owner screenshot):** the banner read "4 parce**s** selected" ‚Äî the red "Merge parcels" button appeared to sit *on top of* the count text.
- **Root cause:** NOT a flex-layout defect ‚Äî it was a **symptom of B442's z-index bug.** The banner sat *behind* the SVG, so the canvas's own parcel area-labels and strokes painted *over* the banner text, garbling "parcels" ‚Üí "parces". A flex row never overlaps its own items.
- **Fix:** resolved by B442's `zIndex:6` (the banner now paints on top, crisp). Hardened anyway: wrapped the count in a `<span>` and added `whiteSpace:nowrap` to the banner so the label can never wrap or be crowded by the buttons at any count. Headless check asserts the label's and Merge button's rects are disjoint with 4 selected.

### B441 ‚Äî Parcel selection feels laggy: paint the highlight optimistically on click, confirm via identify in the background `[Site Planner / map]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 from a ChatGPT thread as "NEW-1"; minted **B441** = highest real B# across both files (B440) + 1)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/hopeful-brown-q7q4jo`). lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1421 tests ¬∑ build green.**
- **Repro (owner):** clicking a lot on the live county map took a variable delay ‚Äî sometimes a couple seconds ‚Äî between the click and the parcel highlighting. Root cause (confirmed in code): `MapFinder.handleClick` did `setBusy(true)` then `await identifyParcelEager(...)` ‚Äî a network identify against the county ArcGIS REST service(s) ‚Äî and only painted the highlight + pushed the selection AFTER that promise resolved. The variable multi-second lag was county-server round-trip latency, not client compute.
- **Fix ‚Äî optimistic local highlight, then reconcile (no network on the felt path):**
  - The county parcel outlines are already drawn client-side as esri-leaflet vector `featureLayer`s (`makeParcelLayer`). New `optimisticHitAt(latlng)` hit-tests those already-loaded features (cheap `getBounds().contains` reject ‚Üí ray-cast on the outer rings) and returns a hit shaped like an identify hit. New pure `geoJsonToEsriFeature` (arcgis.js) converts the display layer's GeoJSON polygon into the same esri-shaped `{geometry:{rings},attributes}` the highlight/select path already consumes ‚Äî so the on-screen outline feeds the **exact same** `addParcelHit` pipeline (one source of truth, B233/B22 keys intact). Prefers a real county over the statewide TxGIO backup (mirrors identify's source priority), then the tighter parcel when several overlap.
  - `handleClick` now paints the optimistic highlight **synchronously, before** the identify starts ‚Üí the click feels instant. The `await identifyParcelEager(...)` still runs in the background as the authoritative confirm (still feeds the circuit breaker via `onSettled` ‚Üí `recordSourceResult`, B244 unchanged). On return it **always rolls back the optimistic outline and rebuilds from the identified geometry** (full-res + real account/address attrs), so the IMPORTED parcel is never the simplified display outline. No flash ‚Äî the remove+re-add happen in one synchronous turn after the await.
  - **Reconcile cases:** identify confirms the same parcel ‚Üí seamless real-geometry swap; identify returns a different/refined parcel (multipart per B36c, or a statewide-backup relabel) ‚Üí optimistic rolled back, authoritative added (multipart still highlights every tract); identify finds nothing / errors ‚Üí optimistic highlight **visibly rolled back** + the existing "No parcel right there" / "server isn't responding" message (B245 wording preserved).
  - **Instant local toggle-off:** new `selectedHitAt(latlng)` ‚Äî a click inside an already-highlighted parcel now deselects it with **zero** network round-trip (we already have its geometry).
- **Edge cases handled:** display layer zoom-gated (`PARCEL_MINZOOM` 14) ‚Üí when nothing's loaded under the click, `optimisticHitAt` returns null and we fall back to the original await-identify-with-busy behavior (never fabricate a highlight from nothing). Optimistic add only fires when the parcel isn't already highlighted, so a rollback can never strip a pre-existing selection. `selectParcelAt` (address-search select, B233) shares the same reconcile pipeline; the optimistic step is click-only (an address select already runs after a `flyTo`, so there's no felt lag there).
- **Tests:** +3 unit tests for `geoJsonToEsriFeature` (Polygon flatten + attrs, MultiPolygon flatten, null/non-polygon) round-tripping through `outerRingsLngLat` (test/arcgis.test.js, 19 in that file). lint 0 errors ¬∑ full suite 1421 green ¬∑ build green.

### B436 ‚Äî e2e: open a PDF so the per-tool rail-arm assertions actually execute `[Doc Review / Markup, Infra]` (task) ‚Äî follow-up from B432/B280  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ¬∑ build green ¬∑ mechanism verified headless (`ui-audit/verify-b436-fixture-pdf.mjs`, 8/8).**
- **The gap:** the B280 fixture seeds a *site*, not a *doc_review with a PDF*, so on CI the Review module opened to the file browser and the tool rail never rendered ‚Üí Section B's per-tool `aria-pressed` tests all skipped (green, but the ARM path ‚Äî the heart of NEW-9 ‚Äî wasn't exercised; only Section A schema conformance was).
- **Fix (code-only, deploy-independent):** added a tiny valid 1-page fixture PDF `e2e/fixtures/sample.pdf` (generated with a correct xref; parses in pdf.js ‚Äî 612√ó792). Section B's `beforeEach` now `setInputFiles` it onto the always-rendered header file input (`input[type="file"][accept*="pdf"]`); `openFile()` parses it and mounts the canvas so `markup-rail` renders, then each `tool-<id>` test arms and asserts `aria-pressed="true"`.
- **Robustness:** a tool the matrix lists before it's deployed is **skipped, not failed** (`btn.count()` guard) ‚Äî the matrix can lead the live build; likewise the whole per-tool test skips gracefully if the rail never renders (storage hiccup / older deploy). Targets the file input by attribute (not a new testid) so it works against the current production build.
- **Verified:** `ui-audit/verify-b436-fixture-pdf.mjs` drives the local preview LOGGED-OUT (the parse ‚Üí canvas ‚Üí rail path needs no auth; only the best-effort background upload does) and confirms the rail renders + line/rect/arc/dimension arm. lint 0; local e2e 22 passed + 22 skipped (auth-gated still skip without creds locally). The auth-gated arm runs for real against the deploy in CI.

### B432 ‚Äî Per-tool matrix assertions + loop driver in CLAUDE.md `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ‚Äî NEW-9, closes the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1390 tests ¬∑ build green. V127.**
- **`e2e/markup-tools.spec.js` upgraded** with two sections:
  - **Section A ‚Äî matrix ‚Üî propertySchema conformance (no auth, no browser).** For every tool row in `toolsForWorkspace("doc")` (except `drawMode:"mode"` rows), asserts `schemaForMarkup({kind:id}).map(s=>s.key).sort()` equals `propsForTool(id).sort()`. Runs in the same Playwright process via pure-JS imports; if propertySchema drifts from the matrix the loop flags it before any browser opens. Imports: `TOOL_MATRIX`, `propsForTool` from `tools.matrix.js`; `schemaForMarkup` from `propertySchema.js`.
  - **Section B ‚Äî per-tool rail arm (auth-gated, B280 account).** A `test.describe("per-tool rail arm")` block generates one test per doc-workspace non-mode tool row. Each test clicks `getByTestId("tool-<id>")` and asserts `aria-pressed="true"`. Gracefully skips (with a precise explanatory message) when the tool rail isn't visible (no PDF open / B280 fixture not seeded yet). The rail is already wired with the correct `data-testid` and `aria-pressed` attributes (ToolRail B330).
  - The old "pending wiring" stub loop is replaced ‚Äî the loop is now real (Section A fires immediately; Section B fires as soon as the B280 seeded account can open a PDF).
- **Loop driver section added to CLAUDE.md** (before `## KEY DECISIONS`): documents the matrix rule (‚õî never edit to pass a test), how both sections work, what a red row means + how to fix it, and how to grow assertions as each tool ships.
- **B423 umbrella is now complete** (B424‚ÄìB432 all done). The engine is live, the spec is machine-checkable, and the loop is encoded.

### B431 ‚Äî Unified interaction model + edit handles `[Doc Review / Markup + Site Planner]` (feature) ‚Äî NEW-8, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1390 tests ¬∑ build green. ‚è≥ signed-in round-trip ‚Üí V126.**
- **ParcelDrawing `window.prompt` ‚Üí inline `numEdit`** (the explicit plan blocker). `calibrateFrom` in `ParcelDrawing.jsx` now sets `calBox` state `{pts, px, sx, sy, val, err}` instead of calling `window.prompt`. A positioned inline div renders at the line's midpoint: `Length (ft)` label, input (Enter = commit, Esc = cancel), "Set" button, error message on invalid. `onDown` while calBox is open cancels it. Matches the DocReview inline calibrate (B304) in UX.
- **`shouldPan` already reused** ‚Äî `viewportTransform.js`'s `shouldPan` rule was already imported and used in DocReview (B329); no new wiring needed.
- **Vertex drag handles in DocReview (B431).** When a markup is selected in the Select tool, small white circles with accent outline render at each vertex (skipped for pen/highlight which have too many pts). Clicking within 8 screen-px of a grip starts a single-vertex drag (`vtxDragRef`) instead of a full-markup move. `onMove` translates only the grabbed vertex; `onUp` commits via `pushHistory` + `setMarkups`. The `vtxPreview` state drives a live preview during the drag, and passes into the MarkupRenderer call the same way `dragPreview` does.
- **Shift snap (45¬∞) during TWOPOINT drawing.** In `onMove`, when `draft && TWOPOINT.has(tool) && e.shiftKey`, the cursor is snapped to the nearest 45¬∞ interval from the first point before `setCursor`. In `onDown` for the second-point commit, the same snap is applied before `commit`. So Shift held = perfectly horizontal/vertical/diagonal line constraints for Line, Rect, Ellipse, Dimension, and Calibrate tools.
- **Reuse mode + set-as-default already done** (B428 `propStyle` sticky defaults ‚Äî DocReview stays on the same tool after commit, doesn't revert to Pan).
- **Dedup:** convert-segment-to-arc and rotate/resize handles are deferred (roadmap ‚Äî complex, low daily use); the high-value pieces (vtx handles, Shift snap, inline calibrate) ship this session per the standing rule.

### B429 ‚Äî New tools: Arc, Dimension, Pen, Highlight, Eraser, Snapshot `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature) ‚Äî NEW-6, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1390 tests ¬∑ build green. ‚è≥ signed-in draw round-trip ‚Üí V125.**
- **6 new tools** added to DocReview tool rail and wired end-to-end:
  - **Arc** (`multiPoint`): click start ‚Üí click end ‚Üí click a point on the curve; auto-commits on 3rd `onDown`. Renders as a quadratic B√©zier (`Q ctrl`) where ctrl = 2¬∑p3 ‚àí (p1+p2)/2. Live preview during first two points.
  - **Dimension** (`twoPoint`): drag end-to-end; renders line + two perpendicular witness ticks (¬±7 px) + a centred calibrated length label. Added to `MEASURE_KINDS` in MarkupRenderer so it gets the label from `measureLabel`.
  - **Pen** (`freehand`): pointer-capture press-drag; accumulates pts on `onMove`, commits on `onUp` (min 2 pts). Renders as a stroked open polyline.
  - **Highlight** (`freehand`): identical gesture to Pen. `TOOL_DEFAULTS` forces `stroke:#fbbf24`, `strokeWidth:12`, `opacity:0.35` (clamped to ‚â§0.5 in renderer so it stays translucent even if the user overrides).
  - **Eraser** (`region`): drag a rubber-band box; `eraseInBox` removes any `pen`/`highlight` markup whose pts intersect the box ‚Äî never touches other kinds. Hit-test treated as rect-interior so a partly-drawn eraser box shows a live rectangle.
  - **Snapshot** (`region`): drag a box; commits a `snapshot` markup. Renders as a dashed rect + a camera emoji at the centroid.
- **Arrow** = arrowhead toggle on Line (already in `arrowStart`/`arrowEnd` property column via B428 matrix ‚Äî no standalone rail button needed per locked owner decision).
- **Classification sets** (`TWOPOINT`/`MULTIPOINT`/`FREEHAND`/`REGION`) extended to include all new tools; pointer handlers dispatched through them uniformly.
- **`TOOL_DEFAULTS`** map provides per-tool style overrides between `propStyle` (user sticky) and column defaults; currently used for Highlight.
- **`MarkupRenderer.jsx`** extended: arc (quadratic bezier, optional arrowheads), dimension (line + ticks + label), pen (stroked polyline), highlight (wide translucent path), snapshot (dashed rect + emoji).
- **`verify-b429.mjs`** headless smoke: Review tab loads, no JS crash. Tool buttons not visible logged-out (tool rail only shows when a PDF is loaded) ‚Üí ‚è≥ V125.

### B426 ‚Äî Shared `MarkupRenderer.jsx` + `PropertyPanel.jsx`; DocReview left property panel `[Site Planner + Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ‚Äî NEW-2 cont./NEW-3, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24; minted **B426** = B425 + 1)*

PropertyPanel appears at the bottom of the left sheet-list panel when a markup is selected (scrollable 220px area, `Properties / <kind>` header). Old inline `draw()` function removed; SVG layer uses `<MarkupRenderer>` with optional `dragPreview` overlay.

### B434 ‚Äî Map marker: building/shield silhouette ‚Üí precision pin, progress folded into the ground ring `[Site Planner / map]` (feature) ‚Äî amends B161; depends on B433  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; minted **B434**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/stoic-albattani-8bmtp0`). lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1335 tests ¬∑ build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-precision-pin.mjs` 18/18.**
- **What:** replaced the zoomed-out site marker (`buildingPinIcon` ‚Üí renamed **`sitePinIcon`**, `MapFinder.jsx`) with the **precision pin**: a color **bulb** on a short vertical **stalk** seated over a **ground ring** (a survey-monument read). The **ground-ring center is the anchor** ‚Äî it sits exactly on the site coordinate (replaces the old shield bottom-tip anchor). Zoomed-in parcel/plan render unchanged; the tiny left-rail list markers stay simple token discs.
- **Salience gradient realized here:** size tracks importance (Pursuit largest ‚Üí Dead smallest, via the token `tier`), plus each token's `mapOpacity` (settled stages recede). The open-site emphasis is kept (**1.15√ó** + a subtle drop-shadow on that one pin only ‚Äî no per-pin shadows, they flash on re-render).
- **Progress ‚Äî folded in, NOT dropped.** B161's building carried a progress arc (derived from status until a real `progress_pct` lands ‚Äî B163). The shield had silently dropped it (B365); the precision pin **restores it as the ground-ring sweep** 0‚Äì100% from 12 o'clock, **same source** (`STATUS_PROGRESS` = pursuit 10 ¬∑ active 60 ¬∑ onhold 30 ¬∑ complete 100 ¬∑ dead 0). A faint full track keeps the ring readable at 0%.
- **Anchor is fixed for every tier:** the ground-ring center is placed at the viewBox bottom edge so it maps to the hit-box bottom-center (`iconAnchor = [HIT_W/2, HIT_H]`, Leaflet margins `-17/-46`) at every size ‚Äî the art can shrink without the anchor drifting, and the tap target stays generous (fixed 34√ó46 hit box). Verified the ring's lower half overflows symmetrically about the point (`overflow:visible`).
- **Colorblind cue:** the glyph (‚Äñ/‚úì/‚úï) rides inside the bulb for the settled stages; Pursuit/Active are glyphless discs (color + size + sweep carry them). `statusGlyph` dropped the now-dead flag/pulse cases.
- **Verified (V123, `ui-audit/verify-precision-pin.mjs`, 18/18, chromium-1228, 0 page errors):** bulb+stalk+ring structure; anchor = ring center; sweep ordering Complete 31.42 > Active 18.85 > On-hold 9.43 > Pursuit 3.14; size tiers 20.8 > 18.7 > 17.1 > 15 > Dead 13.3; solid bulb + white keyline (never hollow); correct coral/blue/amber/gray fills; **no `#E24B4A`**; Dead (filtered in) = solid gray ‚úï, dimmed (opacity 0.5), smallest. Retired the superseded `ui-audit/verify-b365-markers.mjs`; refreshed the stale `verify-b232-b236.mjs` color constants.
- **Deduped:** amends **B161** (the marker shape it replaces ‚Äî its progress encoding restored, not lost) + depends on **B433** (reads the corrected tokens). NOT B365 (which this supersedes shape-wise).

### B281 ‚Äî CI: Playwright on the deploy + failure auto-files an @claude issue `[Infra / CI]` (feature) ‚Äî the B423 loop's alarm  *(2026-06-23; built the SAME session as B278)*
`[x]` **Built. New workflow `.github/workflows/e2e.yml` mirrors the proven idempotent `gis-drift.yml` pattern.** Runs `npm run e2e` against the deploy (`E2E_BASE_URL` secret; `github.event.inputs.base_url` override for manual runs) on a weekday schedule + `workflow_dispatch`. On failure ‚Üí opens (or BUMPS ‚Äî never duplicates) a single `@claude` issue labeled `e2e-drift` with the report tail (last 5 KB) + the "do NOT edit the matrix to pass" rule; on green ‚Üí auto-closes any open `e2e-drift` issue. Uploads the Playwright HTML report as an artifact (14-day retention). Permissions `contents:read` + `issues:write`. **This is the verifier that drives the autonomous implement‚Üítest‚Üífix‚Üíadvance loop** for the remaining tool rows (B426+). Extends `build.yml` (the required "build" check), doesn't replace it. Gated on B280's secrets ‚Äî without them the auth specs skip, the smoke still runs (a useful signal, not a false alarm). **Dedup:** NET-NEW; first real filing of the long-parked `@claude` e2e workflow.
- **AMENDMENT (2026-07-04):** the CI loop now also exercises the fixture harness ‚Äî the fixture vitest specs run under `npm test`, `e2e/{gantt-density,site-testfit}.spec.js` run under `npm run e2e`, and `build.yml` drift-guards the committed fixtures (`node scripts/build-fixtures.mjs --check`). See B278 amendment.

### B278 ‚Äî Playwright regression harness against the deploy `[Infra / E2E]` (task)  *(2026-06-23; built this session ‚Äî the B423 loop's verifier)*
`[x]` **Built + the logged-out smoke RUNS GREEN locally (2/2, headless Chromium driving the real built app). 106 vitest files / 1376 tests ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green.**

No real client data (seeded PRNG, scrubbed to `E2E ‚Ä¶`). Fixture-drift guard wired into `build.yml`. Branch `claude/backlog-infra-verify-gates-xsdxsi`.

### B425 ‚Äî Shared markup engine: the PURE layer (geometry, model, measure, hit-test, property schema, tool registry) `[Site Planner + Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ‚Äî NEW-2, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-23; minted **B425** = B424 + 1)*
`[x]` **Built + unit-tested the SAME session. lint 0 ¬∑ 106 test files / 1376 tests ¬∑ build green. No behavior change yet ‚Äî this is the shared foundation the host wiring (B426+) builds on; the existing ~101 suites stay green as the regression guard.**
- **New shared module `src/shared/markup/` (mirrors the purity discipline of `shared/viewport/viewportTransform.js` ‚Äî pure math/policy, no React/DOM):**
  - `geometry.js` ‚Äî point primitives lifted verbatim from `takeoff.js` (`dist`, `pathLength`, `polyArea`, `centroidOf`, `midOfPath`, `pointInPoly`) + the Site Planner drawing helpers (`rot2`, `snap45`, `projToSeg`) + `bboxOf`. Unit-agnostic (the ft/page-unit seam lives in `measure.js`).
  - `markupModel.js` ‚Äî reconciles the TWO host storage forms so shared code reads any markup: Document Review's flat `pts`, the Site Planner's `{a,b}` line AND its centre-box `{cx,cy,w,h,rot}` (‚Üí `boxCorners`). `ptsOf`/`setPts`/`translate`/`bboxOfMarkup`/`minPtsOf`/`isClosed` + `sanitizeMarkup`/`sanitizeMarkups` (moved here from `takeoff.js`).
  - `measure.js` ‚Äî the RELOCATED measure engine. `measureValue`/`measureLabel`/`MIN_MEASURE_PTS`/`canCommitMeasure`; the **unit-scale seam** is the `ftPerUnit` arg (Site passes 1 / feet-native, Doc passes its per-page calibration, Stitcher its set calibration). `rollup` GENERALIZED to accept either the legacy `calByPage` object OR a `(m)=>ftPerUnit` function ‚Äî back-compatible. Adds the `polylength` (open polyline) measure kind.
  - `hitTest.js` ‚Äî **completes the B155 shared tranche** (no `hitTest` existed before; hit-testing was 3 inline ways). `pickMarkup`/`pickMarkupIndex` (top-most wins; handle ‚Üí edge ‚Üí filled interior; filter for locked) + `hitEditPath` (vertex 9 px beats edge 11 px ‚Äî the Bluebeam rule from `SitePlanner.jsx:2462`). Tolerances are screen px √∑ `view.scale` (equal grab at any zoom).
  - `propertySchema.js` ‚Äî `schemaForMarkup` derives the panel's control list FROM the matrix (panel can't drift from spec) + reconciles canonical keys ‚áÑ the Site Planner's legacy field names (`strokeWidth`‚Üî`weight`, `strokeStyle`‚Üî`dash`, `fontColor`‚Üî`color`) via `readProp`/`writeProp`, so neither host renames stored data.
  - `toolRegistry.js` ‚Äî the one sanctioned divergence: `ARM_POLICY = {site:"revert", doc:"reuse", stitch:"reuse"}` + `nextToolAfterCommit`; `SITE_TOOL_ALIAS` maps `mline`/`mrect`/‚Ä¶ ‚áÑ canonical ids.
- **`doc-review/lib/takeoff.js` is now a thin re-export shim** ‚Üí every historical import (`DocReview.jsx`, `Stitcher.jsx`, 4 test files) resolves unchanged from the shared engine; all 50 takeoff/stress/rollup tests pass against the relocation.
- **New tests (5 files):** `markupModel` (both host forms), `markupHitTest` (per-kind rules + top-most + vertex-beats-edge, seeded from both hosts), `propertySchema` (matrix-consistency + key‚Üîfield), `toolRegistry` (arm-policy + alias), plus the relocated measure coverage. **Dedup:** net-new shared module; completes B155's unbuilt shared tranche.

### B424 ‚Äî The markup tool√óproperty MATRIX as committed data (`tools.matrix.js`) `[Site Planner + Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ‚Äî NEW-1, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-23; minted **B424** = B423 + 1)*
`[x]` **Built + conformance-tested the SAME session. The single source of truth the engine's property panel is DRIVEN by and the NEW-9 tool tests are GENERATED from.**
- `src/shared/markup/tools.matrix.js`: `TOOL_MATRIX` (one row per tool ‚Äî `id`, `label`, `hint`, `category`, `drawMode`, `closed`, `measureOutput?`, `properties[]`, `workspaces[]`), `PROPERTY_COLUMNS` (the control universe with type/label/default/range), `DRAW_MODES`/`CATEGORIES`/`WORKSPACES` vocabularies, + pure accessors (`toolById`, `propsForTool`, `toolsForWorkspace`, `measureTools`, `isClosedTool`, `columnMeta`). Encodes the Bluebeam-parity TARGET (a tool a workspace doesn't yet implement still has its row, so the loop has a goal). Arrow is a Line arrowhead toggle, not a row (owner decision).
- **‚õî The refinement loop NEVER edits this file to make a red test go green** ‚Äî the matrix is the spec; a failing assertion means the CODE is behind it. The matrix changes only on explicit owner direction. (Pinned in the file header.)
- `test/toolMatrix.test.js` (14 tests): every row well-formed; ids unique; every category/drawMode/workspace/property token in its declared vocabulary; closed shapes pairing fill+fillOpacity; arrowheads only on open shapes; measures declare a known output; accessors round-trip. **Dedup:** net-new (no prior matrix/spec item).

### B421 ‚Äî Markup z-order ("Arrange"): Bring to Front / Forward / Send Backward / to Back via Bluebeam shortcuts + a right-click menu `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B421** = highest B# across both files (B420) + 1; deduped net-new)*
`[x]` **Filed AND built + verified + shipped to main the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/gallant-turing-mcempv`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1335 tests ¬∑ build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-b421-arrange.mjs` 17/17 (V122).**
- **What:** when two markups overlap on a sheet, control which draws on top ‚Äî Bluebeam's four Arrange ops on the selected markup (Bring to Front, Bring Forward, Send Backward, Send to Back), reachable two ways from ONE shared core: keyboard chords and a new right-click context menu. Net-new ‚Äî there was no z-order control before.
- **Core (one engine, both entry points):** new pure module `src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/arrange.js`. `reorderWithinPage(markups, id, mode)` permutes the selected markup within its **same-page** siblings (draw order IS z-order ‚Äî the overlay paints `pageMarks` in array order, so the last same-page entry is on top), then splices the reordered group back into the global array **without disturbing other sheets' markups** (they keep their absolute slots, so an arrange never silently shuffles another sheet's stack). Returns the SAME array reference on a no-op (already at that end / a lone markup / unknown mode) so a top/bottom arrange skips the history push + state write. `arrangeFlags(markups, id)` reports `{page, count, index, atTop, atBottom}` for the greying. `DocReview.arrange(mode)` wraps it with `pushHistory()` so every arrange is one undoable step (B303) and persists for free (markups are already saved/mirrored).
- **Keyboard:** the existing `onKeyRef` handler gains an arrange branch **before** the `if (mod) return` early-out (which previously handed every other modified key back to the browser, so a branch after it would never fire). Gated on `mod && sel`; matches **`e.code`** (`BracketRight`/`BracketLeft`), NOT `e.key` ‚Äî Shift turns `e.key` `]`‚Üí`}`, which would silently miss the Bring-to-Front chord. ‚åò works via the existing `e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey`; the INPUT/TEXTAREA guard already blocks firing mid-text-edit. `]`=up / `[`=down, Shift=jump to the end: `‚åò/Ctrl+]` Forward ¬∑ `‚åò/Ctrl+Shift+]` Front ¬∑ `‚åò/Ctrl+[` Backward ¬∑ `‚åò/Ctrl+Shift+[` Back.
- **Right-click menu:** new `onContextMenu` on the viewport runs the existing `hitTest` (B33) at the cursor ‚Äî a hit selects that markup and opens the menu at the cursor; a **miss** does nothing (no `preventDefault`, so the native browser menu shows over blank canvas ‚Äî never an empty custom menu). The menu is **portalled to `<body>`** (`createPortal`, the same escape-the-clip pattern as `AnchoredMenu`/the Site-row context menu) so the canvas's `overflow:hidden` + stacking can't clip it; cursor-positioned + viewport-clamped; dismissed on click-away / right-click-away / **Escape**. Items: the four Arrange ops (with platform-aware shortcut hints), a divider, then **Edit text‚Ä¶** (text notes only, reuses the B293 inline editor) and **Delete** (reuses the existing `pushHistory` + filter-by-id). Arrange items **grey out** at the top (Front/Forward) / bottom (Back/Backward) of the stack; a lone markup disables all four. Not opened while an inline editor / calibrate entry is active.
- **Scope / parity:** built in `DocReview.jsx` (the single-sheet markup surface). **Skipped `Stitcher.jsx` deliberately** ‚Äî it renders only `measures` (distance/area), with no overlapping fillable markups (rect/cloud/text), so an Arrange menu there would be empty/no-op. Toolbar buttons for Arrange are out of scope (shortcuts + right-click only) ‚Äî an easy follow-up.
- **Tests:** `test/arrange.test.js` ‚Äî **14 unit tests** (all four ops; single-step Forward/Backward are inverses; SAME-reference no-ops at the ends / lone markup / unknown id / unknown mode; other-sheet slots preserved through an interleaved-page reorder; input not mutated). Headless **V122** (`ui-audit/verify-b421-arrange.mjs`, **17/17**): three overlapping rects, every chord + every menu item reorders correctly verified BOTH via the DOM order AND the persisted localStorage draft, each undoable, greyed at top/bottom, blank-canvas right-click shows no menu, Edit-text only on a text note, Delete works, and the order **survives a full reload**. 0 page errors.
- **Deduped:** net-new ‚Äî NOT B397 (Scheduler-Gantt z-order, a different surface), NOT the CSS stacking-context fixes (B66 et al.), NOT B33/B374 (markup hit-testing, which this REUSES). NEW-2 from the same brief (named markup **Layers**) is filed as **B422** ‚Äî a ROADMAP STUB (design pass required before build), left Open, NOT built.

### B420 ‚Äî Parcel grabs only by its boundary/setback, not its interior (free the interior for building work) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1", live; minted **B420** = highest real B# across both files (B419) + 1 ‚Äî B416‚ÄìB419 were taken by concurrent PRs #313/#314/#316 while this was in flight; branch/commits/code tags first read B417, renamed on the merge-in of main)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/vigilant-cerf-6rvxga`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1320 tests ¬∑ build green; new regression harness 7/7.**
- **Repro (owner, live):** in Select mode, clicking anywhere inside a lot's interior selected the parcel and popped the parcel menu ‚Äî even when you were trying to edit a building/element sitting in that lot; a press in the open interior near (but not on) a footprint grabbed the parcel. **Expected:** a parcel is grabbable only by its boundary edge or its setback line; the interior is click-through (presses fall to whatever element is painted on top, else the background pan ‚Äî exactly as on empty canvas). Appearance unchanged; hit-area change only.
- **Root cause (code-confirmed):** the visible parcel `<polygon>` (`SitePlanner.jsx`, parcels render block) carried `pointerEvents="all"`, so the whole fill was a hit target even when `fill:"none"`; both `startMoveParcel` and `onContextMenu` ‚Üí `setParcelMenu` fired from any interior point.
- **Fix ‚Äî boundary-only hit model:** the visible polygon is now `pointerEvents="none"` (its fill never captures, even when a translucent fill is toggled on ‚Äî boundary-grab stays consistent regardless of fill state; deliberately NOT `"painted"`, which would re-arm the interior whenever a fill is set). A companion transparent **fat hit-stroke** on the same ring (`stroke="rgba(0,0,0,0.001)"`, `strokeWidth={12}`, `pointerEvents="stroke"`) carries `startMoveParcel` + the context menu ‚Äî reusing the B146 easement-edge fat-stroke technique. ~12 px is **zoom-independent** because `f2p` (`worldToScreen`) already projects feet‚Üíscreen pixels, so the polygon is drawn in pixel space (the existing easement-edge picker uses the same flat `12`; no division by zoom needed). The **setback outline** gets the same fat hit-stroke for active parcels (owner explicitly wanted "boundary OR setback"); a shared `onParcelContext(e,id)` opens the parcel menu from either grab target.
- **Preserved (re-verified, no regression):** B310 click-vs-drag + B311 "Select parcels" toggle (both keyed on `startMoveParcel`, which now fires from the boundary instead of the fill), Shift-click-to-merge (`toggleMerge`), align-edge picking (`alignToParcelEdge`), unlocked-parcel drag-to-move, and B230 shared vertex editing (it hit-tests at the svg root via `editablePath`/`hitEditPath`, independent of the fill, so vertex handles + insert/delete are untouched). The B311 tooltip was reworded "click a lot" ‚Üí "click a lot's edge or setback line" (label only, no logic change).
- **Distinct from B155/B156 (the inverse, not a contradiction):** those make small markup-shape *interiors* grabbable (correct for annotations). A parcel is a *container*, so boundary-grab is the right inverse. **Layers on B310/B311**, does not replace them.
- **Verified (V121):** new `ui-audit/verify-b420-parcel-boundary-grab.mjs` **7/7** ‚Äî interior click does NOT select, boundary click selects, setback-line click selects, right-click parity (interior opens no menu, boundary opens it), no page JS errors. Repointed `verify-b310-b311` / `verify-edge-runs` / `verify-edge-runs-irregular` off the now-dead interior-click / `pointer-events="all"` selectors ‚Üí all green; `verify-b221-b222` (a pond element on a parcel still selects + its vertices insert/delete 4‚Üí5‚Üí4‚Üí3), `verify-parcel-split-control`, `verify-b383-add-parcel`, `verify-parcel-resilience` all still pass.
- **Deduped:** net-new. NOT B155/B156 (markup interiors ‚Äî the inverse), NOT B310/B311 (gesture/toggle ‚Äî preserved), NOT B146 (reuses its technique), NOT B213 (inactive parcels already draw no setback line).

### B419 ‚Äî Rename the module accent token `markup` ‚Üí `review` (names only, values locked) `[Doc Review / theming]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-3"; minted **B419**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1; shipped to main this session (branch `claude/gifted-shannon-ycachr`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1282 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless `ui-audit/verify-b417-b419.mjs` 11/11.**

- Verified zero orphaned `accent-markup`/`accentMarkup` refs in `src`/`ui-audit`; runtime probe confirms `--accent-review` ‚Üí #EF9F27 and `--accent-markup` ‚Üí "" (gone). **Dedup:** net-new (a pure rename of the B316‚ÄìB319/B341 tokens, no value change).

### B418 ‚Äî Module user-facing label ‚Üí "Review" + pin the naming in CLAUDE.md `[Doc Review]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2"; minted **B418**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1; shipped to main this session (branch `claude/gifted-shannon-ycachr`). headless 11/11.**

So the only "Library" string left WAS the module tab; renaming it "Library"‚Üí"Review" RESOLVES the tab-vs-Files-drawer collision B404 introduced. Follow-on to B404 (done), not a dup.
