### B417 ‚Äî Paste lands at the cursor in BOTH the Site Planner and Review canvases `[Site Planner + Doc Review]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B417**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1; shipped to main this session (branch `claude/gifted-shannon-ycachr`). lint 0 ¬∑ 1282 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ headless 11/11. One live copy‚Üípaste click-through logged in VERIFICATION.md V120.**
- A pasted copy drops **centered under the mouse** (Bluebeam). **Site Planner** (`SitePlanner.jsx`): a `lastPtrFt` ref records the live cursor in WORLD feet on every canvas move (ref-only, no setState in the hot path); `pasteClip()` anchors there via the existing `snapPt` (grid/snap toggle + held-Alt bypass), setting a rect's `cx/cy` and translating a polygon so its bbox center sits under the cursor; a null/non-finite ref falls back to the old fixed offset (never a no-op). Ctrl/‚åò+D duplicate keeps its fixed offset (duplicate ‚âÝ paste).
- **Review** (`DocReview.jsx`): net-new copy/cut/paste (there was none) ‚Äî a component `clip` ref + `copyMarkup`/`cutMarkup`/`pasteMarkup` wired into `onKeyRef` (‚åò/Ctrl+C/X/V; only `preventDefault` when they act, else native). Paste clones with a fresh `uid()`, sets `page` to the current sheet, centers the markup's bbox on the live `cursor` (text = its single point), with a fixed page-unit fallback. Markups stay on the editable overlay ‚Äî the immutable PDF backdrop is untouched.
- Centering math is a shared, unit-tested helper `src/shared/geometry/pasteGeom.js` (`centerOn`/`bboxCenter`, 9 tests) so both canvases agree and it can't silently regress. Scope: single-element clipboard; repeated Ctrl+V restamps at the cursor. **Verified:** placement math unit-tested; headless boots the planner + confirms the Ctrl+V wiring no-ops on an empty clipboard. The interactive copy‚Üípaste click-through pends a real-browser pass (selecting a canvas element via *synthetic* pointer events isn't reliable headless) ‚Äî V120. **Dedup:** net-new (builds on, not a dup of, the fixed-offset `pasteClip`/`duplicateEl`).

### B416 ‚Äî "Split a parcel" control unreachable where users now look ‚Äî surface it in the Parcel panel `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1", live; minted **B416** = highest B# across both files (B415) + 1)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/affectionate-wright-gf3kgt`). 1273 tests ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green; new regression harness 11/11.**
- **Repro (owner, live):** "no way to reach the Split tool ‚Äî the 'Split a parcel' control is not shown on screen."
- **Grounded the report against the live code FIRST (the brief's own instruction ‚Äî find why it stopped rendering before touching downstream).** The geometry path (`performSplit` ‚Üí `lib/polygonSplit.js`) is intact (B128, unit-tested) and so is the rail control: the right-rail **Boundary ‚ñæ** flyout still renders **Draw new parcel + Split a parcel** (unchanged since 2026-06-21 per `git blame`; headless **scenario A passed on the unfixed build** ‚Äî the rail item is present, opens, and arms split). So this was **NOT** a deleted/ungated control ‚Äî none of the three suspected failure shapes (button gone / menu won't open / item removed) held.
- **Real cause ‚Äî a discoverability regression from the B383 reorg.** B383 surfaced parcel operations (**Ôºã Add parcel**, and **Merge parcels** already lived there) into the left-hand **Parcel panel**, which is now where a user reaches for parcel actions. **Split is the inverse of Merge but was left only in the rail menu** ‚Äî so a user looking in the panel finds Add + Merge but no Split, and reads it as "Split is gone." The brief anticipated exactly this: *"if the entry points were reorganized, Split must get a deliberate home in that same reorg, not be left orphaned."*
- **Fix ‚Äî surface "‚úÇ Split a parcel" in the Parcel panel, beside Merge** (`SitePlanner.jsx`, the `leftPanel === "parcel"` Section): a full-width chip shown whenever `parcels.length >= 1` (you need a parcel to split; Merge stays `> 1`), active-styled when `tool === "split"`, calling the SAME `selectTool("split")` as the rail ‚Äî no new pipeline, no second split path. The rail Boundary ‚ñæ ‚Üí Split is kept unchanged (the drafting-tools home); this adds the panel home, it doesn't move it. Helper line mirrors the tool hint (click points ‚Üí double-click/Enter ‚Üí divides in two).
- **Smoke-tested the full path live, per the brief** (it could have been hidden because half-broken ‚Äî it wasn't): activate ‚Üí point capture (`snapSplit`) ‚Üí finish (`finishActiveDrawing` on double-click/Enter) ‚Üí commit (`performSplit`) splits one parcel into two end-to-end.
- **Regression guard added (the brief's explicit ask ‚Äî a "control silently disappears" / silence-is-a-crash class can't recur uncaught):** `ui-audit/verify-parcel-split-control.mjs` asserts Split is reachable from BOTH the rail AND the panel, each arms the tool, and the end-to-end cut splits 1‚Üí2. Hardened so a missing control reports a clean fail instead of crashing the run. **11/11.**
- **Deduped:** net-new. NOT B128 (concave-cut split *geometry* ‚Äî a different, intact concern), NOT B96 (the shared Enter/double-click finisher), NOT B130's "Boundary" rename (which *created* the rail menu). No existing Open/Done item covered Split's UI reachability.

### B414 ‚Äî Doc Review (Library) sheet flashes white on zoom-settle ‚Äî double-buffer the re-raster `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; first filed as B412, but a concurrent `main` (PR #311) took B412/B413, so **renumbered B414** = the next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/confident-edison-lpbco9`). 1273 tests ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green; headless-verified.**
- **Repro:** open a sheet in the Library/Markup canvas, zoom in/out; ~140 ms after the gesture settled the sheet flashed blank/white before the sharp render appeared (Bluebeam never flashes on the same file).
- **Root cause (code-confirmed):** the single visible canvas was re-rastered on every zoom settle; reassigning `canvas.width/height` clears it to transparent, revealing the white page-box `<div>` (`background:"#fff"`) behind it, and the `page.render()` that refills it is async (pdf.js worker) ‚Üí a visible blank gap each settle.
- **Fix:** `pdf.js` `renderInto` now rasterises into an OFF-SCREEN canvas and blits the finished frame onto the visible canvas (`drawImage`) in one synchronous step ‚Äî the compositor never sees the cleared canvas. It bails on `isStale()` so a superseded render never blits a stale frame (B40 guard kept; off-screen also sidesteps the same-canvas throw). The biggest contributor in practice is B415's backdrop (the whole-page layer no longer re-rasters on zoom at all), but the double-buffer makes EVERY swap (page change + the detail settle) flash-free.
- **Verified:** `ui-audit/verify-b414-b415-render.mjs` D1 (backdrop backing dims unchanged across a zoom ‚Üí can't blank) + D3 (detail layer polled through a settle, never caught blank ‚Äî min opaque centre pixels > 0). Foundation for B415.

### B415 ‚Äî Doc Review (Library) render is softer than Bluebeam on large sheets ‚Äî viewport-clipped two-layer rendering `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2"; first filed as B413, renumbered past main's B412/B413 (PR #311) to **B415**; depends on B414)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/confident-edison-lpbco9`). 1273 tests ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green; headless-verified.** Net-new architectural item that **supersedes the whole-page raster path B247/B265/B327/B329** (extends that path, does not fork a second renderer).
- **Repro:** Planyr's linework/text was visibly softer than Bluebeam side-by-side, most pronounced on large (E-size) civil sheets and when zoomed in.
- **Root cause:** the viewer rasterised the ENTIRE page into one canvas at a single density; the ~24 MP backing-store budget (`renderBudget.backingScale`, B327/B247) was spent across the whole sheet, so on a big sheet density dropped below full (even <1√ó) everywhere to stay under budget ‚Äî even though only a small window is visible. Bluebeam rasters only the visible region at full resolution.
- **Fix (two-layer, additive over B414; the page-box + SVG-overlay CSS model is byte-for-byte unchanged, so markup/zoom-anchor geometry is untouched):**
  - **Backdrop** ‚Äî the whole page at a FIXED, zoom-independent density (`backdropDensity`, ~8 MP budget) painted once per page, filling the page box. It never re-rasters on zoom (so it can't flash white) and is always present as a no-white floor.
  - **Detail** ‚Äî only the visible page-rect (+ a 25% margin) at full device dpr (cap 2), budget-bounded on the REGION not the page (reuses `backingScale` on the region size), re-rastered on pan/zoom settle (double-buffered via B414) and positioned over the backdrop. Because the detail backing store is ~viewport-sized, the budget stops forcing low density ‚Üí on-screen resolution stays native at any sheet size/zoom.
  - Pure region/density math (`visibleRegion`, `tileCovers`, `backdropDensity`, `BACKDROP_PX_BUDGET`) in `renderBudget.js` (+13 unit tests in `test/renderBudget.test.js`); region render path (`renderInto` with a `transform` offset + canvas-clip) in `pdf.js`; two-canvas viewport + settle-driven `renderBackdrop`/`renderDetail` in `DocReview.jsx`. The B40 race guard + B39 destroy are kept; the imported drawing stays a display-only backdrop.
- **Verified (`ui-audit/verify-b414-b415-render.mjs`, 11/11, E-size sheet @ deviceScaleFactor 2):** zoomed to 2.6√ó, the **detail layer renders at 2.00√ó native device density** while the OLD whole-page raster would be **0.95√ó at the same zoom ‚Üí 2.11√ó sharper**; backdrop fills the page box and stays opaque through the zoom; markup overlay still draws. Regression: the B329 viewport harness (`verify-markup-viewport.mjs`) still 13/13 (pan / cursor-anchored zoom / calibration survival / measurement geometry / sheet-switch), plus `verify-docreview-viewer` 13/13 and `verify-delete-markup` 10/10.

### B412 ‚Äî Title-block reader: read bare sheet codes + stop mislabeling civil sheets (MATCH LINE / project banner / "C-14 SHEET NUMBER") `[Doc Review / reader]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-23 with a screenshot of wrong Markup labels + a real GPL topo upload; minted **B412** = highest B# (B411) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE this session on branch `claude/planner-drive-file-testing-r0vh2a` ‚Äî 1264 tests ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green; verified on the owner's real GPL topo slice + 7 new unit tests.** Fixes the shared positional reader `src/shared/files/sheetMeta.js`, so both the Markup sidebar AND the Stitcher benefit.
- **Bare sheet-code read (the topo unblock).** A scanned/reference sheet prints its number as a big standalone "C-2" with the "SHEET NO." label drawn as graphics, not text ‚Äî the label-anchored `parseSheetNumber` read it as "". New `prominentSheetCode` in `readSheetNumberInZone`: when no labeled number is found in the title-block zone, take the TALLEST lone sheet-code-shaped token that carries a real discipline letter-prefix (`disciplineFromSheetNumber`), so "C-2" is read but the set page-count "46" (no prefix) is not. On the owner's file C-2/C-3/C-4 now read (confidence 1.0) ‚Üí the three topo pages group as one **"Topographic Survey ¬∑ C-2‚ÄìC-4 ¬∑ 3 sheets"** set (was 3 separate singles).
- **Title scoped to the title-block zone (the GPL civil mislabels).** `readSheetTitle` now restricts candidates to the band (or, with no band, the right/bottom edge strip ‚Äî the same discipline the number read uses) instead of scanning the whole page; so a large drawing-area "MATCH LINE ‚Ä¶" annotation and the big "GRAND PORT LOGISTICS" project banner can no longer outscore the real title. Falls fully open only for older callers with neither band nor dims.
- **Reject match-line + field-label rows.** `isTitleish` drops any "MATCH LINE/for continuation" line and any title-block FIELD-label row (new `looksLikeFieldRow`, matched ANYWHERE in the line, not just at `^`) ‚Äî so a merged "C-14 SHEET NUMBER" is rejected and the real title wins with the number read separately.
- +7 unit tests (`test/sheetMeta.test.js`): bare-code read vs page-count, NTS scale, the 3-sheet grouping, MATCH-LINE/banner/field-row title rejection. **Remaining stitch gap ‚Üí B413** (these sheets carry no match-line text/scale, so AUTO-alignment is data-limited ‚Äî its own item).

### B410 ‚Äî Auto-filing: per-page discipline + multi-discipline split into separate PDFs `[Doc Review / auto-filing]` (feature)  *(owner-requested 2026-06-23: "test all the Drive files for sheet-title/file-name recognition, and if a drawing has multiple disciplines, split it up properly"; minted **B410** = highest B# (B409) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE this session ‚Äî engine + read path + byte-split actuation all shipped on branch `claude/planner-drive-file-testing-r0vh2a`. 1257 tests ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green; validated against 7 real Drive PDFs + a real end-to-end byte carve.** Owner picked "split into separate PDFs" (AskUserQuestion, 2026-06-23), which is built.
- **Problem (found by testing real Drive files):** the filing read looked at only the first 2 pages and stamped ONE discipline on the whole file ‚Üí real misfiles: a Mesa Mechanical set filed Architectural (cover named the architect); a Jacintoport make-ready filed Fire Sprinkler off its cover. And a combined set (Architectural with a bound-in Structural set) filed as one discipline.
- **Engine ‚Äî `src/shared/files/disciplineSplit.js` (pure, 13 tests):** PREFIX-FIRST per-page discipline (a sheet code's letter prefix, e.g. `A5.00`‚ÜíArchitectural / `M-1`‚ÜíMechanical, is the engineer's own filing code ‚Üí overrides a keyword tally that stray cross-refs skew ‚Äî fixed the real `A5.00`-read-as-Structural case) + STICKY smoothing (covers/notes/unreadable pages join their block; a lone codeless misread snaps to two agreeing neighbours) ‚Üí discipline segments + merged sets; a set is standalone only with ‚â•2 pages OR its own code (no junk 1-page filings). `dominant` = majority vote (the single-file answer, fixes the first-2-pages misfile). `buildFilingPlan` makes a COMPLETE page partition (orphan/cover pages ride with the dominant ‚Äî no page lost).
- **`titleBlockParse.disciplineFromSheetNumber()`** exported as the single prefix‚Üídiscipline source (classifyDiscipline's fallback reuses it).
- **Read path ‚Äî `doc-review/lib/pdf.js` `extractAllPagesText` + `localRead.js`:** reads EVERY page's text, classifies each, returns majority `discipline` + `multiDiscipline` + per-discipline `sets` (page ranges) + `numPages` on the filing decision.
- **Actuation ‚Äî `doc-review/lib/pdfSplit.js` (pdf-lib, lazy-loaded; 6 tests):** `splitPdfByPlan` carves the source into one clean PDF per discipline ("Bergstrom Phase 2a - Structural.pdf"), each filed in its own folder via `FileBrowser`/`ProjectFilesDrawer` (both drawers); single-discipline files file as before; a split failure falls back to single-file filing (never worse).
- **Real-file validation:** make-ready Fire Sprinkler‚ÜíArchitectural; Mesa Mechanical Architectural‚ÜíMechanical; **Bergstrom Arch IFP byte-split into Architectural (9p) + Structural (pages 5‚Äì8) as two PDFs**; surveys/fire unchanged. Residual gaps tracked in **B411**.

### B407 ‚Äî Record the Supabase org/project naming convention in CLAUDE.md `[Infra]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B407** ‚Äî B405 was the max when filed, but a concurrent `main` (#305/#306) took B406 for Shared team workspaces, so B407 is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND done the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/blissful-hopper-zcijkh`).** Doc-only ‚Äî no code paths touched. The owner had already applied the Supabase-side rename himself; this item is purely recording the agreed scheme so future env work references the right project and the "Planar vs Planyr" confusion is logged as resolved.
- **Convention recorded in CLAUDE.md's "## Supabase" section** (right beside the existing build-time-env note): **org = `Planyr`**; **live/production project = `planyr-production`** (AWS `us-east-1`, the old "Site Planar" dashboard label, serving planyr.io); **`planyr-staging` reserved** for a future second environment. Rationale captured: one app ‚áí the org carries the brand and projects are named by environment (lifecycle stage), not by feature ‚Äî readable as the commercial/VC direction adds a staging env, and no redundant "Planyr / Planyr" nesting.
- **Safety fact captured (the whole point of the doc):** renaming a Supabase org/project **display label is cosmetic** ‚Äî it does NOT change the project ref, the API URL (`VITE_SUPABASE_URL`), or the anon key, so **no rebuild** and cloud features don't break. The client binds to the **immutable 20-char project ref** in the URL, never the display name; only a *different* project (new ref) or rotated keys would change the build-time env.
- **Two-project reality captured (corrected mid-session after a code check ‚Äî prevents a data-loss footgun):** the suite spans TWO live Supabase projects ‚Äî the main app (ref `lyeqzkuiwngunutlkkmi`, via Cloudflare `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`) AND the **Scheduler** (`/sequence/`), which **hardcodes a DIFFERENT ref `ksetjztkplttbcehyicv`** in `public/sequence/index.html` (tables `planar_data`/`planar_history`/`planar_suggestions`). So the us-west-2 "Planar" project is most likely the Scheduler's LIVE backend, not a spare. The doc now says: match each dashboard project's ref against BOTH live refs before any rename/delete; rename live projects for what they are (the Scheduler's is NOT `planyr-staging`), delete only a project matching neither ref. Plus a noted open question: whether to consolidate the Scheduler onto the main project. Renaming stays cosmetic; deleting a live project is irreversible.
- **README reconciled:** the README "Deploy secrets" note references Supabase only via "the anon key" (no project name), so nothing there needed changing. No build/test impact (Markdown only).

### B207 ‚Äî Google Drive storage backend behind the adapter ‚Äî LIVE & DEPLOYED `[Server]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B204, renumbered **B207** ‚Äî `main` took B203‚ÄìB205; closed out 2026-06-23)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî code complete + unit-tested AND provisioned + deployed live in production.** Drive is the active storage backend on planyr.io: store/fetch/list/move/rename **bytes only** in a Workspace Drive, the substrate beneath auto-filing (which writes through it; the queryable index records stay in Supabase Postgres). Moving storage to Drive **removes the Supabase 50 MB-per-file ceiling** on the happy path.
- **Code (built across sessions; a prior session advanced it past the original "scaffold", confirmed by a 2026-06-23 ground-truth read ‚Äî NOT a rebuild):** the thin Drive REST `client` (`server/storage/backends/driveClient.js` ‚Äî create/get/list/move/rename/delete + share-link on `drive.file`; OAuth refresh‚Üíaccess-token via `server/oauth/googleAuth.js`); the Planyr-key‚ÜîDrive-id map persisted on **Supabase Postgres** (`server/storage/idStoreSupabase.js` + migration `server/storage/db/drive_files.sql`, own-row RLS) so Cloud Run / Function scale-to-zero can't wipe it; the same-origin **`POST/GET/DELETE /api/files`** handler (`functions/api/files.js`) that validates the Supabase Bearer JWT server-side (`server/auth/supabaseAuth.js`) before any Drive op and honors `X-Planyr-Key/-Folder/-Name`; the one-time OAuth consent flow (`functions/api/auth/google/{start,callback}.js`); a guarded Drive round-trip smoke test (`functions/api/drive/selftest.js`). Tests: `test/driveClient.test.js`, `test/idStoreSupabase.test.js` (incl. bind‚Üíresolve persistence end-to-end), `test/storageAdapter.test.js` (backend swap + 404/not-connected degrade).
- **Item (4) "Cloud Run + proxy" met more simply:** Drive byte-I/O runs IN the Cloudflare Pages Function (`functions/api/files.js`) ‚Äî same-origin, no CORS, the sanctioned thin-Pages-Function pattern (same as the OAuth callback) ‚Äî instead of a separate Cloud Run hop. The frontend keeps its same-origin `/api/files` (`reviewStore.storeSource` Drive-first/Supabase-fallback, `downloadFromDrive`/`deleteFromDrive`); a 404/503 degrades to the Supabase fallback (no regression).
- **Provisioned + deployed (owner-verified 2026-06-22, Cloudflare Pages "planyr" ‚Üí Production):** `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` (plaintext, existing OAuth client `263720424027-‚Ä¶`), `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` (secret), `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN` (secret), `PLANYR_STORAGE_BACKEND=drive`, `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (un-prefixed server-side auth pair; the `VITE_`-prefixed frontend copies are also present). All carried by the green Production deploy **912de2b** ("Library tab rename"). The temporary `PLANYR_SELFTEST_TOKEN` gate was **removed** after use (correct ‚Äî it's a world-triggerable Drive writer otherwise). Decisions held: OAuth **Internal** (skips Google verification + the 7-day refresh-token expiry), scope **`drive.file`** (least privilege), all creds **server-side only** (never a `VITE_` var, same isolation as the APS key).
- **‚õî Operational guardrail ‚Äî DO NOT re-run the OAuth setup or re-mint `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN`.** A fresh consent mints a NEW refresh token that no longer matches the deployed secret and would take Drive filing offline. The setup is complete; treat the deployed token as load-bearing.
- **Only optional follow-up:** a signed-in end-to-end re-confirmation on planyr.io (drag a fresh PDF into Project Files ‚Üí expect no "Drive copy failed" + a new `drive_files` row). This already passed (2026-06-20 notes); re-run only for a post-deploy re-confirmation. Owner/cohort signed-in test (not a logged-out Cowork self-test).
- **Follow-up (separate, still open):** route the adoption targets (`overlayStorage.js`, `reviewStore.uploadSource/downloadSource`) through the adapter now that live creds are verified end-to-end ‚Äî a deliberate later pass, not part of standing up the backend.

### B405 ‚Äî Files browser: opening a file failed with one conflated/silent banner ‚Äî split into a precise taxonomy `[Document Review]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2"; minted **B405** ‚Äî renumbered from a provisional B402 that a concurrent `main` (#297) took for a Scheduler item; folds in the standalone "oversize banner copy" item)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + unit-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/brave-brown-jb1n2l`).**
- **Grounded against the real repo FIRST (the brief was written blind).** The corrected open path is `FileBrowser.open(f)` ‚Üí `onOpenReview(row)` ‚Üí `openReview` ‚Üí `loadReview(row.id)` (re-fetches the FULL Postgres record by id) ‚Üí `loadSingleReview` ‚Üí `fetchSourceBytes`. So the brief's "the Files-browser reshape drops storageKey/driveKey" hypothesis does **NOT** hold at the open hop ‚Äî the lightweight browser row is never the `src`; the full record (with `data.sources[]` carrying the keys) is re-fetched. Confirmed and flagged rather than rebuilding the (working) upload path.
- **Real defect = the read failure was ONE bucket, and one case was SILENT.** `fetchSourceBytes` returned silently on a missing source (`if (!src) return` ‚Üí a blank canvas, no banner) and collapsed every other cause into a single `"Couldn't fetch ‚Ä¶"`, so oversize / never-stored / stored-but-unreachable / signed-out all read identically (and the oversize copy said "was too large to store in the cloud", which would go stale once B207's Drive cutover lifts the cap).
- **Fix ‚Äî one taxonomy, four hard-distinct states** (`src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/sourceState.js`, new ¬∑ pure ¬∑ **15 unit tests** `test/sourceState.test.js`): `classifySource` + `sourceUnavailableMessage` + `fileWarn`. States: **oversize** (names the 50 MB per-file cloud limit ‚Äî durable, never "coming soon") ¬∑ **not-stored** (a source record with no key ‚Üí re-open to UPLOAD; this is the old silent path, now surfaced) ¬∑ **fetch-failed** (had a key but bytes didn't come back ‚Üí transient/retryable "couldn't load just now") ¬∑ **signed-out** (sign in to view). `DocReview.fetchSourceBytes` classifies pre-download and, after a keyed download returns nothing, distinguishes fetch-failed vs signed-out; every line ends "(your markups are saved)".
- **"Re-open it" already uploads-and-rebinds (verified by reading the path, no change needed):** the banner's "Re-open file‚Ä¶" ‚Üí `openFile` reuses the srcId on a same-name re-drop and never changes `reviewId`, so markups stay bound to the same document id (no orphaning), `storeSource` re-uploads, and `buildSnapshot` persists the new key.
- **One vocabulary across surfaces:** `FileBrowser` + `ProjectFilesDrawer` queue warns now call the shared `fileWarn` (was three drifting copies).
- **Live caveat (the brief's own note):** the universal "every file fails" symptom is a LIVE storage/auth class the logged-out sandbox can't reproduce (most likely the `doc-review-files` Storage bucket/RLS not provisioned, or legacy keyless rows) ‚Äî the code fix makes whatever the live cause is **name itself** precisely + offer a working recovery; a signed-in pass on the deployed site (cohort/owner) confirms the render. lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1228 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `DocReview` lazy chunk intact.

### B403 ‚Äî Silently persist & restore ALL export-screen state (no badge) `[Scheduler / Export]` (feature)  *(arrived as the corrected owner "NEW-5"; minted **B403**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).** On reopening the PDF/Print Exhibit, the user's last choices come back, defaulting to them, with **NO badge** (no "restored" indicator).
- **Persisted (localStorage `planar:exportPrefs:v1`, per browser/user):** page size, orientation, margins (preset + custom), name-align, every column toggle (ID/Task Name/Start/End/Duration/Status/% Done/Owner/Predecessors/Cost), Header/Cover options, the **expand/collapse state of each sidebar section**, and the **timescale/pan** state (`timeUnit`/`zoomMul`/`panFrac`) from B361.
- **Written synchronously** (not debounced) so closing the modal right after a change still saves it ‚Äî the earlier debounce dropped the last change on a quick close.
- **Per-user/session was used** (localStorage); column **width** keeps riding `data.exportColWidths` (B392, cloud-synced). `docDate` intentionally defaults to "today" each open rather than freezing a stale exhibit date; task selection/collapse stay data-specific (not persisted).
- `loadExportPrefs`/`saveExportPrefs` + an `EXPORT_PERSIST_KEYS` whitelist; the `cfg`/`secOpen`/`headerOpen` initializers overlay the saved values. Verified: 11√ó17‚ÜíPortrait + Owner off + Margins collapsed all restore after close+reopen, no badge.

### B402 ‚Äî De-conflict co-dated labels + route dependency curves into the TOP (12 o'clock) of the target `[Scheduler / Export]` (bug)  *(arrived as the corrected owner "NEW-4"; minted **B402**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).**
- **Connector reroute (curves KEPT):** in `buildGanttSVG`, the source still departs from its bar center, but the curve now terminates at **12 o'clock ‚Äî the top-center of the target glyph** (new `topOf()` per kind: diamond top vertex / summary bracket top / leaf bar top), with a vertical final control point (`ctrl.x == end.x`) and a **down-pointing arrowhead** descending into the top. Clears the endpoint date label that sits at 9 o'clock. NOT converted to orthogonal ‚Äî stays a cubic b√©zier (B396 preserved). The "Dep. arrows" toggle still turns them fully off.
- **Label de-collision:** the in-chart names are collected during the row pass, then a post-pass nudges any overlapping label boxes apart vertically (capped, defensive) before emitting them on a top `nameLayer`. A pure no-op when nothing overlaps (the common per-row case), so existing layouts are untouched. Reinforced by B401's right-edge framing room, which removes the right-cluster pile-up at its source.
- Verified: 87/87 connectors curved + on-palette, 87/87 vertical entry, 87/87 down arrowheads; V114 (print connectors curved + no-NaN) still PASS.
- **Follow-up fix (same session, owner-reported "clearly not working"):** the first pass entered at the target's geometric **center-top**, so on a long bar the connector swept far across to the bar middle (messy diagonals). Corrected to enter at the **connection x** (start for FS/SS, end for FF/SF) at the top edge, with adaptive departure/descent handles (small lead for near rows ‚Üí no hump). Re-verified V116 + the SEED 87-connector view; all drop straight into the top right where the link lands.
- **Follow-up #2 (owner refinement ‚Äî natural arcs, not rigid):** forcing a strict 12-o'clock (vertical) entry and a 3-o'clock (horizontal) exit looked stiff. Now the connector **leaves from the source's BOTTOM edge** (‚âà4‚Äì6 o'clock ‚Äî clears the date at the source's side) and **arrives at the target's TOP edge** (‚âà9‚Äì12 o'clock ‚Äî clears the date at the target's side), with tangents that follow the natural travel direction (a short link drops near-vertically; a long horizontal link enters at a shallow angle from the side). The arrowhead is oriented along the entry tangent. Re-verified **V116 17/17** (87/87 curved, 87/87 arrive-from-above, 87/87 leave-downward, arrowheads point into the target).

### B401 ‚Äî Export default time window auto-fits all labels (extend the frame, never move the labels) `[Scheduler / Export]` (bug)  *(arrived as the corrected owner "NEW-2"; minted **B401**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).** On open, the default visible window auto-extends its edges so every start/end label sits on the sheet without clipping ‚Äî **framing only, no label repositioned** (no gutter, no flip). `buildGanttSVG` measures each label's natural (unclamped) overhang past the chart edges using the SAME `placeGanttLabel` helper as the render, converts the widest left/right overhang to lead/trail days, and extends `[minD,maxD]` ‚Üí the visible window. **Capped at ~22% of the natural span per side** so a long label can't crush the schedule into a sliver (it clamps/flips as before past the cap). Default-only: the timescale/pan controls (B361) still adjust from there. Verified: no name clips (x0=10/x1=312 in W=322) and the earliest bar has lead room (x=17, not 0).

### B361 ‚Äî Export Gantt time-axis controls: discrete scale selector + continuous timescale zoom + horizontal pan + fix the dead preview toolbar (PDF/Print Exhibit) `[Scheduler / Export]` (feature + bug)  *(renumbered from a colliding B159 ‚Üí B361, 2026-06-21; **amended 2026-06-22** to absorb the corrected owner "NEW-1")*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).**
- **Dead/intermittent toolbar FIXED:** the floating preview toolbar (was the broken `float`/`position:sticky`-inside-a-flexbox "+ 82% Fit Move" from B400 ‚Äî clicks no-op, intermittently visible) is rebuilt as an **absolute overlay pinned to the top-right of a non-scrolling wrapper** (the scroller is now a child) ‚Üí always visible, always clickable (`elementFromPoint` returns the buttons themselves).
- **Discrete time-scale selector** (the original B361 ask): a sidebar **Time scale** SegBtn ‚Äî **Fit ¬∑ Qtr ¬∑ Mo ¬∑ Wk ¬∑ Day** ‚Äî sets `cfg.timeUnit`; re-renders the preview; export-only.
- **Continuous timescale** (amended NEW-1): toolbar **Time ‚àí/+** multiplies `cfg.zoomMul`. The discrete unit and the continuous multiplier feed **one `vis` span** in `buildGanttSVG` (`opts.timeUnit`/`zoomMul`/`panFrac`), so they compose and never fight.
- **Pan** (amended NEW-1): "Move" renamed **"Pan"** (hand cursor); a drag slides the visible time window (`cfg.panFrac`, an offset from a **today-centered** default landing when zoomed in). Page **Zoom** (‚àí/%/+) and **Fit** (reset zoom + axis) kept. Off-window names are suppressed so zoom/pan don't pile labels at the edge.
- **Distinct from B160** (the whole-split divider ratio ‚Äî left Open). Verified: toolbar clickable; "Mo" drops month rules 46‚Üí10, Time+ composes 10‚Üí9, Pan-drag shifts the window, Fit restores to 46.

### B400 ‚Äî Exported Gantt: whole timeline fits (viewBox) + Move/zoom drag-pan in the preview `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped as "NEW-4" 2026-06-22; minted **B400** ‚Äî concurrent session took B393‚ÄìB396)*
`[x]` **Filed AND built + headless-verified (V115, 12/12) + merged the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/gifted-hypatia-na62be`).**
- **Fit (no clipping):** `buildGanttSVG` gained `viewBox="0 0 svgWidth svgH"` so the full min‚Üímax range scales to fill the page Gantt column exactly (with `style:width:100%`) ‚Äî the "fit the whole timeline to the page width" guarantee.
- **Move + zoom (preview):** the `PDFExportModal` preview gained a discoverable control cluster ‚Äî **‚àí/+ zoom**, a **% readout**, **Fit** (back to whole-sheet), and **Move** (drag-to-pan). Move toggles an overlay that captures drag‚Üí`scrollLeft/Top` + wheel-zoom; when **off** the overlay is `pointer-events:none` so the in-preview collapse triangles + column drag-handles still work. Zoomed wider than the pane ‚Üí the pane switches to `flex-start` so pan/scroll reaches both edges.
- **Dedupe / cross-ref:** distinct from **B160** (table-vs-Gantt width split) and **B361** (time-scale density) ‚Äî left Open. Verified V115.

### B399 ‚Äî Exported Gantt: light two-tier Year‚ñ∏Month header + weighted year>quarter>month grid rules `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(owner-art-directed in-session; minted **B399**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND built + headless-verified (V115, 12/12) + merged the SAME session (branch `claude/gifted-hypatia-na62be`).** The owner rejected the old flat dark month strip ("dog shit for the header"), reviewed rendered mockups of MS-Project / P6 / Smartsheet / TeamGantt styles, and picked a **light two-tier** band (option "D") with weighted gridlines.
- **Header:** replaced the single dark month strip with a **light Year ‚ñ∏ Month** band (`YEAR_TIER=14 + MON_TIER=16 = HEADER_H 30`); year labels centered per span, month labels (single-letter when tight), quarter-month labels bolded.
- **Weighted grid rules** drop from the header THROUGH the body, ranked by weight per the owner: **year `#8b95a3`/1.3 (full height) > quarter `#c2c9d2`/0.8 > month `#e7ebf0`/0.4** (quarter is a line only, no label), all light and painted BEFORE the bars (B397) so nothing crosses a bar.

### B397 ‚Äî Exported Gantt: vertical rules paint BEHIND the bars (z-order) `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; minted **B397**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V115, 12/12) + merged the SAME session (branch `claude/gifted-hypatia-na62be`).** The month/year/today rules + dependency connectors painted on TOP of the bars (the export's `${gridLines}${rows}${yearLines}${arrows}${todayLine}` put `rows` ‚Äî the bars ‚Äî before the rules). Split the per-row string into explicit **layers** (`rowBands` / `barLayer` / `labelLayer`) and recomposed in the on-screen B222 order: **row bands ‚Üí grid rules ‚Üí left edge ‚Üí today ‚Üí dependency (main's curved) ‚Üí bars + milestones ‚Üí header labels/lines ‚Üí bar/in-chart labels**. ### B396 ‚Äî Dependency connectors: keep the CURVE, bind it to the bars (revert B395's orthogonal elbow) `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner feedback right after B395 shipped: "the serpentine things were fine, i preferred those, revert, the issue was just that they weren't bound to anything")*
`[x]` **Fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** B395 had replaced the curved (cubic-b√©zier) dependency connectors with orthogonal elbows ‚Äî but the owner preferred the curves; the real defect was that the connectors **floated at the row's vertical mid-line** while the on-screen bars sit at the **bottom** of the row, so a curve looked unbound (it left from / arrived at empty space above the bar).
- **Reverted to the curved b√©zier** in both render paths (`GanttView` `depLines` render + `buildGanttSVG`); removed the `depElbow`/`depElbowD`/`DEP_DIRS` elbow router entirely.
- **The actual fix ‚Äî bind to the bar:** each endpoint's **y is now the bar's true vertical center per kind** (`depYCenter` in `GanttView`; `ycOf` in `buildGanttSVG`) ‚Äî leaf bars at the row bottom, summaries on their span, milestones on the diamond ‚Äî instead of the old `ROW_H/2` row-center. So a curve now cleanly leaves the predecessor bar and enters the successor bar. x still anchors to the correct bar edge per type (FS/SS/FF/SF); arrowhead direction by `x2‚â•x1`. Pure geometry from the row index, so off-screen virtualized rows still compute.
- **Supersedes B395's visual** (the elbow); keeps the SS color `#7c3aed`, dashed `4 3`, layer order (z3, under bars), and the blank-date guard (`verify-unscheduled-deps` still 1/1, no phantom NaN).
- **Verified:** build green ¬∑ JSX OK ¬∑ headless **V114** (`ui-audit/verify-gantt-labels-deps.mjs`, updated): every connector is a curve (not an elbow), **0** NaN, and every endpoint binds to the bar band (below the row mid-line) on screen + in the print SVG, across right/center/left.
- **üîÅ RE-REPORT #3 (2026-07-03) ‚Äî folded here per owner ("this arrow-anchor issue has been raised at least twice before, don't mint a new B#"). NO new B# for the anchor half; it shipped in the same PR as B635 (the low-zoom label de-collision; code/commit keep the provisional **B629**).** B396 bound BOTH endpoints to each bar's vertical CENTER (9 o'clock), which had two consequences the owner then hit live on Pappadoupolos (~33% zoom): every outbound link left from ONE point (the LOI task's links bundled unreadably), and every entry arrowhead landed at the target's left-middle, directly over the start-date label ("8/7"/"2/8"). **Fix (the B635 batch; code labels it B629):** replaced center-anchoring with **directional edge anchoring** in ONE shared pure helper `depAnchors()` used by BOTH paths (on-screen `GanttView` + print `buildGanttSVG`), so they can't drift again (B402 had fixed only the print path). A DOWN link now leaves the source's BOTTOM edge near its finish (‚âà4‚Äì6 o'clock) and enters the target's TOP edge near its start; an UP link mirrors it (top‚Üíbottom); a bar's outbound links FAN across the exit arc; and the entry x is nudged clear of the start-date-label rect (`ARROW_REACH`) so an arrowhead can never overprint it. On-screen `depYCenter`/`edgeYOf` are gone, replaced by per-path `glyphEdges` feeding `depAnchors`. Verified: **V202** headless (`ui-audit/verify-gantt-dep-anchors.mjs` ‚Äî 0 arrowhead‚Üîdate-label collisions, exits fan, up/down edges correct, print path fanned+curved+NaN-free) + V114 updated (down-links exit the underside, enter below the source, multi-out bars fan). Live ~33%-zoom sign-off on the real Pappadoupolos schedule tracked in V202.

### B393 ‚Äî Gantt label alignment (Left / Center / Right) + uniform near-black ink with weight hierarchy + never-on-fill auto-fit `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; first minted B390, **renumbered B393** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (PR #293, the exhibit-columns batch) took B390‚ÄìB392 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** Gantt task/summary/milestone names now have a user-selectable **Left / Center / Right** alignment, render in ONE uniform near-black ink with hierarchy carried by **font weight**, and auto-fit so they're **never painted on the bar fill** ‚Äî fixing the reported defect where centered names sat on the fill and got covered (the top-level summary "Utilities"/"Electric Transmission" overlapping their span).
- **Uniform ink + weight hierarchy:** every in-chart label uses `GANTT_INK = #1a1a1a` (both render paths). The **B210 depth-navy was REMOVED from the name TEXT** (that fade-by-depth was the "grey" Michael spotted ‚Äî exactly the B320 ban). Prominence is now weight: `ganttNameWeight` ‚Üí top summary **800** ‚Üí child summaries **700/600** ‚Üí leaves **500** (milestones 600; explicit `task.bold` lifts to ‚â•700). No text fades toward the background.
- **Bracket GRAPHIC keeps its ramp (verified NOT inverted):** `["#2B3340","#46506A","#6E7790","#8A93A8"][min(level,3)]` ‚Üí depth 0 (top) = #2B3340, darkest/thickest, lighter/thinner as nesting deepens ‚Äî correct. The "seemed backwards" report was the navy on *text*, now gone. Span/leg magnitudes were compressed a touch (SPAN `[6,5,4,4]`, LEG `[0.16,0.12,0.09,0.07]¬∑ROW_H` on screen; matching compression in print) so the always-above caption has clear air within the row ‚Äî the ramp itself is unchanged.
- **Placement helper `placeGanttLabel`** (pure, in `index.html`, used by BOTH paths; mirrors the overflow-outside logic of `site-planner/lib/labelLayout.js` ‚Äî can't import, separate iframe bundle): **Leaf** ‚Äî Right (default) = name after the bar end, flips Left then lifts above when it would hit the right clip; Left = before the bar start, lifts above near the left edge; Center = on a **contrast plate**, overflows to a caption above when wider than the bar. **Summary** ‚Äî caption **ALWAYS above** the thin span (Left=above left leg, Center=above midpoint, Right=above right leg), clamped in the row band so the topmost summary can't ride into the timeline header. **Milestone** ‚Äî Left=before / Center=above / Right=after the diamond. Data labels (start/end/owner/%) are cleared via `padBefore/padAfter` so the name never overlaps them.
- **Scope & persistence:** view-level, per project ‚Äî `proj.labelAlign` written via `writeLabelAlign` (a mirror of B212's `writeGridCfg`), riding the existing cloud-save/autosave path; default `"right"`. Control surfaced in the Settings panel ("Task name alignment", 3-way, edits the active project).
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors ¬∑ 1201 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ JSX OK ¬∑ headless **V114** (`ui-audit/verify-gantt-labels-deps.mjs`) ‚Äî pure-helper unit checks (weight ramp, summary-always-above, leaf after/flip/plate/overflow, milestone before/after/above) + live DOM (every name `rgb(26,26,26)`, "Utilities" caption mode=above & above the bar band, summary bolder than leaf) across **right/center/left**, 0 page errors. Existing `verify-unscheduled-deps` (1/1) + `verify-scheduler-no-crash` (6/6) + `verify-scheduler-bugfixes` still green.

### B394 ‚Äî Printed/PDF Gantt now emits in-chart task names; honors alignment + uniform ink `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; first minted B391, **renumbered B394** ‚Äî PR #293 collision)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** The PDF/print exhibit showed task names in the left grid columns but **none on the Gantt bars** ‚Äî `buildGanttSVG` never emitted the in-chart name layer that `GanttView` draws. It now does, consuming the **same** `placeGanttLabel` helper, alignment, `GANTT_INK`, and `ganttNameWeight` as on-screen, so the two paths can't drift.
- **Uniform black here too** (no B210 depth-navy on summary names ‚Äî the bracket rects keep their navy, the `<text>` is `#1a1a1a`); names escaped via the existing `escapeHtml` (B248).
- **Sidebar controls:** added a **"Bar names"** toggle (default **ON**) + a **"Name align"** Left/Center/Right segment to the PDF export Gantt section (seeded from the active project's `labelAlign`), wired through `opts.barNames` / `opts.labelAlign`. **Checked B159/B160 first:** the export `cfg.columns` governs the grid TABLE name COLUMN, not in-chart bar labels ‚Äî no conflict/duplication.
- **Edge cases honored:** the bar widths from the time-scale/width controls feed straight into the helper, so labels reflow and re-resolve their flip/lift; the chart clip is `[LABEL_W, svgWidth]`, so a trailing/leading label flips or lifts rather than overrunning the exhibit page edge.
- **Verified (part of V114):** `buildGanttSVG` called directly in-browser ‚Äî print SVG emits ‚â•6 names in `#1a1a1a`, **0** navy on any `<text>`, includes summary + milestone names, "Bar names" off drops them, and the print connectors are orthogonal with no NaN.

### B395 ‚Äî Dependency arrows route as clean orthogonal elbows, not serpentine zig-zags `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-3" 2026-06-22; first minted B392, **renumbered B395** ‚Äî PR #293 collision)*
`[x]` **‚öÝÔ∏è SUPERSEDED by B396 (same session):** the owner preferred the original curved connectors ‚Äî B396 reverts the orthogonal elbow and instead fixes the real defect (binding the curve's endpoints to the bar centers). This block records what B395 shipped before that feedback. ‚Äî **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** The dashed dependency links were single cubic b√©ziers with direction-flipped handles that wandered into multi-bend serpentine shapes. Replaced the path generation in BOTH the `GanttView` `depLines` useMemo and the `buildGanttSVG` connector emitter with a deterministic **orthogonal elbow router** (`depElbow` + `depElbowD`, pure, shared) keyed on dependency type via `DEP_DIRS` (FS `[1,1]` / SS `[-1,1]` / FF `[1,-1]` / SF `[-1,-1]` exit/entry directions).
- **Shape:** short exit stub off the predecessor edge ‚Üí a single vertical run ‚Üí a horizontal into the successor edge, arrowhead pointing along the entry direction. A **normal forward link is ‚â§2 bends**; a **backward link** (the successor's entry edge sits on the far side) routes out ‚Üí vertical ‚Üí back across ‚Üí in (‚â§4 bends), around the bars ‚Äî never diagonally through them.
- **Edge cases:** milestone endpoints anchor to the **diamond tip** (center ¬± half-width in the travel direction), not a bar edge; endpoints are pure geometry from `tasks.indexOf`, so a link whose row is scrolled out of the virtualized window still computes and clips at the viewport; adjacent rows give a minimal vertical run; many-row spans give one straight vertical run, not per-row jogs. Kept the layer/paint order (connectors z3, under bars) and the per-type colors incl. SS `#7c3aed`; still dashed `4 3`.
- **Verified (part of V114 + the existing `verify-unscheduled-deps`):** `depElbow` unit-checked per type (every segment orthogonal for forward AND backward geometry, forward ‚â§2 bends, backward ‚â§4, no NaN in `depElbowD`); the live on-screen scenario draws 4 connectors all orthogonal/no-NaN incl. a backward link routing around; the print path likewise. `verify-unscheduled-deps` still passes (exactly 1 connector, no phantom NaN paths) ‚Äî the blank-date guard is intact.

### B392 ‚Äî Drag-to-resize columns in the PDF/Print Exhibit `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-3" 2026-06-22; filed B387, **renumbered B392** ‚Äî concurrent `main` (PRs #290/#291/#292) took B385‚ÄìB389 while this exhibit batch was in flight, so B390‚ÄìB392 are the real next free IDs; branch commit titles still read B385/B386/B387)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V113, 10/10) + merged via PR #293 the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/practical-edison-ggcm1k`).** The exhibit columns can now be tuned by dragging a column's right edge in the preview ‚Äî the same interaction as resizing a column in the live schedule grid ‚Äî with the preview reflowing live and the tuned layout persisting.
- **Handles + live drag (`buildPDFHtml`, `public/sequence/index.html`):** every column header in the **preview** (never the print output ‚Äî `@media print{.col-rs{display:none}}` + emitted only when `forPreview`) carries an absolutely-positioned `.col-rs` `col-resize` handle on its right edge. A small preview-only script tracks the drag and resizes the dragged column **live across every paginated page** (sets the width on all `th[data-k]` + each `.split-table`/`.split-gantt`), trading the delta against the Gantt ‚Äî which keeps `EXHIBIT_MIN_GANTT` (240px) ‚Äî and clamping to the column's `[min,max]`. On release it posts `{type:'planarColResize',key,w}` to the parent.
- **Persistence (`PDFExportModal`):** a `planarColResize` message handler stashes the width as a per-column override (`cfg.colWidths[key]`), flushes the preview immediately (like the collapse-toggle path), and writes through `setData` to **`data.exportColWidths`** so a tuned exhibit comes back next time the modal opens (`cfg.colWidths` is seeded from it). A **Reset** affordance under the Columns section clears the overrides back to content-fit. (Persisted at schedule-data level ‚Äî the exhibit renders one shared column layout across all selected projects, so a per-project-keyed store wouldn't apply; this is "comes back the way you left it" at the right granularity.)
- **Starts from B390's auto-fit:** an override wins for its column; everything else stays content-fit. Drag handles render only in the split (Gantt) view, where the table-vs-Gantt trade-off is well-defined (the reported screenshot).
- **Pagination fix bundled:** the paginator rebuilds the split and the Gantt container had **lost its `.split-gantt` class** (a no-class `div`); restored it so the live width-trade (and any `.split-gantt` selector) lands on the real post-paginate element instead of pushing/overflowing the chart.
- **DEDUPE / RECONCILE with B160 (left Open):** B160 is a single *ratio slider* for the whole table-vs-Gantt split; B392's **per-column** drag now changes the table block's total directly and the Gantt auto-takes the remainder (‚â• MIN_GANTT) ‚Äî complementary, not a duplicate. B392 sets the default total from content-fit; if B160's slider is built later it sets the overall budget B392's columns fit within. Added a reconcile note to B160 in `BACKLOG.md`; not built here (it wasn't in this drop and is a distinct control).
- **Verified:** see the shared verification note under B390.

### B391 ‚Äî Year-boundary divider lines on the exhibit Gantt timeline `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; filed B386, **renumbered B391** ‚Äî concurrent `main` took B385‚ÄìB389 while this batch was in flight)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V113, 10/10) + merged via PR #293 the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/practical-edison-ggcm1k`).** A multi-year exhibit Gantt (the example spanned 2025‚Üí2027) had no visual break between calendar years.
- **Root cause (read the code, not the brief):** `buildGanttSVG`'s month gridlines were drawn **behind** the opaque per-row background bands (`<rect width=svgWidth fill=bg>`), so in the chart **body** they were invisible ‚Äî only the dark header strip showed month labels. There were effectively no body gridlines at all, year or month.
- **Fix:** a new `yearLines` string drawn **over** the row bands (in the return, `${gridLines}${rows}${yearLines}${arrows}${todayLine}`): a heavier solid **medium-slate `#7b8290`, 1.3px** vertical at each Jan 1 (`yearMarkers`, the existing `m.mo===0` filter), so the split between years reads at a glance. Solid slate keeps it distinct from the red **dashed** Today line and the colored **dashed** dependency arrows. Also **emphasized the January month-axis labels** (bold + white vs the faint `#e8e8e8` other months) so each year's start is legible on the axis itself (doubles as the year label). A single-year exhibit (no January boundary in range) gets no divider ‚Äî correct.
- **Scope:** the exhibit only (the explicit request ‚Äî "the Gantt's result ‚Ä¶ should at least have divisions between the years"). Mirroring onto the on-screen `GanttView` was deferred (different render path; not requested).
- **Verified:** see the shared verification note under B390.

### B390 ‚Äî PDF/Print Exhibit table columns mis-sized: oversized Task-Name gap + truncated Start/End/Duration `[Scheduler]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; filed B385, **renumbered B390** ‚Äî concurrent `main` (PRs #290/#291/#292) took B385‚ÄìB389 while this batch was in flight, so B390‚ÄìB392 are the real next free IDs)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V113, 10/10) + merged via PR #293 the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/practical-edison-ggcm1k`).** Three symptoms, **one** cause: in the split-view (table + Gantt) exhibit the columns were sized by **fixed widths, not fitted to content** ‚Äî a large dead band between TASK NAME and START, Start/End rendering clipped (`06/2‚Ä¶`, `09/0‚Ä¶`), and DUR clipped (`1‚Ä¶`, `4‚Ä¶`).
- **Root cause:** the split table used `table-layout:fixed` at a hardcoded ~50%-of-page width, with fixed per-column px from `COL_DEFS` and the **Task-Name column at `w:null`** ‚Äî so Name absorbed ALL the leftover width (the giant gap) while the fixed **42px** Start/End and **28px** Duration columns were too narrow for `MM/DD/YY` / multi-digit durations and clipped via `overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis`.
- **Fix ‚Äî content-fit width model.** New pure, DOM-free module-scope helpers (above `buildPDFHtml`, so preview + print + tests agree): `approxTextPx(str,fs)` approximates rendered width from per-character advances for the exhibit's Inter face (deliberately generous so a fixed-format value never clips); `layoutExhibitCols(specs,opts)` lays columns out from their content-fit base, lets a user drag-override win, clamps each to `[min,max]`, makes the **table block exactly its columns' total**, and gives the Gantt the rest ‚Äî never below `EXHIBIT_MIN_GANTT` (240px), shrinking the **flexible** columns (name/owner/predecessors/cost) toward their min, then a uniform scale, only if the columns would crowd the chart. `buildPDFHtml` measures each visible column's widest value across **all** selected projects (the exhibit stacks them under one shared header) via a plain-text `cellText` + a `COL_FIT` profile (font px, clamp range, flex flag, the Status dot+gap extra), then sizes the split from the result.
- **Per the brief:** the short fixed-format columns (Start/End/Duration/%/Status) are **NOT flexible** ‚Äî a date is never the value that gets clipped to make room; only Name/Owner/Predecessors/Cost cap + ellipsize. **Respects the column on/off checkboxes** (only the turned-on `colDefs` are measured/sized). Tables-only mode (no Gantt) is **untouched** ‚Äî its auto-layout already content-fits, so the bug was split-only; the fix is scoped there.
- **Shared verification (B390/B391/B392):** lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **1210 tests** (1201 + 9 new in `test/exhibitCols.test.js`, with a faithful copy in `ui-audit/stress/exhibit-cols.mjs` + anti-drift guards) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ JSX transpiles (`jsxcheck-sequence`) ¬∑ `Scheduler` lazy chunk intact. Headless **V113** (`ui-audit/verify-exhibit-columns.mjs`, **10/10**, drives page ‚Üí sequence iframe ‚Üí preview iframe on the real app): Start **and** End dates render in full with no ellipsis clip (`12/27/26`, `07/23/25`, `02/02/27`); Duration full (`389d`, `21d`); Task-Name content-capped (no runaway gap, name=280px); Gantt keeps a real share (322px); year-boundary divider lines present; a resize handle drags **live** (53‚Üí87px) and the new width **round-trips/persists** through the parent re-render. Screenshot: `ui-audit/screens/exhibit-fixed-zoom.png`. (Re-verified after merging `main` ‚Äî main's B388 Schedule-toolbar lift moved the Export control into the shell header, so the harness opens it there; still 10/10.)

### B389 ‚Äî Move the Light/Dark/System theme picker into account ‚Üí Settings `[Header/Nav ¬∑ Theme]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat 2026-06-22: "put the light v dark option under profile settings"; minted **B389**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/inspiring-bohr-46gql9`).** The Light/Dark/System picker that lived in the row-1 ‚öô gear (B342) now lives in the **account ‚Üí Settings** panel (`AuthPanel`), next to Change password ‚Äî where a signed-in user expects their preferences.
- **One picker, one home, no duplication:** extracted the picker UI into a shared `src/shared/theme/ThemePicker.jsx` (self-contained ‚Äî reads/sets the ThemeProvider via `useTheme`, theme tokens only, no raw hex). `AuthPanel`'s Settings tab renders it; the row-1 gear (`AppHeader` `SettingsMenu`) now renders the SAME component (no copy-paste).
- **Signed-out access preserved (B342):** the row-1 gear is kept **only when signed out** (`{!accountActive && <SettingsMenu/>}`) ‚Äî a logged-out visitor can still switch theme, a signed-in user finds it in account ‚Üí Settings, and it's never duplicated. `accountActive` is `!!user` from the Shell, threaded to every AppHeader, so the gate is reliable. (Owner trade-off, flagged: signed-out is now the only place the standalone gear shows; signed-in is account ‚Üí Settings.)
- **Code:** new `ThemePicker.jsx`; `AppHeader.jsx` (gear ‚Üí shared picker, gated on `!accountActive`, dropped the now-unused `mode`/`setMode`/`THEME_OPTS`); `AuthPanel.jsx` (Settings tab renders `<ThemePicker/>`). Pure relocation ‚Äî the ThemeProvider, the `data-theme` mechanism, and the System matchMedia listener are unchanged.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ¬∑ **1201 tests** ¬∑ build green; headless **V112** (`ui-audit/verify-b389-theme-in-profile.mjs` + `theme-in-profile-harness.{html,jsx}`, **7/7** ‚Äî signed-out gear present, signed-in gear gone, the Settings-tab picker offers Light/Dark/System and clicking Dark/Light actually flips `<html data-theme>`, 0 page errors). The B387 (9/9) + B388 (17/17) harnesses still pass ‚Äî the logged-out gear they rely on is preserved.

### B388 ‚Äî Lift the Schedule toolbar into the unified AppHeader over the iframe bridge `[Scheduler / Header-Nav]` (feature) ‚Äî built on B387  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; filed B386, **renumbered B388** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (PRs #289/#290) took B385/B386 while this was in flight, so B387/B388 are the real next free IDs. The branch commit titles still read "B385/B386" ‚Äî written before the collision surfaced; the canonical IDs are **B387/B388**.)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fully built + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/inspiring-bohr-46gql9`).** Schedule's two stacked header rows are now one: the embedded Gantt app's action toolbar (`public/sequence/index.html`) is LIFTED up into the shell's unified `AppHeader` Row 2 over the existing postMessage bridge (B203), so Schedule matches Site/Markup ‚Äî one header, no double toolbar.
- **What moved up:** the **Grid/Split/Gantt** toggle + **review inbox** (unread badge) ‚Üí the B387 `toolbarCenter` slot; **zoom (‚àí/%/+), export, save, version history, contacts, automation, format, settings** ‚Üí `toolbarContent` (right). Module tabs stay left.
- **The architectural worry was UNFOUNDED:** the embedded app already renders its panels (Settings/Format/Automation/Contacts/History/PDF) as **self-positioned `position:fixed` modals/drawers toggled by boolean state ‚Äî never anchored to their toolbar buttons** (they take only `data/setData/onClose`). So lifting the *trigger buttons* to the shell is clean: the buttons post commands; the panels still open inside the iframe exactly as before. Only the Export dropdown was button-anchored ‚Äî its menu is now rendered shell-side (reusing `AnchoredMenu`) and each item posts the chosen export.
- **Bridge extended, not rebuilt:** iframe ‚Üí shell adds `planar:toolbar-state` (view, section, zoom %, zoomable, reviewCount, reviewOpen, saveStatus/savePulse/fileLinked, activePanel) with its own signature-dedup; shell ‚Üí iframe adds `planar:view-set / review-toggle / zoom / export / save / history / contacts / automation / settings / format`, handled by a second `inShell`-gated listener in the App. **Strict same-origin guards kept** (exact-origin target + `planar-seq`/`planar-shell` source discriminators) ‚Äî not loosened for the new types. The iframe is the **single source of truth**: the shell controls only display reported state + post intent; the unread badge is the reported count (never hardcoded), and `toolbar.ready` gates rendering so a control never shows a fabricated value before the iframe reports.
- **Hide-in-embed:** `.in-iframe .app-header { display:none }` (the whole header) now that the shell hosts the toolbar ‚Äî re-widening what B381 had narrowed (B381 narrowed it only because the toolbar had no shell home *yet*; now it does). Standalone `/sequence/` is untouched ‚Äî the hide is scoped to `.in-iframe` and both bridge halves are gated `inShell` (headless-confirmed).
- **Brief gotchas honored:** two settings gears stay separate (Row-1 app/theme gear B342 vs the lifted Schedule Settings); the lifted save is the embedded app's own indicator (Row-1's cloud badge is not wired for Schedule ‚Üí no redundancy); Fullscreen (F) collapses the header so the lifted controls vanish with it for free; "share" in the brief = the existing **Contacts** control (no speculative control invented, per B381's lesson).
- **Code:** new `src/workspaces/scheduler/components/ScheduleToolbar.jsx` (`ScheduleCenter` + `ScheduleActions`, shell theme tokens, reuses `AnchoredMenu`); `Scheduler.jsx` (tracks reported `toolbar` state, feeds the two AppHeader slots, posts commands via the existing same-origin `post`); `public/sequence/index.html` (toolbar-state emit + command listener, both `inShell`-gated; CSS hide). Consumes B387's `toolbarCenter`.
- **Deduped:** net-new, the completion of the B203 bridge. NOT B203 (the *nav* half) nor B381 (which *restored* the in-iframe toolbar ‚Äî B388 relocates it). NOT the concurrent `main` **B386** (the Scheduler dependency-connector bug ‚Äî different surface, just an ID collision). **Supersedes B381's harness** `verify-schedule-toolbar.mjs` (retired ‚Äî it asserted the in-iframe toolbar is visible, the opposite of B388).
- **Verified:** lint 0 ¬∑ **1201 tests** ¬∑ build green ¬∑ JSX OK (`jsxcheck-sequence`) ¬∑ `Scheduler`/`AppHeader` lazy chunks intact; headless **V111** (`ui-audit/verify-b388-toolbar-lift.mjs`, **17/17** ‚Äî embedded toolbar hidden, every lifted control renders, two gears separate, cross-frame round-trips (Gantt‚Üízoom appears, zoom 17%‚Üí33%, Settings opens the iframe panel + button reflects reported state), badge never fabricated, 0 page errors, AND standalone `/sequence/` toolbar still intact).

### B387 ‚Äî Add an optional center toolbar zone to AppHeader Row 2 `[Header/Nav]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; filed B385, **renumbered B387** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (PR #289) took B385 while this was in flight; canonical IDs for this pair are B387 + B388)*

Added an optional third **center** slot: a new `toolbarCenter` ReactNode prop, rendered between the tabs and `toolbarContent`, mirroring how Row 1 already centers `centerContent` between its left/right zones.
- **Layout:** with `toolbarCenter` present, Row 2 becomes 3 zones ‚Äî left tabs (`flex:1`, basis-0), center group (`flex:0 1 auto`, shrink-to-content), right toolbar (`flex:1`, justified end) ‚Äî so the center is TRULY centered the same way Row 1 is (verified center mid-x **719** vs row mid-x **720**). The row `flex-wrap`s (center/toolbar flow to a second line; `minHeight:44` lets it grow) on a too-narrow viewport instead of overlapping ‚Äî never absolute positioning.
