### B386 ‚Äî Dependency connectors draw phantom arrows to unscheduled (blank-date) tasks `[Scheduler]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-22; arrived as cross-chat "NEW-1"; filed B385, **renumbered B386** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (PR #289) took B385 for the Site Planner parcel-identify feature while this was in flight, so B386 is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + SHIPPED the same session per STANDING RULE #1 ‚Äî merged to `main` via PR #290 (squash `e4fdb2f`), branch `claude/gifted-rubin-0adp7h`.** (The PR/commit title still reads "B385" ‚Äî written before the concurrent-collision renumber surfaced; the canonical ID is **B386**.)

**Repro (Pappadoupolos, Schedule ‚Üí Split/Gantt):** the "Wastewater Treatment Plant Design" branch (rows 11‚Äì13) are *unscheduled stubs* ‚Äî blank start/finish, 0d, so no bar and no milestone diamond. A dependency link still terminated at/from one of them and the arrow was drawn into empty space (the "purple pointing at nothing" the owner saw).

**Diagnostic fork (done first, as the brief required) ‚Üí branch #1: genuine unscheduled stubs, NOT a silent date-calc failure.** Blank/unscheduled tasks are a deliberate, first-class state: `mkt` creates them (`start=""`, `dur=""`), `rollupParentDates` leaves a parent blank when *all* its children are blank (so the blank parent over blank children in rows 11‚Äì13 is the *designed* outcome), `cascadeDates` leaves a task blank when no predecessor yields a start, and BOTH the bar renderer (`isBlankDates` guard) and the PDF-export connector loop (`!pt.start||!pt.end||!t.start||!t.end`) already skip blank-date geometry. The date engine is correct ‚Äî the rows are intentionally undated. The single defect: the live `GanttView.depLines` was the one draw path *missing* that guard, so it computed `xOf("")` ‚Üí `NaN` and emitted a phantom path.

**Fix (`public/sequence/index.html`):** (1) `depLines` now skips any link whose predecessor OR successor has blank dates ‚Äî exactly the guard the export already uses, so no connector is drawn to no-geometry. (2) Per the "don't silently hide it" preference, each blank-date Gantt row now shows an explicit, horizontally-sticky **"Unscheduled"** tag, so the empty row reads as intentional rather than a glitch. 0-day *dated* milestones (rows 10, 16) keep their diamond AND their links (`start===end`, both truthy) ‚Äî no regression.

**Verified:** headless **V109** (`ui-audit/verify-unscheduled-deps.mjs`) ‚Äî phantom connectors gone (dep-line count **1**, **zero** `NaN` paths), the one fully-dated link still draws, both blank rows tagged & visible; **proven to FAIL with the guard reverted** (3 dep paths, 3 `NaN` coords, 0 tags). lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **1201** unit tests green ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `Scheduler` lazy chunk intact ¬∑ existing scheduler harnesses still pass (no-crash 6/6, bugfixes ALL PASS, picker 8/8, rowfill-bars PASS). Cloudflare Pages deploy succeeded on the PR.

**Deduped ‚Äî NET-NEW.** Not B380 (Scheduler.jsx nav-state render-crash ‚Äî different file/cause), not B381 (toolbar hidden by a CSS over-reach ‚Äî different surface), and NOT the concurrent **B385** (Site Planner parcel-identify ‚Äî a different module entirely, just an ID collision). No existing Open or Done item covers dependency-connector geometry.

### B385 ‚Äî In-planner "Identify from county GIS" works like the map's Select-parcels tool: parcel lines light up, click to add one or many `[Site Planner]` (feature) ‚Äî owner follow-up to B383  *(owner-dropped from chat 2026-06-22; minted **B385**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/determined-volta-hzjxmc`).** Owner: *"it should work like the map select parcel tool where the parcel boundaries light up and you can easily click to add one or multiple."* B383 shipped the Ôºã Add parcel menu, but its "Identify from county GIS" path was still the old one-at-a-time *click ‚Üí preview card ‚Üí Ôºã Add to plan* flow. Now it mirrors the map's Select-parcels tool:
- **Boundaries light up.** While identify mode is armed, the county parcel OUTLINES render on the aerial via the SAME magenta esri-leaflet `featureLayer` the map uses ‚Äî extracted to a shared `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/parcelDisplay.js` (`makeParcelLayer` + `PARCEL_MINZOOM` + the `ADD_CURSOR`/`REMOVE_CURSOR`), imported by BOTH MapFinder and the planner so there's one source of truth (MapFinder's local copies removed, its now-unused `esri-leaflet` import dropped). The planner mounts the layer on its non-interactive basemap (turning the aerial on for context) and pulls it when identify ends. Outlines load at zoom ‚â•14 like the map, with a "zoom in to see the lines" hint below the status row.
- **Click to add one or many.** A click adds the lot under the cursor straight to the plan (no preview-then-confirm); click more lots to add more; a re-click on a just-added lot toggles it back off; a DRAG pans the map (click-vs-drag resolved in `onUp` by travel, mirroring B310's locked-parcel pattern). The `+` add cursor shows while armed.
- **No regression.** The card morphs from "preview ‚Üí add" into "‚úì Added <lot> ‚Äî here's its appraisal + ‚öñÔ∏é Jurisdiction & road authority"; removed/already-in-plan states read honestly. The standalone preview path + `addIdentifiedParcel`/`identifyAt` were replaced by the single `quickAddAt` add path (reusing the shared `parcelsFromRings` + `queryAtPoint`); added parcels still `locked:true` (B99), multipart-aware (B36c), deduped by a stable per-lot key (CAD OBJECTID, else first-vertex) so the same lot can't be added twice.
- **Gotcha fixed during verify:** a selected parcel's interior is a hit target AND its acreage label (`startAcChip`) stops propagation, so clicks on an existing lot never reached the background handler ‚Äî both `startMoveParcel` and `startAcChip` now defer to a shared `beginIdentifyPress` in identify mode, so clicking anywhere on a lot toggles it.
- **Deduped:** the headline-UX completion of B383 (same feature family), filed as its own ID because it shipped separately. NOT B233 (the map's address‚Üíselect card), NOT B231/B36c (the multipart import it reuses), NOT B370 (the map-display registry migration).
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors ¬∑ **1201 tests** ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact; headless **V108** extended (`ui-audit/verify-b383-add-parcel.mjs`, **22/22**) ‚Äî adds a MOCKED-HCAD Scenario C that drives the real click‚Üíquery‚Üíadd pipeline: click adds (Parcels ¬∑ 1), click another adds (Parcels ¬∑ 2 ‚Äî add MANY), re-click toggles off (Parcels ¬∑ 1, "Removed"); plus the armed status row, the + cursor, and the lit-outline arming. (The live county host is CORS-blocked in the sandbox, hence the route-fulfil mock.)

### B383 ‚Äî "Add parcel" action in the Parcel left-hand panel `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; minted **B383** ‚Äî highest B# across both files was B382)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + merged the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/determined-volta-hzjxmc`).** The only obvious way to add parcels lived back on the map (MapFinder); once you were in the Site Planner there was no single front-door for "add more land to this plan." Now the Parcel panel leads with a primary **Ôºã Add parcel** control (accent chip, full-width, under the `PARCELS ¬∑ N` header) that opens an `AnchoredMenu` ‚Äî the same portal flyout the right-rail Boundary menu uses ‚Äî with the add methods in priority order:
- **Identify from county GIS (headline)** ‚Äî arms the existing `identifyMode` (`setIdentifyMode(true)`) ‚Üí click-to-identify ‚Üí result card ‚Üí `addIdentifiedParcel` ‚Üí `setParcels((a) => [...a, ...pcs])`. **Origin gate:** when `origin` is null the menu shows the disabled copy ("Identify needs a georeferenced plan. Bring a parcel in from the map to enable it.") instead of a live control.
- **Draw a new boundary** ‚Äî `selectTool("parcel")` (the existing parcel draw tool), reachable from the Parcel panel now, not only the right-rail Boundary submenu. Needs no `origin`, so it stays enabled regardless ‚Äî the fallback when there's no GIS frame.
- **Consolidation:** the standalone `üîç Identify parcel` toggle at the panel bottom was folded into the menu's "Identify from county GIS" path ‚Äî no two entry points for the same action. The result card, `Ôºã Add to plan`, and `‚öñÔ∏é Jurisdiction & road authority` behavior are unchanged; an in-panel "Identifying ‚Äî click a spot (Esc to stop)" status row is the off-switch (so is Esc, which already cleared `identifyMode`/`identifyRes`). The bottom block now renders only when `identifyMode || identifyRes`.
- **Reuse, not fork** (same single-pipeline rule as B232/B233): `addIdentifiedParcel`, `setParcels`, `identifyMode`, and the parcel draw tool already existed ‚Äî this surfaces + groups them, no new add pipeline. Added parcels keep `locked:true` (lock-by-default, B99). Salience: the accent fill (`--canvas-selection`) reads as a primary action, distinct from the per-parcel rows and the (only-when-armed-accent) Merge button.
- **Code:** `SitePlanner.jsx` ‚Äî new `addParcelMenu` state + `addParcelAnchor` ref; the Ôºã Add parcel button + `AnchoredMenu` at the top of the Parcel `Section`; the bottom identify block rewritten to drop the duplicate toggle and gate on `identifyMode || identifyRes`; empty-state copy now points at Ôºã Add parcel.
- **Deduped:** net-new. NOT B233 (the *map's* address‚Üíselect+info card), NOT B231/B36c (the multipart-import pipeline it reuses via `addIdentifiedParcel`), NOT B99/B100 (the lock/active model it inherits).
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors ¬∑ **1201 tests** ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact; headless **V108** (`ui-audit/verify-b383-add-parcel.mjs`, **14/14** ‚Äî primary-salience accent bg, both menu methods, Identify arm/disarm, Draw arms the parcel tool, toggle consolidated away, origin-null disabled-copy + Draw-still-works, 0 app errors). The deferred "Add by address" stretch is filed as **B384** (Open) ‚Äî the geocode logic lives in MapFinder and needs a clean shared extraction, explicitly out of this pass per the request.

### B382 ‚Äî Parcels in the Fort Bend / Harris seam "take forever to load" then "can't be clicked" while a county CAD host is down `[Site Planner / Map]` (bug)  *(owner-reported from chat 2026-06-22 ‚Äî "Fort Bend, Cadd ‚Ä¶ isn't working ‚Ä¶ takes forever to load, and then ‚Ä¶ it doesn't work"; minted **B382** ‚Äî highest B# across both files was B381 (concurrent main PR #285 took B381 for the Schedule-toolbar restore while this was in flight); a recurrence of B244/B137 territory, deduped against them)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/confident-meitner-j3tv25`).** Two symptoms, ONE upstream cause confirmed with LIVE probes (not guessed): **FBCAD (`gis.fbcad.org`) was down again** ‚Äî timed out / slow-503 at 12‚Äì15s from both Node and headless Chromium ‚Äî while HCAD and the statewide TxGIO host were up (the exact B244 condition, recurred 2026-06-22). The two existing mitigations didn't fully cover it:
- **Symptom 1 ("forever to load") ‚Äî the DISPLAY layer had no timeout.** B244's 8s `AbortController` lives only in `fetchJson` (the click/identify path). The visible purple parcel outlines are an esri-leaflet `featureLayer` (`makeParcelLayer`, `MapFinder.jsx`) that bypasses `fetchJson`, so when FBCAD hangs the outline layer spins with no recovery. **Fix:** `addDisplay` now arms an 8s hang-guard for a real county layer (armed on the layer's `requeststart`, disarmed by `load`, so a below-outline-zoom view never false-flags); on timeout/`requesterror` it pulls the dead layer, records the host as failing (feeds the breaker so CLICKS skip it too), and leans on the always-present statewide TxGIO outlines for that ground ‚Äî keeping what you SEE == what you can SELECT (the B137 rule). The **statewide layer is exempt** from the guard: a slow statewide outline is allowed to finish rather than be pulled (a slow outline beats no outline ‚Äî that "took a while but worked" wait IS this layer; pulling it would leave nothing to see OR click).
- **Symptom 2 ("can't click the lots") ‚Äî every click waited on the hung host.** `identifyParcelDetailed` used `Promise.allSettled`, which waits for EVERY candidate ‚Äî so even though TxGIO answered, each click stalled the full 8s on FBCAD's timeout before resolving, reading as "clicking does nothing." **Fix:** new `identifyParcelEager` (`lib/arcgis.js`) resolves the moment the FIRST source returns a lot (real CADs are queried before the statewide fallback, so a healthy county still answers first), and feeds the circuit breaker the full per-source health via an `onSettled` callback once the slow ones finish (so the next click skips the dead host). The no-hit verdict still waits for all to settle, so "reached a server, no parcel" vs "couldn't reach any server" stay honest (B245). `handleClick` + the address-search `selectParcelAt` both switched to it.
- **Tests/verify:** +3 unit (`test/arcgis.test.js`) ‚Äî eager returns the hit WITHOUT tripping/awaiting the hung source's timeout then feeds the breaker for both; the no-hit path reports responded/errors honestly; a real-CAD hit is preferred over the statewide one. Full suite **1201 green** (unchanged after merging main ‚Äî PR #285 added a Playwright harness, no vitest tests), build green, esri-leaflet 3.0.19 `requeststart`/`load`/`requesterror` event contract confirmed present. Headless **V107** (`ui-audit/verify-parcel-resilience.mjs`) ‚Äî run against the LIVE failing hosts tonight: select mode loads with **0 uncaught errors**, the map stays alive through the full FBCAD hang window (no freeze), and a click resolves in a **bounded 8.1s** (the AbortController cap on the worst-case no-hit path) instead of the open-ended 12‚Äì30s+ the raw hung hosts give. (A real lot with a fast source resolves in ~1‚Äì4s, per the unit tests.)
- **Forward note:** tonight the statewide TxGIO *query* op was itself slow (its metadata was fast, but spatial queries timed out 30s+ from Node) ‚Äî so when FBCAD is down AND TxGIO query is degraded, even the universal fallback is slow. This fix makes the click *bounded and non-freezing* and keeps see==click, but it can't make a slow statewide host fast. If TxGIO-query slowness becomes chronic, the roadmap **GIS layer caching** item (stale-while-revalidate parcel outlines) is the durable answer.

### B381 ‚Äî Restore the missing Schedule toolbar controls (regression) `[Schedule]` (bug)  *(arrived as cross-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; first filed B380, renumbered **B381** ‚Äî concurrent main PR #284 took B380 for the Schedule render-crash fix while this was in flight, so B381 is the real next free ID; net-new, the completion of B203)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V106, 15/15) + merged the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/vigilant-newton-rovl4l`).** Owner: opening the Schedule module on any project, the Gantt/timeline toolbar was reduced to the **single floating Columns button** above the grid ‚Äî timeline zoom, Contacts, Export (PDF/print), Version History, the Grid/Split/Gantt view switcher, Automation, Settings and the Review toggle were all gone.
- **Root cause ‚Äî hidden, NOT deleted (read the code + git, not the brief).** The Schedule module embeds the standalone sequence app (`public/sequence/index.html`) in an **iframe**; the shell's Row-1 breadcrumb takes over project nav, so the sequence app hides its own duplicated nav when `.in-iframe` (the embed app even documents this at the postMessage bridge: "runs in an iframe with its OWN header ‚Ä¶ hidden"). But the rule was `html.in-iframe .app-header { display:none }` ‚Äî it hid the **ENTIRE** header, and the whole action toolbar (view switch, Review, Format, Zoom, Export, Save, Version History, Contacts, Automation, Settings) lives inside that same `.app-header`. So every tool vanished; only the in-grid **Columns** button (rendered inside `GridView`, not the header) survived ‚Äî exactly the reported symptom. All handlers were intact the whole time; a pure CSS over-reach. Confirmed hidden vs deleted (the fix is re-show, not re-add).
- **Fix (`public/sequence/index.html`, 2 small edits).** Narrowed the `.in-iframe` rule to hide ONLY the genuinely-duplicated branding/nav ‚Äî the logo (gave it a new `.hdr-logo` class), the Dashboard/Projects toggle (`.hdr-mode`) and the project picker (`.hdr-project`), all of which the shell breadcrumb already provides (bridged via the B203 postMessage nav). The rest of `.app-header` (view switcher + Review + the full action group) is visible again, in its original position, with its original handlers ‚Äî no re-wiring needed because nothing was ever removed.
- **Authoritative restored set (from the actual `.app-header` markup, not the brief):** Grid/Split/Gantt view switcher ¬∑ Review toggle ¬∑ Format ¬∑ timeline Zoom ‚àí/%/+ (shown in split/gantt) ¬∑ Export (PDF¬∑Print Exhibit / Web Snapshot) ¬∑ Save indicator ¬∑ Version History ¬∑ Contacts ¬∑ Automation ¬∑ Settings. **The brief's speculative "fit-to-view / today-date-jump / status-filter" controls do NOT exist in this app** ‚Äî none were ever shipped, so none were restored (per "do not rebuild from a guessed list").
- **Verified working, not just present (V106, `ui-audit/verify-schedule-toolbar.mjs`, 15/15 headless):** toolbar visible in the iframe; logo/mode/project correctly hidden; every action control renders; switching to Gantt reveals the zoom controls; zoom-in changes the % (17‚Üí33, proving the handler is wired, not a no-op); Contacts opens its panel. New Playwright regression harness so this can't silently regress again. **Site + Markup checked ‚Äî NOT affected** (they aren't iframes; they feed their toolbars through the shell's `toolbarContent` slot, which `.in-iframe` can't reach). lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **1186 tests** green (1198 after merging main's B380) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `Scheduler` lazy chunk intact.
- **Dedup:** NET-NEW, the **completion of B203** (DONE). B203 diagnosed this same `.in-iframe .app-header{display:none}` hide and bridged the **nav half** (project picker + Dashboard/Projects toggle) up to the shell breadcrumb ‚Äî but the **action-toolbar half** the same rule hid was never re-homed and stayed dark; B381 restores it. NOT a dup of main's **B380** (a separate intermittent Schedule *render-crash* race in `Scheduler.jsx` ‚Äî different file, different cause, shipped the same day), nor B341 (chrome hardcoded-color/token regressions), nor the Markup `toolbarContent` relocation.

### B380 ‚Äî Intermittent render crash on Schedule load ("Cannot read properties of undefined"), caught by the workspace ErrorBoundary `[Scheduler]` (bug)  *(arrived as cross-chat diagnosis brief "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; minted **B380** ‚Äî highest B# across both files was B379; net-new, deduped vs B221/B239 stale-chunk + B315 undo-race)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V105) the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/relaxed-dijkstra-sokq4a`).** Opening the Schedule module would intermittently show the workspace ErrorBoundary's **non-chunk "Try again / Reload page"** fallback with a `<pre>` of the "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '‚Ä¶')" form; **Try again** (a state reset, not a reload) recovered cleanly. Confirmed NOT the stale-chunk family (B221/B239) ‚Äî the boundary took the genuine-render-crash path and a pure re-render fixed it.
- **Root cause ‚Äî reproduced, not guessed.** The Schedule shell embeds the standalone Gantt in an iframe (`/sequence/`) and bridges that app's project list to the shared Row-1 breadcrumb over postMessage (`planar:nav-state`). The shell derives the breadcrumb's current project with `projects.find((p) => p.id === activeId)` (the brief's #1 candidate, `Scheduler.jsx`). The embedded app builds its list as `Object.values(data.projects).map(p => ({id, name}))`; while ITS own data is still loading it can transiently emit a **sparse / not-yet-resolved** list containing an `undefined`/null entry. One such entry makes `p.id` throw **inside the Scheduler's render** ‚Üí caught by the workspace boundary. A pure re-render recovers because the steady-state list is well-formed, which is exactly why it presents as intermittent. The brief's other candidates (`AppHeader`/`ProjectBreadcrumb` `p.id`/`p.name`, `useProfile/displayName` in `Shell.jsx`) were each verified **already null-safe** (signed-in + real-iframe headless repro, 0 crashes); the deref that actually throws is the Scheduler's `.find`.
- **Fix ‚Äî guard at the source (not optional-chaining at every call site).** New pure, unit-tested `src/workspaces/scheduler/lib/navState.js` (matches the B315 pure-module precedent): `parseNavState(message)` validates source/type and routes through `sanitizeProjects(list)`, which coerces the inbound list to an array of plain `{id, name}` objects ‚Äî **dropping the null/undefined/primitive entries that are the only values that throw on a later `p.id`/`p.name` read**; `deriveCurrentProject(projects, activeId, section)` never throws and never returns `undefined` (Dashboard/reports ‚Üí null; a stale activeId not in the list ‚Üí null, the empty/loader state). `Scheduler.jsx` routes both the inbound message and the current-project derivation through these. One **data-entry guard** in the shared `ProjectBreadcrumb` (`(controlled ? controlledProjects : internalProjects).filter(Boolean)`) hardens its own `p.id`/`p.name` map for any controlled caller. The race is **removed** so the (now-rare) genuine crash still stays visible ‚Äî NOT widened `isChunkLoadError`, NOT a blanket ErrorBoundary auto-retry (per the brief).
- **Behaviour preserved.** For the real embedded app's well-formed `{id,name}` payload the output is identical to the previous inline logic ‚Äî only the not-ready/malformed shapes are made safe.
- **Tests/verify:** +12 unit (`test/schedulerNavState.test.js`) ‚Äî sanitize drops the throwing entries / keeps real objects, parse validates + sanitizes, derive is null-safe on the race/Dashboard/stale-id paths. Full suite **1198 green**, lint **0 errors**, build green, `Scheduler` lazy chunk intact. Headless **V105** (`ui-audit/verify-scheduler-no-crash.mjs`, 9/9) ‚Äî the first-render race shows the loader/empty (no boundary), a deliberately malformed nav-state doesn't crash the header and the good projects still render, signed-in-with-late-profile doesn't crash; **proven to FAIL (boundary + page error) with the guard off**, so it's a real regression net. Existing `verify-schedule-picker.mjs` still **8/8** (no regression to the bridge/switch/dashboard).

### B378 ‚Äî Sheet labels are garbage on text-dense (general-notes / specs) sheets `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as cross-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; minted **B378** ‚Äî highest B# across both files was B373, so B378 is the real next free ID; extends/hardens B266 + the shared `sheetMeta` reader, deduped against B266/B348)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/ecstatic-maxwell-6y8aq1`).** A structural general-notes set labelled "atrociously": body boilerplate shown AS the sheet title ("‚Ä¶reproduced or used without‚Ä¶", "CJ DENOTES‚Ä¶"), the same cross-referenced number on several rows ("Structural ¬∑ S202" √ó4), and a mix of those with plain "Sheet N." Root cause: every read was tuned for a normal drawing (sparse plan + one dense title-block strip); a notes sheet (wall-to-wall text, no drawing area) violates all of those assumptions at once. Fixed at the shared reader so BOTH the Markup sidebar and the Stitcher benefit:
- **Title scorer was backwards (`readSheetTitle`, `sheetMeta.js`).** It scored `lineHeight √ó letterCount`, which *rewards* long sentences ‚Äî so the copyright block and a legend row outscored the real short title. Re-weighted to **prefer font height, with a small word/letter cap** (a title is a few words, not a sentence): candidates over 7 words / 48 letters are dropped, height dominates the score, letters only break ties. Added a **boilerplate filter** (`property of` / `reproduced` / `copyright` / `denotes` / `continued` / "may not be ‚Ä¶") to the existing skip list. Net: "GENERAL NOTES" wins over the boilerplate it used to lose to.
- **The "band-first" guarantee was fictional for the number (`readSheetMeta`).** `parseSheetNumber` ran over the whole-page joined string, and its `(?:no\.?|#)?` is optional, so a body cross-reference ("SEE DWG S202") was grabbed as THIS sheet's number ‚Äî identical on every sheet that referenced it. Made the number read **title-block-zone-scoped**: from the detected band, or ‚Äî when a dense notes sheet defeats the density-based band detector ‚Äî from the right/bottom edge **strip** where title blocks live; only a non-dense sheet may fall back to the whole-page read. So the sheet's OWN number ("S-001") is read and the body cross-ref ("S202") is excluded.
- **`reconstructLines` merged across the full page width.** Two items sharing a baseline but ~900 pt apart (a title-block label and a far-left body note) were joined into one "line", which then failed the title filter as "too wordy" ‚Äî losing the title entirely on a notes sheet. Now **splits a row on a large horizontal gap** (generous threshold ‚Äî far bigger than any intra-phrase word gap, so "MATCH LINE ‚Ä¶ SEE SHEET C-6" still joins), so a title-block title can't bleed into a body line.
- **Sidebar gate too weak (`DocReview.jsx`).** The old `hasReal = sheetNumber || titleBlock` let a bare band authorize a title-derived label. Replaced with a `trustedTitle` gate: a title is surfaced only when it came from a real band, OR is corroborated by a zone-read number, OR the sheet is a recognized text page (general notes/specs) ‚Äî else it falls back cleanly to the discipline item, then the number, then "Sheet N." Singles now show the real title ("GENERAL NOTES ¬∑ S-001") instead of a generic discipline label.
- **Identical adjacent numbers = a misread ‚Üí drop it.** New pure `markAdjacentDuplicateNumbers` (`sheetGroups.js`) clears a sheet number that repeats on an adjacent page (a real set never prints the same # twice in a row) and lowers confidence, so the no-band fallback can't produce a run of identical wrong rows. Applied in both the Markup consumer (`DocReview` `orderedMeta`) and the Stitcher path (`readSheets`).
- **Tests:** +9 unit (`sheetMeta`/`sheetGroups`/`sheetRead`): the title scorer ignores boilerplate, the number is zone-scoped (reads S-001 not the S202 cross-ref), `textDense` is flagged, the dedup clears adjacent duplicates. Full suite **1153 green**, lint 0 errors, build green, `DocReview` lazy chunk intact. Verified **V104**.

### B379 ‚Äî False auto-calibration ("¬∑‚âà") on general-notes / specs sheets `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as cross-chat "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; minted **B379** ‚Äî next free ID after B378; relates to B267 `statedCalibration`, deduped against it)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/ecstatic-maxwell-6y8aq1`).** On the same structural set, pure-text notes sheets (S00x) showed "¬∑‚âà" (auto-calibrated) even though they carry no plan scale, while sheet 1 correctly read "NOT TO SCALE." Root cause: `parseSheetScale` matches a scale-looking string **anywhere** in the dense notes text, and `statedCalibration` only gated on a standard plot size ‚Äî so a 36√ó24 general-notes sheet with a stray "1\"=20'" in its body auto-calibrated. Auto-calibrating a non-drawing sheet silently mis-scales it (crash-severity per "never auto-guess" ‚Äî a wrong calibration poisons every downstream takeoff).
- **Fix ‚Äî classify the sheet, then gate.** `readSheetMeta` now computes a **`textDense`** flag (a general-notes / specifications / legend sheet): true when the title is a notes/specs/legend/index title, or the drawing area is saturated with sentence-like prose (‚â•10 lines of ‚â•6 words ‚Äî plans carry only short labels). `statedCalibration` **returns 0 when `textDense`**, so such a sheet is left uncalibrated ("not present" ‚âÝ "couldn't parse", per B267) rather than silently scaled. A real plan sheet (sparse drawing-area text, a stated scale in its title block) still auto-calibrates exactly as before.
- **Tests:** +1 unit (`sheetRead`: a textDense sheet with a stated scale on a standard plot ‚Üí 0) plus the `textDense` true/false assertions under B378. Verified **V104** (the same harness asserts the notes sheet shows no "¬∑‚âà" while the plan sheet still does).

### B374 ‚Äî Area measurements can't be selected by their filled interior (dead-centre hit target) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-reported from chat 2026-06-22 ‚Äî "no easy way to delete this little triangle ‚Ä¶ insane"; minted **B374** ‚Äî highest B# across both files was B373; batch B374‚ÄìB377)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/youthful-mccarthy-xjcotz`).** Michael's "triangle" was an **uncalibrated Area measurement** ‚Äî its value label falls back to the literal string **"set scale"** when a sheet has no feet-per-page-unit (`measureLabel`, `lib/takeoff.js`). Clicking the triangle's body to select it (the natural first step to delete it) did nothing.
- **Root cause:** in `DocReview.jsx` `hitTest`, **rect/cloud** got a point-in-bbox interior test (B33) but **area fell through to the generic measures branch ‚Äî nearest vertex/segment only**. The Area polygon renders FILLED (`fill="#0e749022"`), so it reads as a solid clickable object while its **interior was a dead hit target**; only a click on the thin edge/a vertex registered, and at low zoom (e.g. 26%) that band is nearly impossible to hit ‚Üí the measurement felt undeletable.
- **Fix:** added an `m.kind === "area"` branch to `hitTest` ‚Äî `pointInPoly(p, pts)` returns d=0 for a click anywhere inside the polygon. `pointInPoly` is now **EXPORTED** from `lib/takeoff.js` (the same ray-cast already behind `centroidOf`, not a 2nd copy; handles concave outlines). A <3-pt / degenerate area still selects by its edge or vertex via the fallback (now incl. the **closing edge**, which the old branch skipped ‚Äî also applied to perimeter). Added a **smaller-shape-wins tie-break** on interior (d===0) hits so a small markup sitting on a big filled area isn't swallowed by the area underneath.
- **Verified (V104):** new `ui-audit/verify-delete-markup.mjs` **10/10** (chromium-1228, 0 page errors) ‚Äî draws an uncalibrated "set scale" Area, then: a **centroid click selects it** (stroke 2‚Üí3), a click **outside does NOT** select (hit test stays specific), and Delete removes it. No regression: V72 viewer **13/13**, B300‚ÄìB302 green. lint 0 ¬∑ 1144 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B375 ‚Äî No on-canvas delete control for a selected markup (deletion was keyboard-only) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature) ‚Äî pairs with B374
`[x]` **Same session/branch.** Even once a markup is selected, removing it was **Delete/Backspace only**, plus a "Delete selected" button buried in the (collapsible) Takeoff panel ‚Äî nothing on the canvas where the user is looking, so deletion wasn't discoverable. Added a clear **√ó button anchored to the selected markup's top-right**, rendered OUTSIDE the `pointerEvents:none` overlay (like the inline text/calibrate editors) so it takes its own click; `stopPropagation` on pointer-down keeps that click from starting a pan/draw underneath. Runs the same one-undo-frame path as the Delete key (`pushHistory` ‚Üí filter out the id ‚Üí `setSel(null)`). Hidden while editing text / calibrating. Theme tokens only (`--danger-text` fill + `--on-accent` glyph ‚Äî AA in both themes; no raw hex). Verified in V104 (√ó appears on the selection and deletes the markup).

### B376 ‚Äî Per-item delete in the Takeoff list (all markups) + a deletable measure list in the Stitcher `[Doc Review / Markup + Stitch]` (feature)
`[x]` **Same session/branch.** The hit-test-independent removal path, so anything can always be taken off ‚Äî even on a dense sheet or when not in Select mode.
- **Markup:** the Takeoff "This sheet" list now lists **every markup** (measures + redline rect/cloud + text notes), not just measurements; each row is click-to-select **and carries its own √ó delete**. Empty-state copy ‚Üí **"Nothing on this sheet yet."** (the old "No measurements yet" would mislead now that redlines/notes are listed).
- **Stitcher:** it previously had **no way to delete a committed measure at all** ‚Äî the keyboard handler only trimmed a mid-draw vertex, so a stray "set scale" area on the stitched canvas was permanently stuck. Added a per-measure list under the stitched takeoff totals, each row with a √ó delete (undoable via the Stitcher's existing `{measures, ftPerUnit}` history snapshot). Full on-canvas select-on-stitch is deliberately **not** built (it never existed there; the list closes the gap without that larger refactor).
- **Verified:** Markup rows in V104 (row √ó deletes independent of canvas selection); new `ui-audit/verify-stitch-delete-measure.mjs` **ALL PASS** ‚Äî draw an Area over the auto-aligned base sheet ‚Üí it's listed with a √ó ‚Üí the √ó removes it (0 page errors). Updated the B300‚ÄìB302 harness's empty-state copy assertion to the new text.

### B377 ‚Äî Scanned-sheet OCR worker threw "logger is not a function" on every page (error storm) `[Doc Review / Stitch + OCR]` (bug)  *(found while headless-verifying B374‚ÄìB376; minted **B377**)*
`[x]` **Same session/branch.** Surfaced by the Stitcher harness runs: a page with no text layer triggers the B352 Tesseract OCR fallback, and the worker threw **`TypeError: logger is not a function`** repeatedly (13√ó in one B300‚ÄìB302 run). **Root cause:** `defaultMakeWorker` (`lib/ocr.js`) passed `logger` ‚Äî always `undefined`, since no caller supplies one ‚Äî straight into `createWorker({ ‚Ä¶, logger })`. Tesseract invokes `logger` on the **main thread** for every progress packet the worker posts back, and an explicit `logger: undefined` **clobbers Tesseract's own default no-op**, so each message threw. **Fix:** default `logger = () => {}` (still injectable for a real progress UI later). Confirmed gone in the unminified dev build (0 `PAGEERROR`) and across all Stitcher harnesses ‚Äî **B300‚ÄìB302 uncaught JS errors 13 ‚Üí 0**. OCR unit tests unaffected (they inject the recognizer, bypassing the default worker). NB the WASM core + English model still load from the jsDelivr CDN at runtime; only the logger crash was in scope.

### B373 ‚Äî Restore the compact, app-wide cloud-sync indicator in AppHeader Row 1 `[App Shell / all workspaces]` (feature)  *(arrived as cross-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-22 ‚Äî a provisional label colliding with the older NEW-1 open-file intent + the B365/B372 NEW-1 markers/delete items; first filed **B366**, renumbered **B373** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (PRs #277/#278) took B366‚ÄìB372 (GIS Site Analysis resilience + source registry, delete-persistence) while this was in flight, so B373 is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND shipped + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/vibrant-dijkstra-i3ddzg`).** The small save-status indicator the owner expected in the header had drifted away: the Site Planner showed a **text** chip ("Synced ‚úì"), Document Review had moved its chip out of Row 1 down to the Row-2 Reviews control (B358), and the Stitcher carried its own ‚Äî so there was no single, compact, app-wide indicator, and the owner's design intent (a **cloud glyph + check, no "Saved" text**) had been lost. Worse, the failure mode that made it "disappear" was a silent drop-to-blank on error. Restored as **one shared component, app-wide, that goes LOUD on a failed write and never silently vanishes.**
- **New shared `src/shared/ui/CloudSyncBadge.jsx`** ‚Äî a compact cloud glyph (no text label) driven by the **normalized `saveState` every workspace already computes** (so it reflects the REAL cloud write, never an optimistic always-green). Pure exported `cloudBadgeView(state)` is the whole truth-table ‚Üí glyph + color + loud/actionable flags. States, each visually distinct (not text-dependent): **synced** = quiet green cloud-check (the resting default); **saving** = amber cloud-up, pulsing; **offline** = amber cloud-pause (saved on device, will sync); **error** = **LOUD** red cloud-slash + a red ring, click ‚Üí popover with what-failed + **Retry now**; **local** = muted "device" glyph (signed out); **null** = nothing (no project/doc loaded ‚Äî legitimately empty, not a hidden error).
- **The crash-severity guardrail in code (the headline ask):** the badge wraps itself in a small error boundary (`CloudBadgeBoundary`) that, if the badge ever throws while rendering, shows the **loud error glyph instead of returning null** ‚Äî the exact "silent unmount" that made the old indicator vanish. Keyed on `state` so a transient throw clears once inputs change, and so it tracks live state across module/project switches without going stale.
- **`AppHeader` renders it once, in Row 1, from `saveState`** ‚Äî so it's genuinely shared across Site / Schedule / Markup. New optional `onRetrySave` + `saveDetail` props let a workspace supply the retry action and a tailored popover message (e.g. "reload to merge" for a conflict, where a blind retry is wrong).
- **Site Planner:** retired the text `plannerSaveSlot` chip (the compact badge replaces it); wires `onRetrySave={retryCloudSave}` (suppressed for a conflict, which needs a reload) + a conflict `saveDetail`. The loud failure **banners** (cloudSaveFailed / cloudConflict) stay as the can't-miss interrupt; the badge is the always-present at-a-glance state.
- **Document Review + Stitcher:** new pure exported `docSaveState(status, signedIn, idle)` (in `usePersistence.js`) maps the doc-review raw status onto the normalized vocabulary ‚Äî **a failed/conflicting write ‚Üí "error" (LOUD)**, idle (nothing loaded) ‚Üí null (no cry-wolf at rest), signed-in ‚Üí synced, signed-out-with-content ‚Üí local. DR feeds it to AppHeader; the **Stitcher has its own chrome (no AppHeader), so it renders its own `CloudSyncBadge`** in its toolbar ‚Äî same glyph, same loudness, everywhere.
- **Retired the now-redundant Row-2 ReviewsBar save chip** (its `chipFor` + `SaveIcon`) so a module never shows two competing save indicators ‚Äî the app-wide Row-1 badge is the single source. ReviewsBar is now purely the filing control. Removed the obsolete `test/reviewsBadge.test.js`; its "failed-write-is-loud / no-cry-wolf" contract migrated to the new badge test.
- **Tests:** new `test/cloudSyncBadge.test.js` (13) locks the contract ‚Äî **a failed save is never visually identical to "all good"** (distinct glyph + color + loud flag), null shows nothing, the crashed fallback is loud, and the doc raw‚Üínormalized mapping keeps failures loud. Full suite green post-merge (lint 0 errors, build green; `SitePlannerApp`/`DocReview` lazy chunks intact).
- **Verified (V103):** two headless harnesses (chromium-1194, 0 page errors). `ui-audit/verify-new1-cloud-badge.mjs` **20/20** ‚Äî every state present and visually distinct, error is loud + click-to-Retry fires the handler, null renders nothing, and **a crashed badge falls back to the loud glyph (does NOT vanish)**. `ui-audit/verify-new1-header-integration.mjs` **5/5** ‚Äî the badge is wired into the **live** Row-1 header with a project open, shows the honest on-device state logged-out (not a fake green "synced"), and **survives switching modules** (Site ‚Üí Markup hides it when nothing's loaded ‚Üí back to Site restores it). Screenshots in `ui-audit/screens/new1-*.png`.

### B372 ‚Äî A deleted site reappears (delete not persisting) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-22 from chat as "NEW-1"; first filed B366, renumbered **B372** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (PR #277) took B366‚ÄìB371 (GIS Site Analysis resilience + source registry) while this was in flight, so B372 is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + pushed the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/cool-ride-pm0m2l`).** Deleting the HOLLISTER site brought it back ‚Äî on a hard reload (path A) **and** mid-session with no reload (path B). Crash-severity per the silent-failure principle: a delete that looks successful in the UI but doesn't stick is silent data divergence.
- **ONE root cause for both paths (so kept as one item ‚Äî the owner said split only if path B were independent).** The site you delete from the map is, in the common case, the one you had **open**: opening it sets `activeSiteId`, and "Back to map" only flips the view ‚Äî the `SitePlanner` stays **mounted (hidden)**, keyed to that `activeSiteId`. Deleting it calls `deleteSiteGroup` ‚Üí `setActiveSiteId(null)`, which **unmounts** that planner; its unmount cleanup `persistOrDrop()` (and the `beforeunload`/visibility flush + any queued debounced autosave) then ran `saveSite()` for the just-deleted id, **re-writing the row**. (a) The existing in-planner `deletedSelfRef` guard only covered the *single-plan* delete from inside the planner, NOT the map's whole-site delete. (b) **Path B:** the resurrected record sits in the local store and re-surfaces on the next `refreshSites()` (any map‚Üîplan nav). (c) **Path A:** on reload, `pullCloud`'s heal-the-split sees a local-only row the cloud is missing and **re-pushes it to the cloud**, making the resurrection permanent. This also explains "maybe it's just that project" ‚Äî it was the one he had open; sites never opened this session had no mounted planner to resurrect them.
- **Fix 1 ‚Äî kill the resurrection at the one chokepoint (`lib/storage.js`).** A per-tab session tombstone `recentlyDeleted` (Set): `deleteSite` adds the id; `saveSite` refuses to **re-create a deleted, currently-absent row** (`if (!existing && recentlyDeleted.has(id)) return false`). EVERY resurrection path (unmount persist, beforeunload/visibility flush, debounced autosave, a cross-tab storage echo) funnels through `saveSite`, so this closes all of them ‚Äî not just the one the bug surfaced. A normal edit-save (record still present) and a brand-new site (id never deleted) are untouched; `stageLegacySite`/`importOneSiteToCloud` lift the tombstone so a deliberate same-id re-import still works; it's per-tab and clears on reload (by then the delete has settled).
- **Fix 2 ‚Äî make a real cloud-delete failure LOUD (never a phantom success).** `cloudDelete` now uses `.delete(‚Ä¶).select("id")` so a 0-row no-op (already gone, or an ownership/RLS mismatch) is distinguishable from a real removal, and a thrown/4xx error returns `{ok:false}`. `deleteSite` **returns** that promise; `deleteSiteGroup`/`deletePlan` AWAIT it, and on a genuine error show a red **"Couldn't delete ‚Ä¶ from the cloud ‚Äî it may reappear when you reload"** banner and **re-pull** so the list reflects the honest truth instead of a fake success. (Decision: a 0-row delete is treated as success, not an alarm ‚Äî a local-only site never pushed to the cloud legitimately removes 0 rows, so alarming on 0-row would cry wolf; RLS also means a row a user can *see* is a row they *can* delete. We alarm only on an actual error, which is the silent case that mattered. This refines the owner's literal "0 rows = failure" into the safer rule that won't false-alarm.)
- **Why the ownership-mismatch theory didn't pan out as the cause (checked first, as asked):** RLS `select` is `auth.uid() = user_id`, so a row visible in the list is owned by the current user ‚Äî a foreign-owned row would never appear to be deleted in the first place, and wouldn't return from `cloudList` on reload either. The real mechanism was the mounted-planner resurrection above. `cloudDelete` is still hardened to surface a true permission/network error loudly.
- **Verified (V102):** new `ui-audit/verify-b372-delete-durable.mjs` ‚Äî boots into the planner for HOLLISTER, returns to the map (planner stays mounted), deletes it, and asserts on the **store source-of-truth** + the rendered list: **6/6** (Chromium, logged-out, 0 page errors), incl. not-resurrected immediately, no mid-session reappearance after a list refresh, and still-gone after reload (Schiel preserved). **Proven to have teeth ‚Äî with the guard disabled the harness FAILS exactly as reported (`store ids = [s2,s1]`, card returns, survives reload).** Plus **9 new unit tests** (`test/deletePersistence.test.js`: `interpretDelete` row-count/error matrix + the end-to-end resurrection guard). **lint 0 ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp`/`DocReview` lazy chunks intact** (full suite re-run green post-merge).
- **Note for the signed-in tail:** the loud-banner path on a *genuine cloud-delete error* can't be driven logged-out (the sandbox CORS-blocks Supabase auth); its logic is unit-locked (`interpretDelete`) and the local resurrection (the actual bug) is fully headless-verified. A one-line signed-in confirm is noted in VERIFICATION V102, not owed from Michael.

### B368 ‚Äî Repoint Oil&Gas wells + Pipelines to the authoritative statewide RRC source; pin the Wetlands tier `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (bug ‚Äî data correctness / false-clean)  *(arrived as coworker-brief "NEW-3" 2026-06-21; minted **B368** ‚Äî highest B# across both files was B365, batch B366‚ÄìB369)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-22 (branch `claude/nice-cori-ghm37q`).** A live walkthrough on planyr.io found the Wells/Pipelines screen pointed at a **Harris-County GIS republication** (`www.gis.hctx.net/arcgishcpid/‚Ä¶/TXRRC/*`), ~99.8% incomplete outside Harris ‚Äî a **silent false-clean**: Chambers County read **14 wells vs the true 8,014**, Grand Port / Mont Belvieu **7 vs ~1,469**. Reporting "no wells" on a Mont Belvieu industrial site is a correctness/liability failure.
- **Fix:** repointed both to the **Railroad Commission of Texas statewide service** `https://gis.rrc.texas.gov/server/rest/services/rrc_public/RRC_Public_Viewer_Srvs/MapServer` ‚Äî **wells = layer 1** (`API, SYMNUM, GIS_SYMBOL_DESCRIPTION, GIS_WELL_NUMBER`), **pipelines = layer 13** (`OPERATOR, COMMODITY_DESCRIPTION, DIAMETER, STATUS, SYSTEM_NAME, COUNTY_NAME`). Summarizers + detail updated to the RRC columns. The retired hctx endpoints are gone from the screen.
- **Wetlands:** still on the USFWS **"Test"** folder (the production `Wetlands/MapServer` root has an empty `/layers` + 500s on `/query`, so there's no drop-in production query endpoint). Registered as a **`tier: "monitored-exception"`** in the new registry (B369) with a reason ‚Äî acknowledged + machine-tracked, not a silent `/Test/` URL.
- **Endpoints now live in the registry** (B369), not inline. `minScale 100000` gates RRC *tile rendering* only, not `/query`, so the screen is unaffected.
- **Verify:** the live coverage fixtures (`gis-verify/gis-source-coverage-verify.mjs`) assert Chambers wells/pipelines ‚â• 1000 + a Mont Belvieu well ‚â• 1 ‚Äî a county-clipped source fails immediately. RRC is **not on the sandbox egress allow-list**, so those fixtures run **in CI / on planyr.io** (VERIFICATION V101); FEMA/NWI/TxGIO/TxDOT fixtures pass in-sandbox. Unit-locked in `test/siteAnalysis.test.js` + `test/gisSources.test.js`. lint 0 ¬∑ 1129 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B366 ‚Äî Screening resilience + honest error taxonomy (a 503 is not "CORS") `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-brief "NEW-1" 2026-06-21; minted **B366**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-22 (branch `claude/nice-cori-ghm37q`).** The screen fired ~8‚Äì10 ArcGIS `/query` calls **all at once**; under that burst hosts (even Esri-hosted ones) returned transient **HTTP 503**, with **no retry** (one blip ‚Üí permanent UNKNOWN) and **every** failure mislabeled "Couldn't reach the GIS server (network or CORS)" though CORS is fine on every endpoint. (A 503 whose error response lacks the CORS header surfaces to `fetch` as an opaque "Failed to fetch" ‚Äî which is exactly why the catch-all blamed CORS.)
- **New shared `lib/gisFetch.js`:** `fetchArcgisJson` = one resilient fetch ‚Äî **AbortController timeout** + **jittered exponential-backoff retry** (3 attempts) on a transient 5xx/429/network/timeout + automatic **GET‚ÜíPOST** when a dense-polygon URL gets over-long. `classifyGisError` ‚Üí an honest `{kind, message, retryable}` taxonomy: `http-5xx`/`http-4xx`/`arcgis`/`timeout`/`network`/`offline` ‚Äî **never** a blind "CORS" (a bare "Failed to fetch" reads "couldn't reach / temporarily unavailable", since in-browser a header-less 503 is indistinguishable from a real CORS block and a retry usually clears it). `pLimit` concurrency limiter.
- **`runSiteAnalysis` now throttles the WHOLE fan-out** (environmental + jurisdiction + road) through ONE pool of **3** ‚Äî the burst that provoked the 503s is gone.
- **State machine:** new **`unavailable`** finding status (retryable; amber; a per-source **‚Üª Retry** control), **visually + semantically distinct from `absent`/"None found"** ‚Äî a failure is NEVER read as clear (the silent-error guard). The honest reason (e.g. "HTTP 503 ‚Äî temporarily unavailable") is surfaced; jurisdiction/road use the same shared classifier.
- **Tests:** `test/gisFetch.test.js` (15 ‚Äî retry-then-succeed, give-up-after-budget, no-retry-4xx, arcgis-200-error-body, POST-for-long-URL, pool max-in-flight, honest-not-CORS). Live headless `gis-verify/site-analysis-resilience-verify.mjs` **11/11** (V101): forced-503 ‚Üí honest "503", Retry present, 0 "network or CORS", 0 page errors. lint 0 ¬∑ build green.

### B367 ‚Äî GIS screening cache: keep last-good on a failed refresh + data age (stale-while-revalidate) `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature)  *(arrived as coworker-brief "NEW-2" 2026-06-21 ‚Äî the existing roadmap "GIS layer caching" item; minted **B367**. Phase 2 = B371, Later.)*
`[x]` **Phase 1 done + verified 2026-06-22 (branch `claude/nice-cori-ghm37q`).** Folds the long-standing roadmap "#1 GIS layer caching" detail into the screen and builds on the existing SWR cache (B96). A transient source outage must never **blank** a layer.
- **Behavior:** `analyzeSource` now KEEPS the last-good copy when a refresh fails ‚Äî the finding stays `present`/`absent` (its real last value) flagged **`stale` + `refreshError`**, and the panel shows "**‚ü≥ Showing the last good result (as of <date>) ‚Äî couldn't refresh: <reason>**" rather than downgrading to UNAVAILABLE or blanking. Only a failure with **no** cached copy is a hard UNAVAILABLE. Every result already carries its data-age + a screening-only caveat (generalized from the Floodplain "last-good" UI to all rows).
- **Tests:** `test/siteAnalysis.test.js` "a failed REFRESH keeps the last-good copy (stale, not blanked)"; headless V101 ages the cache past TTL then forces a 503 ‚Üí flood/wetlands show last-good + "couldn't refresh", uncached RRC rows show honest 503. lint 0 ¬∑ build green.
- **Phase 2 (deferred ‚Üí B371, Later):** a regional Houston-metro snapshot/offline pre-cache so a never-seen parcel resolves offline. Heavier (storage + refresh job); gated behind this Phase 1 per the brief.

### B369 ‚Äî GIS Source Registry + verification harness (stop shipping test / non-authoritative endpoints) `[Site Planner / Platform]` (feature / infra)  *(arrived as coworker-brief "NEW-4" 2026-06-21; minted **B369**. Map-display-layer migration tail = B370, Open.)*
`[x]` **Done + verified 2026-06-22 (branch `claude/nice-cori-ghm37q`).** Answers the owner's "why do we keep mapping to test / non-final places?" ‚Äî two failure modes now have a machine guard: **(1)** we mis-wire it (NWI on `/Test/`, Wells/Pipelines on a Harris republication) and **(2)** agencies move/rename/retire services.
- **Registry** `src/shared/gis/sources.js` ‚Äî ONE versioned source of truth: per row `key, label, provider, serviceUrl, layerId, geometryType, fields, coverage, tier, lastVerified, fixtures`. The screen (`siteAnalysis.js`) + jurisdiction identify (`jurisdiction.js`) now read EVERY endpoint from here ‚Äî **zero inline service URLs** in the analysis path.
- **Tier/lint guard (CI ‚Äî blocks merge):** `ui-audit/gis-source-audit.mjs` + `test/gisSources.test.js` fail the build on a `/Test/`,`/staging/`,`geogimstest`,‚Ä¶ URL that isn't an acknowledged **`monitored-exception`** (the only one is Wetlands, locked down), or if an endpoint is re-inlined in the analysis connectors. Wired as a named step in `build.yml`.
- **Coverage / authority fixtures (the centerpiece ‚Äî what catches 14-vs-8,014):** each row carries known-truth assertions (Chambers wells ‚â• 1000, Mont Belvieu well ‚â• 1, ‚Ä¶); `gis-verify/gis-source-coverage-verify.mjs` checks reachability + that every `outField` still exists on the live layer (schema-drift catch) + the coverage minimums. **Verified in-sandbox** against FEMA/NWI/TxGIO/TxDOT (all pass); RRC rows run in CI (host not on the sandbox allow-list).
- **Drift detection + alerting:** `.github/workflows/gis-drift.yml` re-runs the live verify **weekly**; on any regression it opens/bumps a single **@claude** GitHub issue with the report (auto-closes when green).
- **Runtime fallback chain (#6):** a row may carry `fallbacks`; `analyzeSource` tries the mirror before failing, then the SWR cache (B367) ‚Äî degrading honestly (B366 taxonomy). Tests: `test/gisSources.test.js` (10) + `test/gisFetch.test.js`. lint 0 ¬∑ 1129 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B365 ‚Äî Redesign project-status map markers for correct visual hierarchy `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as cross-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-21; first filed **B362**, renumbered **B365** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` took B362‚ÄìB364 (bump-out resize, bonded-child rotation, scanned/DWG reading) while this was in flight, so B365 is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND shipped + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/trusting-cori-rkn2x3`).** The status markers inverted the hierarchy ‚Äî Pursuit (most important) was a thin **dashed cool-blue hollow outline** that vanished into the aerial, while settled Complete shouted. Fixed so salience now tracks importance: **Pursuit = eye-magnet, Complete = recessive.**
- **One source of truth (already existed ‚Äî extended, not duplicated).** `src/shared/ui/statusTokens.js` (`STATUS_TOKENS`/`statusToken`, the B234 set shared by map pins, list rows, filter chips, group headers, and the status menu) is the single map the item asked for. Re-hued + extended it with map-pin fields (`halo`, `tier`, `mapOpacity`, `z`, `mapHollow`, `hideByDefault`); every surface updates together. These status constants stay **deliberately separate** from the module-accent tokens (Site/Schedule/Markup), per the locked rule.
- **Color reassignment (deliberate):** Pursuit blue‚Üí**coral `#D85A30`** (warm advances), Active green‚Üí**blue `#378ADD`** (blue, NOT green, so it never collides with the Site accent green `#1D9E75`), On&nbsp;Hold amber `#BA7517`, Complete gray `#888780`, Dead red `#E24B4A`. `src/index.css` `--status-*` light + dark mirrors updated to match (dark = lighter variants per B320). **Contrast held:** `contrast-audit.mjs` green in BOTH themes (status glyph-on-card floor 3.0 ‚Üí coral 3.9 light / 6.6 dark; blue 3.6 light / pass dark).
- **Map pin rebuild (`MapFinder.jsx` `buildingPinIcon`):** solid status fill; **white halo only** (a fattened white copy under the body ‚Äî no drop-shadow, which flashed on re-render + cost perf) sized by importance (~3/2.5/2/2 px); **size tiers** Pursuit‚ÜíComplete (largest‚Üísmallest, ~25% smaller overall than before); crisp **inline-SVG white glyphs** ‚Äî flag (Pursuit) / pulse (Active) / pause ‚Äñ (On&nbsp;Hold) / check (Complete); Complete at 0.65 opacity (recessive); **z-order by importance** via `zIndexOffset` (Pursuit on top ‚Üí Complete bottom; open site floats above its tier) so a settled pin never occludes a Pursuit. **Dead is hidden by default** (shown only when the user explicitly filters to Dead) and rendered as a faint hollow outline when surfaced.
- **Hit target + anchor (gotchas handled):** a **fixed 34√ó44 hit box** for every state (‚â• the old ~32√ó41 largest) holds the smaller art bottom-centered ‚Äî so shrinking the art never shrinks the tap target, AND the bottom-center anchor never drifts when a pin changes status/size. `riseOnHover` for overlap clicking.
- **Shape (resolved with owner):** the brief's "keep the existing flat-top hexagon shape" was a **voice-to-text artifact** ‚Äî owner confirmed he never said hexagon. First shipped keeping the old gabled building silhouette (B161), then mocked up 5 candidate shapes (`ui-audit/mockup-marker-shapes.mjs`); owner chose the **FLAT-TOP SHIELD** (flat top edge ‚Üí straight sides ‚Üí a point at the bottom that lands on the exact spot): body `M14,35 L5,22 L5,10 L23,10 L23,22 Z`. `statusGlyph` was re-parameterized to draw each glyph **centered on the head** (cx,cy = 14,16) so the symbol sits dead-center regardless of glyph; verified by `getBBox` (pursuit 14.5/15.85, active 14/16, onhold 14/16, complete 14.2/15.5).
- **One judgment call (flagged to owner):** "Hide Dead by default" was applied to the whole Dead site (pin + zoomed-in plan), the simplest coherent reading; trivially scoped to pin-only if desired.
- **Deduped ‚Äî NET-NEW, a redesign of (not a duplicate of):** **B161** (the building-marker shape + the now-fixed hollow-dashed Pursuit treatment ‚Äî its shape is preserved, its inverted-salience treatment replaced), **B234** (the shared status token set ‚Äî extended in place), **B163/B236** (the progress-arc `progress_pct` encoding ‚Äî untouched, a SEPARATE encoding deliberately not drawn here). No new open item overlapped.
- **Verified (V100):** new `ui-audit/verify-b365-markers.mjs` ‚Üí **19/19** headless (chromium-1228, 0 page errors) ‚Äî Dead hidden (4 markers not 5); correct coral/blue/amber/gray fills; size tiers 21>18.9>17.2>15.1; white halo + flag/pulse/pause/check glyphs present; **each glyph centered on the head (getBBox ~14,16)**; Pursuit z-index above Complete (737>437); fixed 34√ó44 hit box. Screenshots confirm the flat-top shield + crisp centered glyphs + clean halos over both bright and dark tiles. **1062 unit tests pass, lint 0 errors, build green.**

### B364 ‚Äî Mobile: pinch-zoom crash ("null is not an object ‚Ä¶ .mid") + multi-tab banner falsely nagging logged-out users `[App Shell / Site Planner / Doc Review]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-21 from a mobile screenshot: opened on phone, "moved around for a sec" ‚Üí Site Planner error boundary fired; **separately** a "open in another tab" banner appeared though he wasn't signed in and shouldn't carry cross-session state.)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/mobile-session-state-bug-omeec4`).**
- **Crash root cause:** the two-finger pinch handler (`applyPinch`, B331) read `pinchRef.current.mid` *inside* the `setView` updater. On mobile a finger can lift between scheduling the update and React running the updater ‚Äî `reseedPinch` nulls `pinchRef.current` ‚Äî so the deferred updater dereferenced null (`null is not an object (evaluating '‚Ä¶‚Äã.current.mid')`) and the workspace's error boundary showed "Site Planner hit an error and couldn't load." **Fix:** snapshot `pinchRef.current` into a local before `setView` in **both** `SitePlanner.jsx` and `doc-review/DocReview.jsx`, so the updater uses the captured baseline and can't hit null.
- **Multi-tab banner root cause / scope:** the B313 same-project-in-another-tab warning fired for **logged-out** sessions too, where it's just a false mobile nag (the banner exists to protect saved *cloud* work, not anonymous device-only browsing). **Fix:** gated to signed-in accounts only ‚Äî new `accountActive` prop on `AppHeader` (defaults off) plumbed `Shell (!!user) ‚Üí SitePlannerApp/SitePlanner/DocReview/Scheduler ‚Üí AppHeader`; presence is fed a null project and the banner is suppressed when logged out. Logged-out = fresh, quiet.
- **Tests:** `test/multiTab.test.js` (10) still green (pure protocol unchanged); `ui-audit/verify-multitab.mjs` rewritten to assert the new behavior (two logged-out tabs on the same project ‚Üí **no** banner in either) ‚Äî **3/3 (V99)**. build green.

### B362 ‚Äî Bump-out (dog-ear) resize doesn't persist; reverts to the 55‚Ä≤√ó60‚Ä≤ default on any host refit `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-21, confirmed live on project **Jacintoport** ‚Äî cloud site `smqdxst8pf3g`, bump-outs `e8986`/`e8987` both stuck at default 60√ó55. First filed **B357**; renumbered **B362** ‚Äî a concurrent `main` (#268/#269) took B357‚ÄìB361 (Markup header de-clutter + Scheduler) while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID. **Folds in** the long-deferred triage item "building bump-outs aren't clamped when the host shrinks" ‚Äî now closed here.)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/festive-newton-90t0zh`, PR #271).**
- **Repro:** select a building bump-out, resize it, then resize/edge-drag/numerically-resize the host ‚Üí the bump snapped back to 55‚Ä≤√ó60‚Ä≤. **Root cause:** a dog-ear stored no size of its own ‚Äî `dogEarGeom` always recomputed w/h from the constants `DOGEAR_W`/`DOGEAR_D`, and `refitChildren`‚Üí`fitDogEar`‚Üí`dogEarGeom` ran for every dog-ear on each host resize, discarding the user's dimensions.
- **Fix:** extracted the bump geometry to a pure, unit-tested **`lib/dogEar.js`** (`dogEarGeom`/`dogEarSize` + the defaults). A resized bump now **records its span along the dock wall + its projection** (`along`/`proj`) on its `dogEar` tag (captured in `refitChildren` the moment the bump is the resized element); `fitDogEar` re-anchors **position + host angle only**, preserving the stored size. The along-span is **CLAMPED to the wall ‚Äî never reset** ‚Äî when the host shrinks past the corner (closes the deferred "not clamped" item), and **springs back** when the host grows again. Knock-on correctness: the **dock-door layout** now avoids a bump by its actual span (not a flat 55‚Ä≤), and the **yield panel** says "N bump-outs ¬∑ sizes vary" instead of falsely claiming "55‚Ä≤√ó60‚Ä≤" once a bump is resized.
- **Schema:** `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 6‚Üí7; the new `along`/`proj` fields ride inside `els`, lossless/idempotent through `createSiteModel` (absent ‚áí the 55‚Ä≤√ó60‚Ä≤ default, so every existing bump is unaffected).
- **Tests:** new `test/dogEar.test.js` (5) ‚Äî default 55√ó60 + flush-at-corner, the `dogEarSize` inverse, **the exact repro (resize bump ‚Üí resize host ‚Üí size preserved)**, clamp-then-spring-back on host shrink, host-rotation carry. Coordinated with B363 (resize re-syncs angle *without* resetting size). **lint 0 ¬∑ 1066 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.** Live: `ui-audit/verify-b362-b363.mjs` 6/6 (V98).

### B363 ‚Äî Bonded children (bump-outs, court, sidewalk) keep a stale rotation when the host is re-angled `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-2" 2026-06-21, confirmed live on **Jacintoport** ‚Äî host `e8984` at rot 0¬∞ but all four bonded children (`e8985` court, `e8986`/`e8987` bump-outs, `e8988` sidewalk) at 359.035¬∞, the whole assembly ~1¬∞ off the building. First filed **B358**; renumbered **B363** ‚Äî `main` #268/#269 took B357‚ÄìB361 in flight.)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/festive-newton-90t0zh`, PR #271).**
- **Root cause:** a bonded child's rotation was only copied from the host at create/refit time (`dogEarGeom`/`makeStrip`/`oppTrailerGeom` set `rot: host.rot`); the only thing that re-copied it was `refitChildren`, which runs on host **resize only**. So any state where child rot ‚âÝ host rot ‚Äî a host placed at a parcel-bearing angle, children added, the host later straightened by a path that didn't carry them ‚Äî was left visibly off-angle until the next resize.
- **Fix (1) ‚Äî derived invariant, re-applied on ANY host geometry change.** A bonded box child's angle is now a derived value: `host.rot` + its fixed quarter-turn offset (0/90/180/270 ‚Äî sidewalks/courts/bump-outs share the host angle; side-parking/wall-trailers sit at a +90/180/270 turn). A shared pure `bondedChildRot`/`quarterOffset` (`siteModel.js`) + a `resyncBondedRot` pass is now folded into **every** host-geometry path ‚Äî resize (`refitChildren`) **and** rotate (`rotateAssemblyTo` *and* the rotate-handle drag) ‚Äî so a child can no longer be stranded off-angle (previously only resize re-synced, and only some child types).
- **Fix (2) ‚Äî one-time repair in migrate.** `normalizeBondedRotations` runs inside `createSiteModel` (the migrate path ‚Üí fires on every load), re-anchoring a drifted child to the host's **current** frame: its angle snaps to `host.rot + offset` **and** its centre is rotated about the host centre by the stale delta (a host straightened without its children leaves their *positions* stale too, not just the angle value). **Idempotent + identity-preserving** for already-correct data; safe because a bonded box child is only ever at a quarter turn, so any other angle is drift, not intent (points-based children skipped). This **repairs Jacintoport and any other already-diverged project the moment it's opened**, and persists on the next save.
- **Coordinated with B362:** `refitChildren` captures the bump size **before** the rotation resync, and resync only touches `rot` ‚Äî so re-syncing a child's angle never resets a resized bump.
- **Schema:** `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 6‚Üí7 (shared with B362).
- **Tests:** new `test/bondedRotation.test.js` (6) ‚Äî the quarter-turn offset math, **the full Jacintoport repair (rot AND position re-anchored)**, idempotency + identity-preservation on correct data, points-children skipped, version-bump guard. **lint 0 ¬∑ 1066 tests ¬∑ build green.** Live: `ui-audit/verify-b362-b363.mjs` 6/6 (V98) ‚Äî drifted assembly straightens on load, user-sized bump keeps 80√ó70 across a save/load round-trip.

     `main` consumed B355 (#265 lettered plan concepts) and B356 (#266 invisible site-name) while this
