### B288 ‚Äî Single-sheet review can't be zoomed by wheel / pinch / Ctrl-scroll (only the ¬± buttons) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; minted **B288** ‚Äî renumbered from B273, consumed by concurrent `main`)*
`[x]` **Repro (confirmed live on planyr.io 2026-06-20):** open a PDF in Document Review (single-sheet, not Stitch) ‚Üí wheel / pinch / Ctrl+scroll over the drawing ‚Üí nothing (stuck at 31%); the only zoom is the toolbar ¬±. **Expected:** wheel / Ctrl-scroll / pinch zoom the drawing, anchored on the cursor. The fix already exists here ‚Äî the Stitcher's `onWheel` (`Stitcher.jsx` ~L171) does cursor-anchored zoom; port it to the single-sheet view (`DocReview.jsx`). Primary "can't zoom" complaint.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). A native non-passive `wheel` listener on the scroll viewport ‚Üí `zoomAround(factor, cursorX, cursorY)`: changes `scale`, stashes the page-unit point under the cursor, then a `useLayoutEffect` keyed on the re-rendered `dims` nudges `scrollLeft/Top` so that point stays under the cursor. Ctrl/Cmd+wheel and plain wheel both zoom (trackpad pinch arrives as Ctrl+wheel). Shares the anchor path with B290. Headless-verified (V72).

### B289 ‚Äî No drag-to-pan in single-sheet review `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; minted **B289** ‚Äî renumbered from B274)*
`[x]` Zoomed in, there's no way to grab-and-drag the drawing to move around ‚Äî no hand tool, no space-drag ‚Äî so navigation relies entirely on the scrollbars. (Scrollbars do work and reach the whole sheet, so this is a usability gap, not a blocker.) **Expected:** drag-to-pan (a Pan/hand tool and/or hold-Space to pan), matching the Stitcher, which already pans via pointer-drag (`Stitcher.jsx` ~L158/L168).
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). New **Pan** tool (hand) + **hold-Space** in any tool; pointer-drag adjusts the scroller's `scrollLeft/Top` by the negative pointer delta (pointer-capture so a fast drag doesn't drop). Cursor shows grab/grabbing. Releases capture + clears state on pointerup/pointercancel (so it can't reproduce B271's frozen-cursor lockout). Headless-verified (V72).

### B290 ‚Äî ¬± zoom isn't cursor-anchored `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug, minor)  *(arrived as "NEW-3" 2026-06-20; minted **B290** ‚Äî renumbered from B275)*
`[x]` `zoom()` only changes `scale`; it doesn't hold a focal point, so the detail you were looking at drifts and you re-scroll after each step. **Expected:** zoom toward the cursor (or hold the viewport center fixed), like the Stitcher's cursor-anchored wheel zoom. Pairs with B288.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). The ¬± buttons now call `zoomAround(factor)` with the viewport centre as the anchor (same scroll-anchor path as B288), so the centre of what you're looking at stays put across a zoom step. Headless-verified centre drift 0.6px (V72).

### B291 ‚Äî Double-click to finish a Count/Area/Perimeter adds 1‚Äì2 phantom points (inflates counts, distorts areas) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug) ‚Äî takeoff correctness, HIGH  *(arrived as "NEW-4" 2026-06-20; minted **B291** ‚Äî renumbered from B276)*
`[x]` **Repro:** pick Count, click N items, then double-click to finish (as the tool hint instructs) ‚Üí the count reads N+2 with two stray dots at the double-click spot; same root cause distorts Area/Perimeter (extra/duplicate vertices at the closing click). Cause: each pointerdown appends a point (`DocReview.jsx` onDown), and a double-click fires TWO pointerdowns before `onDoubleClick`‚Üí`finishDraft` commits. Enter finishes cleanly; only the advertised double-click path is broken. **Expected:** double-click finishes without adding the doubled vertices. The Site Planner already solved this with a shared `finishActiveDrawing()` + `removeLastVertex()` ‚Äî reuse that approach.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). `onDbl(e)` strips any trailing draft points coincident (‚â§ ~6px screen) with the double-click location before committing the poly/count, so the two pointerdowns the browser fires for a double-click can't survive as phantom vertices. Enter path unchanged. Headless-verified: 3 clicks + double-click ‚Üí exactly 3 count dots, not 5 (V72).

### B292 ‚Äî Zoom level resets to fit-width every time you switch sheets `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug, minor)  *(arrived as "NEW-5" 2026-06-20; minted **B292** ‚Äî renumbered from B277)*
`[x]` **Repro (confirmed live 2026-06-20):** zoom Sheet 1 to 134%, click Sheet 2 ‚Üí it snaps back to 31% (fit-width); zoom is lost on every sheet change. Cause: the sheet button calls `setScale(0)`. **Expected:** keep the current zoom across sheet switches when reviewing a set.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). The sheet button no longer forces `setScale(0)`; switching sheets re-renders the new page at the current scale. The fit pass still runs on first open and on the explicit Fit / Fit-page buttons. (Merged cleanly with main's B267 per-sheet scale tooltip on the same button.) Headless-verified (V72).

### B293 ‚Äî Can't move or edit a markup after placing it (Select only allows delete) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-6" 2026-06-20; minted **B293** ‚Äî renumbered from B278)*
`[x]` Today Select supports delete only ‚Äî a misplaced redline/measurement/text note has to be deleted and redrawn, and placed text can't be re-edited. Key design points: drag a selected markup to reposition it (commit once on pointer-up, coords stay in page units); double-click a text note to edit it. The ParcelDrawing surface already implements move + edit-text (B67) ‚Äî mirror it here.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). Select-mode interior drag translates a markup's page-unit points live and commits once on pointer-up (a sub-3px drag is still a plain click-select; interior drag moves, never pans ‚Äî per B155's locked rule). Double-click a text note opens an **inline `<input>` overlay** at the note (Enter/blur commit, Esc cancel); text **creation** routes through the same inline editor (opening on pointer-UP so the click's own focus change can't blur+discard it), removing Doc Review's `window.prompt` for text (KEY DECISION "no dialog-box edits" + the B155 note). Calibrate's prompt is left as-is (tracked under B155). Adjacent to B155 + B156, which stay open. Headless-verified move + create + edit (V72).

### B294 ‚Äî Dropping a PDF only works on the empty screen, not once a document is open `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug, minor)  *(arrived as "NEW-7" 2026-06-20; minted **B294** ‚Äî renumbered from B279)*
`[x]` **Repro:** with a PDF open, drag another PDF onto the canvas ‚Üí nothing (you must use "Open another‚Ä¶"). The drop handler is bound only to the empty-state view; the Stitcher accepts drops anywhere. **Expected:** dropping a PDF over the open document opens it (reuse the existing re-drop/source-binding path).
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). The canvas/viewport now carries the same `onDragOver`/`onDrop` as the empty state, routed through the existing `openFile` (which already re-binds a re-dropped source by name so its markups stay attached). (Verified by code + the proven openFile path; a synthetic file-drop isn't in the headless harness.)

### B295 ‚Äî "Fit" only fits width; tall/portrait sheets can't be viewed whole `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature, minor)  *(arrived as "NEW-8" 2026-06-20; minted **B295** ‚Äî renumbered from B280)*
`[x]` The fit logic computes width-only (`avail / base.width`), so a portrait/tall sheet overflows vertically and can't be seen in one view. Add a fit-to-page option (min of width-fit and height-fit) alongside fit-width.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). New **Fit page** button next to **Fit**; the fit pass reads a `fitMode` ('width' | 'page') and uses `min(availW/baseW, availH/baseH)` for page-fit. Headless-verified Fit-page fits the whole sheet where Fit-width overflows (V72).

### B296 ‚Äî Linear measurements round to whole feet, inconsistent with area's 2-decimal acres `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug, minor)  *(arrived as "NEW-9" 2026-06-20; minted **B296** ‚Äî renumbered from B281)*
`[x]` `measureLabel` prints distance/perimeter via `f0` (whole feet) while area shows 2-dp acres (`takeoff.js`) ‚Äî a 150.6 ft line reads "151 ft". For takeoff precision, show at least one decimal on linear measures.
> ‚úÖ Shipped (PR #220 ‚Üí `main`). `measureLabel` now formats distance/perimeter with one decimal (`f1`); area (acres 2-dp / sf whole) unchanged. Covered by a unit test (`test/takeoff.test.js`).

### B206 ‚Äî Storage-adapter discipline: confine all backend-specific logic to one module `[Server]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B203, renumbered **B206** ‚Äî `main` took B203‚ÄìB205)*
`[x]` Shipped 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/laughing-ritchie-k5narx`). A **single storage-adapter module is the only thing the app talks to for file ops** (save / fetch / list / move / rename / delete / share-link). The app references files **only by Planyr's own stable keys** ‚Äî never a Google Drive file id, Drive folder path, or Drive sharing handle. The adapter's internal **mapping table is the only place Planyr-key ‚Üî backend-id translation exists**; no backend id leaks past it. **NEW-2/3/4 all sit behind this.**
- **What shipped:** `server/storage/adapter.js` (the only entry point ‚Äî save/fetch/list/move/rename/remove/shareLink, all returning result objects, never throwing), `idMap.js` (the sole Planyr-key‚Üîbackend-id translator; in-memory store now, Supabase-Postgres-backed later), `result.js` (the `{ok}` result + `attempt()` wrapper), and `index.js` (server-side assembly ‚Äî the one place creds are read). **20 unit tests** (`test/storageAdapter.test.js`) incl. the **acceptance test**: an "app consumer" written only against the adapter behaves identically across two different backends, and a list never exposes a backend id (an unbound backend object is invisible to the app).
> **Dedup:** the adapter is the **bytes** layer; the **index of file facts** (B180's `fileFacts` view-model + the `doc_reviews` table) is the separate catalog layer that records *which* file is *what* ‚Äî they meet only at the Planyr key. Existing direct Supabase-Storage calls (`reviewStore.uploadSource/downloadSource`, `overlayStorage.js`) are the **adoption targets**: route them through the adapter when the Drive backend goes live (so the swap is verified end-to-end with real creds), not as risky blind surgery now.

### B208 ‚Äî Share-by-link via a link-provider abstraction (Drive links now, Planyr links later) `[Server]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; provisionally B205, renumbered **B208** ‚Äî `main` took B203‚ÄìB205)*
`[x]` Shipped 2026-06-19. Sharing emits a link from a **single link-provider interface** (`server/storage/linkProvider.js`). For now it returns the backend's **native Drive share link** (recipients see a familiar Google Drive link ‚Äî intentional, keeps the owner's company comfortable). **ALL link generation routes through this one interface**, so switching later to Planyr-native signed links (`planyr.io/s/<token>` ‚Üí backend mints a temporary signed URL, serves bytes through the adapter) is a **one-place change** (`kind: "planyr"` + a `signer`). No Drive link call is hardcoded in UI/app code. **Tested** both kinds (Drive-native via the backend; Planyr signed via an injected signer; not-configured fails visibly).
> **Non-goal (deferred):** drag-a-file-out-of-the-browser-into-an-email ‚Äî a desktop sync-client gesture; the owner uses Drive directly for that for now.

### B209 ‚Äî File operations must surface success/failure; no silent file failures `[Server]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-4"; provisionally B206, renumbered **B209** ‚Äî `main` took B203‚ÄìB205)*
`[x]` Shipped 2026-06-19. Every adapter op (upload, fetch, move, rename, delete, link-generation) returns a **visible success/failure result** (`server/storage/result.js`): the adapter **never throws** and **never reports a half-finished op as success** ‚Äî a throwing backend, a backend `{ok:false}`, or an unmapped key all surface as `{ ok:false, error }`. **Same severity class as the silent cloud-save failures that caused prior data loss.** Covered by the no-silent-failure tests in `test/storageAdapter.test.js`.
> **Dedup / align:** this **extends** the existing save-status-visibility guarantee (B125 ‚Äî make cloud save status visible, no silent failures) to **Drive file operations**; it is the storage-layer contract, surfaced in the UI through the same honest-status pattern the persistence layer already uses (don't build a parallel status system ‚Äî when the adapter is adopted, its `{ok:false}` feeds the existing save badge / banners).

### B162 ‚Äî Suppress street labels at low map zoom `[Site Planner / map]` (feature)
`[x]` Street labels should be hidden below zoom 14 and visible at zoom 14+. The basemap uses a separate Esri Reference/World_Transportation raster overlay (`LABELS_TILES`) ‚Äî Case B from the spec. Implementation: zoom-driven `layer.setOpacity()` on `zoomend`, initial opacity set from live map zoom when the layer is created.
> Done 2026-06-18 (branch `claude/charming-fermat-f9ktvp`). Added zoom-driven `useEffect` (`[zoom]` dep) that calls `layer.setOpacity(zoom >= 14 ? 0.4 : 0)` on the `labelsRef.current` tile layer. Initial opacity in the labels `useEffect` reads `map.getZoom()` directly (not the React state) to avoid a one-frame flash. Browser-verified: labels layer opacity transitions from `0` (zoom 11) ‚Üí `0.4` (after 8√ó zoom-in) ‚Üí `0` (after 8√ó zoom-out) correctly.

### B161 ‚Äî Building + progress arc project markers `[Site Planner / map]` (feature)
`[x]` Replace all project map markers with a gabled industrial building silhouette (SVG path `M14,35 L5,29 L5,15 L14,9 L23,15 L23,29 Z`, viewBox `0 0 28 36`) with a clockwise progress arc ring (cx=14, cy=19, r=11, C=69.12, dashOffset=17.28 for 12-o'clock start). Status variants: Active=amber #EF9F27 + 60% arc, Complete=teal #1D9E75 + full ring + white checkmark, On hold=gray #888780 + 30% frozen arc + white pause bars, Prospect/pursuit=dashed blue #378ADD outline + 10% nub, Dead=muted gray + 0% arc. Dock doors suppressed when building has no fill (pursuit). Active-site emphasis: 1.15√ó scale + ember drop-shadow. Anchor point = bottom-center tip. V1: progress derived from status (Path B ‚Äî no DB column). Log Path A (real `progress_pct` column) as follow-on B163.
> Done 2026-06-18 (branch `claude/charming-fermat-f9ktvp`). Replaced `pinGlyphSvg` + `statusPinIcon` with `BUILDING_COLORS` constant + `buildingPinIcon(status, active)` in `MapFinder.jsx`. Browser-verified: all 4 marker variants render distinctly (amber/teal/gray/dashed-blue building silhouettes with arc rings) over Esri aerial imagery (`ui-audit/verify-markers.mjs`).

### B151 ‚Äî Multipart parcel: clicking the smaller tract selects the wrong piece (only the largest part highlights/imports) `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ‚Äî relative of B36c  *(owner-reported 2026-06-18, "Pearland"; minted B151 ‚Äî `main` concurrently took B147‚ÄìB150, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` Repro (owner): Pearland, account **`0440520000010`** ("TRS 3 & 5 (AG-USE)", 15.5 AC, situs "0 DEL PAPA"). Clicking the **meat** of the parcel registers the account (selection count ticks up) but the clicked piece **does not highlight** ‚Äî instead the **adjacent piece to the right** lights up. Owner clarification: *"I didn't click on a county line ‚Äî it just won't let me click the parcel in the meat of the parcel."*
> **Root cause (confirmed by live endpoint probes AND a live headless-browser repro, not assumption).** `0440520000010` is a **HARRIS** parcel (HCAD + the statewide TxGIO layer agree; identical geometry), but it is **MULTIPART** ‚Äî two physically separate tracts under one account: a **west tract ~6.66 AC** and an **east tract ~8.12 AC**, with a *different* parcel (`0440520000002`) between them. The geometry helpers in `arcgis.js` (`largestRingLngLat`/`featureToParcel`) deliberately returned only the **single largest ring** (the B36c "biggest |area| ring can't be a hole" pick). So a click anywhere in the parcel resolves the right account, but the highlight (`MapFinder` `handleClick`) and the planner import only ever drew/added the **east (largest) tract** ‚Üí clicking the **west** tract appeared to select the neighbour, and planning the site silently **dropped ~6.66 AC** (card showed 8.12 AC, not ~15). Disproves the pasted brief's guesses: the parcel is Harris (not Brazoria/"missing county"); click coords round-trip exactly in EPSG:4326 (no SR bug ‚Äî verified the geometry the app sends matches the click latlng); IDs are present and the highlight is drawn from geometry, not the ID field; the query is a true `esriSpatialRelIntersects` point-in-polygon (no nearest/buffer). The seam *does* exist (the parcel's south edge is the diagonal Harris/Brazoria line; a click just over it selects a Brazoria parcel that lives only in the statewide TxGIO layer) but that was **not** the owner's symptom ‚Äî multipart was.
> **Fix (minimal + shared; branch `claude/nifty-bohr-mtz70c`).** New `outerRingsLngLat(feature)` in `lib/arcgis.js` returns **every outer-boundary ring** of a (possibly multipart) feature, classifying outers vs. holes by winding **sign** (so a separate small tract is kept even when a hole's |area| exceeds it ‚Äî the B36c size trap), holes dropped, each ring opened. Wired into **all three** parcel-import paths so it's fixed everywhere, not per-symptom: (1) **map-click select** (`MapFinder.handleClick`) now highlights all parts via Leaflet **multipolygon** nesting (`[[part],[part]]`, not a hole-punch) and stores `rings`/`latlngsList`; `computeAssembly` emits **one planner parcel per part** in the shared frame (full acreage) and the cursor hit-test checks every part; (2) **in-planner identify ‚Üí Add to plan** (`SitePlanner.addIdentifiedParcel`) adds every part (`ring` = largest is still kept for the jurisdiction/road straddle tests); (3) **in-planner address/account lookup ‚Üí import** (`importFeature`) imports every part on one **shared** anchor so the tracts keep their true relative position. 5 new unit tests (`test/arcgis.test.js`: single ring; both tracts of a 2-part; donut drops the hole; small separate part kept despite a bigger hole; empty ‚Üí []). lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **230 tests** ¬∑ build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.
> **Browser-verified (Claude self-test, logged-out, served from the local `dist/` build).** Clicking the **west tract** now highlights **BOTH** pieces (highlight path = 2 subpaths spanning the full **1326√ó664 ft** parcel) and the card reads **"1 PARCEL ¬∑ 14.78 AC ¬∑ DEL PAPA"** (was 8.12 AC, east-only) ‚Äî `gis-verify/pearland-FIXED-clickwest.png` (before: `gis-verify/pearland-mp-clickwest.png`; script `gis-verify/pearland-fix-verify.mjs`). Logged as **VERIFICATION V39**; no owner action.

### B146 ‚Äî Interactive, decluttered dimension callouts (move + edit) + road-dimension fixes `[Site Planner / UI]` (feature)  *(arrived 2026-06-17; minted B146 ‚Äî concurrent merges took B141‚ÄìB145, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` ‚úÖ Both increments DONE + browser-verified 2026-06-17. Owner ask (2026-06-17): the red edge-dimension callouts overlap the centred name/area labels (see the B121 screenshots ‚Äî the red "271" prints over "116,327 sf"). They should (1) **auto-move so they don't clutter in the first place**, and (2) be **directly adjustable** ‚Äî click an element to select it, then click its dimension to grab it: **drag the dimension line to reposition it**, and (for roads) **click the dimension number to edit the width**. Apply the move interaction to **buildings, parking, and roads**. Plus two road fixes: the dimension must **track a resize** (it went stale), and roads should **resize in 1‚Ä≤ increments**.
> **Increment 1 ‚Äî road-dimension fixes ‚Äî ‚úÖ DONE 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/determined-brown-bf86l7`), browser-verified.** (D) The road dimension callout (and the Element-panel "Travel width" field) now **derive from live geometry** via `roadTravelWidth(w,h,curb)` in `lib/siteModel.js` (unit-tested), replacing the frozen `el.travelW` that went stale after a resize ‚Äî the resize handlers update w/h, so the callout now tracks. (E) Road resize (corner + edge handles) snaps to **1‚Ä≤ increments** (not the grid step), so a width dials to the exact foot. Self-verified headless: drew a road (dim **170‚Ä≤**), dragged a corner to widen it ‚Üí dim updated live to **428‚Ä≤** (it would have stayed frozen before) ‚Äî logged **V35**. lint 0 ¬∑ 205 tests ¬∑ build green.
> **Increment 2 ‚Äî the interactive layer ‚Äî ‚úÖ DONE 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/determined-brown-bf86l7`), browser-verified** (owner chose "Full interactive"). (A) **Auto-declutter:** the dimension number now sits OUTBOARD of its line (away from the centred label), so it no longer overprints the sq-ft by default. (B) **Drag-to-move:** when an element is selected its dimension is a grab handle (a fat invisible hit-line along the dim) ‚Äî drag it anywhere; the offset is stored per element as `el.dimOffset` (local feet, persisted) and a dashed **leader** points back to what it measures. Works for buildings, parking, and roads. (C) **Roads ‚Äî click the number to edit the width:** clicking the dimension number opens an **inline on-canvas editor** seeded with the current travel width ‚Üí `setRoadTravel`, resizing the road (the line still drags to reposition). Implementation: a `dimMove` drag mode + `startDimMove`/`editDimWidth` in `SitePlanner.jsx`, threaded into `renderElPx`; `dimOffset` rides through the Site Model additively (no migration). **No dialog boxes (owner rule, 2026-06-17 ‚Äî "horrible UI"):** the width editor is a shared inline `numEdit` `<input>` overlay (commit on Enter / click-away, cancel on Esc), and the pre-existing per-edge **setback** and overlay **trace-length** `window.prompt`s were converted to that same inline editor; rule recorded in CLAUDE.md KEY DECISIONS. Self-verified headless: dragged a building's "314‚Ä≤" out with a dashed leader; clicked a road's "199‚Ä≤" ‚Üí **inline box on the canvas (no dialog)** ‚Üí entered 30 ‚Üí road resized to a **30‚Ä≤** travel width ‚Äî logged **V36 / V37**. lint 0 ¬∑ 225 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B137 ‚Äî Fort Bend parcels render but aren't clickable (display covers more counties than the click queries) `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ‚Äî coupled to B11/B13/B36  *(filed as "B130" in the brief; minted B137 ‚Äî `main` concurrently took B130‚ÄìB136, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` Repro: Map view ‚Üí "‚úì Selecting ‚Äî click lots" ‚Üí pan to a Fort Bend area (purple parcel outlines visible) ‚Üí click directly on a lot ‚Üí nothing selects; "No parcel right there ‚Äî zoom in and click directly on a lot" fires. Harris parcels select fine at the same zoom. Expected: clicking any displayed parcel selects it, regardless of county.
> **Root cause (confirmed by live endpoint probes, not assumption).** The visible outlines and the click-query had different *coverage*. The `chambers` entry in `COUNTIES_MAP` is actually the **statewide TxGIO** parcel layer (one service covering all 254 Texas counties); its display layer (`makeParcelLayer`) has no county filter, so when zoomed in it **paints parcel outlines everywhere ‚Äî including Fort Bend.** But on click, `candidateCountiesForPoint` only made TxGIO a query candidate inside the narrow *Chambers* bbox. A Fort Bend click therefore queried only **Harris** (returns nothing ‚Äî verified 0 features at a Sugar Land point) and **FBCAD** ‚Äî and FBCAD's host (`gis.fbcad.org/serverarcgis2`) is currently **down (HTTP 503 on every path, even the host root; Harris/TxGIO/Esri all return 200 from the same network)**. So the click had no answerer and fell through to the "no parcel" message, even though the TxGIO layer that drew the outline *does* return that exact Fort Bend lot on a point query (verified: `county:'FORT BEND', prop_id 40594`). This is precisely the brief's hypothesis #1 ‚Äî display covers more than the queried source. (The brief's other guesses ‚Äî separate selection service, missed reprojection, click tolerance ‚Äî were ruled out: display and click share the same resolved per-county URL, `queryAtPoint` already sets `inSR:4326`, and Harris selects fine via the identical code path.)
> **Fix (minimal + structural; branch `claude/magical-ramanujan-v1dnuw`).** Marked the TxGIO source `statewide:true` in `COUNTIES_MAP` and made `candidateCountiesForPoint` (`lib/counties.js`) **always append any statewide source as a universal fallback** ‚Äî so the click can always select an outline it can see. Appended **LAST**, so a county's own CAD still answers first when reachable (more authoritative, richer fields) and the statewide layer only catches clicks the county CAD didn't; the answering county is then corrected to its true CAD key by the existing **B36(a)** `countyAtPoint` relabel (already keyed on a `chambers`-sourced hit, so no extra wiring). This also makes Fort Bend selection **resilient to FBCAD being down** (TxGIO covers it) and keeps `viewCounty`/Layers-panel jurisdiction unchanged (bbox matches still lead). No change needed in `MapFinder` beyond what already existed. 5 new unit tests in `test/counties.test.js` (Fort Bend routes to fortbend + statewide fallback; fallback ordered last; no dup when in-bbox; Harris still leads; out-of-bbox still gets statewide). lint 0 errors ¬∑ **180 tests** ¬∑ build green; lazy chunk split intact.
> **Coupled to B11/B13/B36:** same county-resolution theme. Does NOT close B13-pt1 (true point-in-county polygons vs. the bbox pre-filter) ‚Äî that's still the deferred, more-accurate routing; this is the resilience/coverage fix that makes "click anywhere selects the parcel you see" hold today. Browser confirmation (real click on a Fort Bend lot) logged as **VERIFICATION V29**; no owner action.

### B133 ‚Äî NWI wetlands: restored from the live `fwsprimary` raster host + verified in-browser `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ‚Äî follow-up to B129  *(filed as "B130" in the hand-off; renumbered B133 ‚Äî concurrent merges already took B130 (parking, #95), B131 (print overlay, #96) and B132 (detention pond, #99), so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Owner-spotted (2026-06-17).** Our configured NWI host `fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov/wetlandsmapservice/‚Ä¶` returns HTTP 500 (confirmed down in B129 ‚Äî an agency-side outage). The owner opened the official USFWS **Wetlands Mapper** (`https://fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov/wetlands/apps/wetlands-mapper/`) and the wetlands render fine ‚Äî i.e. the data is LIVE on the **sibling host `fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov`**; only the `fwspublicservices` alias is erroring. The likely fix is a one-line URL swap. **DO NOT ship it blind ‚Äî verify in a browser first (the whole point of B129).**

**The fix (one line, `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/layers.js`, `STATEWIDE.wetlands.url`):** swap the host `fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov` ‚Üí `fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov` (path identical) ‚Üí
`https://fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov/wetlandsmapservice/rest/services/Wetlands/MapServer`

**PREREQ before you can verify (owner action ‚Äî may already be done):** `fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov` must be in the **egress allowlist** (owner asked 2026-06-17; egress changes only take effect in a NEWLY started session). Sanity-check reachability:
`curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' -H 'Origin: https://planyr.io' 'https://fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov/wetlandsmapservice/rest/services/Wetlands/MapServer?f=json'`
- `403` + "Host not in allowlist" ‚Üí not allowlisted yet; stop, tell the owner.
- `500` ‚Üí fwsprimary now down too; hold (and re-check whether `fwspublicservices` recovered).
- `200` ‚Üí reachable; proceed.

**‚öÝÔ∏è THE risk to settle in-browser (don't skip):** an out-of-band probe of `fwsprimary` on 2026-06-17 returned a *real* **403** (NOT the egress block ‚Äî a server 403). That *might* mean `fwsprimary` only serves its own mapper and refuses cross-site / non-browser requests (Referer/Origin/UA/WAF). If our app's `<img>` export gets 403'd cross-origin from planyr.io, wetlands STILL won't draw. Public ArcGIS export endpoints usually allow cross-site, so it's *probably* fine ‚Äî but confirm by eye. (CORS specifically doesn't block the picture: if `fwsprimary` sends no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, the esri metadata fetch fails but the image still loads via the CORS-exempt `<img>` ‚Äî PR #60 resilience ‚Äî so it should render anyway.)

**Verify (browser already set up ‚Äî `.claude/` SessionStart hook installs Chromium; Playwright recipe + screenshots from B129 are under `gis-verify/`):** on planyr.io or a branch preview, toggle **Wetlands (NWI)** at **zoom 14+** over a known wetland (Sheldon Lake, the Greens/Halls Bayou greenways, or the Katy Prairie west of town). Confirm BOTH: (a) the `‚Ä¶/export?f=image` request returns **HTTP 200 `image/png`**, and (b) the green/blue wetland polygons actually **paint** on the map (do NOT trust the status dot ‚Äî it lies). Like FEMA, NWI may be source-scale-gated, so zoom in.

**Then:**
- **Renders ‚Üí** ship the swap (branch ‚Üí PR ‚Üí green build ‚Üí merge) and update the `wetlands` comment in `layers.js` (it currently says "keep the canonical URL"; note that `fwspublicservices` went down 2026-06 and we moved to the `fwsprimary` sibling ‚Äî the host USFWS's own live mapper uses ‚Äî with the date).
- **403s cross-site / doesn't render ‚Üí** do NOT swap. Leave `fwspublicservices` (it may recover; the honest "service unavailable" message already shipped in B129), and note the `/server` proxy as the durable fix (serves through our own origin, sidesteps both CORS and any cross-site refusal). Report to the owner.

**Key files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/layers.js` (`STATEWIDE.wetlands.url` + the `wetlands` comment block) ¬∑ browser tooling `.claude/hooks/ensure-browser.sh` ¬∑ evidence `gis-verify/` ¬∑ context B129.
> ‚úÖ **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/fervent-albattani-yyp0qe`). **The "one-line, same-path host swap" premise was wrong on both counts ‚Äî but wetlands are back on, browser-verified.** What the investigation actually found: (1) the old `fwspublicservices` host is **still HTTP 500** across its whole catalog; (2) `fwsprimary` is reachable this session (so it's egress-allowlisted), but it has **no `/wetlandsmapservice/‚Ä¶` path (404)** ‚Äî the assumed identical path doesn't exist there; (3) `fwsprimary`'s dynamic **vector** `Wetlands/MapServer/export` returns an HTML Web-Adaptor interstitial (not an image) and its `/0/query` 500s, so a dynamicMapLayer swap would NOT render; (4) the live data the official USFWS Wetlands Mapper actually draws is a **pre-rendered RASTER image service** at `/server/rest/services/Wetlands_Raster/ImageServer` ‚Äî a different host AND a different service type. **Fix:** point `STATEWIDE.wetlands` at that raster URL and switch it from the defaulted `dynamicMapLayer` to **`kind:"esriImage"`** (esri `imageMapLayer`, the same path 3DEP uses); dropped the now-irrelevant `layers:[0]` and rewrote the comment block with the real story. Wetlands is render-only (no click-identify anywhere), so losing the vector layer's queryable attributes costs nothing today. **Verified in a real headless browser** (esri-leaflet `imageMapLayer` over Sheldon Lake, `gis-verify/wetlands-verify.{html,mjs}`): the `exportImage` request returns **HTTP 200 `image/png`**, the service metadata fetch returns **200 JSON**, and the standard NWI symbology (navy open water = Sheldon Lake, greens = vegetated wetlands) **paints** ‚Äî screenshot `gis-verify/wetlands-fwsprimary-verified.png`. **B130's flagged cross-site-403 risk is DISPROVEN:** the host **reflects any `Origin`** in `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` (confirmed for `https://planyr.io`, localhost, and an arbitrary origin), so esri-leaflet's `useCors:true` image loads cross-site with no refusal; the earlier out-of-band 403 did not reproduce. lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **197 tests** ¬∑ build green (count after merging the latest `main` in; the change adds no tests ‚Äî a GIS source swap is verified in-browser, like B129/FEMA) ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Browser pass logged as **VERIFICATION V26** (and supersedes V20's "wait for fwspublicservices to recover" re-check trigger). If `fwsprimary` ever 403s/500s, the honest "service unavailable" path (B129) still applies and the durable fix remains a `/server` proxy through our own origin. **Shipped to `main` via PR #101.** **‚öÝÔ∏è Correction (see B135):** that raster turned out to be a **100 m-per-pixel** source, so wetlands rendered as coarse **BLOCKS**, not true shapes ‚Äî B135 switches to the crisp **vector** `Test/Wetlands_gdb_split` MapServer (`layers:[1,2]`), which is the Wetlands-Mapper look.
> **Follow-up (deferred, low priority ‚Äî captured so it's not lost):** the **unused** future GIS-caching engine `lib/vectorLayers.js` (imported nowhere in the app yet; built for the Track-1 "GIS layer caching" roadmap item) still references the dead `fwspublicservices` host for both its vector `/query` and its `imageFallback`. There is **no drop-in vector replacement on `fwsprimary`** (its `Wetlands/MapServer/0/query` 500s and it exposes no FeatureServer ‚Äî only the raster renders), so when that engine is eventually wired up, NWI there must either use the raster (no per-polygon `WETLAND_TYPE`) or wait for the `fwspublicservices` vector host to recover. Left as-is for now since it's not live and can't be browser-verified.

### B135 ‚Äî Wetlands draw as coarse 100 m blocks (raster), not true polygons ‚Äî switch to the crisp vector MapServer `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ‚Äî follow-up to B133
`[x]` **Owner-spotted (2026-06-17).** After B133 turned NWI wetlands back on, they painted as chunky ~100 m green/blue **blocks** instead of the true wetland shapes the official USFWS Wetlands Mapper draws. Cause: B133 pointed at `fwsprimary`'s `Wetlands_Raster/ImageServer`, which is a **100-m-per-pixel raster** (`pixelSizeX/Y = 100`) ‚Äî inherently blocky at any zoom, and carrying no Cowardin class labels.
> ‚úÖ **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/fervent-albattani-yyp0qe`). Found and switched to the real **crisp vector** source. Investigation: `fwsprimary`'s production `Wetlands/MapServer` is an empty dynamic shell (export + query both 500); the polygons the Mapper draws live in the **staging** service `‚Ä¶/server/rest/services/Test/Wetlands_gdb_split/MapServer`, where layer 0 ("Wetlands") is empty and the data is split into **layer 1 = Wetlands_CONUS_East** and **layer 2 = Wetlands_CONUS_West** (Texas is in West). **Fix:** `STATEWIDE.wetlands` is now a **`kind:"dynamic"`** esri `dynamicMapLayer` (esri `/export f=image`, like FEMA) at that MapServer with **`layers:[1,2]`** (covers the lower-48). **Verified in a real headless browser** (`gis-verify/wetlands-verify.{html,mjs}`, now the vector variant): the `‚Ä¶/export?‚Ä¶layers=show:1,2&f=image` request returns **HTTP 200 `image/png`** with **true-shape polygons + NWI class labels** (PFO1A / PSS1A / PUBH‚Ä¶) over Sheldon Lake ‚Äî screenshot `gis-verify/wetlands-fwsprimary-vector-verified.png` ‚Äî and the host is **CORS-clean** (echoes `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://planyr.io`). Source-scale-gated (layer `minScale ~1:250k`) ‚Äî like FEMA, zoom to about city level to see polygons. **CAVEAT (also in the code comment):** `Test/` is a USFWS **staging** path during their host migration; it may be renamed/removed when the production `Wetlands/MapServer` is repopulated (revisit then, or if `fwspublicservices` recovers) ‚Äî and the app already degrades to the honest "service unavailable" path (B129) if it disappears. lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **197 tests** ¬∑ build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Browser pass logged as **VERIFICATION V27**. How I found it: loaded the live USFWS Mapper in a headless browser to read its real service refs (`gis-verify/mapper-capture.mjs`).

### B117 ‚Äî Draw-tool palette can't scroll to its bottom on desktop ‚Üí SHAPES group unreachable `[Site Planner]` (bug)
`[x]` Repro: open Site Planner on a normal laptop viewport; the dark draw-tool rail (the **Tools / Site elements / Shapes** palette) is taller than the screen but won't scroll to the end ‚Äî the SHAPES group and anything below it sits past the fold and is unreachable. Expected: the rail scrolls cleanly to its last row at any viewport height, with the full list reachable. **Confirmed cause:** the rail container in `SitePlanner.jsx` sets `overflowY: narrow ? "auto" : "visible"`, so it only scrolls inside the phone/`narrow` overlay (B113); on the desktop path `overflowY:"visible"` + `flex:none` with no height bound lets the content spill off-screen with no scrollbar. Fix: give the rail `overflow-y:auto` on the desktop path too and bound its height to the viewport ‚Äî it already sits in a flex row, so add `min-height:0` on the column so it can shrink-and-scroll; confirm the last row is fully visible.
> Not a dup of **B107** (that reworked the *mode-tab* column ‚Äî Yield/Parcel/Element/Aerial/Overlay/Setup ‚Äî and found it already fits) or **B113** (that added only the *phone* slide-in overlay, with its own `overflow:auto`; the desktop layout was left byte-identical, so this desktop scroll gap is untouched). Michael described it as the "left" rail; in the current build this palette renders on the **right** (dark chrome) ‚Äî same element, identified here by its Tools/Site-elements/Shapes content. Pairs with **B118** (shorter rows overflow less often), but this is the underlying bug ‚Äî even a short rail must stay fully reachable.
> ‚úÖ **Done 2026-06-16** (branch `claude/eager-brahmagupta-7sagg0`). The rail container (`SitePlanner.jsx`) now uses `overflowY: "auto"` on the desktop path too (was `narrow ? "auto" : "visible"`) plus `minHeight: 0`, so it shrinks inside the height-bounded flex body row and scrolls to its last row at any viewport height ‚Äî the SHAPES / Measure / Annotate groups below the fold are now reachable. Desktop layout otherwise byte-identical; the phone/`narrow` overlay path is unchanged. lint 0 errors ¬∑ 138 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B118 ‚Äî Tighten draw-tool button height to fit more of the rail without scrolling `[Site Planner]` (task)
`[x]` The site-element and shape buttons are two-line (a primary label + a secondary descriptor ‚Äî e.g. "single-load", "drive / court", "24‚Ä≤ travel", "free draw", "back-in storage", "detention basin") with generous vertical padding, so the rail overflows and forces scrolling for the core tools. Increase density: (1) cut the top/bottom padding on each row and tighten the gap/line-height between the primary label and the secondary descriptor; (2) shrink the secondary-descriptor text one step (it's supporting detail, not the label). Goal: fit at least through the whole **Site elements** group without scrolling on a standard laptop. Keep rows tappable ‚Äî don't drop below ~36‚Äì40px row height (this is density, not shrinking to unusable). Apply uniformly across **Tools / Site elements / Shapes** so the rail reads consistently.
> Builds on **B109**, which standardized these rows to one two-line anatomy (icon + label + one-word sub-label) ‚Äî keep that uniform anatomy, just tighten it. Complements **B117** (the scroll bug): denser rows overflow less, while B117 guarantees the remainder stays reachable.
> ‚úÖ **Done 2026-06-16** (branch `claude/eager-brahmagupta-7sagg0`). Tightened the shared right-rail button style in `SitePlanner.jsx`: `rbtn` vertical padding 8px‚Üí6px (uniform across Tools / Site elements / Shapes / Measure / Annotate), the two-line primary label given `lineHeight: 1.15`, and the secondary descriptor shrunk 9.5px‚Üí9px with `lineHeight: 1.05`. Two-line rows drop ~47px‚Üí~40px (still tappable); the whole Site elements group now fits without scrolling on a standard laptop, and pairs with B117 for anything still below the fold. lint 0 errors ¬∑ 138 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B127 ‚Äî Measure-type dropdown (and the other rail/header menus) render behind/clipped by the rail `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted B127 ‚Äî next after B126)*
`[x]` Repro: in the right tool rail, open the **Measure** tool's mode dropdown (the **‚ñæ** caret next to "Length"). Expected: the menu opens above all adjacent UI ‚Äî fully visible and clickable. Actual: the flyout was partially/fully hidden ‚Äî clipped by the rail, painting "behind" the neighbouring rail/zoom rail. **Root cause = the two classic CSS layer traps, both introduced/exposed by B117.** The rail container (`SitePlanner.jsx`) sets `position:relative; zIndex:30` (a **trapped stacking context** ‚Äî caps every child's z-index against the canvas/map siblings no matter how high) **and** `overflowY:"auto"` (B117 changed this from `narrow ? "auto" : "visible"` to always `"auto"` to fix the desktop scroll-to-bottom). Per the CSS spec, once `overflow-y` is non-visible the `overflow-x:visible` **computes to `auto`** too, so the rail now clips horizontally ‚Äî and these flyouts open sideways out of the rail (`right: calc(100% + 10px)`), so they got cut off. You cannot have a vertically-scrolling rail that also lets a child overflow sideways, so bumping z-index can't fix it.
> ‚úÖ **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/sweet-pasteur-5kd1p4`). Built the **durable, shared fix** the brief asked for: a new reusable **portal** popover layer `src/shared/ui/AnchoredMenu.jsx` (`ReactDOM.createPortal` to `document.body`) ‚Äî the menu mounts at the document root, escaping **both** any ancestor stacking context **and** any `overflow:auto` clipping at once. It positions itself against its trigger via `getBoundingClientRect` (`position:fixed`, viewport-clamped, repositions on scroll/resize), measures-then-reveals so there's no flash, and keeps the click-away backdrop. z-index 4000/4001 = the app's existing modal layer (matches `ParcelDrawing`'s portal; above the map, below `AuthPanel`'s 5000). **Migrated every instance of the trapped pattern in the workspace** ‚Äî not just the one menu: the 5 rail flyouts (**Measure**, Boundary, Building dock, Car-Parking rows, Road width) and the 3 header menus (**Site ‚ñæ**, **Plan ‚ñæ**, **File ‚ñæ**). Visuals/placement/widths unchanged. lint 0 errors ¬∑ **152 tests** ¬∑ build green; lazy chunk split intact (`AnchoredMenu` rides in the `SitePlannerApp` chunk). **Follow-up candidate (not done):** the Document Review `ReviewsBar` "Reviews ‚ñæ" dropdown is the same class in the other workspace ‚Äî it opens downward and uses its own styling (not `menuPanel`), so it's left for a focused doc-review pass; the shared component now lives in `src/shared/ui/` so adopting it there is trivial. Browser pass queued as **VERIFICATION V16**. **Not merged to `main` ‚Äî owner review first.**

### B126 ‚Äî Data-loss ROOT FIX: sync kept the whole last-saved copy (a thinner one erased a fuller one) + automatic local backups `[Persistence]` (bug) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS, critical
`[x]` Follow-up to **B124/B125**. The shipped B124 fix stopped whole *sites* from vanishing on pull, but buildings could still disappear *inside* a surviving site: both `saveSite`/the cloud and `mergePulledSites` reconciled two copies by **whole-record newer-`updatedAt`-wins** with **no content merge**, so any copy saved last with fewer buildings (a stale tab, a second device, a partial/mid-load save) silently overwrote the fuller one ‚Äî and `pullCloud`'s re-push then propagated the thin copy to the cloud. Owner hit this: ~12 projects emptied while the last-edited one (SCHIEL, intact at ~10 buildings / 24 building-elements once bump-outs are counted) survived. Free-tier Supabase ‚Üí no PITR, so the emptied sites weren't server-recoverable; owner opted to **fix forward** (quick to recreate) and harden against recurrence.
- Part of: **B648** (Persistence & Sync epic).
> **Fixed 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/zen-sagan-9otrll`).** (1) **Content-preserving merge** ‚Äî `siteModel.js` gains `mergeSiteContent(a,b)`: scalar/meta come from the newer copy, but every drawn collection (els, markups, measures, callouts, parcels, sheetOverlays, parcelDrawings, elevation cross-sections) is **unioned by id**, so a building present in EITHER copy survives; a stripped inline raster is healed from the copy that still has it. `mergePulledSites` now uses it (was whole-record newer-wins) and re-pushes whenever the merge produced more content than the cloud had, so kept work reaches the cloud instead of being stranded. (2) **Automatic local version history** ‚Äî `storage.js` snapshots the prior version of a site on every `saveSite` into a capped, slimmed (geometry-only), local-only ring (`planarfit:sites:history:v1`); `snapshotVersion`/`listVersions`/`getVersion` back the planner's **Plan ‚ñæ ‚Üí Version history‚Ä¶** dialog, which restores any earlier version (the replaced state is itself snapshotted, so a restore is reversible). **Trade-off documented:** a delete made in only one copy can reappear once (recoverable by deleting again) ‚Äî per-item tombstones are the fully-correct future hardening, and the version history makes any surprise recoverable meanwhile. lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **152 tests** (8 new: union-keeps-all, two-device union, per-id newer-wins, image-heal, end-to-end no-thinning + re-push, history captures/restores/de-dupes) ¬∑ build green. Browser pass queued as **VERIFICATION V15**. **Not merged to `main` ‚Äî owner verification first.**
> **‚òÖ Status corrected 2026-06-17 (this session): MERGED + DEPLOYED** via **PR #83** ‚Äî confirmed live on planyr.io (the deployed `SitePlannerApp` chunk carries `planarfit:sites:history:v1` + the **Plan ‚ñæ ‚Üí Version history‚Ä¶** dialog). The "not merged" note is stale; the Version history restore is available to the owner now (key to **B136** recovery ‚Äî local/per-device only). Effectively DONE (eligible to move to ‚úÖ Done).

### B127 ‚Äî Two-tab convergence: a stale tab could thin the durable store; open tabs didn't live-sync `[Persistence]` (bug) ‚Äî follow-up to B126
`[x]` Found by the **V15** cohort run. **No data loss** (B126's automatic backup recovered the fuller copy), but a rough edge: with the SAME site open in two tabs, a stale tab's save/flush **blind-overwrote** the durable store with its thinner copy, so the two tabs could **disagree on reload** until the stale tab closed + one edit healed it.
> **Fixed 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/zen-sagan-9otrll`).** (A) **`storage.js` `saveSite` now folds a stale write into the store** instead of blind-overwriting: a per-TAB `lastSeenAt[id]` (set by `loadSite`/`saveSite`) distinguishes "I'm the current writer" (plain replace ‚Üí single-tab deletes still stick) from "another tab advanced the store since I last synced" (content-merge via `mergeSiteContent` ‚Üí never thins). So the durable store + every reload now always hold the union. (B) **`SitePlanner.jsx` adds an idle-guarded `storage`-event listener** that folds another tab's save into the open canvas (union ‚Äî never drops either tab's work; only-if-changed to avoid churn/ping-pong; skipped while mid-drag/draw/typing), so two open tabs converge **live** without a reload. 2 new unit tests (stale-write folds; single-tab delete still sticks). lint 0 err ¬∑ **154 tests** ¬∑ build green. Re-test under **VERIFICATION V15** (two-tab case).
> **‚òÖ Status corrected 2026-06-17 (this session): MERGED + DEPLOYED** via **PR #89** ‚Äî the persistence cluster (content-merge, version history, two-tab fold) is confirmed in the live planyr.io bundle. Effectively DONE (eligible to move to ‚úÖ Done).

### B130 ‚Äî Parking tool: "Split into rows" tiles single-loaded rows ‚Äî generate double-loaded modules + correct curb logic + rename `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B130 ‚Äî next after B129)*
`[x]` **Repro:** draw a car-parking area, click **"Split into rows."** **Current (wrong):** the field is tiled as `[stall row + drive aisle]` repeated down its whole depth ‚Äî single-loaded throughout, a full aisle against every row (the most pavement-hungry layout; ~40% more depth per row than needed, so fewer rows fit and stall yield comes in low). **Expected:** tile a **double-loaded module** ‚Äî two stall rows sharing one aisle.
- **Generation logic.** Repeating module = double-loaded: stall row + aisle + stall row. At 90¬∞: 18‚Ä≤ + 24‚Ä≤ + 18‚Ä≤ = **60‚Ä≤ module**; stall width 9‚Ä≤ default. **Single-load only where geometry forces it:** the top row against a building face/sidewalk, a row against a property line, or a final strip too shallow for a full 60‚Ä≤ bay. **Remainder:** if the last band can't fit a full 60‚Ä≤ module, drop a single-loaded row or hand the strip back as leftover/landscape ‚Äî **never insert another full aisle to fill it.**
- **Orientation:** rows parallel to the building face by default; fall back to the polygon's **longest edge**.
- **Parameters to expose:** stall width (8.5‚Äì9.5‚Ä≤, default 9‚Ä≤), stall depth (default 18‚Ä≤), aisle width (default 24‚Ä≤ at 90¬∞), parking angle (90/60/45¬∞), single- vs. double-loaded.
- **Curb logic (6‚Ä≥ curb).** Apply one 6‚Ä≥ curb around the **perimeter** of the paving, wherever pavement meets non-paving (dirt, landscape, building face, property line). **Never at internal seams** ‚Äî aisle-to-stall-row inside a module, or stall-row-backing-onto-stall-row between stacked modules (that's continuous paving). **No curb across drive-aisle openings** where vehicles pass (entry throat, aisle-to-aisle, aisle-to-street) ‚Äî there pavement meets pavement, so the meets-non-paving rule already skips it. A **dead-end aisle terminating against landscape DOES get a curb** (terminus, not blockage). Test: vehicles cross the edge ‚Üí gap, no curb; vehicles stop at the edge ‚Üí curb.
- **Rename:** change the button **"Split into rows" ‚Üí "Split rows/aisles"** (it splits the field into stall rows + drive aisles). *(Owner 2026-06-17: explicitly NOT "Generate parking" / "Lay out stalls" ‚Äî those don't fit; "Split rows/aisles" is the label.)*
- **Out of scope (tracked separately, large lift):** clipping the field to the parcel boundary.
> **Dedupe / reconcile ‚Äî EXTEND the existing parking + curb code; do NOT build a parallel generator.** A double-loaded parking engine, the depth-stepping model, and a derived-curb system already exist (built in **PR #38** + **PR #86**), so most of NEW-1 is implemented and only specific gaps are genuinely open. Map of exists-vs-open:
> - **Double-loaded field-fill is already built and correct** ‚Äî not single-loaded. `carStalls(w,h,cfg)` (`SitePlanner.jsx` ~375) tiles `mod = 2¬∑rowDepth + aisle` (two stall rows per aisle) with a single-loaded trailing remainder only when a full 60‚Ä≤ module won't fit; defaults are already `stallW 9 / stallDepth 18 / aisle 24 / parkAngle 90` (~line 656) with 90/60/45¬∞ + per-element `cfg` overrides surfaced (Setup ~5765, per-element ~5380). So the **un-split** field already matches the spec (**B69**, Done). **The "single-loaded throughout" repro is `splitParkingRows` (~line 3619), not the fill:** clicking "Split into rows" evenly divides the depth into N independent one-row elements, each of which re-renders single-loaded ‚Äî that is the live bug to fix.
>   - **Open Q (confirm, not blocking):** is the complaint the **split output**, or do you also see single-loading in the **un-split** field? The fill code double-loads, so if it's the latter please attach a screenshot. Filed `[ ]` (actionable either way); resolve before the curb-rule pass.
> - **Genuinely OPEN net-new work:** (1) make **`splitParkingRows` preserve double-loaded modules** (split at module boundaries / keep two rows + one aisle together, not one row + a full aisle each); (2) **free-field auto-orientation** ‚Äî rows parallel to the building face, else the polygon's **longest edge** (today a free rectangle just uses its own local axes; building-attached "first bay against the wall" is already **B119**); (3) the **fuller curb rule** ‚Äî `curbEdgesOf` (~line 499) today only curbs the **terminal depth-ends + a sidewalk transition**, with no full-perimeter / skip-vehicle-opening / dead-end-terminus logic ‚Äî extend it to NEW-1's meets-non-paving / never-internal-seam / no-curb-across-openings / curb-a-dead-end contract; (4) the **button rename**.
> - **Already DONE ‚Äî reconcile against, do not re-do:** **B69** (one-row stepping `parkDepthForRows`/`parkRowsForDepth`/`growParking`), **B70** (derived 6‚Ä≥ curbs: drawn + counted in `curbAreaOf` + excluded from the displayed dimension, never building-side), **B71** (per-element 6‚Ä≥‚Üí12‚Ä≥ trailer curb `curbW`), **B119** (building-attached first-bay-against-wall orientation), **B120** (stepping verify), **B59/B29** (per-element `cfg` plumbing), **B6** (sidewalk-resize depth contamination), **B78** (degenerate-config freeze guard). Keep B70's calc/draw/dimension three-way contract and the 6‚Ä≥ default ‚Äî NEW-1 only tightens *where* the curb lands. Polygon/irregular pads are currently out of scope for the auto-curb (B70 deferred nuance); confirm whether NEW-1's perimeter rule should now cover them.
> **Progress 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/blissful-feynman-0ijfr2`): the core split bug + the rename are SHIPPED; the two enhancements stay open.** Fixed the actual repro ‚Äî `splitParkingRows` now splits a field into **double-loaded module elements** (two stall rows sharing one drive aisle, 60‚Ä≤ at 90¬∞) plus **at most one trailing single-loaded row** for an odd remainder, instead of one single-loaded `[row + aisle]` per row. **Total depth and stall count are preserved exactly** (the piece depths sum back to the field's `h`), so yield doesn't change ‚Äî only the split geometry. Extracted the pure math to **`lib/parking.js`** (`parkDepthForRows` / `parkRowsForDepth` / new `splitParkingPieces`) with **`test/parking.test.js`** (10 cases: module split, odd-remainder single row, total-depth preservation, sub-row fold-in, ‚â§1-module no-op, degenerate-config guard). Renamed the button **"Split into rows" ‚Üí "Split rows/aisles"** in both places (the selected-element panel + the right-click menu) and tightened its gate to `‚â•3` rows (it now appears only when a split yields ‚â•2 pieces). The **un-split** field already double-loaded via `carStalls` (B69), so it's untouched. lint **0 errors** ¬∑ **185 tests** (10 new) ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Shipped to `main` (owner said push to main). **Browser pass DONE** ‚Äî self-verified live 2026-06-17 in a headless browser (rename + double-loaded split confirmed on planyr.io / branch preview), logged as **VERIFICATION V24**. **Still OPEN (deliberately deferred this pass):** (2) **free-field longest-edge auto-orientation** ‚Äî interacts with B119's building-attached orientation + the depth-stepping, wants its own careful pass; (3) the **fuller curb rule** (full perimeter / skip internal seams + vehicle openings / curb a dead-end terminus) ‚Äî extends `curbEdgesOf`, which feeds yield math, and has the open design Q above (polygon pads + opening detection), so not safe to guess into production yet.
> **Progress 2026-06-17 (follow-ons SHIPPED ‚Äî B130 now fully DONE):** items (2) + (3) landed via **PR #100**. **(2) free-field orientation** ‚Äî a parking box drawn deeper than it is long swaps `w`/`h` and rotates 90¬∞ at draw-commit, so its rows run along the long edge (identical footprint; preset-depth + building-attached draws untouched). **(3) fuller curb rule** ‚Äî `curbEdgesOf` rewritten to curb the whole perimeter where pavement meets non-paving and skip it where pavement meets pavement (drive openings, continuous paving, the seam between split modules), via the new pure, unit-tested `edgeAbutsPaving` (sample-just-outside adjacency). **Building-side curb DECISION (resolved): NO curb against the bare building face** ‚Äî owner-confirmed 2026-06-17 ("no curbs against buildings, that doesn't make sense"); **B70 stands and B130's original "building face" wording is overridden.** A sidewalk between still gets a transition curb. lint 0 ¬∑ **191 tests** (+6 adjacency) ¬∑ build green. **Self-verified live** (headless): a tall field orients its rows along the long edge; an isolated pad shows a full-perimeter curb; a split field stays continuous with no seam curbs ‚Äî logged as **VERIFICATION V25**. The only remaining piece ‚Äî clipping the field to the parcel boundary ‚Äî was always out of scope here and is now filed as its own item **B131**.

### B131 ‚Äî Print-dialog toggle to include the site-plan overlay in the printed/exported output `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B130, renumbered B131 after `main`'s #95 concurrently took B130 for the parking "Split rows/aisles" fix ‚Äî the implementing commit + branch still say B130)*
`[x]` Add a checkbox to the print-frame dialog (the Letter/Tabloid + Landscape/Portrait toolbar) that controls whether the placed **site-plan overlay** (B72 ‚Äî the drag-dropped PDF/image backdrop) is included in the print / PDF / PNG output. **Confirm-before-build resolved:** both the print dialog and the overlay live in **Site Planner** (`SitePlanner.jsx`) ‚Äî the print-frame toolbar's `printPaper`/`printOrient` and the `sheetOverlays` layer ‚Äî **not** Document Review, so it was built here (no retag). All design points met: label **"Print overlay"** (a print-action verb, not the screen-toggle "Show"); the control is **only shown when an overlay is actually loaded** (no dead control); it **defaults to the overlay's on-screen visibility** (checked when a rendered overlay is on screen); and it acts as a **master include/exclude** across multiple overlays, forward-compatible with a future per-overlay hide (`visible !== false`).
> ‚úÖ **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/adoring-pascal-clrp0k`). **WYSIWYG by construction:** the export path **clones the live SVG**, so an included overlay keeps its exact transform ‚Äî real-world feet‚Üípixel position (EPSG:2278-anchored origin), scale, rotation, opacity, and page selection (the chosen page is already the rasterized `src`) ‚Äî composited **above the aerial backdrop** in the same z-order as on screen. New pure helper `lib/overlayPrint.js` (`isOverlayPrintable` / `printableOverlays` / `hasPrintableOverlay`) is the single source of truth for the checkbox's visibility + default and the compositing filter; `buildExportSvg(frame, includeOverlay)` tags each overlay raster `data-overlay-image` (kept or dropped per the toggle) and the on-canvas selection chrome + unsynced placeholder `data-export="skip"` (so neither ever prints). Also fixed a **latent inconsistency**: overlays used to **silently vanish from output whenever an aerial underlay was present** (the underlay pass stripped *all* `<image>`s) ‚Äî now overlays are honored in both **Print** and **PNG export**. lint 0 errors ¬∑ **181 tests** (6 new in `test/overlayPrint.test.js`) ¬∑ build green; lazy chunk split intact. Browser pass queued as **VERIFICATION V24**. **Shipped to `main` via PR #96** (owner said "ship").

### B113 ‚Äî Planner is not responsive at mobile width `[Site Planyr / UI]` (feature)  *(branch "B99")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-16.** At ~390px the fixed 168px right rail + the left rail/panel consume most of the width and the canvas collapses to a sliver; the header wraps. The planner is effectively desktop-only. Needs a mobile treatment (collapsible/off-canvas rails, a bottom toolbar). Larger effort ‚Äî roadmap, not a quick fix. (Audit L1; see `ui-audit/screens/planner-mobile.png`.)
> Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`): a `matchMedia("(max-width: 760px)")` `narrow` flag drives a phone treatment ‚Äî **every mobile style is `narrow ?`-gated, so the desktop layout is byte-identical**. When narrow: (1) the right 168px tool palette no longer eats width ‚Äî it **slides in from the right as an overlay** (with a tap-to-dismiss backdrop) summoned by a floating **"‚úé Tools"** button, and auto-closes when you pick a tool so you can draw; (2) the left menu panel (Yield/Parcel/‚Ä¶) **overlays the canvas** (`position:absolute`, capped to `min(320px, 100vw-74px)`) instead of pushing it, its drag-resize handle hidden; (3) the thin 54px left rail stays inline; (4) the header scrolls horizontally instead of wrapping (condensed gap/padding). Net: the canvas keeps full usable width on a phone. Verified headless at 390√ó844: `planner-mobile.png` (collapsed + FAB), `planner-mobile-tools.png` (tool drawer), `planner-mobile-panel.png` (yield drawer); desktop `planner-plan.png` unchanged. Live touch pass logged in `VERIFICATION.md` V10. lint 0 ¬∑ 138 tests ¬∑ build green.

---

### B15 ‚Äî Import JSON drops callouts & markups, and leaves stale ones on the canvas `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS, high
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. `importJSONFile` (SitePlanner.jsx ~2459) set parcels/els/measures/settings/underlay but **not** `callouts`/`markups`, although `exportJSON` writes them. Result: (a) imported callouts/markups are discarded; (b) the currently-open plan's callouts/markups aren't reset, so they bleed into the imported plan and get persisted on the next autosave. Fix: `setCallouts(d.callouts || [])` + `setMarkups(d.markups || [])` in import (symmetric with export).

### B16 ‚Äî `isBlankSite` ignores `markups` ‚Üí markup/encumbrance-only sites are silently deleted on leave `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS, high
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. `isBlankSite` (SitePlanner.jsx ~795) omits `markups`, so a site whose only work is drawn markups or a plotted metes-and-bounds **encumbrance** is treated as blank: the autosave guard skips it and `persistOrDrop` *deletes* the un-located record on unmount. Fix: include `markups` in `isBlankSite` and in the autosave guard object.

### B17 ‚Äî Doc Review local mirror saved without `updatedAt`; `reconcile` compares mismatched fields `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS risk, high
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. `buildSnapshot` (DocReview.jsx/Stitcher.jsx) omits `updatedAt`, so `flushLocal()‚ÜíwriteDraft` mirrors a record whose `data.updatedAt` is stale/absent; `reconcile` then compares cloud `updatedAt` vs draft `_localAt` (different events/clocks) and can let a stale local draft shadow a newer cloud copy. Fix: stamp `updatedAt: Date.now()` in `buildSnapshot` so the mirror and the cloud `data` carry a consistent timestamp.

### B18 ‚Äî Last-debounce-window edits flush local-only and are lost when `pullCloud` blind-replaces on next login `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî DATA-LOSS, high
`[x]` The beforeunload/visibility/unmount/site-switch flushes call `saveSite` (local cache) but **never** `pushSiteToCloud`; `pullCloud` then *replaces* the per-user cache wholesale on next login, discarding any edit made in the final ~400 ms before close that the debounced cloud push missed. Fix options: make `pullCloud` merge by `updatedAt` (keep the newer side), and/or push on `pagehide` via `fetch(..., {keepalive:true})`/`sendBeacon`. (Bigger change ‚Äî left for review.)

### B19 ‚Äî Resume-on-mount re-saves the just-loaded review with a fresh `updatedAt` `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/b19-resume-resave`): `useReviewPersistence` exposes `suspendSave(ms)` which parks a suspend window; the autosave effect early-returns while it's active. `loadSingleReview` arms it once (single commit); `loadStitch` arms it at start and re-arms before its async `setPdfs`/`setPlaced` commits, so a programmatic load never re-stamps just-loaded data with a fresh `updatedAt`. `loadSingleReview`/`loadStitch` set state ‚Üí the debounce deps change ‚Üí autosave writes the loaded snapshot back with `updatedAt: now`, so a resume can stamp stale data as newest and (multi-tab) clobber a newer cloud edit. Fix: a `suspendSave` ref set during programmatic load, early-returning from the autosave effect for that tick.

### B21 ‚Äî Down overlay re-probes & repaints the error banner every 45 s forever; background errors share the click-instruction slot `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, high
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. `PROBE_TTL` (40 s) < the 45 s re-probe interval, so a genuinely-down enabled layer re-probes and re-fires `onError` every tick, repainting the bottom-left banner indefinitely; that banner is also the only `err` slot, so a background layer hiccup masks the "click a lot" guidance until a user action clears it. Fix: de-duplicate identical error toasts and auto-clear background layer errors after a timeout.

### B22 ‚Äî Rapid parcel clicks race ‚Üí duplicate selection + leaked highlight polygon `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/map-gis-robustness`) ‚Äî defensive: `handleClick` now removes any pre-existing hilite for a key before overwriting (no orphaned `L.polygon`) and dedupes the `selected` entry by key (no double-counted acreage). NOTE: in the current code the toggle check runs AFTER the `await`, so two clicks resolve sequentially into a correct toggle and the described double-add doesn't actually reproduce ‚Äî these guards close the theoretical gap without an in-flight ref that would drop a legitimate second-lot click. `handleClick` is async with no sequence guard; two fast clicks on the same lot can both take the "add" branch (the toggle check runs before the first `await` resolves), producing a duplicate `selected` entry (double-counted acreage) and an orphaned `L.polygon` left on the map. Fix: guard with an in-flight ref / request id, and remove any pre-existing hilite for a key before overwriting.

### B23 ‚Äî `ditchStats` returns `NaN` profile distance for a single elevation sample `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, high
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. elevation.js only guards `length === 0`; with exactly one sample, `i/(n-1) = 0/0 = NaN` flows into the cross-section profile/labels. Fix: treat `< 2` samples as insufficient (`return null`), letting the caller's "no samples" path handle it.

### B24 ‚Äî `measureLabel` prints `"NaN"` / can crash on degenerate measurement points `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. takeoff.js `measureLabel`/`measureValue` have no finite/length guard; an empty `distance` markup dereferences `m.pts[0].x` (throws) and short polys can render `"NaN ft/ac"`. Fix: guard missing/short `pts` and non-finite values.

### B25 ‚Äî Metes-and-bounds: curve calls silently parsed as straight chords; no unreadable-call flagging `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, high
`[x]` `CALL_RE` has no `radius`/`arc`/`delta`/`chord` handling; given a curve call it matches the chord bearing+distance and dead-reckons it as a straight segment, with no `dropped`/`unparsed` signal ‚Äî so the traverse is silently wrong and the UI can't warn. (Roadmap already wants "parse curves; flag unreadable.") Fix: detect curve calls and either tessellate the arc or return `{unparsed:true, raw}` so callers can flag them.
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from the round-2 correctness PR ‚Äî flagging/tessellating curve calls needs parser + UI work validated against real deed text (risk of breaking the working straight-call parser for Harris/FBCAD); left Open for a focused pass with sample deeds.

### B26 ‚Äî Metes-and-bounds: parser gaps (dash-DMS unparsed, deg>90 accepted, loose closure floor) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): (a) `-` added to the degree/minute separator classes so `S 12-15 W` parses; (b) `parseCalls` skips any call with `deg > 90`; (c) the `pathCloses` absolute floor dropped 25 ft ‚Üí 5 ft (the 2% relative term kept), so a small lot can't falsely "close" on 25 ft of misclosure. (a) The header advertises `S 12-15 W` but the DMS separator classes exclude `-`, so dash-separated bearings return `[]` (silently dropped). (b) The degrees group `[0-9]{1,3}` accepts a quadrant bearing > 90¬∞ (e.g. `N 145 E`) and plots it. (c) `pathCloses` floors tolerance at `max(25, 2%¬∑perim)` so a small lot can be declared "closed" with a 25-ft misclosure, hiding parse errors. Fix: add `-` to the separator classes; reject/flag `deg>90`; drop/shrink the absolute closure floor and always surface the misclosure.

### B27 ‚Äî Enter-to-commit power-line trace uses stale `tracePts`/`traceMode` `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed in audit PR. The keydown effect closes over `traceMode`/`tracePts` but they're not in its dep array, so pressing Enter to finish a trace reads stale values (often `[]`). Fix: add `traceMode`/`tracePts` to the effect deps (double-click already worked because it's a fresh prop).

### B28 ‚Äî `mergeRings` collinear-cleanup threshold is area-based (scale-dependent) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): the corner test now thresholds the perpendicular deviation `|cross|/hypot(c‚àía)` (> 0.1 ft), so it's scale-independent. The corner test `Math.abs(cross) > 1` compares twice-triangle-area in ft¬≤ (scales with edge length), so on long edges a real slight bend can be dropped (or noise kept), distorting a merged parcel boundary/acreage. Fix: threshold the *perpendicular distance* `|cross|/hypot(c‚àía)` against a small foot tolerance.

### B29 ‚Äî Parking +/‚àí shrink guard ignores per-field `cfg` overrides `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): `parkBand(el)` and `canShrink` now derive the band from `cfgOf(el)`, matching `growParking`. `parkBand()`/`canShrink` use global `settings.stallDepth/aisle`, but `growParking` uses `cfgOf(el)`; for a field with per-element cfg the on-canvas ‚àí handle is enabled/disabled against the wrong row size (guard disagrees with the actual op). Fix: compute the band from `cfgOf(el)` in the guard too.

### B30 ‚Äî Markup/measure minimum size is in feet, not pixels ‚Üí silently discarded when zoomed out `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): the minimums are now `3 / view.ppf` ft (a ~3px drag), so a deliberate drag isn't silently dropped at any zoom. The `dist(a,b) >= 2` / `w,h >= 2` minimums are world-feet; at low ppf a deliberate multi-pixel drag can be < 2 ft and the markup/measure is dropped with no feedback. Fix: make the minimum pixel-based (scale by view ppf).

### B31 ‚Äî Two-point Split returns null when both crossings share an edge `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor, low
`[x]` `if (lo.i === hi.i) return null` uses only the extreme-`t` hits, so a valid straight cut across a concave parcel (whose first/last crossings land on the same edge while valid interior crossings exist) silently no-ops. Fix: when `lo.i === hi.i`, pick the two crossings with distinct edge indices that yield two ‚â•3-vertex rings.
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from round-2 ‚Äî the same-edge multi-crossing case is fiddly concave-polygon geometry (minor/low) that's hard to validate without runtime cases; left Open to avoid shipping a bad split.
> **Superseded 2026-06-17 by B128 (Done):** the whole concave multi-crossing case (not just the same-edge sub-case) is now handled by a correct general slice in `lib/polygonSplit.js` ‚Äî see B128 for the validated fix.

### B32 ‚Äî Undo stack polluted by `pushHistory` on no-op edits `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor, high
`[x]` `beginEditCallout` and the dock/type `onChange` handlers call `pushHistory()` before/without an actual change, so cancelling a callout edit or reselecting the same dropdown value adds an undo frame (first Ctrl+Z appears to do nothing). Fix: push history only when a mutation is actually applied.
> Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): the **callout** half is fixed ‚Äî `beginEditCallout` no longer pushes history on open, and `commitEditCallout` pushes only when the text actually changes (cancel or commit-unchanged adds no frame). Re-checked the **dock/type dropdown** half ‚Äî it's a NON-ISSUE: a native `<select>` `onChange` fires only on an actual value change (re-selecting the already-selected option doesn't fire it), so it never adds a spurious frame. The callout half was the real bug; B32 is resolved.

### B33 ‚Äî Doc Review redline shapes (rect/cloud/text) are unselectable except at corner points `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî minor, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/followup-minors`): `hitTest` is now shape-aware ‚Äî rect/cloud select across their whole body (point-in-box ¬± tolerance), text selects across its rendered label box, and measures select on nearest vertex OR segment (so a line body selects, not just its endpoints). `hitTest` measures distance only to stored vertices; rect/cloud store two corners and text one point, so clicking an edge or body never selects them (hard to select/delete redlines). Fix: shape-aware hit testing (point-in-rect, distance-to-segment, text bbox).

### B34 ‚Äî Doc Review single-viewer `render()` calls `setScale` internally ‚Üí re-entrant render race `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-tail-grind`): the fit-to-width scale is computed in its OWN effect (when `scale===0`); `render()` returns early until a concrete scale exists and no longer calls `setScale`, so it's a pure draw and the double-render / `dims` mismatch is gone. In fit-to-width mode `render` calls `setScale(s)` then keeps drawing; the `scale` change re-fires the render effect, so two renders run and `dims`/canvas can briefly mismatch the overlay. Fix: compute fit scale in a separate effect; keep `render` a pure draw at a concrete scale.

### B35 ‚Äî `listProjects` status display is fragile (newest-row-wins; fallback drops status) `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî minor, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/followup-minors`): `listProjects` returns `status:"unknown"` (not `"active"`) when the value is missing or the status read fell back to the no-status query, and `ProjectLibrary` renders a neutral "Status?" placeholder for an unknown status (still settable) ‚Äî so a failed read no longer silently reports every project as "active." (Group-consistency is unchanged: status is group-wide per B7, so newest-row stays a fair representative.) Status is read from whichever group row sorts first by `updated_at`, and if the `status:data->>status` select errors the fallback query omits status so every project shows the default "active" ‚Äî silently misreporting lifecycle. Fix: resolve status group-consistently and surface "unknown" rather than defaulting to active on a failed status read.

### B37 ‚Äî Cloud/auth hardening `[Site Planner / auth]` (bug) ‚Äî low/minor
`[x]` üîß partially fixed in audit PR. (a) **fixed:** `cloudDelete` now scopes by `user_id` AND `id` (was RLS-only). (b) **fixed:** the autosave cloud push had no `.catch`, leaving the save badge stuck on "Saving‚Ä¶" on a rejected upsert. (c) **fixed:** `metaRef` omitted `county`, so a save whose merge-base lacked county could normalize it to null ‚Äî now self-contained. (d) **fixed:** `testConnection` now accepts any https origin (custom Supabase domains) ‚Äî PR #27; the `pullCloud`-failure-empty-library half was fixed separately as B54 (#21).

### B39 ‚Äî PDF.js documents are never `destroy()`'d ‚Üí growing memory leak across a review session `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî leak, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): the prior doc is destroyed before swap (DocReview `setPdfDoc` helper) and the Stitcher destroys docs as they leave state via a diff effect, plus an unmount cleanup in both. `loadPdf` returns a `PDFDocumentProxy` (worker + retained ArrayBuffer); every replacement just overwrites `pdfRef.current` / `pdfs[].doc` (openFile, load, resetSingle, resetStitch, loadStitch) with no `.destroy()`, and there's no unmount cleanup. A repo-wide grep for `.destroy()` in doc-review is empty. Heavy construction sets leak MBs per open; the stitcher holds N docs at once. Fix: destroy the prior doc before replacing, destroy each removed/replaced source in the stitcher, and add an unmount effect that destroys the live doc(s). (Deferred from the audit PR only to avoid editing files a parallel agent was reading ‚Äî will fix in the consolidated pass.)

### B40 ‚Äî Superseded PDF render tasks are never cancelled `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî perf, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): `renderPageToCanvas` now exposes its `RenderTask` (an `onTask` callback); `render()` keeps it in a ref, `cancel()`s the prior one before each render and on unmount, and swallows `RenderingCancelledException`. `renderPageToCanvas` creates `page.render(...)` and only awaits its promise; `render()` discards the stale *result* via the `renderTok` token but never calls `task.cancel()`. Rapid page/zoom changes pile up overlapping renders and can hit PDF.js's "cannot use the same canvas during multiple render operations" (throw/torn output). Fix: return the `RenderTask`, keep a ref, `cancel()` before starting a new one and on unmount; treat `RenderingCancelledException` as a no-op.
> **Amendment ‚Äî residual same-canvas race, fixed 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/modest-turing-wixo1y`).** (Folded in from a coworker-chat note, not a new item.) The B40 fix had a hole: `renderTaskRef.current` is only populated AFTER `await pdf.getPage()` inside `renderPageToCanvas`, so during render #1's getPage await, render #2 starts, finds `renderTaskRef.current` still null (nothing to cancel), and both proceed to `page.render()` on the same canvas ‚Äî the exact "Cannot use the same canvas during multiple render operations" error B40 targeted, swallowed, leaving the canvas on a superseded sheet. **Fix:** `renderPageToCanvas` now takes an `isStale` predicate and checks it right BEFORE `page.render()`; `DocReview.jsx` passes `() => tok !== renderTok.current`, so a render superseded mid-getPage bails (returns null) before it touches the canvas ‚Äî only the newest render draws. Verified: lint 0 ¬∑ 802 tests ¬∑ build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-b322-b40.mjs` shows 0 same-canvas errors across rapid switches (V85). Shipping via PR #240 ‚Üí `main` (with the B326‚ÄìB328 batch).

### B41 ‚Äî Global keydown effects have no deps array ‚Üí re-subscribe on every render (hot path) `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî perf, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): both now bind the `keydown` listener ONCE (`[]` deps) and dispatch through an `onKeyRef` refreshed each render, so the handler keeps live closures without re-subscribing on every render. DocReview.jsx and Stitcher.jsx register their `keydown` listener in an effect with no dependency array (the `}); // eslint-disable-line` pattern), so the handler is removed+re-added after *every* render ‚Äî and `onPointerMove`‚Üí`setCursor` re-renders dozens of times/second while drawing. Fix: register once with `[]` and read `draft`/`sel`/handlers from refs inside `onKey`.

### B42 ‚Äî `SitePlannerApp` rebuilds `siteGroups` (a Map over all sites) every render `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî perf, medium
`[x]` ‚úÖ already addressed (verified 2026-06-15): `siteGroups` is already `useMemo`'d on `[sites, activeSiteId]` in `SitePlannerApp.jsx` (with a "stable identity" comment) ‚Äî the IIFE the audit flagged had since been converted, so no code change was needed. `const siteGroups = (() => { ‚Ä¶ })()` runs unconditionally each render, allocating a fresh array passed as the `sites` prop to `MapFinder` ‚Äî O(n) per render and a referential-stability hazard that defeats child memoization. Fix: `useMemo(‚Ä¶, [sites, activeSiteId])`. (Safe, small ‚Äî will fix in the consolidated pass.)

### B43 ‚Äî `applyUser` auth-event handling has no sequence guard ‚Üí races on fast auth transitions `[Site Planner / auth]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
