`[x]` `applyUser` is async (`setActiveUser` ‚Üí `await pullCloud` ‚Üí `refreshSites`); overlapping auth events (INITIAL_SESSION‚ÜíSIGNED_IN, or fast sign-out/in) can interleave so `prevUid.current`/`clearCloudCache` run out of order and clear/point at the wrong user's cache. Fix: capture a monotonic token before the await and bail stale completions (the file already uses a `live` guard elsewhere).

### B44 ‚Äî Doc Review async refresh/flush lack in-flight guards `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/b19-resume-resave`, alongside B19): (a) the flush now gates its cloud upsert on a `dirtyRef` (set on a real edit, cleared on a successful write), so a single‚Üîstitch toggle with no change no longer re-upserts; (b) `ReviewsBar.refresh` is token-guarded (latest wins) and its outside-click listener moved to its own effect; (c) `ProjectLibrary.refresh` is token-guarded too, so an overlapping/late refresh (incl. `fileDrop`'s) can't clobber newer state. (a) `useReviewPersistence`'s unmount cleanup unconditionally fires a cloud `upsert` on every single‚Üîstitch mode toggle (wasteful double-write even when nothing changed) ‚Äî gate on a dirty flag. (b) `ReviewsBar` re-runs `listReviews()+listProjects()` on every `signedIn` flip while open and has no in-flight guard, so a slow resolution can clobber newer state; (c) `ProjectLibrary.fileDrop` calls `refresh()` in `finally` even if the drawer closed, and overlapping refreshes can interleave. Fix: split outside-click vs fetch effects and add an `ignore`/AbortController guard; `useCallback` the refreshers.

### B45 ‚Äî Stitcher keeps large PNG data URLs for every placed sheet in state `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî perf, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): `renderPageToImage` now returns a `URL.createObjectURL` blob URL (data-URL fallback), and the Stitcher revokes URLs as sheets leave state via a diff effect + unmount cleanup. `renderPageToImage` returns `canvas.toDataURL("image/png")` at scale 2 and each placed sheet stores that multi-MB base64 string in `placed[].href`; reloads/re-binds regenerate them without releasing the old ones (data URLs can't be revoked). Fix: use `canvas.toBlob` + `URL.createObjectURL`, track and `revokeObjectURL` on remove/replace/unmount.

### B46 ‚Äî `LayerPanel` Mapillary token desyncs across its two instances `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` The token is seeded once via `useState(() => mapillaryToken())` in each `LayerPanel` (map + planner both render one); typing in one doesn't update the other's local `tok`, and an externally-set token isn't reflected on reopen. Fix: lift the token to shared state (alongside `overlays`) or read from a subscribable source.
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from the map/GIS robustness PR ‚Äî minor. Clean fix = either lift the token to shared app state (thread alongside `overlays`) or add a tiny pub/sub in `evidenceLayers.js` (`setMapillaryToken` notifies listeners; each `LayerPanel` subscribes via an effect and updates its `tok`). Left Open for that small refactor.

### B47 ‚Äî ArcGIS `where`-clause hardening (LIKE wildcards + auto-detected field names) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî security, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/map-gis-robustness`) ‚Äî **part (b)**: the parcel-lookup `where` builder now validates `idField`/`addrField` against `/^[A-Za-z0-9_.]+$/` before interpolating, so a hostile/compromised (or user-pasted) layer's metadata can't inject a field name that escapes the `scopeWhere` county confinement. **Part (a)** ‚Äî escaping `%`/`_` LIKE wildcards via an `ESCAPE` clause ‚Äî is **deferred** (must verify Harris/FBCAD accept `ESCAPE` before shipping; low risk ‚Äî read-only public data). A security pass found the rest of the app **clean** (Supabase queries are parameterized with no injection surface; RLS/storage policies sound; secrets handled correctly; all text/SVG render paths are React-escaped). The one item: the parcel-lookup `where` builder escapes the user *value*'s quotes (`'`‚Üí`''`, blocks the realistic breakout) but (a) does not escape LIKE wildcards `%`/`_` (a value of `%` enumerates rows ‚Äî minor, public read-only data; the statewide TxGIO/Chambers layer is still confined by `scopeWhere`), and (b) interpolates `idField`/`addrField` **unescaped** ‚Äî and those come from live layer metadata (or a user-pasted "Service / layer URL"), so a hostile/compromised endpoint could inject a field name that defeats the `scopeWhere` county confinement. Read-only/public so not critical. Fix: validate the detected field name against an identifier allowlist (`/^[A-Za-z0-9_.]+$/` and/or `meta.fields.some(f=>f.name===idField)`) before interpolating, and escape `%`/`_` with an `ESCAPE` clause (verify the CAD datasources accept `ESCAPE` before shipping ‚Äî don't break Harris/FBCAD search).

### B48 ‚Äî Client-side Anthropic key (title reader) + reusable HTML-escape helper `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî note, low
`[x]` `lib/titleReader.js` instantiates the Anthropic SDK in the browser with `dangerouslyAllowBrowser:true` and the user's own key from `localStorage` (`planarfit:anthropicKey`). This is the documented BYO-key, no-backend pattern (not a committed/bundled secret), but the key is localStorage-resident and reachable by any script on the origin. Acceptable for a personal tool; harden later by moving the call behind the planned `/server` (key stays server-side) or keeping it in memory only. Also: `printPDF`'s `esc` helper only escapes `&`/`<` (fine for its text/title contexts today) ‚Äî make it also escape `"`/`>` before reusing it in any attribute/style context.

### B49 ‚Äî Document Review file-input validation `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî crash/correctness
`[x]` üîß partially fixed in audit PR. (a) **fixed:** the single-sheet viewer's open/drop accepted ANY file and read the whole thing into memory via `arrayBuffer()` before failing (OOM risk on a huge/non-PDF drop) ‚Äî now validated by type + non-zero size before `loadPdf`, mirroring the stitcher. (b) **left for review:** the stitcher sniffs only by `.pdf` extension, and its multi-file load loop is in a single `try`, so one bad file silently skips the rest ‚Äî wrap each `loadPdf` per-file and size-check.

### B50 ‚Äî Export/print & prompt robustness `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` üîß partially fixed in audit PR. (a) **fixed:** the per-edge setback `window.prompt` treated an empty/whitespace confirm as `0` (`+""===0`) instead of cancelling ‚Äî now requires a non-blank numeric. (b) **left for review:** `exportPNG` swallows raster failures (an `image.onerror` rejects with no `.catch`/feedback; `canvas.toBlob`‚Üínull silently produces no download) and `printPDF` can leave a stranded blank print window if serialization throws after `window.open` ‚Äî wrap both in try/catch with a user alert (and `win.close()` on failure). `titleReader` base64-encodes with no size/type guard. (Export filename sanitization, object-URL revocation, empty-state guards, and calibration numeric guards were all audited and are clean.)
> Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/export-robustness-b50`): part (b) done ‚Äî `exportPNG` now alerts on an image-load / canvas-taint / `drawImage` failure (was a silent no-op + unhandled rejection) and on a null `toBlob` result; `printPDF` wraps its post-`window.open` work in try/catch and calls `win.close()` on failure, so a serialization/inlining throw can't strand a blank "Preparing‚Ä¶" window. Remaining: the `titleReader` base64 size/type guard (BYO-key path, low priority).

### B51 ‚Äî Stitcher async handlers have no in-flight guard ‚Üí re-entrant interleave corrupts placed sheets `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî data-loss, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/async-safety`): `loadStitch` sets a `loadingRef` while it rebuilds `pdfs[]`/`placed[]`, and `openFiles`/`addSheet` bail when it's set ‚Äî so a click during a load can't have its sheet clobbered by the load's blind `setPlaced(out)`. (`bindSource` stays unguarded; it's internal to `openFiles`.) `openFiles`/`addSheet`/`bindSource`/`loadStitch` each `setBusy(true)‚Ä¶await‚Ä¶finally setBusy(false)` but none *reads* busy as a re-entrancy guard, and they mix functional updates (`addSheet`: `setPlaced(arr=>[...arr,‚Ä¶])`) with snapshot overwrites (`loadStitch`: `setPlaced(out)`). Clicking "add sheet" while a load is mid-await lets the load's blind `setPlaced(out)` clobber the just-added sheet. Fix: `busyRef` guard at the top of each, or disable the tray/open while busy; prefer functional updates / reconcile rather than overwrite.

### B52 ‚Äî Doc Review load/open paths lack a cancellation token ‚Üí opening B while A loads mixes the two `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/async-safety`): both load paths capture a `loadTok` at start and bail before each post-await `setState` if a newer open superseded them ‚Äî `loadStitch` before `setPdfs`/`setPlaced`; `loadSingleReview`/`fetchSourceBytes` before `setPdfDoc` (destroying the late doc) ‚Äî so opening B while A loads can't land A's PDF/sheets on B. `loadStitch` (Stitcher) and `openReview`‚Üí`loadSingleReview`‚Üí`fetchSourceBytes` (DocReview) run long await chains (download + loadPdf + render) and then unconditionally `setState` (reviewId, meta, markups, pdfRef, placed) with no "is this still the requested review?" check. Open review A then B quickly and A's late resolution overwrites B's PDF/sheets while ids/markups are B's ‚Äî and the autosave then persists the mix under the wrong id. Fix: capture a token (the requested `rec.id`) in a ref and bail before each post-await `setState` if superseded (mirror the single-sheet `renderTok` pattern). Related to B19.

### B53 ‚Äî In-planner `identifyAt` lacks a token ‚Üí second click shows/adds the wrong parcel `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/map-gis-robustness`): `identifyAt` captures an `identifyTok` at call start and bails before `setIdentifyRes` if a newer click superseded it (matches the single-sheet `renderTok` pattern). `identifyAt` (SitePlanner) sets `{busy:true}`, awaits `resolveLayerUrl`/`queryAtPoint`, then unconditionally `setIdentifyRes({attrs,ring,‚Ä¶})`. Clicking P2 before P1 resolves lets P1 overwrite P2's result, so the panel/`addIdentifiedParcel` use the wrong lot. (Distinct code path from the already-logged MapFinder `handleClick` race, B22.) Fix: a token captured at call start, checked before the post-await `setState`.

### B54 ‚Äî `pullCloud` overwrites the per-user cache with `{}` when a cloud fetch errors `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî data-loss-shaped, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b54-cloud-pull-guard`): `cloudList` now THROWS on a real fetch error (vs. a genuinely-empty result); `pullCloud` catches it, SKIPS the cache write, and returns `{ ok:false }`, so the cache (the user's last-known sites) is preserved; `applyUser` then shows a dismissible "showing your last synced copy" banner instead of a silent empty library. B18's separate half (merge-by-`updatedAt` + `sendBeacon`-on-pagehide) stays open ‚Äî it carries a delete-resurrection subtlety, so it's deliberately left under B18. `cloudList` returns `[]` on *error* (cloudSync.js), so `pullCloud` can't tell "no sites" from "fetch failed" and blind-writes an empty map to the per-user cache; signing in while offline/unreachable thus shows "no sites" and wipes the local cloud cache to empty (data safe in Supabase, but a scary empty state that the user might start recreating into). Fix: have `cloudList` throw/return a status on error; `pullCloud` must skip the cache overwrite on error and surface a "couldn't load" state. (Sharpens B18/B37d into a concrete fix.)

### B55 ‚Äî Fire-and-forget promises without `.catch` ‚Üí unhandled rejections; async continuations touch torn-down objects `[Document Review / Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` `uploadSource(...).then(...)` (DocReview + Stitcher), the `usePersistence` unmount/visibility `flush()` cloud upsert, and the `probeService(...).then(...)` overlay continuation all lack `.catch`, so a rejected Storage/DB/network call is an unhandled rejection. The `probeService` continuation also does `lyr.addTo(map)` after the await with no map-teardown guard (can throw if the map was removed mid-probe). Fix: add `.catch(()=>{})` to the fire-and-forget calls (matching the `pushSiteToCloud(...).catch` convention) and guard `addTo` with `map._loaded`.

### B56 ‚Äî Assorted async UX/robustness `[Site Planner / Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` Grouped: (a) `goAddress` double-submit ‚Äî Enter isn't gated by `busy` (only the Go button is), so two geocodes can race the `flyTo`; (b) transient `overlapWarn` messages each schedule their own `setTimeout(()=>setOverlapWarn(""))` with the id discarded, so a stale clear blanks a newer warning (and `runXSection` has no in-flight guard); (c) `setProjectStatus` upserts group rows serially and ignores per-row failure, returning `ok:true` even if one plan kept the old status (inconsistent group status) ‚Äî use `Promise.allSettled` and report partial failure; (d) the evidence layers drop `moveend` events that arrive while a fetch is in flight (no trailing-edge refresh), so the last view may never load; (e) the doc-review mutation handlers (`del`/`onStatus`/`fileDrop`) call `refresh()` after an await with no open/mounted guard.
> Progress 2026-06-15: (a) shipped in #30, (d) in #33; **(c) done here** (branch `chore/audit-tail-grind` ‚Äî `setProjectStatus` now writes the group's plans in parallel via `Promise.allSettled` and returns partial-failure counts); (e) covered by the B44 in-flight guards (PR #32). **(b)** ‚Äî the `overlapWarn` stale-timer ‚Äî left open: the clean fix is a single timer-ref `flashWarn` helper across ~12 call sites (or a uniform auto-clear effect that would flatten per-message durations); minor UX, not worth churning 12 sites.
> **(b) done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/keen-curie-2ic2cg`) ‚Äî this closes B56 (a/c/d/e already shipped).** A single timer-ref `flashWarn(msg, ms)` helper now owns the warning toast: it cancels any pending auto-clear *before* showing a new message, so a stale timer from an earlier warning can no longer blank a newer one. All 32 message-setting `setOverlapWarn(...)` sites route through it ‚Äî transient ones pass their duration; sticky instructional prompts pass `ms=0` (no auto-clear, but they still cancel a prior timer so a stale clear can't wipe them either). The bare `setOverlapWarn("")` clears (Escape, Cancel, toggle-off) stay as-is: a later `flashWarn` cancels any lingering timer, and a fired timer just re-blanks an already-empty toast. Also fixed the second half of (b): `runXSection` got the missing in-flight guard (`xsecBusyRef` bails a second cross-section run while one is still sampling, reset in `finally`), plus an unmount cleanup that clears the pending timer. lint 0 err ¬∑ 174 tests ¬∑ build green ¬∑ lazy split intact. Browser-verify logged as **V23**.

### B58 ‚Äî Ditch cross-section drops no-data DEM samples without preserving position ‚Üí wrong pond depth `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): `sampleProfile` now keeps no-data as `null` (position preserved), and `ditchStats` places each surviving sample at its true fractional distance and takes banks from the end-most VALID samples. `sampleProfile` (elevation.js) does `.filter(v=>isFinite(v))`, discarding position; `ditchStats` then assumes uniform spacing (`d = i/(n-1)*lenFt`) and takes `bankFt = (elevFt[0]+elevFt[n-1])/2`. 3DEP commonly returns no-data over water/low ground, so surviving samples aren't evenly spaced ‚Äî the profile x-axis is distorted, and if the true endpoints were no-data the "bank" is taken from interior points, so `depthFt` (the headline number applied to the pond's available depth) is wrong. (Distinct from the B23 1-sample NaN.) Fix: map each sample to its fractional position BEFORE filtering; treat leading/trailing no-data as missing banks rather than substituting interior points.

### B59 ‚Äî Parking render/count/panel ignore per-element `cfg` ‚Üí "Drive aisle on the far side" toggle is inert `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, high
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): all three `carStalls(...)` calls now pass `cfgOf(el)` (which merges `{...settings, ...el.cfg}`), so a field's per-element `flipDepth`/depth/aisle/angle take effect in count + render ‚Äî the "Drive aisle on the far side" toggle works. The renderer, metrics, and panel all call `carStalls(el.w, el.h, settings)` with the GLOBAL settings, never `cfgOf(el)`, so a parking field's per-element `cfg` (notably `flipDepth`, and side-parking strips created with `cfg`) has no visual/count effect ‚Äî the panel's "Drive aisle on the far side" checkbox writes `cfg.flipDepth` but nothing reads it for drawing. Trailer rendering correctly uses `el.cfg`, so it's an inconsistency. Fix: use `cfgOf(el)` (which merges `{...settings, ...el.cfg}`) in the three `carStalls(...)` call sites.

### B60 ‚Äî Detention bottom area spuriously non-zero when the basin over-tapers `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî correctness, medium
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): `areaAt` now compares the offset ring's SIGNED area to the footprint's and returns 0 on a winding-sign flip (over-taper), so the bogus positive bottom is gone and the "tapers to a point" guard fires. `detentionStorage`'s `areaAt(down)` returns `offsetPolygon(ring, slope*down)` area; when `slope*depth` exceeds the footprint's inradius, `offsetPolygon` yields an inverted (flipped) ring rather than null, and the self-check uses `polyArea` (abs value), so a bogus positive "bottom" passes. Result: overestimated prismoidal volume and the "Basin tapers to a point ‚Äî reduce depth/slope" guard (`aBottom===0`) never fires. Fix: detect collapse via a signed-area sign flip (or offset distance ‚â• inradius) and return 0.

### B61 ‚Äî Two-click road drawn shorter than it is wide mis-assigns the length/cross axis `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor
`[x]` Road creation sets `w:len, h:roadWidth+2*curb`, but downstream infers the cross axis from `min(w,h)` (curb render `el.w>=el.h`; `roadTravel` `min`; resize `el.h<=el.w`). A stubby road (`len < cross`) has `w<h`, so curbs draw on the wrong edges and the length/width fields control the swapped axis. Fix: tag an explicit length axis at creation and key rendering/resize off it (or clamp `len>=cross`).
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from round-2 ‚Äî a complete fix needs an explicit length-axis tag threaded through render/resize/roadTravel; a creation-time clamp alone leaves resize able to reproduce it. Left Open for that focused change.

### B62 ‚Äî Utility-route corridor leaves a visible gap to the fitting pad `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî minor, cosmetic
`[x]` üîß fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): the route now includes the pad centre (`pts=[source, entry, padC]`), so the corridor reaches the fitting pad. `buildUtilRoute` buffers `pts=[source, entry]` where `entry` is on the wall, but the pad sits outside the wall (`entry + normal*(padSize/2+3)`), so the drawn corridor never reaches the pad. Fix: include the pad center in the route (`pts=[source, entry, padC]`) before buffering. (Math/easement-width/overlap checks are otherwise correct.)

<!-- Filed 2026-06-15 from a parallel chat's backlog (its provisional B13‚ÄìB16 were reassigned here; B1‚ÄìB12 + protocol deduped against existing items, which already shipped). -->

### B67 ‚Äî Attach & mark up a site-plan drawing on a parcel `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-16 (browser-only tranche complete: MVP + increments 2a/2b/2c).** Let the user attach an existing site plan (PDF or JPEG; CAD/DWG out of scope for v1) to a selected parcel and draw on top of it with the full existing markup/element toolset, saved as a persistent, named file tied to that parcel. The pattern mirrors Document Review's **immutable-backdrop + editable-layers-above** model and its Supabase persistence ‚Äî reuse those patterns, don't stand up a parallel drawing engine or store. (Related to but distinct from B14, which files drawings under *projects* in the Document Review workspace; this attaches to a *parcel* in the Site Planner and marks up with the planner's own tools.)
- **Entry point.** After clicking a parcel (e.g. the Schiel Road site), offer "Attach drawing" / "Open markup." Allow **multiple drawings per parcel** (a parcel may have several plan sheets).
- **Backdrop = immutable layer.** The imported PDF/JPEG is the locked backdrop (architecture invariant: imported drawing = immutable; user content lives on editable layers above). PDF ‚Üí rasterize the chosen page to an image for display; if multipage, let the user pick the page.
- **Markup layer = editable.** Reuse the current `els` + `markups` suite (lines, polylines, polygons, text, dimensions, freehand ‚Äî whatever the suite already supports), not a separate drawing engine.
- **Anchoring v1 = pixel-relative to the backdrop.** Store markup coordinates **normalized to the backdrop's intrinsic pixel dimensions (0‚Äì1 fractions, not screen pixels)** so zoom/pan can't corrupt geometry. Nothing may assume the backdrop is screen-sized.
- **Forward-compat for georeference (deferred).** Persist the backdrop's **intrinsic dimensions** and (for a PDF) **page size/DPI** alongside the markup ‚Äî the minimum needed later to compute a pixel‚ÜíTexas-State-Plane (EPSG:2278) transform without re-marking. No georeferencing UI in v1; just don't discard the data that makes it possible.
- **Persistence.** Save as a named file/record associated with the parcel through the existing Supabase save/load + RLS path (**private by default**). Reopening restores backdrop + markup layer.
- **Out of scope v1 (follow-ons):** CAD/DWG import; real-world georeferencing; and **overlaying the marked-up plan onto the live map/aerial** ‚Äî that last is the "overlay onto site" goal that converges with the ‚òÖ north-star ("map ‚Üí drawings ‚Üí latest set") and the shared coordinate spine. Flagged here as a follow-on, not v1.
> Cross-ref 2026-06-15: the deferred "overlay onto the live map/aerial" follow-on noted just above is now a filed item ‚Äî **B72** (drag-drop a site-plan PDF onto the map, place by hand) + **B73** (scale calibration / precise alignment). B67 stays distinct (parcel-attached markup in *pixel* space), but shares the immutable-backdrop + PDF-rasterize + Supabase machinery ‚Äî build them on the same primitives.
> Progress 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **browser-only MVP shipped + screenshot-verified.** Select a parcel ‚Üí Parcel panel ‚Üí **"Ôºã Attach a drawing (PDF / JPG)"** ‚Üí it rasterizes the file (reusing the B72 `openOverlayFile` engine ‚Äî PDF.js via dynamic import, off the main thread; image path too) and opens a full-screen **`ParcelDrawing`** markup modal: the imported file is an **immutable backdrop** and you draw on an editable layer above it with **Select / Pen / Line / Box / Text**, a colour picker, delete, fit/zoom/pan. **Markups are stored PIXEL-RELATIVE (0..1)** of the backdrop's intrinsic dims, so zoom/pan can't corrupt them (verified: a seeded rect + text land exactly on the backdrop). **Multiple drawings per parcel** (listed in the panel, open/delete). **Persistence:** additive Site Model field **`parcelDrawings`** (`SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 4‚Üí5, `parcelDrawingsOf` selector, `slimForCloud` strips the raster like the overlay/underlay); saved via a `saveSite` MERGE + debounced cloud push, so it needs no new wiring through the autosave path and **existing sites are unaffected** (empty field = no-op). **Forward-compat:** the backdrop's intrinsic dims (PDF points) ride with each record for a future pixel‚ÜíEPSG:2278 transform. The modal **portals to `<body>`** so its toolbar clears the shell header (B66 stacking floor). **lint 0 ¬∑ 56 tests (incl. a new parcelDrawings test) ¬∑ build green.** ‚è≥ **Deferred to a focused increment 2 (B67 stays `[ ]`):** (a) the **multipage page-picker** (page 1 only today; `pageCount` is stored); (b) **Supabase Storage backing** of the source file so the raster reloads cross-device (today the raster is local + stripped from the cloud row ‚Üí a re-login shows a "re-attach to view; markups saved" placeholder, exactly like B72's first increment; **markups always persist**); (c) richer markup parity with the planner's full suite (dimensions/measure, freehand smoothing) and on-backdrop markup editing/move (today: draw + select + delete + recolour). Runtime-verified headless via `ui-audit/screens/parcel-drawing.png`; please also try it on planyr.io with a real PDF.
> Progress 2026-06-16 (increment 2a ‚Äî page-picker, branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **multi-page PDFs now prompt a "Pick a sheet" dialog** at attach (lists all `pageCount` pages; the chosen sheet rasterizes via the B72 `rasterizePage`, reusing the already-loaded PDF for page 1; the record is named `"<file> ‚Äî p.N"`). The PDF proxy is kept alive only for the pick then destroyed (no leak). Single-page PDFs and images skip the dialog. Removes limit (a). lint 0 ¬∑ 138 tests ¬∑ build green.
> Progress 2026-06-16 (increment 2b ‚Äî Storage backing, branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **the source file now backs the backdrop in Supabase Storage for cross-device reload.** On attach, the original PDF/image is uploaded (background, fallback-safe) to the existing private `doc-review-files` bucket at `<uid>/parcel-drawings/<siteId>/<drawingId>.<ext>` (new `uploadParcelDrawingFile` in `overlayStorage.js`, uid-first so the existing Storage RLS holds); the returned `storageKey`+`ext` persist on the record (kept through `slimForCloud`, which only strips the raster). Opening a drawing whose local raster is missing (cross-device ‚Äî the cloud row's `src` was stripped) **rehydrates** it: download bytes ‚Üí `rasterizeStoredPdf(bytes, page)` for PDFs, or `downloadOverlayDataUrl` for images, with a "Loading the drawing from the cloud‚Ä¶" state. Delete now also best-effort-removes the stored object. All fallback-safe (logged-out / >50 MB / error ‚Üí keeps the local raster, no regression). Mirrors B72's overlay Storage-backing exactly. This **closes limit (b)**. Remaining for B67: (c) richer markup parity (dimensions/measure, move/edit existing markups). lint 0 ¬∑ 138 tests ¬∑ build green; **cohort to confirm the cross-device round-trip live** (`VERIFICATION.md` V9) ‚Äî can't be exercised headless (needs a signed-in session + Storage).
> Progress 2026-06-16 (increment 2c ‚Äî richer markup, branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **closes limit (c); B67 ‚Üí done.** In `ParcelDrawing.jsx`: (1) **move** any existing markup ‚Äî in Select, press-and-drag a shape/text to reposition it (cursor shows ‚§ßmove; a transient `moving` overlay avoids a save per pointer-move, commits once on release, pixel-relative throughout); (2) **edit text** in place (double-click ‚Äî preserved); (3) a **Measure tool + one-time Scale calibration**: draw a **Scale** line and enter its real length ‚Üí sets feet-per-pixel; the **Measure** tool then labels each line's true length in feet (teal chip), live while drawing. The calibration is stored as a `type:'calib'` markup, so it **persists through the same `onSave(markups)` channel** ‚Äî no extra plumbing. Verified headless (`ui-audit/screens/parcel-drawing.png`: a 0.26-wide measure line reads **124 ft** against a 200-ft / 0.42-wide scale ‚úì). Optional future niceties (not blocking): resize/rotate handles on a selected shape. lint 0 ¬∑ 138 tests ¬∑ build green.

### B69 ‚Äî Paving "+" over-increments from a single-loaded bay `[Site Planner]` (bug)
`[x]` Repro: start from the ~42‚Äì47‚Ä≤ paving cross-section (one parking row + one drive aisle ‚Äî a single-loaded bay = parking on one side of the aisle only) and click "+". **Observed:** it added two parking rows plus another drive aisle (a full double-loaded module). **Expected:** "+" adds exactly one parking row ‚Äî from a single-loaded bay it converts to double-loaded (parking both sides of the existing aisle, ~60‚Äì65‚Ä≤); a new drive aisle is inserted only once the current aisle is already double-loaded; each click steps by one row, never a two-row module. (Filed from a parallel chat as provisional "NEW-1"; minted **B69** here ‚Äî B67/B68 were already taken on `main`.)
> Done 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/nice-wozniak-2k2v9c`). **Root cause:** `growParking` stepped depth by a whole `stallDepth+aisle` band per click, but the renderer/counter (`carStalls`) re-flows the field into *double-loaded modules* (2 rows share 1 aisle), so a single band of added depth re-striped into ~2 extra rows + an aisle ‚Äî the jump the report saw. (Distinct from the already-fixed B29/B59, which were per-element `cfg` plumbing in the same area, not the increment model.) **Fix:** two pure helpers ‚Äî `parkDepthForRows(n)= n¬∑stallDepth + ‚åàn/2‚åâ¬∑aisle` and its inverse `parkRowsForDepth(h)` ‚Äî model double-loading explicitly; `growParking` now reads the current row count and sets the depth for `n¬±1`, so the increment alternates +stallDepth (single‚Üídouble-loads the last aisle) / +stallDepth+aisle (adds a new aisle) exactly as specced. `setParkCfg`, `splitParkingRows`, `canShrink`, and the two "Split into rows" `‚â•2` gates were moved onto the same row model so depth‚Üîrows stays consistent; the "+" button/tooltip relabelled "Ôºã Row" / "Add one parking row" (was "Row + aisle"). Verified: 42‚Üí60‚Üí102‚Üí120‚Üí162 (rows 1‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí4‚Üí5, +18/+42/+18/+42), round-trip `rows‚Üîdepth` clean for n=1..8; build + tests pass.

### B70 ‚Äî Curbs: derived auto-placed bands with visual / calc / dimension rules `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Curbs are derived auto-placed thin bands ‚Äî not user-placed, draggable, or deletable; they float to stay attached to their surface as rows are added/removed. Default = 6‚Ä≥ mono curb ("mono" = single monolithic pour, no gutter pan), occupying 0.5‚Ä≤ of plan-view width. **Placement:** terminal (the outermost band of every section gets a curb on its far/back edge); sidewalk transition (a curb between a sidewalk and the parking/drive it abuts ‚Äî sidewalk behind, pavement dropping at the curb face); never a building-side curb. **Three-way representation, one rule for all sections (standard / truck court / trailer alike):** always *drawn*; always in the *calculations* (area, yield, paving SF, the width feeding any math ‚Üí 18‚Ä≤ stall + 24‚Ä≤ aisle + 0.5‚Ä≤ = 42.5‚Ä≤; a 135‚Ä≤ court = 135.5‚Ä≤); never in the *displayed dimension* (reads 42‚Ä≤, 135‚Ä≤, 185‚Ä≤ ‚Äî to the face of curb; the 0.5‚Ä≤ lives only in geometry + calc).
> Inspection first (what already existed): **roads already implemented the exact three-way contract** ‚Äî a road's footprint includes its 6‚Ä≥ curbs (`h = travelW + 2¬∑curb`), the curbs are drawn as lines, area uses `w¬∑h` (curb included), and the on-canvas dimension shows `travelW` (curb excluded). So the *pattern* was proven; the gap was that **parking, paving/truck-court, and trailer carried no curb at all** (no draw, no calc, no rule). Built only that gap, generalizing the road contract to the other paved types.
> Done 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/nice-wozniak-2k2v9c`). A single derived-curb model (`curbEdgesOf(el, allEls)`, a **module-level** helper ‚Äî no `cfgOf`/component scope, so it's safe inside `renderElPx`; cf. B68) is the one source feeding both the drawn band and the math: terminal curb on the edge pointing **away** from the host building (so never building-side); a sidewalk-transition curb on the inner edge when a sidewalk/landscape strip sits between the pad and its host; both depth ends for a free-standing field. The element's `w/h` stays the **face-of-curb** size, so (a) the displayed dimension already excludes the curb with no change, and (b) the curb is derived on top and *floats* to the terminal edge as rows change ‚Äî no stored geometry. **Calc:** `curbAreaOf` (Œ£ edge-length¬∑width) is added to the element's paving/parking/trailer bucket and thus to impervious ‚Äî so a 200‚Ä≤ bay reads 8 500 sf (eff. 42.5‚Ä≤) and a 300‚Ä≤ court reads 40 650 sf (eff. 135.5‚Ä≤), while the on-canvas label still reads 42/135. **Draw:** a thin band whose width *scales* (so the 12‚Ä≥ option visibly doubles the 6‚Ä≥), kept legible by its stroke when the 0.5‚Ä≤ is sub-pixel at low zoom. Roads untouched (excluded from the new model; they keep their own curbs). Build + tests pass.
> Deferred nuance (kept minor, not blocking): the sidewalk-transition and free-field-both-ends cases are geometric heuristics (same-host + inboard; ¬±depth ends) rather than an explicit stored curb edge; polygon (irregular) pads are out of scope for the auto-curb. Revisit if a future case needs a hand-placed/overridden curb edge.

### B71 ‚Äî Per-element curb override on trailer parking (6‚Ä≥ ‚Üí 12‚Ä≥) `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Selecting a trailer-parking element exposes a curb selector: default 6‚Ä≥, option 12‚Ä≥ (heavier curb to take trailer-tire/dolly abuse). 12‚Ä≥ = 1.0‚Ä≤ of plan-view width (double the 6‚Ä≥ curb's 0.5‚Ä≤) ‚Äî real plan width, not a taller face/reveal (height unchanged). Flows through the same three-way rule as B70: drawn in the visual field, included in calculations (a trailer section with a 12‚Ä≥ terminal curb counts the full 1.0‚Ä≤ in area/width math), excluded from the displayed dimension (185‚Ä≤ still reads 185‚Ä≤). Build the property globally underneath (any band could carry it) but surface the control only on trailer parking for now.
> Done 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/nice-wozniak-2k2v9c`). The width is a global per-element field `el.curbW` (`CURB_6 = 0.5` / `CURB_12 = 1.0`) read by `curbWidthOf(el)`, so any band *could* carry it; the **6‚Ä≥ / 12‚Ä≥ control is surfaced only in the selected-trailer panel** (`setCurbW`). It rides the B70 model end-to-end: the drawn band scales to 1.0‚Ä≤ (visibly double), `curbAreaOf` counts the full 1.0‚Ä≤ in the SF/width math, and the displayed dimension is untouched (still face-of-curb). Trailer fields show no on-canvas dimension number today, so there's nothing to wrongly include the curb in; the rule holds the moment one is shown. Verified: a 12‚Ä≥ curb on a 300‚Ä≤ edge = 300 sf vs the 6‚Ä≥ 150 sf; build + tests pass.

<!-- Filed 2026-06-15 from a parallel chat's backlog (provisional "NEW-1"/"NEW-2" ‚Üí minted B72/B73 here; deduped against B67, which had already flagged the map-overlay goal as its own out-of-scope follow-on). -->

### B74 ‚Äî "New plan, same parcel" drops the site's county ‚Üí identify/jurisdiction defaults to Harris `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `newPlanSameParcel` (`SitePlannerApp.jsx`) copied origin/parcels/settings/underlay but **not** `county`, so a Fort Bend/Chambers iteration plan normalized to `county:null` and the planner fell back to Harris for click-identify, jurisdiction utility layers, and easement rules. Added `county: src.county || null` to the payload (matches `goPlanFromParcels`). (`duplicatePlan` already spreads `...src`, so it was fine.)

### B75 ‚Äî Chambers county-acreage check ~10.76√ó wrong; flags every correct parcel as a huge mismatch `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî high
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. The TxGIO statewide source (Chambers) names its already-in-acres fields `GIS_AREA`/`LEGAL_AREA` (with a sibling `*_UNIT`), which the `countyAcres` acres-regex didn't match ‚Üí it fell through to a projected `st_area` in m¬≤, √∑43560 (ft¬≤/ac) ‚Üí ~10.76√ó too small; the old "tenx" m¬≤ guard also corrected the wrong way (√∑ instead of √ó). `countyAcres` now prefers `gis_area`/`legal_area` when the sibling unit says "Acres"; the geometry-check badge detects an m¬≤-misread Shape area and shows the **corrected** acreage (a correct lot reads ‚úì, not "900% off ‚ñ≤"). Badge-only ‚Äî never fed the yield math (which uses the drawn polygon).

### B76 ‚Äî Cross-county OBJECTID selection-key collision mis-toggles parcels `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ‚Äî medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `MapFinder.handleClick` keyed selected parcels by bare `OBJECTID`, which is unique only within one CAD layer ‚Äî a multi-county assembly with two lots sharing an id would toggle each other off (and corrupt the assembled acreage). Key is now namespaced `"<county>:<oid>"`. (Distinct from the B22 rapid-click race; the dedupe/hilite guards there are unchanged.)

### B77 ‚Äî `fileNewReview` ignores upload failure ‚Üí silently files an unopenable document `[Document Review]` (bug) ‚Äî medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. A non-oversize `uploadSource` failure (network/RLS/transient 5xx) was stored as a normal source (`storageKey:null, oversize:false`) and `fileNewReview` returned `ok:true` with no feedback. It now returns `{ uploadFailed }`, and `ProjectLibrary.fileDrop` collects failures and alerts ("filed, but the upload failed ‚Äî re-drop on open to view"). (On load a null key already showed the re-drop banner/placeholder; the gap was no warning at file time.)

### B78 ‚Äî Parking/trailer stall layout can infinite-loop (freeze) on a degenerate config `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî critical (latent)
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `carStalls`/`trailerStalls` compute `mod = rowDepth*2 + aisle` then `mods = floor(h/mod)` and loop `mods` times; `mod===0` (0 stall-depth + 0 aisle) makes `mods` Infinity ‚Üí an unbounded band loop that hard-freezes the tab (and a 0 stall-width/`tw` made `perRow` Infinity ‚Üí unbounded render loop). The current UI clamps these inputs (`min` 1/8/1), so it's **not reachable from the fields today** ‚Äî the live freeze a sibling audit saw was a transient mid-deploy regression ‚Äî but legacy/`cfg`-merged data could still hit it, so added an early-return guard (`mod>0 && pitch>0`; `tw>0`) to both.

### B79 ‚Äî Negative/zero Grid size infinite-loops the grid render; size fields have no upper bound `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî high (latent)
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `gridLines` used `step = settings.gridSize` directly; a 0/negative step made `for (x‚Ä¶; x += step)` never terminate (freeze). Clamped `step = Math.max(0.1, +gridSize || 10)`. The Grid field's `min={1}` already blocks it from the UI; this hardens the loop itself. Also added `max={MAX_DIM}` (100000 ft) to the element Width/Depth fields so a fat-fingered/pasted size can't produce absurd geometry or SVG-coordinate stalls.

### B80 ‚Äî Underlay calibration divides by zero on a 0 ft/px underlay `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `applyCalibration` guarded `knownFt>0`/`measured>0` but not `underlay.ftPerPx>0`; a degenerate underlay ‚Üí Infinity/NaN placement (image flies off-canvas). Added `underlay.ftPerPx > 0` to the guard (covers the derived `sy` too).

### B81 ‚Äî Self-intersecting / near-zero polygons accepted everywhere ‚Üí silently wrong area `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Parcel/element/measure-area close handlers only gated on vertex count; a bow-tie outline closes and `polyArea` (abs shoelace) returns the *net* area (lobes cancel) with no warning, corrupting siteSqft ‚Üí coverage/FAR/parking/detention. Added `polySelfIntersects` + `flashPolyWarn` on close (parcel / drawn element / measured area): warns on a self-crossing or ~zero-area outline (accepts but flags, so the number isn't trusted silently).

### B82 ‚Äî Calibrate tool is a silent no-op with no underlay `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Calibrate sits in the main toolbar (selectable with no underlay); clicks hit `if (!underlay) return` silently. Now flashes "Calibrate needs an underlay ‚Äî drop an aerial/screenshot first."

### B83 ‚Äî Copy/Duplicate carries dangling host/court metadata `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Paste/duplicate stripped only `attachedTo`, keeping `truckCourt`/`forCourt`/`dogEar`/`oppSide`/side-park/sidewalk tags ‚Äî so a copied child carried court metadata pointing at nothing (refit/trailer logic reads it). Added a shared `detachClone` that strips all relational tags on clone. (Copying a host building's whole attached assembly is a separate enhancement ‚Äî noted, not done.)

### B84 ‚Äî Metes/POB plot silently cancels when no bearings parsed `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `anchorEncumbrance` returned silently when the description produced 0 calls (e.g. an unreadable format). Now warns "No bearings were recognized‚Ä¶", with a separate message when the calls can't form a shape. (The underlying curve/dash-DMS parser gaps stay under B25/B26.)

### B85 ‚Äî Empty callout left on Escape; zero-length cross-section makes a degenerate profile `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. The global Escape cleared `calloutDraft` but not an in-progress empty *new* callout ‚Äî now calls `cancelEditCallout()` (added `editCallout` to the keydown effect deps so the closure stays fresh). `runXSection` now requires `lenFt > 1` (a same-point cross-section produced NaN profile distances).

### B86 ‚Äî Split tool corrupts area on concave parcels `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî high
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16 (complements B31). `splitPolygon` keys off the extreme-`t` crossings, so a straight cut across a concave parcel (4+ crossings) can yield overlapping/omitting halves whose areas don't sum to the original ‚Üí wrong siteSqft + every downstream yield. Added an **area-conservation + self-intersection guard** in `performSplit`: if the two pieces don't conserve area (¬±2%) or come out self-intersecting, the cut is rejected with "that cut crosses the parcel ambiguously ‚Äî try a straight cut between two opposite edges," instead of saving corrupted geometry. (A full polygon-clipping rewrite for multi-piece concave cuts stays deferred per B31.)

### B87 ‚Äî `Infinity` / scientific-notation in a numeric field bypasses the min-clamp ‚Üí NaN geometry, persisted as null `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî high
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `NumInput.commit` rejected only `isNaN`, but `parseFloat("1e999")`/`"Infinity"` return `Infinity` (not NaN) and `Math.max(min, Infinity)===Infinity`, so a non-finite value slipped through ‚Äî Grid=Infinity made `snap` return NaN (every new coord NaN), a size=Infinity drew nothing, and `JSON.stringify` then persisted NaN/Infinity as **null** (permanent corruption). Now requires `Number.isFinite(n)` and applies a default `1e7` upper cap when no explicit `max`; `snap` also guards a non-finite/‚â§0 grid (falls back to 10). Closes the persist-as-null source (B91).

### B88 ‚Äî Type confusion: a record whose `parcels`/`els` isn't an array crashes the whole app `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `createSiteModel` kept any truthy value (`p.parcels || []`), so a tampered/legacy/bad-sync record with a non-array collection flowed into `parcels.reduce(...)` ‚Üí `TypeError` ‚Üí blank app on load. Now coerces every collection (`Array.isArray(...) ? ... : []`) and `settings` to an object. Added a unit test (40 tests pass). (The B68 error boundary already contained the blank-screen; this stops the throw so the site actually loads.)

### B89 ‚Äî "Travel widths" free-text feeds road geometry ‚Üí NaN roads `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. The road-width preset list `.split(",")` of the free-text field offered any token (e.g. `abc`) as a selectable width; drawing with it did `+"abc" = NaN` ‚Üí a NaN road persisted as null. The preset list now keeps only finite, positive numeric tokens.

### B90 ‚Äî No cross-tab reconciliation: a second tab's finder list goes stale `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî medium‚Üílow
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Added a `window` `storage` listener that refreshes the finder list when another tab changes the site store. **Re-scoped on inspection:** the audit's "whole-store clobber" doesn't actually reproduce ‚Äî `saveSite`/`deleteSite` already read-modify-write the latest store per call, so a second tab editing a *different* site can't wipe the first's work; the real symptom was a stale list, which this fixes (logged-in tabs now also pick up each other's cloud-cache writes).

### B91 ‚Äî NaN/Infinity coordinates persist as `null` (corruption amplifier) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Addressed-at-source 2026-06-16. The non-finite *sources* are now closed (B87 NumInput/snap, B89 road widths, plus the B78/B79 freeze guards), so no path feeds a non-finite coordinate into a save. A deep on-save geometry sanitizer was deliberately NOT added ‚Äî rewriting stored points risks corrupting valid geometry, worse than the rare amplified case it would catch.

### B92 ‚Äî Unbounded text/emoji/RTL in annotations bloats the SVG/record `[Site Planner]` (bug) ‚Äî low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Callout/text annotations now cap at `maxLength={2000}`. (Injection was already safe ‚Äî XSS payloads render escaped.)

> **Triaged but NOT changed (still open / deferred, by decision):** building bump-outs aren't clamped when the host shrinks (medium, geometry ‚Äî risk-deferred); `deleteSite` cloud delete stays fire-and-forget (zombie only if the delete fails ‚Äî B-class edge); over-quota save still drops the underlay and reports success (graceful-degradation, minor); `currentSite` pointer global-not-per-user (low). County straddle/out-of-coverage messaging stays under **B13/B36**; metes curve + dash-DMS under **B25/B26**; bundle size / Anthropic-SDK / Mapillary-token / Nominatim / deploy-churn remain LOW/infra.

<!-- Filed 2026-06-15 from a parallel chat's GIS batch (its provisional NEW-1‚Ä¶NEW-4 ‚Üí B93‚Ä¶B96 here). (RENUMBERED from a stale B72‚ÄìB75 ‚Äî `main` had since filled B72‚ÄìB92 with the overlay tool + an audit batch; IDs are permanent.) Build order for the batch: B96 (cache) first ‚Üí B93 + B94 ride on it ‚Üí B95 deferred. One generic ArcGIS-REST connector serves B93 + B94 (parameterized per source; no per-city/per-source code paths). -->

### B93 ‚Äî Jurisdiction identify: city limits, ETJ & county `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) ‚Äî SHIP target (promoted from deferred 2026-06-17)
`[x]` Click any point ‚Äî or, **on explicit request**, run on the active parcel ‚Äî and get back which jurisdictions it falls in: incorporated city (or "unincorporated"), any city **ETJ** (extraterritorial jurisdiction), and county (Harris, Fort Bend, Montgomery, Galveston, Brazoria‚Ä¶). **Trigger: click / explicit request only ‚Äî NOT auto-loaded on every parcel.**
- **Interaction:** a map click runs a point-in-polygon "identify" against the loaded boundary layers; also identify-on-demand for the active parcel. **Test the whole parcel, not just its centroid** ‚Äî flag boundary-straddle (e.g. partly City of Houston, partly unincorporated Harris Co.) and list *every* jurisdiction it touches rather than forcing one answer.
- **Coverage:** Houston-metro priority cities (Houston, Baytown, Missouri City, Sugar Land, Pearland, ‚Ä¶), but driven by a **source registry**, never per-city hardcoded logic ‚Äî adding a city = adding a registry row, not new code.
- **Source-priority chain (the crux):** query in fallback order per layer ‚Äî authoritative city source where it publishes good GIS (e.g. Houston / COHGIS) ‚Üí regional backbone (**H-GAC**, the Houston-Galveston Area Council, whose GIS covers the 13-county region) ‚Üí statewide backbone (**TxDOT Open Data**, which publishes city limits + ETJ statewide). **One generic ArcGIS-REST connector** parameterized per source; a registry row stores endpoint URL, layer ID, and a field map. (Same connector serves **B94** ‚Äî build it once.)
- **Field mapping:** every source names the city field differently (`CITY_NM` / `NAME` / `MUNICIPALITY` ‚Ä¶). The registry normalizes each source's schema into one internal shape so the UI is source-agnostic.
- **Reuse existing GIS infra ‚Äî do NOT build a parallel system:** same cache (**B96**), same per-layer honest status + visible data-age, same ~45 s self-heal re-probe, same Texas State Plane frame.
- **Screening-only framing:** ETJ is volatile (Texas law now lets landowners petition out of a city's ETJ; annexations move city limits) ‚Äî label results screening-only, always show source + age, never present as a legal/official determination, keep a visible "verify with the jurisdiction" note.
- **Build step before wiring (evidence-first):** confirm each candidate endpoint actually responds and carries the expected fields, then calibrate against a few parcels whose jurisdiction is already known. Don't assume an endpoint/schema exists ‚Äî verify it.
> **Relationship to B13 / B36 (county-resolution theme) ‚Äî not a duplicate, but adjacent.** B13-pt1 and B36(a)/(d) have been *waiting on a county-boundary polygon dataset* to replace the coarse per-county **bbox** pre-filter (true point-in-county CAD routing + straddle merge). B93 introduces exactly that data path ‚Äî county boundary polygons loaded via the new source registry + point-in-polygon. So B93's county layer **is** the dataset those items need: land it and B13-pt1 / B36(a)/(d) can be closed or folded in. They're still distinct items (B13/B36 = *internal CAD-routing*; B93 = a *user-facing* city/ETJ/county identify) ‚Äî build them aware of each other so the boundary data loads once, not twice.
> **Endpoint verification needs network egress (2026-06-15, filing session):** the build-before-wiring step above is blocked from a default web session ‚Äî outbound to the GIS hosts (`server.arcgisonline.com`, `services.arcgis.com`, `gis.h-gac.com`, TxGIO/TxDOT) returns `403 host_not_allowed` from the environment egress proxy. Recipe: in the environment editor set **Network access ‚Üí Custom** (keep "Also include default list of common package managers" checked so npm/GitHub still work) and add, one per line: `*.arcgis.com` (TxDOT + city AGOL services), `*.arcgisonline.com`, `gis.h-gac.com` (H-GAC), `*.geographic.texas.gov` (TxGIO), plus `www.gis.hctx.net` + `gis.fbcad.org` (HCAD/FBCAD, for calibrating against known-jurisdiction parcels). **The proxy is fixed when a session starts**, so the change applies to a NEW session ‚Äî not the one that saved it. Verify the hosts respond, then do the verify/calibrate pass before wiring.
> **Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/festive-davinci-0oco2n`): egress unblocked ‚Üí endpoints verified + calibrated ‚Üí connector built, tested, wired.** The allowlist recipe above took effect in a FRESH session (the proxy picks up the policy at session start, not mid-session): all GIS hosts now reachable ‚Äî `server.arcgisonline.com` / `services.arcgis.com` (AGOL) / `feature.geographic.texas.gov` (TxGIO) / `www.gis.hctx.net` all 200; H-GAC + FBCAD are *allowed* but upstream-503 right now (not a proxy block). **Verified + calibrated against known ground:** city limits = TxGIO `City_Boundaries/Texas_City_Boundaries/MapServer/0` field `city_name` (downtown Houston ‚Üí "Houston"); county = TxDOT `Texas_County_Boundaries/FeatureServer/0` field `CNTY_NM` (same point ‚Üí "Harris" / FIPS 48201). **ETJ has no statewide layer** ‚Äî only per-city / regional publishers (H-GAC for the metro); kept as a registry row that honestly reads "source not wired" rather than faked. **Built `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/jurisdiction.js`:** ONE generic, registry-driven ArcGIS-REST connector on the B96 SWR cache ‚Äî `JURISDICTION_SOURCES` rows (url/layer/field-map/query-kind), `normalizeFeature` (each source's schema ‚Üí one internal shape), and `identifyJurisdiction(lng,lat,{ring})` which tests the WHOLE parcel via a polygon-intersect so a boundary **straddle** lists every city/county touched (point fallback when no ring); honest per-source status + visible data-age; screening-only. **Wired** into the planner's Identify panel as an explicit-request "‚öñÔ∏é Jurisdiction & road authority" button (never auto-loaded, per spec). **23 unit tests** (`test/jurisdiction.test.js`, injected fetch + cache); `lint` (0 errors) / `test` (68 pass) / `build` green; lazy chunks still split. **Remaining (kept `[ ]`):** wire a real ETJ source (H-GAC when its server recovers, or per-city COHGIS rows); **live browser confirmation** (CORS from the app origin + on-map render ‚Äî same handoff as B96); and the **B13/B36 fold-in** (this county layer IS the boundary dataset those items were waiting on ‚Äî close/merge in a follow-up so the data loads once).
> **ETJ leg wired 2026-06-15 (same branch):** B93's third leg is no longer a gap ‚Äî wired to the City of Houston's own COHGIS ETJ (`COH_ETJ_view/FeatureServer/1`, reflects SB2038 releases), verified + calibrated (Aldine / Spring, unincorporated near Houston ‚Üí in the ETJ ring; downtown / in-city ‚Üí not). Houston-only by design (the priority metro); other cities' ETJ are additive registry rows. `normalizeFeature` gained a `nameConst` fallback so a single-jurisdiction layer with no city-name column still resolves ("Houston"). So **all three legs (city + ETJ + county) are now wired against verified sources**; what remains is live browser confirmation + the B13/B36 fold-in.
> **Amendment 2026-06-17 ‚Äî disposition: DEFERRED ‚Üí SHIP as a released, user-facing feature** (status/disposition change folded into B93 per the arriving note; **no new B#**). Promote B93 from "deferred until the GIS-caching foundation is solid" to a production capability on planyr.io: ship the **full** item ‚Äî the boundary layer(s) + click/explicit-request identify ‚Äî as a real **user-facing** tool, not dev-only/experimental. (The connector, all three legs, and the tests already exist ‚Äî see the progress notes above; what's promoted is the *intent to ship*, gated only on the points below.)
> - **Build order unchanged:** **GIS layer caching (B96, stale-while-revalidate) stays the immediate predecessor.** Ship caching + jurisdiction identify **together as one unit** rather than leaving jurisdiction open-ended. *Owner option:* if Michael confirms standalone-first, drop the cache dependency, ship against live endpoints now, and retrofit the cache later.
> - **Screening-only contract carries into the shipped feature** (already specified above under "Screening-only framing" ‚Äî restated as a ship requirement, not a new ask): a per-layer **data-age stamp** + a visible **"verify with the jurisdiction"** disclaimer are always shown; **never** present ETJ or city limits as authoritative.
> - **Honest fallback disclosure (sharpens the source-priority chain):** when a city's authoritative source is unavailable and the answer came from the **regional (H-GAC) / statewide (TxDOT/TxGIO) fallback**, surface **which source answered** ‚Äî don't silently present a fallback result as if it were the city's own.
> - **Open technical gates unchanged:** live browser confirmation (**VERIFICATION V1**) + the **B13/B36 fold-in** (B93's county layer is the boundary dataset those items wait on) + a real non-Houston ETJ source (H-GAC when up, or per-city rows).
> ‚úÖ **SHIPPED + VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-17.** It's live on planyr.io and confirmed working end-to-end in a real headless browser (logged-out): (1) the four GIS queries answer **from the planyr.io origin with no CORS block** ‚Äî county `services.arcgis.com` ‚Üí 200 "Harris", city TxGIO ‚Üí 200 "Houston", ETJ COHGIS ‚Üí 200 (in-city ‚Üí correctly not in ETJ), road TxDOT ‚Üí 200 (maint-agency 4 = City); (2) the on-screen flow renders ‚Äî map ‚Üí planner ‚Üí **üîç Identify parcel** ‚Üí **‚öñÔ∏é Jurisdiction & road authority** shows **County: Harris ¬∑ City: Houston ¬∑ ETJ: not in Houston ETJ ¬∑ Road maint.: City**, each with a data-age + the "Screening only ‚Äî verify with the jurisdiction" disclaimer. Recorded as **VERIFICATION V1 ‚úÖ** (screenshot evidence). Marking B93 **done** (the user-facing feature ships for the priority metro). **Residual enhancements stay open as their own follow-ups (not blockers):** a real **non-Houston ETJ** source (other cities' ETJ = additive registry rows; today reads "not in Houston ETJ" outside Houston) and the **B13/B36 fold-in** (reuse this county-boundary layer for internal CAD routing so the data loads once). The honest-fallback-disclosure sharpening (show which source answered) is also carried forward under those.
> ‚úÖ **ETJ is no longer Houston-only (2026-06-17).** Owner pushed back that the feature isn't finished while ETJ is Houston-only ‚Äî fixed. Confirmed there is **no statewide ETJ layer** (TxGIO has city limits + county, not ETJ), then swapped the ETJ source from the City-of-Houston-only layer to **H-GAC's regional ETJ layer** (`HGAC_City_ETJ_Boundaries` on AGOL `services.arcgis.com/su8ic9KbA7PYVxPS`, CORS-clean), which carries **every city's ETJ across the 13-county Houston-Galveston metro** (Houston, Sugar Land, Missouri City, Pearland, Richmond, Katy, Baytown, Conroe, Texas City, ‚Ä¶). A single **registry-row swap** ‚Äî exactly the design's "add a city = add a row" intent ‚Äî keyed on the layer's `CITY` field with title-casing of its ALL-CAPS values; the UI's empty-state text changed "not in Houston ETJ" ‚Üí "not in a city ETJ". **Verified live from the planyr.io origin (2026-06-17):** Spring/Aldine ‚Üí **Houston**, SW of Sugar Land ‚Üí **Richmond** (a non-Houston city), in-city point ‚Üí none (ETJ is the ring outside city limits) ‚Äî all HTTP 200, no CORS block. County / City / Road were already statewide (TxDOT / TxGIO), so the whole identify is now metro-wide. lint 0 errors ¬∑ **206 tests** (33 jurisdiction; 2 new: title-case + non-Houston ETJ) ¬∑ build green. **Remaining ETJ follow-up (smaller, separate):** cross-REGION coverage outside H-GAC ‚Äî e.g. Dallas via **NCTCOG**'s ETJ layer ‚Äî is another additive registry row when a deal needs it (would benefit from a tiny multi-source-per-role tweak); the **B13/B36 fold-in** is still the other open thread.
> ‚úÖ **ETJ extended to Austin + DFW, region-routed (2026-06-17).** Owner asked to add the Austin & DFW metros *if it doesn't slow the Houston use case*. Done, with **region routing** so it can't: ETJ is now a bbox-scoped **list** (`ETJ_SOURCES` + `etjSourcesForPoint(lat,lng)` in `jurisdiction.js`), and a click only queries the metro whose box it falls in ‚Äî a **Houston click still fires exactly one ETJ query (H-GAC), unchanged**; Austin/DFW servers are never touched from Houston. Investigated the data honestly: there's **no statewide ETJ** and, unlike Houston (H-GAC's clean regional layer), Austin/DFW publish ETJ **city-by-city** and some authoritative servers aren't CORS-open (Fort Worth's own `mapit.fortworthtexas.gov` is CORS-blocked; NCTCOG has regional city-limits but **no** regional ETJ + no CORS). Per the owner's "big cities now" call, wired the cleanly-reachable AGOL layers: **Austin** = City of Austin 2-/5-mile ETJ (`COA_Jurisdiction/20`, nameConst "Austin"); **DFW** = City of Fort Worth ETJ (`Fort_Worth_ETJ`, 50 polygons, nameConst "Fort Worth"); **Dallas** has ~no ETJ (landlocked ‚Äî empty layer, nothing to add). `identifyJurisdiction` now queries 1-or-many sources per role and **unions** the names (handles a metro-seam straddle). Smaller Austin/DFW suburbs aren't covered yet (additive rows later) ‚Üí such a point reads "not in a city ETJ"; outside all three metros reads "no ETJ layer here". **Verified live from the planyr.io origin (2026-06-17):** all three layers CORS-clean + return features at real ETJ points (Austin: Del Valle / NW edge; Fort Worth: Alliance / SW). lint 0 errors ¬∑ **230 tests** (5 routing tests incl. "Houston ‚Üí exactly one query", + Austin/non-Houston end-to-end) ¬∑ build green. Still open: more Austin/DFW suburb rows on request; B13/B36 fold-in.

### B129 ‚Äî [HAND-OFF] GIS overlays: NWI wetlands CORS + the cached-vector experiment ‚Äî needs a real browser to finish `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug/task)
`[x]` **The probe-resilience fix below LANDED via PR #60 (2026-06-16); this item now tracks the REMAINING browser-only work** (confirming FEMA renders, the NWI CORS question, and the reverted vector experiment). **Written for a future session that HAS a browser.** This session had none (CI container), and the GIS hosts are egress-blocked from it (`curl ‚Ä¶?f=json` ‚Üí HTTP 403 "Host not in allowlist: hazards.fema.gov / fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov"). So map *rendering* could only be judged from the owner's screenshots ‚Äî which repeatedly misled me into saying "it works" when it didn't. **Do not trust a layer's green "loaded" status as proof of rendering: it is set the instant the layer is attached, BEFORE the image loads.** Only a human/browser confirms a visible overlay.

**Symptom (owner-reported on planyr.io + the PR #60 preview):** toggling **Wetlands (NWI)** throws a layer error. First `network / CORS error` (the health *probe*), then after a fix `Could not parse response as JSON ‚Ä¶ CORS` (esri-leaflet's internal *metadata* fetch). **FEMA flood zones** seemed to load (green status; faint hatching in one screenshot) but its *visible* rendering was never actually confirmed ‚Äî the owner's pushback ("what makes you think it's working?") is the honest state.

**Root-cause analysis (UNVERIFIED in a browser ‚Äî confirm before acting):**
- NWI host `fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov` appears to refuse cross-origin browser requests (CORS): both the `?f=json` health probe AND esri-leaflet's metadata/service-info fetch fail. Its `f=image` export *should* still paint via a CORS-exempt `<img>`, but esri's metadata `requesterror` fires regardless. FEMA host `hazards.fema.gov` appears CORS-clean.
- The (now-deleted) `kind:"vector"` overlay never rendered. Strong suspicion: an esri raster/image layer must be added **directly to the map**, not nested inside an `L.layerGroup`. Reverted rather than debugged blind.

**What PR #60 MERGED (the safe, shippable part of branch `claude/nice-dirac-029jok`). Net diff vs `main` = just `layers.js`:**
1. `probeService` returns `unreachable:true` on a CORS/network throw; `syncOverlayLayers` then adds the image layer anyway ‚Äî a CORS-blocked *probe* no longer kills a layer.
2. A layer `requesterror` no longer drops the layer or fires the red toast ‚Äî just a quiet per-layer "failed" status; `'load'` flips it back to "loaded".
FEMA/NWI are on the proven `dynamicMapLayer` (`f=image`) path. `lib/vectorLayers.js` (pure fetch/paging/style/decide engine, 31 tests, from #58) is on `main` but **UNWIRED groundwork**. (The branch's MapFinder/SitePlanner edits were dropped at merge ‚Äî they pre-dated and would have undone main's B105/tab work; only the `layers.js` fix landed.) `lint` 0 / `test` 138 / `build` green.

**Questions a browser settles in minutes (DevTools ‚Üí Network / Console):**
1. Does **FEMA** draw a *visible* flood overlay on the preview? (Confirm/deny ‚Äî ignore the "loaded" status.)
2. Does the **NWI** `export?f=image` request fire, return 200, and produce an `<img>` *despite* the metadata `requesterror`? Or does esri block the export?
3. NWI response headers ‚Äî is `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` present on `/MapServer`, `/export`, `/0/query`? Is the block total or endpoint-specific? Did it change recently (the DONE list says wetlands once worked)?

**Next steps (after browser triage):**
- **FEMA:** confirm it draws a *visible* overlay (the merged probe-resilience fix should keep it alive even if its probe is blocked).
- **NWI:** (a) swap to a CORS-clean wetlands endpoint (browser-test alternates ‚Äî a USFWS ArcGIS Online hosted layer, a tiled service, etc.; edit `STATEWIDE.wetlands.url` in `lib/layers.js`); or (b) route via the `/server` proxy (the documented durable fix for flaky agency hosts ‚Äî serves through our own origin, no CORS); or (c) leave it (screening-only).
- **Vector engine (`lib/vectorLayers.js`):** if revived, render via **direct map-add** and browser-test. Remember these overlays are used at area-scale, where the server-rendered image is the right tool; vectors only pay off zoomed in tight. The deleted `vectorOverlay.js` is recoverable from git (branch commits `49e2a1b` / `2f89c19`).

**Key files:** `lib/layers.js` (`STATEWIDE`, `syncOverlayLayers` dispatch, `probeService` `unreachable`, softened `requesterror`), `lib/vectorLayers.js` + `test/vectorLayers.test.js` (unwired engine), `VERIFICATION.md` V20 (the live-test item for the merged fix).

> **‚úÖ DONE 2026-06-17 ‚Äî verified in a REAL headless browser (Chromium/Playwright, live on planyr.io), branch `claude/gallant-wright-c3sjwd`.** Prior sessions had no browser and guessed; this one settled the open questions with evidence.
> - **FEMA flood zones ‚Äî CONFIRMED RENDERING.** `/export?f=image` ‚Üí **HTTP 200 `image/png`**, `<img>` paints (sampled **25.9%** non-transparent at zoom 15: teal `(0,224,224)` Zone AE, orange `(224,128,0)` floodway, red `(224,0,0)` boundaries ‚Äî standard NFHL symbology tracing Buffalo/White Oak Bayou through downtown Houston). **CORS-clean:** `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://planyr.io` on the metadata GET, `/export`, AND the OPTIONS preflight. **Scale caveat (the source of prior "is it even working?" confusion):** NFHL sublayers 27/28 are gated AT THE SOURCE to `minScale ~1:36,112`, so they draw nothing at city-wide zoom (‚â§~z13) and only appear at ~zoom 14+. Blank-when-zoomed-out is expected, not a bug ‚Äî added a user-facing layer note + a code comment so it isn't re-misdiagnosed.
> - **NWI wetlands ‚Äî root cause is NOT CORS (the original hypothesis was wrong); the USFWS service is DOWN (HTTP 500).** The configured host `fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov/wetlandsmapservice/‚Ä¶` returns **HTTP 500 across its ENTIRE catalog** (metadata, `/export`, `/query`, REST root, `/info`), confirmed three independent ways: sandbox `curl` (3/3 persistent), planyr.io in a real browser (layer errors, no `<img>`, zero successful responses), and an out-of-band fetch on a different network (also 500). Genuine **agency-side outage**, hitting production users too. esri-leaflet's "could not parse JSON / CORS" text is a misleading catch-all.
> - **The configured URL is canonical & correct ‚Äî confirmed NOT to swap it.** The official USFWS "National Wetlands Inventory" ArcGIS web map (arcgis.com item `da9a3343‚Ä¶`) uses this EXACT MapServer. The only alternates are `www.fws.gov/wetlands/‚Ä¶` (older mirror, egress-blocked from the test sandbox so not in-browser-verifiable) and non-authoritative state re-hosts ‚Äî swapping to either would repeat the blind guess this item exists to stop. **Decision: option (c) ‚Äî keep the canonical URL** (it returns when USFWS restores the service) and make the failure honest.
> - **Shipped (this branch):** `lib/layers.js` ‚Äî the `requesterror` status now reads a plain *"the map service is not responding ‚Äî it may be temporarily unavailable (screening only)"* instead of esri's misleading CORS text (the `'load'` handler still flips it back to "loaded" if the image lands, so a healthy-but-CORS-blocked layer is unaffected); documenting comments on FEMA's source-side scale-gating and the NWI canonical-but-outaged endpoint; and the user-facing FEMA "zoom in to see flood zones" note. **lint 0 errors ¬∑ 175 tests ¬∑ build green.**
> - **Browser now persists for future sessions** (STEP 3): Playwright Chromium is installed to `/opt/pw-browsers`, a repo SessionStart hook (`.claude/hooks/ensure-browser.sh`) re-ensures it on every web session, and egress to the GIS hosts + `cdn.playwright.dev` is open. Evidence screenshots committed under `gis-verify/`.
> - **‚ñ∂ FOLLOW-UP ‚Äî owner found a LIVE sibling host (2026-06-17):** the official USFWS Wetlands Mapper renders fine from `fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov` (sibling of our down `fwspublicservices`). So the data is live ‚Äî only our specific host is erroring, and the fix is likely a one-line URL swap. Handed off as **B130** (pending an egress allowlist + in-browser verification). `lib/vectorLayers.js` stays unwired groundwork (unchanged).

so clicking a parcel while a draw/measure tool is active goes to the **tool**, not the parcel ‚Äî it does
NOT fire while drawing. **(2)** Parcel info already shows in the **left side panel** (opened on
select-tool click), not a re-appearing popover; the map uses hover **tooltips** + click-to-open-site
(no Leaflet `bindPopup` on parcels). **(3)** "Trim to essentials": the panel leads with area + geometry
check + **Active/Lock** (added in B99/B100), and the full county appraisal fields are already behind an
"all fields" expand. So no risky rework was warranted; the residual is cosmetic ordering best judged in a
live view. (If a specific popover is still observed on the live site, reopen with a repro ‚Äî couldn't find
one in the code.) Original spec:
inside parcels constantly, so it fires far too often and gets in the way; the on-parcel overlay is also
busy/unclear (line + vertex handles + length labels all at once). **Expected:**
- When a **draw/measure tool is active**, clicks go to the **tool** and do **NOT** open the popup.
- Parcel info opens only via an **explicit action** ‚Äî selecting with the Parcel/Select tool, a
  **double-click**, or a small **"i" button** ‚Äî and shows in a **side or pinned panel** rather than a
  popover that keeps reappearing.
- **Trim the popup to essentials** ‚Äî name/ID, area, status, active/inactive, lock state ‚Äî with details
  behind an **expand**.

> **B6** select tool). The "active/inactive" + "lock state" essentials come from **B100** / **B99**.
> Gate the popup behind the active-tool check rather than adding a parallel identify path; relates to
> the click-reliability work in **B64**.

### B139 ‚Äî Detention pond "Expand this pond" mode (replaces B132's "Lock as existing pond") `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1", filed B133‚ÜíB137 in chat; renumbered B139 ‚Äî B137 collided via a concurrent merge with the already-done "Fort Bend parcels" bug, which keeps B137; this is the next free ID. Companion labels item is B140.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). Reframed pond enlargement around the goal and fixed the two confusions the owner hit (a second "Lock" button + no visible way to expand). Reuses B132's volume math + ghost ‚Äî no schema change.
- **Entry point:** replaced the buried "Lock as existing pond" with a primary, accent **"Expand this pond"** button directly under the Stored-volume readout, above the caveat. Clicking auto-snapshots the current pond as baseline (geometry + depth/freeboard/slope) and draws the dashed ghost ‚Äî baseline capture is now an automatic side effect, never its own button.
- **Explicit expand controls (steppers primary, drag secondary, one readout):** "Push banks out (ft)" applies a uniform OUTWARD offset of the footprint from the baseline ‚Äî new `expandPolygon()` (outward sibling of the inward-only `offsetPolygon`, with a self-intersection fallback ‚Üí "drag instead") for irregular ponds, and `w/h += 2N` about the baseline centre for rectangles; "Dig deeper (ft)" raises Total depth (derived = depth ‚àí baseline). Free drag still works and feeds the same Existing‚ÜíProposed delta. "Reset to existing" reverts geometry + depth to baseline; "Done" exits keeping the new size (feeds downstream coverage/detention) and clears the ghost.
- **Naming-collision fixes:** the only "Lock" left is the generic element pin, relabeled **"üìå Pin"/"üìå Unpin"** (panel + right-click menu) ‚Äî no "lock" verb near the expansion entry; footprint plan dimension **"Depth" ‚Üí "Length"** for ponds (so dimensions read Width / Length; "Total depth" is now the only "depth", and it's vertical).
- **Guards (amber, non-blocking):** warns when the expanded footprint overlaps a building/parking/trailer (`ringsOverlap`) or extends past the property line (new `pointInRing` vs. parcels); the over-taper case reuses **B60** (bottom area 0) and now surfaces "Side slopes meet before full depth". Steppers work even when the pond is pinned. Honest one-liner "Screening estimate ‚Äî confirm with your engineer" under the gain.
- **Verified (self, headless browser, 2026-06-17):** Expand button shown / old button gone / "Length" label / Pin relabel; enter mode ‚Üí steppers + Existing/Proposed/gain 0; push banks out 40‚Ä≤ ‚Üí uniform grow + ghost even on all four sides, **+18.33 ac-ft**; dig deeper +6‚Ä≤ ‚Üí **85.17 ac-ft**; Reset ‚Üí 0; Done ‚Üí keeps size; Pin toggles; **zero console errors.** lint 0 errors ¬∑ **202 tests** ¬∑ build green ¬∑ `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Browser pass recorded as **VERIFICATION V30** (supersedes V25).
- **One deviation from the brief:** Pin/Delete kept as the existing buttons (relabeled), not relocated into the Section header ‚Äî that's a shared all-element-types layout change, deferred as not worth the regression risk for this pond feature.
> Replaces **B132**'s entry point (B132 stays Done as the original). Companion **B140** (on-map pond area labels) stays Open. The "Length" rename + Pin relabel touch the shared element panel ‚Äî coordinated with **B121**.

### B100 ‚Äî Active / Inactive parcels driving calculations `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/blissful-babbage-liboyn`). Per-parcel `active` flag (default
**active**; missing = active, so existing sites are unchanged until a parcel is toggled off). New
`activeParcelsOf` selector + a unit test. `siteSqft` now sums **active parcels only**, so every
downstream metric (coverage, FAR, impervious, detention %, open space) follows automatically. Added an
**‚úì Active / ‚óØ Inactive** toggle in the Parcel panel (beside Lock) + `toggleParcelActive`; inactive
parcels render **dimmed + dashed** on the canvas (visible for context, clearly excluded) and show
"¬∑ inactive" in the saved-parcel list; the Yield panel notes "Excludes N inactive parcel(s)." No
`SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump ‚Äî `active` is a per-parcel field (defaulted at read, like `locked`/`fill`),
so no migration is needed. **Scope note:** the *map's* site-acreage label (MapFinder) intentionally still
shows the full footprint (all parcels) ‚Äî B100 targets the planner's yield/coverage/detention math.
Original spec:
- Add a per-parcel **Active/Inactive** toggle.
- **All area-based calculations ‚Äî yield, coverage, detention, and similar ‚Äî count ONLY active parcels.**
- Inactive parcels stay **visible for context** but render **dimmed or with diagonal-line shading** so
  it's obvious they're excluded.
- **New parcels default to Active.**
- Expose the toggle in the **parcel info panel (B98)**, and ideally **inline** on the parcel itself.
> Cross-ref: independent of lock (**B99**). The "active only" rule must thread through the
> yield/coverage/detention math (the `siteSqft` / `developableArea` inputs) ‚Äî additive Site Model field
> ‚Üí bump `SITE_MODEL_VERSION`, extend `migrate`, expose via a selector; the self-intersection/area
> guards from **B81/B86** feed the same totals, so keep them in step.
