### B360 ‚Äî Title-block intelligence: unify the readers, expand the discipline taxonomy, tune on the real corpus (V79 filing + V67 scale) `[Doc Review]` (task)  *(2026-06-21; filed B356, renumbered **B360** ‚Äî `main` #268 took B356‚ÄìB359 while in flight)*
`[x]` **Done + tuned on the owner's real drawings ‚Äî merging via PR #270 (branch `claude/bold-cori-okbxu5`).** The Drive connector was re-authed to michael@planyr.io mid-session, so the empirical tuning ran here (no second session needed). 1078 tests, lint 0, build green, lazy chunks intact.

**Unify (one reader, one extractor).** Relocated `parseSheetScale` ‚Üí `src/shared/files/sheetScale.js` (re-exported from `overlayScale.js` for back-compat); `sheetMeta` imports it from shared (removes the shared‚Üíworkspace import). `readTitleBlockText` returns `scale` in the same pass; `readSheetMeta` consumes that one read. `firstPagesText` folded into `doc-review/lib/pdf.js`. Civil-only `parseScaleNote` untouched.

**Scale-reader bug (V67).** `parseSheetScale` returned null on an architectural scale when a number (a date) was printed right before it (`‚Ä¶10/24/2025  1/8"=1'-0"`) ‚Äî the mixed-number branch swallowed the date digits + fraction. Fixed with `(?<!\d)` + a ‚â§2-digit mixed whole-inch.

**Discipline taxonomy (owner decision 2026-06-21).** Added dedicated buckets in the owner's order: Architectural, Structural, Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing, Landscape, Fire Alarm, Fire Sprinkler (+ Survey/Environmental/Geotech/CAD/Other). Fire split (Alarm vs Sprinkler); Structural/M/E/P out of "Other"/"MEP". Canonical list now in `titleBlockParse.js`, re-exported by `reviewStore` (+ the walled-off server reader kept in lockstep) so the reader / store / filing-UI can't drift.

**Corpus tuning** (`ui-audit/lib/filingScore.mjs` + `score-filing.mjs`; Jacintoport + Mesa sets read via the Drive connector, ground truth = the descriptive filename). Reader fixes the real sheets drove:
- `classifyDiscipline` ‚Üí WEIGHTED dominance (a definitive sheet-type like "floor plan"/"foundation plan" ‚â´ a bare cross-reference name like "structural"); dropped bare "elevation(s)" as a keyword (a structural sheet is full of spot elevations). Fixes a real STRUCTURAL set misfiled Civil (deep stray "grading") **and** the inverse ARCH case.
- `findDates` requires a consistent separator ‚Üí a dimension "5-29/32" no longer parses as 2032 and poisons the latest-date pick.
- `parseRevision` maps "ISSUE FOR CONSTRUCTION" (no "D") ‚Üí IFC, and no longer reads the heading "REVISIONS" as "Rev S".
**Result (8 readable sheets): project 8/8, discipline 6/7, date 3/6, revision 3/5.** Remaining misses are ground-truth nuances (a sheet's latest revision date vs the owner's package date; a mixed-revision "make-ready" package) or genuine data gaps (image-only scans with no text layer) ‚Äî not reader bugs. Real-snippet unit tests lock every fix in.

**Remaining tail (separate, not a regression):** image-only / scanned sets (e.g. Mesa Plumbing/Electrical have ~no text layer) and the `.dwg` files can't be Tier-1 text-read ‚Äî they fall to the already-built-but-dormant Tier-2 AI/OCR (B299/B352) + the DWG‚ÜíDXF server path. Eligible to move to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.

### B121 ‚Äî Site element labels and dimension callouts overlap into an unreadable pile `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) ‚Äî ‚úÖ DONE 2026-07-02 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
Final tranche shipped (increments 1 / 2a / 2b landed earlier ‚Äî the shared `lib/labelLayout.js` collision + level-of-detail engine, zoom-gating, and leader lines). This session closed the remaining owner re-report:
- **Edge-dimension callouts folded into the collision pool.** The red per-edge dimension numbers (drawn in `renderElPx`) are now tested against the committed centred-label boxes and **HIDDEN when they would overprint one** ‚Äî resolving the "red dimension piling onto the Building name / sf" pile-up. New pure helpers: `dimNumberBox` (`lib/dimSlide.js` ‚Äî the screen box of the number, mirroring renderElPx's anchor / nudge / posF / offset math exactly) + `suppressedDimIds` (`lib/labelLayout.js`); `layoutLabels` now also returns each placement's committed `box`. Computed in the collision pass, threaded into `renderElPx` as a `dimSuppressed` set.
- **Preserves B592** (the dimension stays ON the footprint, slides along the length, NO leader) and the selected-element edit-handle exception ‚Äî we only HIDE an overlapping dimension, never move or leader it. The older B121 ask to pull dimension text off its line with a leader was **superseded by B592 and deliberately NOT built**.
- **"Show dimensions" + "Show areas" toggles** (default ON) added to `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` + the ‚öô Setup panel so a crowded layout can shed the dimension tier or the sf/acre tier. `showAreas` drops only the area line ‚Äî name, dimension line, and the pond "Holds ‚Ä¶ ac-ft" storage line stay.
- Verified: **2215 unit tests green** (new `dimNumberBox` / `suppressedDimIds` / `buildingLabelLines`-falsy / `layoutLabels`-box tests) ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green + `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact ¬∑ headless `ui-audit/verify-b121-b148.mjs` (14 checks): dims render, **0 dim‚Üîlabel overlaps at fit AND zoomed-in**, `showDims` / `showAreas` gate the render, the Setup panel shows both checkboxes, and unchecking "Show dimensions" live-hides the callouts (3 ‚Üí 0). Screenshot eyeballed.

### B148 ‚Äî Site-summary readout renders as an oversized world-space banner over the map `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) ‚Äî ‚úÖ RESOLVED (not reproducible) 2026-07-02 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
Closed after a full code trace + headless verification: there is **no world-space summary banner and no zoom-scaled font anywhere** on the planner canvas ‚Äî the canvas is screen-projected via `f2p` (no global zoom transform), and a grep for a ppf-scaled font-size returns zero hits. The site totals (SF / acreage / perimeter) live in the fixed-size **Site Yield side panel** (`SitePlanner.jsx`) + the print sheet ‚Äî neither balloons. The "stroked-orange monospace" the report described is the **Area measurement tool** (a user-drawn measure), locked at `fontSize=12` at the polygon centroid, which git history shows was always fixed-size. Headless proof (`ui-audit/verify-b121-b148.mjs`): on-canvas text height held **CONSTANT (42.0px ‚Üí 42.0px)** while the drawing grew 490px ‚Üí 1522px on zoom-in ‚Äî a world-space text would have scaled with the drawing. The only *unbuilt* reading was an optional on-canvas viewport summary chip (an enhancement, not the bug); **owner declined it this pass (2026-07-02)** ‚Äî a future session can pick it up as net-new, reusing `lib/sheetFurniture.js` `screenFurniturePlates` (the scale-bar / north-arrow corner furniture).

### B490 ‚Äî PDF viewer: optional-content (OCG) layer toggle `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature) ‚Äî ‚úÖ DONE 2026-07-03 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
A "‚ñ§ Layers" control in the Review toolbar shows/hides a PDF's optional-content groups (e.g. turn off Electrical to see just the site plan). New pure `lib/ocg.js` (`ocgLayerList` + `deriveLayerVisibility`) iterates pdf.js's OptionalContentConfig ‚Äî v6 has **no `getGroups()`**, so it uses the `[id, group]` iterator; `renderInto` threads the retained config via `optionalContentConfigPromise`; `DocReview` extracts the config on load (`readOcg`, guarded on `pdfRef` against a superseded load), lists the groups in a **portaled `AnchoredMenu`** popover (escapes the toolbar row's `overflow:hidden` clip ‚Äî a hand-rolled popover was clipped), and re-rasters both layers on toggle. Visibility is **in-memory** (a view filter, never the immutable markups). **Required a pdf.js polyfill:** the modern ESM build calls `Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed` (a TC39 proposal) but ‚Äî unlike the legacy build ‚Äî doesn't bundle it, so `getOptionalContentConfig()` threw; added a guarded polyfill in `lib/pdf.js`.
Verified: new `test/ocg.test.js` + full suite **2225** green ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green + DocReview lazy chunk intact ¬∑ headless `ui-audit/verify-ocg.mjs` (**8 checks**, hand-authored 2-layer OCG fixture `e2e/fixtures/sample-ocg.pdf` via `ui-audit/make-sample-ocg-pdf.mjs`): the Layers button appears, the popover lists Electrical+Plumbing and is genuinely **visible (not clipped)**, unchecking Electrical re-rasters red‚Üí0 while blue persists, re-checking restores it, no page errors. Screenshot eyeballed.

### B489 ‚Äî PDF viewer crispness: sub-pixel seam + continuous-pan re-raster + useSystemFonts `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ‚Äî ‚úÖ DONE 2026-07-03 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
All three held-back refinements from the B488 audit shipped:
- **Sub-pixel seam** ‚Äî `renderInto` now returns the **device-rounded** region in page units (`region:{rx:ox/S,‚Ä¶}`, via a new pure `deviceRect` in `renderBudget.js`), and `DocReview.renderDetail` builds the detail tile from `d.region` (not `reg.rect`). The detail canvas's page‚ÜíCSS factor is now exactly `1/density` at origin `ox/density`, matching the backdrop ‚Üí the ‚â§0.5px seam/ghosting on thin lines is gone. Proven by algebra in `renderBudget.test.js` (round-trip idempotence + the density-match; the OLD unrounded region provably did NOT display at `density`).
- **Continuous-pan re-raster** ‚Äî a leading-edge throttle (~140ms, pinch-excluded) on the settle effect re-rasters the crisp detail window mid-pan, not only on settle; self-limiting via the existing `tileCovers` no-op + superseded-render cancellation.
- **useSystemFonts:false** ‚Äî set in `loadPdf`'s `getDocument`, so non-embedded fonts render from the shipped `standardFontDataUrl` substitutes (consistent across machines); harmless to the text-extraction callers.
Verified: **2225 unit tests** green ¬∑ lint 0 ¬∑ build green. The precise sub-pixel-seam look + pan-feel + font fidelity are logged as **V283** (renumbered from V197 on 2026-07-11 to resolve a duplicate-V197 collision) for a browser-equipped eyeball (the harness proves the math + that the paths fire, not the pixel-perfect visual).

### B155 ‚Äî Markup selection hit-testing: grab unfilled shapes by their interior, consistently at any zoom `[Document Review + Site Planner / UI]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B155 ‚Äî `main` concurrently took B147‚ÄìB154 (easement tool, multipart parcel, Delete-key fix), so this is the real next free ID; provisionally B150 then B154 in earlier drafts ‚Äî both numbers were taken by `main` while this was in flight)*
`[ ]` **Repro (owner-reported 2026-06-18):** drawing markups are too finicky to select ‚Äî an **unfilled rectangle only selects when you click its border stroke** (the thin line), not its interior, so you have to "grab exactly on the line." At low zoom even the stroke is hard to land. **Expected:** forgiving, predictable selection for **every** markup type ‚Äî clicking anywhere inside a closed shape grabs it, and the "grab feel" is the same whether zoomed way in or out.
- **Rendering approach (inspected ‚Äî fix differs by surface, all THREE confirmed SVG):**
  - **Document Review (`DocReview.jsx`)** ‚Äî *already correct, the reference implementation.* A centralized `hitTest(p)` (added under **B33**) already does interior-selection for `rect`/`cloud` (point-in-expanded-bbox), bbox for `text`, and nearest-vertex-OR-segment for measures, with a **screen-space tolerance** (`tol = 10/scale`). **Generalize THIS pattern; don't reinvent it.**
  - **Site Planner main canvas (`SitePlanner.jsx`, markup render ~L4698)** ‚Äî neutral shapes (`rect`/`ellipse`/`polygon`) rendered `fill:"none"` with selection wired to each element's own `onPointerDown` ‚Üí interior was dead, **stroke-only**. (Filled `els` and the special `utilRoute`/`encumbrance`/`traced` markups already select fine because they paint a real fill.)
  - **Parcel-drawing markup (`components/ParcelDrawing.jsx`, B67)** ‚Äî the **Box** tool, identical defect: rect `fill:"none"` + element-level `onPointerDown` = stroke-only. (Text markups are positioned `<div>`s, so their box already selects.)
- **‚úÖ Increment 1 ‚Äî interior-grab for CLOSED shapes ‚Äî DONE 2026-06-18 (branch `claude/busy-knuth-3q9cyn`):** the minimal, owner-requested rectangle fix. `pointerEvents:"all"` on closed markup shapes (`rect`/`ellipse`/`polygon` in `SitePlanner.jsx`; the `rect`/Box in `ParcelDrawing.jsx`) makes the **fill area a hit target even when unfilled**, so a click anywhere inside selects (and drags ‚Äî both surfaces' move handlers already `stopPropagation`, so an interior grab won't also pan). This is the **same `pointerEvents="all"` technique B142 already shipped for text/callout boxes**, now extended to the neutral shape markups that B142 missed; Doc Review had the equivalent via B33. Open paths untouched (see below). **Self-verified headless on the Site Planner (V41): drew an unfilled markup rect ‚Üí Escape deselects (handles vanish) ‚Üí clicking its INTERIOR centre re-selects it (resize/rotate handles reappear) ‚Üí PASS.** ParcelDrawing's identical one-attribute change was not separately driven (needs a drawing attached + rasterized), logged under V41 as low-risk-by-analogy.
- **üî≤ Remaining (the "all the other elements" tranche ‚Äî the actual handoff):**
  1. **Shared `hitTest`, one source of truth.** Lift Doc Review's hit-test into shared code (`src/shared/` ‚Äî alongside the coordinate stub) as `hitTest(point, element, { tolerancePx, zoom }) ‚Üí boolean|distance`, and route **all three** surfaces' selection through it, replacing the per-type/per-element click logic. The ported Site-Planner measurement tools (B116) and Doc Review share the same coordinate spine and should select identically ‚Äî don't fork per workspace.
  2. **Tolerance is screen-space, converted at test time.** Default **6px**, `tolerance_world = tolerancePx / zoom` (Doc Review already does `10/scale`; standardize). This is what makes the grab feel zoom-independent.
  3. **Per-element rules:** *closed* (rect/ellipse/polygon/cloud) ‚Üí point-in-shape **+** stroke buffer (cloud hit-tests its underlying polygon, not the scalloped path); *line/arrow* ‚Üí point-to-segment ‚â§ tol, arrowhead included; *polyline/freehand/pen* ‚Üí nearest-segment ‚â§ tol (factor in stroke width); *text* ‚Üí full bounding box (single-click select, double-click edit), even with no border/fill; *callout* ‚Üí text-box bounds OR leader line; *point/count* ‚Üí generous ~8px screen radius (no area to aim at); *measurements* (linear/polyline/area) ‚Üí matching shape rule **and** the value label is part of the hit region. **Measurements are takeoff-critical ‚Äî treat hard-to-select or wrong-element selection as HIGH severity (same as a wrong number).**
  4. **‚úÖ Open-path forgiving hit area ‚Äî DONE 2026-06-29 (Site Planner; owner re-reported the Line being far harder to grab than a Polyline).** `line`/`polyline` were still stroke-only after Increment 1 (the `<line>`/`<polyline>` rendered `fill:"none"` so only the 2px painted stroke caught clicks). Both now carry a transparent **fat hit-stroke** (`MK_HIT_PX = 12`px ‚Üí ~6px each side, zoom-independent since `f2p` projects feet‚Üíscreen px) on a wrapping `<g>` that owns the select/drag handler; the visible stroke is `pointerEvents:"none"`. Reuses the B146/B420 technique. Headless-verified (`ui-audit/verify-markup-fixes.mjs`: a click ~5px off the line selects, ~40px off does not). Doc Review's open paths were already covered by the shared `hitTest.js` (`PICK_TOL_PX = 6`). The `pen` kind only exists in the shared engine (Doc Review), already handled. **Still open below:** the shared-`hitTest` unification for the Site Planner surface (increments 1‚Äì3, 5‚Äì7).
  5. **Z-order resolution:** hit-test top-of-stack downward, select the first hit; tie-break **smaller-area wins** (a small shape on/inside a large unfilled one stays grabbable). Empty-canvas click deselects.
  6. **Move-by-interior-drag vs. pan** stays explicit: select-mode interior drag = move element; pan-mode drag = pan. (Already true post-Increment 1; keep it true in the shared path.)
  7. **Resize/move handles win** over anything behind them, with a few px of screen-space slop.
- **Acceptance:** click inside an unfilled rect ‚Üí selects; within ~6px of any stroke at any zoom ‚Üí selects; zoom 10√ó in then out ‚Üí identical grab feel; two overlapping shapes ‚Üí topmost, and a small shape inside a big unfilled one ‚Üí the small one; line/polyline/pen segment within tol ‚Üí selects; anywhere in a text box ‚Üí selects, double-click ‚Üí edits; interior drag moves and never pans; empty click deselects.
> **Dedup / reconciliation ‚Äî build on what's shipped, don't fork:**
> - **B142 + B143 (DONE) ‚Äî the direct precedent.** B142 already set `pointerEvents="all"` on the **text/callout box** so its whole interior selects even with no fill (explicitly "to match Doc Review's shape-aware hit test"); B143 finished the Bluebeam-style text editing (Enter = newline, click-away/Esc to finish, via a `pointerEvents:"all"` click-out catcher). So **text-box selection + editing is already done on the Site Planner side ‚Äî do NOT redo it here.** Increment 1 is the same fix applied to the shape markups B142 didn't cover. B141 likewise added measurement vertex-editing "consistent with Doc Review's shape-aware select."
> - **B33 (DONE)** is the reference hit-test ‚Äî Increment 1 brought the other two surfaces up to its rectangle behaviour; the shared-`hitTest` tranche generalizes B33 rather than replacing it.
> - **B146 (DONE)** already introduced (a) the **transparent fat hit-stroke** for grabbing a thin line and (b) the shared inline **`numEdit`** on-canvas editor that replaced `window.prompt`. Reuse (a) for open-path markups here.
> - **Owner "no dialog boxes" rule (KEY DECISIONS, 2026-06-17):** Doc Review's **Text** tool is inline (B293) and **Calibrate** (Doc Review + Stitcher) is now inline with validation (**B304**) ‚Äî no more `window.prompt` in those flows. **Only remaining:** **ParcelDrawing's calibrate** (`window.prompt`) ‚Üí convert to the inline `numEdit` pattern; fold in here, don't file separately.
> - **Distinct from B149/B121 (LOD + label collision):** those govern what's *drawn* at a given zoom; this governs what's *selectable* when clicked. Keep separate.

### B156 ‚Äî Hover highlight on the markup under the cursor (pre-click affordance) `[Document Review + Site Planner / UI]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted B156 ‚Äî next free ID after B155)*
`[ ]` In **Select** mode, highlight the markup currently under the cursor ‚Äî the element that *would* be selected on click ‚Äî before the click lands, so you can see what you're about to grab. Subtle outline/glow; never disturbs the immutable PDF/drawing backdrop layer. **Depends on B155's shared `hitTest`** and must use the **same topmost/smaller-area resolution**, so the hover preview always matches what a click selects. Out of scope for B155 (a separate, lower-priority affordance) ‚Äî minted net-new so B155 can ship without it.
> **‚úÖ SHIPPED 2026-07-03 (B155 + B156 together, branch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`) ‚Äî one shared selection picker + a pre-click hover preview.**
> - **B155 ‚Äî the JS-picker surface now shares ONE picker (`src/shared/markup/hitTest.js` `pickMarkup`).** The shared picker was upgraded to the Document Review REFERENCE feel (it was previously *weaker* than DocReview's inline picker): a new `scoreMarkup(m, p, tol, markerTol, scale)` returns `{ d, interior }` mirroring DocReview's inline `hitTest` exactly ‚Äî rect/cloud/snapshot tol-expanded box interior, ellipse radii+tol, the full **text box** (6.5 px/char √ó 16, screen-sized via `scale`) + **callout** body & leader, `area`/`polygon` `pointInPoly` interior, nearest vertex/segment for open & measure kinds ‚Äî and `pickMarkupIndex` scores every markup and picks **nearest ‚Üí smallest bbox-area (B374) ‚Üí top-most** on ties. **Document Review** was re-routed onto it: its 66-line inline `hitTest` is deleted, replaced by a one-line `pickMarkup(pageMarks, p, view, { tolPx: 10 })?.id` wrapper (behaviour-preserving, verified). **The Site Planner was DELIBERATELY left on its SVG-native picking** (per-element `pointerEvents:"all"` interiors + a 12 px fat hit-stroke on lines + top-most via DOM paint order): it already delivers the shared rules declaratively via the browser's own hit-testing, and re-routing it to the imperative picker would have REGRESSED `easement`/`encumbrance` (the shared tool matrix doesn't classify them closed -> they'd lose interior-grab) and `ellipse` (bbox vs. true ellipse), while rewriting the mature multi/group/drag/vertex-edit interaction stack for **zero user-visible gain**. So the *selection semantics* are unified (one rule set, honoured on both surfaces) even though the Site Planner's *mechanism* stays SVG-native ‚Äî the smaller, safer edit (CLAUDE.md "keep shared edits small + additive; no big-bang rewrite"). The owner-reported interior-grab bug that opened B155 was already fixed on all three surfaces (increment 1 + the open-path fat-stroke); this closes the "one source of truth" tranche for the JS-picker surface + gives DocReview the WYSIWYG text-box/smallest-area rules from one place.
> - **B156 ‚Äî a subtle pre-click hover glow shows what a click will grab, on both surfaces.** A shared `.mk-hover` CSS class (a two-tone light-under-dark `drop-shadow`, mirroring the selection chrome so it reads on both a light sheet and a dark aerial) glows the markup under the cursor in Select mode ‚Äî never recolouring it, never touching the immutable backdrop. **Document Review** computes the hovered id on the idle pointer-move path with the SAME `pickMarkup` wrapper the click uses (hover == selection by construction) and wraps ONLY the hovered markup (overlay DOM otherwise unchanged, so existing selectors/exports are untouched). **The Site Planner** sets the hovered id from each markup's own `onPointerEnter`/`onPointerLeave` ‚Äî the browser's hit-testing, the exact geometry that decides a click ‚Äî so its hover matches its click by construction; the hovered subtree carries `.mk-hover`. Never shown on the already-selected markup.

### B757 ‚Äî Ôºã Add parcel menu: mismatched header font sizes + over-explaining copy `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) ‚Äî ‚úÖ SHIPPED 2026-07-11 ‚Äî `Verify: sandbox`
Origin: filed 2026-07-11 from owner chat (screenshot of the Ôºã Add parcel menu). Owner report: "one of the fonts are all different sizes," and the descriptions were over-explaining the obvious ("Add by address" ‚Üí "Type a street address," "Draw a new boundary" ‚Üí "of course you're gonna have to click points") rather than adding real information; also asked for "county GIS" to read more like something a real-estate person recognizes.
- **Root cause of the size mismatch:** the "üîç Identify" and "‚úèÔ∏è Draw" option headers live inside `<button style={menuItem(...)}>`, which sets `fontSize: 12.5` on the button (inherited by its child `<div>`s). The "üìç Add by address" header is a plain `<div>` (not a button, since it also hosts the text input), so it never picked up that font-size and rendered at the browser's ambient size instead ‚Äî visibly bigger than its neighbors, exactly matching the screenshot. Fixed by giving all three option headers an explicit `fontSize: 13` (and keeping descriptions at the existing `fontSize: 11`), so sizing no longer depends on which wrapper element happens to host each option.
- **Copy pass (all three options), `SitePlanner.jsx` ~L9504‚Äì9547:**
  - **"Identify from county GIS" ‚Üí "üîç Click a lot on the map"**, description ‚Üí "Property lines from the county appraisal district light up on the aerial ‚Äî click one lot or several." (plainer term than the jargon "GIS"; explains what the click does rather than just naming the mechanism).
  - **"üìç Add by address"** header kept (already clear); description trimmed to "We'll look up that address at the county and add the lot." ‚Äî dropped "Type a street address" since the input field right below it already makes that obvious.
  - **"‚úèÔ∏è Draw a new boundary"** header kept; description replaced "Trace a lot by clicking points on the canvas; close on the first dot" (stating the obvious mechanic) with "For a lot that's not in county records yet" (states *when* to reach for this option instead).
  - Disabled-state copy for both GIS-dependent options (no georeferenced plan yet) unified from two slightly different jargon-y sentences ("Identify needs a georeferenced plan...", "Add-by-address needs a georeferenced plan...") to one consistent plain sentence: "Add a parcel from the map first to turn this on."
  - The one other place this menu's old label was echoed in copy ‚Äî the "align to deed" empty-state warning (`flashWarn` at ~L8663) ‚Äî was updated to reference the new "Ôºã Add ‚Üí Click a lot on the map" path so the two don't drift.
- **Verified:** `npm run build` green. `ui-audit/verify-b383-add-parcel.mjs` updated for the new copy (its two now-passing assertions were already stale against unrelated hint text, fixed in the same pass) ‚Äî **22/22 checks pass** headless against the built app, incl. the disabled-state copy, the Identify‚Üíclick‚Üíadd flow, and Draw arming with no origin. `ui-audit/verify-b384-add-by-address.mjs` ‚Äî **5/5** unchanged (it didn't assert the removed sentence). Screenshot confirms all three headers now render at identical size. Pure client-side copy/style change, no data path touched ‚Äî `Verify: sandbox` is correct, no live check needed.
> - **Verified:** 21 shared-picker unit tests (`test/markupHitTest.test.js` ‚Äî reference-parity: interior `d=0`, outline distance, text-box far-end hit, callout body+leader, perimeter interior-grab, **smallest-area-beats-top-most incl. the small-UNDER-big case**, tolerance override) + the full **2249-test** suite green ¬∑ lint **0 errors** ¬∑ build green with `SitePlannerApp`/`DocReview` lazy chunks intact ¬∑ **headless both surfaces** (`ui-audit/verify-b155-b156.mjs`, logged-out): hover glow appears on interior-hover, clears on leave, carries `.mk-hover`, and **clicking the exact hovered point selects the same markup (hover == click)** ‚Äî 15/15 checks, no page errors. Real-display / signed-in / touch feel logged as **V200**.

### B720 ‚Äî Canonical thoroughfare-segment data model + jurisdiction registry `[Site Planner / GIS ¬∑ data model]` (feature) #thoroughfare #gis #persistence #coordinates  *(filed 2026-07-08 from chat, provisional NEW-1; foundation of the Thoroughfare-Plan / ROW-exposure epic B720‚ÄìB726)*
`[x]` **DONE 2026-07-11 ‚Äî shipped + verified LIVE on `planyr_production`.** The empty data spine for the epic: a PostGIS `thoroughfare_segments` table (normalized centerlines across all jurisdictions) + a `jurisdiction_row_standards` (jurisdiction, classification)‚ÜíROW/building-line lookup + a `jurisdictions` config registry. Segment geometry + verified ROW-standard widths are populated by ingestion (B721/B722), not here.
- **Verified live (2026-07-11, planyr_production via Supabase apply_migration):** PostGIS 3.3 enabled; all 3 tables + 3 public-read RLS policies + 5 indexes on thoroughfare_segments (incl. 2 GiST spatial) created; 7 jurisdictions seeded (coh, harris, fortbend, pearland, montgomery, sugarland, hgac). **Security confirmed as the actual `anon` role:** a signed-out `select` returns all 7 jurisdictions (public read works) and a signed-out `insert` is rejected ‚Äî `42501: new row violates row-level security policy`. Idempotent re-run proven on the dev project first. Closes VERIFICATION V275 (minted V273; renumbered on merge ‚Äî B759 took V273). (The "run the SQL" step Michael delegated ‚Äî applied for him.)
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/db/thoroughfare_segments.sql` (migration ‚Äî 3 tables, public-read RLS + explicit `grant select` to anon/authenticated, GiST spatial indexes, jurisdictions seed) ¬∑ `src/shared/thoroughfare/classification.js` (canonical enum + `normalizeClassification`/`normalizeStatus`, shared by schema/ingestion/overlay/analysis) ¬∑ `test/thoroughfare.test.js` (12 tests).
- **Design call (first of its kind):** PUBLIC REFERENCE DATA ‚Äî readable by anon+authenticated, writable only by service-role ingestion (no write policies + SELECT-only grants). Deliberate departure from private-by-default: zero user data, same class as the public GIS layers. ROW width is resolved from `jurisdiction_row_standards` at ingest, never trusted off the source feature.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-08 from chat (provisional NEW-1).
- DEDUPE-FIRST (verified net-new). Nearest prior art reused, NOT dupes: B369 (config-driven GIS Source Registry) ¬∑ B93 (jurisdiction identify) ¬∑ B13 ¬∑ B602.

### B760 ‚Äî Layers panel de-text: per-row ‚ìò, kill group disclaimers, plain-English renames `[Site Planner]` (task) #site-planner #ui  *(owner review 2026-07-11 as "NEW-1"; ¬ßA1‚ÄìA3. Shipped PR1 same session.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî moved ALL persistent explanatory text (source lines, "as of"/vintage lines, row notes, and the four group disclaimer paragraphs) behind a per-row ‚ìò info popover, so with the panel open + all layers off every row is exactly ONE line (checkbox + label + ‚ìò + status dot) and there are zero paragraphs. One muted footer "Screening data ‚Äî verify before relying on it." replaces the four disclaimers.**

Full suite 3429 green, `eslint` clean, build green.

- **A1** ‚Äî new module-scope `components/RowInfo.jsx` built on `AnchoredMenu` (portal ‚Üí never clipped by the Layers card's `overflow:hidden`; Esc + click-away reused). Kept "Contour lines (1 ft)" + "Poles & hydrants from street imagery".

### B762 ‚Äî Fold single-layer county groups `[Site Planner]` (task) #site-planner #ui #gis  *(owner review 2026-07-11 as "NEW-3"; ¬ßA5. Shipped PR1 same session.)*

### B779 ‚Äî Concurrent-mint B#/V# collisions: catch loudly at PR time + prevent by late-binding `[repo / tooling ¬∑ workflow]` (tooling) #infra  *(owner chat 2026-07-11 ‚Äî "I thought I fixed the issue where branches use the same numbers ‚Ä¶ now you have to renumber your stuff?" after a concurrent floodplain/detention session (B763‚ÄìB773) collided with this session's Layers-panel mints. Best-practice fix; sources: `scripts/next-id.mjs`, `test/idUniqueness.test.js`. `B779` late-bound against fresh main with `next-id -- --against-main`, the very rule this ships.)*
`[x]` **DONE this session ‚Äî `next-id` (B755) stops a session MISREADING the next-free number, but it can't stop two parallel branches (each branched from the same main, neither seeing the other's un-merged mint) from grabbing the SAME number ‚Äî the distributed-counter impossibility. Made a collision RARE, AUTO-CAUGHT, and CHEAP without changing the id scheme or a big migration:**
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî 22 tests (`test/idUniqueness.test.js` + extended `test/nextId.test.js`); the guard was PROVEN to go red on an injected duplicate heading then green on revert; full suite **3577** green, lint clean, `next-id` output unchanged in default mode.
- Origin: owner chat 2026-07-11 (numbering collisions still forcing renumbers).
- **Guard (centerpiece):** `test/idUniqueness.test.js` fails the build if two ACTIVE items share a `B#` (in `BACKLOG.md`) or `V#` (in `VERIFICATION.md`) ‚Äî so a colliding PR goes red BEFORE merge and renumbers then, instead of shipping silently (which is how ~50 historical archive dupes + a duplicate `V197` slipped onto main). `findDuplicateIds`/`findDuplicateIdsIn` added to `scripts/next-id.mjs`.
- **Prevention:** `next-id --against-main` folds in `git show origin/main:BACKLOG.md` etc., so a LATE mint (assign the number as the last step before push, `git fetch origin main` first ‚Äî new CLAUDE.md rule) steps around ids merged after you branched. Collapses the collision window from a whole session to a few seconds; code/tests keep the provisional label so a rare clash renumbers only heading lines.
- **Scope (honest):** the guard enforces uniqueness on the LIVE files only. The write-only `*-DONE.md` archives carry ~50 historical cross-file collisions (genuine ones like B755=BFE-vs-next-id, V275=toggle-vs-thoroughfare, plus benign same-item re-listings) that predate the guard and can't be renumbered in place; `--against-main` prevents minting over them, and the full-pair audit stays available via `findDuplicateIds(REPO, B_FILES, "B")` for a future archive cleanup. Fixed the one live dup (`V197`‚Üí`V283`) as part of this.
- **Rejected (with reasons):** collision-proof ids (session/date tag or hashes) ‚Äî repo-wide migration + loses "highest+1" ordering; auto-renumber-on-merge bot ‚Äî fragile (must rewrite scattered prose + V#‚ÜîB# cross-refs). Both trade a rare, cheap, now-automated reconcile for a large or brittle system.

### B780 ‚Äî Audit the 58 historical duplicate ids + close the cross-file (live‚Üîarchive) collision gap `[repo / tooling ¬∑ workflow]` (tooling) #infra  *(owner chat 2026-07-11 ‚Äî "Should we examine the 50 duplicates to see if there's a risk to the codebase?" Follow-up to B779, which surfaced them. `B780` late-bound against fresh main.)*
`[x]` **DONE this session ‚Äî examined every duplicate id the B779 warning surfaced and closed the one guard gap the audit exposed. AUDIT VERDICT: 58 collisions (37 B# + 21 V#), ALL genuine (two different features sharing a number ‚Äî zero benign re-listings; classified by title-similarity, `sim < 0.35`), but ZERO runtime risk: the ids appear in code only inside comments (provenance breadcrumbs), nothing executes on them, no data path reads them. The residual risk is workflow-only (a lookup landing on the wrong item ‚Äî bounded by AUDIT-FIRST) and is now capped by the guard below.**
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî the cross-file guard was PROVEN red on an injected live‚Üîarchive collision (a fresh id heading added to BOTH `BACKLOG.md` and `BACKLOG-DONE.md`) and green on revert, with the live-only test correctly unaffected; baseline-honesty + not-a-no-op unit cases included. Full suite green, lint clean.
- Origin: owner chat 2026-07-11 (risk question on the B779 duplicate findings).
- **The gap:** B779's uniqueness guard checked the LIVE files only ‚Äî but most of the 58 historical dupes formed ACROSS files, via the race where session A ships and ARCHIVES its item while session B's same-numbered item stays open (the two headings land in different files, so the live-only check never sees them). That path was still open for future collisions.
- **The fix:** `KNOWN_LEGACY_ID_COLLISIONS` (`scripts/next-id.mjs`) ‚Äî the 58 audited ids frozen at their exact counts (B445/V45 √ó3, rest √ó2) ‚Äî plus `newCrossFileCollisions()` and two new `test/idUniqueness.test.js` cases over the FULL live+archive pair: any id newly collided, or any grandfathered id collided one more time, fails the build. A baseline-honesty case forces the allowlist to only ever SHRINK (clean a legacy dup ‚Üí delete its row in the same commit), so a stale baseline can't mask a collision on a recycled id.
- **Why grandfather instead of renumber:** the legacy dupes are load-bearing history ‚Äî renumbering either side breaks scattered cross-references in the write-only archives (V entries citing B#s, code comments citing both meanings), `--against-main` already prevents minting over them, and they carry no runtime dependence. Recorded, capped, and shrinkable beats risky archaeology.
- CLAUDE.md's B779 minting note updated to describe the two-layer guard accurately.

### B794 ‚Äî Six fire-and-forget cloud pushes swallow failures (rename / duplicate / status / new site) `[Site Planner]` (bug) #site-planner #persistence #ui  *(found by the 2026-07-12 data-deletion-safety audit as "NEW-F6" ‚Äî a LOUD-FAILURE gap, not data loss: the device copy is authoritative and the heal re-push recovers. Notify-only ‚Äî no retry loop (the existing next-edit/pull heal already re-pushes). Signed-out stays silent (`{ok:true, skipped}`).
- Verify: sandbox ‚Äî build green + full suite green (behavioral change is a banner on an error path; the heal path itself is untouched and already covered).

- **Recurrence: 2026-07-17 (√ó1) ‚Äî CONFIRMED live by the Cowork pass + FIXED (DEDUPE-FIRST ‚Äî no new number).** The pass saw a **RE-CHECK chip rendering INSIDE the Stormwater section** ("flood pull predates the drawn area / unknown ‚Äî enter the missing input") ‚Äî a per-section stale pathway, when the owner rule is **stale state lives in the HEADER line ONLY.** Root cause of *why it survived the prior fix:* the original NEW-2 fix didn't leave a pre-existing pathway ‚Äî it **introduced** this per-section stale chip (`d.mitStalePending ‚Üí mitChip "RE-CHECK" ¬∑ mitSub "flood pull predates the drawn area"`) as part of the anti-false-all-clear design, and B874's env-rounding bug then made `drainFetchStale` ambiently true so it appeared on every fresh load. **Fix:** deleted the `mitStalePending ‚Üí RE-CHECK "predates"` section chip and the "¬∑ stale" section suffix ‚Äî staleness now surfaces only in the header status line (B874's bounded machine). While a genuine refresh is in flight with no last-good, the section reads an honest UNKNOWN (never a fresh-0 all-clear). Also made the UNKNOWN **bar** copy compliant: dropped the generic "unknown ‚Äî enter the missing input" (‚Üí just "unknown"); the SECTION rows name the specific input + jump-to-field (`Set BFE ‚Üí`). The clean B867 wins held (no null/undefined, no truncation, no overlap). The separate "stuck-refresh state machine" is B874. V350 re-runs post-B874.
- **Stale never all-clears:** a new `drainLastGoodMitRef` captures the last NON-stale live compute; while `drainFetchStale`, the readout serves that last-known-good stamped `stale` (its data age still rides the header), or UNKNOWN-pending (`stalePending`) if none exists ‚Äî never a fresh hard 0. The real-0-vs-UNKNOWN rule now applies only to CURRENT geometry; on the next fetch the flag clears and the verdict flashes (existing behavior). The mit verdict honors `mitStale`/`mitStalePending`.
- **One place for stale:** the `assumptionsBlock` header no longer morphs into the dev-speak "the fetch predates the drawn area" banner and no longer demotes to a dashed border ‚Äî it's a plain "Assumptions ‚Äî correct if needed" INPUTS group (the reviewing-agency selector is no longer trapped in a notice container). Fetch staleness surfaces in EXACTLY one place: the status line.
- **OVER-DUG retired:** the chip / balance line / bar status / print pair all read COVERED for an over-provided cut (a zero-requirement surplus must never out-shout a real shortfall); the surplus note demotes to a quiet method fold.
- **req-0 quiet state:** no fill in the trigger floodplain ‚Üí chip COVERED (muted), "nothing to offset".
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** extends B860's fetch/recompute split (the `fetchStaleForEdit` signal now reaches the pricing path); not a recurrence.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `yieldBar.test.js` (over ‚Üí covered; shortfall stays loud). Full suite (4196) + lint 0 errors + build green; headless mount clean.
- **‚è≥ V350 (live):** see VERIFICATION.md.

### B874 ‚Äî Stuck flood-refresh: the "Refreshing flood data‚Ä¶" spinner never clears (√ó1 ‚Äî edit path) `[Site Planner / yield]` (bug ‚Äî crash-severity class) #site-planner #yield #floodplain #gis  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-16 post-ship as "NEW-1"; minted B874 by the B873 audit, IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-17 after the Cowork live pass confirmed it AMBIENT. Branch `claude/detention-verdict-delta-bugs-l3mm1l`. **V renumbered 357‚Üí358 on merge ‚Äî main's V357 (single-occupancy dock) merged first.** Recurrence 2026-07-17 (√ó1) ‚Äî see below.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî timing/race + real-project-data, needs a signed-in Tsakiris fresh-load) ‚Üí V358.** Cowork live: a fresh hard-reload with ZERO edits already showed "Refreshing flood data for the drawn area‚Ä¶" stuck >60s, Detention frozen at 38.30 ‚Äî ambient, not edit-triggered. The single blocker (freezes/starves steps 2,3,6,7,9 and gates B868/B869). **Measure-before-patch root cause (traced in code):** the fetch-record writer stored `fetchRec.env` via `Math.round` (a min can round UP, a max DOWN ‚Üí the stored envelope SHRINKS below the drawn geometry); the reader (`drainFactsNow`) compares the RAW geometry with `envelopeContains(..., tol 0)`. So on a fresh load with unchanged geometry the containment FAILED ‚Üí `revalidationNeed` returned `env-exit` (need:true) permanently ‚Üí and `drainAutoRefreshing = drainReval.need || busy` showed "Refreshing‚Ä¶" forever even though the single auto-attempt was spent and nothing was in flight.
- ‚úÖ **DONE ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-18 (V358 PASSED, planyr.io verification handoff):** the edit-path flood-refresh is bounded (success ~10s / terminal by ~30s, never endless).
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-16 from chat (post-ship block); confirmed live by the Cowork pass.
- **Root fix ‚Äî canonical envelope:** new `canonEnv` (`factRevalidation.js`) rounds OUTWARD (floor mins, ceil maxs) so the stored env always CONTAINS the geometry; `ENV_TOL_FT = 2` slack in `fetchStaleForEdit` + `revalidationNeed`'s env-exit absorbs the whole-foot gap. Writer + reader now agree by construction ‚Äî no ambient env-exit.
- **Bounded state machine:** the spinner is decoupled from `drainReval.need` ‚Äî it shows only for a REAL in-flight/armed auto fetch; a wanted refetch whose one attempt is spent + not in flight is a TERMINAL `drainAutoStalled` state ("‚öÝ Couldn't refresh ‚Äî ‚Üª Re-check"), never an endless spinner. The whole pull is raced against a 30s timeout (a black-holed fetch can't leave `busy` latched). A manual ‚Üª clears the one-attempt guard so the target can auto-retry.
- **Also fixes** the ambient false `drainFetchStale` (NEW-2's stale-hold) and the in-section RE-CHECK chip (B867) that rode the same env-rounding trigger.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** distinct new symptom on B860's refresh surface (cross-linked); the env-rounding is a defect in B832/B860's fetch-record.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `factRevalidation.test.js` (+6: canonEnv contains, REGRESSION proof the old Math.round tripped env-exit, unchanged-geometry not-stale, real growth still trips, guards). Full suite (4241) + lint 0 errors + build green.
- **üîÅ Recurrence 2026-07-17 (√ó1) ‚Äî the EDIT-PATH re-fetch was still unbounded (Cowork live pass on the deployed #656 build).** The #656 fix bounded the ambient ON-LOAD spinner (canonEnv), but on any boundary change the re-fetch spinner still hung >60s with NO success-settle and NO timeout‚Üíterminal (mitigation stuck "UNKNOWN", terminal error never shown), on a normal nudge AND a far-outside drag. **Measure-before-patch:** the fetch itself is 30 s-race-bounded + the FBCDD samplers are 8 s-bounded, so the hang lives ABOVE the fetch in the DERIVED spinner state ‚Äî two unbounded branches: (a) the "armed" branch (`drainReval.need && !attemptSpent && !busy`) stayed true forever when the auto-refetch timer bailed on the 20 s rate floor / a hidden tab WITHOUT marking the attempt spent ‚Äî no fetch, no terminal, no re-arm; (b) a `busy` left latched by a superseded early-return or a black-holed promise had no ceiling.
- **Recurrence fix ‚Äî an independent WATCHDOG bounds BOTH branches (`DRAIN_STUCK_MS = 45 s`, > the 30 s fetch timeout so a slow-but-completing pull isn't cut off; pure `fetchWatchdogFired`):** Watchdog #1 ‚Äî a `busy` fetch still in flight at the ceiling is ABANDONED (bump `drainTok` so its late resolve no-ops) and dropped to a terminal error with a working ‚Üª. Watchdog #2 ‚Äî a WANTED-but-unfired armed episode at the ceiling spends its one attempt (‚Üí the terminal `drainAutoStalled` state) + forces a re-render. Plus the rate floor is FOLDED INTO the auto-refetch delay (`max(base, floorRemaining)`) so the attempt always fires (‚Üí busy ‚Üí watchdog-bounded) instead of being silently dropped. Guarantees the spinner ALWAYS terminates (success ‚â§ fetch latency, else terminal ‚â§ 45 s).
- **Recurrence verified (sandbox):** `factRevalidation.test.js` (+5: ceiling > 30 s, fresh-episode-not-fired vs over-ceiling-fired, custom ceiling, no-episode-never-stuck, non-finite guard). Full suite (4255) + lint 0 errors + build green. V358's acceptance already tests the edit path (boundary edit ‚Üí refresh + flash; forced-fail ‚Üí terminal, never endless) ‚Äî it stands.
- **‚è≥ V358 (live):** see VERIFICATION.md ‚Äî now covers BOTH the ambient on-load AND the edit-path re-fetch (success-settle, forced-fail terminal).

### B875 ‚Äî Pond discoverability: click / label / right-click reach the pond inspector, purpose + sizing assistant (√ó1 ‚Äî label right-click) `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #pond #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-16 post-ship as "NEW-3"; minted B875 by the B873 audit, IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-17 to the owner's full spec. Branch `claude/detention-verdict-delta-bugs-l3mm1l`. **V renumbered 358‚Üí359 on merge ‚Äî main's V357 (single-occupancy dock) merged first.** Recurrence 2026-07-17 (√ó1) ‚Äî see below.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî real-project-data (Tsakiris) UI affordances) ‚Üí V359.** Cowork live: single-click ‚Üí nothing; double-click ‚Üí generic inspector; right-click ‚Üí generic menu (Duplicate/Pin/Attach/Delete). None of the pond affordances existed (the filing-without-shipping defect). Built to spec:
- ‚úÖ **DONE ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-18 (V359 PASSED, planyr.io verification handoff):** pond discoverability + right-click menu confirmed on Tsakiris. (Auto-purpose "Mitigation" for a fully-below-WSE pond is correct.)
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-16 from chat (post-ship block); shipped to the owner's full spec.
- **Selection ‚Üí reveal + flash:** single-clicking a pond opens the Properties companion + scroll-flashes its card (`revealPondInspector` + a `data-pond-card` scroll target + the `planyr-flash` keyframe) ‚Äî a deliberate, owner-requested discoverability exception to B750's "single-click = select only" (ponds carry far more per-element data). Double-click (existing `onElDouble`) and **Enter** (keyboard) open the inspector too.
- **Right-click ‚Üí pond menu:** the `typeMenu` gains a "Detention pond" section ‚Äî ‚öô Pond settings‚Ä¶ ¬∑ üìê Sizing assistant‚Ä¶ ¬∑ **Set purpose ‚ñ∏** (Auto / Detention / Mitigation / Hybrid, with the active one ‚úì'd, one atomic undo). Rides the existing off-screen clamp (menu stays fully on-screen at the map edge).
- **Map label = click target:** a pond's (often leadered) label selects + reveals the pond (`pointerEvents:auto` for pond labels only; other labels stay transparent).
- **Purpose prominence + hint:** the inspector shows a bold **"Purpose: Hybrid ‚Äî auto"** line (effective role + source) and a one-time, dismissible **Hybrid** explainer (persisted `planarfit:pondHybridHintSeen`).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** relatives ‚Äî B656/B657 Properties-rail discoverability; net-new pond affordances, not a recurrence.
- **Verified (sandbox):** build green; the affordances reuse tested paths (`setPropsFor`/`setEls`/`pushHistory`/`typeMenu`); the four entry points + flash need a live UI drive. Full suite (4241) + lint 0 errors.
- **üîÅ Recurrence 2026-07-17 (√ó1) ‚Äî right-clicking a pond opened the generic MAP menu, not the pond menu (Cowork live pass on the deployed #656 build).** Root cause (reproduced headless): #656's own "map label = click target" made the pond LABEL pointer-enabled (`pointerEvents:auto` + `onPointerDown`) but gave it NO `onContextMenu`. The label renders ON TOP of the basin, so a right-click on the visible pond hit the label `<g>`, whose `contextmenu` event had no handler and bubbled to the canvas ‚Üí the empty-map menu (Zoom to fit / Paste / Export to Google Earth‚Ä¶). The pond BODY was already wired (`renderElPx` ‚Üí `onElContext`); only the label-over-body was the hole.
- **Recurrence fix:** route the pond label's right-click to the same `onElContext` the body uses, so the "Detention pond" section (Pond settings / Sizing assistant / Set purpose ‚ñ∏) appears wherever you right-click a pond.
- **Recurrence verified (sandbox + headless):** new live guard `e2e/pond-context-menu.spec.js` (logged-out, real canvas): draw a pond ‚Üí right-click it ‚Üí the ‚öô Pond settings / üìê Sizing assistant menu shows and the map menu's "Export to Google Earth" row does NOT; **proven a true regression guard** (temporarily reverting the label `onContextMenu` fails it ‚Äî the label IS the hit target). Full suite (4255) + lint 0 errors + build green. This converts part of V359's manual right-click gate to an automated e2e (LIVE-VERIFY ‚Üí e2e mapping).
- **Purpose auto-classification (brief item 3, low ‚Äî verify-only, NO code change):** the live inspector read "Purpose: Mitigation ‚Äî auto (from elevation)" where the brief expected "Hybrid". This is LIKELY CORRECT (with ~100% of pond volume below the flood WSE, auto ‚Üí Mitigation is right; Hybrid is for a split) ‚Äî but the WSE it read is the remembered/last-known-good value gated on B874's edit-path refresh. **Re-confirm after B874's live fix flows fresh WSE (folded into the B878 gate); only a real defect if it still misclassifies with a genuine above+below split.** No auto-classifier change this session ‚Äî deliberately.
- **‚è≥ V359 (live):** see VERIFICATION.md ‚Äî the right-click entry point is now e2e-guarded; the flash / off-screen-clamp / purpose-classification-with-fresh-WSE remain the live checks.

### B876 ‚Äî Single-occupancy left dock: element inspector REPLACES the docked panel, never stacks above it `[Site Planner]` (feature) #site-planner #ui #selection  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-16 as "NEW-1"; minted **B876** ‚Äî renumbered from a late B873 collision: a concurrent session's B873/V356 (verification-targeting audit) merged to main first, so this item reconciles-on-merge to B876/V357. Branch `claude/single-occupancy-left-dock-lcml17`. Amends B656/B733 ‚Äî supersedes the companion-stacking layout.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session; parks in ‚è≥ Verify (Verify: live ‚Äî owner asked for a Cowork/planyr.io eyeball; the deterministic interaction is already proven by a headless e2e) ‚Üí V357.** Before, opening a selected element's inspector while a rail panel (e.g. Yield) was docked rendered the inspector STACKED above the docked panel ‚Äî two panels at once. New rule: the dock holds at most ONE panel, always. The inspector now TAKES OVER the dock; to see the inspector AND another panel, detach one to a floating card (B717) first.
- ‚úÖ **DONE ‚Äî self-verified headless 2026-07-18 (V357 PASSED):** single-occupancy dock takeover + restore + floating-coexist proven by `e2e/single-occupancy-dock.spec.js`.
- Verify: live
- Origin: filed 2026-07-16 from chat (owner NEW-1).
- **Takeover, not stack:** when the inspector OPENS on desktop (`propsMatches` ‚Äî B750's explicit open: a double-click, or the Properties tab; a plain single click still just SELECTS, B750 preserved) a layout effect sets `leftPanel ‚Üí "properties"`, memoizing the panel it replaced in `dockMemo`. `companionOpen` on desktop is now simply `leftPanel === "properties"` ‚Äî the 45%-cap "rides above another panel" layout survives ONLY on narrow (B556). Runs in a `useLayoutEffect` so the swap lands before paint (no stacked-panel flash); dock width is constant (both panels dock at `leftWidth`) so the canvas never shifts (VIEWPORT-STABLE, trivially).
- **Restore-on-close:** deselect or the inspector ‚úï drops `propsFor` ‚Üí the same effect hands the dock back to the memoized panel (or closes it) via the pure `dockAfterRelinquish`. A deliberate manual rail switch drops `dockMemo` first, so the manual choice wins over the memo (the resolver also no-ops when `leftPanel` already moved off "properties"). The ‚úï now shows on the desktop docked inspector (was hidden in the Properties tab).
- **Floating interplay:** a floating panel is untouched by selection ‚Äî floating Yield + docked inspector coexist (the intended two-at-once path). Properties stays dock-only (B733), excluded from the B717 float system.
- **Narrow/phone (B556 intact):** the takeover effect early-returns on `narrow`; a tap still only selects and the ‚úé Properties pill opens the companion overlay (never a dock takeover).
- **New pure helpers:** `shared/ui/floatingPanel.js` `shouldInspectorTakeDock` + `dockAfterRelinquish` (host owns the `dockMemo` state + wiring), unit-tested in `test/floatingPanel.test.js`.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** amends B656 (companion) / B733 (docked Properties home) ‚Äî no prior item makes the dock single-occupancy; not a recurrence. Preserves B750 (single-click selects only; its live guard `e2e/click-behavior.spec.js` stays green). Code/tests keep the provisional `NEW-1` label (house rule ‚Äî a late renumber touches only these heading lines, never code).
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/floatingPanel.test.js` (+9: the two helpers) + the NEW-1 source guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` green ¬∑ full suite **4244 green** ¬∑ lint 0 errors ¬∑ build green ¬∑ MAP/backlog-index clean ¬∑ **headless interactive drive** (`e2e/single-occupancy-dock.spec.js`, logged-out, real app): (1) Yield docked ‚Üí double-click a building ‚Üí Yield replaced by the inspector (NOT stacked) ‚Üí ‚úï/deselect ‚Üí Yield restored; (2) detach Yield to a float ‚Üí double-click a building ‚Üí floating Yield + docked inspector coexist ‚Äî both with **0 page errors**; the B750 guard (`e2e/click-behavior.spec.js`) + all floating-panel guards stay green.
- **‚è≥ V357 (live):** see VERIFICATION.md ‚Äî a signed-in Cowork/planyr.io spot-check on a real site (select a detention pond with Yield docked ‚Üí replaced, not stacked) per the owner's stated Verify path.

### B828 ‚Äî Undo records + reverts edits that previously slipped the history stack (four "undo doesn't work" gaps) `[Site Planner / Review]` (bug) #site-planner #doc-review #ui  *(owner report 2026-07-14: "debug the undo button, it doesn't seem to be working"; a fan-out audit of every undoable mutation across the three undo surfaces confirmed four distinct gaps; minted **B828**)*
`[x]` Four separate user gestures mutated undoable state without recording a history frame (or read a STALE snapshot), so clicking Undo did nothing for them ‚Äî or worse, jumped back past an unrelated earlier edit. The core undo button + Ctrl+Z were never broken (markup draw/move/delete undo cleanly, e2e-proven); these are the specific paths that slipped.
- ‚úÖ **DONE ‚Äî self-verified headless 2026-07-18 (V319 PASSED):** undo/redo one-step round-trip proven by `ui-audit/verify-b828-undo.mjs` (8/8).
- Verify: live ‚Äî the item bundles a timing/race sub-fix (the render-time undo mirror), a mandatory LIVE-VERIFY class; the headline case is already sandbox-proven end-to-end (below). ‚Üí V319.
- Origin: filed 2026-07-14 from owner chat.
- **(1) Parcel fill "Translucence" slider** (`SitePlanner.jsx:~10361`) ‚Äî a bare `onChange ‚Üí setSelParcel` (which never pushes history) meant dragging translucence recorded no undo frame, though `fillOpacity` IS in the snapshot; the next Undo then misfired past it. Fixed: spread `{...sliderHistory(...)}` like every other opacity slider (the RC-5/RC-7 house rule). **SANDBOX-VERIFIED end-to-end** ‚Äî `ui-audit/verify-b828-undo.mjs` (8/8, logged-out): draw parcel ‚Üí enable fill ‚Üí drag Translucence 0.12‚Üí0.24 ‚Üí click the Undo BUTTON reverts it in ONE step (parcel+fill survive, no overshoot) ‚Üí Redo restores.
- **(2) Raster overlay "Width (ft)" input** (`SitePlanner.jsx:~9918`) ‚Äî typing a new width (patchOverlay `hist=false`) had no `onFocus` checkpoint, so rescaling an image overlay wasn't undoable though `ftPerPx` IS snapshotted. Fixed: `onFocus={() => pushHistory()}`, mirroring the opacity number input directly above it.
- **(3) Stitcher auto-calibrate** (`Stitcher.jsx:~362`) ‚Äî auto-setting `ftPerUnit` from a grouped set's stated scale had no `pushHistory()`, so one Undo skipped straight past it and DELETED a measurement drawn before the group was added. Fixed: checkpoint before the auto-set.
- **(4) DocReview + Stitcher undo mirror was a passive effect** (`DocReview.jsx:~372`, `Stitcher.jsx:~82`) ‚Äî the B315 anti-pattern: the `docStateRef`/`editRef` mirror lagged one paint behind the live state, so an undo fired right after an edit compared against a stale snapshot, treated the top frame as a no-op, and did nothing (the button stayed enabled, so it just looked broken). Fixed: assign the ref DURING RENDER, exactly like SitePlanner's `stateRef` (the proven B315 fix).
- **Guards:** `test/undoHistoryWiring.test.js` (4 tests) locks all four wirings against a silent merge revert; the pure history stack is already covered by `test/history.test.js`; full suite 3920 green + lint clean.
- **Live-pending (V319):** the overlay Width resize undo (needs an image overlay dropped in), the Stitcher auto-calibrate undo (needs a stitched multi-sheet set), and the DocReview/Stitcher rapid-edit-then-undo timing (the intermittent render-mirror race) ‚Äî confirmable only in the live app.

### B644 ‚Äî Scheduler embed: first COLD boot throws `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'projects')` + the loader overlay exceeds the 6s backstop (self-recovers) (√ó2) `[Scheduler / robustness]` (bug ‚Äî first-paint hardening) #scheduler  *(Cowork live verification 2026-07-03, from V153; minted **B644** = highest real B# across both files (B643) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî self-verified headless 2026-07-18 (V211 PASSED).** Original report: the FIRST cold Site‚ÜíSchedule switch on planyr.io threw a one-time `‚öÝÔ∏è This view failed to load ‚Äî Cannot read properties of null (reading 'projects')` (~10.5s overlay, self-recovered on re-nav).
- Verify: live
- Recurrence: 2026-07-05 ‚Äî owner repro (chat "NEW-5", DEDUPE-FIRST fold ‚Äî same error string, same first-paint null-`projects` read): create a project via **Start blank ‚Üí Schedule tab** ‚Üí the same error renders **behind the connect-a-schedule modal**. Deterministic (not only the cold race); expected a clean empty state only.
- **Root cause (pinned by a live sandbox repro, not a guess):** NOT the render path ‚Äî the `if (!data) return <loader/>` gate was already sound. The **shell‚Üíiframe nav bridge** (`planar:nav-select` / `nav-select-by-site` / `nav-link` / `nav-create-linked` / `nav-dashboard` / `nav-new` / rename / delete in `public/sequence/index.html`) queues `setData(d => ‚Ä¶)` functional updaters when the shell announces the active project on a tab switch; before the async `hs-v1` load resolves `d` is **null**, and React runs the queued updater INSIDE the next `useState` mount ‚Äî `Object.values(d.projects || {})` throws and lands App in the error boundary. Cold boot = the race version (the bridge message beats the slow cloud load); fresh project = the deterministic version (no schedule linked, load still pending). Captured stack: updater ‚Üí `Object.useState` ‚Üí `App`.
- **Fix shipped (minimal, matches the in-file `planar:view-set` precedent):** one early gate in the nav-bridge handler ‚Äî drop `planar-shell` messages until `latestData.current` exists (the shell re-posts nav intents on every tab switch and retries `nav-request`, so an early drop self-heals the moment the load lands) ‚Äî plus null guards in the three bridge-called helpers (`addProject` / `renameProject` / `deleteProject`) so a future caller can't regress the class.
- **Sandbox-verified 2026-07-05:** `ui-audit/verify-b644-fresh-null.mjs` (self-contained, fetches the four CDN deps once via curl, serves them by request interception): pre-fix it reproduces the exact owner error + stack; post-fix it PASSES (embed renders, zero null-`projects` errors). `jsxcheck-sequence` OK ¬∑ build green.
- **‚úÖ Live-verified 2026-07-18 (V211 PASSED, this session):** re-ran `ui-audit/verify-b644-fresh-null.mjs` (still PASS on the merged HEAD) plus a new harness `ui-audit/verify-v211-schedule-coldboot.mjs` (2/2) driving two independent brand-new browser contexts through the cold Start-blank ‚Üí Schedule path ‚Äî no failure banner, no null-`projects` error, clean loading skeleton confirmed by screenshot. planyr.io itself isn't directly reachable from this sandbox's headless Chromium (egress `ERR_CONNECTION_RESET`, the same external-host block documented elsewhere in `VERIFICATION.md`), so the check ran against a local build of the identical merged-HEAD code `main` deploys. The loader-timing observation (item 3) is non-blocking per B494/B495 and unaffected by this fix.

### B895 ‚Äî Yield-panel + pond-card "provenance" readability: SourceTag system, ONE footer disclaimer, consistent watch-out chips `[Site Planner / Yield]` (feature) #site-planner #yield #ui #floodplain #pond  *(owner brief 2026-07-18, chat "yield panel provenance readability" task. Minted **B895 / V374** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-18 (V374 PASSED, deployed Tsakiris).** Presentation-only refactor of how the left Yield panel + pond-card sub-cards (Stormwater/Detention, Floodplain Mitigation, Buildability/FFE, Road Cost, Earthwork Cost, pond inspector) show WHERE each headline figure comes from ‚Äî no computed value, unit, threshold, or classification logic changed anywhere.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V374 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from the owner brief.
- **New central module + component set** (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/provenance.js`, `components/SourceTag.jsx`, `components/SourcesLegend.jsx`, `components/ActionLink.jsx`, `components/WatchOutChip.jsx`, `components/YieldFooterDisclaimer.jsx`): a fixed six-word source-tag vocabulary (**CODE** violet = adopted ordinance/criteria value ¬∑ **PLAN** green = measured from drawn geometry ¬∑ **SURVEY** blue = public measured data ¬∑ **ESTIMATE** amber = derived where nothing published ¬∑ **YOURS** gray = user-entered ¬∑ **UNVERIFIED** red hollow = placeholder default), each with its own theme-token color (new `--source-code-text` CSS token, AA-audited both themes/both surfaces ‚Äî `ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs`), a right-aligned pill + a keyboard-reachable "Basis" popover (built on the existing `RowInfo`/`AnchoredMenu` primitives ‚Äî real `<button>`, focus/blur opens it, Escape closes it, portaled).
- **Sources legend:** one "Sources ‚ìò" popover in the Yield-panel header (`SourcesLegend.jsx`) decoding all six colors ‚Äî a first-time user reads trust level from tag color, full basis one hover/focus away.
- **Disclaimer consolidation (the biggest win):** deleted the ~7 inline "screening only ‚Äî confirm with your engineer / reviewing authority" variants scattered across the panel (Detention rollup, the Stormwater IIFE's trailing caveat, Road cost, Earthwork cost, the pond inspector's storage/rate-control/expand cards); item-specific info they carried (Prismoidal method, "berm-toe" language, etc.) relocated into the relevant SourceTag's Basis popover. Added ONE persistent `YieldFooterDisclaimer` at the bottom of the panel; `test/yieldFooterDisclaimer.test.js` asserts it renders exactly once and the sentence appears nowhere else inline.
- **Watch-out chips:** `WatchOutChip.jsx` is the one consistent ‚öÝ rendering (non-italic bold amber/danger, native-title detail ‚Äî unchanged mechanism from the old `warnNote`/`warnLine`, so the `title=`-based e2e assertions in `ui-audit/verify-b807-zonea-draft100.mjs` etc. still pass unmodified) for item-specific risks (Atlas-14 study required, NGVD29-vs-NAVD88 datum, pond-berm-into-floodplain, dock-approach grade break, "haul/compaction is your engineer's"). `warnNote`/`actionWarn` (Yield drainage IIFE) and `warnLine` (pond inspector) now build on it ‚Äî one definition, ~35 call sites unchanged.
- **Headline figures tagged:** Detention required/provided, Floodplain mitigation required/provided/combined, Required FFE, Road cost paving/curb/subtotal, Earthwork excavation/mitigation-cut/graded-surface/net-dirt/subtotal, and the pond inspector's Stored volume / Pond floor / Provided / Required-rate-based / Existing-Proposed storage rows. `pondAuto.freeboard`/`.slope`'s existing `verified:true/false` flag maps directly to CODE/UNVERIFIED (`classifyVerified`); WSE_PROVIDER_LABEL codes (`bfeSrc`, etc.) map to SURVEY/ESTIMATE/YOURS via `classifyWseSource` ‚Äî both pure presentation mappings of signals the engine already computes.
- **Freshness line standardized:** the drainage status line now reads "As of {date} ¬∑ {source} ¬∑ flood data {age}" consistently whether the facts are remembered or a fresh live check (previously "As of ‚Ä¶ remembered‚Ä¶" vs "Facts as of ‚Ä¶" diverged); the ‚Üª Re-check control rides the same row as an `ActionLink`.
- **Actions distinguished from tags:** `ActionLink.jsx` (the global `--accent`, never one of the six tag colors) now renders "Set BFE ‚Üí" / "Set pad ‚Üí", "Enter rate ‚Üí" (jumps to + focuses the pond's Allowable-release field), "Set $/SY ‚Üí" / "Set $/CY ‚Üí" (jump to the matching unit-price bid field in Road/Earthwork cost), and ‚Üª Re-check.
- **Verified (sandbox):** full suite + build green + `node ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs` (0 fails) + `node scripts/build-map.mjs --check` + `node scripts/build-backlog-index.mjs --check`. `ui-audit/verify-b895-yield-provenance.mjs` (headless, no GIS needed ‚Äî draws a pond + building, no drainage check run): Sources legend opens on click and lists all six words; the Detention-storage row's PLAN tag's Basis popover opens on **keyboard focus** (not just mouse) and names "Measured from your drawn geometry" + the Prismoidal method detail; the footer disclaimer renders exactly once and no stray "confirm with your engineer" text remains ‚Äî 13/13 green. `ui-audit/verify-b718-b722.mjs` (14/14) and `ui-audit/verify-b629-detention.mjs`'s screening-caveat assertion updated for the new footer wording and pass against the built app.
- **‚úÖ Live-verified 2026-07-18 (V374 PASSED, deployed Tsakiris, after a live GIS re-check ‚Äî the freshness line flipped from "remembered" to "live check ¬∑ 3:32 PM"):** the source tags populate correctly against real drainage-check data ‚Äî SURVEY on existing grade (~153.1‚Ä≤, 3DEP), ESTIMATE on BFE (1% WSE), YOURS on the Pad/FFE field, CODE on required compensating storage / criteria, PLAN on pond-geometry volumes. Confirms `classifyWseSource` / `classifyVerified` resolve correctly against real GIS-fed data, not just the pure-function unit tests.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new (no prior provenance/SourceTag/disclaimer-consolidation item). Touches, but doesn't change the logic of: B824 (the drainage-readout redesign this builds on), B862 (StatusChip/FlashValue, reused as-is), B807/B882 (the WSE-provider codes `classifyWseSource` reads), B822 (`pondAutoValues`' `verified` flag `classifyVerified` reads).

### B896 ‚Äî Unify the numeric-value typeface to Inter (tabular-nums, slashed-zero) app-wide ‚Äî retire the second monospace stack `[Site Planner]` (feature ‚Äî design decision) #site-planner #ui #yield #pond  *(owner design decision 2026-07-18, chat "Follow-up" to B895: "why are we using different fonts" ‚Äî words in Inter, numbers in monospace. Minted **B896** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî self-verified headless 2026-07-18.** Per Apple (SF Pro) / Google (Roboto/Material) convention: ONE type family with TABULAR LINING figures for column alignment; monospace reserved for genuinely code-like content, never product data.
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from chat, follow-up to B895.
- **New shared token** `src/shared/theme/typography.js`: `NUM_FONT` ("Inter, system-ui, sans-serif") + `TABULAR_NUMS` ("tabular-nums slashed-zero") ‚Äî a concrete string, not a CSS `var()` (SVG-attribute usage and the canvas/PDF export rasterization can't resolve custom properties, same reason `palette.js` mirrors `index.css` in hex).
- **Repointed at the token:** the Yield panel's `YMONO` constant (renamed `NUM_FONT`, was `"ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace"`), Road/Earthwork cost (`metricRow`), the pond inspector (`pondRow` + release-credit/delta/routing/storage spans), the shared numeric `numInput` style, the coordinate/GPS readout (both `SitePlanner.jsx` and `MapFinder.jsx`), the bottom-left calibration badge, every on-canvas measurement/dimension label (parcel edge lengths, setback pills, easement/trace lengths, the Measure tool's live count/length/area readout, drafting dimension callouts), parcel acreage chips, and the shared cross-workspace `RotationStepper`.
- **Left as monospace (genuinely code-like, or a separate exhibit ‚Äî not swept):** hex color swatches (`PropertyPanel.jsx`), the error-boundary stack trace, deed/title import filenames + recording references + free-text legal description, raw county-appraisal-record / taxing-unit dumps (heterogeneous external data), and the cut/fill heat-map SVG legend (a distinct exhibit, not named in the brief).
- **PDF-PARITY:** mirrored into the print/export path ‚Äî `printSheet.js`'s `SW_MONO` (was the same monospace stack, now Inter) + `yieldBar.js`'s `bulletBarSvg` `mono` default ‚Äî plus the Leaflet terrain contour index label (`terrainLayers.js`) ‚Äî so the exported sheet and the map overlay never drift from the screen. Added `slashed-zero` alongside every existing `tabular-nums` in the export path too.
- **Layout-shift check (no regression):** `ui-audit/bottom-furniture-verify.mjs` 91/91 green at every pane width (scale bar, calibration badge, north arrow, coordinate chip, zoom controls never overlap ‚Äî the calibration badge's scale-ratio text is width-MEASURED via a ref, not fixed, so it self-adjusts). Headless screenshots of the Yield panel, pond inspector, and canvas (acreage chip, setback pill, dimension labels) confirmed no text clipping/overflow with real data. One pre-existing (NOT introduced by this change) placeholder-text clipping bug was found and ruled out via a `git stash` before/after comparison against unmodified `origin/main` ‚Äî the Top-of-bank/Permanent-pool/Receiving-flowline elevation inputs' "optional"/"dry bottom" placeholders were already clipped under the old monospace font (Inter renders very slightly WIDER here, if anything) ‚Äî left unfixed as out of scope (not requested, not a regression).
- **Preserved:** every existing `font-size`/`font-weight` ‚Äî only `font-family` changed, so the numeric hierarchy (e.g. the Yield panel's ~650-weight hero values) reads identically.
- **Verified (sandbox):** full suite 4531 + build green + `ui-audit/bottom-furniture-verify.mjs` 91/91 + headless screenshot spot-checks (Yield panel donut/rows, pond inspector, Road/Earthwork cost, GPS/coordinate readout, calibration badge) ‚Äî no clipping, no overlap, no layout shift.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new (no prior font-unification item); does not touch Doc Review / Scheduler (separate workspaces, not named in the brief ‚Äî a future pass would need its own review).

### B897 ‚Äî Fix the doubled "ESTIMATED (ESTIMATED (...))" copy bug on flood watch-outs `[Site Planner / Floodplain]` (bug) #site-planner #floodplain #ui  *(owner-reported 2026-07-18 from the live Tsakiris B895 verification pass, chat "Follow-up". Minted **B897** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`.)*
`[x]` **DONE ‚Äî self-verified 2026-07-18.** Live text read "1% WSE is ESTIMATED (ESTIMATED (grade @ Zone A boundary)) ‚Äî screening only." and "BFE is ESTIMATED (ESTIMATED (grade @ Zone A boundary))" ‚Äî the confidence word "ESTIMATED" was being prepended to a `wseProvLabel(...)` string that, for all four accepted-estimate provider codes (`est-boundary-grade` / `est-ebfe` / `est-fbcdd` / `est-maapnext`), already reads "ESTIMATED (...)".
- Verify: sandbox
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from chat (owner live-verification report).
- **Root cause:** four `warnNote(...)` compositions in `SitePlanner.jsx` wrapped `` `... is ESTIMATED (${wseProvLabel(src)})` `` (or, for the 0.2% WSE variant, `` `... is a screening estimate (${wseProvLabel(src)})` ``) around a label that already carries the word. Only the two the owner saw live (mitigation 1% WSE, BFE) were literally reported, but the same composition bug existed at two more call sites (FFE's required-elevation 1% WSE and 0.2% WSE readouts) ‚Äî fixed all four, not just the two observed.
- **Fix:** drop the redundant wrapper ‚Äî `` `1% WSE is ${wseProvLabel(src)} ‚Äî screening only.` `` etc. ‚Äî since `wseProvLabel` already returns "ESTIMATED (grade @ Zone A boundary)" for these codes. No change to `WSE_PROVIDER_LABEL`, `classifyWseSource`, or any threshold/classification logic ‚Äî copy composition only.
- **Regression test:** `test/estimatedWseLabels.test.js` ‚Äî documents the invariant (every `EST_WSE_SRCS` label already reads `^ESTIMATED \(`) and source-scans `SitePlanner.jsx` for the two doubling patterns (`ESTIMATED (${wseProvLabel(` and `screening estimate (${wseProvLabel(`), asserting zero matches.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/estimatedWseLabels.test.js` (8/8, +4 new) + full suite 4531 + build green.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new bug (no prior "doubled ESTIMATED" item); the underlying `WSE_PROVIDER_LABEL` labels themselves are unchanged, from B882.

### B892 ‚Äî Public-data screening PHASE 6: access tier ‚Äî traffic (TxDOT AADT), rail access (BTS/FRA), airport proximity (FAA Part 77 proxy) `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature) #gis #site-planner  *(sixth and final phase of the public-data industrial-screening track ‚Äî owner brief 2026-07-18, branch `site-planner/access-tier`. Minted **B892 / V373** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Code/tests carry no B# label; a late renumber touches only this heading + V373.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V373 CONFIRMED PASS.** Three new Site Analysis cards screen the active-parcel footprint for **access** ‚Äî all INFO facts, not pass/fail constraints: **Traffic (AADT)** (the average daily traffic on the nearest TxDOT-counted road, an access/visibility proxy), **Rail access** (distance to the nearest BTS/FRA rail line + its owner; a line **crossing/abutting** the site flags a potential rail-served siding ‚Äî an industrial plus), and **Airport proximity (FAA Part 77)** (distance to the nearest FAA airport as a **proxy** for Part 77 height-restriction surfaces, with a Form-7460 caution near a public-use airport).
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V373 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from the owner brief (public-data screening, PHASE 6 ‚Äî final phase).
- **Reuses the shared proximity engine (PHASES 2‚Äì5), no new engine code:** `aadt` (point, half-mile buffer), `rail` (line proximity, half-mile buffer, `onSiteLabel:"crosses/abuts the site"`), `airports` (point, 3-mi buffer ‚Äî the Part 77 neighborhood). All ride `screenMode:"proximity"` + the `classifyProx` source-supplied classifier hook.
- **Endpoints (registry `aadt` / `rail` / `airports`, all `tier:"production"`):** AADT = TxDOT District/MPO AADT review layer (point; AADT_PRELIM + Located_On); rail = USDOT BTS/FRA North American Rail Network lines (RROWNER1); airports = FAA AIS airports (NAME/TYPE_CODE). Reachability + data verified via direct query 2026-07-18 (AADT ~45‚Äì47k on the Katy I-10 corridor; UP-owned rail, 90 segments within 2 mi of downtown; Hobby + type codes).
- **Access classifier (pure, `lib/accessScreen.js`):** `summarizeAadt` / `summarizeRail` / `summarizeAirports` + `railroadName` (reporting-mark ‚Üí carrier, incl. the Houston PTRA/HBT marks) and `airportTypeLabel` (FAA TYPE_CODE ‚Üí plain). The Part 77 caution fires only for a **runway (AD-type) airport within the caution radius**, and is explicitly labeled a PROXY (the real Part 77 surfaces are computed from runway geometry + an FAA study). New map overlays `txdot_aadt` / `bts_rail` / `faa_airports` (registry URLs, zoom-gated) drive each card's "‚óç Map" toggle.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/accessScreen.test.js` (mark/type labels + AADT/rail/airport summaries incl. blank-road-name + Part-77-caution-vs-distant) + orchestration route in `test/siteAnalysis.test.js` (all three cards in display order, a crossing rail line, a Part-77-caution airport). Full suite (4522) + `gis-source-audit` (30 sources) + MAP/backlog-index + lint (0 errors) + build green.
- **‚úÖ V373 (live, planyr.io, Bain, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Live Site Analysis screening pass on Bain: "Traffic (AADT)" read "~7,060 vehicles/day on FM1463"; "Rail access" read "no rail within 0.5 mi"; "Airport proximity" read "no airport within 3 mi" (no Part 77 caution ‚Äî correct, Bain isn't near Hobby/IAH). Not separately re-tested: a rail-crossing site and a Hobby/IAH-adjacent site, nor the `txdot_aadt`/`bts_rail`/`faa_airports` overlay paints.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new cards; no prior access-tier / AADT / rail / airport backlog item. Reuses the proximity engine (B887) and the registry pattern (B369). Completes the six-phase public-data industrial-screening track (B885/B886 ¬∑ PHASE 2 ¬∑ B889 ¬∑ B890 ¬∑ B891 ¬∑ **B892**).

### B891 ‚Äî Public-data screening PHASE 5: electric power ‚Äî transmission-line easement + nearest-substation service proxy `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature) #gis #site-planner  *(fifth phase of the public-data industrial-screening track ‚Äî owner brief 2026-07-18, branch `site-planner/power-screening`. Minted **B891 / V372** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Code/tests carry no B# label; a late renumber touches only this heading + V372.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V372 CONFIRMED PASS.** Two new Site Analysis cards screen the active-parcel footprint against HIFLD electric infrastructure: **electric transmission** (a line **crossing the site** is flagged as a likely transmission easement ‚Äî no building under it; a line only nearby is context) and the **nearest electric substation** (the distance as a **service / interconnect proxy** for a heavy-power industrial user, an info fact ‚Äî never a good/bad constraint).
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V372 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from the owner brief (public-data screening, PHASE 5).
- **Reuses the shared proximity engine (PHASES 2‚Äì4), no new engine code:** `transmission` is a line-proximity source (quarter-mile buffer, `onSiteLabel:"crosses the site"`), `substations` a point-proximity source (3-mi buffer to reliably capture the nearest in a metro; "none within 3 mi" is itself informative ‚Äî farther from grid). Both ride `screenMode:"proximity"` + the `classifyProx` source-supplied classifier hook.
- **Endpoints (registry `transmission` / `substations`, both `tier:"production"`):** transmission = HIFLD US Electric Power Transmission Lines (Esri Living Atlas org, polyline; OWNER/VOLTAGE/VOLT_CLASS); substations = HIFLD Electric Substations (national point layer; NAME/CITY/MAX_VOLTAG). Reachability + data verified via direct query 2026-07-18 (transmission: CenterPoint 138/345 kV lines near Katy; substations: 18 within 3 mi of downtown Houston). **Rejected** a candidate substation service that held only ~68 South-Texas points (the B369 clip trap ‚Äî a regional subset, not national) in favor of the full continental-US layer.
- **Power classifier (pure, `lib/powerScreen.js`):** `summarizeTransmission` flags a crossing as a likely easement + names the nearest owner/voltage; `summarizeSubstations` reports the nearest-distance service proxy. Cleans the redacted dataset's withheld voltages (0 / "NOT AVAILABLE") and anonymized ("UNKNOWN#####") substation names. Map overlays: the existing `hifld_tx` transmission overlay drives the transmission card's "‚óç Map" toggle; a new `hifld_substations` point overlay (registry URL, zoom-gated) drives the substation card.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/powerScreen.test.js` (voltage/owner/name cleaners + transmission crossing-vs-nearby + substation service-proxy) + orchestration route in `test/siteAnalysis.test.js` (both cards in display order, a crossing line ‚Üí present easement, the nearest substation ‚Üí info). Full suite (97 focused) + `gis-source-audit` + MAP/backlog-index + build green.
