Not separately re-tested: a site with a transmission line crossing the parcel (the easement flag), nor the `hifld_tx`/`hifld_substations` overlay paints.

### B889 ‚Äî Public-data screening PHASE 3: Houston-area active surface growth faults ‚Äî traces crossing / near the site `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature) #gis #site-planner  *(third phase of the public-data industrial-screening track ‚Äî owner brief 2026-07-18, branch `site-planner/growth-faults`. Code/tests carry no B# label; this renumber touches only this heading + V370.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V370 CONFIRMED PASS.** A new Site Analysis card + a Layers overlay screen the active-parcel footprint for **Houston-area active surface growth faults** (aseismic slow-slip faults that crack foundations/slabs/pavement): whether a fault trace **crosses the site** or is **within a quarter-mile**, with the nearest trace's name + distance.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V370 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from the owner brief (public-data screening, PHASE 3).
- **Line-distance engine (extends the PHASE-2 proximity core, reused by PHASE 6 rail):** `lib/proximityScreen.js` now handles **polyline** features, not just points ‚Äî `segmentsIntersectFt` / `distSegSegFt` / `distPathToRingsFt` compute the exact nearest feet from the parcel to a fault trace (0 ft = the trace crosses/touches the footprint), and `featureDistFt` dispatches point-vs-line. `screenProximity` + the `siteAnalysis.js` proximity path now accept `geometry.paths`; a source `onSiteLabel` makes a 0-ft nearest read **"crosses the site."**
- **Source (registry-driven, `shared/gis/sources.js`):** `growthFaults` = the University of Houston GIS republication of **USGS SIM 2874** (Shah & Lanning-Rush, "Principal faults in the Houston metropolitan area"). Marked **`tier: monitored-exception`** (honest): the authoritative USGS dataset is a **download only** ‚Äî no live authoritative REST exists and the USGS-derived AGOL copy is token-gated ‚Äî so we depend on the UH republication (the full dataset, anonymously queryable, 228 traces) until we self-host the shapefile. NW-Houston coverage fixture. `gisSources.test.js` acknowledges the new exception.
- **Map overlay (Layers panel):** `faults` (esriFeature line, zoom-gated), URL from the registry.
- **SCOPED FOLLOW-UP (flagged, not silently dropped):** the brief also asks to *flag individual buildings/ponds straddling a trace*. The pure engine for it is DONE (`distPathToRingsFt` on an element ring = 0 ‚Üí straddles), but wiring a **per-element on-canvas flag** needs the planner to feed building/pond geometries (planner feet ‚Üí lon/lat) into the screen ‚Äî a `SitePlanner.jsx`/`SiteAnalysis.jsx` canvas integration larger than this screening card. Still owed ‚Äî not exercised by the V370 live pass below.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/proximityScreen.test.js` line block (segment intersect/distance, path-crosses-ring = 0, a fault line crossing the parcel ranks at 0 ft) + `siteAnalysis.js` orchestration (a fault line ‚Üí "crosses the site"). Full suite + lint + `gis-source-audit` (25 sources) + MAP/backlog-index + build green.
- **‚úÖ V370 (live, planyr.io, Bain, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Live Site Analysis screening pass on Bain: "Active surface faults" card read "none found" (the honest absent path). Not separately re-tested: a site with a fault trace crossing/near the parcel, the `faults` overlay paint, and the still-owed per-element straddle follow-up above.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new (first fault screen + first line-distance engine). Relatives: B887 (PHASE 2 proximity core this extends to lines), the Site Analysis engine.

### B887 ‚Äî Public-data screening PHASE 2: environmental contamination pre-screen (TCEQ LPST + EPA Superfund/RCRA), by proximity `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature) #gis #site-planner  *(second phase of the public-data industrial-screening track ‚Äî owner brief 2026-07-18, branch `site-planner/environmental-prescreen`. Minted **B887** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Code/tests carry no B# label; a late renumber touches only this heading + V368.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V368 CONFIRMED PASS.** New Site Analysis cards + Layers overlays screen the active-parcel footprint for nearby environmental contamination ‚Äî **count, nearest distance, and names within ~1 mi** ‚Äî explicitly labeled a **Phase I ESA PRE-SCREEN, never a substitute**.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V368 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-18 from the owner brief (public-data screening, PHASE 2).
- **Shared engine (the reusable nearest-distance core):** `lib/proximityScreen.js` (pure) ‚Äî projects parcel rings + feature points to **EPSG:2278 feet** and returns count + nearest-distance + ranked names within a buffer (0 ft = on/under the site), plus `fmtDistFt`. This is the core Phases 5 (power) and 6 (rail) reuse. The fetch/interpretation seam is `siteAnalysis.js`'s new `screenMode:"proximity"` path: a **server-side `distance`-buffered `/query`** (distance+units) with returned geometry (exact nearest measured in feet, not degrees), the true total via `returnCountOnly`, honest present/absent(verified)/unknown/unavailable states, riding the same SWR cache + B366 resilient fetch.
- **Sources (registry-driven, `shared/gis/sources.js`):** `lpst` = TCEQ `Public/LPST` MapServer (statewide leaking-tank sites; `SITE_NAME`/`REM_PROG`). `epaCleanups` = EPA "Cleanups in My Community" FeatureServer (Superfund NPL + RCRA; `MAP_SYMBOL_CODE` ‚Üí a program tag via `epaProgram`). Both production, each with a Pasadena/Ship-Channel coverage fixture; both support server-side distance buffering (verified live 2026-07-18). Replaces the old **pending "contamination"** placeholder row.
- **Map overlays (Layers panel):** `env_lpst` (TCEQ MapServer `/export`) + `env_cleanups` (EPA FeatureServer vector points, zoom-gated). URLs from the registry, no inline endpoint.
- **Verified (sandbox):** `test/proximityScreen.test.js` (10 ‚Äî feet geometry, real-projection distances, on-site=0, nearest-first ranking, bad-coord skip) + the `siteAnalysis.js` proximity block (`buildProximityParams`/`epaProgram`/present/on-site/absent/unavailable) + the orchestration test extended. Full suite (4482) + lint (0 errors) + `gis-source-audit` (24 sources) + MAP/backlog-index + build all green.

Not separately re-tested: an on-site/adjacent source ("on/under the site"), and the `env_lpst`/`env_cleanups` overlay paints.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new ‚Äî first proximity/nearest-distance screen and first contamination pre-screen. Relatives (not dupes): the superseded pending "contamination" row, the Site Analysis engine it extends, B886 (Phase 1 CCN ‚Äî sibling public-data screen).

### B886 ‚Äî Public-data screening PHASE 1: water & sewer CCN service areas ("who is certificated to serve the site") `[Site Planner / Analysis]` (feature) #gis #site-planner #entitlements  *(first phase of the public-data industrial-screening track ‚Äî owner brief 2026-07-18, branch `site-planner/ccn-service-areas`. Wired as two `ANALYSIS_SOURCES` rows (`ccnWater`/`ccnSewer`) ‚Üí the existing `SiteAnalysis.jsx` cards; both drive the one `ccn_service` map overlay (B190 "‚óç Map").

### B850 ‚Äî Scheduler's "open in another tab" banner was oversized, falsely claimed a read-only lock, and turned out to be unnecessary entirely `[Scheduler / Shell]` (bug) #scheduler #ui #infra  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 in chat, with a screenshot: "now fix it for the scheduling module, i dont know that its a bug but i dont need this large pop up" + "especially bc its just me that has it open even if i have it open in other tabs"; owner then pushed back further in the same thread ‚Äî "shouldn't it just auto-reload... if I have it up in two tabs" ‚Äî which triggered a second, deeper AUDIT-FIRST pass in the same session. Minted **B850** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main`. Branch `claude/loving-albattani-mdqvse`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED this session in TWO passes ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V335 CONFIRMED PASS.** Screenshot showed the shared B313 "this project is open in another tab" banner as a large, bold, full-width (660px), non-dismissible dark strip covering part of the Scheduler's grid ‚Äî for a benign case (the owner, alone, with the same schedule open in two of their own tabs).
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V335 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat (owner screenshot + three follow-up messages).
- **Pass 1 (banner too big + wording wrong):** the banner text claims "Only one tab can edit at a time; the others are read-only until you take over editing there or close them" ‚Äî true for Doc Review (which wires the shared `editorLock`, `usePersistence.js`) and pre-multiwriter Site Planner, but the **Scheduler never adopted that lock at all** (grepped `src/workspaces/scheduler/` and `public/sequence/index.html` ‚Äî zero hits for `editorLock`/read-only enforcement). Shrunk the banner from a bold 660px dark strip to a small dismissible pill (theme tokens replacing hardcoded hex `#3f2d12`/`#f59e0b`, a KEY DECISIONS violation fixed along the way) and gave the Scheduler its own honest copy via a `lockEnforced` prop.
- **Pass 2 (the owner was right that it shouldn't need ANY notice) ‚Äî a deeper AUDIT-FIRST pass on `public/sequence/index.html` found the Scheduler is genuinely safe for two tabs, full stop:** its embedded app already runs a "multi-device live-refresh" ‚Äî every 20s, plus on focus/reconnect/tab-switch, it asks the cloud whether a newer version exists. A clean, backgrounded tab reloads **silently**. A tab you're actively looking at (or that has unsaved edits) gets a small one-click "Reload" banner instead of its screen being yanked mid-look. Separately, the save path's Layer-0 concurrency guard **BLOCKS** (never applies) a write that would clobber a newer cloud version, and the blocked copy is snapshotted to Version History ‚Äî nothing is silently lost. So a second Scheduler tab was never actually read-only, AND routine two-tab use converges on its own without the user needing to think about it ‚Äî the outer generic banner was adding noise, not safety, and the embedded app's own precise in-context "a newer version was saved" notice already covers the one real case (a blocked save) better than any cross-workspace banner could.
- **Final fix:** the `lockEnforced` dual-copy complexity from Pass 1 was removed entirely (dead once no caller needed the softened variant) and the banner reverted to its single, always-accurate "read-only" copy ‚Äî now correct for its only remaining reachers (Doc Review; Site Planner only when the multiwriter escape hatch is off). `Scheduler.jsx` instead passes **`multiEditOk`** (the same flag Site Planner/Library already use to suppress the banner outright), so the Scheduler shows NO cross-tab notice at all ‚Äî matching what the owner expected.
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new ‚Äî the sibling B313 banner (added originally for Doc Review/Site Planner) never had a Scheduler-specific correctness pass; not the same class as B846/B759/B812 (those are the Site Planner's realtime element-sync toasts, a different subsystem/transport/module entirely).
- **Verified (sandbox):** 3 source-guard tests in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` ‚Äî the banner is theme-tokened + dismissible + re-arms on a new episode + carries only the single accurate copy (no `lockEnforced` remnant); the Scheduler passes `multiEditOk` with genuinely no `editorLock` wiring; and a THIRD guard reads `public/sequence/index.html` directly to confirm the actual safety mechanism the suppression relies on (the 20s/focus poll's silent-reload-when-hidden vs. one-click-when-visible branch, the Layer-0 concurrency guard blocking a stale write, and the `stale-block` Version History snapshot). Full suite (4036) + lint 0 errors + build green. The banner only renders `accountActive` (signed in) ‚Äî the sandbox's egress proxy blocks the Supabase auth handshake, so the visual click-through is signed-in-only ‚Üí parked live.
- **‚úÖ V335 (live, planyr.io, two signed-in tabs, Goose Creek, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Opened the same Goose Creek schedule in two signed-in tabs simultaneously ‚Äî no "open in another tab" banner appeared in either tab, confirmed via both page text and an accessibility-tree check. Not separately re-tested: the real-conflict path (edit both tabs within a few seconds) and the Doc Review banner-unchanged comparison (avoided to not touch real markup data).

### B829 ‚Äî Remembered check credits GROSS pond volume as detention (surplus/short verdict flips on reload) `[Site Planner / yield ¬∑ persistence]` (bug) #site-planner #pond #yield #persistence  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-14 as "NEW-9"; minted **B829**)*
`[x]` Live repro 2026-07-14, Bain: the owner's live check reads Detention ‚àí54.73 ac-ft SHORT (req 76.54); a fresh tab restoring the remembered snapshot reads +42.78 ac-ft surplus = exactly gross 119.32 ‚àí 76.54 ‚Äî the restored path loses the per-pond dead-storage split (WSE/pool inputs absent) and falls back to gross-as-usable: an optimistic fabrication, the B804 class but for detention, and worse (it flips the verdict, not hides a row).
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V323 passed)
- Origin: filed 2026-07-14 from chat ("NEW-9").
- Root cause (AUDIT-FIRST, confirmed in code): `slimDrainageContext` drops flood polygons by design ‚Üí hydrate returns `floodGeo:null` ‚Üí `fmZones` empty ‚Üí `pondSplitFor` gets `wseFt:null` ‚Üí `usablePondVolume` falls through anchored/estimate to its `gross` branch ‚Üí `deadCf` 0 ‚Üí `providedUsableCf` = gross.
- Fix: persist the split INPUTS (per-pond `{wseFt, inTrigger}` + the check-time `estPoolDepthFt`, provenance-stamped) as `settings.drainage.lastCheck.detSplit` ‚Äî the B804 slim-mirror pattern ‚Äî and restore them inside `pondSplitFor` (the ONE split seam); where absent (legacy snapshots that carry flood zones), demote to "usable unknown ‚Äî ‚Üª re-check", NEVER gross (LOUD-FAILURE).
- Regression test: a restored context must never report usable > live-computed usable for the same plan.
- Recurrence weighed (DEDUPE-FIRST): NOT re-opened as a B708 recurrence ‚Äî B708's "never present gross as usable" invariant governed the live-compute path it shipped; the split's persistence layer was never built (unbuilt scope, not a non-sticking fix). Sibling of B804 (slim-snapshot honesty family).
- **IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-14 ‚Äî live-verified 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V323 CONFIRMED PASS.** `lib/pondLedger.js` (new): `accumulatePondLedger` ‚Äî the site fold extracted pure, with the LOUD-FAILURE contract that any `factsKnown:false` entry poisons usable/dead/mitCandidate to null (gross keeps summing ‚Äî a geometric fact). The guarded persist effect now writes `lastCheck.detSplit = { screened, fmZonesSig, byId: {pondId: {wseFt, inTrigger, estPoolDepthFt}} }` beside the B804 mitigation slim (ONE setSettings ‚Äî no autosave ping-pong; JSON-safe, `?? null` never undefined); `pondSplitFor` (the ONE split seam all consumers share) replays restored facts (persisted pool estimate wins over re-derivation), and a legacy snapshot with flood evidence demotes to `mode:"unknown"` ‚Äî never the gross branch. Honest null rendering everywhere: verdict "usable unknown ‚Äî ‚Üª re-check", provided row labeled (gross) + the OVERSTATES warnNote, Surplus/Shortfall suppressed, the B655 card reads "unknown ‚Äî re-check", the auto-size solver refuses, the print pair reads UNKNOWN (PDF-PARITY). `test/pondLedger.test.js`: slim‚Üírestore round-trip exactness across a WSE sweep + the invariant *restored usable is never a number > live usable*; headless `verify-remembered-drainage.mjs` cases C (SHORT survives reload against a gross that would fake surplus) + D (legacy ‚Üí unknown, no fabricated verdict either way). Full suite + build green.
- **‚úÖ V323 (live, planyr.io, signed in, Bain, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. Bain's live Yield panel Detention verdict reads "SHORT ‚àí54.28 ac-ft req 76.74" ‚Äî the figure has drifted slightly from the ‚àí54.73 in the original spec (presumably real data changed since filing), but the qualitative invariant this item guards is intact: a SHORT verdict, never a gross-credited surplus.

The router bbox-tests the site point (`projectToGrid`, ~1,000 ft seam pad) against extents BAKED into the registry row's `multiplex` table, samples every candidate in parallel, takes the MAX finite (governing WSE); all-empty ‚Üí honest null; ANY candidate failure rejects the whole call (LOUD-FAILURE); zero candidates ‚Üí zero fetches. `_100YR_LOS_WSE` (level-of-service basis) + Depth/DxV products are EXCLUDED by pattern. Wiring: the drainage check's Fort Bend gate `Promise.allSettled`s BOTH samplers (per-value failed/empty/loaded states, supersede token kept); the value rides `fmElev` + BOTH memo sigs (`mitigationForFootprint`, `pondFloodFacts`) + the pond-split call. ### B846 ‚Äî Idle second same-account tab makes cascade-derived elements fire false "you (another window) changed X you just edited" toasts `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug) #site-planner #persistence #ui  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-15 as "NEW-1" ‚Äî the TWO-tab path of the 7/14 multiwriter-cascade handoff (`claude/handoff-2026-07-14-multiwriter-cascade-false-conflict.md`, never minted until now ‚Äî the miss that motivated B847); minted **B846** via `npm run next-id -- --against-main` ‚Äî RENUMBERED from a provisional B844 on merge (PR #633, the Scheduler meeting-calendars pair, took B844/B845 first; code/tests keep the provisional NEW-1 label per the house rule). Branch `claude/loving-albattani-mdqvse`.)*
`[x]` **IMPLEMENTED + sandbox-verified 2026-07-15; live-confirmed 2026-07-19 ‚Üí V334 CONFIRMED PASS.** Repro (owner, √ó2: 2026-07-14 truck-court depth; 2026-07-15 Bain `smr9olizi5ue` building edit ~15:55Z): two signed-in tabs of one account on one plan, second tab idle; a cascade edit in the active tab ‚Üí burst of B673 remote-upsert toasts for the DERIVED elements the gesture re-laid (paving area, sidewalks, parking fields ‚Äî never the element actually touched), each "you (another window) changed a ‚Ä¶ you just edited ‚Äî their version is showing"; bonded aprons can visibly separate for a beat; refresh clears it.
- Verify: live ‚Äî CLOSED (V334 passed).
- Origin: filed 2026-07-15 from chat (owner-reported 2026-07-14 + 2026-07-15).
- **Root cause (per the handoff diagnosis, confirmed in code):** (1) `processResults` stamped `recent` for EVERY ok result with no user-touched vs derived distinction, so cascade outputs answered `authoredRecently` ‚Äî any foreign-looking echo of them toasted "you just edited"; (2) since B674 the second tab is a live writer, and the pending-edit journal was ONE KEY PER SITE (`planyr:elements:pending:<siteId>`), so an idle tab's refetch could fold-and-clear the active tab's mid-commit journal ‚Äî re-committing its in-flight edits as byte-divergent copies that passed every single-tab self-echo guard (`SitePlanner.jsx` conceded the clobber in a comment); (3) `reconcileSeedRows` could substitute MIXED shadow pairings (a newer adopted rev over an older kept json) into a refetch re-seed ‚Äî the "aprons separate for a beat" churn.
- **Fix (all three handoff directions shipped):** **#1** `createElementSync` takes `isDirectEdit(kind,id,el)`; every enqueued op carries a `direct` tag (preserved through conflict/missing/exists re-enqueues; `restore()` always direct) and **only direct ops stamp `recent`** ‚Äî SitePlanner supplies "bonded (`attachedTo`) elements are derived unless the gesture/selection (drag/sel/multi, stamped every render into `directTouchRef`) targeted them". **#2** new exported `semanticallyEqual(a,b,eps=1e-6)` (numbers within eps, key sets must match ‚Äî gates only WHO IS TOLD, never what is written): a semantically-equal live row is now SILENT on all three read paths (conflict branch ‚Üí adopt rev with our bytes, no re-commit ping-pong, no event; pending branch ‚Üí no `remote-while-dirty`; no-pending upsert ‚Üí no `remote-upsert`). **#3** the journal is **per-SESSION** (`planyr:elements:pending:<site>:s:<tab>`, sessionStorage-backed id): a live sibling's journal is untouchable, orphans (frozen `at` > 5 min) + the legacy key are adopted-and-swept, same-tab reload recovery unchanged; plus shadow entries whose json was KEPT under a newer adopted rev are flagged `stale` and `reconcileSeedRows` never substitutes them (fetched row stays canonical; the post-seed reconcile re-commits any newer local truth).
- **DEDUPE-FIRST:** net-new ‚Äî the TWO-tab path. B757/B759/B811/B812 are the SINGLE-tab family (implemented, parked V273/V301/V303/V304) ‚Äî not reopened; their guards are regression-tested unchanged. B673/B674 built the toast matrix + multi-writer cutover this hardens.
- **Verified (sandbox):** 16 new unit tests ‚Äî `test/elementSync.test.js` (derived ops never claim authorship + direct guard + tag survives re-enqueue + restore/fail-open/default-direct; semantic-equality unit cases + all three silent paths + genuine-difference guards; stale-pairing seed hardening + clean-entry B759 behavior preserved), `test/elementJournal.test.js` rewritten (per-session isolation, live-sibling hands-off, orphan adoption + sweep, legacy migration, own-beats-orphan dedupe, reload recovery, session-id stability), `test/elementRows.test.js` (stale guard), `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` (SitePlanner wiring source guards). Full suite + lint 0 errors + build green; e2e smoke/canvas specs green. Element sync runs signed-in only ‚Äî the sandbox can't sign in, hence the live park.
- **Adversarial review (4-lens find‚Üírefute workflow) ‚Äî confirmed findings all fixed same session:** (1) `sweepJournals` unconditionally deleted a FRESH legacy journal `readJournal` had refused to fold (a live pre-upgrade tab's crash protection destroyed) ‚Üí sweep now consumes exactly the read's key set; (2) read/sweep used two `Date.now()` calls, so a journal crossing the 5-min orphan boundary in between could be swept unfolded ‚Üí one shared timestamp; (3) the autosave effect reconciles BEFORE the render-effect stamp, so a single-shot edit after >10s idle enqueued against aged stamps (a directly-edited bonded child mis-tagged derived) ‚Üí `stampDirectTargets()` also runs at the top of `reconcileElems`, same tick as the enqueue; (4) skipKeys-filtered journal entries were discarded with zero telemetry ‚Üí now reported via `onDiscard` (LOUD-FAILURE). **Accepted, documented limitations:** browsers copy sessionStorage on "Duplicate tab", so a duplicated pair shares one journal id ‚Äî for that pair only, behavior falls back to the pre-fix shared journal (strictly no worse; a liveness protocol isn't worth the boot cost, and foldJournal's baseRev guards cap the blast radius); with sessionStorage BLOCKED (but localStorage working) the per-load fallback id makes a reload's own journal look foreign for the 5-min orphan window ‚Äî recovery is delayed, never lost; panel-driven edits to bonded children (e.g. "+ add zone" with the building selected) tag derived ‚Äî quiet-side-only cost (a softened heads-up, never data).
- **‚úÖ V334 (live, planyr.io, SIGNED IN, two tabs, Bain ‚Äî Fort Bend, 2026-07-19):** PASSED ‚Äî see VERIFICATION-DONE.md. A cascade edit (Building 3's Length spinner 769‚Üí770ft) in tab A propagated live to tab B with no manual reload (770'√ó280', 222,200 sf, 55 trailers) and the bonded trailer-parking apron stayed fully attached with no gap/separation; no false "another window changed X you just edited" toast appeared in either tab (screenshot-sampled, not continuous-recorded). Reverting the edit converged both tabs back to the exact original state (221,920 sf, 769'√ó280', 54 trailers).
